description:
Zombies stroll through the city looking for a new resting place. To the residents' horror, they find zombies in the dog basket, in the trunk or even in bed! Your job is to lure the zombies to your graveyard so that peace returns to the city! In Collect a Zombie, you play zombies tactically in the graveyard and collect the zombies on the right grave cards in your personal graveyard log. The more zombies you have of the same color on a grave card, the more points you receive!

—description from the publisher (translated)

VOID: The Frontier is a competitive card game for 2-6 players, where you collect rare Specimens, secure Favors from the locals, and deploy Maneuver cards to benefit yourself or sabotage opponents. Sell completed sets to gain the most points and win the game!

The game begins with three Expedition rounds, where players simultaneously reveal their chosen route. If they choose to travel to VOID, they are dealt cards. If they travel to the Hub, they gain favors.

Each player can then play cards: either Specimens, by laying them down in front of them (their Warehouse), or they can play Maneuver cards, which help the player or hinder their opponents.

Gameplay then moves to Market rounds, where players choose different Transactions, including sell sets of 3 Specimens to score points, or use one of the Favors they have accumulated.

Hidden within the deck are Empty cards. Once 8 of these cards have been drawn by the players, gameplay immediately moves to the last Market rounds and the game ends. The player with the most points from selling sets wins the game.

—description from the designer

A set of 2 new longships and 4 new shields (1 per player) to add to the game.

During setup, the 2 longship tokens are added to the draw bag. Additionally, every player gets 1 of the new shield in their colour. During the game, players can now use this new shield power as well as the other 3 already found in the base game.

Playing the new shield allows players to place their Vikings on any resource spot on the board, ignoring adjacency rules.

During scoring, these new longships give points per axe on your board. So far, this is the only way to score points for axes that isn’t collecting a shield.

The French version of this promo is included in Plato Magazine Issue 165 (May 2024).

Siena Sudlow thought she was living the popstar dream, until she becomes embroiled in a scandal she never saw coming. She needs to escape and has locked away a secret message in an online vault and left 7 clues to unlock the vault – who’s up for cracking the case?

As a bear, try to collect as much honey as possible, but hurry up - as soon as the bees notice the loss, you have to run home with what you have managed to collect.

On your turn, take the top card from any pile lying on the hive card. Check it secretly and take one of the following actions:

     Put the card to your can. Choose any card from your can discard it. Place the acquired card instead of the discarded one.
     Play an action card. Apply the effect of the action card, then discard it.
     Discard the taken card. If you don't want to put a card to the can or play an action card, just put the card you took in the discard pile.


You then end your turn and pass it to the next player clockwise.

During the game, you cannot look at the cards in the cans, pass them to each other or exchange them. This can only be done through the effects of some action cards.

Action cards and their effects

     Binoculars allow you to check any two cards in cans.
     Magic mirror copies the value of the adjacent card.
     Exchange allows to swap a card from your can and someone else.
     The Umbrella will protect the card in your can.
     An Interesting Find will allow you to swap a card from any can with the top discard card.
     The Dance of the bees will make everyone exchange a pair of cards!


End of the game
When any player takes the last card from the pile on any hive card, players continue to take turns until they reach the first player. After that, the game ends - all players turn over the cards in their cans and start counting points. The player with the most points wins!

A more interactive game mode is also available for advanced players.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In this funny game, monster hunters (you!) help the monsters to pacify the town of Gruselbruch. Each round, the monster pointer is increased by the number of monsters on the table. Afterwards a new monster comes into the city which must be defeated along with any older one. To do this, all dice must be rolled at the same time and the items rolled or secured item token from the last rounds must be placed on the corresponding symbols of the monsters.

If a monster is defeated (no free normal symbol slots), you get one point. If even the individual optional symbol field on the monster is still covered, you get two points instead.

For every two Grandpa symbols on the dice you can reveal a helping Grandpa card. The Grandpa dice can be re-rolled at any time if they are not needed to get useful items.

Unused dice can be converted into item token that can be used in the next round as long as you can store them.

The game ends when 10 victory points have been reached or the monster pointer has reached the last field. You win in the first case.

The game where players create their own clues.

It’s the new fun family game where players take turns to create their own one-word clues to the hidden word.

If the clueless player guesses the hidden word from another player’s clue, then BOTH players score points. But the more clues needed to guess the word means less points are scored unless you’re in a DOUBLE or TRIPLE point round.

Fail to guess the word before the marble lands on the final score track space and FATE will play its part!

boardgame: Dune: Imperium – Uprising: The Beast's Spoils Promo Card
description:
This promo card was given out at Dune Imperium: Prerelease Tournaments

Type: Imperator Card
Cost: 3 persuasion
Agent symbol: Blue
Agent Ability: Gain rewards for your face-up battle Icons: 
Crysknife (green): Trash a card, muad'dib (yellow): 1 spice and/or Ornithopter (blue): 1 troop

Reveal ability: Increase combat strength by 3.

In this beautifully decorated game, you use tools to explore asteroids and unearth galactic treasures that earn victory points. But be careful: there are pitfalls, aliens and bot glitches.

The game initially consists of 9 stacks of 9 cards each. In each turn, 2 of 6 tools are available. Only 3 tools can be used at the beginning.

Each round you choose two of the available tools and then reveal 3 face-down cards. You can use the tools at any time to collect score cards or to protect yourself from attack cards. If you reveal an event card, it is executed immediately and then removed from the game.

The game ends when either a player has collected all 3 Holomap Fragment cards or when 3 Black Hole cards have been revealed. The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Full Disclosure: Strip, Truth or Dare Game for Lovers
description:
From the Publisher:
Want to have a sexy night you'll never forget?  With "Full Disclosure" you'll not only disclose all your naughty little secrets, you'll also learn all about your lover's kinks, desires and fantasies - all while getting stripped down in the buff. Our naughty spin on the classic truth or dare game features 120 enticing truth or dare activities and a whole lot of naughty extras.  Do you dare to play?

Decide who spins first, spin, and land on one of the 5 strip activities to determine how many articles of clothing to remove and how.  Continue until both players are naked.

Now take turns drawing the Truth or dare activity cards, reading the card aloud, and performing 1 of the 2 options on the card. Once completed the next player chooses a card and so on.

Bonus Sex Spin: once players are in the buff and disclosed all their naughty little secrets, its time to take it to the next level of sexual fulfillment.  Spin the spinner numbered 1-10, which corresponds to positions on the bonus sex card.  Each player spins once to select a position for the night, and then both players will perform the two selected activities.

Game would probably be considered a cooperative game, as if played correctly you both win, and are guaranteed pleasure for you and your lover.

There are additional options listed for how to play the game.  You can also decide on points for the truth or dare and most points after a set number of cards wins, and winner gets to decide the fantasy reward.

Engage in adventurous affairs with your lover.
The game of seductive fantasies, creative kissing, and intimate foreplay for two lovers.

You and you lover travel around the board engaging in adventurous afairs while acting out a number of different roles and foreplay.

Players control the length of the game by quickly earning Fantasy tokens through foreplay and roleplay actions.  After five tokens are earned, the game ends and sexual fantasy begins. First player to get 5 tokens wins

Can you restore peace to the land and rise as the new emperor? In this suitless trick-taking game, 2-6 players will lead their clans of samurai cats into battles and negotiations, building their political influence in premodern Japan.

Players will send their envoys into each encounter and can call on fate to transform them into alternate ranks. Although it is a time of warring factions, each season ends with a peace treaty where players must follow a strict etiquette to avoid bringing disgrace upon their house.

—description from the publisher

ded. is a whacky social deduction game that features three decks of cards: role cards, action cards, and personality cards. The groups is split in two teams, the Reapers and the Neighbors.

In ded., the neighbors are being killed off by the treacherous Reapers. The neighbors need to identify and eliminate the Reaper before all the neighbors are murdered.

ded. is played in rounds, each round consists of a Reaping where players can be killed, protected, and saved. The second part of each round is the Town Council, where the players vote to eliminate the Reapers.

The game ends with the Reapers eliminating all the Neighbors, or the Neighbors eliminating all the Reapers.

An exploration and adventure game designed for one or more players set in a dark fantasy world where monstrous creatures watch over ancient treasures and powerful magical objects hidden in the shadows of the most dangerous dungeons.

The players slip into the roles of improvised adventurers, whose dubious pasts will often lead them to unlikely and glorious fates.

Dungeon Tales Dark Fantasy Adventures is a modern version of the great Old School tabletop RPG and Dungeon Crawler classics of the 1990s.

Ideal to be played both with pen and paper or with 28mm and 32mm scale miniatures with modular dungeons tiles.

"Grab your favorite weapon, make sure you have memorized the best spells and don't forget to fill your backpack with torches and healing potions. You're about to tell your first... Dungeon Tales!

—description from the designer

The Advanced Game System III is an EXPANSION for the famous Board Game: HeroQuest. It is compatible with the expansions: "The Mage Of The Mirror" and "The Rise Of The Dread Moon" (new and old editions).

With the Advanced Game System, you can choose a "Class" and "Level Up" your Hero. You can also purchase "Advanced Equipment" during the game.

—description from the publisher

A crazy chaotic collect 5 card game about cute dogs and collecting bones.

Keep your friends close and your frenemies closer as you attack protect and collect your way to victory!

—description from the back of the box

Game play:
Play dog cards to collect bone cards, steal them from other players or protect your own. If you collect 5 bone cards, you win.

Each year the forest fairies host the most remarkable festival.  The highlight of the festival is the bubble tournament.  Champions from all over the forest kingdom gather to collect the most magic bubbles from the each of the treetop platforms.

The champions blow bubbles out on to the platforms not knowing which colour bubbles will emerge.  All they need to do is combine enough bubbles of their colour to collect them.  As the competition gets closer to the final, some cheeky fairies even nudge a few playful hedgehogs onto the platforms, to pop bubbles as they appear.

Can you become the fairy champion at the bubble tournament this year or will you leave empty handed?

In Disappearing Act, players compete to pop as many of their bubble tokens off the board before the bag is empty. By combining bubble tokens of the same colour to the appropriate level, players can remove stacks of their tokens… beware though. When creating stacks of your own colours, you may also create stacks of colours for your opponents too. The player with the most bubble tokens of their colour off the board wins the game.

Use powerplay cards to gain rule breaking benefits but beware each one you trigger will cost you some points at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

It has to be a joke! Playing high and low with a card and flicks!??

That's right, now you can carry the ball in your pocket and play high and low anywhere!

Remember to register who owns the ball and go in search of your new high and low record! Want to share the ball with the crowd? So play high in a group and look for a record as a team!

The player or group that manages to tap the card the most without letting it fall wins the game!  So don't let the ball drop!

Vegas 3478 is a game of luck in which players want to run out of cards as quickly as possible.
Each turn, the active player rolls a small roulette wheel present in the game, and the result of this will give the possibilities of action for this player, ranging from discarding cards of a certain value from their hand to making their opponents draw cards.
Some high-risk plays also exist in roulette, allowing players to bet to try and win the round immediately, but risking their opponents doing so.
When a player runs out of cards in their hand, everyone else must add up points for the remaining cards and a new round begins.
The game ends when one player reaches a certain number of points. At this point, the player with the fewest points is the winner.

It's time to turn the tables on these dragons!
Each damsel lures dragons into their OWN dungeon.  To determine which dragon goes to which damsel, the damsels secretly assign mini poker hands to each dragon.  High hand takes the dragon and adds it to their dungeon.
Each round a new display of dragons to lure is presented.
The first damsel to fill their dungeon with all 6 types of dragons wins!

—description from the publisher

You are going to through the best slumber party ever!  And that means getting a lot of guests and the most popular guests to come to your party.
Then you make your guest pillow fight your opponents' guests, so they leave.
Each turn players play one card into their tableau-their slumber party.  This card represents 1 guest, and that guest has a pillow power you use to attack your opponents' slumber parties.
Play out the deck and when the deck runs out, whoever has the most guests wins!

—description from the publisher
Caveiras de Sedlec is the boxed Brazilian version of Skulls of Sedlec, containing the base game plus the 10 first expansions in a bigger box than the wallet original version.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description: This is a Simpsons-themed Cards Against Humanity clone. Follows the standard Cards Against Humanity rules.

In the enigmatic city of Ulthar, it is well known that no feline can suffer the slightest offense. Thus, countless cats, with their piercing and mysterious gazes, are drawn to this adorable refuge. However, even these innocent-looking creatures have arcane desires and earthly needs such as food, hydration, fun, and rest. Who can help the Cats of Ulthar?

We present a card game for 1 to 4 participants, recommended for ages 4 and up, where players are challenged to satiate the hidden desires of these kittens.

Among the needs to be satisfied, the one who fulfills two of these will emerge victorious in the match!

Objective
Complete TWO Ulthar Cats Quests to win and finish the game.
Each letter of need needs FIVE points to complete.
Playing Cats of Ulthar cards with a need is worth one point. If the letter has the same need printed twice, it is worth TWO points.

Gameplay
The game is played through several rounds consisting of three turns each.
In each round:

     Receive three cat cards;
     First Turn: Playing a cat card on a need card.
     Second Turn: Playing a cat card on another need card (you can't play two cards on the same need in the same round).
     Third Turn: Playing a cat card on a need card (not yet used in this round).
     Pass the First Player card to the player on the left, who shuffles and deals three cards to each player to start a new round.


—description from the designer

Na enigmática cidade de Ulthar, é sabido que nenhum felino pode sofrer a mais leve ofensa. Assim, inúmeros gatos, com seus olhares penetrantes e misteriosos, são atraídos para este refúgio adorável. Contudo, mesmo essas criaturas de aparência inocente possuem desejos arcanos e necessidades terrenas como alimentação, hidratação, diversão e repouso. Quem poderá ajudar os Gatos de Ulthar?

Apresentamos um jogo de cartas para 1 a 4 participantes, recomendado para maiores de 4 anos, onde os jogadores são desafiados a saciar as vontades ocultas desses gatinhos.

Entre as necessidades a serem satisfeitas, aquele que cumprir duas destas emergirá vitorioso na partida!

Objetivo
Completar DUAS necessidades dos Gatos de Ulthar para vencer e finalizar o jogo.
Cada carta de necessidade precisa de CINCO pontos para ser completa.
Jogando cartas de Gatos de Ulthar com uma necessidade vale um ponto. Caso a carta tenha duas vezes a mesma necessidade impressa vale DOIS pontos.

Gameplay
O jogo é jogado através de várias rodadas formadas por três turnos cada uma delas.
Em cada rodada:

     Recebe três cartas de gatos;
     Primeiro Turno: Jogar uma carta de gato em uma carta de necessidade.
     Segundo Turno: Jogar uma carta de gato em uma outra carta de necessidade (não pode jogar duas cartas na mesma necessidade na mesma rodada).
     Terceiro Turno: Jogar uma carta de gato em uma carta de necessidade (ainda não utilizada nesta rodada).
     Passar a carta de Primeiro Jogador para o jogador à esquerda, este embaralha e distribui três cartas para cada jogador para iniciar uma nova rodada.


—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Through engaging and interactive challenges, children embark on a journey of discovery and conquest, where numbers and operations become valuable allies in the quest for knowledge.

Conquistadores da Matemática is an educational card game, developed in the environment of municipal elementary schools.

Aimed at students from 4 years of age, this game is an ingenious tool that aligns fundamental mathematical skills with the guidelines of the São Paulo Curriculum and the precepts of the National Common Curricular Base (BNCC).

'Objective
To win, the player must complete the TEN mathematical operation cards correctly before their opponents.

Gameplay
Each player is dealt ten mathematical operation cards.
The cards with the results of the trades are left open in the center of the table within reach of all players.
When given the command to start, the players simultaneously open the mathematical operation cards and place the result of each operation on top of each one.
At the end of the resolution of the ten cards, if the player who finishes correctly first is the winner of the game.

—description from the designer

Através de desafios envolventes e interativos, as crianças embarcam em uma jornada de descoberta e conquista, onde números e operações se transformam em aliados valiosos na busca pelo conhecimento.

Conquistadores da Matemática é um card game educacional, elaborado no ambiente de escolas municipais de ensino básico.

Destinado a estudantes a partir dos 4 anos de idade, este jogo é uma ferramenta engenhosa que alinha as competências matemáticas fundamentais com as diretrizes do Currículo Paulista e os preceitos da Base Nacional Comum Curricular (BNCC).

Objetivo
Para vencer o jogador deve completar as DEZ cartas de operações matemáticas de forma correta antes dos seus adversários.

Gameplay
Cada jogador recebe dez cartas de operações matemáticas.
As cartas com os resultados das operações ficam abertas no centro da mesa ao alcance de todos os jogadores.
Ao ser dado o comando de começar, os jogadores simultaneamente abrem as cartas de operações matemáticas e vão colocando em cima de cada uma o resultado de cada operação.
Ao final da resolução das dez cartas, caso o jogador que terminar primeiro de maneira correta, é o vencedor do jogo.

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

boardgame: 커피 러시 프로모: 파주 장단콩 (Coffee Rush: Paju Jangdankong Promo)
description:
Coffee Rush has released two promo cards in 2024 Paju spiel.

When setting up the game, return two random order cards to the box, then shuffle the promo cards together with the order card deck for the base game.

Three player only and three suits, with a rock-paper-scissors relationship. Must follow. High card of lead suit wins, unless RPS trumping occurs. After 3 tricks, bid with a card from your hand (rank=bid) how many tricks you’ll win. Goal is first to win three hands. Winner of a hand is a hierarchy - all tricks, no tricks, hitting your bid, most tricks.
designers: ジュウゴ ゴトウ (Jyugo Goto)
artists: ジュウゴ ゴトウ (Jyugo Goto)

description:
Missionaries embark on a journey across the world evangelizing. With 40 missions to play and an ever-changing game dynamic this game offers a replay value. Play, build, travel, and trade in this worker placement game. Journey and build with the custom made 3D pieces across the board, our beautiful world, strategizing your way to victory as the greatest missionary. Watch out as your opponents may use distraction cards to slow you down on you travels. Play a miraculous card to change the very fabric of the game. Be the first to complete 2 mission trips by connecting your your trip with crosses and upgrade the beginning and endpoints with churches. On your turn draw a card. Choose one of 4 actions: play, build, travel, trade. Set out to be the first to complete your mission trips.

—description from the designer

Little turtles just came into being and need to reach water as soon as possible! Their path will not be easy, as they are so small, and the coast is grassy and pebbly here and there—not to mention that crawling on the sand is not easy at all! And to top it off, a stealthy pelican is waiting for a misstep to disturb them.

In Миссия Черепашки ("Mission Turtles"), players help baby turtles make their first journey: to reach the water despite all the obstacles.

boardgame: Хранители ключей против Дракона (Key Keepers vs the Dragon)
description:
In the game Хранители ключей против Дракона ("Key Keepers vs. the Dragon"), the players need to find the right keys to all the locks and save the Princess until the Dragon climbs to the top of the tower.

The game's rules are straightforward yet strategic: players roll a die and strategically choose which key to try on the lock of the corresponding color. They then flip the lock and check if the key fits. Each lock has only 1 unique key, and there are 16 of them in the game.

The locks are arranged so that the keys can only be inserted from the backside. Therefore, players will not only have to try their luck but also memorize which keys are needed to the already-tried locks.
At the same time, the fearsome dragon rises to the princess if the Dragon side falls on the die.
The player's goal is to open all the doors in the tower and get to the princess before the fearsome Dragon.

All players act together, win or lose together, so the game is ideal for the youngest players and will teach them to memorize the location of the right components and enjoy saving the princess as a family.

—description from the publisher (translated)

On Nekojima, the landscape of the Cat Island transforms with the construction of the Torii. You’ll need to install the electrical poles with even greater caution around this sacred gateway. But that’s not all: the famous fortune-bringing cat, the Maneki-neko, roams from district to district. Is it your lucky day?
The Yggdrasil Expansion is a mini expansion that adds new custom meeples and Blessing abilities to the existing game.

The first expansion for Karak 2 brings a number of new features, most notably the player vs player mechanics and the new Imperium mode. In the new mode, players compete for the Emperor's Crown, which brings great power but also great responsibility, in addition to the usual objective. The expansion also introduces five new heroes, two new monster types and several new landscape types.

—description from the publisher

Expansion for card game Karak: Goblin that adds two new game modes: alternative boss fights and Survival. Boss Fights that use new boss action card decks are now much more exciting and dynamic (the expansion also adds two new bosses to the roster). Survival mode is played simultaneously and players have to face the waves of ever stronger opponents (this mode use draft mechanics) while there is no respawning. In addition to these, the expansion also adds two new heroes and new potion type.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Пілоти: KBP Бориспіль (Sky Team: KBP Boryspil)
description:
An additional airport for Sky Team.

The expansion represents KBP Бориспіль (Boryspil) airport located near Kyiv, Ukraine.

Available as pre-order promo for Ukrainian edition of the Sky Team (Пілоти).

Note: Available in Sky Team: Turbulence.

OctoWords is a flip-and-write word game.

In the first phase, players will flip letter cards, allowing them to choose 4 letter pairs to add to their page. After adding 8 letters, they will then flip 8 word cards, and use these to create a crossword on their word grid.

Then, in the second phase, players will fill in their own cross word with the letters they chose, to create as many words as possible.

When everyone is done, words are scored, bonuses are assigned, and a winner is announced!

The game is scalable for different age and skill levels.

—description from the designer

Get your character as big as you can.  These women want to appear bigger and will do so by any means necessary.  They'll use padding, botanicals, and even tattoos!

But you have to keep your methods a secret, or you opponents can expose you and knock you down a size.

In this card game the cards are dual purpose.  They can either upgrade your size, or attack your opponents to expose them or knock them down a size.

The first character to get to and keep the largest size wins!

—description from the publisher

Rail Line is a simple yet clever card game set in the world of trains calmly puffing throughout the landscape.

Each player becomes one of the railroad tycoons of the Industrial Revolution - your tracks will slice through the landscape, steam trains puffing in their wake. In practice, it's very simple - players play cards, with each card having three tracks that build on each other, crossing and intersecting in various ways.

—description from the publisher

Nutventure is a children's game by the debuting author Eliška Holubová and Albi’s very own Marek Vejborný. The players will take control of squirrels trying to hoard nuts for a winter. They will move on and around the 3D-tree using 3 coloured dice and try to plan their movements to avoid the predators – fox and owl – that can scare them and strip them of their loot.

—description from the publisher

Your team of paranormal eliminators answered the call and raced to a farmhouse in Ecto-1.  Now work together to roast some ghosts and solve a spooky mystery.

Typical Clue game, with a Ghostbusters flair, farmhouse locations and the addition of paranormal cards which you draw if you roll a magnifying glass on you movement roll.  With the paranormal cards, you and another player either battle a ghost, which if you win, you can name a ghost and if someone has that card they place it faceup.  Or you have a PKE Surge!, where you and another player battle to determine a location.  If you win, you name a location and if someone has it they place it faceup.

Based on the 2021 movie "Ghostbusters Afterlife"
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
Millions of years ago the planet Valerius was once the jewel of the galaxy, filled with an abundance of resources and home to a grand civilization known for its advanced technology.

Suddenly, a massive asteroid slammed into its surface, tearing the planet apart. From this destruction arose a thousand moons, each carrying the shattered remnants of Valerius' golden age.

With the promise of a new life and the chance to unlock the mysteries of a forgotten civilization, aliens from across the universe flocked to these moons forming moon colonies. As the colonies continued to evolve and adapt, they developed the technology needed to stabilize the mother planet and harvest Valerius' untapped resources essential for the advancement of their own worlds. This race to the top ignited an epic interstellar war, where each species now fights fiercely to claim the riches and secure their future among the stars. The battle rages on, with the fate of the entire galaxy hanging in the balance. Will your civilization survive? The battle is on.

TO WIN
As the leader of a moon, players must hire alien mercenaries to extract resources from the planet, while engaging in battle with rival moon colonies who also attempt to advance their civilizations. To win, be the first player to collect 21 Vallerium.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fall of Berlin: The End of the Thousand Year Reich
description:
Fall of Berlin, 16 April - 2 May 1945, The End of the Thousand Year Reich is the second game in Michael Taylor's Russian Front Series.  It is an operational-scale game covering from 16 April to 2 May 1945 in the area around Berlin: from the River Oder, Frankfurt an der Oder and Seelow in the east, and from Torgau and the River Elbe in the south-west, through to the forests north of Berlin at the northern edge of the map.

There is some subtlety in play to generate the correct strategy.  Designer Michael Taylor said:  “….. you will probably lose badly until you figure out the right strategy”.  There are three options for playing the game.  The Basic game is 7 turns long; the Extended game is 9 turns; and the third option is a 3-player game with 2 Russian players competing against each other (which can be either 7 or 9 turns).

Game scale:  About 12-18 km per hex; 2 days/turn (except for 1st turn which covers 16 April 1945 only); 7 or 9 game turns long.  German units are mostly depleted divisions.

—description from the publisher

Objective:  
Master the ramp and showcase your extreme sports skills to become the VertStyle champion.

Setup:  

      Each player chooses a color and takes the corresponding game pieces: a character card, six trick chips, and a scoring pad.
      Set up the common game area with the ramp and scoring zone.


Game Play:  

      Players take turns performing tricks on the ramp. Use your trick chips to execute maneuvers.
      Special judges give extra points for specific tricks each round, enhancing the strategic depth.


Scoring:  

      Play spans three rounds in a Best-of-Three format, where the highest single-round score determines the winner.
      Record scores on the scoring pad. After three rounds, the player with the highest score in any single round is declared the champion.


Winning:  

      The player with the highest score in any of the three rounds wins the VertStyle contest and is crowned the champion.


—description from the designer

Ziel des Spiels:  
Beherrsche die Rampe und zeige deine Fähigkeiten im Extremsport, um der VertStyle-Champion zu werden.

Spielvorbereitung:  

      Jeder Spieler wählt eine Farbe und nimmt die entsprechenden Spielfiguren: eine Charakterkarte, sechs Trickchips und einen Wertungsblock.
      Richte die gemeinsame Spielzone mit der Rampe und der Wertungszone ein.


Spielablauf:  

     Die Spieler führen abwechselnd Tricks auf der Rampe aus. Nutze deine Trickchips, um Manöver auszuführen.
     Spezielle Richter vergeben in jeder Runde Extra-Punkte für bestimmte Tricks, was die strategische Tiefe erhöht.


Punktezählung:

     Das Spiel umfasst drei Runden im Best-of-Three-Format, wobei der höchste Punktestand einer Einzelrunde den Gewinner bestimmt.
     Punkte werden auf dem Wertungsblock notiert. Nach drei Runden wird der Spieler mit dem höchsten Punktestand in einer der Runden zum Champion gekrönt.


Spielende:  

     Der Spieler mit dem höchsten Punktestand in einer der drei Runden gewinnt den VertStyle-Wettbewerb und wird zum Champion gekrönt.


—description from the designer (German)

Objective:
Return all the monkeys to their enclosures within a set number of rounds before time runs out.

Setup:

     Place the forest plan in the center and arrange the base station and 12 monkey enclosures around it.
     Distribute 24 monkey/forest tiles facedown in the forest.
     Depending on the difficulty level, place 1-3 banana tiles as time markers on the base station.


Gameplay:

     The Director starts at the first empty enclosure. The Keeper indicates whose turn it is.
     Active players reveal as many forest tiles as there are missing monkeys in the current enclosure. Finding the right monkey places it in the respective enclosure.
     Incorrectly revealed monkeys can be repositioned to facilitate future searches.
     After each turn, all revealed tiles are turned facedown again. The Director moves to the next enclosure needing monkeys.
     Crossing the base station removes a banana tile, reducing the number of rounds.
     The game ends when all monkeys are correctly placed or no tiles remain in the forest.


Difficulty Levels:

     Easy: 3 banana tiles, 4 rounds.
     Medium: 2 banana tiles, 3 rounds (recommended).
     Hard: 1 banana tile, 2 rounds.


Note: This cooperative game can also be played solo.

—description from the publisher

Ziel des Spiels:
Bringe innerhalb einer festgelegten Rundenzahl alle Affen zurück in ihre Gehege, bevor die Zeit abläuft.

Spielvorbereitung:

     Legt den Waldplan in die Mitte und platziert rundherum die Basisstation sowie die 12 Affengehege.
     Verteilt 24 Affen-/Waldplättchen verdeckt im Wald.
     Je nach Schwierigkeitsgrad legt 1-3 Bananenplättchen als Zeitmarker auf die Basisstation.


Spielablauf:

     Der Direktor startet am ersten leeren Gehege. Der Wärter markiert, wer am Zug ist.
     Aktive Spieler decken so viele Waldplättchen auf, wie Affen im aktuellen Gehege fehlen. Der Fund eines passenden Affen führt zur Platzierung im entsprechenden Gehege.
     Falsch aufgedeckte Affen dürfen umpositioniert werden, um die Suche in späteren Runden zu erleichtern.
     Nach jedem Zug werden alle aufgedeckten Plättchen wieder umgedreht. Der Direktor bewegt sich zum nächsten Gehege mit fehlenden Affen.
     Überquert der Direktor die Basisstation, wird ein Bananenplättchen entfernt, was die Rundenanzahl reduziert.
     Das Spiel endet, wenn alle Affen korrekt platziert oder keine Plättchen mehr im Wald sind.


Schwierigkeitsgrade:

     Leicht: 3 Bananenplättchen, 4 Runden.
     Mittel: 2 Bananenplättchen, 3 Runden.
     Schwer: 1 Bananenplättchen, 2 Runden.


—description from the publisher (German)

Wherever You May Roam...
Metallica and Monopoly have collaborated once again to bring you an all new, second edition of this American classic.  This time we're celebrating life on the road, including the World record breaking, Whiplash-filled year of 2013 when Metallica became the first and only band to perform on all seven continents. Tour around the globe with your collectible token to some of the band's favorite cities in North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Australia, and Africa.  You'll even find yourself trapped under ice in Antarctica!
Purchase property with your Metalli-bucks, acquire all the cities on one continent, and add arenas and stadiums to increase your value.  Land on one of the "Carpe Diem" or "Shortest Straw" spaces and be rewarded with sticks, picks, and snake pit passes, or fined with parking and stage repair fees.  Travel the world, Metallica-style, while building your real estate empire!  Hit the Lights!

The game has a simple rule: play a card from your hand that matches the colour or shape of the one on the table.  
With short rounds, Shapes helps kids learn through play, enhancing motor skills, focus, and emotional resilience along the way!

Shuffle the deck and deal 3 cards to each player. Place one card face-up in the middle of the table.

Taking turns clockwise, each player must play a card from their hand that matches the colour or shape of the card on the table, placing it on top of the pile. If a player cannot make a match, they draw a card from the deck and pass their turn.

The first player to run out of cards wins the game.

What was once a gambling game played by fairies is now an 'Earthly' dice-based, push-your-luck game with elements of deck-building.

The goal of the game is to accumulate 'Glim', the  mysterious energy the powers the fairies' magic. 2-4 players choose one of four 'Dominions', each with unique tactics and styles of play.

Players roll up to six custom eight-sided dice and deploy influence tokens to win additional tactic cards, creating increasingly complex strategies as the game progresses.

With beautiful art and a deep backstory, Glim is both a game and an artifact of the 'Emerald Anvil' fantasy universe.

—description from the designer

Fridge, Fork, Fruit... STOP!
Grab a question, read it aloud, and draw a letter. Take turns answering the question, and keep the game rolling. Stay alert – shout "STOP" to test your luck if you get stuck.

Game Instructions

Objective:
The goal of this game is to answer questions creatively and avoid accumulating points. The player with the fewest points after 10 rounds wins.

Setup:

     Separate the cards by their different backs (QUESTION cards, LETTER cards and STOP cards).
     Take a paper and pen for scoring.
     Choose an object to mark turns and give it to the first player.


Gameplay:

     Starting the Round:
         Flip over one QUESTION card and read one of the questions out loud.
         Flip over a LETTER card.
    
     Answering:
         Everyone who has an answer to the question raises their hand.
         The first player answers the question first, followed by each player taking turns clockwise.
         The round ends when a player cannot answer the question. That player gets a point.
    
     Special Action * "STOP":
         Players can also say "STOP" and try their luck with the Stop cards if no hands are up.
    


Ending the Game:

     After 10 rounds, the player with the fewest points wins!


Scoring:

     Keep track of points on the paper.
     Players earn points when they cannot answer a question.


Winning the Game:

     The player with the fewest points at the end of 10 rounds is declared the winner!

In Worta, you step into the shoes of a farmer!
Grow your garden by planting vegetables, and strategically use rabbits, foxes, droughts, and barns to have the highest vegetable production by the end of the game. 
However, no farm is complete without animals, and in this game, they might determine the real winner!

Game Instructions

Objective:
In this game, you are a farmer striving to cultivate the best garden. Your goal is to gather animals and grow vegetables to earn the most points.

Setup:

     Separate the decks.
     Give each player 2 distinct vegetable cards (place them face-up on the table) from the blue deck.
     Deal each player 2 additional cards from the blue deck.


Gameplay:

     Drawing Cards:
         On your turn, draw 2 cards from the blue deck.
    
     Playing Cards:
         Play 2 cards by discarding them onto the pile and utilising their power.
    


Scoring:

     Every vegetable you cultivate in your garden adds 1 point to your score.
     The game ends when a player gathers 4 different animals. Completing this set adds an additional 4 points to the total score.


Winning the Game:

     Count the points from your cultivated vegetables and any bonus points from gathered animals.
     The player with the highest total score is declared the best farmer in the county!

Step into the role of a curator in this art game.
Build unique collections by combining paintings according to their groups. Make strategic decisions about which art pieces to spare or trade, all while pursuing your objective. 
Earn points for each painting added to your gallery and strive to curate the most valuable exhibition to win.

72 paintings recreated for kids, full of colours and fun.

Game Instructions

Objective:
In this game, you are a museum curator tasked with creating an assigned exhibition. The goal is to curate the best collection of art pieces and earn the most points.

Setup:

     Separate the cards by their different backs.
     Give each player 1 Exhibition card and 5 Painting cards.
     Your play area is your museum, where you will place the collections of art pieces to exhibit.
     You can exhibit any collection, even if they are not part of your exhibition objective.


Card Breakdown:
The game consists of 72 cards divided into 6 categories of paintings:

     Still Life
     Sacred and Myth
     Portrait
     Landscape
     Masters
     Genre Scene


Each category has 12 cards:

     Paintings: 5 points
     Masterpieces: 10 and 15 points


Creating Collections:
To create collections for display, players must assemble either:

     3 cards of the same category, or
     3 masterpieces from distinct categories.


Art Market:

     On the right side of your play area is your Art Market, a place to put your surplus cards or any other cards you want to trade.


Gameplay:
1. On your turn, draw 2 cards by collecting from the deck or trading on the market.
2. Before ending your turn, you can display collections in your play area.

Winning the Game:

     The first curator to complete their Exhibition card earns an additional 15 points and ends the game.
     The winner is the curator with the most points from exhibited art pieces.

This is a game of hidden roles.
Each player receives a role at the start of the game, which must be kept secret. While most of the players are hotel staff, trying to unmask the villains, the few villains want to remain incognito and generate doubts in the other players.
In addition to a role, each player is dealt a hand of random cards, which can contain traps, Scooby Snacks, monsters and clues. In the complete deck there are more monsters than other types of cards.

On your turn, you must choose one of the characters from the Scooby-Doo gang to carry out their action, which ranges from simply changing cards in your hand to making players put down unopened cards on the table. When this happens, all the played and unopened cards are shuffled and revealed, one by one, until a monster appears, causing the villains indicator to move one space towards victory. However, if any clues are revealed, the Scooby gang that walks towards discovering the villains.
So, through bluffing and clever use of their cards at the right times, the hotel staff try to discover the identity of the villains and the villains try to arouse suspicion about other players.

The game ends when a villain is discovered or when the progress indicator of the villains or the Scooby gang reaches its final space.

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to the world of strategic cyber warfare, where nation state hackers engage in a constant battle for global network dominance.  Can you become the ultimate Advanced Persistent Threat?

Players begin by choosing an Adversary card to represent them and placing the rest back in the box.

The game consists of Staging Servers, Production Servers, Software, Malware, Exploits and Zero Days.

To begin, players take a Staging Server, Small DDOS Zero-Day card, and reference card and place them on the table in front of them.  This is your Network into which you will be playing cards

Shuffle the remaining cards and deal 5 to each player, placing the rest of the deck face down in the center of the table.

Decide who goes first and take turns going clockwise around the table.

Each turn is made up of 4 steps.

First, check your Network for any cards with mandatory or optional “beginning of your turn” effects.  These trigger now.

Second, draw a card from the deck and place it in your hand

Third, play one card from your hand.

Production Servers, Staging Servers, and Software are played into your Network. Malware is played into other players' networks, and Exploits can be played for an instant effect. When playing any card, read the effects out loud and perform their action.

Software and Malware card effects apply to the whole Network.

Software has positive effects and can only be installed above a server that does not have any other Software or Malware installed above it

Malware can be installed on top of Software canceling out the Software effect.

If a Server is Uninstalled, Deleted, or Stolen, any Software or Malware installed above that Server are also discarded or stolen with it.

If a Server instructs you to “return this card to your hand” only the server card is returned.  Any cards above it are discarded.

Exploit cards can be played for immediate effects and must then be discarded

You can also play as many Zero-Day cards as you want, during any players’ turn

Fourth and lastly, discard down to a maximum of 7 cards

The first player to have 6 servers in their network wins!

If the deck runs out of cards before any player reaches 6 servers, the player with the most servers wins.

—description from the designer

A variant covering the Battle of North Cape, December 1943, an attempt by the German battlecruiser Scharnhorst to attack convoy JW55B.  In real life, the Scharnhorst was sunk by the battleship HMS Duke of York and accompanying British cruisers including HMS Belfast (still on display in the River Thames).

This is an extension of "Arctic Disaster" (from ATO magazine #47) and that game is required to play "Immer Voran!".

Published in ATO Annual 2021

Players become developers of rockets launching artificial satellites. Outwit other researchers and become the leader to ensure a successful launch. It's a bluffing and deduction card game for 2 to 5 players.

—description from the publisher (translated)

プレーヤーは人工衛星を打ち上げるロケットの研究者になります。
他の研究者を出し抜きリーダーとなって打ち上げを成功させましょう。
ブラフと推測が鍵の2〜5人向けのカードゲームです。

—description from the publisher
publishers: ピーナツデザイン (Peanuts Design)

boardgame: The Sixth Realm: Echoes of the Cavern
description:
The ancient curse stirs deep below the Sixth Realm. As the poisonous fog returns, the six races unite once more to uncover the source of the curse and break it. Their research led them to a vast underground cavern, and inside they discovered the ancient runes that both detail the curse, and the way to dispel it.

Each section of the curse board is paired with a certain Envoy and Seal type. As those elements are gathered by players, the cursed enegy at the center of the board spreads to their regions, giving scoring penalties to affected players.

The Prophecies of the cavern are visions of the future as seen detailed in the ancient sigils. When a player satisfies the condition of a Prophecy, they will be able to remove curse tokens from the inscriptions, limiting the spread of the curse and earning victory points for their heroic deed.

Desperate times call for desperate measures. Queen Tabitha realizes that this is a time to call in the experts with Royal Summons to save this new realm. These allow players to gain several envoys at once, giving them more control over the colors they take. These Royal Summons will then need to be paid off, before another one can be issued.

boardgame: Blanc Manger Coco 7: Le bonheur partagé
description:
In this ultimate party game, complete the blank sentence with one of your cards to create the funniest, shocking or offbeat combination! Trigger the Question Master's laughter to win!
The 7th opus of Blanc-manger Coco is a co-creation of the authors of the game with the fans of Blanc-manger Coco who, through their card proposals and their votes, have contributed to constituting this collaborative and multi-thematic volume by nature.

Soluna Explorers is a dynamic card game that offers a simplified D&D experience in under 15 minutes per round.

In Soluna Explorers, players set up a 5x5 grid of location cards, with a boss card at the end. During their turn, players spend gold to move as a group, revealing and battling enemies along the way. Combat involves rolling dice to attack, using loot cards for advantages, and surviving enemy attacks. Players earn gold and loot by defeating enemies. The game is won by the player who deals the final blow to the boss. Quick to learn and play, Soluna Explorers offers an exciting and collaborative adventure. Or you can attempt to complete a journey all on your own.

The game is perfect for both seasoned adventurers and newcomers alike.

—description from the designer

The colors of the world have been stolen and everything has turned gray. But the guardian of the clouds has a plan: she has summoned brave guides like you to lead the Sky Painters, winged creatures with tails that emanate magical paint. Together, you can restore the planet's vibrant beauty. Your task is to complete sky cards with colored dice and recruit tiles from the most talented Sky Painters to achieve this mission. Are you ready for the challenge?

Sky Painters is a strategy game for 2 to 4 players. The game's rhythm is very simple: you place dice to complete cards as contracts. Completed cards grant you spot chips and heart tokens, which you then use to acquire Sky Painter tiles and unlock their powers. The real challenge lies in the players' tension, as there are three ways to win:


     Completing 9 cards in a 3x3 grid.
     Completing a 5-pointed star with 5 Sky Painter tiles.
     Having the most points at the end of the game, if neither of the other two conditions are met.


Who will become first the greatest master of colors?

—description from the publisher

How Brave is Mave? is a quirky card and dexterity game designed to entertain the entire family. Similar to Jenga and the Water Cup Challenge, this game features a goat named Mave and is played off the edge of a table. The objective is to keep Mave from taking a nosedive off the path during your turn. If Mave falls on your turn, you lose and everyone else wins.

—description from the designer

All the faces are scattered on the table.
When an emotion is revealed, the game kicks off! 
Players need to find the identical ones – and fast! 
Beware, someone might spot them before you! 
This fast-action card game is ideal for helping kids learn about emotions while enhancing coordination and agility.

Game Instructions

Objective:
The goal of this game is to find sets of three cards with the same face. The player with the most cards at the end wins.

Setup:

     Separate the cards by their different backs (white deck and black deck)
     Spread the white deck face-up on the table.
     Shuffle the black deck and remove 2 cards.
     Flip over the top card of the black deck to show everyone.


Gameplay:

     Finding Matches:
         Look at the flipped-over card 
         Find 3 cards on the table with the same face
         Tap the cards, say their colour, and take them for yourself.
    
     Continuing Play:
         Continue flipping over cards from the black deck and finding matches.
         Repeat the process until the black deck is empty.
    


Ending the Game:

     When the black deck is empty, count the number of cards each player has collected.


Winning the Game:

     The player with the most cards is declared the winner!

You just finished your Bachelor’s studies in Necromancy, and you kind of like this type of magic. You are thinking Yes, I can raise dead, but they only last a couple of hours or so…that is not enough to make a dent in the World. And so, you venture forth to become an even more knowledgeable and skilled necromancer. In this solo print-and-play 10–15 minute card game, you have to complete a Master’s degree in Necromancy in two semesters by completing courses and raising dead study buddies. However, completing courses drains your sanity. Will you succeed? Or will you become an insane fiend haunting the university’s corridors?

To graduate in necromancy, you have to a) collect at least 45 credits from the completed courses, b) finish your thesis, and c) avoid insanity in the process of the academic year consisting of two semesters. Each turn you draw a card from the current semester deck. If it is a course card (which is mostly the case) you decide whether you:


     sign into a new necromancy course but you lose some sanity,
     discard the card to raise dead to get a bonus from an already completed course,
     discard the card to push forward your thesis a bit,
     put the card on the "Maybe later" pile that gets shuffled later into the second-semester deck.


In terms of categorizing this card game: It is a light-weight engine-building game since your completed courses allow you to sign in to courses with higher requirements but bigger rewards; it is a push-your-luck game since you can strategize but you also don't know what is coming next in the deck; there are deck-building elements involved in the creation of the "Maybe later" pile that gets shuffled into the second-semester deck; finally, Graduate in Necromancy is highly replayable - the two semester decks are different every playthrough.

—description from the designer

Each player starts with 5 letter cards and chooses one to play openly in their reserve. On their turn they can:


     play, where they must 1) play a letter card into the common area (as a first, somewhere in the middle, or last letter of a word), and 2) choose one card to place in their personal reserve. Optionally, they can replace a card from their reserve with a card from the common area. The letter put into the common area can go anywhere in the word.



     pass. If a player chooses to pass, they place all their cards from their reserve into their face down points pile.


Players end their turn by drawing letter cards until they have 4 in hand. The player who passes first takes the first player card. Once a single player who has not passed remains, that player must announce a possible word made up of the letters in the common area with the letters in the order they were placed. If the word is valid, they place one card from their hand into their reserve, and then place all cards in their reserve into their points pile. If the word is not valid, they must discard all the cards in their reserve. For example, if the cards in the common area are OTAN a valid word could be mountain. At the end of a round the cards in the common area are discarded. Once a player has reached at least 12 cards in their points pile, the game ends. Whoever has the highest total points wins.

The goal of this game is to introduce young kids to card games while helping them learn about and share their feelings. The game can be played in four different ways.

1. Learning about Feelings
Kids explore different feelings by looking at the expressions on Singa's faces. The objective is to collect as many cards as possible, but the real goal is to learn about feelings and have fun!

Setup:

     Separate the decks by their different backs (red and black).
    . Spread the colorful cards face-up on the table (red back).
     Shuffle the rest of the cards, stack them, and place them on the table (black back).


Gameplay:

     Take one card from the black deck and say: "I feel ... (name of the feeling)".
     Ask the child to find the same feeling on the open cards.
     If the child finds the matching face, hand over the card from your hand and compare them.
     If the child doesn't find it, the adult shows the matching card from the table and takes both cards.
     When the pile is over, count the cards!


2. Finding the Feeling
Kids learn about feelings while also memorizing their corresponding expressions. This game enhances memory, attention to detail, and recognition of feelings.

Setup:

     Separate the red deck by their different backs (red and black).
     Spread the black-backed cards face-up on the left side of the table.
     Spread the red-backed cards face-down on the right side of the table.


Gameplay:

     Pick one of the feelings on the table and say: "I feel ... (name of the feeling)".
     Flip over a red card and see if the feeling matches.
     If it matches, claim both cards for yourself and pass your turn.
     If it doesn't match, close the card you just flipped and pass your turn.


3. How Was Your Day?
This game offers a way to explore the feelings both you and the child experienced throughout the day in an easy and fun way. Are you ready to embark on this journey together? The objective is to use different faces to encourage engaging conversations.

Setup:

     Separate the red deck and set it aside.
     Spread the black deck face-up on the table.


Gameplay:

     Take one card from the pile and say: "I felt ... (name of the feeling) today when/because..."
     Take the card for yourself and then encourage the child to do the same.


4. Telling a Story
In this game, you will craft a story together using your imaginations. The goal is to collaborate on telling a story while guessing the feelings indicated by the other player.

Setup:

     Separate the decks by their different backs (red and black).
     Spread the colorful cards face-up on the table (red back) and remove the rest of the cards (they can be used as a guide, if necessary).


Gameplay:

     The adult starts the tale by saying "Once upon a time..." and begins talking about the place and the main character.
     Let the adventure roll and remember to keep the child engaged.
     When ready, add a feeling to your story. Pick a card that matches the feeling you want, then say, "The (name of the character) feels... (show the card)".
     If the child guesses the feeling correctly, give them the card and invite them to continue the story from where it left off. Encourage them to do the same.
     If they guess wrong, reveal the feeling and take the card for yourself. Continue the story, and when ready, select another card to repeat the process.

A social assessment game.

Players take on the role of a political party seeking election in the 2024 UK General Election. Each round, one player acts as the Voter and selects from the policies presented by the other players. Players earn votes for putting forward the policy selected, and for correctly predicting the Voter's intentions.

Dixit Meets Politics.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Datos No Opiniones: La Historia de la Selección
description:
How many seconds passed between the Hand of God and the Goal of the Century? How many matches did Messi play for the national team before scoring his first hat-trick for Argentina? Or what is said to be the "fatal minute" for the national team?

Datos No Opiniones: La Historia de la Selección includes facts about achievements, feats, life (and some blunders) of Argentina's National Team and those who crowned it with glory!

On your turn, you are going to grab the first question from the deck and, without seeing the answer, you are going to place it to the left or right of the card you used as your starting point. If you think the answer is a greater number than the one in the data row, you put your card on the left and, if you think it is smaller, on the right. When you have chosen where to put the card, you turn it over to see if you were right. If the card is placed correctly, it's the next player's turn, if it's wrong, you take it out and keep it. If you place two cards wrong, you are immediately eliminated.

As the game progresses there will be more cards in the data row and you will have to take them all into account when placing the question that comes your turn.

The little town of Fallbrook, once haven of demons and threshold of apocalypse, is doomed. Despite the victory of the Angelic Host over the great serpentine beast, they could not stop the cataclysmic earthquakes that shook the town to its’ very foundations. A sinkhole opened in the wilderness just beyond the edge the town has revealed a castle old as time, and uncovered a new plot. Demons sworn to a secret rebellious sect: The Council of The Fallen. Now a saint has returned to Fallbrook. Guided by heavenly visions, and prophesying doom. Walking alone and facing insurmountable opposition against forces both spiritual and corporeal, he has few bulwarks against failure. Except you. His guardian angels.

Deliverance: Council of The Fallen is an expansion to the acclaimed Deliverance board game, featuring 6 new playable angels, new minions and Princes to battle, a challenging Campaign that further explores the mysteries of Fallbrook, an arsenal of new cards and effects that add even greater variety and replayability to the base game, a 5th player mode, and more. Delve deeper into the secrets of the demon infested castle, and determine the fate of Fallbrook through your choices.

In this expansion to Deliverance, experience a branching campaign where your ultimate level of victory depends on your choices and secondary objectives earned throughout the campaign. Will Fallbrook be destroyed? Who will survive its destruction? Or will you triumph and see it granted total deliverance?

—description from the publisher

A set collection card game where you produce the perfumes you have collected and earn rewards.  Each turn players will potentially collect perfumes, use a once-per-game refresh card, or sell perfumes.
publishers: 世紀末Games (End of the Century Games)

boardgame: Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory – Rapid Resolution Promo Card
description:
A promo card delivered at UK Games Expo 2024.


Rapid Resolution
Spend 1 Influence to call for an Immediate Vote on a Bill already proposed by another player. Place this Influence on your preferred Vote Result area on the board before drawing Voting cubes. (Award Victory Points as normal, based on who proposed the Bill.)

Pizza Roles is a ten-minute "filler" hidden roles game where your role is what you want on your pizza but are too timid to say.  Play simulates a conversation about what kind of pizza to order with each player vying for what they want while trying not to seem overbearing.

Eight Topping cards represent the game state. Gameplay consists mostly of moving these Topping cards back and forth between the "not on the pizza" zone and the "on the pizza" zone. Each player is dealt a secret Role card showing their objectives in the game and a small hand of Conversation cards they can play to manipulate the Topping cards.

Players take turns either playing a Conversation card from their hand to change the Toppings or choosing to pass. Players can always choose to pass, but the game will end as soon as the majority of players pass in succession.

When the game ends, players reveal their secret Roles and any Toppings in the "on the pizza" zone will score. Points are totaled and all players with a positive score are joint winners.  Sometimes, everybody wins!

There is also a cooperative mode with slightly modified rules:  Players must work together to make sure that everybody is happy with a pizza because if a single player loses, ALL players lose.  Luckily, players make two pizzas instead of one and only score using the best pizza for each Role.

—description from the publisher

A game for people who will never make it in Hollywood.

The object of Greenlight is simple: come up with the best movie idea. Players develop their movie ideas by selecting cards that complete a one-sentence synopsis of the movie. Each round, one player acts as “Studio Head” and chooses their favorite movie to greenlight. The player with the most greenlights at the end of the game wins.

Greenlight is a game for people who have no business being in the movie business.

Greenlight is a game for you.

—description from the publisher

Atrapamoscas y Mosquitos is a skill based single card game for 1 or more players. Each player makes 3 attempts, in which players throw up the card and try catch it. They get points equal to the number of flies they are touching (10 in total) multiplied by the number of fireflies (3) that remain untouched.

But be aware! A mosquito bites you if you touch its sting but not its body. If you do, you don't win any points!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Step into the sweetest arena in Sugarpow: Dice Battle Royale, where confectionery champions clash in a fast-paced, sugar-fueled showdown! This 2-5 player party game combines quick thinking, strategic drafting, and thrilling dice battles to create an exhilarating gaming experience.

In Sugarpow: Dice Battle Royale, players draft their team from a pool of 33 unique candy-themed fighters, each with their own special abilities and powers. Will you choose the fierce Caramel Champion, the agile Yummy Gummy, or the unpredictable Licorice Ninja? With a diverse cast of characters, each game offers new strategic possibilities and endless replayability.

Once teams are assembled, the battle begins! Players take turns rolling dice to determine their attacks and trigger special abilities, aiming to outmaneuver and outlast their opponents. The game's combination of speed and strategy ensures that no two battles are alike, and quick reflexes and tactical thinking are equally important.

Victory goes to the last team standing in this delectable duel of dice and wits. Whether you're a seasoned gamer or new to the tabletop scene, Sugarpow: Dice Battle Royale offers a deliciously fun and engaging experience for all.

—description from the publisher

When the stakes rise, there’s only one solution: a stick of dynamite! Light the fuse and inflame your game nights with BANG! Dice Explosion.

Includes:

     BANG! The Dice Game
     BANG! The Dice Game: Old Saloon
     BANG! The Dice Game: Undead or Alive
     Four brand new BANG! The Dice Game characters: (Flint Westwood, John Pain, Big Spencer, Teren Kill, AKA "the Brave Four")


—description from the back of the box

description:
Status: A Victorian Affair is a worker placement game set in 1888 London, where you compete to build the most prestigious name in high society. As the day progresses round by round, you will place social tokens to take actions such as acquiring furniture and art from a shared market, hosting soirées, and attending grand events tied to the specific time of day. As you build out your estate, you will physically place estate pieces into a unique three-story player house, creating an immersive table presence and unlocking powerful room bonuses on your player board.

The game rewards careful planning and foresight. Interaction is centered on competing for cards in a shared market and carefully timing when to host and join events, rather than direct conflict. Each player chooses a unique character board that provides a special social ability and a distinct set of room bonuses. Replayability is high due to the variety of cards, the unpredictable timing of grand events, and the different combinations of player boards at the table. Victory belongs not to the wealthiest, but to the player who can most cleverly navigate the social season to earn the ultimate prize: Status.

—description from the designer
family: Theme: Rooms: Interiour Design, Home Decoration, Tidying

description:
Embark on a journey and explore some of Canada's most iconic sights.

This game allows players to immerse themselves in the beauty and diversity of Canada's landscapes and landmarks. From the majestic Rocky Mountains to the stunning Niagara Falls, players will have the opportunity to discover and appreciate the country's natural and cultural treasures.

Collect pairs of cards based on iconic Canadian locations, then use Block cards to defend your stack while using Swipe cards to steal your opponents'! Be the player with the highest stack when the last card is flipped over to win!

boardgame: It's Kind of a Fun Story: Pride Pack – Expansion Pack
description:
Extra cards for a game made by actual queer people, for actual queer people.

It’s time for coming out stories, celebrating your growth as a part of the LGBTQIA+ community, and forcing your friends to get into quick drag, for points! Add this deck to your copy of It’s Kind of a Fun Story for a Pride party that you don’t have to wait until June for.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: It's Kind of a Fun Story: Girls Trip – Expansion Pack
description:
We’re bringing shocking truths and thrilling stories to this all-girls getaway.

Unwrap this pack of cards that skip the small talk and make your next girls’ night unforgettable (without the random dares to leave 20 eggplant emojis on a stranger’s social media account). Dish about relationships, sisterhood, and which one of your friends is just a little too into astrology.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: It's Kind of a Fun Story: Straight To Hell – Expansion Pack
description:
Sex, lies, and a rant about that one piece of $%!& you’ve had it up to here with.

This pack filled with taboo and R-Rated cards will instantly turn your copy of It’s Kind of a Fun Story into an after-hours party. Uncover the risqué and hilarious thoughts in your friends’ heads as you reveal your own tantalizing tales.

—description from the publisher

Players compete to be the richest Millionaire!

There are 52 playing cards and 10 million dollar cards in the deck. 2 rule cards one English and the other French.

Each player gets 4 playing cards to start develop your strategy to acquire the most million dollar cards in each round. When your 4 playing cards are gone you grab 4 more from the deck and keep going till all the Millions are gone.

6 rounds, 10 hands in each with battles when 2 or more players play the same highest value card. Creative bets can amp up these battles!

The million dollar cards get totaled every round and the player with the most millions at the end of the 6 rounds is the winner (The Millionaire)

This game can also be helpful for children learning monetary value.

Solar Tactics is an introductory, print-and-play, Tactical level board-wargame inspired by Avalon Hill classics like Tactics II and Blitzkrieg.

Two players face off as the Red and Blue planet commanders in this classic surround and overwhelm style game of conquest. Simple combat resolution, random fleet activation, and thoughtful transport management combine in this beginner friendly game.

—description from the designer
