description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 80 wins the game.

—user summary

Operation Walküre is a single-player-only game that recreates the plot to assassinate Adolf Hitler in Nazi Germany during his dictatorial rule.

Players negotiate with the Allied Forces while gaining generals and civilians as allies, with the aim of establishing a new government after Hitler's assassination. The game covers the period from the summer of 1941, when the war between Germany and the Soviet Union began, to the winter of 1944, when Germany's defeat was assured.

The player controls an anti-Hitler unit consisting of generals, counter-intelligence and civilians, whose goal is to find opportunities to assassinate Hitler and ‘eliminate’ him by increasing their influence by attracting key Allied military and intelligence personnel, German generals and civilians to their side. The Hitlerites, on the other hand, are ‘sabotaged’ by the system's manipulation of the SS and by investigating and placing anti-Hitler and collaborators under arrest and surveillance.

By the winter of 1944, as the war draws to a close, the Fatherland must be saved from the ‘dictatorship’ that is pushing the German people to the brink of destruction.

Players must prepare dishes in chaos and make the most of their preparations. The game plays in three phases:

Phase 1: Preparation
Draw food tiles and prepare dishes according to the customer card.

Phase 2: Chaos
Chaos cards are revealed and mix up the dishes.

Phase 3: Presentation
The chefs present the prepared dishes in their own words.

The chefs then vote for the best presentation and gain stars based on the conditions met on the customer card.
The chef with the most stars at the end wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Les joueurs doivent préparer des plats dans le chaos et tirer le meilleur de leurs préparations.
Le jeu se déroule en trois phases :

Phase 1 : Préparation
Piochez des tuiles aliment et préparez les plats selon la carte client.

Phase 2 : Chaos
Des cartes du chaos sont révélées et mélangent les plats.

Phase 3 : Présentation
Les chefs présentent les plats préparés dans leurs propres mots.

Les chefs votent ensuite pour la meilleure présentation et obtiennent des étoiles en fonction des conditions remplies sur la carte client.
Le chef avec le plus d'étoiles à la fin gagne.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wir fahren und fliegen gen Engeland!
description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

This NS-propaganda game was published as a supplement to the newspaper "Die Post - die Zeitung für jeden" during WW II.

—user summary
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
The corner-pillars are covered with 2 figures of the same color. The figures must travel to the opposite corner-pillar and must take the way over all the four bridges and through a custom-house. The players move by turns. The adversary orders how many spaces the other is obliged to move; he only has the choice to order four, five or seven spaces. For instance white says to blue: "move four spaces!"

Movement is restricted by the following rules;

     Who comes to a red space at the end of the ordered movement is obliged to go back one space.
     Who comes to a white space in the middle of a bridge is obliged to go back three spaces.
     He who comes to one white square in the middle of the custom-house is obliged to return six squares.
     Who arrives at a red square or on a bridge in going back has the right to stop there, only in the custom-house no figure has the right to stop, it must go back to one square in  front of the custom-barrier.
     Who  is at the  move, has the  choice, which of his 2 figures he likes to move.
     If a figure would get on a space, occupied by a figure of another color, it has the right to jump over the adversary or more, if the spaces behind them are free.


The players try to make their adversary's move slowly. This happens if one directs his adversary to a space from which he is obliged to move back a number of spaces. 
The winner is the first player who has his 2 figures at the opposite pillar.

—user summary

A sandwich building game with looney llamas, spy squirrels, and inconvenient camel and more!  Be the first to feed your llama three specialty sammiches!

Play cards each turn to add the necessary ingredients to your sammiches and/or disrupt other players.

boardgame: Monopoly: The Super Mario Bros. Movie Edition
description:
With this action-packed Monopoly The Super Mario Bros. Movie Edition kids board game, fans can explore the Mushroom Kingdom, as they buy, sell, and trade locations from The Super Mario universe! Enjoy iconic Mario artwork, characters, and themes inspired by the film. Gold coins take the place of Monopoly cash in this race to see who can collect the most coins to win. Earn even more by owning matching Property sets. But beware of Bowser, who can swipe properties, steal coins, or send players to Jail! Discover Warp Pipe shortcuts, and gather or lose coins with Question Blocks and Piranha Plants!

—description from the publisher
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Robin / Water Vole Promo Card
description:
Robin and Water Vole Promo Card given at UK Games Expo 2024

Robin Ablity: 1 Point Per Insect
Water Vole Ability: Immediately play any number of cards from hand as tree saplings

Promo Card given at UK Games Expo 2024

Scores +1 for each other animal in the same enclosure.

Gaius Julius Caesar used the Helvetii tribe’s migration as casus belli (cause for war), initiating almost a decade worth of campaigning in Transalpine Gaul and beyond. This supplement follows the year-by-year narrative of Caesar’s campaign, presenting Hail Caesar scenarios for each of the major encounters and battles.

Caesar’s Gallic Wars includes a detailed history of the campaign, featuring 44 scenarios, and army lists for Caesar’s Roman Legions and the Helvetii, Belgae, Gaul, Germani and Briton tribes they fought.

Following the events of Back for Seconds, the culinary kingdoms of the world have been rigorously forging gastronomical gladiators to wield tools of tremendous power, known as the S.P.O.R.K.S. (Super Powerful Object to Revolutionize the Kitchen). Hailing from the beaches of Hawaii, to the rivers of Thailand and even to the sands of Morocco – five new titans of the table have landed and it’s time for a culinary throwdown. If you want to claim kitchen supremacy, it’s time for the food gloves to come off, because it’s SPORKS OUT!

In SPORKS Out, 5 new chefs, championed by 5 new restaurants have joined the ascension for culinary stardom. Each of the new chefs comes equipped with a physical 3D SPORK that fully integrates with their chef power. If you can properly wield the SPORKS, kitchen domination will not be far behind.

SPORKS Out also adds a new phase to the day, the Action Auction. Before Money and Move, players will get to bid with their leftover money from the previous day to buy one or two action cards from the powerful Snackle Box.

—description from the designer

A card game where you will have to defeat your opponent.
To do this you have to play creature cards, advance along the board and reach the end of it to deal damage to the enemy player.

You will have to pay everything with gems. Gems are everything in this universe: the way to get energy and the currency that everyone uses.

You will also use machines and forces that will help you advance faster, avoid your opponents or make them not move.

In your hands you have an investigative case that you can play alone or with friends. It takes little space, a bit of time and a lot of ingenuity.
But remember, one case leads to another!

Six chapters for six compact boxes to carry with you in every situation: the box itself contains the rules of the game, and along with the deck of cards contained in it, it will be enough to get all the clues necessary to the resolution!

Mini Crimes is a cooperative investigative game in which you'll put your observation and deduction skills to the test to solve a mysterious case. You'll analyse a crime scene and up to 10 clues.
Choose carefully and solve the mystery!

In Mini Crimes: The Best Defense, the body of Mike Miller, owner of the Big Brothers Gym, lies a few meters from the doorway of the gym. Still, he didn’t seem to have any enemy…

—description from the publisher

Good Grief is a two-player card game that combines hand management, take-that mechanics, and deck-building in a quirky, supernatural theme. Over the course of five rounds, players take on the roles of a living scientist wife and her ghostly ex-husband, battling for control of the Annoy-O-Meter. Each turn, players play cards to inflict Misery, protect themselves, and save up coins to acquire Griefdoms, which make their decks more powerful. With easy-to-learn rules and engaging gameplay, Good Grief offers a fresh, entertaining experience for casual and competitive gamers.

—description from the designer

A set of 18 tabletop-themed cards for Manic Minute that was given away to people who purchased the game at the UK Games Expo.

Cards include:

     Bosses
     Legendary game weapons
     Fantasy towns and cities
     Things at the UK Games Expo

boardgame: Blood on the Sands: A Game of Gladiatorial Combat
description:
Blood on the Sands is a historically inspired skirmish game that immerses players in thrilling one-on-one gladiatorial combats using miniatures, featuring six distinct gladiator classes, each with its own unique abilities and armaments: Hoplomachus, Murmillo, Provocator, Retiarius, Secutor, and Thraex.

Players roll a set of six action dice that allow you to take direct control of your gladiator. Action dice represent a gladiator’s ability and opportunities to manoeuvre, maintain endurance, defend from, and attack, their opponents. During the course of the game gladiators will become fatigued, wounded, or hindered, losing access to some of their action dice, placing them at a serious disadvantage.

The ambition behind Blood on the Sands is to recreate the dynamic, back-and-forth “dance” of a duel, reflecting the balanced intricacies that the Romans infused into their deadly blood sport. The mechanics produce narrative combats that move across the arena, demanding tactics, skill, and a degree of luck from the players, while avoiding the gameplay devolving into a simple static dice fest.

Promo Card given at conventions and via the online store.

Immediately wins the game if kept in an enclosure with Elephant, Grizzly Bear and Jellyfish.

Promo Card given at AireCon 2024

Scores 3x points but has 0 capacity.

Flip some delicious donuts to reach the number 12! In Donuts by the dozen, players flip numbered donut tiles and try to make equation to reach 12. In the meantime, the other players have bee tokens they can play to steal your donuts by making the right math! Be the first to get a full box of Donuts to win!

—description from the publisher

In Begone, 2-4 players take the roles of Yokai-banishers, trying to send these mythical creatures back where they belong. Of course, this is not as easy as it seems. Moreover, Yokai can be quite useful. Players will balance utilizing the benefits of some Yokai while attacking others in a race to be the first to both score 30 points and banish a Yokai.

As play progresses, players will add Yokai to a communal play area, increasing the potency of the various Yokai powers as they do so. These powers will allow players to manipulate the cards in hand, steal cards from other players, banish other Yokai more easily, or maybe even score points in unique ways.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flesh and Blood: Part the Mistveil
description:
Venture into the cloaked valleys of Misteria to uncover ancient truths and traditions that will reshape your mind, body, and soul! Part the Mistveil features three new heroes across Assassin, Ninja, and Illusionist classes, as well as the incredible new Mystic talent.

Master the sacred arts to transcend beyond your natural limitations, unlocking hidden abilities powered by a unique new resource - Chi! Seduce your enemies into letting their guard down with Nuu, harness the power of Crouching Tigers with Zen, and ward off threats with the mysterious Enigma!

Manic Minute is a fast-paced talking game where players must attempt to speak non-stop for one minute without hesitation, deviation, or repetition on a given topic in order to win some moves and maybe the game!

Sounds so simple but when you're doing the talking and everyone else is waiting for you to 'trip up' so they can hit that bell, steal a move and take over the round, it's anything but easy.

So take a deep breath, grab some family and friends and get ready to play the game where a MINUTE can feel like a lifetime.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Die Abenteuer des Robin Hood: Die Verstärkung
description:
Originally available at SPIEL 2023 at the KOSMOS booth during a signing session with the author.

This mini-expansion comes as a postcard with illustration one one side, and rules on the back.

Rules:
Each time, when a noble escapes, add two violet dice to the pouch.
Each time, when a noble is overcome, add two white dice to the pouch in addition to the regular reward.

German Version:
Anleitung: 
Jedes Mal, wenn ein Adliger entkommt, werden 2 violette Würfel in den Beutel geworfen.

Jedes Mal, wenn ihr einen Adligen überwältigt, werden zusätzlich zur normalen Belohnung, 2 weiße Würfel in den Beutel geworfen.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description: The strategic stacking puzzle game. Roll the die, then stack your puzzle pieces without bridging gaps or hanging off the board. The first player to use all their pieces wins! This 3D puzzle game for adults and kids is the perfect brainteaser, puzzle game, and brain puzzle all in one!

You're a spy inside the enemy complex, an underground building, a labyrinth of corridors. Empty, except for the security drones, always vigilant, you search every inch, look inside every desk and trash can, scan every computer.
Your goal is to gather enough intelligence to get 2 complete Unlock actions, and then escape before time runs out...

Impossible Mission is an action-selection, hand-management solitaire game, where you hold a hand of actions, and acquire new ones by searching through the many hiding spots in the building.
Managing your hand is critical to complete your mission, since every round you lose stamina and remove actions from your deck.
Every turn, the drones chase after you, and they can also cause you to lose action cards.

If you complete 2 Unlock actions, matching all 5 symbols in a single play, and then make it to the exit, you win the game.

[GAMEPLAY]
Each turn, you will:

     Play action cards from your hand in any order, as many as you can or want to.
     Discard all cards from your play area onto a discard pile.
     Move the drones towards your pawn.
     Reset the action deck if it’s empty.
     Draw 5 cards (or as many as possible) from the action deck into your hand.


Additionally, whenever you complete an Unlock card or when you're caught by a drone, you must also reset your deck.

—description from the designer

"All the fun of Cards Against Humanity with none of the penis jokes."

Mandatory Fun is a safe-for-work party game in the tradition of Cards Against Humanity and Apples to Apples. Instead of focusing on the obscene (Cards Against Humanity) or generic (Apples to Apples), it focuses on themes relevant to the players. For example, if playing with your Dungeons and Dragons group the deck may include cards referencing notable events from your campaigns.

The game is played as follows (from game booklet): At the beginning of each round, each player draws until they have 10 White Cards. A player is selected as the first Card Commander and plays a Red Card. They read the question or phrase on the card with authority and demand other players submit their most entertaining (& appropriate) White Card. Each other player submits a White Card, face down, to the Card Commander. After shuffling the other players' submitted cards, the Card Commander reads each possible Red-White card pairing to the group. The Card Commander then decides whose White Card is most entertaining (& appropriate) and that player gets one point. The next round, a different Card Commander is selected to dispense more Mandatory Fun.

Rise to Glory is a civilization-building game in which players explore a random map and compete to earn the most glory by researching technologies, building wonders, conquering cites, acquiring great artworks, and launching a starship.

Players bid for technologies, trade resources and buildings, and pay each other royalties to build units. They can wage war or join forces against other players, barbarians, or pirates.

What makes Rise to Glory different and unique?

     Epic feel as players develop technologies and deploy big armies.
     Random map that players gradually unveil and explore.
     Straightforward mechanics (minimal fuss, maximum fun!).
     Extensive player interactions (bidding, trading, fighting...).
     Balanced game system that keeps all players engaged (no elimination).
     Barbarians &  pirates to spice things up—hordes can be unleashed!
     Player differentiation with leaders, wonders, unit levels....
     Engaging combat system with surprise attacks and alliances.
     Random events that can be player-specific or affect all players.
     Extensively play-tested over many years.


—description from the designer

Tentacle Tantrum is an aquatic-themed, matching, dexterity game for 2-6 players. Each turn starts with a player rolling a set of custom dice, one with 6 colors and one with 6 sea creatures. Then all players race in an attempt be the first to stick the suction cup of their octopus arm to a tile of a colored sea creature which matches either of the rolled dice, or both for a bonus. The player who rolls the dice rotates after each turn. Play continues until all the tiles have been collected, at which point the player who collected the most is the winner.

Players place a large flexible game mat on the floor. The players will pick and race dinosaur figures around the mat by rolling a soft die. The first dinosaur to get to the middle of the board wakes and then becomes the Tyranosaurus Rex. The first Dinosaur to escape back to the start is the winner, unless they all get eaten by the T-Rex!.

Quite traditional ludo dice game, where players race their game pieces around the game board. The game’s goal is to lead the small animal pups from their winter nest to the spring meadow. Players move the pups by dice throw results, with the animal’s mother protecting them from other animals, who otherwise get scared and run back to their nests.

Thus unlike traditional ludo, the game piece that the player most recently moved, wont be returned to the start if other player end her move directly on it , as the mother animal guards its pup.

In the serene Cotswolds village of Garenton, a corpse is pulled from the murky waters of the River Severn. The victim, dubbed John Doe, met a tragic fate not only through drowning but also a brutal stabbing. As an esteemed detective, player will receive an extensive case file comprising over 40 meticulously crafted pieces of evidence, including forensic reports, crime scene photos, call records, CCTV footage, and eyewitness statements. Evidence in this case is organized in three packs, Pack 1, Pack 2 and Pack 3.

The first task in the case is to uncover the identity of John Doe by analyzing evidence from Pack 1. Once players have cracked this crucial piece of the mystery, they will delve into Pack 2, where they will identify potential suspects and unravel the complex motives behind this murder. Then in Pack 3 players will navigate through a labyrinth of secrets and lies, piecing together the evidence to solve entire mystery. With each clue, players will be able to eliminate suspects, inching closer to the killer and the final solution of this mystery.

In Khatun, players take on the role of a Custos, competing against other Custos to control the red-light district of the city of Khatun. Your deck of cards includes Courtesans, actions, spells, magic, items, alchemical tools, and constructions. Use these resources strategically to gain the upper hand and achieve victory.

Khatun is a collectible card game (or CCG) set in a medieval fantasy world. Cards are categorized as common, uncommon, rare, and mythic rare. Players collect these cards and build decks from their collection to duel against each other. As a Custos, your goal is to earn enough silver in the streets of Khatun to bribe powerful Supremos to switch to your side. Supremos provide unique advantages, and the first player to convince three Supremos (or meet another win condition) wins the game.

The game features six distinct factions—five colored and one neutral—each with a unique focus and mindset, allowing for diverse deck construction. Additionally, jewels of different colors represent the compensation needed to bring cards into play.

Your deck may include various card types: actions, alchemy, items, magic, spells, constructions, and courtesans, each requiring specific compensation to use. Some cards, known as persistent cards, remain on the board and continuously impact the game, while flash cards have a one-time effect.

Players draw cards randomly from their deck each turn and play based on their available compensation. This element of randomness introduces strategic depth, as you must decide when to play cards or save compensation to play flash cards during your opponent’s turn. The game’s robust mechanics offer rich tactics and challenging decisions each turn.

While Khatun is traditionally a two-player duel, several formats allow for more players to join the game.

—description from the designer

Kekri is a memory game for small children, where they try to find mathing colors or pictures, to form a route to the center of the gameboard, where Kille Kotilo (Sam the Snail) is having birthday party!

Players turn memorytiles one at the time, to match their previous tile. But sometimes a strong gust of autumn wind shuffles all the tiles, adding extra challenge for the players.

Kekri is the name of a traditional Finnish and Karelian harvest festival, celebrated in the fall

Always 10 good reasons to improve your efficiency at work!

Make meetings fun and unique with this educational game, ideal for all meeting and seminar leaders!

Each participant answers a question (adapted to a professional context, original and fun) according to their number (between 1 and 10). Then the team tries to put all the answers in the right order to win! It’s a great way to generate ideas, build a shared vision and communicate effectively.

Six chapters for six compact boxes to carry with you in every situation: the box itself contains the rules of the game, and along with the deck of cards contained in it, it will be enough to get all the clues necessary to the resolution!

In Mini Crimes: The Value of a Book, the Liber Ludi, a precious medieval manuscript, is stolen from the library in which it was held. Many things don’t add up, in this nocturnal theft.

Jakub Wędrowycz: Upiorne Wczasy is a standalone sequel to Jakub Wędrowycz: Dziki Samogon. It is a cooperative card game for 2 to 4 players set in the imaginative world created by the famous Polish humoristic fantasy author Andrzej Pilipiuk.

In Upiorne Wczasy, players take on the roles of Jakub, Semen, Piotruś, and Urszulka and work together to fight the monsters - Północnica, Krwawa Syrena and Król Szczurów as well as their minions. Players use various tools to perform attacks, throw exorcisms and brew liquor. Other players can help them with their actions by using additional cards outside of their turns.

Upiorne Wczasy can be played as a standalone game or as an expansion to Dziki Samogon.

—description from the publisher

In this game, you are the leader of a sect of cultists and seek to be primarily responsible for breaking the seals that keep the ancient Elders away from our reality.
You can achieve immediate victory or triumph by accumulating Power Points, after completing 21 Rituals (rounds). But be careful, Insanity could be your undoing!

Lonnie Machin (Anarky), a gifted teenager who misguidedly believes he is the Joker’s son, is a soldier of anarchy and uses the gadgets he invents to attack all forms of authority – particularly those managed by the government.

This expansion adds Anarky to the game.

Season 3 expansion.

An hilarious game of cheeky characters and ridiculous matches based on the good old days of newspaper personal ads.

Use your cards to create a profile and place your ad for all to see.  Other players use their cards to create excellent and hilarious matches.  Funniest wins!  It’s easy to learn and hugely fun to play.

—description from the publisher

Test your manual dexterity, visual attention and mental calculation to be the first to build your tower of numbers according to the challenges posed and achieve victory.


     Reveal a challenge card
     Build your tower using numbers
     Try to be the fastest one to pick up the score card to get more points


The boardgame has 4 set of number of 1 to 5 for build the tower.

Your goal is to be the first to secure each of the resource types before losing all your lives. A game is played over fast rounds which can be ended by any player at any time. On a turn you choose one card to play, this can benefit yourself, or sabotage an opponent.

Keep track of the resource cards in your deck and monitor your opponents' to tactically disrupt them so they lose track of their own. You can choose to sacrifice lives to gain an advantage, be warned high-risk strategies may bring greater rewards, but could backfire spectacularly!

The ancient dragons who once terrorized your realm have returned in search of new treasure. Rally your forces and wield elemental magic to guard against their invasion.

Defeat the three ancient dragons to protect your lands! Each turn you must call upon your elemental skills to improve your position without falling victim to the dragons’ curse. Lay out sets of cards exactly matching each dragon’s banish requirements before they steal away all your wealth.

Begin with 3 of the 5 unique dragons in play. You must banish all three to win.

Each turn, draw a new card into your hand or deploy one to a battlefield by discarding cards that match or exceed its value. Deployed cards have an immediate effect based on their element type, though you can gain even greater abilities by holding groups of the same element in your hand.

When drawing a card, be wary of the dragons’ curse: should your hand's ranks ever total 15 or more, the dragons steal cards from your hand for their hoard. If their hoard reaches 15, you lose!

Arrange one of the battlefields to include only the cards required by a dragon’s unique requirement in order to banish it. Once all three dragons are banished, you win and the realm is safe once more.


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

boardgame: Batman: Gotham City Chronicles – Batman Who Laughs
description:
The Batman Who Laughs is the villainous alternate version of Bruce Wayne from Earth-22 in the Dark Multiverse. He is a Batman who murdered the Joker only to be driven insane and adopt the Joker's sadistic anarcho-nihilistic personality for himself, obsessed with corrupting others into the worst version of themselves.

This expansion adds the Batman Who Laughs to the game.

Season 3 expansion

The wild magical energy permeating the world calls to each spellcaster differently, inspiring countless unique branches of arcane tradition. Each style's adherents seek to prove their chosen methods superior both for casting ability and for managing the unpredictable surges of magical power.

Wild Magic is an expansion to the Mysticana deck for 1-2 players. The players compete to have the strongest collection of magical energy, denoted by cards they’ve captured from the shared Aether. Each turn, the active player adds one card from their hand to the Aether. This determines which cards they may capture. Newly added cards capture anything of the same rank as well as anything which is the same element and exactly one rank lower. A 7 also captures the lowest card of the weaker element. At the end of play, each player checks for any Backfires: if a player has exactly 2 cards of the same rank, the weaker element is flipped down and provides a penalty. Next, the two players compare their total score across each of the 3 elements; the player with the higher total rank in each element wins that element, and the player who scores the most elements wins the game. Optional Discipline cards add strategy-shaping special abilities for each player.


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

Oracles have powerful visions of potential futures, yet even the most gifted among them—one such as you—still requires assistance to divine the most favorable outcome.

Oracle's Sight is a tool for guided mindfulness, using the Mysticana deck's elements and values to provide insight into personal questions and experiences.

Lay out the Oracle's Sight spread. Divine insight using the unique cards.


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

Jurassic Feud is a standalone spinoff of Catapult Feud, and the goal of both games is the same: Knock down all of your opponent's warriors and be the last one standing.

In short, choose your warriors, then use your bricks to build a prehistoric-style fortress on your player board. Place your warriors on top of your fort, then take turns using your dino-catapults to launch boulders in an attempt to destroy your opponent's fortress and knock over their warriors. Each turn consists of four phases: tactics (playing an action card), aim, fire, and clean-up. When all warriors of one side are knocked over, the battle is over. The winner must have at least one warrior miniature standing upright.

Jurassic Feud is a standalone game, but can be played with Catapult Feud and its expansions.

—description from the publisher

Three horrific harbingers herald the crises to come. As a powerful seer, use your mystic visions to prepare against the ever-worsening perils.

Harbingers builds on the existing Mysticana solo game Nine Perils with new Harbingers, special abilities, escalating challenges across three rounds, and a cooperative mode for 2 players.

Harbingers is an expansion to the Mysticana deck that can be played solo or in a 2-players co-op mode. It is based on the gameplay of Nine Perils and introduces 6 double-sided cards with Harbingers on the front and Visions on the back. Each game uses 3 Harbingers and 3 Visions: Harbingers are placed above the timeline and introduce additional restrictions to some perils, while Visions provide one-time effects that help the player. The game is played over 3 rounds, with an increasing number of Harbingers in play each round. Win all 3 rounds to win the game. In the cooperative mode, players strategize together without revealing their cards and, each turn, choose one player to play.


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

Within a realm shattered by war, shrewd nobles back opposing factions to secure their place in the future. Lend your support at the right time to wrest control of the realm from your rivals.

Players square off as influential nobles vying for leadership of a war-torn realm. Each turn, players manipulate three elemental factions, backing one in their fight against their rival. Over a series of battles, one player can solidify control of the realm and earn the victory.

Element of Surprise is a 1-2 player expansion to the Mysticana deck. In this game of strategy and manipulation, players battle to control the shifting power among the factions.

The game is played over a series of battles. For each battle, players first build up the three elemental factions by allocating cards from their hand to the three elemental factions. Next, they secretly assign two cards to the shadows and select a Herald by placing a card face-down, which represents the faction they wish to support.

Battle is then joined: each player reveals their Herald, totals the value of their chosen faction, and multiplies that value by the lowest matching element card in the shadows. The player with the highest total wins the battle, and the first player to win 3 battles wins control of the realm!


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

Realmseekers serve as the Queen’s arm, commissioning joint expeditions of newly-discovered realms. Adventurers they hire are expected to return with promising information…if they return at all.

Realmseekers 6-card expansion game for the Mysticana deck and tasks players with exploring a new realm. With each expedition, use savvy judgment to assess the growing threats. The longer the expedition takes, the more threats must be overcome. Each remaining participant may use their unique defensive skills to bolster their own chances. Exit the journey early to keep what meager knowledge you have or stick it out to the end in hopes of chronicling the full reaches of this unknown land!

--description from publisher

The veil between realms has torn, unleashing unimaginable horrors. Two champions are called to kill or banish these monsters using primal energies. Show your strength and defeat the most beasts to secure your place in history.

Take turns attacking the rampaging beast to drive it back. Each beast’s strange nature requires unique strategies. Defeat three beasts before your opponent to win the day!

In Unnatural Beasts 6-card expansion game for Mysticana, players take turns adding a card to the attack row, potentially capturing previously-played cards. If the beast's kill condition or its banish condition is met, the player defeats it. The first player to defeat three beasts wins the game.


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

War threatens the realm and only a mighty conjurer could turn the tide of battle. Consult the ancient tomes and summon four powerful titans to save the realm!

Conjurer's Tome is a solo-only expansion to the Mysticana deck. Complete summoning circles, each of which contains two tomes whose unique requirements must be simultaneously satisfied, to summon powerful titans with unique abilities. Shifting tomes create new puzzles to solve every round. Summon four titans to win!
---

The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

The queen has called a council meeting. As her two most trusted advisors, push your preferred interests to garner her favor and guide the realm’s future.

Convince the queen through shrewd manipulation and strategic gambits. Match your agenda to your secret interests while battling to keep the queen interested in your side of the conversation. The advisor who impresses the queen most at the end of the council meeting wins!

Deal out a row of 5 Mysticana cards between the two players, positioning the queen below the middle card, and deal each player a secret interest.

Each turn the active player draws a card, plays a card to their face-up agenda, and swaps a card from their hand with one in the court. The card played to the agenda is compared to the topic matching the queen’s current position, and the queen is shifted based on whether the agenda card’s element is weaker or stronger than the topic.

After both players have 5 cards in their agenda, they score it based on the unique conditions found on their interest. If either player managed to get the queen all the way to their end of the court, they also score the court using both players’ interests.

Track scores across multiple rounds until one player reaches 40 points and thus earns the queen’s favor!


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

The three Windsprites are fickle and indifferent. Entice them to bless your village through offerings of sincere devotion.

Prove your village’s devotion to the Windsprites by making offerings that appeal to them in this 6-card expansion game for Mysticana. Use the cards in your hand or field as offerings, slowly revealing face-down cards as additional offerings. Competing offerings are resolved by element priority and rank. Wind cards offer the chance to secure the current sprite’s blessing or simply call for the next one. Accumulate successful offerings to earn favor and score a total of 4 favor across multiple rounds to win the game!

Each player receives a hand of 2 cards, 1 Wind card, and 1 Avatar, as well as a field of 5 cards (2 face-up covering 3 face-down). One remaining card is revealed to show the goal: an exact number of offerings players are trying to win. The leader makes a single offering from their hand or their face-up field cards to the current sprite, then the opposing player makes a similar offering (matching the led card’s element if possible).

Compare the two offerings. The higher rank wins if both cards share an element; otherwise the element which best matches the current sprite’s preferences wins. The player with the better offering claims both cards. Wind cards are judged by the sprite’s element preferences, then advance the sprite to the next one. Reveal any face-down cards that are uncovered and repeat until 9 pairs of offerings have been made.

If either player wins offerings equal to the goal at the end of the round, they score 2 favor. Otherwise, the player closest to that goal scores 1 favor. Play through a new round. The first player to score 4 favor wins the game!


Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

In Petty Kingdoms, you play as rulers of rival villages competing to be the first to reach kingdom status!  Recruit the best and most diverse population of tradespeople to build the most powerful kingdom.  Add buildings to your village to take advantage of abilities or hinder other rival kingdoms.

Petty Kingdoms is a competitive city building game with a play time of 45-60 mins.  This game is easy to teach, packed with strategic decisions.

Gameplay allows for multiple paths to victory.  Construct buildings to attract specific citizens to your village and gain bonus actions.  Build the most of any building and gain that majority power to use against rival kingdoms.  Be the first to grow your village to a Kingdom and win the day!

—description from the publisher

Heavy Hitters brings the biggest, strongest, hardest hitting fun to Flesh and Blood gaming tables yet! Featuring six heroes across Brute, Guardian, and Warrior classes, crossover hybrid cards, and more weapons and equipment than any draftable set before, the number of options for how you bring your fight to the arena are huge!

Heavy Hitters returns to classic big-hitting melee gameplay with three of the original four classes of Flesh and Blood. Whether it's Sealed Deck, Booster Draft, or Preconstructed Blitz Decks, Heavy Hitters is the perfect set to try Flesh and Blood for the first time!

Heavy Hitters features breathtaking Marvel heroes, as well as the return of the Expansion Slot, where you can find all manner of cards to support a range of heroes and classes.

It's your first campout as a Pathfinder. Hike, swim, learn the Pathfinder Pledge, and more. In this engaging game of coordination and friendly competition, the most adventurous, efficient, and helpful Pathfinder will win.

This is a combo-tastic Roll-n-Write game based on Pathfinders, the youth leadership development program of the Seventh-day Adventist Church. Over 15 rounds, all players share a two six-sided dice result to make progress on any of five activities on the game sheet. There is limited interaction available with neighbors, tactically handshaking with them to reuse dice rolls.

The Friend game sheet is the first in a series that will be released over the next few years.

—description from the publisher

Each player randomly draws a family of monsters which will become their family for the duration of the game.

In turn, each player will be able to place a monster card on the playing area, covering it as desired: one half card, two half cards or one whole card. The goal is to succeed in expanding your family of monsters on the playing area, without the other players guessing your identity. 
Knowing that each monster placed surrounded by the 4 ribs is protected and can no longer be covered (except Black Monster card).

As the cards are placed, players will also be able to cover some of them including the famous energy drink "Monster Bull" which will earn 1 more point at the end of the game. Once each player has placed all their cards, the Black Monster appears. Scaring away other monsters, the Black Monster card can be placed on any whole card already placed. Once the Black Monsters have been placed, the cards placed underneath are removed from the game and new monsters reappear.

It's time to count the points! Each monster in your family and each drink token in your possession earns you 1 point. The player with the most points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Guess one vowel without bursting your bubble!

Shuffle the 5 vowel cards and place them into a row of 5 cards.

Starting with the left card, lift it so only the other player can see it. State a word outloud and write it down. The other player must tell you whether the vowel appears in the word or not.

Continue with the next card in line, using a new word each time. After all 5 cards have been played, you may reorder the line, then start over from the left to right.

After any turn, you may end your play by guessing the vowel on your current card. The score is 1 point for each word you've stated. If you've guessed correctly, add these points to your score. If you guess incorrectly, subtract these points from your opponent's score instead. Lowest points wins, after each player has had 3 turns with the cards.

Chthonian Wrath is a whole new small expansion for the Pantheon mythology.

This expansion contains the following new content:


     2x Dashboards
     10 cards
     1x scenario


2 miniatures:

God

     Demeter


Monsters

     Erinye

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Pantheon – Keepers of the Soul
description:
Keepers of the Soul is a whole new expansion for the Pantheon mythology.

This expansion contains the following new content:


     3x Dashboards
     5 tokens
     15 cards
     1 Scenario


3 miniatures:

God

     Thanatos
     Hypnos


Monsters

     Charon

Fruity ASAP is a speed game in which players try to reproduce as many fruit waterfalls as possible to win the game. Rebuild the fruit cascades by rotating and assembling your cards as quickly as possible! The first to obtain the requested configuration wins the card.

Expansion for Mythic Battles: Isfet

Contains:

Gods:

     Anubis
     Osiris


Heroes

Enter the seas of Midgard and the terrible entity that threatens sea travelers and even gods: the Kraken!

This expansion contains the following new content:


     1x Double Sided Board (Noatun/Vinland)
     9x Dashboards
