boardgame: You're Getting on my Nerves! A Race to the Axon Terminal
description:
You're Getting on my Nerves! A Race to the Axon Terminal was created by Case Western Reserve University Neuroscience Professor Dr. Ashley Nemes-Baran to be used in introductory and advanced neuroscience courses in order to teach cable properties in a fun and engaging way. Students enjoy the break from lecture while getting a chance to interact with their peers with this educational board game.


     Identify which Cable Properties lead to faster and slower Action Potential propagation
     Summarize how changes in Resistance within an axon or across the cell membrane will affect signal propagation and decay
     Practice vocabulary used by Neuroscientists to describe Action Potential propagation


Players compete with each other to propagate their action potential from the site of initiation (the Axon Hillock) to the end of the axon (the Axon Terminal). Players will draw a Signal Propagation Card which tells them what to do, such as "propagate 2 spaces forward". For each turn, players will reduce their Action Potential Signal by 10% which is tracked using a card throughout the game. The player's Game Piece will land on one of 6 different colored squares which will indicate a different cable property that impacts the action potential, such as "Myelin Sheath: Increased Membrane Resistance - Advance 2 Spaces without Signal Decay". The first one to reach the axon terminal wins the game!

—description from the designer

Congratulations! You are the newly-appointed chair of an academic neurology or pediatrics department. It’s time to recruit faculty to fulfill your tripartite mission of excellence in clinical medicine, education, and research. Luckily, using strategies learned at a recent contract negotiation workshop, you’ve secured a time machine as part of your startup package. Your master plan: recruit some of the greatest physicians of all time to propel your department to greatness!

But hurry, your opponent is the new chair at a competing department across town, and will be vying for the same luminaries with the help of her own department-funded time machine. (She must have attended the same workshop.)

In this hilarious two-player game, you will navigate the vagaries of academic medicine - facing scandals, staff shortages, and lazy emeritus professors. But master the right tools, and your department will triumph!

In more detail
In Endowed Chairs, players try to earn points in each of their three missions: clinical, research, and education.

Players use action cards in their hands to help them earn points or weaken their opponent's position. The most common way to earn points for one of their three missions is to recruit a great neurologist or neuroscientist from the job pool and assign her to that mission. Each of the 12 luminaries has a unique set of strength and weaknesses, so she may be worth more points in some missions than others.

Each turn consists of playing an action card or discarding unwanted action cards, and then drawing back up to five card from the draw pile. the game ends when there are no more action cards to be drawn from the draw pile. The player who has the most points in at least two of the three mission wins.

Tippy the Dragon is moving house and must carry all his possessions with him. As he hovers above the ground, take turns loading treasure onto his body. But watch out, one wrong move and you will make him tip and he will fall from the sky and you are out. Can you be the last player left?

Ramen calm, and stack on. This souper tense toppling game, requires you to go head to head with friends, stacking yummy-looking ingredients into your ramen noodle bowl. Just don’t spill the soup, or it’s game over! Ramen Stack Game Get ready to use your noodle with this spicy, speedy, soup stacking game! Pieces are cleverly shaped like ramen soup ingredients and are stacked adorably on top of a noodle filled ramen bowl. An un-pho-gettable gift for the hopeless ramen-tic. 2-4 player toppling game. Game is packaged in a familiar take-out container for added cheekiness. Our Ramen Stack Game is a fun coffee table or desktop item.

—description from the publisher

It's Mr. Cloud's birthday. It is going to be a big celebration and the animals have decided to bake a great apple cake. However, sending Ota the donkey to get the apples turned out to be a fatal mistake. The clumsy donkey spilled all the apples into the pond and the animals pounced on them in an attempt to grab as many as possible. After all, everyone wants to have the best cake!

In Apple Showdown, each player plays as their chosen animal faction and struggle for apples placed on the game map. Each herd is controlled by a different game mechanic, which gives not only children the opportunity to try modern gameplay. So get to collecting and beware of chicken poop!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Universe of Legends Trading Card Game
description:
Universe of Legends is a trading card game that puts players in the role of a D-Ranger who is assigned to protect their respective Universe from destruction.  To win the game, players must destroy the opponents 5 barriers and making a final attack directly before the opponent can do the same.

To do this, each player can summon legends or minions from their hand or deck(40 to 50 cards) to either attack barriers/rested units or defend incoming attacks by blocking.  An additional resource players have access to is the Re/Action deck, a 10 card deck that players have access to at all times.  These cards can be used to support your strategy or to interrupt your opponents gameplay.  As long as you have energy to play those cards you can play them.

How to get energy:  at the begining of the game, players pick 2 cards from their deck to be their starting energy.  At the begining of each turn, a player draws 2 cards then takes any 1 card from their hand to place into the energy zone.  The max number of energy is 10.  To spend energy to play cards, you exhaust the energy according the number cost listed on the middle left side of the card.

—description from the publisher

A Kickstarter exclusive expansion requiring only the core set.

This expansion includes alternative Terrain and Event cards which add new twists and variations to the introductory scenario "A New Beginning", ensuring a fresh experience every time you play!

     30 cards
     1 rule sheet
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In the animal kingdom, there is only one game, and that is Animal Rules Football, a heady mix of soccer and rugby that's played on a cricket pitch just for good measure.

The game contains six animal factions based on Australia fauna, and each team has their strengths and weaknesses. The kangaroos use their jumping abilities to get around the opposition's defense, while the dingoes work in packs, supporting each other. The koalas are slow to start, but once they get going, they are tough players, while the wombats present a strong defense. The cockatoos like to flock together, similar to the kookaburras, who also like to steal your energy.

Animal Rules Football is a simple card game for two players. Each player controls a team of animal players, represented by cards. Your options each turn are to place players on the pitch, gain energy, or take a shot. When placing players, energy is important as each has an energy cost. You score points by claiming field cards, and when no more field cards remain, the game end is triggered, with the highest total winning.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Dice Masters: Superman Kryptonite Crisis
description:
Dice Masters is a global hit from WizKids. It uses the same proprietary Dice Building Game platform designed by Mike Elliott and Eric M. Lang.

This set is a call-back to classic dice masters with returning mechanics such as Stun Tokens and Force Attack global abilities.  Plus, returning keywords including Range.

Each Countertop Display Contains 8 Draft Packs. Draft Packs contain cards and dice that can be added to your collection as normal but are ALSO optimized for draft! Whether you’re drafting a team of heroes that normally appear together in comics, or you’re focusing on your favorite type of energy, there will be TONS of ways to draft this set! With more than 35 heroes and villains, this set is perfect for veteran players or beginners! Brand new teams will make their Dice Masters debut! Players will get to collect Superman, his allies, and enemies in all new ways! Even if they’re already in your Dice Masters collection – these heroes will play like you’ve never seen them before! With over 150 different cards (and their dice!) to collect, this set will help you bring some Action Comics excitement to the tabletop!

Each DC Dice Masters: Superman Kryptonite Crisis Draft Pack contains the following: - 3 FULL ART Basic Action Cards! - 12 FULL ART Character or Action Cards! - Randomly inserted FOIL cards! - 24 dice (2 to match each character or action card) - 1 rules insert

The second script collection for "Tragedy Looper", from a script creation competition held by Bakafire. Two scripts are by Bakafire, the rest by competition participants in the 2016-2017 script competition. It contains 50 scripts, and 6 expansion characters, "Illusionist" and "Scientist" from Script Collection 1, "Girl" from a promo set, "Copycat", "Guru" and "Sacred Tree" as new characters.

Tragedy Looper Rei is a new expansion made to commemorate Tragedy Looper's 10th Anniversary. It contains new counters, tokens, and action cards, along with new scripts that use them. It also contains new characters "Part-Timer", "Part-Timer?", "Servant", "Godly Being", as well as new Script sets "Last Liar" and "Another Horizon".

WORDS is an exciting card game for the whole family. Players choose a phrase from one of the phrase cards and are dealt the corresponding number of letter cards. Pick a card from either the draw pile or the discard pile in order to collect the letters in your phrase. As you collect the appropriate letters, start laying them down to spell your phrase.

You can work on any word in the phrase, but here’s the catch….you must spell each word in order…and you can only lay down one card per turn. Oh by the way, you only get a few moments to memorize your phrase before the card is turned over, so don’t forget and discard a letter in the phrase….it might just be the one your opponent needs to win!

—description from the publisher

A sneaky wee pigeon is trying to bluff its way into the flock - but where is it hiding? And who put it there? For the rest of you flockers: spot the imposter and run them from the roost before things get hawkward!

Gather your flock and get ready to ruffle some feathers in this game of birds and bluffing! Unleash avian antics, outwit your bird-brained buddies, and spread your wings to victory in this mischievous and addictive social deduction game.

—description from the publisher
Overlay tile for the Calendar offering new rewards players gain after the full revolution of their wheels.

Promotional Wheel Enhancement tiles:
4 Uncommon Wheel Enhancements
3 Unique Wheel Enhancements

boardgame: Magnificent Flying Machines: London to Manchester Air Race
description:
Promotional race card inspired by the 1910 London to Manchester air race; can be used just like race cards from the expansion (Magnificent Flying Machines: The Great Air Races Expansion).

To compete in the race, use the terrain, weather and cargo tiles depicted.

boardgame: Tatakai: A Game of Covert Warfare
description:
In Tatakai, you play as a warlord caught in an assassination plot. Players take it in turns to deploy mercenaries, dispatch enemy agents and attempt to infiltrate their rival's camp. Can you eliminate your rival before your rival assassinates you?

A fast-paced, two-player game of luck, strategy and covert warfare.

—description from the designer

Fifty two wargame scenarios; most can be played in any time period with any forces. Each scenario describes a situation where the Red Army faces the Blue Army. There is also a map that lays out the battlefield.

Kukulkan is an fast and simple card game, in which you will have to guess and complete words with the cards in your hand.
Your objective will be forming words to build columns and to reach first the maximum score.
If you manage to get all 4 Parts of Kukulkan you will unleash the power of Kukulkan, god of storms! but use it carefully or it will turn against you...

—description from the designer

From the game box: "Lots of fun for every one"

Lone Star is a game published in the 1940s by the Starcraft / Star Mfg. Company in Lincoln, Nebraska, U.S.A. It's a peg game on a pentagram with 31 holes. It was originally patented and published in 1892 by Charles H. Rickert.
The solitaire version is exactly what one would expect: Place a peg in every hole on the board except the center hole. Each turn is then a jump-and-capture move following the lines on the board until no more jumps can be made. The ultimate goal in this game is to leave only one peg on the board and it’s in the center hole.
The two-player game is set up the same way, and moves are always single jump-and-capture moves, but the objective is to be the last player able to jump a peg.
The final variation for Lone Star is a capturing competition. Again, the set up is the same and every move the two players make must be a jump-and-capture move. The major change is that chain jumps are allowed. The winner is the player who captures the most pegs when no more jumps can be made.

Children learn step by step how to orient themselves in traffic and behave correctly. What do I have to pay attention to on the sidewalk, at the traffic lights or at the zebra crossing? The situations in traffic are varied and require precise perception and correct recognition.

In this dice game, the children start in the middle of the board and each have to reach three target markers, which they initially placed on valid targets as far away as possible on the game board. They take turns rolling the dice and moving forward the corresponding number of steps until they reach a traffic light (where any extra steps are lost).

At a traffic light, you take a traffic card and look at both pictures on it. Only one of them is correct and the players each have to choose one of them. You check the answer with the solution wheel. If the active player guessed correctly, he can roll the dice again and move the corresponding distance (but not cross another traffic light). All other players who voted correctly only move one step.

boardgame: Undermined! Pairadice City: Promotional Event Cards
description: Four promotional event cards: Axe crazy, In-descent proposal, Lucky dip, Nearly all mine.

Apophenia is a Co-op Survival Horror Puzzle & Deduction card game for 1-4 players.

The players assume the roles of Hunters who are tasked to explore an area linked to supernatural phenomena. In that area lurks a sinister being of unknown identity. It is the Hunters' job to unveil the wicked and vanquish them once and for all.

The game features 8 different Horror enemies such as the Werewolf and the Ghoul and an ever-changing board to explore.
Wield a variety of tools, from salt and garlic to guns and silver daggers to face the Horrors or die trying.

Hunters win together or fall together but be wary of who you trust! Through the course of the game, some Horrors may corrupt a hunter and turn them into a horror side player. These players become traitors who secretly sabotage and defeat the Hunters on behalf of the Horror. As the game goes on, they are may be unveiled as Demon-possessed or as Werewolves themselves!

Do you have what it takes to tread through the darkness?

—description from the publisher

Today Bobo is at the zoo with his mom and dad. Bobo loves being at the zoo and doesn't know which animal he should visit first. Help him visit and photograph all the animals and interesting places in the zoo before he falls asleep.

In this memory game, all the zoo locations are first revealed, memorized as much as possible, and then covered up again after a short time. The Bobo puzzle is set up. On his turn, a player rolls the color die and then tries to find one of the missing zoo images of the corresponding color. He moves Bobo to an empty field of the color and reveals a piece. If the image matches, it is placed on the zoo field. Otherwise, a piece of the Bobo puzzle is turned over. The cooperative game ends successfully when all the zoo images have been found before the puzzle is turned over (8 attempts).

There is also a competitive variant: in this, the player who was able to match more pieces wins.

boardgame: Port Royal: An Adventure Skirmish Miniatures Game set in the Searover's Caribbean
description:
Port Royal is a campaign based, tactical, small-scale warband-sized skirmish wargame that is easy and fast to learn and play, loosely based on the historical events following the 1692 earthquake that devastated the island of Jamaica. 
In Port Royal, players take control of small companies of pirates, privateers, Native Americans, African Maroons, or local government forces as they battle against opponents for their share of the plunder that litter the ruins of the place. Players will gain loot, experience, and infamy between games while growing their piratical expertise. But take heed! The ruins are fraught with dangers: falling buildings, wild animals, desperate civilians, and opposing companies can bring a quick end to members of your company!

—description from the publisher

Retro toy robots compete in a strategic card and dice game.

With your group of friends, you have discovered in your attic a game box that particularly intrigues you. It seems to have been stored there for many years. Gathered around the box covered in accumulated dust, you open it and discover with wonder that it is the famous Battle Botz whose production has been abandoned for a long time. You have always dreamed of taking part in these mythical clashes, so everyone quickly seizes the famous combat automatons.


     Play free-for-all or in team battles.
     Pick an individual and permanent Special Power.
     In turn order, draw into your hand the best Defense, Attack, Permanent and Instant robot cards that you can get, from a common pool of cards.
     Play cards by programming Attack, Defense and Permanent robots into your play area.
     During the Conflict phase, roll your combat die to see who will be the Attacker and who will be the Defender in each combat phase, and activate your robot cards in play, your Instant cards from your hand, and even your individual Special Power for devastating combos.


Two game strategies will allow you to be crowned winner of a game of Battle Botz. Through Domination, you will need to collect two Domination tokens. By Elimination, you will have to be the last player with remaining HP during a conflict phase.

Prepare your Battle Botz and may the best robot win!

—description from the publisher

The Math Galaxy brings excitement and fun to math training: Turn the rocket, calculate, check the solution and first solve your favorite cats in space puzzle! The Math Galaxy combines fun and math and can be played cooperatively and competitively.

Three of the 5 included puzzles, each made up of 6 pieces, are chosen and mixed. The active player chooses on the wheel and solves the task. If he has solved it correctly, he takes a hidden puzzle piece and tries to complete a puzzle. If he succeeds by round 15, the game is won.

The math tasks practice basic arithmetic up to the number 20.

boardgame: Go For The Krill: Darker Waters
description:
In the darkest reaches of the sea lie the ocean’s fiercest and most deadly creatures. As the commotion overhead signals it’s time to feed, they come up from their slumber to join the fray. Likewise, beasts of the air dive deeper than ever so they, too, can fill their bellies with krill.

Go For The Krill: Darker Waters expands the base game's options by adding more interactive creatures to players’ hands, Mission Cards that raise the stakes by adding new avenues to gain points, and even a way to break ties when cards cancel. Additionally, components to support a seventh player are provided, making games of Go For The Krill even more exciting.

—description from the publisher

Halflings: Best Chef is a competitive dice placement game where players strive to be elected the Best Innkeeper of the 7 Shires. Decorate your inn, collect fine beverages, and cook with top-quality ingredients to earn the highest standing.

Players start by rolling dice, and placing them on action spaces to gather ingredients, collect spices, sell goods, buy decorations, and cook dishes. Each action helps you build your inn’s reputation.

The game ends when a player completes a menu with a starter, main course, and dessert. Points are tallied based on dishes cooked, unused ingredients, decorations, and other resources. The player with the highest standing points wins.

Strategically manage resources, place dice, and use modifiers to outwit opponents and become the most renowned innkeeper in the 7 Shires!

De Havilland Mosquito: Wooden Wonder is a solitaire narrative air combat game. Follow the progression of your Mosquito fighter-bomber and its crew through a series of campaigns or an entire career spanning 1941 to 1945.  Fly missions over Berlin, coastal raids in France or ground attack missions in the skies above cliffs of Norway and Holland.

You are the captain chosen to lead the Tantarian project. The space ship is a colossal spacecraft that contains the essence of life on earth to begin a new chapter for humanity in a new world.

The board is made of two printed sheets, cut some holes in one of the sheets and assemble it with tape and a clip, so lower sheet will rotate and the map will change. There are 8 possibilities of the maze to solve for each map.



Objective: Some missions consist of eliminating creatures. Other missions include eliminating creatures and completing activities that have a turn counter. You must complete these missions before completely depleting the turn counter. Every mission is a short campaign. The more missions you coplete more points you get to win the game. The game ends when you defeat all the creatures or your character has 0 healt points.

You start with a basic weapon and armor but you will be able to find better weapons inside the container box. Taking items from the containers box you can improve your character's weapons and skills or restoring your health points.

Print and play this game at home. You receive the space ship maps board in PDF with instructions to assemble it, creatures and character in a set of cards, missions and items. Rules for solo mode and two players.

What else you need to play this game?

10 d6 dice
d20 dice set.
3 d6 blank dice black color.
I recommend minifigures with base stand 2cm maximum size.
Optional: corner rounder trimmer punch for cards, maximum size 4mm radius.
Optional: card sleeves 61mm x 93mm

https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/gomeznaq/rolling-maze-rpg-sci-fi-space-adventure

—description from the designer

A fast paced, fun, and easy to play card game featuring mind-bending and immersive border to border art. Players enter a magical world where they must search for, swap, and steal mushrooms to achieve the ultimate goal: first person to collect five mushrooms wins.

—description from the designer

Kings of Dust is a tactical card game that has a dynamic playing field that is similar to games like Magic The Gathering or Hearth Stone. Level up your Hero with class cards. Class cards change the way your Hero is played and can turn the tide of battle. Equip stronger equipment and summon powerful minions to help you reign destruction down on your opponent.

To win the game, you must bring the enemy Hero to zero Health Points. If a Hero is defeated, all of the Hero's minions will be destroyed as well. So play wisely!

GETTING STARTED
Determine First Player - Flip a 5/10 Damage token included in the game, if it lands on 10 the player that Flipped goes first, if it lands on 5 the player that Flipped goes
second.

Select Your Deck - Starting in turn order, each player selects a Hero Deck. There ore 4 Hero decks in the King of Dust base game. Find the Hero card in the deck and place it facing the enemy Hero card(See rules for battlefield placement). Shuffle, the remaining cards and place the shuffled deck face down.

Draw up - The first player starts the game with 5 cards, the second player starts with 6 cards. (In 3 or 4 player games, both the third and fourth player starts with 7 cards.)

Start Your Turn - At the start of your turn, Draw a card from your deck and gain 3 action points. (Look at the back of a Hero card to see free actions a player can take on their turn.)

Play Your Cards - Based on the card costs and action points you have available, choose which cards to play and resolve any damage or effects.

End Your Turn - After you have spent your action points, if you can not make any other actions or generate more action points your turn is over. Let the other player know your turn is over. The next player will now start their turn.

Players can not have more than 7 cards in their hand when they end their turn. If you have more than 7 You must discard the extra cards. You don't get anything when discarding this way.

—description from the designer

The year, 1923. The life, a university student, one of daily tedium and nighttime revelry, hours sweating over books at the behest of hectoring professors or carousing with friends through the big city or about the countryside in rattletrap cars. It can be exhilarating, meeting decadent connoisseurs of the arts or practiced masters of esoterica. And there are always the dreams, those nervous fabrications flitting through the restive hallways of one’s sleeping mind.

Yet is this all there is? Is there more to life and existence? Some deeper understanding and adventure and … perhaps even grandeur that one lonely soul may obtain? Despite the nagging feeling that doom hovers over you, you nevertheless begin a quest that may take you who-knows-where to sate your subtle mania…

Cult Poker is a solo card game where you employ a standard deck of cards to select poker hands to unlock and explore an occult universe, looking for the strange secrets at the fringes of reality.

The game is make-as-you-play, meaning you will start with an unadorned deck of 54 playing cards, these rules, a few sheets to track your character, tables locking card names to discover, and a permanent marker. You will modify the playing cards throughout your exploration of the world of Cult Poker. By the end of a campaign of games you will know your fate and possess a deck that reflects your character’s discoveries and memories. It can even serve as the jumping off point for other campaigns with other characters!

—description from the designer

Unruly kittens got into the boxes. Look, guess or remember which puss is under which lid.

A light memory game not only for cat lovers. The players has to remember the color and fur pattern of their pets (e.g. patches, stripes) and their toys. The players place cards on the table with images of boxes in which kittens are hiding. Each card is a different combination of three elements. The roll of the dice will indicate which card and attribute a player has to name. If you remember (or guess), you win.

—description from the publisher

Openly trading information, they move fast or miss out.

A basic difficulty variant setup and gameplay card for Rumors of Gold.

Building a business, they are steadily buying up the town.

An expert difficulty variant setup and gameplay card for Rumors of Gold.

A streamlined, face pace video game like combat simulation 1v1 Tournament style system with quick attacks, instant defense, and "boost-able" combinations to deliver the final blow! Each player chooses one of FOUR available characters in the box. Set up is less than 1 minute.  Players draw up to two cards in their hand of the unique 17 cards in their decks, flip the next card over, move their Mana meter up two if available, roll the BOOST die to determine if you have special options on your activation. Both reveal initiative.  Lower initiative goes first, moves their standee towards or away from their opponent to set up minimum distances for the card they wish to perform, and plays a card in one of THREE levels from their hand. After performing a card from one of those levels and striking with PERCENTAGE dice, the defender may activate their defense card.  Final Damage is determined. If one player drops their opponent to ZERO health. You win.

Bloodstones comes roaring back with Dwarves & Djinn, which features two new factions, a new solo mode, and more maps.

The mysterious Sand Dwellers, led by the powerful Djinn, storm onto the scene. The Djinn are spirits of the sand who "teleport" their troops effortlessly around the map, giving unprecedented mobility to their armies. Born in the deep desert, the Sand Dwellers are here to claim all of Fal for themselves.

The Mountain Kingdom emerges from their mines, with the ferocious Dwarves eager to show their prowess. Terrifying Golems aid them in their claims to be the rightful rulers of the land of Fal.

This expansion introduces a new solo mode by Dávid Turczi featuring automa for the different Bloodstones factions. Fend off mighty Dragons and capture Hill Folk castles as your faction competes to dominate the land of Fal. Quests are designed to increase variety and offer interesting challenges in your pursuit of victory.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: KeyForge Adventures: Fall of the House Gormangeist Nightmare Expansion
description:
The Gormangeist mansion evolves into a nightmare with this expansion!

Fall of the House Gormangeist Nightmare Expansion adds an unspeakable challenge to the KeyForge: Adventures - Fall of the House Gormangeist that only the bravest archons should attempt!

New enemies to overcome, more rooms to explore!

Contains:
8 Nightmare Mansion Cards
12 Nightmare Adventure Cards

Requires KeyForge: Adventures - Fall of the House Gormangeist

boardgame: Roll Camera!: Spy Thriller Story Pack
description:
TOP SECRET BULLETIN: You've done it. You've written the best twist ending in the history of cinema! It's so good that you are sure rival studios have planted spies among your crew, hoping to snoop on your production meetings and leak the plot. You'll need to track meetings on the Conspiracy Board and use new super-cool Spy Gadgets to help you complete your film. Everyone is out to get you! Trust no one! ROLL CAMERA!

Roll Camera! Story Packs give a little thematic cinematic boost to your madcap movie-making, with all-new components and a small rules twist. The Spy Thriller Story Pack adds the new Conspiracy Board to monitor how much information is leaking to your crew, and new Secret Mission cards to give you a hidden objective with meta-game benefits.

This pack includes 10 spy-themed Scene cards, 3 Idea cards, 3 Problem cards, 4 Script halves, 1 Equipment card, 1 Manilla Envelope card, 1 Conspiracy board, 5 Crew tokens and 5 Gadget tokens. The Spy Thriller Story Pack can be played as an isolated module, replacing certain main game components for a highly thematic one-off game, or simply mixed in with the rest of the game and any previous expansions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lupus in Tabula: Edizione Luna Piena – Kit magico
description:
Includes 8 new cards for the "Luna Piena" edition of the game: 
3 special Werewolves 
1 Magic Sword/Magic Shield
1 Cloak of Invisibility
2 blank cards
1 rules card

boardgame: Arguing when money talks: Der Söldner
description: Der Söldner is a ruleset for miniature wargames set in the mid-14th to mid-17th century. It was developed from the Matrix games developed by Chris Engle in the 1980s. Like Matrix games, it uses arguments and a list of keywords, but is focussed more on the developing narrative than using arguments for everything. The mechanism asks a short question: what is going to happen? One player (the narrator) starts the conversation by proposing an event. Others dispute this or choose not to. If not disputed, the event happens as proposed, otherwise arguments are exchanged, judged, and resolved with a pass-or-fail dice roll. This setup is embedded in a simple mechanism for determining the next narrator and how much time is available for events.

A strong headwind from the Pole shakes your cargo and the few passengers you have aboard. Welcome to Antarctica. The all-white landscape suggests a landing strip on the ice floe! In this month of November, the ice is still thick enough to support a landing. This is the first time you’ve seen the famous “Ice Runway” with your own eyes. Landing an airliner in this remote location seems unreal.

An additional airport for Sky Team: Ice Runway (Antarctica).

Contains one postcard with two additional missions (Red and Black).

Note: Available in Sky Team Turbulence and as a Print and Play

"Rumors of a spreading corruption whisper through the streets of the once-serene Everion. Will you be the adventurers who break the cycle of chaos and become renowned heroes?"

Restless is a highly replayable fantasy adventure game where you and up to three other players lose yourselves in a vast open world brimming with dangers, treasures, and excitement. While it focuses on cooperative gameplay, it also includes a competitive variant.

Game Arc
Each playthrough is split into three chapters of roughly 45 minutes per player. Depending on player count and playstyle, it can be played as a one-shot or saved between chapters using the quick-save system.

Play style
Explore the boundless world of Everion and experience gripping adventures. Collect and trade resources, buy and upgrade powerful items, specialize in professions, and befriend animal companions along the way. As each chapter draws to a close, you will team up with the other players to fight a powerful boss.

Storybook
Restless is a storybook game with a focus on short, flavorful stories. Each player experiences their own unscripted adventure. Every session and every playthrough offers new choices, surprises, and challenges that will keep you on the edge of your seat.

—description from the publisher

Players can activate their skills with dice, attack adventurers, and repel adventurers by doing enough damage.
The repulsed adventurers can be turned into crystals or scouted to serve as allies.
Dungeon is filled with magical energy called Mana, but Mana is lost every time an adventurer fails to repel her.
When all the manas are gone, the magic of sealing the deepest princess's door is released.
If the adventurer cannot be repelled and the princess at the back of the dungeon is recaptured, the player will lose.
Can you fight off all adventurers and prevent Princess from recapturing them while recovering various events, items, and dungeon mana?
publishers: JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ)

description:
Phone Wallet Key is a set-collecting casual game where players take on the roles of a family preparing to leave the house. Players have to gather all the necessary items before heading out and remind everyone of the time. Players continuously draw item cards from the deck, being mindful not to collect identical items. At the end of the game, score all the item cards, and the player with the highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

As you run a bar in a certain city, you buy and serve wonderful liquor to suit your taste. With so many rival stores lined up, can I get the title Liquor meister, which is proof of the city's number one bar owner?

boardgame: Star Tycoon: Promo Card Booster Pack
description:
Promo Card Booster Pack available during the Kickstarter campain of the game.

Super-charge Star Tycoon with brand-new content, including the mysterious Abaddon.

boardgame: Arguing when Cultures Clash: The Warrior
description:
Arguing when Cultures Clash: The Warrior is a supplement to Arguing when money talks: Der Söldner. The Warrior spans the period from the earliest battle in recorded history to the use of firearms in substantial numbers in the 15th century.

Arguing When… wargames are miniature tabletop wargames based on the MATRIX wargaming concept developed in the late 1980s. They are played with the players' preferences, insights, and interpretation of the historical record in mind. Not those of the designer and author of the rules. It is a fast-flowing, narrative-generating game that provides detail when required. It does not use an I-Go-You-Go mechanic, but players create the narrative of battles - historic, fictional, or otherwise - as a joint effort by all. It is We-Go.

In Eraser you have to figure out words and concepts with disappearing clues! An easy to learn, very fun party game. Guess the correct concept with the clues that reach you and erase one to make it more difficult to the other players.

In a round of Eraser, the starting player takes one of the category cards and chooses 1 from the 5 available (film and TV, animals, objects, places and historical figures or food). Then they have to write on their whiteboard as many clues are people are playing. The whiteboard gets passed around the table to the other players who will try to figure out the answer. But there's a twist! Each player, once has an answer will erase one clue from the whiteboard before passing it to the next player.

When the whiteboard reaches the original player it's time to reveal the correct concept and give away the points. The person writing the clues gets a point for each player that guessed the concept correctly; the rest of the players only get a point if they figured out the right concept.'

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Das Nilpferd in der Achterbahn Kids
description: This is the kids variant of Roller Coaster Hippo and is a party game where players have to answer questions, mime, or guess whether strange facts are true. The hippopotamus who reaches the finishing line first, wins. Along the way, players are given funny tasks and participate in crazy events.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
V for Victory immerses you in the fiercest battles of World War II. Take command of your squad and engage in perilous missions against a relentless enemy.

Tactical prowess and bravery: the keys to victory
V for Victory is a tabletop miniature wargame for 2 to 4 players, allowing you to recreate the intense battles of World War II on your tabletop. Leading your squad, you'll embark on perilous missions pitting you against a determined foe.

Beyond sheer force, your tactical acumen and ability to inspire your soldiers make the difference. Discover innovative mechanics for morale management and command that will test your strategic skills.

A complete and immersive gaming experience
Within these pages, you'll find all the necessary rules to lead your troops, engage them in combat, and maintain their composure in the face of adversity. Step-by-step explanations will guide you through each phase.

More than just the power of your weapons, it's your tactical sense and ability to rally your soldiers that will make the difference during the game. With its original mechanics for morale management and command, V for Victory offers a straightforward learning system that captures the challenge of maintaining squad cohesion and commanding men under enemy fire.

Crossbows & Catapults is a restoration of Crossbows and Catapults, the 1983 classic game of kinetic warfare. Each player builds their castle, then players take turns using their weapons to fire discs at their opponent's castle, trying to knock over their warrior figures.

This new version features highly engineered weapons and building bricks. The new weapons no longer use rubber bands, replaced by spring-powered and pinch-to-fire technology that ensure reliable velocity and reward player skill.

On the building side, castles are constructed using a variety of detailed components to allow for larger, more intricate, and more varied structures.

The Weapons Cache offers two new weapons to add to your game play. The Ballista fires deadly low shots - great for targeting units and foundation blocks. The Trebuchet launches two pieces of ammo at once for maximum destruction spread.

boardgame: Black Stories: Laugh of the Grim Reaper
description:
Here, no one kicks the bucket in a boring way! Whether with a selfie stick in hand or an air guitar under the arm... The spectacular method some idiots use to send themselves to the afterlife is often – despite all the tragedy – simply hilarious and: at least one less person who could drag humanity to its doom with their stupidity. Who can solve the 50 cases of macabre mishaps?

They are dark, mysterious, and downright evil: black stories – This is THE cult series at moses. In the morbid, creepy, terrifying, and sometimes entertaining puzzles, it’s all about death and accidents that couldn’t be more gruesome. By guessing, these exact stories are meant to be uncovered. Not always that easy…

Power Grid: Bremen/Manhattan is a pair of expansion maps for Power Grid!


     The fight of the electric power companies in Bremen for the most customers is particularly tough and fast. Nuclear power plants are not used to supply electricity in Bremen so you are fighting more for scarce resources. Unlike the other game boards Bremen does not have classic connecting costs between cities, but instead flat district costs. Additionally, several districts are so small, that they are limited to two spaces for networks and afterward do not allow further competition.
     Manhattan has a giant demand for electric power, but does not have much space for the necessary networks. Thus, the electric power companies fight nonstop for the cheapest building costs all over Manhattan. This map only offers a single huge area and the game only runs in Step 1 until the end of the game.


—description from the publisher

Bitesize is a domino climbing-shedding game for 2 players.

Bitesize uses a single set of double 6 dominoes, with only 1-2 domino combinations.

In each round, there are only 5 types of combinations that can be played. Once that type has been played, no one can play that type again until the round ends.

Win by shedding out and avoiding the vise of the combination types!

—description from the designer

A party game para Latinos. Tragos card games embrace US Latino traditions and customs while poking fun at our stereotypes. Pick up a card and follow the prompt. You may be answering trivia, guessing Spanish words, telling crazy stories, or performing challenges. Packed with references from all Latino cultures, Tragos is made by Latinos for Latinos.

—description from the publisher

The Adult Party Game of Solving Picture Puzzles!

3 different sets of cards, Easy, Medium, and Hard.  Each card has an image on it.  The image is actually a puzzle that uses a combination of letters and illustrations to create words or phrases.  Some are common terms, some are dirty words.  Good luck figuring out which is which.

Each round is 60 seconds or until 3 puzzles have been decoded, whichever comes first.  One player starts out as Judge, all other players are guessers.  First player to solve gets the card.  After the round, the next player is the judge.  First to collect 13 cards is the winner.

There are also group rules and sudden death rules.

10 promo cards first available during the Battlefruits Gamefound campaign.

1. A Hug of Ice and Fire (Tag Team)
2. Beetroot Predator
3. Cutecat (I)
4. Duel of the Fruits (Tag Team)
5. Five-Spice Monster
6. Mangolorean
7. Saltemental
8. Shakes Pear
9. Venomash
E. Zencats (II) / Evilcat (III)

Ten sacrifices will satisfy DekaZ’ehn. Awaken the god and you shall be rewarded with power beyond the human imagination…

You’ve been preparing for the arrival of DekaZ’ehn. Ten sacrifices? Easy. However, there is a catch - All ten sacrifices must be made the same night.

The stack of RSVPs are on your desk. The party will begin tonight as will the sacrifices. Little did they know, they RSVP’d to die.

In this game you play as cultists who need to sacrifice 10 guests to your elder god before the party gets out of hand and the authorities are called in.

You do this by playing cards to different actions performing those actions.

You must navigate this party and isolate the guests before there is too many guests and you must shut down the party for the night.

—description from the designer

2023 Promo Card 3

Originally available as part of Secret Cabal 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

POWER: 3
KEYWORDS: POISONOUS
DESCRIPTION: The opponent cannot put cards into their hand.

Additional 2023 promo cards are in Mindbug: Beyond – Promo Pack

Players interact with others by choosing & revealing family cards similar to traditional 'WAR'. Based on card characteristic similarities as well as dice rolls, cards receive an amount of Covid markers. Players strive to interact as much as possible and have the largest 'family' while treating for Covid and trying to reduce the spread! Special event cards allow players to quarantine, vaccinate, or host a super-spreader!

—description from the designer

The Unstable Unicorns are back, and they're not horsing around. The Diamond Edition introduces four Diamond Unicorns with powerful effects that level up the game. With all new foil-detailed box artwork, a sparkle-core D4 dice, and four holographic collector's cards, this deluxe version of Unstable Unicorns will have you building a Unicorn Army and betraying your friends like never before!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This Campaign Reset Kit provides everything you'll need to play through the Sagrada Artisans legacy campaign a second time.

This is not a stand-alone product and is meant as a supplement for Sagrada Artisans.

Drive your taxi as fast as you can! Each additional roll of the dice makes your taxi move exponentially faster. The first player to complete a lap collects the accumulated fare, but beware of other taxis ramming into you from behind or rolling a number that’s too high, causing you to fall into a sinkhole in the city!

The player pieces are mounted piggyback on neutral "carriers". On your turn, you roll up to three dice (1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3), aiming for 4 or less --one die at a time. If you get 4 or less on two dice, you move twice the amount, if you get it on three, you move three times the amount. If you total more than 4, you're busted and your piece goes off board, plus a chip is added to the pot (initially seeded with 1 chip).

As pieces move, they push those in front of them on the track. There are two exit tracks where you get to push pieces over the side. Whenever this happens, you also add a chip to the pot. You can get back on track only if there is a carrier piece available.

As you pass the pot, you grab the chips there (and seed it again with a chip). First player to 10 chips wins.

The ultimate quiz for all Tribute of Panem fans!

Become part of the Panem fan universe and find out in the training center whether you are more of a mentor, sponsor, careero or spectator! Test your knowledge with 300 exciting questions and prove that you are a true Panem fan. Work your way through the four chapters of the moving story about Katniss, Peeta and President Snow alone or together with others. The practical game templates for the joint quiz are easy to download via QR code. You can also expect lots of fun facts and great insider knowledge! Are you ready for your exam?

Game procedure: The game master either asks each tribute a question in turn or reads a question out loud and whoever knows the correct answer comes forward and is called out by the game master. If the answer is correct, the tribute scores a point. If the answer is wrong, the person who answers second fastest gets a chance, and so on.
Whoever collects the most points wins the quiz!

If you get the answer right, the team advances by the number of squares corresponding to its self-assessed score. For example, if you answer question 6 correctly, you'll move up 6 squares.

It's fun, accessible to all sports fans, and there's plenty to do: 280 cards and 2024 questions, with the presence of original cards to spice up the game.

Akropolis Athena is an expansion of the Akropolis base game, set in the Ancient Mediterranean where architects compete to build prestigious cities. The theme revolves around gaining the favor of Athena, the goddess of wisdom, war, and artisans, to earn riches and honor.

In this game, players must use their skills to complete specific Construction cards by building layouts within their cities that match the cards. As players complete these cards, they can acquire parts of the Statue of Athena and gain Mini-tiles to enhance their cities. The goal is to strategically build your city while completing these Construction cards to gain additional points and advantages.

The game integrates the core rules of the Akropolis base game with the added challenge of completing Construction cards. Each architect can complete each Construction card only once and must manage their resources and placements carefully to maximize their points.

The game concludes when there is only one tile left in the Construction Site and all the stacks have been used. Points are counted as usual, but players who have completed the Statue of Athena gain extra points for their Stones, adding a layer of strategic depth to the gameplay. The ultimate goal is to have the most prestigious city by the end of the game, as determined by the accumulated points.

Pet Parade is an enchanting card game where players create their own delightful animal processions. Kids line up adorable creatures and compete to create the most captivating parade!

Game Components:

     54 cute animal cards with numbers and letters
     1 D10 die


Setup:

     Shuffle the animal cards and place down in a draw pile.
     Deal out five cards to each player, face up.


Game Play:

     Any player rolls the D10 die. 
     All players drop sets of cards in front of them as their “pet parade”, based on the following chart.
     All players replenish their hand to maintain five cards.



Die Roll  Action
0,1,2     Drop two or more cards to make a 3- to 5-letter word.
3         Drop two or more cards whose (top left) numbers add up to 3
4         Drop two or more cards whose numbers add up to 4
5-9       Drop two or more cards whose numbers add up to 5-9

Always maintain five cards. Towards the end of the game, deal out an equal number of cards to each player.
Continue to roll the die and form parades until no player can drop any more cards.

ENDING THE GAME 
When no more cards can be played, the game is over - time to score and determine the winner.

SCORING 
Each card has two numbers: top left and top right with its letter. 
1. Add up the total of all the numbers shown on your cards.
2. Deduct the total points of any unplayed cards in your hand. 
The winner will have the highest score. In case of a tie, the youngest player wins.

STRATEGY
Try to drop the cards with the highest letter values early. Keeping these in your hand at the end of the game will count against your score.

A "push your luck" dice game with players competing over four rounds to score the most points.

The dice are standard 6 sided dice except for the number one on each dice.

One die is the "Skull Die" where the skull equals zero (0).

The other die is the "Dragon Die" where the dragon equals seven (7).

Player begin their turn by rolling a die of their choice. The result can be accepted or the second die can be rolled which will multiply them together. The players then are to decide if they go "over" or "under" the target (the number they just rolled). If the player rolls and the dice match their decision they will receive a point. The new number the player rolled becomes their target and they must stick to their decision of "over" or "under." Once they choose to stop they score points based on how many rolls matched their target.

Example of play from the rules:

You roll the "skull die" on its own and get a 2. You stop and have your target set to "2." You state "over" meaning your next roll must be higher. You then roll the dragon dice and get a "4." Four is over 2 so a point is scored. You already chose "over" and 4 is still a low number so you choose to continue. The dragon die is rolled which is a "3." You then roll the skull die and get another "3." You then multiply the two dice making the number "9." Two points have now been scored. With two dice you should be able to get over "9" so you roll the dragon dice and roll a dragon (7) you then roll the skull dice and get a "6," six times 7 equals 42 so you have again gotten "over" your target (which was 9) and score a third point. You can choose to stop or continue. If you continue and do not go over your target (now 42) you lose all points gained that turn. If the player chooses to stop they take their 3 points and play passes on.

In Disney Coup, influence and bluffing are key to victory. Represented by face-down cards in your play area, your influence determines your power. On your turn, you can use the action of one of your characters or bluff by using the action of a character you don't actually have. Choose wisely, as those caught in a lie must lose one of their influences. Players with no influence left are banished from the kingdom and the game. Only the last one standing will be the winner!

The trumpets sound, and the heralds proclaim: The Kingdom of Imagination is in search of a new Advisor! Each version of Disney Coup features five exclusive characters from the magical universe of Walt Disney. Tesouros Traiçoeiros includes Pocahontas, Pinocchio, Evil Queen, Donald and Stitch. Mr. Octopies wants to learn! Can you teach him some words so he can better learn our human ways? Roll dice to generate sets of letters and use them to build words in this solo postcard game. The longer your words, the higher your score -- and the more Mr. Octopies will learn!

Hungry bears need berries and oats. Fill them up with a healthy breakfast to start their morning right! Translated summary of the rulebook:

The throwing round
The task is to pocket as many of the three thrown balls as possible from a distance of two meters from the ball catcher. The player may only throw each ball once.
The player with the ball catcher can lend a helping hand by swinging his hips artistically to guide the approaching ball in the right direction.

The party round
In the party round, you can convert your luck with the dice into reward points.
If you successfully sink a ball, you have earned a task from the party cards of the same color. They take the fan, select a card, choose any one of the four tasks on the card (without reading it first) and perform it.

The reward
Sometimes tasks have to be solved (played) alone, sometimes in pairs and sometimes by the whole game round.
Whoever solves a task receives a point. They often need a fellow player to do this, who then also receives a point. And sometimes the whole group is rewarded if they manage to solve the task together.

Form teams
If you want, you can also play the game in teams of two.

Panache! ("Plume") is a kid's version of L'Osti d'jeu which itself is the Quebecer's version of Cards against humanity.

Play begins with a judge, choosing a black question or fill-in-the-blank card from the top of the deck and showing it to all players. Each player holds a hand of answer cards and a sticker sheet. At the beginning of each round, they passes a card to the judge on which they have added a sticker (if their was none), face-down, representing their answer to the question on the card. The judges determines which answer card(s) are funniest in the context of the question or fill-in-the-blank card. The player who submitted the chosen card(s) is given the question card to represent an "Awesome Point", and then the player to the left of the judge becomes the new judge for the next round. Play continues until the players agree to stop, at which point the player with the most points is the winner.

description:
Theme:
KEI-GO! is a conversational party game centered around learning and using honorific language appropriately. Players navigate social hierarchies and deduce roles based on interactions, making it both an educational and entertaining experience.

NOTE: Yes, knowledge of the Japanese language is required to play this game.

Gameplay:
Players use verb cards to ask questions and engage in conversation. If a player uses language incorrectly, they might be shown an "Oops!" card as a gentle correction. Each player tries to deduce their role based on the tone and mannerisms of how other players talk to them. The roles are secretly assigned and indicate social status, which affects how players should interact.

Goal:
The objective of KEI-GO! is for all players to correctly guess their roles by the end of the round. Success requires understanding and applying respectful, humble, or polite language appropriately. If everyone guesses their roles accurately, the group wins, showcasing their mastery of honorific language.

Components:
The game includes role cards, verb cards, and "Oops!" cards, all of which facilitate the learning and use of proper honorific forms in different scenarios. The cooperative nature of the game encourages players to help each other succeed in using appropriate language and understanding social cues.

The Federation has received a distress call from the Independent Survey Vessel Faraway, which suffered some manner of catastrophe while transiting the mysterious hyperspace used for long distance travel, known to some as the void.

A rescue mission is quickly scraped together from available local assets to save the beleaguered crew; or at least recover the ship (crew members are cheap; survey vessels aren’t). Nineteen individuals have volunteered for this mission (even if only to escape something worse); however the boarding shuttle only has room for eight rescuers. The volunteers include a wide variety of soldiers and civilian consultants. Will you take the detective, whose experience will help determine what happened on the ship? The convict, a wily survivor forced onto the team by a vindictive warden? Or maybe Spanky, an experienced soldier, whose gatling gun can be expected to address any threat the team encounters?

Void Demon is a low-to-medium complexity, solitaire/cooperative sci-fi horror experience (with an option for head-to-head play). There are tactical overtones (let’s face it, the Void Demon wants to kill you), but the game is not simply won by wanton destruction (don’t try to tell that to Spanky, though). The specific victory conditions are unknown at the beginning of play; players accumulate investigation by exploring the Faraway. When enough investigation is accumulated, it can be expended to reveal the specific tasks that must be accomplished to win the game. However, the AI on the Faraway is completing the calculations for a final void jump: one that will spread the infestation to an unsuspecting Earth. When the countdown timer reaches zero, the Void Demon wins.

Ten distinct scenarios maintain the mystery for repeated play.

Choose your team, board the Faraway, and learn her secrets. Battle the horror unleashed from the void and save the Earth…or die trying. And if your mission aboard the Faraway doesn’t go your way, remember: in space, no one can hear you cheat…

Captain's Log, some asteroids pierced and destroyed our spaceship, fortunately the entire crew, although adrift, is safe. I send this help message to request the coordinates of the nearest Space Station, our suits will be able to use the energy of the celestial bodies to propel us in the right direction and keep us safe... for now.

Anomaly is a solitaire game, were you must guide to safety your crew and save them from a deadly black hole affected by the Anomaly.

On your turn you must do 1 Action and then the Anomaly will take a turn. This will continue until the game ends.

Actions:

    Contact: Match and Swap 1 Sector Card from your hand with 1 Sector Card from the space, flip both cards and receive Energy Points from the Sectors of the Space. Then use that Energy to move the Astronauts on the Space board and to the Space Station.



    Investigate: Take the Sector Card on the Black Hole space, shift all the Sectors from the right of the Black Hole, to the left, to refill the empty Sector. Play a Sector Card from your hand to the end of the Space board.


Beware! All the Energy that you didn't use to move the Astronauts, will be consumed by the Anomaly! Causing it to move closer to the Black Hole and altering it!

But don't worry to much, you have Technology Cards that can be use by the Astronauts at the Space Station to help you with this mission.

To win the game, you must guide ALL the Astronauts to the Space Station. You will lose the game if at least 1 Astronaut is trapped by the Black Hole or if the Black Hole reaches the Space Station.

Plan your strategy, Manage your Energy Sources and Save your Crew!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Close The Atlantic: World War Three
description:
Nominated for 2025  Charles S. Roberts Awards (“CSR Awards”) recognize excellence in tabletop conflict simulations.


     Note:  Print & Play version available wargame vault on the web. The PnP version contains the full map in sections and unit counters for home printing. The box version is no longer available, September 2025.


 This is a game of modern naval combat; the focus is mainly on Convoy operations. Additional scenarios introduce rules for battle in the Baltic Sea and the Mediterranean. Ground battle is abstracted, although air and missile strikes against the front lines are introduced in the third scenario.

The three scenarios can be combined to depict a full WW3 war all across Europe.

The time-frame of the game is NOW, 21st Century, beginning in April to September, as decided by a D6 die roll. October brings increased chances of hurricanes which will seriously hamper operations. Turns are weekly, beginning with the first week of the month, decided by the die roll.

The game abstracts the technical details for various equipment types. Counters represent 1 unit (like a submarine) or many (up to 100 aircraft) or a group of ships. Numeric counters are provided to slip under counters to indicate the count during engagements. For instance, a count of missiles fired.

The Print and Play version contains the full map in sections and unit counters for home printing. The box version is no longer available, September 2025. 
     
Special Note:
Launching a helicopter is either an Action or a Reaction.

After launch, a helicopter can do a detection attempt,
but the enemy has a chance first; to Act or React.

Helicopters can be launched as Actions even if no enemy
is present, one at a time. This replaces doing a counter
move or placement on the map.

Helicopters are placed on or near the parent counter and
remain with that counter.

Landing them is not an Action
or a Reaction; simply remove the helicopter counter(s).

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Forgotten Battles: The Battle for Belorussia
description:
The Forgotten Battles (TFB) covers the key fighting between late September 1943 and April 1944 in the Army Group Center portion of the Russian front. This was a war of attrition around cities and rugged terrain in the Belorussian region of the former Soviet Union. The major success in this sector was delivered by the Belorussian Front commander, Rokossovsky, who far outshone his neighboring Western Front commander. This leader was Sokolovsky, a protégé of Zhukov and later Soviet Chief of the General Staff. When the time came to write the official Soviet history of the war, it was not politically acceptable for Rokossovsky, who was Polish, to be seen as more effective than Sokolovsky. The result was that the success of one commander was suppressed just as vigorously as the failure of the other. The campaigns here laid the foundations for the collapse of Army Group Center the summer of 1944, but only recently has the full story of The Forgotten Battles been uncovered.

The game area covers Belorussia, eastern Poland and parts of the Baltic States, from Daugavpils and Velikiye Luki in the north to Chernigov in the south and from Brest-Litovsk and Tilsit in the west to Rzhev and Bryansk in the east. The Forgotten Battles contains scenarios for the major engagemnts: the desperate fighting around Vitebsk, the dramatic advance of the Belorussian Front through the Prypet Marsh, assaults on fortresses such as Bobruysk, Mogilev, and Orsha, and the last attempts to capture Kovel. The Soviet Partisan campaign requires constant reaction by the Axis player. The Forgotten Battles is designed by Tony Birkett, and is fully playable with The Third Winter. In fact, there is a one map-sized scenario that uses both games to cover the combined advance of the 1st Ukrainian Font and Belorussian Front. Both games can be combined for a massive gaming experience using campaign setups that start in September 1943 and January 1944.

The Soviet command and supply structure is modeled through Front HQ markers. Fronts are either in “Offensive” or “Regrouping” status, allowing for shifting axes of attack and the on/off nature of Soviet operational warfare during this time. Each Front has an assigned Air Force. The activities of Army Group South and Army Group North are represented by boundary markers that shift as the war advances off map, and the Axis player must be careful to watch his off-map flanks.

—description from the publisher

A squad level WW2 wargame using a revolutionary Princeps Games 'Fog of War' System (PGFOWS).

Iron Squad is a game for 2 players, where each player chooses 4 out of 7 units to complete the mission. Each unit has it's own movement, health, accuracy, etc.

The game is set in the summer of 1944 right before the D-Day landing. Your goal, as the US player is to gather the intelligence needed to make the landings successful, while the German player must not let this happen.

The game uses PGFOWS which tutorial you can find in the links section.

Fantasic Age is a dice-manipulation fantasy adventure game where the goal is to earn as much fame as possible during the adventurers entire lifespan.

Players start out as young adventurers, and go through adult and elderly age. Each turn, the players choose skills to improve their character, and choose a dungeon on the map to try to conquer. Each encounter is represented by a card, and solving all the encounters in the dungeon allows the player to encounter a boss.

As adults, players get to build a House on the map, and as Elderly, they get to enter a Clan, both of which largely enhance the player's abilities to earn fame. Player skills revolve around dice manipulation: changing die results, getting extra results for certain rolls, or adding dice. Players also have a Class that influences their chances in the dungeons in various ways.

The game can also be played solo, aiming for a certain fame score.

—user summary

description: A set of 33 additional (7 category, 26 word) cards that each feature a picture instead of a word.

Náttúruval is a game of unexpected competition in the animal kingdom.

It's composed of a deck of of over a hundred cards, each with a different animal and their real life stats in six different categories. Players get a set of cards to their hand and roll a pair of custom die to see which category they are comparing. One dice determines the category and the other whether the lowest or highest value in the category wins.

The categories are in order; 
number of letters in the scientific name
number of offspring at a time
average lifespan in the wild
average length, head to toe, of an adult
number of feet (or feet adjacent appendixes)
average weight of an adult

The players pick an animal from their hand that they think is the best for the match and then cards are revealed when everyone has played a card. The winner of that round puts the champion face up in front of them and the others are discarded.
Then the game ends when the deck is exhausted.

The main game is aimed at kids from 6-12 years old but there are multiple variations on the rules. One being a party version for grown ups where they persuade the group that their animal is the best fit for a random prompt like "Which animal would make the best president?" or "Which animal would be chosen as a spokesperson for a children's hospital?". And another for younger children to learn about animals and how to compare numbers.

This is a great game for the whole family.

If you love the atmosphere of hockey games, then you've come to the right place. Our collection of 3 games offers excitement and fun for anyone who likes to enjoy gripping duels or thrilling dice battles. Get ready for an adrenaline ride full of unexpected twists and intense rivalries, whether you're a hockey enthusiast or just looking for new fun challenges.

—description from the publisher

Players aim to get all of their own pucks into the net. Each player starts with 6 pucks in their color. They then take turns rolling a die and placing their pucks at numbered positions around the rink based on the result. If a position is already occupied by an opponent's puck, the opponent's puck is returned to their reserve.

On their turn, players can either add a new puck from their reserve to the rink or move one of their previously placed pucks around the rink. When moving a puck, the player adds the number on the rolled die to the current position's number to determine the new position. If the sum equals 6, the player scores and can place any puck from their reserve or the rink directly into the goal.

The game continues until one player scores with all of their pucks, winning the game. If the sum of the numbers when moving a puck exceeds 6, the puck stays in its current position. If a player rolls a number that would result in landing on a position where they already have a puck, they cannot take any action, and it becomes their opponent's turn.

The key strategy involves both placing pucks strategically and moving them effectively to score while blocking opponents from doing the same.

—user summary

Recode DNA and Restore LIFE
Welcome, I'm Emanuele. I am a geneticist and the DNA VOYAGER Project’s leading designer. Since 1998 I have worked in Toronto in genetic research and until 2041 I had dealt with HIV, HCV, HBV, HLA, SARS, Genomic Fragment Analysis, STR and various criminal cases. 
When it all started, I was lucky enough to be able to reach a shelter in time where I met other scientists with whom we worked for eight years in search of a solution. Perhaps Humanity has hope, but now everything is in your hands. 
The duty to which you are called is profoundly serious, although we will try to introduce you to your mission by telling you about it as if it were a game, to simplify things.

This experience is suitable for four participants, but can also be attempted alone, with two or with three. Participants will have to prepare to board the VOYAGER, a powerful interstellar ship capable of traveling at warp 10 speed.
Once on board, you will need to synthesize the nucleotide triplets and inoculate them into plasmid catalysts before arriving at Proxima Centauri.
Due to my age, I will not be able to follow you in the next stages of your mission. 
So, the time has come to wish you good luck and introduce yourself to Anna, a talented researcher. She will tell you what happened and what you need to do to save Humanity from extinction.

ANNA
Welcome trainee, my name is Anna, and I’m a space engineer.

Chapter 1: The Darkness of the Refuge
Eight years have passed since the doors of the nuclear shelter closed behind us. 
In 2041, a few minutes of madness were enough to change everything. Life on Earth disappeared almost immediately. It was May, but in a flash, it seemed like November. 
 Those who survived the nuclear explosions were unable to survive the deadly radiation. 
There are no more living beings out there: no humans, no animals, no plants. The land and water are contaminated, the air is dense with humidity, the sky is covered by radioactive clouds. 
Eight years of survival, fighting against hunger, despair, and the certainty that there was nothing left out there. 
 Here, at a depth of eight hundred meters, our only light is artificial, and time almost seems to have stopped. The days get confused, and the hope for a better future fade, little by little.
Food stocks ran out months ago. We were eating too much and did not notice that our ribosomes were stopping working and disrupting protein synthesis.
 In fact, we did not know that the barriers we had designed to protect the shelter - although they managed to protect us by containing 99% of the radiation - would not be able to prevent that minimal dose from poisoning our DNA, triggering a slow genetic drift, which began as mosaicism, which in a few months will have completed its course.
The thought of what lies ahead is unbearable, but we cannot give up. Not yet!

THE GAME
During the travel, the four players have to recode the 20 aminoacids. Once the biocabinet is open the degradation step starts.

—description from the designer

Successfully ‘studying’ an astronomical object gives you Science Points (SP). These Science Points are your progress towards publishing papers and eventually getting your PhD.  Studying an object is a combination of playing an astronomer and a tool card and rolling two dice. Matching icons between cards will give you bonuses on your dice roll. By the end of an Astromania game session, you’ll probably learn something new about how astronomy research works, simply with skills as straightforward as icon matching! What’s more, all these icons are inspired by real study techniques astronomers use, detailed in the Glossary section of the game’s rulebook. Each object, astronomer, and tool card also has scientifically accurate descriptions meant to be a casual and quick read for those interested. You can use these as a guide to learn more about what happens behind the scenes of astronomy academic work as well as to learn something new about our universe!

In 1792, Nikolai Ivanovich Lobachevsky, the Russian mathematician and geometer was born.

In 1953, Tom Lehrer borrowed Lobachevsky's name for a song inspired by Danny Kaye's Constantin Stanislavski routine, with the subject changed from acting to mathematics, and in particular to plagiarism in mathematics.  (Lobachevsky's name was chosen purely because it had the right number of syllables and fit the rhythm.)  In the song is the lyric: "I have a friend in Minsk, who has a friend in Pinsk, whose friend in Omsk has friend in Tomsk with friend in Akmolinsk.  His friend in Alexandrovsk has friend in Petropavlovsk, whose friend somehow is solving now the problem in Dnepropetrovsk."

In 2002, this game was designed to turn that series of connections into a game.

In 2022, Tom Lehrer put all of his works into the public domain.

Now in 2024, that act has inspired the release of this game into the public domain.

In Lobachevsky, players collect friend cards from seven stacks to try to build a collection of friends all of the way to Dnepropetrovsk.  Along the way players can publish papers to build up or refine their collection of friends, can extend the work of other publications, and can even request help from other players to fill gaps in their own research strings.  Whoever can publish results researched from Dnepropetrovsk first wins.

—description from the designer

Amidst routine school drop-offs and vet visits, Faith vanished after a clandestine meeting with a mysterious client. No witnesses, no clues just an inexplicable disappearance. Six days later, her abandoned car surfaced in a quiet lane, yet Faith remained elusive. Desperation hung heavy in the air as her loved ones organised a frantic search. The truth, however, lay shrouded in darkness until a determined teenager stumbled upon the horrifying remains of Faith Mooring in the depths of Garenton's woods. Secrets fester beneath the village's picturesque facade, and each twist reveals a web of deception and betrayal.

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case. In the serene Cotswolds village of Garenton, a birthday retreat turns deadly for Sophia Armsby-Ward. Was it a suicide or something far more sinister?

You play a spider who spins a cobweb to catch her prey. However, you are not alone but have to compete with other spiders, spinning their webs on top of yours.

The more discs you have on the cobweb, the more discs you will earn (but only if you have the top position) and the more discs you will be able to place. The player with the most discs on the cobweb when the game ends wins.

The game board consists of a cobweb divided into 18 connected spaces, each of which has one of three different prey symbols. The players take actions to place discs on the cobweb, to move them around, and to claim more discs for the areas where they have the top disc and where the area symbol matches the action. Such claims move their discs to the bottom, forcing them to rebuild their cobwebs.

The actions are selected from a randomly set up action board. Pick up the action markers in one action space and drop them one by one in the spaces of the actions you wish to take. The more previous action markers there are in an action space, the more expensive is the action.

—description from the publisher
