Chasms is the first game released from a series! Behind Chasms is a world called Caznia. Below is a short story from Book I, Tales of Caznia: A Wing of Hope

~~~

It was the usual drizzly season at the small seaside town of Dragon’s Edge. Some of the fishermen were at sea, hoping to haul in some last bit of the ocean's treasures before going home for dinner. The sky was overcast, but the air felt peaceful and calm. A chilly April wind ran through the streets, cheekily tapping on whatever the townspeople hung outside their shops and houses. It was getting close to evening, and the place was winding down; People were scattering, eagerly going back for a nice warm stew, before any possible flurries of rain visit their homes. Oil lanterns around the town were dotted with a warm orange light; gold sparkles slowly swirled out of the lanterns in all directions, before turning into silver stars and fading away into oblivion. ‘The Perri Light’ as the locals would say; The town’s pride and joy, Sir Perison, was an Aurum Knight.

If one would ask ‘What is an Aurum Knight?’ The locals would either flip the traveler a look of surprise or suspicion, at least nothing that would be polite. In the kingdom of Meera, where Knights were revered as Lords, Aurum Knights were at the pinnacle of them.

Leaders of entire armies, Aurum Knights held the strongest Blessings - natural powers granted mysteriously hundreds of years ago to all humankind. Sir Perison was rumored to have driven back an invading yellow dragon some years back single-handedly, making him a hero of the people as well as ‘Mr Attractive’ to the ladies.

Everyone in the lands of Caznia could invoke their Blessings to different extents and create phenomena based on their skills and creative minds. Sir Perison, in gratitude to the town that raised him, created a fire that would sparkle and last much longer than your usual lantern variety.

So the townspeople fondly named it after the Knight.

However, Perison was not in the mood today for anything else. Holding a floating crystal ball in his palm, the tall, muscular looking Knight clad in an armor of velvet and gold was striding quickly through the town, looking for something anxiously. The wind kept his cloak flapping every now and then, and the slow and steady hum of the crystal formed a rhythm to which the Knight was unconsciously striding to. John, the butcher’s fat little son who loved to observe people around him, was staring at Perison with a look of wonder, licking a bar of candy which his mother bought for him at the stall across at the same time. It was his first time seeing a Knight. Anyone who caught a glimpse of Perison and was about to greet him kept quiet, as they could tell the Knight was looking for something earnestly... everyone else until granny Helva.

AS the focused Aurum Knight was striding past the town square into a narrow alleyway, granny Helva caught sight of him through her window; “Perison, my lad, where are you going at this hour? Come in for some salmon-pie, baked fresh today,” Helva chirped, toddling out of her house, stretching out her hand towards the Knight, beckoning him to come over.

Perison stopped in his tracks, his boots clanking to a stop on the stone pavement. Turning around, the Knight loosened his shoulders, took a deep breath, relaxed, gave a big smile and said, “Granny Helva! Thanks for the offer. I am in a hurry today though, maybe tomorrow?”

"Nonsense lad! There is always time for a good strip of baked fish: granny Helva insisted. Trembling, she stretch out her arm again and took a gentle hold on Perison's hand. "Come in"

"Thanks so much granny", Perison replied. The Knight smiled broadly, his crow's feet appearing in fine lines behind his sparkling blue eyes. Perison gently patted the kind old lady. "But I am looking for someth- someone urgently. And-"

Before he could finish, a cry of thunder split through the gray clouds overlooking the town. Shortly after, a tornado started to form and slowly descend upon the beach of Dragon’s Edge. The waves roared in an agitated manner, and a drizzle began to steadily grow in intensity.

“Sir Perison! I have found the family!” Screamed a young voice. Dashing from the left across the street, a young man about 17, cloaked in a grey hood, ran towards the Aurum Knight. Wearing a pair of brown leather boots, the squire slipped on a tiny puddle recently formed by the drizzle and would have given granny Helva a full prostrating bow, but Perison caught his arm and grabbed him in time.

“Careful, Graham, we wouldn’t want to injure anyone”, Perison chided gently at his companion.

Ï am so-sor-thank yo- I mean sorry granny” Graham apologized, grinning sheepishly as granny Helva smiled and patted him slowly on the shoulder.

“Go indoors, granny, I will be right back,” giving Helva another gentle pat on the arm, Perison steered the old lady back onto her porch with both hands, tossed the crystal away, muttering something along the lines of “Dumb little thing” and started sprinting towards where the tornado was moving down upon.

After a few steps, Perison held out his left hand, where a bracelet of gold adorned with seven beautiful rubies were. “Volaris!” shouted the Knight, and a cloud of gold dust and flames sprouted beneath him. Perison shot into the sky and flew.

Fat little John gawked, coughed and dropped his candy.

There's been a viral emergency... and you're in the middle of it. The situation is critical, patient zero just died and you've been given the responsibility of keeping everyone safe and communicating with the people helping from outside the confined perimeter. Or maybe you are the person outside, trying to keep the situation under control and making sure everyone is safe. Your role is to obtain as much information as you can from the person inside and organise the help. Will you be able to do it or will everything fall apart?

Quarentena is a 30-minute narrative game for 2-players where the story is written as you play. Feel like a character in a movie and decide everyone's destiny. To help you write the story, each player has a set of action cards that get triggered as the time runs out. You'll know when to activate one thanks to the game's original soundtrack, which has sound warnings.

You are the owner of your own destiny, write a horror, sci-fi, humor or drama filled story... you can do whatever you want!

Quarentena is a game played best through text chat (you can play long-distance) and for 2-players, but you can stream your games and let friends and family comment on your choices.

A coffee-themed roll and write game. Three game sheets with different combinations of beans and drippers. A special game sheet made in collaboration with real coffee beans is included as an appendix.

The new coffee beans have arrived! To bring out the best in the beans and brew a delicious cup of coffee, you need a little luck with the dice and your skill! Aroma, acidity, bitterness, and richness. The four flavors that express the taste of coffee. Choose one from the dice rolled and write the number from the dice for each flavor. The higher number, the more value of coffee will be extracted, but if you brew it randomly, there will be some ingredients that will not taste good.

This package, "First Batch," includes three types of game sheets with the motif of three beans from Ethiopia, Brazil, and Indonesia. You can enjoy the game with different play sensations.

Sometimes it's important to pour slowly to bring out the beans' potential. Dicey Coffee Dripper is a roll and write game where you roll the dice and write down the results on a sheet. Make the best tasting coffee ever!

In Lemma Lemma, your goal is to yell out the most words based on the letter cards on the table.

Lemur has escaped the clutches of the evil Dr. Knizia! Now he travels through space-time in search of the brightest colors while finding himself out there. Will you find Lemur too?

In Lemur, players will try to get rid of all their hand cards before the rest of the players, or they will receive negative points for the cards left in their hand. Each card has a combination of numbers or the lemur, in a kind of domino, and the player can only play a card if at least one of the numbers coincides with one of the numbers on the card played immediately before (again, like a domino).

By laying down a double card, the player can still give up an additional card from their hand to another player, not only leaving them with fewer cards, but disrupting the other players by filling up their hand. However, if you fail to draw a card, you must draw a new one for your hand (in the best Uno style).

The player who runs out of cards triggers the end of the game and scores zero points, while the others score negative points.

The game allows for several matches in sequence to determine a "big winner" or can be played in single quick matches.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 100 wins the game.

Playing for ingenious little chimps, you can steal anything that's lying around: rubber ducks, hair rollers, and even a lobster necklace! But can you sell off your entire catch on the best terms and make a fortune from it before demand fades away?

In this dynamic trick-taking game, you will outsmart opponents to grab goods, trade them shrewdly during special sessions, and pile up your banana riches.

Players take on the roles of chimpanzees who gather all sorts of clutter and try to sell it for the highest value, aiming to earn the most amount of bananas by the end of the game. After each trick, the player who won the trick starts the card draft, but the last player applies special properties of cards and influences the suit prices in the market.

Sell quickly and become the ultimate CHIMP-ion!

description:
The "Bookworm" expansion is all about creating your very own personal library, thanks to 20 new Book double-cards filled with lore, puzzles and other challenges.

Just like the “Knowledge is power” Season 1 expansion, this new expansion contains 40 cards, 20 of which are in double format.

Note: Requires the core game and The 7th Citadel: The First Garden.

description:
Each player takes the coloured markers of one colour. The letter cards are dealt to the players face down. Each letter corresponds to a letter contained within the eight crops named on the game board.

In the base game, each player in turn order plays a letter card from their hand face up in the centre of the board. They then place one of their coloured markers on a matching letter on the game board.

The alternate game rules allow for bluffing. In this version each player in turn order plays a letter card (face down) from their hand to the centre of the board. They then place one of their coloured markers on a letter on the game board. If challenged and caught cheating, the challenger substitutes one of their markers on the board with the person who cheated. If challenged and found not to be cheating (after revealing their letter card), the person being accused substitutes one of their markers with the accuser.

Once all the letter cards have been played, the hand ends. If you have placed the most coloured markers on one of the eight crops, you win a check for the value of that crop. The game ends after four hands, with the winner being the person having collected the most money in checks.

KeyForge: Tokens of Change is a special new set that revisits the popular token creature mechanic, which was originally introduced in the KeyForge: Winds of Exchange set. This new set features all of the KeyForge houses that were not included in Winds of Exchange, plus a new house with a unique link to the past. House Redemption is making its debut in KeyForge: Tokens of Change, one splintered from house Sanctum. Every house in Tokens of Change will introduce new cards, including its own set of token creatures.

What exactly are token creatures? Every Tokens of Change deck will include exactly one token creature reference card, plus card abilities within the deck that will make token creatures. Each time a card ability instructs you to make a token creature, you take the top card of your deck and add it to your battle line facedown as a token creature. Each one of your token creatures in play is considered a copy of your deck’s token creature reference card.

KeyForge: Tokens of Change decks are completely legal in official KeyForge tournaments.

—description from the publisher

description:
This set is part of the Forged in the Fire wave for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare!

Bloatflies are hideous, bulging monstrosities, mutated forms of the common modern flies. They will attack from afar, peppering their target with spines from their abdomen, but will sometimes fly closer to ensure their larvae can properly feast on their living prey. These predators are a horrific reminder that the Great War changed everything about the world we know.

“It’s a race to the finish!”

During the game, players roll four dice to move each Bitty POP! piece along the board, indicated by a number on the die and a color that matches each Bitty bases They can play cards to help their favorite Bitty, and to earn points, as well as collecting Chase tokens (if the card played shows a CHASE symbol).
The game ends when three Bitty pieces reach the finish line (finishing in places one to three— gold, silver and bronze).
For each card in the player’s hand showing one of the three winning Bitty pieces, players score varying points… 3 points for gold, 2 points for silver and 1 point for bronze, as well as 1 point for each Chase token. The player with the most points wins. 
Ties are decided by the player with the most Chase tokens being the winner. If there’s still a tie, the player with the fewest 0-point cards in their hand wins.

Hodge Podge Dodgeball exists in the small village of Longcoat, England where raccoons are competing in their Dodgeball League Championship!

Players try to dodge getting hit (taking a trick) in this avoidance, team, must-not-follow trick-taker.

At the start of the round, players create their bench with their cards. Then they play tricks which entail any amount of catching, dodging, or blocking balls. Players must be careful, as their hand of cards is their life and if they are hit, they must send a card (a raccoon) to their bench. If a player is out of cards or cannot make a valid play, their team loses!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Guadalcanal: The Battle for Henderson Field, Oct 23-26 1942
description: Take command of the valiant Marines of the 1st Marine Division, supported by Soldiers of the 164th Infantry Regiment, desperately defending Henderson Field against waves of Japanese troops.

boardgame: Last Light: King Targeting Promo Card
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 12 Kickstarter campaign.

Card Description:
When a ship bombardment rolls a *, you may destroy an extractor even if there are enemy ships present.

boardgame: Last Light: 'Til Death Ray Promo Card
description:
Originally available during the The Secret Cabal Gaming Podcast is Outta Control in 2024 Kickstarter campaign.

Card Description:
Exhaust to place an attention token underneath an opponent's ship. The player who destroys this ship gains | from the supply

Stibbles and his family of tiny gnomes have BIG trouble on their hands...

The mischievous creature, Gu'Gu, is up to no good. Opening portals across the land and causing chaos. These magic doors drop dangerous creatures everywhere they don't belong, and the many locations you'll venture through contain challenges the gnomes must face.

This band of unlikely heroes will need to team up with wild Companions along their journey, as well as collect resources and complete Quests with the helpful guidance of the legendary adventurer Stibbles! A long and magical path stands between the gnomes and the troublesome Gu'Gu, venture through the portals, and set things right!

Companion Quest is a cooperative game for 1-4 players, who take on the role of gnomes, tasked with preventing Gu'Gu from causing chaos. Players will collect magical Energy Cubes (dice), tasty Snacks (resources), and fantastical Companions (abilities) along the way. Each Companion has unique abilities that allow players to manipulate their dice to overcome the challenges they will face.

Complete quests before the Mischief Meter reaches the end of the track and things spiral out of control!

—description from the publisher

El juego que les ayudará a estudiar jugando. Podrán aprender parte de los contenidos de enseñanza primaria y secundaria mientras pasan un rato divertido. Para ganar, deberás ir acertando preguntas para moverte por el tablero y llegar a la última casilla, Si aciertas tres de las preguntas de la tarjeta, Aprobado!

The game that will help them study by playing. They will be able to learn part of the primary and secondary education content while having a fun time. To win, you must answer questions to move around the board and reach the last box. If you answer three of the questions on the card, Pass!
These simple rules (three pages) adapt The Rules According to Ral to Japanese warfare in the Sengoku Jidai era in the sixteenth century.
