description: Players compete to be the first ghost hunter to take a picture of the ghost.  This is accomplished by landing on a camera tile on the board and drawing a card.  If the card contains an image of the ghost, a picture has been taken successfully and the player can then work toward escaping the manor.  The card does not contain an image of the ghost, they must keep trying.  Meanwhile, the battery-operated ghost moves around the board and if it touches your player piece, you will be sent back to the starting point!
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Dive deeper into the world of Grimcoven with special add-on, Hunters Personal Stories. This add-on allows you to explore the rich backstories and motivations of each Hunter, offering a more immersive and personalized gameplay experience.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns rolling the die to move their respective beavers in a race to reach the top of the board.  But roll a number on the dark side of the die and the giant log comes rolling down!  Any beavers that are grabbed by the log have to start over from the beginning unless they've managed to reach the checkpoint.  The first player to get their beaver to the dam is the winner.

In Grimcoven, as a player, you will be increasing power by accepting Lament from the fallen enemies. While it will add a lot of benefits, it will also corrupt you and have some negative perks.

Renegade Hunter HATES lament and rejects it 100%.

Generally, Hunters use lament and try to balance carefully between being corrupted and having enough power to defeat the evil. But Renegade Hunter hates everything that Lament touches, but he is too weak to fight bosses, so instead, he goes against Hunters.

So one of the corruption cards can trigger him entering the combat and trying to hunt you down, as you are tainted with Lament to some extent - even if you have full control over it and use it to kill the Boss terrorizing the city.

—description from the publisher

With this expansion for 890 Anno Domini, you can play solo!

Build a winch, host the King’s camp, and welcome a noble knight in shining armor. Discover eight single-player scenarios with new components!

Plus, this expansion includes six brand-new scenarios for 2-4 players to enhance your base game experience.

—description from the publisher

If you were a frog, you would love eating bugs, but would you do what it takes to best other frogs in a competition for the delicacies?

In this exciting trick-taking game, your tactical skills will be tested as you aim to catch more bugs than your frog-ponents and earn the most Victory Points. Hunt in various locations, win tricks, challenge other frogs, snatch victory from them, and be crowned the most agile frog in the swamp.

Players not only need to win the tricks but also strategically play cards from their hand to score it advantageously. They must also skillfully use challenge tiles to earn the maximum number of points. Additionally, players should keep a close eye on opponents because as soon as someone runs out of cards in hand, the round ends, and everyone tallies their Victory Points for the won tricks and challenges.

Be the best and become the fullest frog by the end of the day!

—description from the publisher

The football simulation game at home!
Experience the sensations of a football match in real time on stage! Form your team, play your best cards to score as many goals as possible and win.


     Rules of the game: Form two teams of the same number of players. Place yourself around the game board, alternating teams. Shuffle and deal 8 cards to each player. Place the rest of the cards face down to form the draw pile. Roll the die to determine which team engages. Play clockwise.



     Goal of the game: You must win a football match! Move your team's pawns with your cards and the die to create game actions. Play possession of the ball by passing to your teammates and impose your style of play in defense and attack to score as many goals as possible without cash in. At the end of the game, the team with the most goals wins!


Le jeu de simulation de football à la maison !
Vie les sensations d’un match de foot en temps réel sur plateau ! Forme ton équipe, joue tes meilleures cartes pour marquer un maximum de but et remporte la victoire.


     Règle du jeu : Formez deux équipes du même nombre de joueurs. Placez-vous autour du plateau de jeu en alternant les équipes. Mélangez et distribuez 8 cartes à chaque joueur. Placez le reste des cartes face cachée pour former la pioche. Lancez le dé pour déterminer l’équipe qui engage. Jouez dans le sens des aiguilles d’une montre.



     But du jeu : Tu dois remporter un match de foot ! Déplace les pions de ton équipe avec tes cartes et le dé pour créer des actions de jeu. Joue la possession de balle en faisant des passes à tes coéquipiers et impose ton style de jeu en défense comme en attaque pour marquer un maximum de buts sans en encaisser. A l’issue de la partie, l’équipe ayant marqué le plus de buts gagne !

You're a mobster working for the biggest boss in town, Betty "Two Bricks", but your work ain't the bee's knees and you've made the big cheese unhappy — so unhappy, she sends you a brick in the post. Can you get your gang together and finish three grifts to beef up Betty's power in the city before she sends her second brick?

Betty Two Bricks is a Roaring Twenties-themed party card game for 2—6 players ages 11+ featuring easy-to-learn gameplay with both collection and take-that elements. Over 30-45 minutes per game, you need to glom, cheat, and bluff your way to victory in a race against your fellow players.

—description from the publisher

Embark on an explosive adventure combining treasure hunt and battle royale: explore, survive and win to become the master of Bomb Island! A fun competitive game for the whole family, with strategy, but not too much!
     Treasure Hunt: Explore every corner of the island to gather 5 precious stones and become the best explorer!
     Battle Royale: Wield weapons to knock out your opponents and survive!
     Explosive Adventure: Master the art of bombs to destroy enemy boats and gain the advantage!
     Strategy and Cunning: Skillfully use your 6 cards in hand to develop your strategy and surprise your opponents.
     Endless Adventure: Each game is unique, with unexpected twists and thrills.
     A Game For All: Friendly and fun, for 2 to 4 players from 7 years old. Perfect for livening up evenings with family or friends.



     Embarque pour une aventure explosive mêlant chasse au trésor et battle royale : explore, survis et gagne pour devenir le maître de Bomb Island ! Un jeu compétitif amusant pour toute la famille, avec de la stratégie, mais pas trop !
     Chasse au Trésor : Explore chaque recoin de l’île pour réunir 5 pierres précieuses et devenir le meilleur explorateur !
     Battle Royale : Manie les armes pour mettre tes adversaires hors de combat et survivre !
     Aventure Explosive : Maitrise l’art des bombes pour détruire les bateaux ennemis et prendre l’avantage !
     Stratégie et Ruse : Utilise habilement tes 6 cartes en main pour développer ta stratégie et surprendre tes adversaires.
     Une Aventure infinie : Chaque partie est unique, avec des rebondissements inattendus et des moments palpitants.
     Un Jeu Pour Tous : Convivial et amusant, de 2 à 4 joueurs dès 7 ans. Parfait pour animer les soirées en famille ou entre amis.

In Smart City you become a city planner passionate about sustainability. Your goal is to plan the best city keeping in mind the needs of the citizens and ecology.

Each player has a personal city map, where they'll create their city by placing parks and buildings (which you can stack to create blocks). The city is divided in districts, sectors and plots. You have to place your buildings and parks according to each disttrict sustainability goals to get the best configuration possible.

Smart City is played in 3 phases with 14 rounds. In each round you'll flip a card from the action deck. One side will tell you the area of the city (North, South, East or West) where you can place your buildings and resources in that round. The other side will tell you the available buildings and/or resources for building. Place your buildings and parks strategically because some plots have bonuses that you can only get if you place a social centre as your first building in the plot.  At the end of each phase you choose 1 district to score and gain its bonus.

At the end of the game, the city planner with the most sustainable design will win the game!

—description from the publisher

The day starts early on the farm. You wake up and get out of bed like any other normal day, only to discover that the sun is already over the horizon... Wait, the sun is already up?... The clock says 8 AM! You should already be working on the day's tasks. What happened to the rooster? You head outside to check, but as soon as you step out, you see the devastation all over the farm. During the night, a hurricane destroyed the farm fences and the animals have escaped! You need to recover them. Hopefully, they haven't gone too far. Now, you must go out to fix the fences and rescue them from the nearby forest.

Rolling Ranch: Celeiros e Rebanhos reimplements the original game with a new scoring system and new missions. With a pencil and a Farm Sheet, players use the results of a dice roll to rescue animals and improve their farm. Each player tries to place the animals on their farm in the best way possible and build structures to receive bonuses that will help them achieve the highest score. Everyone plays at the same time! Who will rescue the most animals and be the most successful farmer?

—description from the publisher

These rules explore the day-to-day reality of living in a city surrounded by ruins, where humanity is packed together like the lowest beasts but those with the wealth can find more space than others. Points/Equipment based skirmish level game. Suitable for any scale. Detailed background to Dystopian City but can be used for any setting.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Forbidden Psalm: Endless Horrors From Between The Stars
description:
Endless Horrors From Between The Stars is a game of playing Evil Cults who have decided that the world needs to end, that the Things between the stars should come forth and take this world and make it anew. A game of Cosmic Horror, Body Horror, and playing as the bad guys!

In Forbidden Psalm: Endless Horrors From Between the Stars you recruit a cult of five zealous souls to undertake the call of your very own Endless Horror. Playing across a narrative campaign, you go about summoning your Endless Horror so it may bring about the end of days.

A game of Cosmic Horror, Body Horror, and Ducks, in Endless Horrors From Between the Stars your cult is disposable. Its members suffer Injuries, Flaws, and unleash horrible magic. But, for the most zealous followers, power and faith awaits!

The game starts with each player creating their very own Endless Horror by rolling on three D100 tables, providing over 1 million possible Horrors!

Endless Horrors From Between the Stars contains all the rules you need to play a fully fleshed out miniatures skirmish game. Included in the book is a full narrative campaign and rules so that you can play the game set across different time periods.

Over 160 pages of Cosmic Horror await you.

Endless Horrors From Between the Stars contains

New Monsters to add to your games of Forbidden Psalm and MÖRK BORG TTRPG.
    A full narrative campaign.
    Rules for playing as the good guys. If you want to be the Investigators stopping the End of the World, then we have your back! 
    Rules for setting your campaigns during different time periods. 
    Solo, COOP, and Vs rules for your preferred number of players.

boardgame: Dumb Ways to Die Sudden Death
description: So Many Dumb Ways To Die, in this game three accident-prone Beans, are depending on two players to save them from impending doom. In this player elimination game the two players play rounds of drawing and playing cards to race to stop the Beans from getting killed. In this 2 player game there can be only one survivor. It's the game of sudden death. This is a simplified version of the “Dumb Ways to Die” card game tailored for a quick 2 player experience. This game can also be shuffled into the core game to allow up to six players.

The angels fall, contaminating Eden with their radiant light, bringing with them the love of destruction, drying up the old animal habitats, polluting the air and drying up the earth. The survivors try to build a new civilization, trading between towns and cities, but you must defend at all costs the vehicles of war that protect the consumption that we all need.

This is a single player Roll And Write mechanics game with a minimum duration of 20 minutes.

To play it is recommended to have:

     The truck sheet and the gang sheet, (It is recommended that the sheets have a polypropylene protector)
     An erasable marker.
     Various D6 Dice


Objective
Reach the end of the road with the truck and the driver alive.

—description from the designer

Remember the Land is a solo game where you play as a traveler and cartographer who is trying to register a map of the region and its features before the land is destroyed. There is nothing you can do to save the area, but you can make sure that future generations remember its history. You have to be quick though, or you will be caught in the calamity that's about to happen.

To play this game you will need: standard deck of 52 playing cards with the jokers removed, pen/pencil and note paper, a token representing your character, a flat surface for laying out your card grid.

The game starts by creating a 4x4 grid of face down cards and placing your character's token in the starting point. Move your character through the grid, from the Start Point to the Exit Point, mapping your path and managing the Countdown. Your goal is to map as much of the land as you can AND get to the Exit Point before the Countdown reaches zero.

Each card has a narrative prompt to be answered as you character moves through the grid. All though you are required to draw a map while you play, you don't need artistic skills. The important part is that YOU understand the map, it doesn't matter if it is just a bunch of doodles and words.

—description from the designer
Dice game where they are used as tokens and their value is used for placement on the board and subsequently indicates their ability to move, to achieve a three-in-a-row placement.

boardgame: Veneficus: The Secret Game of Psychics
description:
Veneficus: The Secret Game of Psychics is a game full of introspect, trust, and deceit where each player takes on the role of a psychic trying to prove they are the greatest of them all.

Each turn, one psychic will become the sitter for a reading about their life, but only they know they are the sitter for that turn. A Card of Truth will be revealed that says something about the sitter's life - possibly their past, present, or future. The card will have two choices for the psychics to choose from while making their blind reading of the sitter. The sitter will secretly choose the option that they know to be their truth. Meanwhile, all other psychics will select the option they feel gives them the best chance to match the sitter. Once everyone has selected one of the options, the sitter will reveal themselves along with their truth. Now it is up to the psychics to convince the sitter they correctly predicted their choice and made the proper reading. The sitter will then choose one of the psychics they trust and that psychic will reveal their reading.

In early rounds, psychics will likely choose the option they feel a majority of players would select in order to give themselves the best chance of matching the sitter's answer. As the game progresses, there will be fewer possible sitters available which allows the psychics to make more informed selections for each reading.

If the reading was correct, the sitter and all of the psychics who made the correct reading will earn a Clairvoyance Card. If the psychic deceived the sitter and did not make the correct reading, then this psychic and all others who had the incorrect reading will earn a Clairvoyance Card instead. Additionally, the psychic who deceived the sitter will earn the Veneficus Token which is worth bonus points at the end of the game. This token can be stolen each time a psychic deceives a sitter.

Once all psychics have been the sitter once or twice depending on the amount of players, the game will end and the psychic with the most clairvoyance cards has proven to be the most powerful in the world.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Snoop Cereal: Get in the Game
description: In Snoop Cereal: Get in the Game, players are members of the K-9 basketball team who want to control the court by laying, rotating, and replacing tiles to block opponents. At game's end, whoever controls the most connected portions of the scoring section wins.

Card game in which we take the role of a government organization that seeks to catch the families of mafia animals, we will use the gangsters in our hands (Those who cooperate with us) to catch the others.

Using one of the gangsters from our hand and as many as possible from the table, we must collect 15 points to catch them, but be careful, the ones you have in your hand do not have the same value as the ones on the table, simple game, easy to learn, and with a strategy for the whole family.

Három „Szeszély kártya”, melyekkel tovább bővíthető a játékélmény. Ezeken a szeszélykártyákon szereplő egyedi karakter 3 különböző akcióval teszi még izgalmassá a tengeri kalandot.

These three "Caprice Cards" add 3 new actions to the game to make your sea adventure even more exciting.

The brothel is open for business and you have just a few weeks to build enough reputation to get the king to come to your brothel.

This is a fast-paced tower defense game. Players choose a character, each starting with specific dice, passive abilities, and special skills. The game begins with a set number of tiles based on the number of players. These tiles can be swapped, upgraded, and modified as customers start arriving.

Each round, characters can move one tile if necessary. If a customer is in the same room, players roll their dice to match symbols and remove the customer. However, while peasants and commoners are easy to clear, the arrival of nobles and royals intensifies the game. To build up your brothel, you'll need to purchase upgraded tiles, hire workers, unlock new dice and abilities, and more.

—description from the publisher

The flood is coming... Race to the ark and gather animals along the way. Featuring rules for children as young as five years old, this game grows in complexity along with your child. 2-4 players race along a colorful board, collecting cute animals drawn in a variety of cartoon styles. A quick play the whole family can enjoy, Race to the Ark includes three sets of rules and a double sided game board to accommodate children of all levels of gaming experience.

From simple rules for as young as 5, this game adds new mechanics as your kids grow, with push-your-luck, set collection, and more!

Blokus Shuffle: Pokémon Edition takes the classic strategy of Blokus and mixes in a deck of Pokémon-themed action cards. The object of the game hasn't changed - play as many of your pieces as possible onto the board by following the one rule of Blokus: each piece you play must touch another of your pieces only at the corner. But now each player has a deck of Pokémon action cards to give them an advantage.

On your turn you'll play a Pokémon action card, then play your piece, then refill your hand. Each card features a specific Pokémon's special attack like Quick Attack, Iron Defense, or Confuse Ray.

When no one can play anymore pieces, the game ends and the player with the fewest unplayed pieces wins.

—description from the designer

Players will fake it 'til they make it with the Bad Interviews by Funemployed adult party game. Each round, the Manager presents the job to earn. The rest of the players are Applicants that build a resume using Qualification Cards. They take turns making their pitch, hilariously explaining each ridiculous card. Then, the Manager hits them with a trick question. The Manager's favorite gets the job. Earn the most jobs and win the game!

UNO Flip Attack combines the game-changing strategy of flipping the deck from the easier Mild Side to the tougher Wild Side with the unpredictable excitement of the attack card launcher!

UNO Flip Attack plays just like UNO Attack, except now the cards are double-sided. When you play a Flip Card, watch the launcher flip the deck from Mild to Wild. Mild Side cards like Skip and Hit 2 become Skip Everyone and Hit 5 on the Wild Side. Be the first player to go out to win the game.

Team up to enjoy the word game you know and love with a special Pokémon twist! Cooperate with other players to complete Challenge Cards and gather Pokémon - just use your letter tiles to build crisscrossing words on the board. Finish a Challenge Card and you catch that Pokémon! Gather your fellow Trainers, pick up the Scrabble Pokémon Edition and get ready for some quick, casual fun!

Scrabble Pokémon Edition has two game modes. In classic Scrabble, Pokémon names and places are not only legal to play, you'll earn bonus points for doing so. In Together Mode, work with the other players to complete Challenge Cards and capture Pokémon. If you manage to complete all 20 challenges before the tiles run out, everyone wins.

In the bustling Metropolis, a newly constructed beacon of opportunity, a steady stream of citizens are drawn by the promise of a fresh start. Agents (the players), advocates of the organization known as “The Agency”, are charged with the vital task of fostering a harmonious environment and delivering unparalleled service to the citizens under their protection while also strengthening their own reputation inside the organization.

In the pursuit of victory, players must use their Cheeky Gizmos (Action Cards), to increase their Reputation while hindering their rivals' efforts to do the same. Their Cheeky Gizmos help them to perform strategic moves, such as allocating Citizens or adjusting the Satisfaction Index of the city’s Specialists. This is though easier said than done, as players must be ready to defend themselves against unforeseen events caused by the malfunctioning Event Generator (Event Cards).

Reservoir Cubes is a strategic heist print&play game where players lead a gang of five robbers through a vault to collect as much loot as possible. The game features a diceless mechanic that relies on player choices and interactions. Each turn, players coordinate their robbers' movements, using special abilities to disable security systems and open safes.

The gameplay revolves around planning and tactical decisions. Players start by rolling five cubes to determine the movement of each robber, designating a leader and a lazy robber for each turn. The roles of Leader and Lazy need to be balanced carefully, as each role has implications for the gang's efficiency and stress levels. Robbers move through the vault in a coordinated manner, collecting keys to unlock safes and avoiding security cameras. Special moves can be used to alter outcomes, adding layers of strategy.

Key elements of the game include the Gang Stress Meter, which tracks the overall stress level of the robbers, and the Mexican Standoff mechanic, where robbers must resolve conflicts when extra loot is taken. The robbers generate stress by stealing more than their agreed-upon share, leading to potential conflicts and complications. The goal is to balance the loot collected with the stress level to successfully complete the heist.

—description from the designer

Step into the strategic realm of Age of Myths, set in ancient Greece, a board game where you embody a warlord devoted to a god, driven by the ambition to conquer the world. Accumulate gold, battle formidable mythological monsters, amass armies, and overcome your adversaries to emerge victorious.

Basics of Gameplay

The gameplay unfolds during 20 rounds, with each round comprising individual player turns. For instance, in a game with 4 players, a round concludes after all 4 turns are taken.

Within a turn, players can choose 2 actions from a set of 3: spending, engaging, or gambling. The strategic decisions made during these turns can yield 
significant consequences.

Moving around on the game board and attacking monsters or other players is enabled by using dices. When moving the player rolls 3 D6 dices. When attacking and defending 2 D6 dice is used per player. Attacking or defending with a hero used a D12.

How to Win

A game consist of maximum 20 rounds and there are 3 ways to win:


     Be the first player to finish your mission.
     Be the last player standing.
     Control the most amount of tiles after 20 rounds have been played.


PLAYING A TURN

A player can choose to do 2 actions out of 3 available per round. The actions are:

Spending - Buy units or buildings, repair buildings, resurrect hero
Engaging - Move on the map, attack other players or monsters
Gambling - Try to earn additional money by answering a question

Actions are consecutive. This means that, for example, in a turn the player chooses to buy units and then attack another player, they can’t buy more soldiers after they have moved on from the spending action.

—description from the designer

boardgame: FlickFleet: A Box of Pirate Flicks
description:
The pirates in FlickFleet are a low-tech human faction that can augment either of the original human factions, or be played as a faction on their own. Prior to the Box of Pirate Flicks the pirates faction was way smaller than the other two human factions, but this expansion will make them a full faction with loads more fleet building options. Box of Pirate Flicks content expands the Pirates faction from Box of Flicks 2 allowing more exciting battles, but requires the FlickFleet base game to play.

The Box of Pirate Flicks is a new expansion consisting of loads of pirate ships for FlickFleet each of which contains one or two pirate ships or the wreckage that pirates mine for repairs. A Box of Pirate Flicks adds second copies of the original pirate fleet from Box of Flicks 2 (with new names, so you can easily use both in a battle), plus another Behemoth, which was an add-on to that campaign. In addition, there are loads of new ships too.

With no access to naval dockyards, pirate ships rarely have shields, choosing instead to reinforce their ships' hulls for extra defence. They also don't have engineering actions that can always repair a ship, instead they must use Salvage Drones to raid asteroids and the wreckage left behind by destroyed capital ships for the materials they need.

—description from the publisher

A 20 page booklet of scenarios and suggested fleets for FlickFleet and its expansions.

Contains 12 scenarios:
2 require FlickFleet only
2 require FlickFleet and Expansion 1
2 require FlickFleet and Box of Flicks 1
2 require FlickFleet and Box of Flicks 2
1 requires FlickFleet, Expansion 1 and Box of Flicks 1
1 requires FlickFleet, Box of Flicks 1 and Box of Flicks 2
1 requires Xeno Wars
1 requires FlickFleet and Xeno Wars

Also includes 43 suggested fleets between 50 and 200 points using a mixture of ships from the first five boxes.

A board game about saving your dinosaur from a volcanic eruption! Place tiles cleverly to guide lava down the volcano, and use special tiles to mess with your opponents or save yourself from demise, all while trying to be the first to dino escape!

boardgame: La Famiglia: Expansion for 2 and 3 Players
description:
La Famiglia - 2 and 3 Player expansion.

In the two-player version, players engage in a strategic duel with inspiration from the mechanics of 7 Wonders Duel that highlights the depth and sophistication of the original game.

The three-player version introduces a dynamic 1-vs-2 challenge, where one player faces off against a team of two. This setup adds even more flexibility and tactical depth.

_________________________________________

La Famiglia - Extension pour 2 et 3 joueurs de plus, certaines mécaniques inspirées de 7 Wonders Duel seront intégrées

Dans la version pour deux joueurs, tu te plongerás dans un duel stratégique qui mettra vraiment en valeur la profondeur et la sophistication du jeu original. C'est une occasion parfaite de plonger au cœur de l'action avec un stratège partenaire.

Pour une nouvelle approche, la version pour trois joueurs introduit un défi dynamique 1 contre 2, où un joueur affronte une équipe de deux. Ce réglage ajoute encore plus de flexibilité et de profondeur tactique, faisant de chaque soirée de jeu une expérience unique et excitante.

—description from the designer

Dice throwing game where score is kept on the board with pegs that are potentially in the way of the dice.

Players take turns tossing dice from their side of the board and trying to land in specific zones on the other side.  Dice must be tossed from behind the "Dead Zone".

Zonez:
Center Zone - 1 Point
Penalty Zone - -1 Point
Scoring Zone - # on die, or double if hanging off the edge of board.
Scoring Zone Hole - 10 Points
Scoring Zone Corner - Automatically Win

In Sky Team: Turbulence, terrible weather conditions and technical incidents will be the order of the day for your landings.

Take your seat in the cockpit and test your skills in 20 scenarios that are even more demanding! Turbulence introduces new rules and new modules, such as Turbulence, Low Visibility, and Alarms, that will add depth and diversity to your Sky Team base game.
Now boarding for such wonderful destinations as Beijing, Madrid, Warsaw, Sydney, Cape Town, Kyiv, and Saint Martin.

Hang on to your seats. It is going to be a bumpy ride!

Turbulence is an expansion for the co-operative two-player game Sky Team

—description from the publisher

A worker placement and management game themed around the production of movies.

You play as a film producer. Ambitious, you dream of joining the pantheon of the cinematographic world thanks to the scripts that you will have carefully selected and the actors chosen with care for each of them.


     Hire Assistants, actors and actresses.
     Buy scripts and sets.
     Take loans at the bank to invest in your movies.
     Produce and send movies to the box office.
     Influence the box office with marketing.
     Earn money and prestige during the awards ceremonies.


Places are limited and the competition is merciless. Who will be able to stand out from the crowd at the awards ceremonies, by stepping into the spotlight, and forever mark the history of the 7th Art?

—description from the publisher

In Alien Attack, players represent military forces that need to defend the United States of America from a hostile and aggressive alien invasion.

With their attacks in waves, it will be difficult to know which region they will invade next. The problem is that there are so many of them, it is impossible to protect all of them.

However, not all is lost... world military intelligence managed to develop strategies to survive and minimize humanity's losses.

Players will need to defend the attacked regions using their defense units, seeking to protect cities, national parks, livestock farms, in addition to destroying alien bases that are installed in places that have not been defended.

During their turn, players will need to defend one of the attacked regions, collecting national park markers, scoring in cities and herds, in addition to seeking to fulfill public and private objectives that will be scored at the end of the game.

A game of exploration, resource management, confrontation, and alliances in a science fiction universe.

Explore and dive into the icy universe of Kryos, with its modular board where a ruthless war rages for control of the precious eggs of the fearsome Borgoroth, a vital source of energy in a depleted planetary system.

Play as one of four rival factions: the Krun, greedy natives of Kryos, the reptilian Soloch with advanced technology; the Voltron, intelligent mechanical armors that have escaped the control of their Soloch creators; and the indigenous Krothor, formidable bloodthirsty warriors.

You can also form alliances during the game in order to benefit from the allied faction's ability and passageways, but beware: if you betray an ally, things might not go so well for you. This alliance module can also be played right from the start, with teams of 2vs2 being formed during setup.

Take part in epic battles, forge strategic alliances, and see who will triumph in this battle for control of Kryos.

—description from the publisher

In It's Obvious, the host reveals one of the two hundred questions included in the box, then players secretly choose the "correct" side. Man fish or fish man? Cilantro is soapy or cilantro is delicious?

Once all players have voted, their vote cards are shuffled, then flipped face up to reveal which answer received more votes. The side with the most cards is the obvious choice, while the other is the oblivious choice. Now players debate!

If you're on the oblivious team, it's time to lie! If you're on the obvious team, it's time to find those oblivious players. Toss out outlandish accusations and engage in heated debate about the topic before eliminating a player. If the oblivious player(s) slide by without being caught, they win! If the obvious players can catch all of the oblivious players, then they win!

Will you choose the obvious choice?

boardgame: Adana the Earth Dragon: A game of Friendship and Flowers
description:
A family card game based on the picture book Adana the Earth Dragon.

Once upon a time, there was a small brown, earth dragon called Adana. One day a giant wandered into the Land of the Dragons and started stomping on the flowers. Can you help Adana and her friends defeat the Scary Giant and save the flowers?
Players ages 4 and up build their flower bed and invite friendly dragons to guard their precious blooms to protect them from the Scary Giant. They might even attract the Earth Dragon, Adana, who can protect any flower or scare off the Giant. Because even a small brown dragon can do big things!

Beautiful hand-painted art sets off this gentle game of flowers and friendship.

A tabletop simulation of golf.

The board is made of 4 large segments (22.5 x 23.5 cm) and 8 small triangular segments. By combining these segments in a variety of ways, you can make a huge number of golf course configurations.

On its path, the ball may encounter different types of obstacles, and each type affects the ball differently. By carefully choosing the stroke directions, the ball can bypass any obstacle, and the ball can even fly over obstacles, if it gets hit hard enough.

Carom Golf can be played in the classical way: each player plays by themselves until they put the ball into the hole, and writes the score down. However, there is also a Carom mode, that is much more dynamic and interesting, because all players play one stroke at a time on the same hole. In this mode, it is possible to hit the other player's ball with your own, change its position, and even knock it out of court. In this mode, there are no fair no foul plays, there are just different ways to victory.

—description from the publisher

New, simplified version of the CMG game, as it doesn't contain vertical obstacles nor some other elements.

This variant has everything you need for hours of good fun, and it also has all the elements you need to play in Carom mode (multiple players playing at once on the same course).

Contents:

Board with 6 courses, 6 balls, 6 alternative balls, one die, a set of cards (6), a set of flat obstacles (11), a set of movement slowing obstacles (8), a scorepad, slider, manuals (two booklets: text + diagrams).

—description from the publisher

A dice rolling, set making game based on the events of a murder trial. In each turn, players roll two sets of dice to make matching sets and poll jurors in an attempt to have the highest verdict score at the end of the game.

The Grand Prix board game is a family board game based on the Spanish television show Grand Prix. Official product with 6 famous games in the program. The objective is to win the greatest number of tests and obtain a higher score than the opposing team. Which team will win the game?

boardgame: War of the Ring: The Card Game – Fire and Swords: Promo Cards
description:
Set of two promotional cards that were included with pre-orders for the expansion.

Old Man Willow (Monstrous)
When Played, the Hobbit player must forsake 1 card.

Goldberry (Wizard)
When played, you may take Tom Bombadil from your draw deck into hand.
If in reserve, you may use your action and cycle this card so the Free Peoples player of your choice may draw 1 card.

Scattered Nexus is a battle-style card game based on a growing list of indie comics. Players will build their deck by shuffling together 3 10-card stacks. when players take damage they lose the top card of their deck, also players draw back up to a hand of 5 at the end of their turn. Meaning players have to control their own tempo to not burn themselves out. This new edition fixes some confusion on card text, adds 6 new comic factions to choose from, and adds a new way to play cards called "boosting".

Fluffy Dragons is a very quick and easy, betting & bluffing game, where you'll use the force of the elements to fight your Fluffiest enemies to collect the most gems!

In each round, players will take turns playing an elemental card. Then, they'll place their dragon meeples on the board to claim the element they used, but remember, they could be lying.

There are 5 different elements you could use, each one beating 2 and getting beaten by the other 2.

Before anyone reveals the elemental attacks they used, you can wager gems against other players you think are lying. If you’re right, steal their gems! However, if they were telling the truth, say goodbye to your gems, for now.

Then, players will reveal the elemental attacks they used and move their dragon meeples on the elemental board to match that element.

Players will steal a gem from any dragons who are standing one or two elements in front of them in a clockwise order on the elemental board.

Fluffy Dragons is won by having the most amount of points. Each of your own color gems is worth 1 point at the end of the game, any other color gem is worth 2 points each. You can also get extra points at the end of the game by getting rainbows, a full-color set of gems, and acquiring lost treasures.

—description from the designer

Sourcebound is a TCG set in Telessaire, a unique fantasy world full of wonder. Telessaire provides players with a rich story evolving over multiple chapters, keeping gameplay fresh and thematic for years to come. Players take the role of the Monad, otherworldly beings of immense power who are vying for control of Telessaire, utilizing the world's local populous to further their own ends.

Build a custom deck of cards from one of six main factions, or mix and match them to create new and exciting synergies and combinations.

Utilize the "Source" system, a unique take on modern mana that provides stable, reliable gameplay, along with deep tactical decisions, without artificially limiting deck building and creativity.

Enter the world of Telessaire as Sourcebound sets off to leave its mark, and change the world of TCGs forever.

—description from the publisher

Hermitcraft TCG is a trading card game created by VintageBeef. Through the use of talented fan artists and the company Creo Cards, it has been brought to the real world for viewers to enjoy and play themselves.

Currently on Series 2, it's a easy to learn game where you have three primary types of cards. Hermits, Items and Effects.

Hermit's are your "characters". You can have 5 on the board at a time, and if you don't have any, or lose 3 of them, the game is over. They have special moves that can cause other hermit damage or do unique things to impact the gameplay.

Items are the cards used to power up a hermit's attack.

Effects are cards that have moves to effect game play such as causing an opponents hermit to 'burn', shield your hermit from damage, or swap out a hermit after an attack.

The successful mystery series Black Stories is also available in a "scary-free" version - for families with children from 8 years old. Instead of dark and scary "Black Stories Junior" tend to be funny and colorful.

In Black Stories Junior: Monster Alarm, clever combinations and out-of-the-box thinking are required. Players will encounter 50 monstrously tricky riddles featuring giant octopuses, werewolves, vampires, Bigfoot, Nessie, Godzilla, and more.

Black Stories are tricky, morbid, sinister riddle stories. The players try to reconstruct the particulars of each incident, piece by piece, by asking yes-or-no-questions.

This special anniversary edition contains the 100 best cases from 20 years of black stories. From urban legends to pitch-black puzzle classics: Goosebumps are guaranteed.

Black Stories are tricky, morbid, sinister riddle stories. The players try to reconstruct the particulars of each incident, piece by piece, by asking yes-or-no-questions.

The Serienkiller edition contains 50 real True Crime cases that happened exactly as described. Not for the faint-hearted!

That is the crowning! is a card game centered around common phrases. Instead of directly translating German idioms into English, players create humorous 'Denglish' sentences—where 'D' stands for Deutsch (German). Each player draws a card and attempts to first translate the Denglish sentence into German, and then find the correct English equivalent. Successfully doing both allows them to keep the card and earn points. The player with the most points at the end wins the game.

Oh No! is a card game that turns things upside down, providing a real challenge for those who find Mau-Mau too simple. In this game, players always pay attention to the second-to-last card! Additionally, entertaining special actions like direction changes, skipping turns, and jokers add plenty of variety.

The players take turns playing cards following the familiar Mau-Mau rules (matching color or type of fruit). The twist: Instead of the top card, you have pay attention to the card underneath it, so everyone must try to remember what is covered up

What a bustling scene in the flower meadow! The colorful beetles scurry around, constantly rearranging themselves. Your task cards reveal which beetles should be next to each other and which should be apart. Logical thinking is essential in Käferparade as there's only one correct position for each beetle. Can you figure it out?

—description from the publisher

Introducing SH*THEAD ZOMBIES, the awesome remake of the beloved card game “Shithead/Palace”. With its awe-inspiring designs and captivating artwork, this reimagined version breathes new life into the classic game.

And that's not all – this game takes the gameplay to new heights with the addition of the magic cards “Wild” and “Pick-Em-Up”, which introduce a whole new level of strategy and excitement. These magic cards inject unexpected twists and turns into the game, keeping players on their toes at all times.
The rules have also undergone significant improvements. SH*THEAD ZOMBIES offers a refined and well-balanced set, making every session more engaging and challenging than ever before. It's the perfect combination of nostalgia and innovation, providing a fresh experience for both long-time fans and new players alike.

With the capability to accommodate up to 7 players and open to any age group, be sure that this game is for everyone. Its simplicity allows you the opportunity to develop your gameplay and wipe the floor with your competition. Make and break allegiances at the drop of a hat so you can get rid of all of your cards and avoid becoming the Sh*thead!

So get ready to enjoy the ultimate card game experience with SH*THEAD ZOMBIES and rejoice in hours of thrill, strategy, and a whole lot of fun!

—description from the publisher

A folder lays on your desk where all the materials of a terrible murder are chaotically dumped ... Her name was Anna, and on September 1, 1992, a disaster happened.

This game is a realistic tabletop detective quest. The folder contains archival materials, various records and important reports... and, of course, a bunch of photos - all the important evidence to find the answer to the mystery and punish the perpetrators.

How to play

The starting envelope contains introductory information.

Do not touch the next envelope until you have completed the tasks in this one. Gradually, more and more evidence will appear, and new information will be available for you to rely on.

You need to solve three riddles and determine who the criminal is. And you can verify the correctness of your thesis by scanning a special qr-code.

A folder lays on your desk where all the materials of a terrible suicide are chaotically dumped ... It happened in a residential area of Lviv. A man fell from a nine-story building and died. Several teenagers witnessed this terrible event. Who was this man? And what made him commit suicide? Could it be that it was not suicide at all?

This game is a realistic tabletop detective quest. The folder contains archival materials, various records and important reports... and, of course, a bunch of photos - all the important evidence to find the answer to the mystery and punish the perpetrators.

How to play

The starting envelope contains introductory information.

Do not touch the next envelope until you have completed the tasks in this one. Gradually, more and more evidence will appear to you, and new information will be available for you to rely on.

You need to solve three riddles and determine who the criminal is. And you can verify the correctness of your thesis by scanning a special qr-code.

The stories seem too fantastic to be true; giant sea monsters, sirens leading sailors astray, dragons and princesses, holy relics needing to be found, and all are pressing matters. The quests and adventures pile up as a large smorgasbord in front of you, how could you decide which one is best for your kingdom? Maybe you need to summon your wizard advisor and his lovely assistant?

This is an expansion for Kingdom Legacy: Feudal Kingdom.

It offers 3 rounds of play, and 29 new cards to explore by embarking on adventures. It will take at least 10 rounds to explore all of them, so the contents of this expansion will overlap with future expansions played, so it is recommended to play this as one of your first 5 expansions.

To play, you need to perform a purge 10 and purge 1 permanent card.

Your kingdom is a hub of trade, attracting merchants from near and far with their intriguing and often bizarre goods. You can’t wait to add these rare treasures to your collection – and you’re more than happy to trade your own (slightly less exotic) items for them. Just make sure you have enough to offer, or you might have to break out the old chicken-dancing routine to seal the deal. It’s worked before.

This is an expansion for Kingdom Legacy.

It offers 4 rounds of play, and 26 new cards, most of which you need to purchase from the merchants. But beware, each merchant is only available for 1 round, and once they leave, their offers are lost forever. Most of the 4 rounds of this expansion will be spent gathering the resources needed to purchase cards from the merchants. To actually use and develop those cards, you need to play further expansions, such as the built-in expansions of the base game.

To play, you need to perform a purge 7 and purge 2 permanent cards.

A team of four, brave Storm Chasers work together to survive, and study, a wild, unpredictable tornado. Choose your Chaser and starting equipment, then face off against this dangerous product of Mother Nature. You'll have to balance the risks of getting close enough to collect data points with the dangers that come with being too close to the Twister itself, such as being hit with debris or getting sucked into the vortex. You have six rounds to collect the information you need before the storm dissipates, so act quickly.

At the start of a round, the players will pick one chaser to activate. A chaser gets two actions points to do any of the following: move up to their move value in inches, Focus (grants a future re-roll), use a piece of equipment, or Take Cover (which reduces damage taken by 2 until the start of their next turn). After each chaser, the tornado activates. The current player rolls 3D10 and resolves them in the following order: red die, white die, blue die. The tornado will move a number of inches equal to the die result and in the direction the die was facing. When the tornado contacts a piece of terrain, that terrain will be destroyed and debris will be slung in a random direction, which could injure any chasers in the way. Finally, after the tornado finishes its activation, any chasers within two inches have the possibility of being sucked into its vortex and thrown across the map.

After all chasers have gone and the tornado makes its final move, Data Points are collected: 2 points for each chaser within 0-6 inches of the twister, and 1 point for being 6-10 inches. Players have 6 turns to earn a randomly decided number of Data Points before the tornado dissipates and the game is over.

Clever use of equipment as well as successful skill checks at the end of each chaser's turn will allow for some amount of control over the twister's movement.

Do you have what it take to survive The Chase?

—description from the designer

In the mid-1930’s, Thai military dictator Plaek Phibunsongkhram commissioned a modest naval building program. The Royal Thai Navy quickly received modern coast-defense ships, submarines and torpedo boats from Japanese and Italian shipyards. In 1940, Thailand challenged Vichy France over the provinces lost to French Indo-China in 1893, leading to a Thai invasion of Cambodia and the January 1941 naval battle of Koh-Chang.

Royal Thai Navy is a Second World War at Sea Campaign Study, focused on this conflict and other potential operations by Thailand’s small but modern navy during World War II. All of the pieces needed are included in our Java Sea game; this book gives you scenarios so you can play with them.

You get 11 new scenarios, all of them focused on Thailand’s fleet, which include two coast defense ships, two armored gunboats and a small flotilla of torpedo boats. Thailand also ordered a pair of cruisers in Italy; these were never delivered but they’re in the game and Royal Thai Navy includes scenarios for them, too.

You’ll need Java Sea (and only Java Sea) to play the scenarios. The Thais battle the French, the Japanese and the British. The King of Siam is depending on you to lead his fleet to victory against the imperialists.

—description from the publihser
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
This expansion takes place in a near future. You will be able to build one Arcology tile if you respect the lay out !
Then, you need to provide Hydrogen to take off and win the game!

—description from the designer

Affenzirkus
The Players take turns to roll and move. If a player lands on a yellow space, he may roll again.
If the roll ends on a blue space, the figure continues to move along the blue track until the main track is reached again.
Red spaces lead to a dead end. Anyone who lands on such a space jumps to the last space of this dead end. From there, they move back towards the main track according to the number rolled. The first to reach space E wins the game.

Nanu, Äffchen? (game board backside)
Each player receives 3 meeples of one color, which they place outside the playing field. A dice roll determines who starts.
The game begins at the starting space(S). Everyone takes turns to roll the dice. With even numbers, you move your 3 pieces towards the goal (Z), with odd numbers you go back.
If you move onto a space that is already occupied, your own or another player's piece standing there is pushed forwards or backwards by the same number of points.
The first player to get all of their pieces to space Z wins the game

The points on the game board form the playing field for the figures. You play from the outside, in the direction indicated by the arrow, towards the center, then into the first loop on the left, then back to the center, the same applies for the second and third, then it goes outwards to the goal. If a player lands on the same space as the previous player, the player who has been caught up must start again. If a player lands on the center space, he also starts again.

The numbered circles on the playing field are for the jumpers. You put the small jumper on the piece of cloth and press it with the big one so that it jumps and possibly reaches the center. You write down the number of each circle you jumped into. The player jumps 3 times, resulting in a number, e.g. 401. With each hop, the player chooses where he wants the number written, whether at the front, back or in the middle. If a player has hopped well and speculated well, he has won. If a player does not land on the game board with the hop, it is a zero.

A storm has been building in the magical realm of Lorcana. A powerful Ursula glimmer, unleashed from the Great Illuminary, is at last ready to put plans into motion. How far will the sea witch go to gain power over all Lorcana?

Become an Illumineer and wield magic ink to summon glimmers of Disney characters. Together with your team, you'll challenge opposing Illumineers as you race to collect the scattered lore.

—description from the publisher

Roll and (re)write the 2024 UK general election in the tense electoral strategy game for British politicos. A truly competitive roll & write – NOT your usual multiplayer solitaire!

Pick from seven political parties contesting the 2024 general election with the aim of winning a majority of the battleground constituencies to enter government. The 'main' parties (Labour and Conservatives) aim to win a majority, while the five 'minor' parties (Liberal Democrats, Scottish National Party, Plaid Cymru, Greens, and Reform UK) are intensely thematic asymmetric factions with their own win condition.

Each turn the leading player rolls two dice. All players write these numbers on their party sheets, completing and promoting policies, hammering home slogans, and securing donations. Completed sections of the party sheet allow players to claim voters in battleground constituencies, until one party wins a plurality of voters in each constituency. Winning a majority of constituencies means you enter government and win the game. Available in 2024 as a print at home game – approx. 1 A4 sheet per player, plus 1 map sheet and rules. Grab a pair of dice and pencils, and the game is on!

—description from the publisher

Players are guild masters of rival adventuring guilds who must wisely divide their crews of lizard-riding gophers and spell-casting badgers into questing parties to gain gold, power, and new recruits to strengthen their ranks.

Each round, four quests will be available to compete over, each with their own unique criteria and rewards.

During deployment, players have access to their entire crew, but must be careful not to overcommit. After a hard day of questing, any crew dispatched will spend the next round kicking back in the tavern, so you’ll need to plan ahead.

Whichever guild master has gained the most power over the course of 8 rounds wins!

—description from the publisher

Lively quiz about years

Sooner or Later is a lively quiz about years, where you don't have to know the right answer to win. Players take turns trying to guess the year of an event, and the other players can score points by guessing if the event happened BEFORE or AFTER the year guessed.

—description from the publisher

ARCTICA is a competitive, card-driven, engine-building Eurogame based on the development of Arctic ecosystems, especially fauna. The game features more than 160 animals, and you want to build a viable ecosystem.

In the end, the player with the highest biodiversity score wins.

boardgame: Prince Glitterhorn the Sparkly Unicorn Only Eats Tacos
description:
Congratulations! You’ve just been hired as the Operations Manager for UnicEats, the food delivery service specialising in feeding unicorn royalty. Prince Glitterhorn the Sparkly Unicorn is your most important customer. However, he can be a bit fussy. You see, he only ever wants to eat tacos.

Over the course of five rounds, each representing an hour of the Prince's extended lunch, you need to deliver as many tacos as possible to keep him satisfied. Balancing his diet is crucial because if he eats only tacos, he will get sick, and you definitely want to avoid witnessing a royal unicorn's tantrum or illness.

To deliver food to Prince Glitterhorn, you must navigate your delivery riders through the Magical Forest, where many strange occurrences can disrupt your mission. You start with a set number of riders and food cubes—eight representing tacos and four representing other foods like salads or sandwiches. The forest path is laid out using shuffled cards, and you must strategically send your riders to ensure they reach the Prince with the right food mix. The game is a single-player challenge designed to test your management and strategic planning skills, with the goal of scoring points based on how well you feed Prince Glitterhorn while maintaining a balanced diet.

An abstract strategic solo game, dressed in a very particular theme and with an ethical message, the true story of the Zammit family! The intent was to develop a game mechanic that made the sense of oppression caused by the aggressive overbuilding of the territory feel rsthand.

The result is a tight and pressing game, especially in the final, in which you will fight, as the world around you disintegrates, for the survival of your tokens until the last step.

The player have to bring the 4 player's pawn to the 4 corners to pick up the 4 tokens item, (when reaching the square in the corner the token item will be physically positioned above the player's pawn and from that moment they will move together), then will have to able to bring the 4 items back to the Zammit house in the centre, before the entire green landscape succumbs to aggressive overbuilding and high-density construction.

The game contains 80 cards with questions relating to the 20th century. The questions were divided into decades. 16 questions describe each subsequent decade. To win, you must answer a question from each decade correctly. The results are recorded by moving pieces around the board.

Step into the boundless expanse of the cosmos, where a secretive gem, XO Planet, awaits discovery. 
Can you navigate your way to its enigmatic shores? Gather the elusive doblorings to fuel your journey, but watch out for the treacherous pitfalls of empty tanks!
Are you prepared to embark on this exhilarating adventure?

Objective
In competitive mode, your goal is to be the first player to reach XO planet.
In solo and coop modes, you have a defined numbers of turns to complete your mission.

Game overview
Roll the dice, collect resources, and make the smartest moves to reach XO

Setup
Place the XO planet in the centre of the playing area and scatter the remaining planets around. Add the doblorings to the dobloplanets, roll 3 dice to place on 3 planets at the outskirt of the perimeter, and you are ready to go.

On Your Turn
In XO planet, all rockets are "common" and can be used by any player. Chose your moves wisely, as your opponent can take advantage of it.
Roll the 2 remaining dice, then compare these dice with the ones already in the playing area. 
3 Identical numbers will land a rocket, allowing you to swap planets, but grounding the rocket until refuelling;
Similar dice will give you access to doblorings, which will give you precious advantages during the game (more reach, modifying numbers, refuelling a landed rocket, re-roll the dice);
You can also sum dice up to move to the corresponding planet, as long as it is in reach.

The rocket shaped dice are used in various ways during the game: they give you values, are used to measure reach and can be landed vertically.

End of Game
The game ends as soon as a player reaches the XO planet.

—description from the designer

It’s challenging enough to make a regular cat go somewhere… but what if the cat is a witch?
In Cat Sabbath, a trick-taking card game, players compete to make Supreme Witches come to their witchy conventions. Everyone plays one trick for each witch. Win a trick, and a Supreme Witch joins you!
Remember, if you have too many Supreme Witches, your celebration will turn into chaos. So, just like cats, players must find PURR-fect balance!

Cat Sabbath is a three-round trick-taking card game, where players simultaneously play four tricks to invite Supreme Witches to their festivity and earn the most Victory Points.
In each round, players choose 4 Supreme Witches and play cards below every witch that doesn’t have their card yet. All cards in a Supreme Witch’s row must follow the suit of the first card played there. When everyone plays a card in each Supreme Witch’s row, the round ends. Win a trick—take all witch cards from the row, plus the Supreme Witch as a multiplier. Too many Supreme Witches on hand will lead to chaos, so you must find the PURR-fect cat balance to win the game!

—description from the publisher

Stone Shedding Game is a dynamic card game with a ladder-climbing mechanic where players make sets of stones for the shared garden. The goal is to get rid of all the cards on your hand and gain the most Victory Points for your stone set. Players can't shuffle their hand and must play sets of cards with consecutive or identical numbers to beat the previous one. If you can’t beat the last opponent’s combination, you can pass and exchange some of the cards on your hand.
Create the ultimate winning combination and profit from other players’ actions in this dynamic card battle!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tale of Ale: Viking Saga Begins
description:
In the sagas of old, the glory went to the warriors and kings. But what about the crafters and traders, the true backbone of Viking conquests? Without them, with empty hands there would be no victory! In the warmth of the mead hall, it’s their own hands that raise the ale, their voices rich with tales of skill and trade that lead to great raids!

In Tale of Ale: Viking Saga Begins, you step into the boots of a Viking Harbourmaster, the unsung hero who knows how to acquire enough shields, axes and ale for a whole Drakkar Longboat Crew to power the legendary raids!

The Challenge: The Jarl of Skjoldhavn has blown the horn for a grand raid. Five fierce clans arrived at the harbor with their Drakkars but their fleets are unprepared! As a resourceful supplier, you must gather and trade ale, salted meat, shields, and battle axes to equip the Drakkars. Your savvy trading ensures the success of the raid.

Harbour Hustle: Navigate the bustling Skjoldhavn Harbour, where Drakkars from five clans await your supplies, each having unique needs.

Resource Gathering: Collect and trade to exchange your resources into those that you need the most. Strategize as you supply the Vikings with their raid essentials.

Village Voyage: Navigate Skjoldhavn’s paths by the dice’s decree, seeking runes and symbols to bolster your clan’s honor. A special field allows a bold move—using all three dice to carve your actions into the next round.

—description from the publisher

Others scavenging at the Construction have stumbled upon a strange place. They say the entrance was conjured purely by 
accident, and the colors that poured forth from it indescribably brilliant. The mid-air rent rippled open and folded back on itself in a constant fluid motion. The corridors beyond were merely glimpsed, appearing as pathways of raw energy. Soon after, fast-moving creatures within began bounding toward the portal,and the scavengers fled for their lives. The elders speculate that if that great energy can be collected and cast unto itself, it may be possible to harness its power, or set it on a path of self-destruction. They hold the knowledge for opening the portal, and they’ve passed that knowledge on to you. “Go!  And see what can be learned of this opportunity!”

The Ever Chamber is a special mini-ecology, introducing several new mechanics and challenges to the game. Strange monster in-habitants pursue Heroes across the map. Powerful items may be forged from collected essences. Even the very core of the chamber itself may be destroyed, offering a new path to victory, if you can escape in time.

Dare you to brave these shifting corridors of energy?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Forgotten Depths: The Vault of Yariq
description:
Yariq’s tale is a dark one, nearly lost to time. The elders guard the story closely, but they have shared it with you. Overthrown by his own people, Yariq was entombed alongside his followers within a great maze-like structure, designed to confuse and trap the restless dead. His terrible chain was also sealed there; a powerful implement capable of manipulating the force of life itself. It is this chain the elders seek.

Do you dare enter Yariq’s maze-tomb to take his weapon for yourself?

The Vault of Yariq expansion provides a special area to explore, a unique and epic encounter, and a wondrous new item that can be equipped by a single Hero, or all of them together. Players attempt to find their way to Yariq’s Audience Hall, while disturbing as few of his followers as possible. Locate his hall and  face him, but beware, as those you have disturbed will fight by his side!

—description from the publisher

The exhausted Heroes gaze glassy-eyed into the fire as it crackles and dances. For the time being at least, they’ve found 
peace. As they relax, their minds turn to matters beyond their immediate survival and they begin to quietly converse; seeking 
comfort and renewed resolve.

The Camp Dialog expansion provides a series of branching conversations Heroes experience when they Rest at a Campfire. Also included are custom wooden health tokens for the base 
game’s Final Entity.

—description from the publisher

Seven new and original Varia classes battle in this exciting, accessible, stand-alone dueling game.

Varia is a game of tactical card play, powerful fantasy-themed classes, and unique player-controlled RNG.

A fast-paced 2+ player combat game, Varia has you select from a variety of classes that play and feel completely distinct from one another. Attack your opponent or defend against their blows with unique action cards that represent your chosen class. This box contains seven such classes - the Deathblood Sorcerer, Phoenix Spellsword, Moonlit Cleric, Stoneroot Druid, Psychic Fencer, 4th Blade, and Boreal Ranger.

Deathblood Sorcerer - Scythe in hand, you'll spend health points to fuel your spells, then cast death magic that grows in power the closer to dying you are.

Phoenix Spellsword - Sword in hand, you'll fuse phoenix-themed fire magic with physical attacks and blocks, creating potent combinations that will burn your opponents to ashes.

Moonlit Cleric - Mace in hand, you'll use divine magic that changes depending on the god you choose to worship. Depending on the choice made, you'll be a defensive cleric of healing magic or an offensive cleric of death magic.

Stoneroot Druid - Sword in hand, you'll cast powerful nature spells that lock your opponent down in roots, before charging in with your sword to finish them off.

Psychic Fencer - Foil in hand, you'll attack with precision moves that emulate fencing, while supplementing your attacks with mental assaults and psychic trickery.

4th Blade - Bass Guitar in hand, you'll loop lightning actions in and out of your discard pile in a never-ending performance that will leave your opponents shocked and in awe.

Boreal Ranger - Cannon in hand, you'll skate in and out of melee range, shooting your opponent with harpoons from a distance before launching yourself forward for a final rending blow.

This is a stand-alone release supporting 2 players. Additional classes are required to play with more than 2 players.

—description from the publisher

WayWord Connections

Blends Waypoint Mapping with Words!

WayWord Connections is a cooperative word game that combines spelling with strategic path building. Players work together to connect words of their choice with waypoints.

Cover the waypoints with matching letters, then connect them together by forming words, all before time runs out.

Game Components:

     12 magnetic map sections
     Magnetic underlays to connect the sections
     136 letter tiles including 17 bigram tiles.


Not included: a timer. Use your cell phone to set a time limit.

Goal:
Cover all the waypoints and connect them with words such that no way point is isolated. Do this before time runs out!

Setup:

     Grab two to six map sections.
     Form a map by arranging the sections in any order.
     Place all the tiles upside down between the players.
     Each player takes five tiles placing them face up.
     Set your timer to 10 minutes per section divided by the number of players, so if you have six sections and four players, set the timer to 10 x 4 / 6 = 6.6 minutes.


Simultaneous Game Play:
You are all playing against the clock, at the same time.


     All players place tiles in one of two ways:
         On top of a waypoint with its matching letter.
         Form a complete word attached to an existing letter.
    
     Join two words across a common letter (crossword).
     Replace the tiles in your hand to a maximum of five tiles.
     Play until all waypoints are covered and all words are interconnected.


Notes:
You cannot place a letter over water.
You may place a word anywhere but to complete the game, the word cannot be orphaned (unconnected).
With several players, the orientation of words does not matter. However, if you would like consistency, play all letters and words in the same left to right, top to bottom directions.
If a player cannot make a word, wait until another player has made a word. However, if all players cannot make a word, replace all your tiles. This situation is more likely only to occur when playing solo.
A letter must fit within a grid square.

Ending the Game:
With all the waypoints covered and all words connected, continue to play out remaining letters from all hands. If a player cannot make a word, draw another letter. 
You cannot win if any letters remain. 
Work together to beat the clock!

—description from the publisher

Сластёна ("Sweet tooth") is a fast fun dice game similar to Lotto! In this game you have to collect combinations of numbers and colors.

Roll dice, add up the results of the same color, and choose where to write them down. The catch is that there are few places to write numbers, and the unselected dices will go to the opponents. Assess risks and collect the best combination of numbers to win the game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Heroscape: Cornelius Breech, the Derelict Prince Promo
description:
Cornelius has never been a very nice man. Back on his home world of Feylund, he made his living as the leader of a brutal band of highwaymen. Notorious and feared, he let his cleaver do the negotiating while his hooves kept him one step ahead of any would-be heroes. But no-one outruns their comeuppance forever. The last Cornelius remembers of Feylund is a whisper. The voice of a vigilante and the cold touch of her blade, come to take his head at last. A whisper. A blade. Then the red of his blood mingling with the red of a volcano, far, far away from Feylund. Cornelius doesn’t care much about what happened, or how. He cares about finding out who’s in charge around here, so he can kill them. Once that’s done he can go back to living the life he loves: at-large and in-charge.

Card Text:
FIRST ASSAULT 2: When Cornelius Breech, the Derelict Prince attacks, if the defending figure was not adjacent to him at the start of this turn, Cornelius adds 2 to his Attack Value during this attack.

MUTINY: After a Unique Captain you control receives a wound or is destroyed, any or all Pirate Squads you control that chose that Captain for The Captain's Way Special Power may choose Cornelius to be their chosen Captain for the rest of the game. If any Squads do, remove all Wound Markers from Cornelius Breech.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: Murder and the Mob
