It's the 1920's and there's been a murder! Stan "The Snake" Salami has been found murdered in the meat hanger at Bianco's Restaurant, Little Italy, New York.

You must all play your roles in bringing the murderer to justice! Set in the 1920's this hilarious murder mystery game will provide you with a night you won't forget!

The host and up to 12 friends participate in a thematic murder mystery where everyone plays a role and everyone is a suspect. The game contains two different outcomes.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: Murder in Hollywood
description:
It's the 1950's and there's been a murder! Orson Hells has been found murdered int he props hanger at Studio 5 at the Fairbanks Film Company in Hollywood!

You must all play your roles in bringing the murderer to justice! Set in the 1950's this hilarious murder mystery game will provide you with a night you won't forget!

The host and up to 12 friends participate in a thematic murder mystery where everyone plays a role and everyone is a suspect. The game contains two different outcomes.

This 2 - 4 player family game plays similarly to normal bingo, but is heavily themed to cat breeds.

Players choose their boards and select the bingo game based on 37 different cat breeds. Cat pictures are shown by the caller and if the image shown matches a spot on a player's card, the player will put a chip down. The first player to achieve a Cat Bingo yells, "MEOW!"

This 2 - 4 player game allows players to choose their game amongst bowling, curling, and shuffle board, land on a color, and answer the topic trivia question!

The game features seven trivia topics: films, general, books, sports, music, science, and history.

Compete for points with a deck full of dares, trivia, challenges, questions, and more.

Play until you reach your destination or until you notice any signs of road trip insanity (like anyone staring into the abyss and thinking about every regret they've ever had) and need to take a break from each other to stare at your phones.

(Do not play while driving.)

Deathship One is a solo or co-operative miniatures game in which the player controls a small squad of warriors attempting to fight their way through an alien death trap. It's designed to be fast and fun and to use a minimum of miniatures and terrain. It's also designed to be limited. The whole game consists of 5 scenarios or ‘rooms'. The order and make-up of those rooms can change from game-to-game, but you always play five, assuming you live that long.
The first part of the rule book explains how to assemble your squad. There are three squads available: low-tech, middle-tech, and hi-tech. This means you can play the game using miniatures from any setting or time-period. So, if you want to try the game with Greek hoplites, or US Marines from the Vietnam War, or power armoured space commandos, you can. In fact, why not try all three and see which group lives the longest?
The second part contains the rules you need to play the game. These have been purposely kept as simple as possible, so that you can jump in and start playing quickly, and so there isn't too much to remember that might slow down the action.
After that comes the scenarios. Each of the scenarios represents one of the rooms in the Deathship. It tells you how to set-up the terrain, what markers or monsters are required, and any special rules that are in play. There is also a roster sheet for your team, a set of asset cards, and an easy reference card for each of the four alien menaces.
Finally, one last word about this game. It is not designed for you to win. Quite the opposite, in fact. It is the Deathship after all. That's not to say that winning is impossible. The Deathship can be beaten, it just isn't very likely. Th e fun is in the trying, in the desperate attempt, and seeing how far your guys make it. If you keep trying, who knows, someone might just make it out that last door!

"I have for my enemy a nation of twelve million souls, enraged beyond measure. All that was done here on the second of May was odious. No, Sire. You are mistaken. Your glory will sink in Spain."
 -Joseph Bonaparte to his brother Napoleon.

"Twelve Million Souls" is a strategic game about the so-called Spanish War of Independence, "Peninsular War", "Guerre d'Espagne" or even "French War", during the years 1808 and 1814.

It is intended to be played by 2 to 4 players, divided between the three factions of the game: France, Spain and Great Britain-Portugal (Portuguese-British side). The main feature - very uncommon - that the game brings, is to be designed at regiment/battalion scale, so it is intended to be compatible and adaptable to many other tactical regulations on the so-called "Spanish War of Independence. The total number of units that we will see in the game is simplified and reduced, but always calculated proportionally to maintain the maximum historical rigor and the real balance and disposition of forces that was experienced at the beginning of the war, in July 1808, the starting date of the game.

Objective and duration of the game:

The objective of the game is to maintain control of the Iberian Peninsula and its cities, which provide resources and treasure (if plundered) in order to gain victory points, but also to be able to maintain and improve the armies, which, to a large extent, live off the terrain. However, it is a completely asymmetrical game, so the initial situation and characteristics of each nation require a different strategy. Whether this strategy is more or less faithful to the story depends on the players.

To achieve the objective at the end of the game, it will only be necessary to have obtained more victory points than your rivals. However, it must be taken into account that, on the one hand, the allied side (Spain and Great Britain-Portugal) will play as a team against the Imperials, but, on the other hand, they will also compete between the two factions - and against France - to be the one who gets the most points individually. In other words, you will play collaboratively and competitively at the same time.

The game also has a novel turn-counting system, which will almost always allow players to choose to end a session at the end of the turn they want, and thus be able to continue the war later on. It will be possible to compare the victory points obtained by your faction during the game with the victory points obtained historically by the same faction, at each moment of the war, regardless of the fact that the total VP count is inevitably favorable to one nation or another

Mechanics:

The mechanics of the strategic game is simple: Each player will be able to move his generals or guerrilla leaders (this only the Spanish player) through the provinces and cities delimited on the map of the strategic board. Each leader will control a series of units, which he will move during his movement, being able to dispose of them or leave them in other places or under the command of other allied leaders. These generals can attack, defend, besiege or liberate cities, intercept other armies during enemy movement, set ambushes or make forts in defensive positions. In addition, the units under their command, of very different types and qualities, can be promoted and gain experience, both as a result of planned training and by their participation in battles.

For a better learning experience, the game has a "Campaign Book", which not only helps us in the deployment, but also accompanies the player during each game turn, besides narrating the historical facts (as fixed events of the game) that were affecting or influencing, month by month, in the course of the war. Also, we can read in the campaign book about the most relevant battles and battles of the Peninsula, from 1808 to 1814. It could be considered a tutorial that introduces the players to the game, indicating which parts of the rules they have to read at each moment, so that it is not necessary to read the complete rules before starting to play. The campaign book will indicate which parts we have to understand and which to skip at each moment.

In addition to the campaign book, each faction will have a booklet of reference tables, where we will have all the tables and information necessary to complete each phase of the turn, so as not to have the need to make use of the rules at every moment. We will have casuistry and specific concepts, scripted help for calculating the duration of sieges, application of casualties, promotion scheme, order of battle, specific characteristics of each nation, etc. The simplified rules and regulations at hand.

Another main and completely original element in the game is its battle resolution mechanics. With the intention of giving players the opportunity to choose between playing their own battles with an external tactical miniatures rulebook or to be able to resolve each battle in a faster way, focusing, in this case, on the strategic aspect of the game, Twelve Million Souls features a semi-autoresolving system of battles (of any type). In the "quick battle", the quality, experience and size of each army will be key to the final result, but chance and also the possible tactical and organizational decisions taken by each player -before and during the battle- can be of great importance to change the most probable sign of victory.

Therefore, we are faced with a small and dynamic card game within the main game, which has its own board and supplement to be able to play it.

In a quick battle, the duels between units of both sides will follow one after the other, and each type of weapon (infantry, cavalry and artillery) will have, or not, bonuses to the force depending on which other weapon it faces, reflecting the famous tactical system of the three combined arms (and its sub-types) that proved so effective during the Napoleonic wars.

Game phases and turn sequence:

Each of the turns symbolizes a month of real time, starting in July 1808 and ending in April 1814.

The most relevant phases of each turn, obviating the protocol phases of "updating and checking of counters" (of turn, sieges and cities), "negotiations" or "escape and liberation of prisoners", will be the phases of:


     Events: Fixed and random. Introduction, withdrawal or improvement of units. Application of random events or management of hand cards (shares exchangeable for money).


 

     Income calculation: In addition to the income from the control of the peninsular territory, factions will count on income from their home territory or overseas territories. 



     Recruitment and Promotions: We will strengthen our armies. We will be able to instruct, to promote or to recruit new units of soldiers. 



     Movements: We will direct our leaders in the field and test the numerous possible strategies to take control of the territory. We will be able to use our favor the geographical accidents and the allied maritime transport, but also it will be able to play against us. 



     Battles: We will resolve all the military operations carried out in the previous phase, as well as possible assaults on besieged cities and attempts to liberate them. We will make decisions with the imprisoned armies and with the cities captured from the enemy. 


—description from the designer

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Modern Horizons III
description:
Powerful new cards to shake up the metagame. Returning favorites from Magic's past to join the Modern era. Amazing reprints to build, customize, and expand your collection. All of this—and more—awaits players with Modern Horizons 3.

Modern Horizons 3 follows in the footsteps of its predecessor releases, bringing new-to-Magic cards to Modern alongside exciting reprints, including new-to-Modern additions and popular Modern cards from across Magic's history.

With a skill-testing Draft format and cards ready to leap into Modern, Commander, and beyond, Modern Horizons 3 has plenty for every dedicated Magic fan.

Among the most exciting returning cards are the ally fetch lands, key components to cutting-edge mana bases for your most powerful (and colorful) decks. But what sets Modern Horizons 3 apart are the cards you've never seen before. More Eldrazi? The return of energy? Intriguing graveyard tools? Modern Horizons 3 has them! Plus, fans of iconic Planeswalkers (and Magic Origins) get to see how even more of them sparked to begin their journey throughout the Multiverse and Magic story.

Hungry Oxpecker birds jump on and off the hippo to eat the most bugs.

Discover the next biome tile and secretly decide whether to get on the hippo with your small or big bird or get off and gather up your action cards. Then, score bugs based on your decision. If too many birds are on the hippo, you might lose everything!

Get to know foreign countries even when you are stuck at home. A quick knowledge and observation game, during which you will enjoy learning about flags and basic facts about various states, you will have fun both in the family circle and with a group of friends. And it doesn't matter if you are 10 or 60 years old.

The winner is the one who collects the most flag cards. Only the one who answers the question, determined by the symbol on the cube, the fastest and most importantly correctly will succeed.

If he falls off the raft into the water you automatically lose the game.

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Legendary Scenario – It's a Trap!
description:
A Legendary Scenario is a special game that allows you to play an event from the world of Black Rose Wars: Rebirth by applying a specific set of new rules and using Legendary Scenario Cards dedicated to it.

In It's a Trap! the Mages will have to use the Lodge's powerful Contraptions to their advantage. Those who fail to do so will be lost!

The rules for this scenario were published in the Ashes #9 magazine and also on the LMS website.
The rules contain trap tokens, 3d models of this trap token were stretch goals in the BRWRB Late Pledge Campaign on Kickstarter.

Each player represents a student on their typical school day: lessons to follow, questions to avoid, desks to be scribbled, and the eternal effort to avoid going to the bathroom. You may be wondering why. Well, isn't it obvious? School bathrooms are not equipped with toilet paper, most of the time, and this could mean a hard blow for you, or better to say, for your dignity...

Each player starts the game with a hand of cards. Your goal is to NOT, under any circumstances, go to the bathroom. You go to the bathroom if you have more poopy tokens than cards in your hand. So be careful, because your cards are also your life.

At the beginning of your turn, draw a card. You can choose to pass or attack another player, who can defend themselves with one of their cards if they wish. During your turn, you can use as many cards as you want, but remember, as mentioned earlier, if the poopy tokens outnumber your cards, you lose. If you lose, you stand up and go to the bathroom. Don’t have a bathroom available? Then declare anything you want as the “Bathroom.” A closet, a balcony, a plant, or a street…

The winner is the one who survives at the end of the cards.

—description from the publisher

Bombanoid is a tribute to all "shoot 'em up" arcade video games.
It is especially created to bring back the memories and the nostalging feeling of playing games like Space Invaders (1978), Alpha Mission (1985), Raiden (1990) etc.

It is a solo game and each round is divided into two phases: the UFO phase, where UFOs are approaching the Earth and the Bombanoid phase, where Bombanoid has to hunt them down and intercept them. At the end of the game, if Bombanoid has intercepted more UFOs than the ones that have entered the Earth, Bombanoid is the winner and humanity is saved.

—description from the publisher

FORMULA 1 is a board game in which you take on the role of an F1 driver. Choose your favorite team, among Ferrari, McLaren, Red Bull, Mercedes and all the others, and face the qualifying session! Then, before taking off from the starting grid, imagine your race strategy: 1 or 2 pit stops or even none? Based on this choice, tire management is fundamental. When the red lights go out, shift into first and then each gear gradually to increase or decrease speed. You will really feel like you are driving! Pay attention to how we approach the corners: you could go long on the outside line or even go off the track. But if you get the corner entry right you'll come out onto the straight fast! If you have the courage, you can risk overtaking even if there is no room for two cars, perhaps triggering an accident.
Table Formula 1 is the board game that will drive fans crazy because, without cards or dice, they will move the cars as if they were in their cockpit!

Today, there will be the big night with dances all over the place in our kingdom. But the evil king Balduin stashed all shoes, crones and chains. Luckily, the village people saw him and can give some useful tips, where you find your dresses.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

boardgame: Plan P: From Sideshow to Masterstrike
description:
Plan P was conceived as a add on to the Aragón Front in order to simultaneously replay operations in the Aragón and Extremadura fronts.

Plan P does not integrate in the Aragon Front campaign game but can be played as an extension of scenarios V. Push to Valencia, VI. The Ebro and VII. The Invasion of Catalonia.

The game can also be played on its own in three small scenarios: The Serena Bulge July 38, The August'38 Republican counterattack and the Battle of Valsequillo January 1939.

A new simplified system developed from Barefoot to Glory intended to focus solely in maneuver from an initiative point of view has been developed for Plan P and Aragón Front scenarios.

—description from the designer

description:
Demon Summoner is a team-based social deduction game of strategic summoning and secret sabotage. Each player has a secret role of Righteous or Evil, with the exception of the Demon Summoner, who is the publicly-known strategist of the Evil team.

Throughout the game, players assemble hunts, where they have the chance to smite or release demons summoned by the Demon Summoner. The Righteous players, who win the game by smiting demons, seek to find Righteous teammates to join them in their hunts. Meanwhile, the Evil players hope to release demons in secret, while hiding their true identity. Fortunately, they are assisted by the Demon Summoner, who strategically assigns demons in an attempt to corroborate the deceptions of their Evil teammates.

Amidst chaos and deception, players can invoke Prophecies to reveal demons that would otherwise remain unseen. But be careful not to be too predictable — the Demon Summoner hears every word you say and can adapt to your plans. Can the Righteous team find their true allies, or will they be deceived by the Demon Summoner?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
1902 is an 18xx game, themed around the 20th Century Limited luxury express passenger service operated by the New York Central Railroad between New York City and Chicago.

1902 was published in the Spring 2024 edition of TraXX Mainline magazine, as an expansion of 18NY. The expansion includes a new map and a couple of track tiles and private companies.

Like 18NY, the game features a  number of minor railroads, that will merge to form the NYC railroad.

SKUNK'D is a captivating dice and card game designed for family fun. It combines a unique blend of chance and strategy with a playful theme to engage players of all ages. You can play multiplayer SKUNK'D in groups or teams OR dive into our addictive Solo player game. And there's a twist! You, the players, set the rules for each game! You can keep it simple or introduce total chaos!

SKUNK’D is easy to set up as all you need are the SKUNK'D dice, cards and a score pad. Players roll dice to score points. Players establish the goal, either 10,000 points or any number of points as determined by the group.

Players roll the dice each turn, setting scoring dice in their den then choosing to either put their points on the board or risk rolling the remaining dice for higher scores. 
A player can score with individual dice (Skunks & 5s) or with combinations, including a "Carbuncle," a "Mega" or a variety of other combinations. Scoring values are determined by a set of basic scoring rules plus any number of elective rules as decided by the players. If a player rolls no scoring dice, they get "SKUNK'D!" -- so sad, too bad -- losing all points for that turn.
 The end game is triggered when one player has reached 10,000 or the pre-determined number of points. At this point, everyone continues to play, but they must consistently beat the current highest score on their turn. Failure to do so means they are out of the game. Turns continue until there is only one player left who is declared the winner!

Dead Planet Ceres is a print-and-play tactical team-building game, pitting players against one another in battles to see who is left standing by the end.

Players create teams Frames and Modules through collecting and printing out 'Pattern Groups'. Then, each player constructs a team of three Frames- powerful exoskeletons inhabited by the ghosts of the dead colonies of Ceres.

Each Frame is also outfitted with Modules to customize Weapons, Armor, and Utilities to gain the edge in combat. Create fast, game-ending, aggro engines or play it cool by setting hazards and weathering the storm with deployables.

Each match supports 2-4 players, and rules for both Free-For-All battles and 2v2 Team Deathmatch.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A card game.

From instructions:
“Dr. Eliot, president emeritus of Harvard University, has said that the depth and thoroughness of a person's education can be determined largely by his use of the English language.  Another noted educator has said that nothing is more truly indicative of the culture of an individual than his pronunciation of words.

The purpose of this game is to encourage the use of the dictionary and to emphasize the absolute necessity of frequent reference to a lexicon of recognized authority to make sure of one's pronunciation.

The 105 words represented in the game are words in common use yet commonly mispronounced.
The number might be multiplied indefinitely.  With but few exceptions words with two or more pronunciations or with disputed
pronunciations have not been used. Of the exceptions the pronunciation given is that recommended by a majority of the leading lexicographers.

In addition to its cultural benefits and practical value the game will be found an entertaining diversion. It is played like
the old game of Authors.  The 105 cards are divided into 21 books of five cards each.  The person calling for a card must
pronounce the word correctly in order to receive the card.
SHOULD HE FAIL TO GET THE ACCENT ON THE RIGHT SYLLABLE OR TO PRONOUNCE THE WORD IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE DIACRITICAL MARKS, HE DOES NOT RECEIVE THE CARD.

The Guide to Pronunciation comprising all the diacritically marked vowels will be found on the back of each card, thus enabling the player to make easy reference to it whenever in doubt as to the pronunciation of a vowel.  The child will find it
an invaluable help to memorize thoroughly this Guide.  The cards should be grouped in books according to their numerical
classification. The player receiving the largest number of books wins the game.”

SMÄK! - An action-packed card game with cute unicorns, fearsome weapons and crazy dice battles. Available in bloody or family edition.

How to play:

     Each player gets 7 cards
     The first player to play all their cards wins the round


Play card on discard pile:

     On your turn play a card that matches in color or
     play a card that matches in icon and amount of icons


Play unicorns to sabotage:

     Play unicorn cards to slow down other players
     Opponents can add unicorns and stack their effects


SMÄK! away unicorns:

     Play SMÄK! cards to defend against unicorns
     SMÄK! cards cancel card effects or trigger dice battles


Epic Dice Battles:

     The last person to roll the SMÄK! symbol loses
     The loser will face the card’s penalty


Sound of Terrör:

     Make a unicorn sound when playing the second to last card to let everyone know the end is neigh 


End of round:

     Players score penalty points for remaining cards
     The loser gets the honor to wear the unicorn horn


—description from the publisher

Developed for convention games, this is a set of rules for naval battles 1906-18 that can be played either with miniatures (1:6000 scale is recommended) or counters.

Only battleships, battlecruisers, and the most heavily gunned armoured cruisers are directly represented; the impact of smaller vessels - which was generally minor when capital ships clashed - is represented by the event table.

The rules are fairly abstract and large battles can be fought out in a few hours.

What happened here? I guess a hurricane must have passed through here, because the world hasn't seen such a mess for a long time. A toy here, keys there... Is this the gold ring we've been looking for? Who will manage to clean it all? Players will be tasked with remembering which items are in the room and finding them in the clutter. A good memory will be very useful to you!

—description from the publisher

The game features two variants. In both variants players are dealt 15 mess cards and the goal is the get rid of all of them.

In the first variant 10 item tokens are drawn from the bag and put into the box. Players look and try to memorise them. After a while the box is closed and each player displays 3 of their mess cards face up. Each card shows two items. Now, each player mark each item on their cards with answer tokens to tell which item is present in the closed box and which is not. After that the box is opened and the players check it they remembered correctly. Every card with two correct answer tokens is discarded and the first player to discard all of their cards wins.

In the second variant all of the item tokens are put into the box and then each player displays 3 of their mess cards face down in front of them. One player shouts START and then everyone flips their mess cards and try to pick the right items from the box. When any player fills two of their mess cards or is unable to do so they start to count 10 seconds after which all other players stop. Every card with two correct items is discarded and the first player to discard all of their cards wins.

—user summary

The old wizard lives in the mythical forest. As his students, you are walking through the heart of the forest when you hear his voice echoing through the trees: "I have locked your wizard towers and hidden the keys deep within the mythical forest. Whoever finds three keys first and returns to the magic tower will become my successor."

Are you ready for the challenge of Fabelwald? Then set off on your journey!

—description from the publisher

Gameplay Description:

The game has two modes.

STANDARD GAME:  On your turn you will move four times, each time revealing an adjacent tile.  If it is a monster, your turn ends immediately.  If is the wizard, you must draw a wizard card, which reveals an ingredient you must find in the forest.  If it is a magical place, check against your three magical place cards.  If there's a match, you flip the card over; you have retrieved one of the three keys you need to unlock your tower!  When you have all three keys, race to your tower; if you reach it, with no face-up wizard cards, you win!

ADVANCED GAME:  Similar to the standard game, but you aren't limited to four moves.  You may keep revealing tiles until you choose to stop, find your magical place, or are stopped by a monster.  The main monsters in this version are Will-o'-the-wisps; the first one you see puts you on alert, the second sends you back to where you started the turn.  But there's also the dragon, who sends you back to your home space!  At turn's end, you swap the position of any two tiles.  Then, if you found your magical place, you flip it to its "key" side and draw a new magical place card.  When you have three keys, race to your tower next turn to win!

In both games:  Pay attention to the tiles your opponents reveal on their turns, and try to remember where the monsters, wizards, and magical places are after the tiles are all flipped back over!

SYNOPSIS
The multiverse was united on the same plane, everyone is gathered in the same place with nothing, there is absolutely nothing, just a destroyed place that no one knows. It is also unknown what they do there, will they have to wait? Will they be in hell? Nobody knows, all that remains is to wait..
While people were desperate and suffering, a few people started playing soccer, a little game. Nobody resisted playing, time had to pass, right? This is how this game was created where any character is playable. Characters from anime, movies, books, series... All these characters are here to play without rules and with many random events that will change the game.

WHY IS IT A TCG AND A PRINT AND PLAY?
A print and play and TCG game where every season there will be new game and scenario tokens. It is a Print and Play because there is no board to buy, no tokens, nothing, you will have to make them yourself, or download the boards where you can play. Of course, you can make the tokens however you want, use cardboard, miniatures or whatever you prefer.
And what about TCG? Simple, the more points you get, based on goals, dribbles and injuries, you can get new players, skills and more events to play with, so playing always means getting improvements.
For this there is an Excel where you can see everything there is and what you can get in each envelope. A vice that will make you want to play more and more.
Make your team, try to score a goal, get points and enjoy rolling dice to get to the other field.

RULES
The rules are simple. The turns are alternate, first one player will move and then another, to do this you must put your tokens face down and reveal, at the same time, the tokens from left to right. When they are revealed, Power Opposing is rolled and whoever gets the highest number starts that turn. Then the other player goes. When both players have moved, this is repeated with the next token.
On each player's turn, they can use up all the possible actions, in the order they want, until they use them all up, or until they lose a dribble or a defense. Or until the ball goes out.

ATTRIBUTES
Each player has a series of attributes (steps, dribble, power, shot, defense, control, spring and fatigue). Each of them has a die assigned to them, except steps and fatigue, which are fixed numbers. When you are going to do an action, you must check if that action needs a die. When you activate an attribute you roll a die to see if you can dribble (a roll against the enemy defense), pass (the number of squares you have to reach), control the ball (when you receive a pass, you must get a control by rolling the same number or more than the pass), shoot at goal (see if it goes in, goes out or hits the post) or spring (how many extra steps you take).

HOW TO WIN
To win you have to score goals or injure the opposing team. Easy, right?

EVENTS, SKILLS, GOALKEEPERS AND BALLS
All of these, like the players, you must get over time and buy boosters.
Events are actions that will change the course of the game, they usually have a maximum number of uses and can be used at any time.
Skills are assigned to a player, this player can do that skill. Skills can be weapons, magic, passive actions, bonuses...
Goalkeepers will help you stop goals, or stop an enemy attack, but they can also give bonuses.
Balls are obtained through challenges and these are used to make the game more fun.

CREATE YOUR TEAM
To create your team you must choose a maximum of 8 players and a minimum of 4 (everyone starts with 4). You can choose a goalkeeper, if you have one. You can have up to a maximum of 2 events and 2 skills per player on your team, skills cannot be passed from one player to another. With this you can start playing the game of your life.

GAME MODES
There are several game modes, from King of the Court, to tournaments, exhibitions, best of 3, or simply one goal. This is up to you so if you don't have much time to play or you have a lot, you can choose the one you like the most.

SEASONS
The seasons bring new cards, just like new football fields. It is a zine that will be released talking a little about what is new this season, some tips, and you can even participate in the zine. It does not have a fixed edition, but will be released at different times.

https://asaltaelrol.itch.io/futbol-extrano

—description from the designer

SINOPSIS
El multiverso se vio unido en un mismo plano, todo el mundo está reunido en el mismo lugar sin nada, no hay absolutamente nada, solo un lugar destrozado que nadie conoce. Tampoco se sabe que hacen allí, ¿tendrán que esperar? ¿Estarán en el infierno? Nadie lo sabe, solo queda esperar..
Mientras la gente se desesperaba y sufría unos cuantos se pusieron a jugar al fútbol, una pequeña pachanga. Nadie se resistió a ponerse a jugar, debía pasar el tiempo, ¿no? Así se creó este juego donde cualquier personaje es jugable. Personajes de anime, de películas, libros, series... Todos esos personajes están aquí para jugar sin reglas y con muchos eventos aleatorios que harán cambiar el partido.

¿POR QUÉ ES UN TCG Y UN PRINT AND PLAY? 
Un juego print and play y TCG donde cada temporada habrá nuevas fichas de juegos y escenarios. 
Es un Print and Play porque no hay tablero que comprar, ni fichas, ni nada, tendrás que hacerlos tú, o bien, descargar los tableros donde ponerte a jugar. Eso sí, las fichas podrás hacerlas tú como quieras, usa cartón, miniaturas o lo que prefieras.
¿Y lo de TCG? Sencillo, cuantos más puntos consigas, a base de goles, regates y lesiones, podrás conseguir nuevos jugadores, habilidades y más eventos con los que jugar, por lo que jugar significa siempre conseguir mejoras.
Para ello hay un Excel donde ver todo lo que hay y lo que puede tocarte en cada sobre. Un vicio que hará que quieras jugar más y más.
Haz tu equipo, intenta marcar gol, consigue puntos y disfruta lanzando dados para conseguir llegar al otro campo.

REGLAS
Las reglas son sencillas. Los turnos son alternos, primero moverá un jugador y después otro, para ello debes poner tus fichas bocabajo e ir revelando, a la vez, las fichas de izquierda a derecha. Cuando se revelan se lanza Potencia enfrentada y quien saque el número más alto empeiza ese turno. Después irá el otro. Cuando ya se han movida los jugadores de ambos, se repite esto con la siguiente ficha.
En el turno de cada jugadora podrá agotar todas las acciones posibles, en el orden que quieras, hasta gastar todas, o bien, hasta perder un regate o una defensa. O que el balón se vaya fuera.

ATRIBUTOS
cada jugadora tiene una serie de atributos (pasos, regate, potencia, tiro, defensa, control, spring y cansancio)cada uno de ellos tiene un dado asignado, excepto pasos y cansancio que son números fijos. Cuando vas a hacer una acción debes mirar si esa acción necesita un dado. Cuando activas un atributo lanzas un dado para ver si consigues el regate (una tirada enfrentada contra la defensa enemigo), un pase (el número de casilla que tiene que llegar), controlar el balón (cuando recibe un pase, debe conseguir un control sacando el mismo número o más que el pase) tirar a puerta (ver si entra a puerta, va fuera o da al palo) o spring (cuantos pasos extras das).

COMO GANAR
Para ganar hay que marcar goles o lesionar al equipo contrario. Facil, ¿verdad?

EVENTOS, HABILIDADES, PORTEROS Y BALONES
Todas estas, al igual que las jugadoras, deberás conseguirlo con el paso del tiempo y comprando sobres. 
Los eventos son acciones que cambiarán las tornas del partido, por lo general tienen un máximo de usos y pueden ser usados en cualquier momento.
Las habilidades se asignan a un jugador, este jugador podrá hacer esa habilidad. Las habilidades pueden ser armas, magia, acciones pasivas, bonificadores...
Los porteros te servirán para parar goles, o frenar un ataque enemigo, pero también pueden dar bonificadores.
Los balones se consiguen con retos y estos sirven para que el partido sea más divertido.

CREA TU EQUIPO
Para crear tu equipo debes elegir máximo 8 jugadores y mínimo 4 (todo el mundo empieza con 4). Podrás elegir un portero, si tienes. Puedes tener en tu equipo hasta un máximo de 2 eventos y 2 habilidades por jugador, las habilidades no se pueden pasar de un jugador a otro. Con esto ya podrás ponerte a jugar el partido de tu vida.

MODOS DE JUEGO
Hay varios modos de juego, desde el Rey de la Pista, pasando a torneos, exhibiciones, al mejor de 3, o simplemente a un gol. Esto es a tu elección por si no tienes mucho tiempo para jugar o tienes mucho, podrás elegir el que más os guste.

TEMPORADAS
Las temporadas hacen que haya nuevas fichas, al igual que nuevos campos de fútbol. Es un zine que saldrá hablando un poco de lo que hay nuevo en esta temporada, algunos consejos, incluso tú puedes participar en el zine. No tiene una tirada fija, sino que saldrá en diferentes tiempos.

https://asaltaelrol.itch.io/futbol-extrano

—description from the designer (Catalan)

boardgame: Black Stories: Der Tod auf Reisen
description:
This set contains 50 real (and incredibly foolish) mishaps that occurred during travels.

How to play:
The card set contains 50 tricky, pitch-black puzzle cards for teens and adults to solve by asking clever questions. Cracking black stories together with friends is the most fun. One player, the bidder, takes a card from the deck, reads the short story written on the front of the card. On the back of the card, the master finds the answer, which he of course keeps to himself. Now the guessing people have to "ask" themselves the solution to the story. The questions should be formulated in such a way that the master can answer "yes" or "no". A spooky and fun guessing game for small and large groups - can also be played digitally.

In this set, players must solve 50 cases revolving around horror, ghosts, and goosebumps: ghostly apparitions and paranormal encounters included.

How to play:
The card set contains 50 tricky, pitch-black puzzle cards for teens and adults to solve by asking clever questions. Cracking black stories together with friends is the most fun. One player, the bidder, takes a card from the deck, reads the short story written on the front of the card. On the back of the card, the master finds the answer, which he of course keeps to himself. Now the guessing people have to "ask" themselves the solution to the story. The questions should be formulated in such a way that the master can answer "yes" or "no". A spooky and fun guessing game for small and large groups - can also be played digitally.

boardgame: Halo wieża: WAW Warszawa, Lotnisko Chopina
description:
On November 1, 2011, a Boeing 767 departed from Newark to Warsaw with 231 people on board.

Captain of flight PLL LOT 106 was Tadeusz Wrona. Shortly after take-off, the first signals of a failure appeared. An attempt was made to reach Warsaw. Despite following the procedure, it turned out that the landing gear did not extend.

Ultimately, the plane touched down on the runway, which was doused with firefighting foam. Thanks to the cooperation of the entire crew, no one was injured.

Will you be able to repeat this feat?

An additional airport for Sky Team: WAW Warsaw.

Contains one postcard with two additional missions (Red and Orange).


Note: The WAW airport included in Sky Team: Turbulence has a different approach track.

The Thai dessert making competition was announced by the Royal Palace. Everyone is excited and prepared Thai desserts for the Royal Palace Baker's reputations.

Thai Dessert Kitchen is a REAL-TIME competition game for 2-4 players. Play by making Thai desserts, selling Thai desserts for new ingredients, and collecting points from Thai desserts to WIN the Royal Palace competition. Beware! If you stock up on too much ingredients, you may be caught by the Inspector.

Moving to achieve your goal not only costs energy and careful planning, but also money.
So you should try to reach your goal without putting all your eggs in one basket and without investing all your money. Or should you?

If you don't have anything left on the table, the only thing you have left to achieve your goal is other people's handouts. Maybe that's the way to go?

In any case, the air is getting very thin on the "Schouldenboerg"...

GOAL OF THE GAME:
To place three figures of different sizes on the correct step on each side of the stairs.

—description from the publisher (translated)

"Schuldenberg" means "Mountain of debt"

Setting up GATES and passing through them is the goal of this easy-to-learn but difficult-to-follow game.

Changing your identity, appropriating other people's characters, building traps, as well as secretly sneaking onto and occupying other people's spaces are just some of the ways to achieve this goal.

The confusion caused by the different sizes of the pieces and the uncertainty as to how long you can call a piece "your own" adds extra tension to every minute of the duel.

You are only lucky when you have won the game. Not a move earlier.

AIM OF THE GAME:
To let your own pieces pass by building gates and thus clear other pieces from the board.

—description from the publisher (translated)

The 5 elements named in Feng Shui - fire, water, earth, wood and metal - teach us that nature has the power to develop independently, to constantly change, but also to destroy in order to constantly reinvent itself in new forms.

In this very complex game of the elements, a simple set of rules is used to "regulate" and "change" the 5 elements and turn the 5 element circles to show that the elimination of a single element derails the structure of nature and leads to the loss of this game.

Chess-weary strategists and tacticians with brains should experience a real and new challenge in this game.

AIM OF THE GAME:
Both players own each of the 5 elements twice.
By moving and regulating, you try to take full possession of one of their elements.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Mysterium im Kloster is an escape game full of riddles and puzzles that consists only of cards.

In the Mont-Saint-Michel Abbey, a legendary treasure is said to be hidden, with mysterious clues pointing the way. Solving these riddles brings the players step by step closer to the goal. After the 9th riddle, the grand final puzzle awaits, revealing the secret location of the treasure—if successfully solved.

Can you find a pair from 6 possible combinations? In this new game system, every revealed card can be a match! But be careful, some words look very similar, and the different images of a pair add to the confusion! Let’s get started and don’t get quabbled!

Over the course of the game, more and more cards get revealed showing words written correctly or backwards or images of things. As soon as you see any two cards describing the same object hit the matching card played first. The fastest will be the winner.

boardgame: 7 Wonders (Second Edition): Leaders – Diplomats Guild Promo Card
description: At the end of the game, you earn 1 victory point for each Leader card in your neighbor's city.

boardgame: 7 Wonders (Second Edition): Forgers Guild Promo Card
description: At the end of the turn in which this card comes into play, all players except you lose 3 Coins. Coins are returned to the reserve.

In Fake Shots, a keen eye is essential: each game round features four images and a category. Players secretly guess: How many images match the given category? It's better to underestimate than overestimate—being too high incurs penalty points! The images include photos, drawings, or city maps, testing the players' knowledge in a playful and visual way.

Fake Facts is a quiz game, testing the players throughout different categories and terms from all subject areas. Each card shows 5 terms along with a category, and the players must determine: How many terms fit the specified category? For example, how many of the following terms were invented in the 20th century? Such as chewing gum, cornflakes, tea bags, Wikipedia, and fortune cookies. Everyone secretly makes their guesses, and points are distributed after the resolution. It's crucial not to overestimate because that results in point deduction!

The queen ant is celebrating a big birthday party, and the ants have lined up patiently at the anthill with their instruments to congratulate her.

In Stau vorm Bau, you take turns placing ant cards in the row and, ideally, collecting points — or other players can benefit from the card you placed. Whoever has the most points at game's end wins.

Ligretto Fun Run takes all the action-packed excitement of Ligretto to another level! In this game, you can play cards that are either 1 higher or lower, so get ready to think quickly. Can your legs keep up the pace?

With a pile of cards on each side of the table, you'll need to move fast too. Let the high-speed fun begin!

As with all Ligretto games, the aim of the game is to get rid of all the cards in your Ligretto pile as fast as you can.

In Ligretto Fun Run, you have to place your piles of cards on different sides of the table.
You can only play cards from the pile on the side of the table you are standing at. In other words, you need fast hands and fast legs to end up with the most points.

The race is on! Hey Hey Relay is a fast and frenzied relay game where teams race one another across the game board, rolling character dice and performing silly tasks as quickly as they can. The first team to cross the finish line wins, so choose your favorite animal racer and get rolling! With over 50 different activity-packed Challenge Cards and added Character Ability cards for additional gameplay, the whole family will be racing to play Hey Hey Relay again and again.


     Hey Hey Relay is the fun and frantic relay race game for the entire family!
     Race across the relay board in teams, rolling dice and performing wacky Challenge Card activities to reach the finish line


—description from the publisher

This is a small, pocket-sized trivia game. It contains 50 cards with 3 questions each, most of them with multiple choice options. The backside of each card contains the answers and offers additional information.

The title translates to "Earth".

Folklore & Pilgrimage is a major expansion for An Age Contrived. It introduces 2 new characters and 2 new modules that affect all characters.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Atrum Arena: First Edition Starter Packs
description:
The best weay to grow your collection with 24 Crawlers aditional to the Beginner Box, add variety, reinforce your team of Crawlers or make new teams. It's also a great way to start playing individually.

Each expansion pack includes:

     2 premium foil Crawler cards. One is totally visible to choose it. The second card is random.
     1 random premium Minion card for the base deck.
     1 booklet with the basic rules.

Behold, Princess Carmesina "Deluxe" and her power of Reflection!


    This version of Carmesina is only compatible with Rebel Princess Deluxe Edition. 
    Carmesina Deluxe is no longer a card; She's a punchboard tile exactly like the other 12 princesses in the Deluxe Edition.
    And yes, that is a reflexive substance on the card, so you can see yourself when you hold up the card!


Reflection
Use the power of any princess in the game that has not been activated yet this round.

Carmesina Setup
Shuffle Carmesina into the rest of the princess tiles before dealing them out.

Carmesina Rules
Use the power of any princess in the game (that other players have chosen) that has not been activated yet this round. When Carmesina activates a Princess Power, that princess may not use her own power until the next trick. If all other princess powers have already been activated (tiles have been flipped over), you may not use Carmesina this round.

Capítulo I "Pecados Redentores" (Chapter I, "Redemptive Sins") (unofficial name) contains 16 new Crawler cards to add to your collection, add variety, reinforce your team or make new teas. It's also ideal to start playing individually.

Each expansion pack includes:

     2 premium foil Crawler cards. One card is totally visible to choose it. The second card is random.
     1 random premium foil Minion card for the base deck. There is a chance that instead of foil, the Minion card is "Alterna Ars" (alternative art).
     1 booklet with the basic rules and a QR code to access the full rules online.

Capítulo II "Virtudes Condenadas" (Chapter II, "Condemned Virtues") contains 16 new Crawler cards to add to your collection, add variety, reinforce your team or make new teas. It's also ideal to start playing individually.

Each expansion pack includes:

     2 premium foil Crawler cards. One card is totally visible to choose it. The second card is random.
     1 random premium foil Minion card for the base deck. There is a chance that instead of foil, the Minion card is "Alterna Ars" (alternative art).
     1 booklet with the basic rules and a QR code to access the full rules online.

Shinsoku Cinema is a cooperative party game. Create a movie poster based on a theme by combining cards in your hand and cards on the table. Players then guess the combinations to match the theme. The goal is to collect a predetermined number of "Hall of Fame Movies" by answering correctly.

boardgame: Atrum Arena: Deidades del Lumen, Incursión Alfa
description:
Deidades del Lumen ("Deities of the Lumen") is a complement that allows to play Atrum Arena alone or co-op. One or more players, each with their own team of three Crawlers, will face powerful and destructive multidimensional deities, with the objective of defeating them and expel them from the Atrum.

The alpha incursion pack includes the following Deities cards and a booklet with the rules for this format:

     Yod'Endox “El Quiebra Galaxias”.
     Amend'Thi “El Devorador de Novas”.
     Nedihr'Ithul “La Peste de las Nebulosas”.

boardgame: Atrum Arena: Deidades del Lumen, Incursión Beta
description:
Deidades del Lumen ("Deities of the Lumen") is a complement that allows to play Atrum Arena alone or co-op. One or more players, each with their own team of three Crawlers, will face powerful and destructive multidimensional deities, with the objective of defeating them and expel them from the Atrum.

The beta incursion pack includes the following Deities cards and a booklet with the rules for this format:

     Untoz'Eleth “El Arrasador de Mundos”.
     Nigheb'Vhosh “La Constelación infernal”.
     Onca'Tholpuz “El Triturador de Estrellas”.

boardgame: Atrum Arena: Deidades del Lumen, Incursión Gama
description:
Deidades del Lumen ("Deities of the Lumen") is a complement that allows to play Atrum Arena alone or co-op. One or more players, each with their own team of three Crawlers, will face powerful and destructive multidimensional deities, with the objective of defeating them and expel them from the Atrum.

The gamma incursion pack includes the following Deities cards and a booklet with the rules for this format:

     Ghrizt'Exeler “El Derretidor de Sueños”.
     Thaiog'Nurka “La Presencia del Fin”.
     Yivhe'Urlath “El Negador de la Existencia”.

Pop in the DVD to let loose and Dance along the hottest moves!

Each player lays a plastic, non electronic mat on the floor and follows the dance moves on the supplied DVD.

With over 40 scenarios, players are judged by the other players based on their performance. A tutorial will help the player learn the moves to be able to participate in the final full dance routines. Earn the best rating to win the game.

Yes .... it's Dance Dance Revolution, the boardgame ....

boardgame: Survive in Balkan: The Board Game
description:
Embrace the Chaos, Together (or Not!) in Survive in the Balkans

Survive in the Balkans is a hilarious, cooperative board game for 1-4 players. You and your fellow family members take on the ultimate challenge: surviving in the Balkans for 3, 6, 9, or even 12 months! Work together to overcome the region's unpredictable nature, from car breakdowns and unexpected floods to quirky family events.

United We Stand, Divided We... Emigrate?

While cooperation is key to overcoming these monthly hurdles, individual dreams and desires add a twist. Each family member has their own set of personal goals that might conflict with the family's overall survival plan. Will you prioritize fixing the leaky roof or sneak off for a night at the local pub? Every choice contributes to the family's happiness (or lack thereof). If the family thrives, you all escape to a new life in Austria - a victory for your collective resilience! But neglecting personal happiness can lead to individual family members getting eliminated, leaving the others to face the Balkans alone.

Laughter in the Face of Adversity

Survive in the Balkans injects humor into the often-stressful realities of family life. Prepare for lighthearted chaos as events like Dad's "accidental" pub visit automatically drains Mom's happiness meter! With its strategic gameplay, relatable situations, and hilarious touches, Survive in the Balkans offers a unique and unforgettable board game experience.

—description from the publisher

1824: New York State Railroading is an 18xx game, themed around coal railways in New York State.

1824 was published in the Spring 2024 edition of TraXX Mainline magazine, as an expansion of 1824: Austrian Hungarian Railway. It takes the concept of merging smaller railways into big companies, and adds some variations such as Pullman cars, and an initial draft of the starting railroads.

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Werewolf: Fang & Talon
description:
There is a monster who prowls this world that even vampires fear, and to whom Hunters give a wide berth when the moon is waxing to its fullest power. Garou. Werewolf. This new expansion introduces two tribes from this dangerous society: the Silver Fangs, who strive to lead a new generation of kin in pushing back against the fate of an inevitable Apocalypse; and the Red Talons, a blunt and angry instrument of Gaia who seek to slow the tide of human “progress” by any means necessary.

Features

2 Pre-constructed Werewolf player decks: Silver Fangs & Red Talons!
Play the tribes head-to-head or against Vampire and Hunter decks from previous sets!
Includes Den Pack to customize your decks!

—description from the publisher

“And I looked, and behold a pale horse: and his name that sat on him was Death, and Hell followed with him.” Revelations 6:8

The pale horse is synonymous with death. When a group of friends decide to go on a camping trip at Palehorse Lake, none of them ever consider whether the name of the location should be taken literally or not. Little do they know that their vacation will soon be filled with death and terror and the rider of the pale horse may actually be a member of their own group. Who among them will survive the trip to Palehorse Lake?

This expansion to The Cubes of Europe adds 8 new cards to the game and supports up to 4 players.

—description from the designer

A simple card game of drawing and keeping or discarding.  The object is to come as close to zero after five rounds.  Cards range from -10 to 20.

On players turn do one of three actions.  Draw a card, replace one of your cards facedown.  Draw the last card discarded.  Place face up in your tableau.  Lastly flip one of your face down cards.  Round ends when one person has all cards face up.

Strategy is a must.  Takes about 20 minutes yo play.

FOX TWO: Air Combat is an exhilarating solitaire card game that puts you in the cockpit of the most iconic fighter jets of the 1980s and '90s. With just 18 cards, you'll experience the thrill of aerial dogfights in the golden age of air combat.

Take command of your favorite aircraft and prove your skills in intense, simulated battles to become the king of the skies. Execute either offensive or defensive maneuvers each turn, carefully managing your energy to outmaneuver the enemy fighter. Lock on, launch missiles, and dominate the skies!

Featuring multi-use cards, hand management, and deterministic combat, FOX TWO: Air Combat delivers a very compact yet immersive experience of resource management and strategic decision-making. Are you ready to claim air superiority?

—description from the designer

Be the first to find the area closest to the area of ​​the shape depicted in the subject! The areas to be chosen come in various shapes and colors, so quick and accurate judgment is important in this game. You can enjoy discovering and being surprised by things like, "Are those two shapes the same size?!" or "This one is more heroic!"
So how will you fight and choose? Will you calculate and find the best answer, or will you go for your intuition? Both elements are important in this game, which is also recommended for intellectual development.

Displeased with his subordinates and facing the difficult task of deciding which vampire will be his right-hand ally, Dracula decides to summon the most influential vampires to Transylvania and propose a competition in which, for three days, they must compete to determine who is the most powerful vampire, after Dracula, of course! But they must be cautious, as Van Helsing is in the region.

The game accommodates 1 to 6 players, with each player controlling a vampire. Over three days, they traverse the map of Transylvania, taking actions based on the location and the time of day. Terrorize lesser creatures, engage in a power struggle with other vampires, and prove your devotion to the legendary Dracula.

[Solo Mode]  
Has eternal life made you lazier? 
With multiple difficulty levels and extra cards, prove your worth to Dracula while being hunted by Van Helsing and his allies.

—description from the publisher

As the 19th century draws to a close, the monarchies of Europe struggle to stay relevant in this rapidly changing age. Whether it’s the court of King Frederik in Saxony or the court of the aging Queen Victoria of the British Empire trying to maintain rule over the faraway riches of Australia, they must all contend with the rising political forces clamoring to secure their piece of the pie in the nuclear revolution. As ideologies clash in parliaments, entrepreneurs like yourself must do their best to make sure they have plenty of friends in the halls of power.

Nucleum: Court of Progress introduces several new concepts and rule changes that can be added to the base game and/or Nucleum: Australia. Via the new Court action players get a chance to send their workers to political parties and even become their leaders to gain various benefits. Joining a Party provides instant rewards and opportunities to earn VP during King's Day Scoring, which is now much more significant. The expansion also adds two new Experiments, new action tiles, contracts, etc.

—description from the publisher

Panda Royale: Expansion Set features three new panda clans:


    The Panda Support Team consists of seven additional d6 dice; when drafted, these dice give you the ability to re-roll under-performing dice.
    The Wild Pandas are seven dice that mimic the attributes of other pandas you have already recruited. Do you need more purple pandas? Draft one of these dice, and you will increase the points earned by your purple pandas.
    The Wise Sage Pandas allow you to earn an permanent pink d10 after you have acquired at least six of the existing clans. This die scores big (up to 90 points) because you have acquired experience from many clans.

¡PUNchLINE! is all about using non-English words in funny phrases to impress the "Judge"! 
Guess the language and meaning for more points! 
¡USA Made!

How to Play:
  Set Up: Grab some pens, position the ¡PUNchLINE! card box, pass out the Player Sheets, and get ready for some fun!
  Judge & Pronounce: One player acts as the Judge and reads the pronunciation of a global word from the card.
  Write & Guess: Players have 90 seconds to:
    * Create a funny phrase using the word's syllables
    * Guess the word's meaning
    * Guess its language or region of origin
  When time is up, the Judge collects the Player Sheets.
  Reveal: The Judge reveals the real meaning and origin, and reads out loud each player's phrase. The Judge then selects the best phrase.
  Reward: Beads (points) are awarded for:
    * Best phrase
    * Correct meaning guess
    * Correct origin guess
  Rounds: Continue with a new Judge each round. The game concludes when the recommended number of rounds are complete.
  Winner: The player with the most beads after all rounds are complete is the Champ.
  https://youtu.be/jzkt6aCjwLc?si=55mtXCGj9WJtdToa

Implementation Status:
  Beta testing is complete, improvements to the Game incorporated
  Printing Company selection is complete (will be made in the USA!): Delano Games - Battle Creek, MI
  Artwork is complete and approved
  Crowdfunding: Pre-launch plan in the works

—description from the designer

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): The Filth Faction Deck
description:
Abandon your soul to the lies of demons! The Demagogue has thrust his band of infernal worshipers into the Summoner Wars.

When using The Filth Faction Deck, you can warp their flesh and snap their bones, then shape them into just the right monster for the task at hand. When one is willing to sink to any depth, there is no limit to the power that can be achieved!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Europa Universalis: The Price of Power – Scenarios III Booklet
description:
The scenarios included in this booklet are somewhat experimental and have not been played tested as extensively as we normally do, but since its release as a downloadable PDF and print-on-demand product earlier this year, many of these scenarios have been played a lot by the EU:tPoP community and we have eliminated the typos there were there. However, while the scenarios in this booklet are not exactly off the cuff, they may still have some imbalances here and there.

We have chosen some scenarios that push the envelope of the EU:tPoP system a bit further than we would normally do, because we think that they all provide some really interesting challenges and experiences, and because this release seemed like the perfect opportunity to try out some more experimental content like this.

The Scenarios
Each and everyone one of the scenarios have been picked because we believe that they add something new to the game:

Old and New Pretenders is a low-conflict 4-player scenario which features some of the smaller Realms. It is a good learning scenario, if you have the time available, as there are no huge immediate threats to the players and they are unlikely to be pulled into conflicts with each other early on.
    The Italian Job is a tight 2-player scenario which plays out in a very confined geographical space. It can be played both with and without Bots depending on whether or not that is something you find entertaining, and it only lasts for 3 Rounds, so it can easily be completed in an evening.
    Same Sejm, But Different is a scenario where 3 human players compete against a beast of a Bot, as well as each other, with opportunities to manipulate the Bot to go after the other players rather than yourself.
    Turning the Table is a 6-player scenario that provides an alternative to the Grand Campaign. In this alternative history scenario, you start in the 2nd half of Age I and it is assumed that the 1st half panned out a bit differently than it did in real life, meaning that the starting position of each featured Realm is rather different than usual.
    The Sun God Awakens is a solo/4-player scenario that for the first time introduces the possibility to play a Realm on one of the Distant Continents, namely the Incas (or rather Cusco as you start off). This unique experience also comes with its own scenario-specific rules and Inca Mission tree to accommodate thematic play.
    Baltic Hegemony is a solo/3-player scenario pitting Sweden against Poland and Muscovy/Russia in a battle to dominate the Baltic region. In the solo variant, Sweden races against the clock to overcome their rivals. In the multi-player variant, it is all about dominating the Baltic shores.
    Turmoil in Western Europe is a tight 6-player scenario with lots of interaction that takes place on the Western Europe map. In this scenario, there is no room to grow without infringing on a neighboring PR's lands.
    The 30 Years' War is a highly interactive and fiercely intense 6-player scenario where it is all about the Power Struggles in and around the Holy Roman Empire. Through 4 Rounds in Age III, all the player realms are competing for the same territories, but their starting positions, strengths and weaknesses are very different.

—description by publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Chosen Grove Faction Deck
description: Surrender to the cyclical ways of Nature with the Chosen Grove! A summoning stone has gifted sentience to the most legendary tree in Itharia. Now, the inscrutable Eldiri Nimid bestows the powers of the natural world upon its adherents. Master the ebb and flow of magical energy, letting your warriors embrace the true potential of symbiosis!

Shattered Divinity: The 7 Fragments is a solo and cooperative JRPG turn-based campaign board game for 1 to 4 players. It's a 12-chapter epic where you'll follow me, the Fragment Bearer, as I hunt down the missing shards to restore a god. Along the way, you’ll face countless dialogue and narrative choices that shape our story and path. Each character has a backstory with optional side quests between chapters, and the main storybook alone is over 120 pages, with extra books for character stories and side quests. So we will be spending a lot of damn time together!

Combat in this game is no joke. Inspired by classic JRPGs, it's a fast-paced, turn-based system with a twist. You’ll form a team of four from up to eight characters, each with unique elements and abilities. The key is exploiting enemies' elemental weaknesses. Break their elemental shields and knock them to the bottom of the turn order. Each character comes with a basic attack, special abilities, ultimates, and party combos.

When combat starts, turn order is determined by the speed of enemies and party members. The party starts with one special ability point and gain another with each basic attack. Special abilities use these points, so plan strategically. Ultimate meters are separate, filling every five attacks, and can be used anytime, even outside your turn.

There are four combat slots: 1 and 2 for front liners, 3 and 4 for back liners. Enemies roll to target, with a 66% chance they'll hit the front line and a 33% chance they'll hit the back. These odds change based on enemy types, and some enemies have area-of-effect or cone attacks.

Winning combat earns party experience (exp). At the end of each chapter, spend this exp to get new talents, level up abilities, and more. If you fall behind, you can grind the "Random Encounter" deck, but excessive grinding means we will get a worse ending. There are also 12 special bosses between chapters for extra challenges and secret boss encounters in character and side quests.

Time to shut up and gear up. Let's go save the damn universe. If we fail, everyone dies—yeah, even the ones who already zombies and ghost.

—description from the designer

Handelskunst ("The art of trading") game is a catchy, easy-to-understand card game for 2 to 5 players aged 10 and over. The game lasts 45 minutes.

The game is based on collecting playing cards and trading paintings trading paintings by female artists from Lüneburg and the surrounding area.

One or more paintings by one artist form a collection.
The purchase prices to be paid for paintings are neither fixed nor negotiable. Each time a painting by an artist is purchased, the purchase price for the purchase price for all other paintings by this artist increases.

The trick here is that only the purchase price of the paintings increases, but not their value.

The value of paintings only increases with the number of paintings by the same artist that players own. The more paintings a collection contains, the more it is worth. The winner is the player who has the most most valuable collections.

Bonuses linked to goals, special cards that can be used at any time and not always cards that can be used at any time and not always enough money force players to interact and promise and promise lasting and varied fun.

If you can do the math, you have a clear advantage. However, there is no guaranteed victory.

—description from the designer

In Goofy Sister Gabby, 1-2 players will assume the role of siblings who are pitted against each other in another one of Goofy Sister Gabby’s games. During the game, players will take turns placing or tucking cards into a shared space to form polyomino patterns that potentially earn points for both players. After 8 rounds, tally up the score to see who wins!

Goofy Sister Gabby is the first game in the second collection of the Risible Relatives Series. Each game in the series is a standalone 54-card game for 1-2 players that can also be combined with each game in the collection for a different experience.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Lands of Galzyr: Dreams and Mysteries
description:
Dreams and Mysteries is an expansion to the critically acclaimed open-world adventure game Lands of Galzyr.

The expansion includes the following:

     A new adventurer, Noko and Umi, the inseparable duo
     Extended personal quests for all adventurers
     New special abilities for all adventurers
     And a lot more new content to make your adventures in Galzyr greater than ever before!


Dreams and Mysteries requires the base game to play. All content fits inside the base game box even sleeved and integrates seamlessly. You simply sort the new cards into the card library, enable the expansion in the digital storybook, and continue your adventures in a new, richer, deeper Galzyr!

—description from the publisher

Big Chicken is a dynamic and fiercely competitive card game designed to play the same for 3 to 6 players (with a 2-player variant).  This medium-weight game requires an adaptive strategy as the game unfolds.  The goal is to get rid of your cards as fast as possible, score low, and set your opponents with extra points using special bonuses and clever play. While luck determines the cards you receive, victory hinges on cunning and planning.  Get ready for intense gameplay, spirited banter, and a lot of fun!  Will you end up the Golden Egg, or the Rotten Egg?

Though the game requires strategy, it is still fast-paced, and the rules are designed to encourage good-natured ribbing, daring boasts, lively table talk, and a bit of cutthroat play.  BIG CHICKEN invites fun social interaction.  The dynamics of the game encourage competitive banter and table talk, but players also enjoy the excitement and camaraderie of the “big plays” — regardless of who makes them.

Since no partners are required, and because the strategy is the same for 3, 4, 5, or 6 players (with some variation for 2), plus the game is compact, this versatility makes BIG CHICKEN perfect for gamers to always carry with them.

BIG CHICKEN’s origins lie in the traditional Chinese game Sho Tai Ti (Big Two), with influences from the German game Mau-Mau (Crazy Eights), the Japanese game Daifugō (Tycoon), the English Whist family of games (Hearts, Bridge, Spades, etc), Poker, and a few others.

—description from the publisher

The expansion contains plastic obstacles in forms of some unwanted objects that can be found on golf and mini golf courses, such as chewing gums, banana peels, dropped ice cream cones, etc., and some others that would make this game even more interactive and interesting (water balloons and bombs). The balls interact differently in contact with each of those objects, which means each one has its unique properties.

This expansion is compatible with Carom Golf, Carom Mini Golf 3D, and Carom Mini Golf 2D.

—description from the publisher

Casus Belli is a game of exploration, settlement, and warfare in space.

Over a thousand years ago, humanity spread to the stars, creating a massive empire. This empire grew too big and split into four main factions, a period now known as the 'Great Split', which then led to a time of war. Conflict led to an era of darkness for humanity. Now after many years, the four faction have started to expand again. How they act is determined by by their prevailing ideology, who knows if this will lead to a new war.

Each faction will explore and colonize the systems in the region. In doing so, they will come across ancient alien technologies. However, some of these technologies will lead to corruption. The game starts in a period of peace. Some actions may lead to an increase in tension, which in turn may lead to war. While peace reigns, factions cannot fight each other...unless they have a casus belli. Once war breaks out, the factions will divide into alliances and major battles can occur.

The game plays over seven to nine rounds, and at game's end, each faction scores differently for assets they have on the board.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Have you ever dreamed of using your magical prowess to conquer the lands? Flex those magical muscles in this game of magical dominance over the mystical land of Waywren!

This is a strategy board game of conquest and character growth. Choose to grow your mage's magical prowess to master more powerful spells for late game conquest, or just spread your magic as profusely as possible early on throughout the land of Waywren. Beware the other mages that seek magical dominion in the same domain!

This game features the choice of team play, a hex tile based board that changes for each game play, unique spells to cast, and can accommodate up to 6 players in the base version!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Duplicity: A Game of Shadowy Statecraft
description:
Duplicity is a medium-weight social strategy game where players employ negotiation, bribery, cunning, and foresight to claim the vacant throne of Lunalia.

Each player is a Regent, competing within and outside of the stateroom in order to gather Influence. Taking place over four rounds, each Regent spends time gathering resources, making deals, completing secret agendas, and managing their hand of cards in order to prepare for Summit, the defining moment in each round where players truly enter the stateroom. These actions gain players Influence in the five key Victory Categories, through which a player can win by achieving particular combinations of Influence.

—description from the publisher

A pen and paper game inspired by Tetris.

Players draw shapes on their paper, folding it when a line is fully filled in. Use this to your advantage to fill out previously inaccessible lines!

At the start of the game, a block card is revealed. This indicates the shape, and the column that the block "falls" down. Players can rotate the shape before filling it in using their pencil. The active player draws the shape in the indicated column, and each player to the left moves the column to the right once.

With the starting block drawn, the game proceeds with players drawing a block card on each turn, rotating and filling it in on their sheet.
When a row is fully filled in, the page is folded twice - so that only the filled in row disappears. This allows players to access rows that were previously blocked off. 
You get bonus points for folding a row - additionally, if no other player filled the row during the same round, they place an obstruction block on their sheet.

You can spend points acquired from filled rows to rotate the shapes, or to remove one square from the shape.

The game ends when there's only one player remaining. If multiple players survive, the player who folded the most rows is the winner!

The Little Tin Can that discovered flight in the hearts and minds of Urbie lovers (or haters) has finally found its proverbial wings in reality! Or at least a stronger set of jets. This new Salvage box comes complete with every stage related to its mysterious LAM origins. BattleMech, AirMech and Aerospace fighter modes are all included, along with a jump plume. So Run, Jump, and somehow fly across your tabletop now with the Little Tin Can that could.

Mechs: Urbie, Urbie, Urbie need we say more?

Includes: 1 Pilot Card, 1 Alpha Strike Card

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

In the serene Cotswolds village of Garenton, the discovery of a burning body throws the community into turmoil. The victim, Dr. Gilbert Sullivan, a beloved local figure, is believed to have been murdered, and his illegitimate son, Hendrix Jenkins, is swiftly charged with the crime. Now, two decades later, you are tasked with unraveling the truth.

As lead detective, you'll receive a comprehensive case file comprising over 40 meticulously crafted pieces of evidence, including crime scene photos, forensic reports, witness statements, and decades-old testimonies. Your mission is to re-examine the evidence, scrutinise the details, and determine if a miscarriage of justice occurred. Was Hendrix Jenkins truly guilty, or is the real killer still at large?

Trial by Fire challenges you to delve into the depths of the past, exploring the complexities of family dynamics, community secrets, and long-buried grudges. Your deductive skills will be put to the test as you sift through the evidence, identify viable suspects, and uncover the buried truths that have haunted Garenton for years.

boardgame: Freedom belongs to who looks for it
description:
Freedom is a 2 players fast paced tactical game.

The game is played on a hex tiled board. Both players will play on turns until one of them wins the game.

One player controls the dice, who are the prisoners. They will try to escape either reaching the black tile with one dice or two grey tiles.

The other player will control the meeple (or the meeples, in a game variant). They are the guardians of the prison and will try to prevent the prisoners from escaping.

Freedom is a game for 2 players, with 10 minutes games and from 8 years old.

—description from the designer

Everyone’s going to hide! Let’s get ready for an incredible game of Hide and Seek in the park. Our friend the Wolf is counting, so it’s up to you to find the best hiding places, but watch out for the Wolf, who can look from anywhere. Has he spotted you? No? Well done, you win sweets!

Develop fine motor skills and spatial perception


     An impressive set up to play a real game of hide-and-seek
     A wolf mask to immerse children on the "seek and find" part


—description from the publisher

Deep below the surface is the Underworld ruled mostly by the minotaur.  Surrounded by the crushing darkness, unlike the dwarves, they embraced the powers of evil.

This pack contains; 1 Adventurer, 1 Quest, 40 Crawler, 40 Dungeon cards, a total of 34 images that are all unique to this set.
