This 82 non-random themed card pack is used to expand your Dungeon Crawler™ gaming experience.  A bonus 8 promotional cards will be included in this deck.

Naval Battle is a strategic type board game for kids and families. Mechanics of the play are similar to classic ‘Stratego’ game with some additional features and naval specifics.

The game for 2 players, simple rules, minimum randomness, 1hr play.

Crossroads: Ancient Sagas (Vikings), is a strategic card game that transports you to the heart of Norse mythology. Command legendary Viking warriors, mythical beings, and powerful abilities as you battle for dominance on the battlefield.

How to Play
In Crossroads: Ancient Sagas, players take turns drawing and placing cards on a game board with 16 tiles. The objective is to control the most cards on the board by the end of the game.

Here’s a brief overview of the gameplay:

Draw a Card: At the beginning of your turn, draw a card from your deck.

Place Gold Cards: Use gold cards from your hand to pay the cost of placing other cards on the battlefield.

Deploy Units: Place attack cards on empty tiles to capture opponent's cards by outmaneuvering them with higher attack values.

Activate Abilities: Cast ability cards for special effects and discard them after use.

Capture and Control: Capture adjacent enemy cards and turn them to your color by defeating them in battle.

End of Turn: Pass the turn to your opponent.

Strategy and Fun
The game is designed to be easy to learn but offers deep strategic gameplay. Each turn presents new opportunities for strategic deployment and tactical maneuvers. The unique card orientation, color representation, and deck composition rules ensure that every game is a fresh challenge, with no two games being the same.

—description from the designer

boardgame: MINISTRY: The Fast-Moving Time Managing Field Service Game
description:
Players acquire field service territory by participating in the ministry.
     Through hard work add return visits which are converted into bible studies.
     Test your Bible skills with the Treasures from God cards and get tripped up with the Unforeseen Occurrence cards.
     The last player left wins the Ministry Game and continues to be in Jehovah's favor!

Tussie mussies exemplified the Victorian custom of assigning meaning to the flowers that friends and lovers exchanged. Inspired by the ideals of elegance and discretion, these bouquets were carefully made to convey subtle messages to their recipients. Now you can choose the right flowers to make a winning tussie mussie of your own!

Tussie-Mussie is based on a Victorian fad that assigned meanings to the flowers that friends and lovers exchanged.

Featuring I-Divide-You-Choose drafting, this microgame of 18 cards is played over three rounds. In turn, players look at the top two cards of the deck, then offer them, one face-up and one facedown, to an opponent.  That opponent takes one, leaving the other for the active player.

A round ends when each player has four cards, at which point the scores are tallied. The highest score after three rounds wins!

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Designed by CSR award winning designer Keith Tracton, RDZ – Raider Drop Zone – The Assault on Planet Kraken is a two-player game of company, regimental and divisional combat action between the elite human Terran Fleet Raider Forces and the dreaded alien Kraken, set in the science-fiction universe of Firepower Pass.
As the Terran Fleet Raider commander, you will drop your Raider companies using drop capsules while landing your Supporting Medium Armor companies, Combat Engineer platoons, and sophisticated “Tunnel Detector” Ground Sonar sections in Terran Fleet planetary assault landing boats. Coordinate your forces to root out and destroy the subterranean defenders.
As the Kraken Supreme Neuro-Director (The “Only Mind”), you will coordinate your Dragoon soldier swarms, Storm Armor Heavy Weapon Vehicle swarms, and unarmed but seemingly numberless Drone Legion mega-swarms to defend your home world. Alternating a kaleidoscope of surprise surface raids with juggernaut assaults may annihilate the soft-skinned invaders, or at the very least inflict enough casualties to force them to withdraw….

—description from the designer

The players are different clans of Vikings who will work together to get treasures and get around the island before winter arrives. Depending on the points they get in the game, the objective will be better met at the end.

The players play cards, and move the ship around the island before the sun arrives at the winter´s marker , in this travel the players can take treaures for getting victory points at the end of the game.
Also in our travel we can move by land throwin a dice, dependig the results we can get tresaures or move trthe track od fthe sun to winter.

Coral Sea was the first of the Pacific War’s great carrier versus carrier battles. It was the result of an Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) offensive into the South Pacific, with their South Seas Fleet transporting invasion forces to Port Moresby (at New Guinea) and Tulagi (off Guadalcanal). Their strategic objective was to cut Allied lines of communication to Australia. The US Navy (USN) moved to counter and inflict a defeat on the Japanese fleet. The ensuing actions saw each side launch aircraft carrier strikes against opposing task forces. While the battle ended in an IJN tactical victory, the USN won strategically as the New Guinea invasion force broke off and returned to its base. Coral Sea set the stage for carrier battles which would pay off at Midway a month later.

Coral Sea Deluxe Edition is a one-player (solitaire) wargame of the decisive aero-naval battle that took place between the USN and the IJN in May 1942. The Coral Sea Deluxe Edition design is based on the system used in World at War magazine #2’s Solomons Campaign; however, there are significant differences owing to the different scale of the Coral Sea operation and the rules required for a solitaire design. The game system controls IJN forces via naval and air doctrine tables which initiate a range of strategies.

The player controls various Allied forces, collectively called the USN, through an operational system based on US military staff organization which provides a wide range of actions that can be conducted on the game map. The player can react to various threats and launch counterattacks to exploit opportunities as they occur. This is your chance to fight and win one of the decisive battles of World War II in the Pacific.

—description from the publisher

Make sure the kids are tucked into bed, because things are about to get down and dirty.
Guess the dirty word or phrase from the card on your head as your friends yell obscene clues at you. What would your BFF say to make you guess "beef curtains"? How would you describe a "mangina"? 
The only thing worse than getting the answer wrong is having to explain it.

Submachine is a competitive card game. Gameplay consists of building a maze of rooms with other players. The goal is to be the first to play all cards from your hand.

It's based on the video game series of the same name. This puzzle and adventure series, created over a 10-year span from 2005 to 2015 has captivated the imagination of millions of players. It was brought to the big leagues with a complete remaster released on Steam in 2023.

One of the players takes on the role of the dealer. The deck is shuffled and each player gets dealt 9 cards. 
The first player after the dealer places one room card on the table. This card initiates the rooms grid shared by all players. If the first player doesn't have a room card - the next player with a room card starts the grid.

Players take turns to place their room cards from hand to the table, assembling a grid of rooms one by one. They also use functional cards to manipulate the rooms grid, block or open passages, create new passages, or even block target players.

The game ends when one of the players wins by placing last card from their hand.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Fall of Kingdoms: World of Crisis
description:
Facing a crisis, will you unite or remain divided?

The World of Crisis Expansion introduces thrilling events that impact every player at the table.

These events add an extra layer of complexity, altering the gameplay dynamics. Amid the conflict, will you band together or remain divided?

Will you sacrifice yourself for the group's benefit, or will you exploit the situation to secretly undermine everyone else? The stage is set.

The choice is yours.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Fall of Kingdoms: Call of the Divine
description:
The Call of the Divine Expansion introduces a simple yet massively fun new mechanic to the base game.

With this expansion, players can call upon the Divine Cards in two scenarios:

     when under attack, or
     during their turn.


Each player may invoke the Divine up to three times per game, and it does NOT count as an action.

Upon calling, the player rolls a die to determine if the Divine
responds. If successful, the player can choose from one of three
piles of Divine Cards: Protection, Boost, or Wild. The effect of the chosen Divine Card takes immediate effect - for better or worse.

—description from the designer

A quirky collection of superheroes, each with a unique ability, fight to the death in an ever-changing labyrinth. The gameplay resembles chess, but with four players instead of two, a maze on the board, and pieces who can shoot. The goal is to be the first player to shoot two enemy heroes.

—description from the designer

Big reversal with special rules!? Contains 4 "Gian Recital" cards!

Classic UNO with beloved Doraemon anime characters on the cards and a special Gian Recital card unique to this version. When it is played, you have to cover your ears because Gian's singing is terrible!

Mini expansion including 3 new painting cards for the game.
This expansion was added to base game for Kickstarter backers.

The 3 cards are:


     Horizon's Glow. This card is a Boat card. It has numbers 0/8, which means it can be a 0 or 8. It also has a visitor icon on the card. If the card scores, the player scores one additional point. 
     Lotus of Promenade
     Blue Sea
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

boardgame: A game about words that rhyme, with terrible rules on a line.
description:
You get 48 game cards + rules for 6 simple games.

Game cards have a topic on one side, and 5 rhyming words on the other.

One game is more like an 'activity to provide opportunities for children to learn'. Another is more like a creativity prompt.

'Rhyming Soup, Good for a Group' and 'Keep the Rhyme going; Until Someone starts Pausing' tell players to improvise rhymes - even if nonsensical - and any delays result in a penalty card.

'Search for Rhymes Quickly; Shout Triumphantly' 
is about being the first to find a rhyme that matches the card (but is not listed on it).

'Compete through a Test; of Who Rhymes the Best' is about coming up with a rhyme that the judge will reward. The winner becomes the next judge.

A Monopoly on the theme of Haute-Loire, a French department located in the mountains of the Massif Central. The properties are replaced by towns in Haute-Loire. The stations are replaced by agri-food companies, particularly in relation to the green lens of Puy-en-Velay. The game contains a special dice to reduce the duration of a game.

boardgame: A game about taking turns, bidding on stuff, aiming for majorities.
description:
The flower spirit is spreading seeds all around, giving them to whoever promises the most water.

Each turn, 2-4 random flowers are flipped and players take turns bidding to take those flower, plus a bonus water card (adding to your currency).

at the end of the game, each flower is worth 1pt, but any flower types that form a majority score double.

The game ends when 7 of one flower have come out, which could theoretically happen after 3 auctions or after 11.

You can never make change.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A game about mini missions to maximise joy.
description:
51 game cards give you different 'missions' to do. Usually this will be something involving other objects around you (like finding & touching plants) and sometimes it'll be a task on the card (like connecting some dots).

For missions that don't involve writing/drawing on the cards or giving them away somehow, you'll tick a little checkbox.

When one side is fully completed, you can flip the card to start doing the 'side B' missions and once that side is complete, get rid of the card.

Each day: draw 3 cards, choose one mission to do, then after you've done it put those cards to the back.

Each day you get a new choice of 3 cards as you cycle through the cards.

Once you get rid of 26 cards, you win the game!

—description from the designer

Dracula is stalking you & he's thirsty for your blood!
Any number of players can play this game, only the first player to reach the safety of the inn will be saved from the vampire, because the entrance to the inn is hung all around with garlic & he will not go in there!
Roll a 6 to start & the exact number to finish & always obey the instructions along the way.

The game was published in the annual Dracula's Spinechillers which was published in 1982.

Hey Coaches!
It’s me, Smalker, here to guide you through this special competition of the Legendary Sponsor League!
Hope you’re all ready for action, because the fans are already buzzing!

As Coaches, you have one clear mission: send your Smashers onto the field to demolish the opposing team or to rocket the ball straight into their Smash Zone. No matter the method, the goal is to win!

Before each showdown, the Sponsors (our beloved organizers) will allocate Credits to you. Use them to recruit Smashers, expand your team size, or buy equipment. In your recruitment, you’ll have a choice among a bunch of Smashers, but most importantly, you might come across Legends! They are the best of the best!

But talent doesn’t come cheap! So, you’ll also have to deal with Rookies! So, you’ll also have to deal with Rookies! Unknowns who are looking to make a name for themselves, but mostly serve as cannon fodder for the greatest pleasure of the fans!

After the recruitment, it’s match time! Each Coach sends their best team to face their round’s opponent and deals with the outcome. Then, you restart the recruitment to beef up your team with the earned credits and move on to new matches! The competition continues like this, match after match, until a champion is crowned!

Do you think you have what it takes to make your mark in the Legendary Sponsor League? Then prove it and smash your best!

—description from the publisher

You are the citizens of the Eggria Kingdom, which has seen remarkable progress in the field of primary agriculture. With great honor, you have been entrusted with the responsibility of managing a poultry farm under the king's decree, shaping your future ahead. The king has promised to bestow the title of 'Chicken King' upon the entrepreneur who earns the most victory points within one year! To outperform other entrepreneurs, you must deliver numerous chickens, chicks, and eggs while they are fresh (alive). Now, let's work for the king amidst the beautiful landscapes of the Eggria Kingdom!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Воины сакуры: Первые шаги (Sakura Arms: Basic Set)
description:
Воины сакуры ("Sakura Arms") — known in Japanese as 桜降る代に決闘を and also released as Sakura Arms — is a two-player dueling game in which players first choose two of the included megami (Japanese goddesses), each of which has a different keyword which empowers some of their cards. Players then see what the opponent chose before assembling a deck of ten out of 22 cards, with the players choosing cards both to take advantage of their own megami powers and to exploit their opponent's weaknesses. Players then duel to see who will be victorious.

The game uses a single kind of token to represent life, distance, aura (defense), and flare (special/gauge). The tokens represent this based on the zone they occupy, by moving tokens between zones you attempt to gain the ideal position and set up to use as many attacks as possible, or be prepared to avoid attacks.

The gameplay at its core is simple, and due to its limited environment, it is relatively easy to learn the cards for a deck-construction game. Building a deck and playing normally takes less then 20 minutes.

Lavka Games has teamed up with BakaFire Party to bring the 3rd edition of 桜降る代に決闘を(A Duel for Falling Cherry Blossoms) to Russian. This version features a last rulebook, two starting training decks, and some improved production quality.

Includes 6 start and medium megami and everything you need to play:

     Yurina (Юрина)
     Saine (Саинэ)
     Himika (Химика)
     Tokoyo (Токоё)
     Oboro (Оборо)
     Yukihi (Юкихи)


—user summary

Un Metro de Risa is a family board game designed by Educa that promises fun and laughter for players of all ages. The game features a simple and entertaining dynamic, making it ideal for family gatherings and children's parties.

Game Objective:
The main goal of "Un Metro de Risa" is to be the first player to complete a one-meter path on the game board. To advance, players must overcome a series of fun challenges and tests that will put their creativity, wit, and sense of humor to the test.

Game Mechanics:
On each turn, players roll a die that determines the type of challenge they must complete. Challenges can range from charades and drawing to answering curious and funny questions. Success in each challenge allows the player to advance on the board, while mistakes may cause them to move back or lose their turn.

Highlights:

Social Interaction: Encourages interaction and communication among players.
Guaranteed Fun: Ideal for families with young children, offering moments of laughter and joy.
Varied Challenges: Diverse challenges keep the game dynamic and exciting.
Un Metro de Risa is perfect for those family game nights where fun and laughter take center stage. Get ready to enjoy endless challenges and have a great time with your loved ones!

boardgame: Воины сакуры: Мастера дуэли (Sakura Arms: Master Set)
description:
Воины сакуры ("Sakura Arms") — known in Japanese as 桜降る代に決闘を and also released as Sakura Arms — is a two-player dueling game in which players first choose two of the included megami (Japanese goddesses), each of which has a different keyword which empowers some of their cards. Players then see what the opponent chose before assembling a deck of ten out of 22 cards, with the players choosing cards both to take advantage of their own megami powers and to exploit their opponent's weaknesses. Players then duel to see who will be victorious.

The game uses a single kind of token to represent life, distance, aura (defense), and flare (special/gauge). The tokens represent this based on the zone they occupy, by moving tokens between zones you attempt to gain the ideal position and set up to use as many attacks as possible, or be prepared to avoid attacks.

The gameplay at its core is simple, and due to its limited environment, it is relatively easy to learn the cards for a deck-construction game. Building a deck and playing normally takes less then 20 minutes.

Lavka Games has teamed up with BakaFire Party to bring the 3rd edition of 桜降る代に決闘を(A Duel for Falling Cherry Blossoms) to Russian. This version features a last rulebook, two starting training decks, and some improved production quality.

Includes 6 medium and hard megami and everything you need to play:

     Shinra (Шинра)
     Hagane (Хаганэ)
     Chikage (Чикаге)
     Kururu (Куруру)
     Raira (Райра)
     Thallya (Сарья)


—user summary

boardgame: クロニクル・オブ・ジャパン ～邪馬台国から明治維新まで～ (Chronicle of Japan)
description:
A fast-paced wargame based on Japanese history from Yamataikoku (c. 1st century – c. 3rd century) to Satsuma Rebellion (1877). Basic concept inspired by History of the World.

For each of the six ages, players choose a power, which determines additional bonuses and events, as well as the order in which powers are selected for the next age. In the final age, players also choose a final bonus card.

Resolve power invasions based on the order in which each power appeared in history. When a power has placed all of its army and navy units, it tallies victory points based on its control level for each area.

The winner is determined by the total victory points earned in the six ages and the bonus points at the end of the game.



Issued game for Game Journal #91.

Marsh Hallow is a cooperative flip and write dungeon crawler game played over 18 rounds (each round is referred to as an hour of The Ritual).

Players draw cards and use revealed animals, represented as shapes, from the Animal Deck to navigate the Marshmallow Cultist campground within Marsh Hallow.

To win, players must reach the Pyre before the end of the Ritual and have enough water to douse the flame to save their families.

—description from the designer

Become a Phoenician trader with "Tyre, Again"!
In this fascinating board game, children are transformed into cunning Phoenician traders, sailing their ships through the ancient Mediterranean in search of the most valuable products of the time. "Tiro, otra vez" combines fun and education, offering an interactive experience with which we learn History, Mathematics and Geography, while developing our executive and socioemotional functions in an entertaining and engaging way.
Explore the most emblematic cities, learn about the rich Phoenician heritage and challenge your skills as you fill your warehouse with the products of the past. Embark on this exciting adventure and discover the Phoenician merchant in you!


¡Conviértete en un comerciante fenicio con "Tiro, otra vez"!
En este fascinante juego de mesa, los niños y niñas se transforman en astutos comerciantes fenicios, navegando con sus barcos por el antiguo Mediterráneo en busca de los productos más valiosos de la época. "Tiro, otra vez" combina diversión y educación, ofreciendo una experiencia interactiva con la que aprendemos Historia, Matemáticas y Geografía, al tiempo que desarrollamos nuestras funciones ejecutivas y socioemocionales de manera entretenida y atractiva.
Explora las ciudades más emblemáticas, aprende sobre la rica herencia fenicia y desafía tus habilidades mientras completas tu almacén con los productos del pasado. ¡Embárcate en esta emocionante aventura y descubre el comerciante fenicio que llevas dentro!

The Valley Falls Gardening Club is comprised of some of the city’s most elite and influential women. They have cultivated a prim and proper image while running the Valley Falls Botanical Garden. Now, they are preparing to celebrate the blooming of a rare corpse flower, a plant that only blooms once every year or two. 
The celebration is put on hold, however, when the body of the garden club president is found buried in a flower bed. 
While the overbearing Rose, as she was so ironically named, was disliked by most, it's unclear who hated her enough to want her dead. It’s time to grab a spade and dig deep to uncover the truth of A Rose-Colored Murder.

Volleyball draws some of Championland's strongest and speediest athletes to battle each other across the net. While pure physical ability can make a difference, true masters of the court know how to place the ball perfectly to draw their opponent out of position and enable that game-winning spike.

In Championland: Volleyball, players take turns placing a single card to one of their 3 hit spots. Each card played must extend a run of consecutive cards in that spot. Players compare their hit spots against the opposing player’s to determine who is currently leading: the player with the most consecutive cards (or the highest card, if both players have only one) is leading. Some cards have special abilities that control where the opponent can play on their next turn.

If a player is leading in two spots at the start of their turn, or the opposing player can’t make a legal move on theirs, they win the round. The first player to win 3 rounds is the champion!


Championland is a series of standalone 18-card games which uses a single rules system for all entries, then adds individual rules for each specific game to capture the experience of that sport.

Every Championland game includes two 9-card decks numbered 1-9. Each player takes a deck, shuffles it, and fans it out in their hand. On a turn, they’ll play only a single card and the card played MUST come from the furthest edge of their hand on either side. Players cannot reorder their hand, so they must make the best of the choices they have available!
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
The winds whisper of untold riches hidden upon the fabled island of Gold n’ Grog, drawing the attention of the cunning Maritime Merchants. These savvy traders have brought forth a treasure trove of their own – a vast array of powerful wares fit for any pirate worth their salt. Will you trade the treasures of Gold n’ Grog for the merchants' wares and claim your place as the wealthiest pirate on the island?

—description from the designer

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

The dhampir Andmar -- half human, half vampire -- has long succumbed to her monstrous nature. Channeling ancient sorcery, she cleaved her very soul in two to spawn Daema, a nightmarish reflection of Andmar's true self. Though they are split apart, these bloodthirsty fiends think and hunt as one.

Components:

     Andmar Hero set: 1 Hero, 1 Health, 1 Minion, 3 Action cards.
     Andmar AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

Antithesis is an innovative combination of a card game and a war game, designed for competitive 1v1 play. It also has great potential for additional game modes that have not begun to be explored. The game unfolds on a gridded map of 1-inch squares, measuring 27 inches wide and 21 inches deep. Players engage in strategic battles, blending the unpredictability of card draws with the tactical depth of miniature warfare.

In Antithesis, players choose champions from various factions, each granting access to unique cards that can be added to their decks. Like a traditional trading card game, players draw and play cards. However, the twist is that each card played also brings a corresponding figure onto the game board. This figure represents the card and is placed on the grid, ready to be moved and utilized in battle.

Once on the board, these figures can move and attack as in a traditional war game. Additionally, players can use cards to introduce terrain effects, dynamically altering the battlefield and adding layers of strategic complexity. This integration of card mechanics with board-based movement creates a unique and engaging gameplay experience, where adaptability is key.

A distinctive feature of Antithesis is its innovative use of dice. Unlike traditional miniatures games where dice rolls introduce randomness at the end of your decision tree, Antithesis employs the Dice Bank system. Dice are rolled when you obtain them and are then stored in the Dice Bank as a resource. Players openly manage these dice to play cards, power attacks, or activate card abilities. This system adds a strategic layer to resource management, allowing players to plan and execute their moves more deliberately, reducing the reliance on randomized outcomes.

As the game progresses, players maneuver their figures to control key areas of the board that become critical for earning victory points. Keeping opponents out of these areas while securing them for yourself is essential to winning the game. Players can attack their opponent's cards or figures, employing various strategies to gain an advantage. Whether you prefer aggressive assaults or defensive tactics, Antithesis offers a diverse range of playstyles to master.

—description from the designer

At it's heart Reciprocity: The Quite Revolution is a cooperative experience, you all succeed or fail together.  The idea of reciprocity is to help others without the expectation of receiving anything in return.

Through Reciprocity players will take ideas and turn them into solutions.  These will solve epoch challenges across three ages. Managing your environment is just as important as solving the puzzles, without careful resource management you will find yourselves in a dead-end, unable to solve the last challenge.

Players take turn moving around an island gathering ideas.  They help each other solve turn these ideas into solutions. This all comes together to ensure the players succeed together.

—description from the designer

Explore the weird and fabulous lives of the Catholic saints in this unique game for 2-4 players that condenses the full complexity and strategy of a collectible card game into a single box.  Lightning-fast gameplay and high-stakes decisions mean every moment counts, while strange abilities reward players that use creative strategies.

Each player has a hand of 3 or 4 saints (depending on player count), but each saint has multiple abilities, passive effects, and effects while dead.  Many combos and synergies emerge from just a few such cards.  The saint cards are huge -- much bigger than tarot cards -- and typically have 4-5 abilities each.

Xoli is an abstract board game, it is one of the smallest games in Mexico, the game board has 16 cells, 4 squares x 4 squares, and it has only 9 pieces to play it, Xoli is a game that represents the 3 main cultures of Mexico, the Mayan , the Aztec and the Novohispana, this game is commonly played with 2 players, but it has a very dynamic version for 4 players, the objective is to make a line of 3 pieces horizontally or vertically, never diagonally, in the game there are 9 pieces in 3 different colors, each of the players takes 3 pieces of the same color and with their color they will make their lines of 3 pieces adding movements by relating them along the rows or columns, the 3 remaining pieces are also placed on the board, the players will be able to move the 9 pieces during their turn, and they will do so depending on the number of moves that have been obtained, with the maximum number of moves being 3 and the minimum being 1, they can also use the remaining 3 pieces, that is, for each relationship that have the pieces of their color along the columns or rows, the player will increase moves on his next turn, but..., you must be careful that your opponent does not take away moves from you..., although the game is small in size, is big in strategy!

—description from the designer

Fried Potatoes – Deep Fried Edition

				
				
					OverviewLove fries, chaos, and unexpected twists? Then Fried Potatoes – Deep Fried Edition is the card game for you! This fast-paced, trick-taking party game is all about bluffing, frying, and outsmarting your opponents. Only one player can escape the fryer and claim the title of ultimate crispy champion.

				
				
					How to PlayEach round consists of four tricks, but only the final trick matters. Win that last trick and you avoid penalty points. Lose it, and into the fryer you go!

Specialty cards keep players on edge by bending and breaking the rules. The Marionette and The Magnetic Pulse seize control in unexpected ways, ensuring that no two games ever play out the same.

This edition turns up the heat: Deep Fried introduces specialty cards that not only affect the game itself, but also the players. Forget safe strategies—here, no one is ever fully in control.

				
				
					Trilogy TwistDeep Fried Potatoes can be played on its own or combined with the other editions of the Fried Potatoes Trilogy for even more chaotic fun. Each edition is a complete game with unique specialty cards and twists. Mix them together for the ultimate fried free-for-all!

				
				
					Key Mechanics
     Trick-taking with a twist: only the final trick of the round matters  
     Specialty cards flip the game, the rules, or even the players themselves  
     Bluff, strategise, and adapt to chaos to stay out of the fryer  
     Combine editions for even more variety and unpredictability  


				
				
					Gameplay Facts
     2–6 players | Ages 7+ | 15–25 minutes  
     Easy to learn in under 5 minutes  
     Compact and portable: premium box with magnetic closing  
     Always different, always hilarious  


				
				
					Components
     32 Potato cards (values 1–8 in 4 colours, each with colour-blindness indicators)  
     24 Specialty cards unique to the Deep Fried Edition  
     Rulebook  
     Premium box with magnetic closing

Fried Potatoes – Burned Edition

				
				
					OverviewLove fries, laughter, and unexpected twists? Then Fried Potatoes – Burned Edition is for you. This fast-paced, chaotic trick-taking game is all about bluffing, frying, and strategy. Who will be crowned the ultimate crispy champion?

				
				
					How to PlayEach round consists of four tricks, but only the winner of the final trick avoids the fryer! Think ahead, read your opponents, and play smart to stay in the game.

Specialty cards flip the action upside down. SuperStar boosts your play with dazzling power, while Kamikaze Onion burns everything down and changes the round completely. Every card can turn the tide, so no game ever plays out the same.

Burned Fried Potatoes combines strategy with pure anarchy, perfect for players who love tactics but aren’t afraid of a little chaos.

				
				
					Trilogy TwistThis edition can be played on its own or combined with the other Fried Potatoes games. Each edition introduces its own set of specialty cards and unique twists. Mix them together for even more fried-potato mayhem!

				
				
					Key Mechanics
     Trick-taking with a twist: only the final trick matters each round  
     Specialty cards can bend, boost, or completely break the rules  
     Balance tactical play with unpredictable chaos  
     Combine with other editions for maximum variety  


				
				
					Gameplay Facts
     2–6 players | Ages 7+ | 15–25 minutes  
     Easy to learn in under 5 minutes  
     Compact and premium: box with magnetic closing, perfect for travel  
     Always unpredictable, always hilarious  


				
				
					Components
     32 Potato cards (values 1–8 in 4 colours, each with colour-blindness indicators)  
     24 Specialty cards unique to the Burned Edition  
     Rulebook  
     Premium box with magnetic closing

A micro expansion of Rise of Babel. Innovations introduces 2 new mechanics, a hidden goal/objective mechanic and a flip to sell tokens off the tower mechanic.

—description from the publisher

The backyard housing market in Johannesbug was fine until the lawn was laid. Now every bug needs a six-figure down payment to wrangle even the most humble abode. Add to that, a competing couple seems to be making bids faster than you and yours, so hurry and make an offer!

Bug House Millionaire is a climbing/shedding game where you'll be working with a partner to corner the bug housing market! Players will play 2 simultaneous, 2 player climbing/shedding games where the cards they capture will go to the hand of their parter. They'll also have to hurry as the players are on the clock and the player who sheds out first wins, or has their opponent's clock run out.

—description from the designer

It’s the annual battle of the bands in Chaipour’s number one jazz cafe! Your band is tasked with covering the greatest hits better than the rest. You've just trimmed your claws and you're ready to tickle the ivories and the brasseries and the silveries. So grab the song menu, pick just the right song at just the right time, and blow away all those cool cats!

Chai Tea Jazz is a trick-taking game where players will be fulfilling the same contracts as the other players and seeing which player did the best job at it. In this modern take on Coiffeur Jass, players bet on which mode of play and the strength of their cards, then see how they fare in the card play. It's all about knowing when to sit back and when to make your move.

—description from the publisher

Once in a millennium, the millennium gate emerges and release powerful cosmic energies that shatter the planet and attract the greatest heroes who want to claim the power.  They have to battle in a dangerous realm where they are constantly attacked by unimaginable powerful mythical beasts that are spawned to protect the Gate.  Whoever shall claim the Millennium Gate, shall make the legacy, the Millennium Gate Legacy.
Assemble your team of 3 elemental heroes out of 21 heroes' pool. to go head-to-head with your opponent. Prepare weapons and spells to upgrade, protect your hero from enemy and beasts. 
But beware, time is limited and it's just a matter of time the beast eliminates your hero from the battle.

—description from the publisher

Michi Maguitos is a competitive game for 2 to 4 players where they seek to demonstrate which is the most powerful Michi Maguito in a changing duel arena using a variety of spells.
Use one of the game's characters, enhance their ability and become the best Hunter, Dualist or Survivor to win.
The players will be located in one of the 4 parts of the board in a secret way from the other players, at the beginning of their turn they draw a card and have one action per turn, draw an extra card, heal themselves with a spell card or try to hunt down an enemy player, for the latter he reveals his location on the board and if an opponent is located there there is a combat, 1 to 3 spells are cast per combat at the decision of the duelists, these being Healing, Defense or Attack, if the damage received is greater than Michi Maguito's health, he is defeated and the player is eliminated from the game, otherwise both players are left with less health until they can recover in a later turn. At the end of the turn the player can move, thus hiding his new position, and pass the turn to the next player. At the end of each round the board is reduced until there is only one part in play, thus increasing the confrontations until only one player is left standing.

Michi Maguitos es un Juego competitivo de 2 a 4 jugadores donde buscan demostrar cuál es el Michi Maguito más poderoso en una arena cambiante de duelo y usando variedad de hechizos.
Usa uno de los personajes del juego,potencia y aprovecha su habilidad y logra ser el mejor Cazador, Dualista o Superviviente para llevarte la victoria.
Los jugadores estarán situados en alguno de las 4 partes del tablero de manera secreta para los demás jugadores, al inicio de su turno roban una carta y tienen una acción por turno, robar una carta extra, curarse con una carta de hechizo o intentar cazar a un jugador enemigo, para esto último revela su ubicación del tablero y si algún contrincante está allí situado hay un combate, se lanzan de 1 a 3 hechizos por combates a decisión de los duelistas, siendo estos de Curación, Defensa o Ataque, si el daño recibido es mayor a la salud del Michi Maguito, este queda derrotado y el jugador es eliminado de la partida sino ambos jugadores quedan con menos salud hasta lograr recuperarse en algún turno posterior.
Al final del turno el jugador puede moverse ocultando así su nueva posición y sede el turno al siguiente jugador.
Al final de cada ronda el tablero se va reduciendo hasta ser 1 sola parte en juego aumentando así los enfrentamientos hasta que solo quede un jugador en pie.

In Disney Lorcana: Shimmering Skies, the realm of Lorcana holds a great festival to celebrate its recent victory over Ursula, which concluded the previous set, Ursula's Return. While most of Lorcana participates in the vibrant, fun-filled activities, a new mystery begins as our Illumineers search for a way to repair the worsening damage Ursula caused to the Great Illuminary.

Shimmering Skies features Disney's Wreck-It Ralph with the introduction of characters like Vanellope Von Schweetz and Fix-it Felix, Jr.

Shimmering Skies is the fifth set in the Disney Lorcana TCG line-up, and cards in this set can be used on their own or combined with other Disney Lorcana sets.

On Guard was published in the 1880's. The publisher was Jaques & Son.
Round Game for Four or more Players.
Before commencing, a pool should be formed, each of the players contributing an equal number of counters. The dealer then deals round the cards to the players. The object of the game is for each of the players to collect into his hand the proper cards required for the formation or mobilisation of an Army Corps.
An Army Corps shall consist of the following thirteen cards:—
The Staff Card.
One Regiment of Artillery or Engineers.
Two Regiments of Cavalry.
Three Regiments of Infantry.
A Regiment consists of two cards, one representing the officers and the other the men.

In order to give the game a military character, each of the players should select or be allotted one of the follow- names of Garrison Depots or Towns, by which name they are to be addressed if called on. A forfeit should put into the pool if the names
are called wrongly;
London, Aldershot, Windsor, Portsmouth, Plymouth, Colchester, The Curragh, York, Dover and Shorncliffe.
In order to avoid mistake, the chosen name should be written on a piece of paper, and given to the player.

After the deal, the players should take up their cards, and the player to the left of the dealer commences by asking any of the players for a card he may require toward the completion of his Army. If he is successful, he continues asking for any others from any of the players he chooses, until he fails to draw the card asked for. In asking for a card he must always use the following form of address:— “I call out the (officers or men) of the [Royal Welsh Fusiliers] from [Aldershot).” If the player called Aldershot has not the card asked for in his hand, he replies, “Not at station named,” and the turn passes to that player. Every player who asks for the Staff Card without obtaining it, shall pay a counter to the pool. The game continues until any player has his Army complete, when he declares he is ready for War, and lays down his cards on the table, and, if the cards are found correct, he takes the pool.

Another method of playing is for the players to try and pair up the cards into regiments, and the player who at the end of the game has the highest aggregate score to become the winner.

Feljong is a standalone game created with the aim of teaching the basic fundamentals of Riichi Mahjong (also known as Japanese mahjong) over several complexity levels, by using a deck of cards with the same configuration as a set of Riichi Mahjong tiles. The standard game has 4 levels - you can proceed to the next level, or stay in the current level until you feel comfortable to add more rules (usually by adding more Yakus - ways of getting points in the game). Feljong also comes with the advanced rules of Riichi Mahjong, using cards instead of the standard tiles as components.

Following the same idea as Riichi Mahjong, in each round, 4 players are trying to win by forming a valid hand with at least one Yaku (a hand condition required to score points). These winning hands usually have 4 groups (melds) of 3 cards, either sequences or triplets, and a pair. However you only have 13 cards in your hand, so you will need to use an extra card discarded by a player, or a card drawn from the deck, in order to win.
On your turn you simply draw a card and discard a card, but there are some ways you can use other players' discards to play groups in front of you, though you can also choose to stay with your hand fully closed until it's time to win. Whoever manages to win a round receives points from the other players, either from everyone if you win using a card from the deck, or just from the player who discarded the card you needed.

The Raising Robots: Friends expansion introduces 3 new modules that may be added in separately or together in any combination:


     More Robots
     More Inventors
     Competitions & Friendships


—description from the designer

In the early 1920s there were a few major names in magic, Houdini, Kellar, Thurston, and Professor Conundrum. Tragically, the Professor perished during one of his most famous acts in 1922.  Foreseeing his own demise, he left his widow with a set of instructions so that she may communicate with him in the spirit world a year after his passing.  Now, Lady Conundrum is asking for your help to solve the Professor’s last great magical mystery so that she can once again speak with her departed love. Puzzle your way through a 1920s magician themed escape room in a box to unlock the final secrets of this master of sleight of hand!

Race around George Town as trishaw riders taking passengers on thrilling journeys in Penang.

Trishaw Frenzy is a fast-paced game of routes and riders with a unique blend of luck & strategy that makes which each round an exciting unpredictable experience.

Players take on the role of trishaw riders who roll dice and race along a route of map tiles. Along the way, they encounter a series of events and chaos with their passengers-- map tiles sometimes get rotated, and the route sometimes gets changed altogether!

Each round, the fastest rider to reach the end of the route gets 6 VPs, the 2nd get 4 VPs, the 3rd gets 2 VPs, and the 4th gets 1 VP. The rider with the highest total VP at the end of 3 rounds wins the game.

Bonus mini game: Flip the map tiles and put together the puzzle pieces to form 1 big picture.

The Gods of Mythologies from around the world (Egyptian, Roman, Greek, Nordic, Indian and Chinese mythologies)joined together to confront each other. 
The goal of the game is simple : complete a defined number of turns of the board depending on the playing time you want. On average, a turn on the board lasts 25 minutes. The winner will be the one who first reaches (or exceeds) the last square on the board at end of the different rounds.
Each player starts the game with 6 God cards and 6 power cards, rolling the dice and moving on the board where he can perform actions, using the power of his gods or his power cards.
The player will have to show cunning and ingenuity to win the crazy obstacle course waiting.

Welcome to the historic center of Kuala Lumpur! Step into the shoes of those who make the city's kaki lima (five-foot ways) come alive  with 'sticky' activities like food stalls, potted plants, street buskers, and shoe cobblers. These activities increase the 'stickiness' and vibrancy of a place by enticing pedestrians to stay and linger in the kaki lima.

Game Objective:
Score the most Vibrancy Points (VPs) by taking different actions to win the game.

Ways to Score VPs:

     WALK onto Kaki Lima with other Pedestrians 
     CREATE a Sticky Activity on a Kaki Lima
     ENJOY Sticky Activities on a Kaki Lima
     APPRECIATE a Pattern of Jom! Tiles & Sticky Activites
     At game end, ADD UP the Sticky Time of


all Kaki Lima that your Jom! Tiles & Pedestrian are on

Game Flow:

     Players take turns in clockwise order to roll 3 Dice for an x-number of rounds, x depending on number of players.
     Players use the Dice to take Actions.
     After marking down VPs gained on a turn, the 3 Dice gets passed to the next player.
     Replenish the line of Sticky Activities at the end of each turn.
     Total up VPs scored at the end of the game.


Turn Flow:

     Roll 3 Dice and choose 2.*
     Take 2 Actions with the 2 Dice.
     Other players take 1 Action with the remaining Die.
     Use any unlocked Bonus Abilities alongside Rolls & Actions.
     Mark down VPs gained into the Scoreboard.
    Use all 3 Dice on a triple roll!(Only you take actions this turn)


Game End & Scoring:
Total up all the VPs you have gained. The player with the highest VPs wins the game! Tie-breaker: the most Jom! Tiles on the Kaki Lima grid. If still tied, players share the victory!

—description from the designer

The Motorbike card is a fun poke at vehicles that often block the kaki lima in Malaysian towns and cities. Just like in real life, park the Motorbike on a Kaki Lima card and potentially frustrate other Pedestrians as they go about trying to create and enjoy sticky activities in Downtown KL or reach their destinations in George Town, Penang. Watch out that they don't repark it somewhere that would thwart your plans instead!

In Kaki Lima: Downtown KL, the 1st Player parks the Motorbike on any Kaki Lima card. Subsequently, players can repark the
Motorbike by subtracting any number of steps from their Die Value when they take a WALK Action.

Wherever the Motorbike is parked in DTKL, no new Sticky Activity can be created. Neither can existing Sticky Activities be enjoyed nor Pedestrians walk through that particular Kaki Lima. At game end, the Motorbike also blocks Pedestrians from gaining their ADD UP VPs from that Kaki Lima.

In Kaki Lima: George Town, the 1st player parks the Motorbike on any Clear Kaki Lima after placing their Pedestrian at the start. Subsequently, players can repark the Motorbike by subtracting any number of steps from Langkah cards that they             play, to move the Motorbike by that number of steps.

Wherever the Motorbike is parked in GT, that Kaki Lima is considered a Blocked Path. A Clearing token can be used to move the Motorbike to any other Blocked Kaki Lima, after which is returned to the pool of Clearing tokens.

—description from the designer

A Few Good Knights is a strategic game played across 6 or 9 rounds where players vie to impress the king by accruing Renown. Renown is earned through gathering Gold, Mana, and completing Item Sets. Wounds from Damage can lead to Renown loss and eventual elimination upon receiving a 5th Wound.

Each turn, players select 7 Adventure Dice to roll in order to collect resources and face Damage from Goblins, Warlords, and Bombs. To mitigate risks, players can purchase Items, Potions, and Gem Gamble Cards.

The player with the best mitigation strategies and most Renown will emerge victorious.

—description from the designer

Place all balls on the board. The balls have all the colors of the rainbow. The aim of the game is to strategically move a row of balls so that as many balls of the same color as possible are connected to each other. All adjacent balls may be collected: whoever collects the most, wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Aïllats: El Joc de les Balears
description:
Darkness extends across the Balearic Islands, and phantasmagorical souls possess the most iconic characters.
It's your time! Bring together an army, make yourself strong, and fight to get all four parts of the magical parchment and restore peace before it's too late... Do you dare to defend the heritage of our beloved islands?

In our turn, we will first throw away the dice of apparitions to see if some Aïllats card must be revealed, then we will move around the islands and depending on the box where we are located, different events will be held such as: take one or two cards, reveal an Aïllats card or teleport to another island through a magical portal.
Then we will play the cards of our hand being able to recruit troops in our playing area or playing the skills that some cards have, such as stealing troop cards from other players. Finally, if our meeple is on an island where there is an Aïllats card revealed, we will have to fight it.

To get the Aïllats cards we will have to recruit an army with our cards to fight them. That is why we will look at the total number of swords from our army and the health points of the Aïllats card as we will have to equal or overcome their health points. In case of not exceeding the health points we will throw the battle dice to try to get the Aïllats cards.

Aïllats is a board game of 2 to 4 players from the age of 10 with games of about 35 minutes.
The goal of the game is to be the first player to get four AÏLLATS cards with the pieces of the magic parchment.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood Red Roses: A Game of Battles in the Wars of the Roses
description:
Blood Red Roses is a simple though slightly unorthodox tabletop wargame primarily intended for two players to fight battles representative of the Wars of the Roses. It concentrates its attention at the level of the commanders and their wards, the major organisational divisions of armies at the time. Evidence for how battles of the period were fought is scant and sometimes contradictory. Aiming to accommodate as many differing perspectives as possible, the game takes a somewhat abstract approach in both its perspective and mechanisms.

Unless you choose to construct a scenario of your own design, the game presents a battle situation representative of those fought in the wars. The game generates the composition of your forces as, having issued a summons to battle, real-life commanders could not be sure precisely whom among their supporters would respond nor what forces they would bring. You will organise your armies mimicking the historical amalgamation of smaller contingents into the larger period-groupings of vaward, mainward, rearward and reserve, determining the size, composition and resolve of your wards.

Once deployed your troops primarily focus on the enemy that is in front of them, most having no opportunity to out-manoeuvre their opponents. Instead, you concentrate on the manner of your troops’ approach towards their enemy. You do this by assigning your wards one of a number of different ‘battle stances’. These stances dictate the options for movement and combat currently available to each. Some stances are more ‘aggressive’ than others and emphasise to differing degrees swift or purposeful movement and hand-to-hand combat whilst others are ‘less aggressive’ emphasising more measured movement and effective shooting. Stances can and should be changed as the battle progresses and you must consider the interplay of your stances with those of your opponent when striving for advantage. Bear in mind that having your wards on the desired stances at the appropriate times will not always be possible!

Neither the scale of the miniatures used nor the manner in which they are based has any importance to play of the game. The only consideration is that the various troop types should be readily distinguishable from one another. Each base or element of figures may represent as many real-life combatants as you wish it to as the abstract nature of the game makes a strict interpretation of numbers irrelevant. The game comprises a rulebook, a separate play-sheet and 72 bespoke playing cards that are primarily used to generate the opposing armies.

Winner of the Paul Morris Prize 2024 for innovative wargames.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Each of the 12 crime scenes tell you a story. Ask the suspects,examine the scenes to find clues an deduce who the killer is and what is his motive.

You're a detective and you must find the guilty.

Illustrated Crimes is a collection of gamebooks featuring different illustrated scenes, each of which presents a crime scene that you must investigate to solve the case.

Choose from different cases:
Each scene hides a story and a possible murderer. Analyze each clue and solve the mystery.

Look at each page carefully:
Who is the murderer in this train car?
Each crime scene tells a story, and only by uncovering this story will you be able to find the true perpetrator of the crime.

Access clues through labels:
What did the passengers have in their suitcases?
The different crime scenes are full of clues. Through the labels, you can go to other pages where you'll see even more specific details. You'll be able to figure out what happened if you look closely at all those details (objects, papers, messages, traces, etc.) and then, finally, all the pieces of the puzzle will fit together, and you'll discover the real killer and their motives.

Unleash your creativity by using every resource you can think of:
What does the German text mean? Where do those coordinates lead?
Be very creative in your investigation. You may need to use tools outside the app, such as websites, emails, a translator, Google Maps... Let nothing stop you from solving the case!

Read the QR codes to access interactive clues:
You can hear the suspects themselves explain what they were doing at the time of the crime.
In addition to accessing other interactive clues such as passwords, safes, phone calls, and more,

Once you have a theory about what happened, consult the solution to see if your theory is true:
Step by step, we'll explain how to get to the truth so you can check if your investigation was on the right track.

Mini Expansion that came with the Kickstarter version of 6: Siege - The Boardgame.

Bonus Operator.

Components:


     operator profile and miniature for the alternative version of Zero (and his base ring)
     1 Named Entryway Overwatch token
     2 Special Gadget standees (Argus Cameras)
     1 sheet of self-adhesive labels
     1 Leaflet

boardgame: Second Punic War: A Basic Game of Strategy
description:
More of a game proposal than a full game, the rules are given in Panzerfaust #52.

"The reader is warned that this is not a tested game, and there may well be some contradictions in the rules; yet, it looks as if it might be fun. I apologize in advance for the inevitable mistakes to follow. The purpose of this article is only to make a beginning at planning a strategic game for the classical era."

Initially inspired by Bruce Belcher's Metaurus minigame in Panzerfaust #50, it uses the Anzio board and 60 'chips' to represent control and units.

If you've ever been frustrated by someone beating you in chess only because they didn't just learn the rules but studied some opening and end games, then Keizár gives you the chance to beat them. Keizár is a two-player competitive strategy game that combines the board and the moves from chess with the randomness of a card game. As a result, the board is never the same, you can't prepare by studying patterns and scenarios, you have to come up with a strategy on the spot.

Because

it isn't chess!

The game starts by setting up the board. Set tiles are always the same, the rest shuffled and placed to create a unique experience each time. All pieces are pawns for the purposes of of movement and taking rules. But when you land on particular tiles, your piece will behave as either a rook, bishop, knight, king or queen. Once you make the next move however, back to being a pawn. 
Unlike normal chess, your aim is not to checkmate the king, but instead hold the Keizár tile, a set tile on the board. If you can hold that for 3 of your opponents turns, you win.

boardgame: League of the Lexicon: Junior Edition
description:
Expand the League of the Lexicon world with new and original questions. This expansion includes color-coded question cards to play along with the main game.

Features:

Questions written by world-renowned experts.
Meticulously researched & richly informative ideas.

Includes:

100 question cards
500 questions
5 question categories

boardgame: League of The Lexicon: Global Edition
description:
500 Questions On The World Of Language, From Ancient To Modern, Aramaic To Zulu.

Each expansion features colour coded question cards to play as part of the main game.

Expands the League of the Lexicon world with new and original questions.

Questions written by world-renowned experts.

Meticulously researched & richly informative.

Contents:
100 question cards
500 questions
5 question categories

boardgame: Black Dragon's Guild: Dark Forest Expansion
description:
The expansion to Black Dragon's Guild adds a fifth player to the base game.

Also included are 9 new adventurers and the Dark Forest game board extension including spaces for additional quests and three new city locations: Green Dragon Tavern, Bandit Camp, and Pit of the Damned.

boardgame: Black Dragon's Guild: Kickstarter Deluxe Extras
description:
Kickstarter exclusive extras included with the Deluxe pledge.

Includes a comic book which introduces the game and 4 additional adventurer cards for the base game.

Kickstarter promos included with the Standard and Deluxe pledges.

Includes 5 additional adventurer cards for the base game.

The crypt has arisen — and with it, a queen whose wrath refuses rest.

When a cursed tomb is disturbed, its Immortal Queen rises, her undead command echoing through collapsed corridors and blocked hallways that twist and trap the unwary. Three members of a production house — Ophelia Romero the reporter, Dylan Wan the cameraman, and Caleb Peele the producer — must fight or flee through shattered passageways as the Queen and her soldiers stalk their every step. In Putrefied Enmity, survival means navigating ruins, dodging decay, and outsmarting a relentless horror that will not let its secrets rest.

Inside the box:
- 1 Killer game board with map "The Tomb"
- 1 Survivor game board with map "The Tomb"
- 1 Killer miniature (Immortal Queen)
- 1 Killer board
- 12 Zombie miniatures 
- 3 Survivor miniatures (Ophelia Romero, Dylan Wan, Caleb Peele)
- 3 Survivor boards
- 13 killer skill cards
- 7 item cards
- 11 tokens (1 Relic Chamber, 3 Poison, 3 Encouragement, 1 Toughness, 3 Killer Assistance)
- 8 Collapse tiles
- 1 Special rule card
- 1 Expansion #4 rulebook

The Bad Batch is a squad of highly skilled and unconventional clone troopers with unique genetic mutations, making them the ultimate fighting force. With exceptional combat abilities and cutting-edge weaponry, they operate on the fringe of the war, taking on missions that other clones are not equipped to handle.

—description from the publisher

SecretLore: The Ineffable Charade is a cooperative card game for 2-4 players.

Up to 4 players will delve into a dark abyss, facing sanity-shattering encounters and uncovering the secrets of the Entities, god-like beings living as parasites feeding upon the world.

The objective is to gather knowledge in order to solve the mystery before an Entity unleashes itself upon Utropolis, all while navigating the treacherous paths of sanity and allegiance.

The game consists of 4 Chapters, or Lore Cards, each representing an individual story and difficulty level for the game.

—description from the designer

Building Stonehenge is a dice drafting and manipulation game, set in the time of legend when Stonehenge was built.
As the Wizard Merlin, your goal is to occupy all 12 spaces on the outside circle with posts (dice). This is done by drafting and manipulating the dice through actions, to direct them to their right position. Each new movement earns you actions from the action/direction tiles you go through, and from the lintels you put in place.

Gameplay
Each turn, you may:

     Draft a new die, or
     Move a die according to an action/direction tile, earning a manipulation action.


When you move a die to the outer circle, you

     Roll and add a new die to your dice pool.
     Remove the action/direction tile from the game.


If you have 2 adjacent dice on the outer circle, you must:

     Place a lintel tile and take its manipulation action.


Win by getting the 12th die in place!

—description from the designer

This game is a variant of the classical Mau Mau card game. The aim is to be the first to get rid of all hand cards. But as opposed to the classical game you have to make some funny activities once a corresponding card is played.

Every player starts with four cards and the first card of the drawing pile is revealed. The active player can discard a matching card or draw another card. A card matches if the color or the number of paws is equal the to topmost card on the discard pile.

Once a player has only a single card on the hand he has to say "Mauseschlau". If he forgets it he has to draw a card.

The activity card have only a color and require the following actions (among others):

     Close you eyes and get your forefingers together
     Rotate the left arm
     Clap behind your body
     Sing something

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Mountains of Godai – Return to Ishidan
description:
The Divine Dragons have come. Prepare for their Tempest. Experience the Mountains of Godai in an entirely new way!

Leap into the skies and stop the Divine Dragons from releasing Tenryu.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Mountains of Godai – Return to Caprakan
description:
Dinosaurs. Blood Magic. Aztlant. Kualotekutli: Jaethi's Keeper. Experience the Mountains of Godai in an entirely new way!

Their blood magic has awakened Kualotekutli. Eternal twilight is coming.

—description from the publisher

The Inquisitor will hunt all cultists, introducing a new layer of difficulty to the game.

This Module consists of:

The Inquisitor Miniature
    The 3 Inquisitor Wooden Hex
    The Rules for the Inquisitor competitive mod.
    The Rules for the Inquisitor co-operative mod.

—description from the publisher

A course game that combines cultural and historical interest with the famous mode of transport in Paris. The objective is to be the fastest to reach your 8 stations by strolling along the different lines and discovering the origin of the 383 station names while having fun.
Players roll a die and move on a track.

Depending on where they arrive: 

     they can answer a question allowing them to find a metro station or answer a challenge question to place one of their pawns on the station
     they can collect a playable bonus card immediately or later.


A player wins the game as soon as he has placed 8 of his pawns on the board.

boardgame: Spiel des Wissens: Rund um die Welt
description: Pack your bags and explore the world in this new edition of the best family quiz game: "Game of Knowledge"! Jump from continent to continent on your world tour and answer questions in different categories such as culture, food, nature, architecture and geography. The first to return home with a full passport wins!

boardgame: Chronicards: Les Grandes Dates Du Cinéma
description:
Chronicards is a card game played using 60 cards.
Each card depicts a film on one side with explanation and the year in which that event occurred on the other side.
Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is put in the right place and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

Chronicards is a card game played using 60 cards.
Each card depicts a great masterpieces of French literature on one side with explanation and the year in which that event occurred on the other side.
Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is put in the right place and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

Armored Assault is a war game where you take command of armored units, strategically capture essential resources, and dominate critical sectors of the battlefield. Your ultimate goal is to destroy the enemy command center. The game challenges you to use your tactical prowess and strategic planning to outmaneuver your opponent. Every decision counts as you deploy your forces, manage your resources, and adapt to the changing dynamics of the battle. You must anticipate your enemy's moves and seize every opportunity to emerge victorious in this epic showdown.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Klik's Madness Legends: Shadow of the Forsaken Crown
description:
When an ancient relic awakens with a necromantic fury, only one hero can stop an undead horde from sweeping across the Runecrest Valley: you.
 During your first mission for the Bandit Prince (which had promised to be routine), you become irrevocably bound to the two halves of a mysterious crown, drawing the ire of undead forces driven by an insatiable hunger for the relic’s restored might. As word of this foul awakening spreads across the valley, other sinister forces take note, eager to exploit its untold powers.
 In your quest for answers, you discover two paths to freedom – the return of the crown to its original resting place or your death while trying. On your journey, various factions vie to seize the relic’s awakened powers, forcing you to forge strategic alliances and navigate treacherous waters. Only through clever strategy, steadfast resolve, and perhaps a bit of trust, can you hope to survive your perilous trek and prevent the catastrophic potential of the forsaken crown from falling into the wrong hands.

More info
HEXplore It Legends is a series taking you on a thrilling adventure through a world of choice, chance, and courage, all within a single epic gaming session.

Every Legend is a self-contained journey, offering you the chance to dive into a world of epic quests, daring choices, and limitless possibilities.

Each Legend utilizes the captivating HEXplore It Campaign Books you already enjoy but introduces an entirely new narrative. There is no need to worry about prior adventures or follow-up sessions – each Legend stands alone, ready to be experienced at your leisure. Embark on epic journeys, meet friends new and old, make world-changing choices, and write your own destiny, one legend at a time.

The following content is required to play:


     Shadow of the Forsaken Crown Legends
     Klik's Madness Campaign Book
     The Valley of the Dead King Core box


—description from the publisher

134 pages

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Klik's Madness Legends: Corrosive History
description:
For over one hundred years the Grey Slime has plagued the Runecrest Valley, its origins shrouded in mystery. The valiant have fought and defeated the corrosive creature more than once, only to witness its dreaded resurgence within the heart of Southcrown Cave.

Now, fate beckons you to put an end to this destructive cycle. Will you heed the call to join forces with the enigmatic Shaman Dregnak and her resilient daughter Vorlah to once again take up arms against the magical beast? A grave crossroads awaits, where your choices hold the power to illuminate or deepen the darkness that has enshrouded the Undervalley for generations.

More info
HEXplore It Legends is a series taking you on a thrilling adventure through a world of choice, chance, and courage, all within a single epic gaming session.

Every Legend is a self-contained journey, offering you the chance to dive into a world of epic quests, daring choices, and limitless possibilities.

Each Legend utilizes the captivating HEXplore It Campaign Books you already enjoy but introduces an entirely new narrative. There is no need to worry about prior adventures or follow-up sessions – each Legend stands alone, ready to be experienced at your leisure. Embark on epic journeys, meet friends new and old, make world-changing choices, and write your own destiny, one legend at a time.

The following content is required to play:


     Corrosive History Legends
     Klik's Madness Campaign Book
     The Valley of the Dead King Core box


—description from the publisher

90 pages

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Klik's Madness Legends: A Taste for Adventure!
description:
Join the ranks of Bezzelquark’s Black Market workers and embark on a culinary quest like no other. When the eccentric gnome tinkerer discovers the mouthwatering delight of Milo Goodbarrel’s family recipe for halfling chicken chili chowder, he won’t settle for just one taste.

He assigns you as Milo’s bodyguards on a gastronomical journey through the breathtaking landscapes of Runecrest Valley, hunting for unique and elusive ingredients to recreate this delectable dish. Unfortunately, even the allure of fresh chowder will not stop Bezzelquark from keeping his mobile Black Market on the move. You have just two weeks to scour the Valley for unique ingredients before the Black Market travels to new horizons.

Find the rarest of spices, stalk the strangest of game, and master culinary chaos to create the greatest chowder Runecrest Valley has ever seen!

More info
HEXplore It Legends is a series taking you on a thrilling adventure through a world of choice, chance, and courage, all within a single epic gaming session.

Every Legend is a self-contained journey, offering you the chance to dive into a world of epic quests, daring choices, and limitless possibilities.

Each Legend utilizes the captivating HEXplore It Campaign Books you already enjoy but introduces an entirely new narrative. There is no need to worry about prior adventures or follow-up sessions – each Legend stands alone, ready to be experienced at your leisure. Embark on epic journeys, meet friends new and old, make world-changing choices, and write your own destiny, one legend at a time.

The following content is required to play:


     A Taste for Adventure! Legends
     Klik's Madness Campaign Book
     The Valley of the Dead King Core box


—description from the publisher

146 pages

The wacky "Would you rather" game.
Wild Green: Random World Adventures

250 Questions, 750 Answers
Conversations you've never had!

What will everyone find out in this game of "Who knows who better?"


One player reads a question with three "would you rather?" options and secretly chooses their answer using the A, B, or C card, then the other players use their own cards to guess what the reader picked.   Each player that correctly matches the reader's choice gains a point.   Play continues until each player has read three questions, and the player with the most points wins.

Three additional variants are included that modify the nature of how the choices and guesses are made and/or who they're made for.

The goal is to collect 6 plaid pockets or have the most money at the end of the rounds. Collect sets of 2 of the same pockets. Their total value becomes your score. Play special cards on your turn or on someone else's turn. Follow the rule of two on your turn.

On your turn you:

     Draw up to your hand size
     Buy up to 2 cards from the discard pile
     Set up to 2 pockets
     Offer other players the chance to buy one of your cards
     Sell up to 2 cards from your hand

boardgame: When did Rommel get his goggles?
description:
When did Rommel get his goggles? is a game simulating the fighting between the British and the Axis in North Africa from November 1940 (Operation Compass) to May 1941 (Operation Sonnenblume).

The maneuver warfare and supply issues of the North African campaign are represented with simple rules.

In the early stages, the British forces advance rapidly. However, once the German forces arrive, the Axis powers quickly gain the upper hand.

Predicting the next developments and planning operations during the "Strategy Phase" becomes the key to victory.

Whether Rommel gets his trademark goggles or O'Connor avoids the fate of being captured depends on the decisions of the players.

A treasure is hidden in a mysterious pyramid! The players go on a treasure hunt in the building full of puzzles and secret passages. Which symbol is missing from the logical series? And what number are you looking for? Whoever solves the tricky logic tasks gets closer and closer to the mysterious burial chamber and the hidden treasure. tiptoi promotes concentration, logical thinking and turns players into real logic experts.

The Jungle Olympics brings movement into play. Matching the funny monkey cards, tiptoi tells various gymnastics exercises such as jumping jacks or balancing on one leg. The ACTIVE game promotes team spirit and teamwork because numerous movement tasks are solved together. Music and dance motivate 1 to 6 children to move.

Even if it's raining, storming or snowing outside, you can always do fun movement tasks with the children's game "Jungle Olympics". Who can complete the task quickly and with concentration within a time limit? Funny songs, noises and numerous task variants as well as two levels of difficulty offer long-lasting fun.

With tiptoi ACTIVE, exercise is fun.

boardgame: Escape Box: Minecraft – Saurez-vous déjouer les pièges?
description:
All the parents are on an expedition, you children are alone in the village. It could be so relaxing, staying up late, spending the whole day in your pyjamas, playing with friends ... But the dream could turn into a nightmare: An invasion of zombies, slimes and skeletons is threatening your village! You have 45 minutes - can you save your village?

The unofficial escape box for Minecraft fans with thirteen exciting puzzles!

—description from the publisher

Three Arrows can be played by 2, 3, or 4 players. The object of the game is to capture as many of your opponents' Arrow Pieces as possible.

The board is a 6x6 grid.  A player's basic pieces have arrows on them. Players place their pieces on the board or attack with those pieces.  A player's first piece can be played on the edge but not the corners of the board.  Subsequent pieces are placed adjacent to the player's existing pieces on one of the squares pointed to by their arrows.

The attack strength of a piece, or line of pieces, is the number of arrows pointing at the target piece.  To make an attack, nominate a target, add your base attack strength to a die roll (-1,0,+1) and compare it to the defence strength.  The losing piece is removed.
