description:
Each player has a goal keeper and 10 field players. Movement and ball handling are determined by the starting spaces of the playing figures. After the third turn and after every turn following the playing field is changed by shifting inserts with playing spaces printed on them, which are visible through transparent spaces on the game map. The inlays are also shifted before resolving certain standard situations (foul, corner ball)
After 11 turns halftime is called and after another 11 turns the game ends. The player who manages to achieve the most goals wins the game.

—user summary

boardgame: ドール工房と気まぐれな魔女 (The Doll Workshop and the Whimsical Witch)
description:
Players are craftsmen and create dolls to satisfy customer's orders.
On one's turn, a die is rolled to determine what part of a doll can be acquired. Then the player take actions to get doll parts, earn money, trade with other players, or replenish their karma. Karma is spent to make a finished doll become your assistent. Every assistent provides the player one more action per turn.
The witch might visit a player if the eye of the witch is rolled. She provides a player stronger actions but ask for valuables in return.
Every round, after all players has finished their turn, an auction phase happens, during which players bid for rare doll parts. After that, players can assemble their dolls as many as they what.
The game ends after certain number of rounds. The best doll award is rewarded by voting. Then players satisfy orders with their finished dolls to earn points. The player who has the highest point becomes the best doll crafteman and wins the game.

Carpé is an entertaining social board game for 2 to 6 players, ideal for families, friends, and parties. It blends the thrill of craps, Monopoly's property prowess, and the excitement of Bunko. With a captivating Japanese aesthetic, Carpé invites players aged 10 and up on a journey where fortune pursuit becomes a thrilling game of chance and decision-making, evoking either collective groans or bursts of cheer with every turn. In Carpé, every dice roll is a gamble with high stakes! Buy or rent valuable lily pads and name your price like a seasoned gambler. Outwit opponents through strategic gem maneuvers. The winner is the player with the most money after three thrilling rounds lasting approximately one hour.

​Get ready to feel the pulse-pounding excitement as each person takes their turn at the dice. With each roll, you'll be on the edge of your seat, will you reel in some big money or get carpéd?

​Carpé is a lot of luck, probability, and some strategy. Plan your bets and place your gems, outsmart your opponents, or collaborate. Will you play it safe or take a risk for a chance at glory?

​No two games of Carpé are ever the same. With its dynamic gameplay and endless possibilities, you'll find yourself coming back for more, time and time again. Whether you're playing with friends, family, or as a regular social gathering, Carpé guarantees entertainment and excitement.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Thinning Veil: Cormac Mac Airt on the Other Side of Midnight
description:
A solo dungeon crawling adventure game now featuring a 2 player mode, set in the world of The Thinning Veil & featuring Cormac Mac Airt, High King of Inis Fael!

Learn the game in a highly replayable 3 act path as Young Cormac! Then play 'On the Other Side of Midnight' consisting of a four act path playable as Cormac in solo, Grainne in solo, or as father & daughter together! Heed the call to adventure, on the Other Side of Midnight!

Inspired by Celtic mythology and the folklore of the British Isles, this game uses the Thinning Veil game mechanics, and is compatible with all future releases. It is a rogue-like game with narrative trigger points that uses a tile stack system, ensuring a lot of replayability.

—description from the publisher

This expansion includes:
- The Baby Barbarian Figurine (equal model as the one from the kickstarter base game, but without the blood in the sword).
- 15 New Dungeon Cards.

Compatible with Check Your 6 core rules. This is not a stand-alone rules set.


     Desperate Measures provides 48 WWII scenarios from the Late War Pacific Theater in "Check Your 6" format
     Scenarios extensively researched and play-tested
     Scenarios featuring such diverse elements as:
     Kamikaze attacks against all types of naval vessels including Fleet and Escort Carriers, Heavy Cruisers, Destroyers and transports where you actually have to fly your planes into ships
     Escorted B-29 raids against the Japanese Home Islands where the bombers actually drop bombs, while facing ramming attacks and air-to-air bombing
     Actions involving the British Pacific Fleet (BPF) and the iconic Seafires
     Low-level airfield attacks including strafing and bombing
     Fighter versus Fighter and Fighter Bomber scenarios featuring famous aces such as Tommy McGuire and Eugene Valencia
     A large selection of aircraft for the Allies and Japanese including, but not limited to F6F Hellcats, F4U Corsairs, P-51 Mustangs, P-38 Lightnings, B-29s, N1 K2 Georges, Ki-84 Franks, Ohkas, Ki-1 00s, the ubiquitous A6M Zero and many more.


What's the Same

     The "Check Your 6" movement and combat system simplicity
     Games with a few or many aircraft to suit all sizes of gaming groups. Small scenarios can last an hour or less, larger scenarios can take a few hours


What's Different

     Scenario specific victory conditions so that each side has a chance to win
     6 Mini-campaigns covering the period of the most intense Kamikaze attacks including the Philippines, actions around Okinawa and the Home Islands
     Rules additions including how to conduct Kamikaze attacks, Ohkas, and deliberately ramming B-29's


—description from the back of the book
