Dive into the heart of World War II's Pacific Theater with Pacific Flyboys, an immersive strategy board game that puts you in command of a mighty aircraft carrier and its fleet of naval aircraft. As tensions rise and the fate of nations hangs in the balance, you'll take on the role of either the United States Navy or the Imperial Japanese Navy in a high-stakes battle for naval supremacy.

Game Overview

In Pacific Flyboys, every decision counts. You'll navigate the treacherous waters of the Pacific, managing your limited resources as you launch reconnaissance missions, deploy squadrons, and engage in thrilling dogfights. Will you press the attack and risk it all, or play it safe and bide your time? The choice is yours, but remember – your enemy is just as determined to find and sink your carrier.

Key Features


     Historical Experience: Relive pivotal moments of World War II naval warfare, including the Battle of Coral Sea.
     Strategic Gameplay: Manage resources, position your forces, and outsmart your opponent in a game of cat and mouse on the high seas.
     Diverse Card-Driven Mechanics: Utilize a variety of cards – from Reconnaissance and Squadron Leaders to Aircraft and Carriers – each adding layers of strategy and decision-making.
     Dynamic Combat System: Engage in tense air-to-air battles and daring bombing runs with an engaging dice-based combat resolution system.
     Asymmetric Gameplay: Command either the U.S. Navy or the Imperial Japanese Navy, each with unique strengths and strategies.
     Modular Board: Experience a different game every time with a variable setup using Zone cards.
     Solo and Multiplayer Modes: Test your skills against an AI opponent or challenge your friends in multiplayer battles.


Whether you're a history buff, a strategy game enthusiast, or simply looking for an engaging tabletop experience, Pacific Flyboys offers a perfect blend of historical authenticity and strategic depth. Can you outmaneuver your opponent, find their carrier, and change the course of the war? The fate of the Pacific is in your hands!

Prepare for takeoff, Admiral. The battle for the Pacific begins now!

—description from the designer

The hero Malannar has an outstanding command of water. He can create small bodies of water and use them as well as spring cracks as a starting point for ranged attacks, which gives him an extreme weapon range and that even without line of sight on the opponents.

Hammond: Naval Warfare is a symmetrical, two-player competitive simulation of early 20th century tactical naval battle. Players take turns controlling movement on a grid of five vessels, each with unique rules governing movement, attack, and supply capabilities. The board is divided into regions of land and sea, including a central, neutral island region, fixed defensive land fortifications, foggy areas, and a protected city for each player. The objective is to successfully penetrate the opponent's land defenses and attack their city.

US Patent 1023375, Naval War Game Apparatus, was the first patent issued (in 1912) to prolific American inventor John Hays Hammond Jr. Over a century later, the naval strategy game was adapted and published by the Hammond Castle Museum, which preserves and curates Hammond's former home and art collection, along with information about his inventions and life, in Gloucester, Massachusetts.

Pythia has long been considered a reliable oracle. Thus, allied units can ask Pythia about their fate and regenerate life or morale points. With other prophecies, it is possible for her to inflict damage on opposing units, but beware, prophecies can also be dangerous!

It's the year 2025. An unforeseen blizzard has struck the planet, quickly erasing most of life on Earth. The hardier people of the North survived, climbing the mountains where few animals and trees could survive. They made small caves their refuge, and now their only chance at survival is to rediscover old skills and tools.

Warmth of Fire asks the player to lead a small group of survivors, initially formed by only 2 people, in their struggle against the deadly cold. During the course of the game, the player can assign their survivors to various actions, like hunting, crafting tools, discovering adaptations, and more. The game uses a "long actions" system, where some of the more intensive tasks - like hunting - will resolve at the start of the next gameplay phase during the round. This will force the player to carefully plan all actions and resource expenditure.

The player must always tend to their survivor's needs like hunger and health. If one of the survivors dies, the game ends.

Ultimately, the objective of the game is to survive for at least 10 days, while gameplay variants may introduce additional rules or win conditions.

In a remote mountain range, unnatural weather changes have occured for several months. Sunshine, rainfall, snowstorms, coming and going in seemingly random fashion. As a result, the flora is showing previously unobserved developments. Thunderstrikes and avalanches have revealed curious minerals.

Now scientists and researchers from various disciplines race to collect valuable research data, never knowing where they have to go next. This research data is shared with various sponsors, which promise better equipment in return.

The overseas caravans have reached the new domains of the noble Roman families. The merchants set up their tents and offer lucrative contracts. As before, you’ll create your domains from the pieces of the Roman Empire, sharing them with other players. However, a new type of buildings, additional plots of land and contracts that you can receive for placing trade tents on your land plots are added to the game. Each round, you'll decide which contracts you keep in hopes of earning more victory points, and which ones you use in the trade auction to purchase double land plots, new buildings, or other bonuses.

Lotta Rome. The Merchants is an expansion to Lotta Rome that adds depth to the gameplay, increases the interaction between players, and opens up new strategies.

Knights Over Stalingrad is primarily a Solitaire Game, but can be played by two opponents, with one side playing the Axis units during combat and the other the Soviets.
 The game models the Air Combat in and around Stalingrad immediately following the encirclement of the Sixth Army following the highly successful Soviet Operation Uranus.  Operation Uranus kicked off on November 17th, 1942 and totally encircled the German 6th Army with the capture of Karpovka on November 24th, 1942.  The game continues through the Soviet Tormosin Offensive (Battle of the Chir River Bridgehead), Manstein’s German Counter Offensive (Operation Winter Thunderstorm), Soviet Little Saturn (that crushed the Italians and defeated the remaining Romanian resistance), and the Soviet Deep strikes at Tazinskaya and Morosovsky Airfields.  Overall, the game covers the time period of November 23rd through December 28th, 1942.

On the Axis side the player will have the choice of controlling German Bf 109G’s, Bf 110’s, Romanian IAR 80’s, or Romanian Bf 109E’s.  A Soviet player will have the option of controlling 1 of 6 IAP’s assigned to the Stalingrad area, featuring; Yak-1’s, LaGG-3’s, La-5’s, or Yak-7b’s.  
Axis Ju-52 transports and He 111 bombers will attempt to make their way through Soviet fighters and flak to airfields inside the Cauldron in an attempt to supply the German 6th Army.  Dive Bombers, Ground Attack aircraft, and bombers on both sides (Ju-87’s, IL-2’s, Pe-2’s, A-20’s, and more) will attempt to engage ground forces following their historic tracks, while fighters duel overhead.  Horrific losses were incurred by both sides, as some of the greatest Aces of WWII faced off in grueling air-to-air combat.

The player chooses the campaign’s duration, either 10 or 15 Missions, or they can play the entire Campaign from November 23rd through December 27th, 1942.  The player draws Squadron specific Mission Cards to determine the objective of each mission, some Mission Cards are date specific to highlight important battles and/or events.  The player’s flight moves across a historical map grid that encompassed the territory covered by Operations Uranus and Little Saturn, including Stalingrad and the Cauldron.  Combat is executed using an “Initiative Track” for up to three rounds of combat, where pilot skill, aircraft performance, Dogfight Cards, and most importantly – player decisions, all come into play to determine the outcome.

Can you keep your squadron together, will you be successful enough to keep up their fighting spirit in the face of such daunting foes?

—description from the designer

'ポリゴンワインダー' is a minimal auction game in which the auctioneer is decided later based on the total of each player's bid.

In order to acquire the cards that are laid out on the table, players bid with coins every round. First, there is an auction for the right to acquire the cards, followed by an auction to decide who will be the auctioneer for this auction. The player who ultimately becomes the auctioneer must give up the right to acquire the cards, but instead acquires coins. This is repeated for several rounds, and the player with the highest score wins. This is a game in which you must simply take turns to navigate two types of auctions (sometimes there is a third type) that come endlessly with a minimalist rhythm. When to step in and bid? When to become the auctioneer and compromise? It's all about judging them.

—description from the publisher

Dragon Soul is a strategy and war medieval-themed board game inspired by the RTS computer game genre. Players build castles on grass tiles, draw soldier cards equal to the number of castles owned, and deploy soldier cards into stacks called battalions which move along the corners and centers of hexes doing battle in a rocks paper scissor style format. Trade in the rare soul stone card for a dragon at a boneyard hex. Dragons can wreak havoc on battalions but have no effect on castles.

Battalion allowance is equal to the number of uncontested castles owned. Standing next to an enemy castle renders it useless, which is called "contested". The first player to reach and hold a designated number of uncontested castles wins.

—description from the publisher

Bloody Omaha: D-Day Amphibious Assault is a challenging solitaire and 2-player board game that comprises multiple landing waves and drift, beach obstacles, fortified defensive positions and strongpoints, hedgerow terrain inland, large 1-inch counters representing American infantry companies and battalions, U.S. engineers and tanks, hidden defense die-cut marker tokens including German armored vehicles, plus playing-card-enhanced tactical opportunities and challenges.

A once powerful and magical realm has been cast to ruins by angry gods. The witches of this realm have been banished and the land torn asunder. Legend has it that someday one witch will return to appease the gods by building altars in their names.

Sacred Grounds is a competitive 3-4 player adventure board game. You will attempt to become this witch of legend by exploring the realm, uncovering mystical artifacts, and using them to outwit your opponents and build altars to the gods. But beware, the gods are still active and fickle, and your fate could change at any time.

Each hexagonal map tile has either artifacts, events, or special actions players can take. The race is to use limited turn actions to make offerings to the gods in the most efficient way possible. Roll special Fate Dice to make your offerings of artifacts or defeated monsters, or else risk removing your tile and starting fresh. The first to make 3 successful offerings and build their altars will win the game!

In each game of The Murder, you are the suspects. Over the course of three acts, the suspects will remember details of the evening and must share them with the investigator in order to solve the case... well, as long as they haven't committed the murder.

boardgame: Twilight of the Emperor: Wargame Rules for European Battles in the Age of Napoleon – 1800-1815
description:
The Twilight of the Emperor rules is a set designed to fight large historical battles on a reasonable sized table and with a reasonable number of figures.

The player is the army commander seeking to marshal the full forces that their historical counterpart had to achieve victory and not some brigade or division commander controlling a few battalions or squadrons in the fight for some sector of a battlefield. The concentration is on the 'big picture' rather on relatively unimportant details. The rules reward historic tactics and practices while making typical 'gamer' tactics obsolete. So, players will want their forces to be in at least two lines with a reserve and not strung out in a line as is commonly done in other rules.

The system is intuitive and easy to pick up. The units are typically around 3,000 foot or 1,500 mounted and so the units are often the equivalent to a 'brigade' or large continental regiments.

The scale is flexible with all distances, ranges, movements based on half of the frontage of the units you use. For example, if your units have a frontage of perhaps 120mm (about 6 inches) the standard measurement unit in the game will be 60mm. There is no figure removal so you can base you units as you wish, with any number or size of figures on a base, etc. As long as the units are the same frontage it does not matter and indeed neither does it if the armies involved are based differently.

While any unit size can be used if the frontage of a unit is in the region of 120mm then many battles can be fought on a table 1.8m by 1.2m (6 feet by 4 feet) or similar. Most standard battles should be playable in a typical afternoon or evening playing session.

The rules cover warfare in Europe in the era 1800 to 1815, and perhaps those a little earlier. The rules include scenarios for Austerlitz (1805), Abensburg (1809), Talavera (1809) and Dennewitz (1813).

—description from the publisher

In Pluto Dinerama you’ve just opened your very own diner on podunk, wayside, last-chance-to-gas-up-before-you-leave-the-Solar-System Pluto. Now it's time to add to your menu, install new ambience, and hire talented employees in this fascinating to master 2-5 player tableau-building strategy card game.

Pluto Dinerama is both collaborative and competitive, as players will have to work together to defeat the mega-corporate Rival Diner before turning their attention to each other. Pluto Dinerama makes for a great gateway game; it has a rich underbelly of strategic choices and inter-diner trading mechanics, sure, but what makes it so special is how it strategifies party-game elements, making playing an inclusive and hilarious time.

—description from the publisher

Baby, you made it! You're a professional performing drag queen. And what's more, younger drag queens want you to mentor them as their drag mother. You better ‘werk’ to get the best tips, take all the glory, and snatch that tiara.

Drag Dynasty is a crunchy euro about being a professional performing drag queen and drag mother. It uses action selection, movement programming, and a hybrid mancala/engine building system to cycle meeples (fans) to different spaces. There are three interacting systems of developing your fanbase for discounted actions and selling them tickets to make money, programming a dance routine on the Main Stage for your Queen, and sending Drag Daughters to face off against each other in ballroom showdowns where the best combination of skill and fans will win!
On their turn, players will assign one of their Drag Daughters to a shop as an apprentice, and then will take an action there, paying the cost according to their player mat. These actions include investing in your Drag Daughter's skills, rehearsing Performances that move your Queen figure around the main stage, or gaining new Drag Daughters. At the end of their turn, players will send Fans to different shops or adding them to their Fan Club in order to get discounts on their turn.

The gameplay involves opportunity cost decisions as players decide how best to use their money, and where to allocate their fans, either to get discounts on the actions at the shops, sell them tickets to get money to pay for their actions, or send them to cheer on their Drag Daughters as they compete in ballroom events and win trophies.

—description from the designer

Pick up a quick mission and run it to a destination on the board to earn a bonus. The more drops you deliver, the more points you'll earn at the end of the game.

Incudes: 15 Dropsite cards and 2 rules cards.

Setup: Shuffle the Dropsite deck and place it face down. Reveal the top card and place it face up on the location on the game board matching the pickup site (the left side of the card).

Gameplay: When a player ends their movement on a location with a dropsite card, they may take the card and place it face up near their runner board. This is a free action.
Then, immediately reveal a new Dropsite card (face up) on the board in the correct location. Each player may only have one face up Dropsite card at a time.

When a player with a face-up Dropsite card ends their movement on the Dropsite (the location on the right side of the card), they flip the Dropsite card over.

Scoring: During final scoring, earn points based on the number of face-down Dropsite cards.
1 -> 1 VP
2 -> 3 VP
3 -> 6 VP
4 -> 10 VP
5+ -> 15VP

Solo mode: If a rogue token ever shares a location with a Dropsite card, discard is and reveal a new one.

Eldur is a cooperative Action Sequence game for 2–4 players, ages 8 and up. Players work together & activate Helpers that roam the lands to find lost famillies, goods or animals before the mighty Volcano claims the Island with its lava & ash.

Will you manage to evacuate everyone or will the Volcano reclaim the lands once again?

There are 4 workers that players cooperatively activate to fulfil the task; the Gatherer seeks goods, the Herder searches the mountains and hidden valleys for sheep and the Seeker looks for lost families. All of them are safely transferred to the harbours where the Knerrir sails them to safety on the newly discovered island.

However, the volcano is unpredictable; no one knows where the lava will flow. Ash falls, cracks, or new fissures may open at any time, making no place safe. The challenge is to see whether the humans can complete their evacuation or if the volcano will regain control of the island. Which side will prevail in the end?

In Eldur, players work together to pick up the local people, goods or animals and bring them to safety on the new Island. During each players turn there are 5 phases that players do:

- Activate the Alþingi board (optional)
- Draw an Incident card & resolve it (mandatory)
- Match and activate Action cards (players match with other players' cards, to activate helpers), move up to 2 activated helpers (mandatory)
- The volcano draws its Volcano card & all players resolve it in the best possible way (mandatory)
- Refill the Action cards (mandatory), next players turn

As with other games in the Nordic Heritage Series, the game takes up wery small table space. The game is using the whole gamebox; 
- The bottom part of the magnetic box lid is the play area, along with ocean tiles that are interconnected with it
- The back side of the box side is the scoring track, where a balance marker is moved back and forth as the balance switches between humans & the volcano
- The front part of the box side is a placeholder for the active Incidents cards, that will influence all players both in a good way and a bad way

The game comes with 8 preset setup & winning scenarios, but as players get a better understanding of the game, players can create their own.

A box packed with cooperative suspense, who will win, the humans or the volcano?

Eldur is the 4th game in The Nordic Heritage Series from Gamia games; Sagnir (2018/2023), Runir (2020/2023) & Vedur (2023).

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Each player has several face down piles of four random cards in their play area. A central "gator pit" of four face up cards sits between the players.

During play, each player picks up a pile from their play area and checks to see if any of the cards in the "gator pit" are a match. If so, players swap out non-matching cards for cards that match.

The goal is to end up with all piles having only one type of card in each (for example, one pile would have four basketball cards, another four baseball cards, etc).

Involves memory (which pile has cards matching the cards in the gator pit) and matching skills utilized in simultaneous play.

Duat offers a captivating solo-play fantasy adventure where your heart defines your fate, skillfully balancing simplicity with profound challenges, and chance. Step into the role of a fearless adventurer, ensnared within the intricate tapestry of the ancient Egyptian underworld known as Duat. This immersive journey will take you through a realm filled with mysticism and peril, where every decision tests your courage and wisdom. Amidst the labyrinthine pathways and cryptic temples, you'll seek out wondrous treasures, each a beacon of hope illuminating your path toward salvation. These treasures, guarded by supernatural underworld forces and hidden within the depths of ancient tombs, hold the key to your progression, granting you the strength and resources needed to confront the formidable final guardian.

As you delve deeper into the enigmatic underworld, the stakes grow higher, and the challenges more daunting. Battling the spectral forces will test your strategic prowess and bravery, but the ultimate trial lies ahead. The final guardian, a powerful god of ancient lore, stands as the ultimate test of your mettle. However, it is not just the accumulation of treasures and victories in battle that will determine your fate. Throughout your quest, your actions and choices will be weighed against the divine scales of Ma'at, determining if your heart is worthy of passage into Sekhet-Aaru, the divine Egyptian heaven.

Duat weaves an intricate narrative of bravery, and destiny, ensuring an unforgettable experience that keeps adventurers on the edge of their seats, eager to uncover the next secret of the ancient world.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 100 Hands of Robot Bridge: A Playing Card Bridge Automaton
description:
100 Hands of Robot Bridge: A Playing Card Bridge Automaton presents a contract bridge solo experience, playable with just two decks of standard playing cards. The games included hope to compel the player to make efficient bridge decisions, and consider each outcome against their intuition.

The player is able to deal cards to themselves and to the three simulated players while still preserving the challenge and surprise of each game. Once dealt, the player is tasked with finding the efficient solution to each trick, in an experience simulating table bridge play.

The limited edition "Saint Joan" luminary card was originally included with vinyl copies of The Decemberist's album "As it Ever Was, So It Will Be". The card will also be included as a bonus card for the French edition of Illimat.

The "Saint Joan" luminary card was inspired by the Jules Bastien-Lepage painting Joan of Arc.

The rules for "Saint Joan" are:
"If you possess Saint Joan at the end of the round, you gain two points instead of the usual one point for a luminary. However, this blessing comes at a price. When you collect Saint Joan by clearing a field, you must immediately reseed the field using all the cards in your hand. This occurs regardless of whether or not you collected an okus, and takes the place of any other reseeding of this field. Once you’ve placed your cards in the field, redraw until you have four cards in your hand."

1, 2, 3, Lucha! is a quick, fun, and strategy-filled game where you'll need to have a great read on your opponent to win! Hire luchadores based on your prestige, assemble your team, and step into the ring. When the confrontation begins, play cards to modify your luchador's attribute and reveal them simultaneously with your opponent. With each fight, earn points that will lead you to victory!

boardgame: Odalin: Dungeons of Doom – Kaerun, the Master of Thousand Faces
description:
Kaerun's strength is his versatility, being able to gain mastery over other heroes' attacks for unique combinations. If not chosen by you, he may appear as a villain, depending on your choices.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Odalin: Dungeons of Doom – Heroes of War
description:
This pack introduces four new frontline heroes, each with unique mechanics.

In this pack, you will find:

Lyria, the Death Knight: Able to gain power from killing enemies, she is a force of death on the battlefield.
    Mordak, the Blackguard: A master at wielding corruption while he remains on the edge of losing control, he has unmatched strength.
    Aridya, the Templar: Her shield protects those around her, and her holy powers stave off corruption from her and her allies.
    Durik, the Dwarven Warrior: As a King of the Mountain, he refuses to let his world be taken by demons. This unyielding dwarf is a wall of defense for his allies.

Note: Each hero above has a unique mechanic that plays differently from all others.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Odalin: Dungeons of Doom – Heroes of Magic
description:
Meet four new spellcasting heroes with special mechanics.

In this pack, you will find:

Vorath, the Lich: He cannot overtake the world if the demons do it first, so this once hero’s great foe will now use his dark magic to aid them.
    Selene, the Elf Wizard: With a wide array of unique effects, Selene can use her versatile magic to shape the battlefield to her advantage.
    Daram, the High Priest: A versatile support character who can wear heavy or light armor, bless allies, and stand at the forefront of battle while healing.
    Balin, the Halfling Druid: Together with his trusted companion, Drakko, he fights to protect nature, blending his magic with his mount’s powerful bite attacks.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Odalin: Dungeons of Doom – Pack of Demons: Hunger & Darkness
description:
Two new demons can now be added to your collection, greatly increasing the number of possible builds. Each demon comes with its own set of plastic cards and demon cards and can also be used as a Boss monster in a special side quest.

In this pack, you will find:

Megera, The Dark Lady:The demoness of night and darkness curses enemies and protects her allies, making her a powerful support demon.
    Gorgonus, The Hunter:A fearsome predator, Gorgonus hunts the battlefield, devouring enemies to gain strength. This Demon Lord will terrorize his foes as he teleports around the battlefield to consume them.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Odalin: Dungeons of Doom – Pack of Demons: Lust & Wrath
description:
This pack introduces two new demon characters to your game, offering a wealth of strategic options. Use their powers to combine with your heroes, enhancing your team’s abilities and tactics.

Alternatively, if not selected as allies, these demons can become formidable enemies, adding a thrilling challenge to your adventures. Expand your gameplay with these versatile and dynamic additions.

—description from the publisher

The Signature Series expansion delivers 24 exciting new adventure scenarios penned by some of your favorite role-playing celebrities.

Can you sweet talk a troll? Are you ready to battle the demon of sleep? Will you be able to retrieve your magic items by chasing down an interdimensional thief? Don’t worry, even if you roll a “1,” it isn’t a failure - it’s just hilarious!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Odalin: Dungeons of Doom – Sands of Destruction
description:
Your Core Box offers over 65 hours of gameplay. 
The Sands of Destruction Box doubles this content, leading you to the final confrontation with Abaddon and the epic climax of your adventure.

With the Djinns now at your side, strike back against the demonic hordes!

Hunt down the Four Knights of the Apocalypse, the Great Generals of Hell.

Forge a plan, lead the resistance, and with the Djinns' blessings, confront Abaddon in an epic battle to reclaim your world!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kampf gegen das Spiessertum: Big Box
description: A big box with space for over 1500 cards, in contains a mini expansion (labeled M2) with 5 extra yellow cards and 15 white cards with themes around heavy and big.

The "soccer" expansion is about the most beautiful minor matter in the world (who would have thought it). From sausages to the Bundesliga to summer fairy tales! Madrid or Milan? The main fight against philistinism!

—description from the publisher (translated)

100 new cards (30 yellow and 70 white) with soccer theme.

Smoothie Wars is a strategic game in which players compete to sell fresh fruit smoothies at different locations on a tropical island. The game takes place over an imaginary week, with each turn representing one day.

The goal is to finish the week with the most money from selling smoothies. The player with the most cash at the end wins.

Each turn, players must use their strategic skills to decide the location at which to sell smoothies, the number of smoothies to sell at that location, and the price of their smoothies. Players may banter, bargain, and verbally mislead others about their intentions before each turn.

—description from the designer

Stack It Kitten! is an all-ages card game, made for everyone looking for a fast and portable game to play during parties or downtime. The game is very intuitive and super easy to learn (even for the little ones). It can be played by 2 players up to a group of 8. Matches are really fast and usually last about 10 minutes.

The game is "set" in a house, where 5 kittens find themselves facing the rising water level. The water continues to rise and the only way to stay dry is to create towers with objects they find around them. Become one of the kittens and play against your friends in a battle to the last block!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zoo Tycoon: The Board Game – Additional Species Pack
description:
The Additional Species Pack contains 90 silkscreen-printed meeples and 32 animal cards (4 per species / 8 per player) and everything you need to use these animal species with the expansion's modular animal exchange (e.g. 8 animal exchange cards).

The Additional Species Pack needs the New Shores expansion to work!

Rubico is a collaborative team game in which you will help your faction cross the river and reach Rome or try to stop the advance with the opposing senators . Anticipate your opponents' moves and play your cards well. The fate of Rome is in your hands.

Note: This is a totally new edition of Rubico.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grave Robbers From Outer Space: Director's Cut! – Cannibal Pygmies in the Jungle of Doom
description:
In Grave Robbers From Outer Space, you as a B-Movie director craft your film with Location, Character, and Prop, and Special Effect cards while attacking with and defending against Hollywood Creatures.

And Cannibal Pygmies in the Jungle of Doom expands play into the tropes and memes of your favorite Action/Adventure movies!

Players build their own custom movie decks, CCG style, to play head-to-head. New players can make use of Feature Packs, which are sets of 16 cards that are designed to work well together while making fun of a particular movie or theme. Shuffle up 3 Feature Packs (plus a few cards) and you're ready to play. However, those who are savvy can build their own decks from scratch to make fun of their favorite flick.

The new official 50 card format re-incorporates Roll the Credits cards as the game ending mechanic: Once your Movie reaches 20 Defense, you can play a Roll the Credits to provoke The Climax, a final panic round where players rush in a cutthroat bid to change the final score or put a halt to the Credits and continue play.

—description from the publisher
publishers: The Academy of Resurrected Games and Holdings (ARGH)

description:
Scanzle is a speedy puzzle party game. It's fast-paced and exciting, and it'll put your memory skills to the test. The best part? It's super easy to play and understand. So what are you waiting for? Get ready to bring your A-game as you face off against your pals in this thrilling challenge where every move counts!


     Win points by collecting the card if your team wins a round
     Points can be used to buy a Trophy or Wager Card
     Trophies cost 3 points and Wager Cards cost 1 point


The team that purchases 5 Trophy Cards first will officially be crowned the ultimate Scanzle champions – but only if they can prove without a doubt that they're the absolute best team!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Midwinter Gala: Scenario Pack
description:
It’s a party to die for!

It’s the dead of winter, and Arkham’s social elite have arrived in mist-shrouded Kingsport for an exquisite gala hosted by the enigmatic Lantern Club. Unfortunately, it doesn’t take long before nightmarish creatures crash the festivities, and it falls to you to uncover the Club’s dark schemes before Arkham’s most powerful leaders all meet a terrible fate!

In The Midwinter Gala, one to four investigators ally with a faction of their choice and work together to claim the powerful relic at the center of the Lantern Club’s nefarious plot. The scenario can be played on its own or inserted into any campaign as a side-story, and it can also be played semi-competitively between multiple teams of investigators!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Воля: Штурмовий загін (The Will: Assault Squad)
description:
Воля. Штурмовий загін ("The Will. Assault Squad") is a fast, exciting and easy to understand tactical strategy board game.
Play as an assault squad of heroes from the legendary Ukrainian comic "Volya" in 7 modes!
Competently combine the attributes of the heroes and create your own victory tactics!
Time to learn the rules is only 10 minutes.
Fans of the eponymous comic "The Will" will especially like it.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Winterfell Guards
description:
Many have taken up arms to fight in service of Winterfell. Those that have survived many campaigns can find themselves honored with entry into the Winterfell Guard. Normally, they only protect Winterfell from within. But in these dark times, they are once more headed to the front lines.

The Winterfell Guard unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives House Stark players a new melee unit for their armies. These troops gain bonuses as House Stark’s NCUs strategically play across different parts of the Tactics Board.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description: This map is the “Prairie Development” variant of Iowa. Only three towns on the map have card references at the beginning of the game; towns are given further card references during play as more railway lines reach those towns, aiming to simulate the way in which some towns became important during the history of the state and some did not.

Welcome to Magic Hour! With a keen eye and dedication to your craft, you can become the greatest photographer this world has ever seen! Take photos around town, in nature, or in your local studio, develop your shots or make them works of art, fulfill orders from buyers and collectors, and grow your business. Learn new abilities, hire new workers, and sell your work to become a successful photographer! Remember, there’s only so much room in this town for photographers, so work smarter and not harder! Good luck – we can’t wait to see all the wonderful photos you bring to the table!

Magic Hour is a worker-placement and order-fulfillment game that gives you the camera! You'll have a team of photographers who will go out and take photos either in the city, out in nature, or in the studio. You can develop the photos as they were taken, or edit them into works of art! Then, you'll sell your shots to postcard companies, magazines, or art purveyors. You can also learn new abilities and hire new workers to capture more great photos and become more efficient. You've got 5 workdays to collect the most victory points and income in this winner-takes-all scenario!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Railway Rivals Map SV: London & Western
description:
This map for Railway Rivals is a map of South England and South Wales. In the east-to-west direction it extends from London to Taunton; in the north-to-south direction it extends from Cheltenham to the Isle of Wight, and it features the Severn Tunnel and extends as far into Wales as Cardiff.

(It should not be confused with South Sweden, also given the map code SV.)

boardgame: Mini Rogue: The Occultist and The Tinkerer
description:
The Occultist and the Tinkerer are two new characters for Mini Rogue. Like all Mini Rogue expansions, they also fit perfectly into the Council (all Mini Rogue components are compatible with each other).

Sold separately but also included in the Mini Rogue: Beyond the Portal expansion.

The Broken Isles is a 2-4 player pirate themed strategy game.

Each player is a band of pirates searching for one of each of the lost gemstones – a ruby, a sapphire, and an emerald. The first player to unearth one of each wins, sailing off into the sunset with their newfound treasure.

The game is highlighted by:

     Dynamic board rearrangement (the islands are alive)
     Chance bag draws (dig for gemstones)
     Area Control (pirates and captains)
     Strong comeback opportunities (combat power swings)


There are billions of possible starting boards. Each time you play is unlike the last.

For two experienced players, the game may resolve in 20 minutes. For newcomers a game may last 45~90 minutes.

—description from the game designer

Sciterra is a unique trading card game where players compete to be the first to craft all 10 of their faction sigils. Each player takes control of a hero, who will lead the deck in your strategy to craft sigils, and a supporter, who will help you play cards by generating action points. Each main deck will consist of 40 cards that could be monster cards, magic spells, ancient relics, new locations, and charms to control your opponent's zone. This game allows for players to mix and match to create a game plan that fits their game style.

Each player comes to the table with a hero card, a supporter card, a 10 card deck made up of only sigils, and a 40 card main deck. The first person to craft all 10 of the sigils in your sigil deck wins the game. Players take turns playing cards from their hand to deal damage to the opposing heroes knocking them out or triggering the hero power to craft sigils.

—description from the designer

Bring the hilarity and drama of Tinseltown into your home with Casting Call, a dynamic, interactive party card game that is brilliantly easy to play.

Your home becomes a Hollywood battlefield as friends and family transform into aspiring thespians clawing their way onto the silver screen. Watch them ham it up as they compete for a chance to make it big and become a movie star!

Casting Call is your ticket to lights, cameras, and action-packed fun.

Casting Call is an interactive card game for 4-10 players aged 14 and up, where the objective is to become a movie star by collecting three Star Cards. The game is played in rounds, each consisting of three main steps: The Audition, The Callback, and The Star Card award. Players draw and perform lines from Screenplay Cards, then refine their performances based on Callback Cards. Strategic use of "Do You Know Who I Am?" wild cards can change the game by allowing players to disrupt opponents or gain advantages. The director, chosen for each round, evaluates performances and awards Star Cards. The game continues until a player achieves movie-star status by collecting three Star Cards.

—description from the designer

Cordoba is pure beauty. At the May Fair, the city is transformed by the rhythm of flamenco and eye-popping colors. Experience an adventure full of excitement and discover the charms of Córdoba.

In Córdoba, players jostle for space in the crowd trying to enjoy the moment in the Spanish city. With so much going on at the same time, it's hard to decide what to watch. How much time are you willing to invest in each stop?

Created by Reiner Knizia and using a mechanism reminiscent of Battleline, by the same author, players alternate turns playing cards that indicate an amount of time spent in one of the city locations, without initially revealing how much time was played.

When a location has time cards from both players, they are all revealed and that location is temporarily owned by the player who invested the most time there. But this can change, because players can still play time cards at the location.

When both players have used all their time cards, the game ends and whoever has the most location points wins.

Córdoba is the number 8 of the Microline from PaperGames

boardgame: ASL: March Madness 2021 The Three-Player Pack
description:
The Kansas City ASL Club's scenario pack published for their annual March Madness ASL tournament. This pack is dedicated to 3-player scenarios, also including a list of classical scenarios that could be played with three players.

Scenario list taken from the official website:
MM68 Oliwa’s Escape - Near Kowel, Poland, 10 April 1944. Polish Partisans attempt to escape past German and Soviet forces.
MM69 Marsch Rückkämpfer - Near Pripyat Marshes, 28 June 1944. Remnants of Army Group Center attempt to make their escape through Soviet and Partisan units.
MM70 Vilnius Breakout - Vilnius, Lithuania, 13 July 1944. German troops attempt to break out of Vilnius through Soviet and Partisan units.
MM71 The Brits Lend A Hand: Foy-notre Dame - Foy-Notre Dame, Belgium, 25 December 1944. British and American units attempt to cut off lead German elements during the Battle of the Bulge - Part I.
MM72 The Brits Lend A Hand: Boisselles - Foy-Notre Dame, Belgium, 25 December 1944. Following up on their success at Foy-notre Dame, British and American crush Kamfgouppe Böhm during the Battle of the Bulge.
MM73 Guerillas In The Mist - Mt. Mutumbo, Belgian Congo, 1 June 1941. Ahistorical scenario depicting Italian-Partisam-Congelese battle in the heart of Africa.

Enter the Shaolin Temple, and be the first to create the 36th Chamber in this Wu-Tang-inspired adventure.

In Shaolin, each player is a new student ready to train at the Shaolin Temple, earning "cream" — a tangible currency symbolizing respect and skill — by correctly answering Wu-Tang Clan trivia questions. Cream enables a player to master up to five fighting styles, which can be won or lost in one-on-one duels between players.

Once a player has mastered enough fighting styles, they must face off with one of the Temple's Five Masters. If victorious, the player must face the Abbot's final challenge to determine whether they are worthy of creating the 36th Chamber to win the game.

—description from the designer

The Devil is played with any standard 78-card Tarot deck. Free the court cards from the Devil’s grasp by playing cards
from your hand that match the suit of an available court card. Two cards meeting or exceeding the rank and also matching the suit of a court card will free it. Each time you play a card, draw a new one from the deck.

Drawing one of the 8 Disaster cards may cause you to lose cards in play or in hand. However, cards lost to the Disasters become martyrs and their power (from beyond the grave, as saints) will help you towards the end of your mission to defeat the Devil.

If you can free all the Court cards, you will battle against the Prince of Darkness himself in an attempt to banish him eternally.

—description from the designer

A set of WWII miniatures rules presented in Panzerfaust #52 intended to get someone playing with a minimum of time and expense.

The rules are very brief, and basically consist of writing orders to move the tanks (and turn turrets), followed by fire. A number of tanks are given in two charts describing speeds, traverse, armor, and armor penetration.

Home office – an integral part of our working world since Corona at the latest and one of the most relaxed workplaces in the world. At least if neither external parties, colleagues nor your boss ruin your day. But what happens when you have a boss who controls you 24/7 and constantly changes all your passwords? A day in the home office can quickly turn into a gauntlet especially when you find out two minutes before the next meeting that two-factor authentication is now available. Only if you demonstrate a sharp mind, ingenuity and deduction skills will you be able to solve the tricky puzzles in this game and join the meeting on time. And against time!

—description from the publisher

This is a drawing/storytelling game that can be played by groups of all ages or mixed ages. It is fun as a family game, a classroom game, a couples night out game, a teen sleep over game, etc. Players start by drawing one line on their paper. The papers get passed around every 20 seconds and players add to the developing picture - changing what's there if they desire, but without erasing anything. When each player receives his/her own paper back, the timekeeper draws a card from the pile. Each player must tell a story about the picture that ended up in front of him/her that starts with whatever prompt is on the drawn card. Players vote for the most hilarious or best or favorite story they feel should win the round. The first person to collect three cards wins the game.

—description from the publisher

The year is 1930, a time of shadows and hidden secrets. The city, once vibrant and hopeful, now murmurs with ominous rumors. In the dark alleys, the inhabitants whisper about ancient entities, forbidden and unnameable. The economy, like an ancient ritual, twists and turns, feeding something beyond human comprehension.

Forgotten places emerge from the mists, decrepit houses that shouldn't exist, crooked streets that lead to unfathomable chasms. Dreams are haunted by visions of cosmic creatures, snaking tentacles, and all-seeing eyes. Insanity spreads like an evil fungus, corroding minds and tearing veils between our world and the afterlife.

Lonely investigators scour forbidden manuscripts, searching for unspeakable truths. Fear seeps into souls, like an icy mist, and the city becomes a cosmic chessboard, where ancient forces plot their return. Hope is a flickering candle, about to go out, as unspeakable horrors await in the shadows.

Objective of the Game:

Find and stop the Ritual before the Great Old Ones are summoned.

Playing the Game:

The game is played in turns, alternating between the investigators, the monsters' turn and the news from the Arkham Gazette newspaper that brings more mystery and a lot of information.

During their turn, players can investigate, attack, walk, guide allies, spend items to regain sanity and hit points.

Players lose the game if they are devoured, killed, go insane, or the ritual is completed and the Great Old Awakens is completed.

—description from the designer

O ano é 1930, uma época de sombras e segredos ocultos. A cidade, outrora vibrante e esperançosa, agora murmura com rumores sinistros. Nas vielas escuras, os habitantes sussurram sobre entidades ancestrais, proibidas e inomináveis. A economia, como um antigo ritual, se retorce e se contorce, alimentando algo além da compreensão humana.

Lugares esquecidos emergem das brumas, casas decrépitas que não deveriam existir, ruas tortuosas que levam a abismos insondáveis. Os sonhos são assombrados por visões de criaturas cósmicas, tentáculos serpenteantes e olhos que tudo veem. A insanidade se espalha como um fungo maligno, corroendo mentes e rasgando véus entre nosso mundo e o além.

Investigadores solitários vasculham manuscritos proibidos, buscando verdades inomináveis. O medo se infiltra nas almas, como uma névoa gélida, e a cidade se torna um tabuleiro de xadrez cósmico, onde forças antigas tramam seu retorno. A esperança é uma vela trêmula, prestes a se apagar, enquanto horrores indizíveis aguardam nas sombras.

Objetivo do Jogo:

Encontrar e interromper o Ritual antes que os Grandes Antigos sejam invocados.

Jogando o Jogo:

O Jogo é jogado em turnos, alternando entre os investigadores, o turno dos monstros e as notícias do jornal da Gazeta de Arkham que trás mais mistério e muitas informações.

Durante seu turno, os jogadores podem, investigar, atacar, andar, guiar aliados, gastar itens para recuperar sanidade e pontos de vida.

Os jogadores perdem o jogo se forem devorados, mortos, ficarem insanos ou o ritual ser completado e o Grande Antigo Acordar.

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Kuroi is a cyberpunk setting which pays homage to the greatest giants of the Cyberpunk genre, of which the author has been a die-hard fan since the early 90s.

In this game, you become a Covert ops specialist, taking on illegal jobs like robberies, kidnappings, and sabotage.
It's dangerous job, the kind of work clients want to keep off their records. You usually won't know their names. But that’s ok, because all that matters is getting paid.

Kuroi is a solo skirmish tactical game with yahtzee-style dice mechanisms.

Discover the card game easySPRINT, made to discover and improve your skills in Quality by Design.

You are biotechs competing to be first on the market. To do this, you must demonstrate to the health authorities the EFFICACY, QUALITY and SAFETY of your new medicine.

To achieve each of these three objectives, you must collect three separate SPRINT cards marked Q, b, D. The first biotech to achieve these three objectives wins the game.

—description from the designer

THE RACE GAME WHERE COUNTING IS NOT NEEDED
Are you ready for the most colorful speed race on the planet?
Six funny Martians dealing with colors, shapes, and a touch of strategy… who will be the winner of this fun challenge?
The track, which can be composed as desired, allows for 4 game modes that gradually introduce strategic skills.
The presence of cards with turn sequences and the use of color to move the pieces are elements that facilitate playability for everyone, ensuring fun regardless of age and abilities.

Rave is a music themed trick taking card game with special cards that cause unpredictable results.

DJ's accumulate points by announcing how many hands they will win per set and how to close they are to that number in reality.
A swedish trivia game about Jönköping, inspired by Monopoly, Trivial Pursuit and Nya Finans.

This is a limited edition Pokemon-themed card game for 3-6 players based on the traditional card game known as Old Maid. However, there are also special rules in Pokémon Babanuki that players can opt to play with.

For example, players can decide that every time they draw a card, they have to make the sound of the Pokémon. This way other players can figure out where the Old Maid or Gengar card is. Players can also decide to play with the special play cards that give a new existing twist to how old maid si played, such as giving 2 cards to a players or swapping cards.

The game can also be played as a Memory game for 2 to 6 players.

All cards are in Japanese, but the game comes with English instructions

Jump right into the battle by taking command of a team of Heroes or Villains to go head-to-head against your opponent. Start the battle off by moving up your force's Minions to attack your opponent. As you fight your opponent you will earn points for your team. You can use these points to call in the help of super-powered individuals. Build up your forces to help you eventually take down your foe. To do this, keep attacking your opponent until there are no enemies left to fight or until the enemy surrenders.

—description from the designer

The year, 1974. The life, a best-selling author, but the pressure of recent success is crushing the imagination… that and the muttering in the recesses of the mind. Perhaps a trip to the idyllic Pacific Northwest at a lakeside cabin is just what’s needed to kickstart the next novel and soothe troubled thoughts. Pleasant drives on isolated roads. Thoughtful hikes into the woods and high elevations. Perhaps even lingering by the lake for a little fishing or gazing at the night sky for inspiration. Whatever it takes to get the next book done… and keep sane. Hopefully, the disturbing long-running rumors of local disappearances won’t interfere with all this time-sensitive work.

Cult Poker: The Writer is an expansion to the solo card game, Cult Poker. As usual, you use a standard deck of cards to select poker hands to unlock and explore a world of odd secrets. This time you are also desperately trying to overcome your crippling writer’s block and finish your latest novel, but whether you succeed or how you devote your time once you start exploring is up to you.

The game is make-as-you-play, meaning you will modify a deck of playing cards throughout a campaign of games with a permanent marker. Ultimately, you will know your fate and possess a deck that reflects your character’s discoveries and memories. And maybe even you’ll have a marketable novel!

—description from the designer

Escape the room game in a box that takes place in a 20's crime noir setting.  The game is split into two sections and players can compete to see who can figure out the culprit first.

—user summary

It was New Year’s Eve.  A posh party filled with Hollywood elites was being held on the top floor of the Hypnos Hotel, right in the heart of Tinsel Town.  It was a who’s who of movie stars, executives, and underworld figures and not many got an invite to such an occasion as this.  Early in the evening, a well-known actress vanished from the party… and it wasn’t long before reporters got wind of such a juicy story, as they mobbed the hotel for news.  Not long after, someone else wound up dead…right there in the infamous hotel.  The police have given you access to investigate the scene, but be warned, they have also have it on good authority that another Private Eye may have gotten into the crime scene.  Could it be that you will have to compete to figure out whodunit first?  Can you solve both crimes and before the stroke of midnight?

—description from the back of the box

Make split-second decisions to survive. Use sharp judgment and keen awareness to be the last one standing in this intense run-and-gun battle.

Move secretly behind a screen, use items through cards, and roll dice to shoot enemies. Avoid the encroaching blue zone and be the last one standing. WINNER WINNER CHICKEN DINNER!

boardgame: Going the Distance: Boxing in its Golden Age
description:
In Going the Distance, players get to live the drama and excitement of boxing’s golden age. You will wince when your fighter takes a heavy blow, feel exhilaration when your boxer evades a determined flurry, and feel euphoria when your fighter knocks out what seemed to be an indestructible foe. Set in the 1930s and 1940s, players create sporting history with an ensemble cast of unique and larger than life boxers.

There’s boxers like Guido ‘Fats’ Gorgonzola, a big-hearted Italian kid from the Bronx that loves his mama and her cooking, ‘Fair Dinkum’ Frank Kearny, the leather-skinned larrikin from down under who trains with boxing kangaroos, and Everett Hughes, English adventurer, turned Hollywood star, turned elite boxer, to name but a few.

There are 6 boxer classes that have their own style, while every boxer has their own card deck, stats, special rules and strategies that make them unique. Players start the fight with a 40-card Fight deck. When fighters get hit and throw big punches, they take Fatigue Cards which replace cards in their deck. At the same time if the number of times they get hit equals or exceeds their Stamina rating their hand limit drops. Fighters may also either target the head or the body. A head punch is more likely to knockout your opponent while a body punch will cause more fatigue. So, players need to strike a balance between offence and defence while managing their boxer’s energy levels.

A round is made up of 5 sequences in which boxers move in the ring, program three cards and reveal and compare these cards and resolve them. There are ten different card types. The main three are Light, Medium, and Heavy punch cards which defeat each other based on a Scissor Paper Rock mechanic. Other card types are Evade, Block, Combination, Dirty Fighting, Reaction, Counter punch, while some fighters have special cards they can play during a bout.

—description from the designer

In search of the delicious Candy Gems abundant across the land of Crossing, the Usagyuuun are setting off to find the precious sweet gems and satisfy their sweet tooth!

Once peaceful and quiet, the land of Crossing is now crowded with the playful Usagyuuun coming from all kinds of backgrounds: explorers, pirates, or warriors, all hoping to collect the Candy Gems and claim them back to their territories.

Late arrival to the Candy Gem deposits does not mean you miss the chance to get your hands on the shiny treats... because sometimes the gems already excavated by the others are far more easily to collect!

In Usagyuuun Crossing, you have to collect Candy Gems, but players make their choices simultaneously and not everybody will get what they want when desires clash — and even if you are lucky during the initial confrontation, you aren't safe from the greediness of other players.

Through bluffing and cunning, you want to collect more Candy Gems than your opponents. To do this, you place a set of cards side by side on the table, with each card featuring a varying number of gems. When signaled, each player simultaneously places a finger on one of the cards. You collect the Candy Gems only if you are alone on the card!

—description from the publisher

Within the prosperous cities of the Golden Carrot Kingdom do the Usagyuuun and the Nekogyuuun live happily? together. Despite the peace and security of the kingdom, there is a struggle of power secretly going on under the rug. Powerful Usagyuuun and Nekogyuuun are plotting against each other in order to claim all the delicious carrots in the kingdom for themselves. You need to accumulate your carrot wealth and devise cunning plots to get the others out of your way!

In Usagyuuun Coup, you want to be the last player with influence in the game, with influence being represented by face-down character cards in your playing area.

Each player starts the game with two Carrots and two influence – i.e., two face-down character cards; the fifteen card deck (for 2-6 Player) consists of three copies of five different characters, each with a unique set of powers:

Duke: Take three Carrots from the treasury. Block someone from taking foreign aid.
Assassin: Pay three Carrots and try to assassinate another player's character.
Contessa: Block an assassination attempt against yourself.
Captain: Take two Carrots from another player, or block someone from stealing Carrot from you.
Ambassador: Draw two character cards from the Court (the deck), choose which (if any) to exchange with your face-down characters, then return two. Block someone from stealing Carrots from you.
On your turn, you can take any of the actions listed above, regardless of which characters you actually have in front of you, or you can take one of three other actions:

Income: Take one Carrot from the treasury.
Foreign aid: Take two Carrots from the treasury.
Coup: Pay seven Carrots and launch a coup against an opponent, forcing that player to lose an influence. (If you have ten Carrots or more, you must take this action.)
When you take one of the character actions – whether actively on your turn, or defensively in response to someone else's action – that character's action automatically succeeds unless an opponent challenges you. In this case, if you can't (or don't) reveal the appropriate character, you lose an influence, turning one of your characters face-up. Face-up characters cannot be used, and if both of your characters are face-up, you're out of the game.

If you do have the character in question and choose to reveal it, the opponent loses an influence, then you shuffle that character into the deck and draw a new one, perhaps getting the same character again and perhaps not.

The last player to still have influence – that is, a face-down character – wins the game!

A variant character. The Inquisitor character cards may be used to replace the Ambassador cards.

Inquisitor: Draw one character card from the Court deck and choose whether or not to exchange it with one of your face-down characters. OR Force an opponent to show you one of their character cards (their choice which). If you wish it, you may then force them to draw a new card from the Court deck. They then shuffle the old card into the Court deck. Block someone from stealing coins from you.

—description from the publisher

Chàm is a party game for 5 to 10 players. The main mechanism is deduction and bluffing. Unlike other social deduction games, Chàm set itself apart by not allow for players the feeling of getting focused and lose in frustration.

SPECIAL:
Game is released in 3 distinct themes: Wizards, Knights and Pirates.

Pucks & Blades is a two-player game that lasts approximately 90 minutes and simulates an ice hockey match.

The player who scores at least one more goal than their opponent wins the game.

The game rules mimic the core dynamics of ice hockey, allowing for a variety of strategies that ensure an exciting and engaging experience for all types of players.

Each player can assemble their favorite team from available athlete cards and challenge their opponent by implementing tactics and, with a bit of luck, capitalizing on favorable dice rolls.

—description from the designer

Become a master Builder - the architect of destiny on a newly discovered land of Sungrave. Your beloved king has assigned you the task of building towns there for the new settlers. 
This roll-and-write board game invites you to craft a thriving medieval town from the ground up, using your wits and resources to shape the future of Sungrave Island.

This land is blessed with a wealth of materials: wood, stone, iron, and bricks. Use them to construct vital structures like a farm to feed your people, inn to rest weary travelers, harbor to trade with distant lands, bank to secure your wealth, stable to house your noble horses, and much more!

As a sign of their appreciation for innovation, the islanders will grant additional rewards to the first builder who constructs a unique structure, as they eagerly embrace new opportunities.

Knight will also send the settlers each with various talents: Knights, Merchants, Craftsmen, and Peasants. They will help you with your mission by increasing the value of buildings they want to live next to.

Manage your own tiny town, use settlers' skills to your advantage and become the governor of Sungrave Island as that is the reward for best architect among builders.

—description from the publisher

Atlas is a tableau-building game where you take the role of a backpacker with a special ability, personality, and language. You have saved up money for a journey of a lifetime around the world! Like any long journey, strategic decisions have to be made as you balance traveling and exploring with your available money and energy level.  With 235 Countries and 865 unique destinations to visit, each play of Atlas creates a unique journey and offers interesting real world facts about each of these areas. Where will you go next?

When you explore a destination you gain experience icons. Each experience will reward points in the end based on its popularity which the players will shift during the game.

Develop as you learn new languages, make new friends, complete personal goals while racing against other players for Public Goals, collecting souvenirs first or posting the most popular Blog Posts. Tough decisions have to be made for every action, boost yourself by experiencing things you like to gain possibilities to perform bonus actions. This might bring you closer to achieving even more points.

The game ends when a player places their last explorer token. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
What wonders are hiding in these curious pictures? Is that a crouching lion? Or a shy rabbit? Or is it both? Find out who’s waiting on the other side of the looking glass! Turn halves into a whole with the help of mirror cards: a curved line upon closer look turns out to be a ripe eggplant, and funny squiggle is actually a giant palm tree. Play against each other or in teams: the first player to find 10 objects wins!

In Half-and-Seek, various objects are hidden on the large colorful cards... The players, however, must recognize them only by their halves! 
With the help of a mirror, look for hidden objects on the picture and be the first to collect 10 small cards.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Verlag Arbeitsmittel für Lehrer und Schüler

boardgame: Kemet: Blood and Sand – The Baboon
description:
This game-up gives you access to the Baboon Miniature, a creature that is part of the new Amethyst tiles.

The Baboon will increase the strength and mobility of the troop it joins, as well as providing you with more veteran tokens!

In addition to a miniature, you'll also get access to the baboon amethyst tile, as well as two new tiles, one for the Sapphire set and one for the new Amber set.

boardgame: Kemet: Blood and Sand – The Hippopotamus
description: Though the Hippopotamus may look a bit friendly and clumsy, it's actually one of the deadliest animals in Africa. Its mastodon jaws and speed of movement make it and absolute menace. That's why we've chosen to make it a simple creature, but one with devilishly effective combat characteristics. And that's not, all, because you also get two other lvl 3 amber tiles with it!

Gift-giving. Enchanting thematic abilities. More wondrous creatures. As the winter season approaches, it's now your zoo's chance to make an impression during its biggest event of the year – ZooLights!

The highlights from this release include:
• 8 new polar animal cards
• 2 new habitats; Prairie & Woodlands
• ZooLights cards that can be worth up to 9 stars!
• 14 new Zoovenirs cards to make the Most Memorable Zoo award more attainable and competitive
• A Statue card that can change winning conditions
• A White Elephant Party event where everyone gets a free animal...and wait until you see how!
• So much more! So let's play ZooLights and... LET’S LIGHT UP THE ZOO!

ZooLights continues to amp up the nostalgia and excitement of each zoo-building game night! You'll likely be playing with it year round!

-description from Kickstarter campaign

boardgame: Zoo King: Clouded Leopard & Orangutan Promo cards
description:
Two additional cards to add to your Animal market deck to increase variety in your games:
the Clouded Leopard and the Orangutan.

This treasure map is not a Room card.

Don’t shuffle it with the others, but instead keep it aside.

With this independent module, roll the Dungeon die at the start of each Area to determine which path you need to take in order to get a nice bonus! Roll the die again to determine which bonus you can earn.

These Lore cards have been inspired by masterpieces of the literature.

These three campaign exclusive cards are:
- The Tower of the elephant;
- The Inn; and
- The Bounty Hunter.

These cards integrate perfectly into the game if you own The Council, the Depth of Damnation expansion and/or the Sentinel and Crypt promo cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zoo King: ICONS Vol. 1 – Down Under
description:
Introducing the new ICONS series! These thematic booster packs will bring some unique additions to the game, each focused on a specific type or region of animals. Packs will contain 7 animals and 1 staff, facility, or event.

The first ICONS booster pack features Australian animals from the outback:
• Koala
• Wombat
• Tasmanian Devil
• Emu
• Dingo
• Thorny Devil
• Cassowary
• Tree Kangaroo
• Surprise Animal
• TV Personality
• Reptile House

- description from publisher

Unleash the cuteness and conquer the competition! Pet Domination: Kittens vs Corgis is an engaging and fast-paced party game where adorable pet influencers go head-to-head for social media supremacy. Collect Followers, attack your friends, and defend against rival players in this fun-filled experience. Will Kittens or Corgis dominate as the most popular social media pet? You'll have to play to find out!

Gameplay Overview:
Turn Structure: Each turn, play a card from your hand to build Posts, which contain Followers, or to attack rivals. Optionally, play a defense card facedown to protect yourself from future attacks. End your turn by drawing a card.
Follower Collection: Followers are gained by playing Post cards from your hand.
Attacks & Defense: Steal Followers or sabotage opponents with attack cards. Defend your Posts with defense cards.
Character Dynamics: Begin as an Underdog, protected from some attacks, and flip to Fat Cat at 7 million Followers for extra cards in your hand but increased vulnerability.

Winning the Game:
1) Achieve the Follower Goal with at least 4 million more Followers than any other player,
OR
2) Have the most Followers when the Post deck and discard pile run out of cards.

Dive into the competitive world of Pet Domination, where strategy, timing, and a bit of luck will determine the top influencer!

—description from the designer

Smallfolk is a cosy tableau builder about myths and folklore from around the world.

Each player takes on the role of an Adventurer and journeys through the world, collecting helpful smallfolk, finding powerful relics, and visiting mythic wonders. Find heroes along the journey to help you, and amass Victory Points by playing cards into your story.

The game ends when all the Victory Points in the middle have been claimed. Count up the Victory Points you've collected and the value of your cards to determine who won, and then shuffle the story deck and play again!

—description from publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Humanity has defied the Abyss. The changes brought about by the Renaissance led men to question themselves, and among them, one had the revelation that salvation would no longer be achieved through faith. He, the Supreme Leader, founded the 
Order with the sacred purpose of reaching Heaven through Inferno, conquering the territory by force. Embodying the role of one of the generals of this brotherhood, organize your troops and extend your influence to claim the Circles in the name of your mission. However, be careful not to become like those you came to fight.

Play your cards strategically to score more points and become the winner,or demonstrate your skill by achieving an immediate victory through the accumulation of sins! Be careful, for you are in the Inferno. Here, your sins can lead you to defeat!

—description from the publisher

May 7, 1824, Vienna, Austria

The premiere of Beethoven’s ninth symphony becomes a stage of death. You are a hardened chief of police who has seen better days. Can you solve the most bitter case of your career?

Everyone in the crowd becomes a suspect in a double murder. Can you unravel the deadly plot?

—description from the publisher

Joyride: Unleashed introduces the dreaded Beetle-Gator, a mutant beast that wreaks havoc as it scurries across the track.


    Safari 4x4 is on the hunt - a car with incredible cornering flexibility but only at very low speeds.
    Balance the odds with Gum Traps that force your opponents to slow down too!
    Four mini-obstacles to help design your own tracks.


Every element is fully compatible with Joyride: Survival of the Fastest and Joyride Duel: Next Gen.

With the Joyride: High Seas expansion,


    Be a menace on the track with the Pirate Van, stealing items from other racers then fleeing the scene at speed.
    Use the Harpoon to reposition other cars at a distance, or drop the Anchor for a high-stakes emergency brake.
    Place boost strips around the track to give racers a surge of speed whether they like it or not! 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Richard Scarry's Busytown Seek and Find Adventure Game
description:
In Richard Scarry's Busytown Seek and Find Game from Briarpatch, young children will play a fun, engaging game to race around a jumbo-sized board to find hidden characters like Father Cat, Sally Cat, and the Pig Family. This board game also features two levels of play, suitable for both younger and older players. With its competitive gameplay, large game board, search-and-find elements and colorful design, it's sure to be a hit with both kids and parents alike.

The game comes with a board, a bell, 4 pawns, a Goldbug card deck, and two Seek and Find decks of cards. The Level 1 Seek and Find deck contains one image that can be found on the board, while the Level 2 Seek and Find deck has two images, one of which is a decoy image and cannot be found on the board.

Players take turns spin the spinner, and moving their pawn around the board. If the player lands on a magnifying glass icon, that is a "Seek and Find" space and each player takes a card from the Seek and Find deck, flips them over at the same time, and races to be the first player to find their image on the board. The first player to find their image rings the bell, winning the card they drew. If you land on a Goldbug space, draw a card from the Goldbug deck which includes things like: Take a Seek and Find card, discard a Seek and Find card, move forward or backwards a space.

If you can be the first player to collect 5 Busy Town Seek and Find cards you win!

This is yet another variation of The Game of Life. In this game, the players try to accumulate as much money as possible by completing four major "life" events:  Employment, Marriage, Children and Retirement.

The object of the game is to complete a set of three "life events" in order to "check" that event in the player's life.  After acquiring three matching "event" cards, the player acquires a "check card" which indicates they have completed the event.  The "check" cards come with varying amounts of money, and may affect all players...not just the player completing the event!

The first person to complete the four events ends the game, but the most money wins!

Run the Rails transports players into a vibrant world of train travel, where the thrill of building and navigating railways through stunning landscapes takes center stage. The game's charming illustrations and engaging components bring the railway theme to life, creating an immersive experience that captures the essence of adventure and strategic planning.

In this competitive game, players take turns playing Rail Cards in descending order from Center Rail Cards, ensuring they match the designated train series. Pass Tokens offer strategic flexibility, allowing players to skip turns or fold when necessary. Completing train series or parts of them grants opportunities to swap or claim Elite Status tiles, adding layers of strategy. The objective is to accumulate points, with the game concluding when a player reaches 175 points. The player with the highest score emerges victorious, crowned the master of the rails!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot: Almond Crisp Expansion
description:
Our Half Color Bunnies give players like you, a choice of color between the two. The Killer Bunnies Almond Crisp Booster Deck adds 55 cards to your existing set plus six multi-colored Pawns!

Your opponent's threats may seem vague, until he unleashes an Urban Plague! A squeezie, an eclair, a sandwich of squid, are never the cards you want to forbid. Grand Gamp Dole will help you protect, opponents' bunnies that weapons affect. Carrot claiming Seasons may earn you four, but Zero Zero may leave you with no more!

Pawns give privileges and extra uses to powerful P-cards and low roll abuses. Log Lady and Aubergine are not quite as mean, as the virus named Epideme! Boredom will egress in a dissolving wisp, while enjoying the next Booster Deck, Almond Crisp!

—description from the publisher

Anirollz the Card Game is player over several turns. Each player is dealt a collection of Anirollz character cards to start the game, which they place facedown in front of them.

Each turn, players draw a card from the Anirollz deck and decides to swap the card with an existing card in their collection, discard the card, or trigger the card's special ability.

Play continues until a player has completed their Anirollz collection, in which they are declared the winner.

The cute gameplay may sound easy but beware of the special ability cards which allow players to disrupt everyone else's collections.

This games includes characters from all your favourite Anirollz collections including: Pandaroll, Kittiroll, Puppiroll, Owlyroll, Foxiroll, Bunniroll, and Chickiroll.

—description from the publisher
24 full colour interlocking map templates for Legions of Steel.
24 full colour interlocking map templates for Legions of Steel.
24 full colour interlocking map templates for Legions of Steel.
24 full colour interlocking map tempates for Legions of Steel.
24 full colour interlocking map templates for Legions of Steel.

This third expansion contains 120 new cards. It’s compatible with the basic game and its previous expansions.

This expansion reworks the Deserter and Soldier tribes from 0, after the rotation of the Fundamentos set from the Domination Format. It develops the Mage tribe, that was introduced with Pacto Secreto, but not yet a tribe as such with several cards of that subtype. And also, it brings support for the Pirate tribe.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Crosswits is a crossword puzzle-based parlour game that may be played by two to eight people separated into two teams. Each team receives a blank crossword puzzle and the opposing team's completed crossword puzzle and clues. Teams alternate in giving and receiving clues in order to complete the crossword puzzles. The winning team is the team that completes its crossword puzzle first.*

There is an A card and B card for each of 10 rounds. There is a 30 second hourglass timer. There are 2 notepads, 2 pencils & 10 stump chips.

—description from the publisher

Doug Norton is a veteran of railgun units and carries his experimental armor into the field. This experimental armor channels energy into the railgun, greatly increasing the penetrating power of his weapon. Norton's skills as a leader speak for themselves and inspire other railgun units on the field, allowing them to repeat failed hit rolls.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 20
