—description from the publisher

The MSON-12 drone hovers autonomously over the playing field, providing excellent support to its battle group. It specializes in completing mission objectives and completes its task so efficiently that it even scores additional victory points.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 10

—description from the publisher

Clayton Baker impresses with his ability to unexpectedly appear anywhere on the playing field during the game. This tactical superiority allows his opponents to come under direct fire or to immediately claim distant mission objectives for victory.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 20

—description from the publisher

Welcome to a land far before our time, where dinosaurs populate the world and adventure lurks behind every tree! You are young dinosaur explorers, fearlessly fighting your way through the wild thicket of the forest, which is full of shady Baddies and valuable surprises. An epic & competitive adventure awaits you on the hunt for precious Dino Points!

15 exciting modules allow Dino Dungeon to grow with the demands of your little Dino explorers. Choose the modules based on your mood and adapt the complexity to your playgroup.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Raquel is able to support her teammates in distress with valuable healing. Always close to the action, where the battle is raging most fiercely, the agile medic can even bring lost allies back to life or evacuate them from the playing field to deny the enemy victory points they thought were safe.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 15

—description from the publisher

The Railgun Squad takes on its opponents with the most advanced kinetic weapons of the New World Order. Its unparalleled fire penetrates cover, keeping opponents on their toes and securing terrain for hazard-free maneuvering of all friendly units.

Type: Squad
Point Costs: 20

—description from the publisher

This add-on brings gorgeous shiny companion cards into the game. One special multicolored card per companion.

During the final scoring, each of these multicolored companion cards must be assigned to exactly one color. This color assignment must be maintained throughout the final scoring and applies to the scoring of the other modules as well.

The base game is required.

—description from the publisher

Morael spreads fear and terror on the battlefield and manipulates the morale of his opponents. If the morale of the opponents dwindles, Morael prevents them from making moves or lowers their attack power against him. Morael moves across the battlefield flying and can ignore terrain.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 20

—description from the publisher

Galmon excels in defense against imminent dangers. Thus, he can put away enemy fire with ease and protect his allied angels. Galmon moves across the battlefield flying and can ignore terrain.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 20

—description from the publisher

In battle, Zephaniel takes control of a squad of Malakim and uses them for two entirely new strategies. He lets the individual Malakim perish in frightening, grotesque shadows to break the morale of his enemies. Alternatively, he plunges himself and his entourage directly into the heart of the enemy forces to snatch selected targets from the enemy line and hurl them at the feet of the remaining angels, straight to their certain death.

Note!
Although a battlegroup may contain 2 Malakim-Squads, only 1 Zephaniel is allowed to be played!

Type: Squad Upgrade
Point Costs: Epsilon: 10 / Sigma: 10

—description from the publisher

When Puriel attends a battle, rarely anything is left unburned. Whenever he pleases, he can create an ever-recurring Flame Shard (Hero). Together, they inflict tremendous fire and area damage on the opponent through skillful positioning. As quickly as he creates it, Puriel can also spontaneously summon his creation back to him when needed. Drawing a furrow of destruction in its wake, the Flame Shard heeds his call, becoming one with Puriel once again and closing its wounds in the process, only to reappear on the battlefield shortly thereafter.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 30

—description from the publisher

The Malakim squad swoops down on its opponents as a swarm and rakes them with its claws. Should the need arise, the Malakim can also initiate a self-destruct protocol and take their opponents down with them. The Malakim move across the battlefield flying and can ignore terrain.

Type: Squad
Point Costs: 10

—description from the publisher

The world of Bheta-Decima slides towards utter ruination. From the lightless void comes a Nemesis Claw of the Night Lords Traitor Legion, twisted warriors motivated by cruelty and selfish desire. Yet as these ancient predators hunt for technological treasures on the ocean-rigs of Bheta-Decima, they are hunted in turn by the Mandrakes of Aelindrach – and it may be that even these sadistic monsters have met their gruesome match.

Kill Team: Nightmare is the latest expansion for Kill Team, plunging you into a ghastly shadow-war amidst the industrial ruins of a dying world. Take command of two new kill teams – a Nemesis Claw squad of murderous Chaos Space Marines, and a band of slinking, supernatural Mandrakes – as they fight over a large Generatorum Hub terrain piece that upgrades your killzones. You'll also find a book packed with background information, new missions, and rules for both kill teams, plus two sets of datacards and tokens to keep your games running smoothly – all exclusive to this box.

This boxed set contains:

     96-page softcover Kill Team: Nightmare book: All the background, artwork, and rules for both of the two kill teams in the box, plus the ongoing story of the anarchy spreading on Bheta-Decima, as well as new Narrative Twists, a Shadow Operations: Nightmare mission pack, and rules for the Generatorum Hub terrain piece.



     Nemesis Claw Kill Team: Chaos Space Marines drawn from the vicious and sadistic Night Lords Legion. These multipart plastic miniatures include 10x Night Lords with a variety of weapons and cosmetic options, as well as a Chaos Space Marines transfer sheet. You'll also find upgrade parts for specialist operatives like a cruel Fearmonger, grotesque Ventrilokar, and a Skinthief wielding a vicious Nostraman chainglaive.



     Mandrakes Kill Team: Otherworldly shadow-assassins from the outer depths of the Drukhari's Dark City. These multipart plastic miniatures include 10x Mandrakes armed with glimmersteel blades, as well as optional upgrades to create specialist operatives such as a balefire-slinging Abyssal, a terrifying Nightfiend, and a greatblade-wielding Chooser of the Flesh.



     Generatorum Hub: A large, multi-level mechanical marvel which powers the extraction-rig over which both kill teams are fighting. This multipart plastic terrain piece is intended for use with the missions found in the book, alongside the Killzone: Bheta-Decima terrain set.



     Datacards: Keep games running smoothly with reference cards detailing each team's operatives, abilities, equipment, Ploys, and Tac Ops, plus reference cards for the accompanying token sheets. Includes a deck of 20x Nemesis Claw Kill Team cards, and a deck of 21x Mandrakes Kill Team cards.



     Token Sheets: Easily track abilities, objectives, equipment, and Command points throughout your game with team-themed tokens. Includes 1x Nemesis Claw token sheet, containing 47x double-sided cardboard tokens, and 1x Mandrakes token sheet, containing 49x double-sided cardboard tokens.


—description from the publisher

The hero K319-X is our special event miniature and will be available on special occasions (e.g. fairs). K319-X is a mercenary and can be used by all factions. At the beginning of the game, special loyalty cards are shuffled and two cards are drawn, these determine K319-X's loyalty and abilities. So he has a different ability in each game and can influence the game itself. Is he a machine? Then he can deal direct hits on his opponents as an assassin. Is he a human? Then as a Whistleblower he can reduce the damage he receives. As an outlaw, own and opposing units give victory points!
K319-X was developed in cooperation with our partner Godot Games and their game Human Punishment.

Type: Hero
Point Costs: 20

—description from the publisher

The world of Bheta-Decima is a ruined corpse, a toxic and crumbling nightmare. Even in this desolation, a Genestealer-infected regiment of Brood Brothers musters for global insurrection. All their martial expertise is tested to its limits, however, when they encounter a ruthless and resilient band of Hernkyn Yaegirs. These hardy survivors of a crashed vessel will baulk at no deed to see that their legacy outlasts the alien-tainted soldiers hunting them down.

Kill Team: Termination is the latest expansion for Kill Team, plunging you into a desperate fight for resources and control on a dying world. Take command of two new kill teams – zealous Brood Brothers, led by a scheming Broodcoven, and a band of determined and heavily-armed Hernkyn Yaegirs – as they fight over two Plasma Generatorium terrain pieces that upgrade your killzones. You'll also find a book packed with background information, new missions, and rules for both kill teams, plus two sets of datacards and tokens to keep your games running smoothly – all exclusive to this box.

This boxed set contains:
– 88-page softcover Kill Team: Termination book: All the background, artwork, and rules for both of the two kill teams in the box, plus the ongoing story unfolding upon the ravaged planet of Bheta-Decima, as well as a Shadow Operations: Termination mission pack, and rules for the Plasma Generatorium terrain pieces.

– Hernkyn Yaegir Kill Team: Hernkyn Yaegirs are ruthless commandos who protect the claims of the Leagues of Votann. These multipart plastic miniatures include 10x Hernkyn Yaegirs with a variety of weapons and cosmetic options, as well as a Leagues of Votann transfer sheet. You'll also find upgrade parts to build specialist operatives like a sniping Yaegir Riflekyn, stealthy Tracker, or determined Ironbraek.

– Brood Brothers Kill Team: Unswervingly loyal to their cultist comrades, Brood Brothers are Genestealer Cultists concealed within the Astra Militarum, ready to turn Imperial weapons and training upon their family's foes. These multipart plastic miniatures include 10x Brood Brothers armed with scavenged lasguns, who come with a Cadian Infantry transfer sheet and plenty of upgrade parts for specialists like heavily-armed Gunners or a faithful Iconward. There are also 5x Broodcoven models to lead your team, including a psychic Magus, scurrying Familiars, and even a looming Genestealer Patriarch.

– Termination Plasma Generatorium: Two modular pieces of versatile industrial infrastructure, integrated into the extraction-rigs of Bheta-Decima. These multipart plastic terrain pieces are intended for use with the missions found in the Kill Team: Termination book, alongside the Killzone: Bheta-Decima terrain set.

– Datacards: Keep games running smoothly with reference cards detailing each team's operatives, abilities, equipment, Ploys, and Tac Ops, plus reference cards for the accompanying token sheets. Includes 23x Kill Team: Termination Datacards, 22x Hearnkyn Yaegir Kill Team cards, and 24x Brood Brother Kill Team cards.

– Token Sheets: Easily track abilities, objectives, equipment, and Command points throughout your game with team-themed tokens. Includes 1x Hearnkyn Yaegir token sheet, containing 62x double-sided cardboard tokens, and 1x Brood Brothers token sheet, containing 54x double-sided cardboard tokens.

You'll need a copy of the Warhammer 40,000 Kill Team Core Book to use the contents of this set. You'll also need the Killzone: Bheta-Decima terrain set to create the mission maps in the book

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Warhammer: The Old World – Arcane Journal: Orc & Goblin Tribes
description:
Vast tribes of Orcs, Goblins, and Trolls dwell far from the palaces, cities, and castles of the Empire and Bretonnia. Should these warlords ever stop fighting themselves and unite into a cohesive force, they would sweep away all opposition in a brutal tide of violence.

This supplement takes a close look at the brutal badlands where the Orcs and Goblins hold their claims. This is a place where might makes right, and the largest, meanest, and most cruel dominate their rivals. Used in conjunction with Warhammer: The Old World – Ravening Hordes, this book arms you with even more in-depth rules, allowing you to use your collection of brutish Orcs and cunning Goblins in new and exciting ways.

Within this 48-page supplement book, you will find:

History of the Badlands – A closer look at the rough, arid landscape that the Orcs & Goblin Tribes call home.

A Waaagh! Abroad – Follow in the tracks of a Nomadic Waaagh! Warboss as he loots and burns his way through the Border Princes and beyond, as well as a narrative scenario that features a classic mountain pass ambush.

Heroes of Legend – Background and rules for two infamous characters: Kiknik Toofsnatcha and Ogdruz Swampdigga.

Armies of Infamy – Rules to field two unique Orc & Goblin Armies of Infamy: Nomadic Waaagh! And Troll Hordes.

This is an expansion to Warhammer: The Old World – you'll need copies of the Warhammer: The Old World Rulebook and Warhammer: the Old World – Ravening Hordes books to use the contents of this book.

Yssantis is a descendant of those who once came to Earth from the planet Nibiru to prevent the Earth from falling again. Due to the longevity of the Nibirians, it has been possible for him to travel the world in the course of many human generations. He had made it his task to find out the secret threads behind the veiled history of the world, to unravel them, and to record this knowledge. He refuses to hide his true form, but also mostly travels only in a meticulously selected circle of people. This circle is limited – with one or two exceptions – to representatives of the most influential interest groups with whom it seems worthwhile for him to cultivate diplomatic relations. Yssantis does not take sides with any of the many earthly factions in this conflict of gigantic proportions and is careful to maintain his absolute neutrality. It is thanks to the extensive knowledge collected in his notebook that he shares with those in these encounters that humanity is not completely unprepared to face the fate of the earth. Only a select few, however, are actually allowed to take a look at this precious ring binder themselves.

—description from the publisher

With the severe reduction of the human population, sightings of creatures long-thought to be extinct have grown more and more frequent. Use the Creatures-Add-On to throw up to four entities into your game that act independently of the players. Some of them are aggressive, others are well-disposed and interact with the player, while still knowing how to defend themselves. Creatures move and fight entirely on their own and influence the game with their unique skills. Give your Rapture a wild, untamable twist.

—description from the publisher

The most unexpected treasures are usually found in the most deserted places - you only need to keep your eyes open. With the Loot-Box-Add-On you can find countless pieces of equipment, weaponry, and handy paraphernalia and put them to use. All of them offer special skills and bonuses and open up entirely new possibilities. The add-on gives you two options: Quickly utilize ready-to-use resources in a single game or keep your equipment over the course of multiple games, distribute it among your troupes, and develop an undefeatable battle force! Loot everything that's not nailed down and claim your victory!

—description from the publisher

Expand your game with additional missions that are only included in this set.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Jim Henson's Labyrinth: The Board Game – 40th Anniversary Collection
description:
Will Sarah manage to defeat Jareth and his Labyrinth, or will the Goblin King turn little Toby into a goblin babe? You have thirteen hours to find out!
Relive your favourite movie with the Collector’s edition of Jim Henson’s Labyrinth: The Board Game! New “Humongous” expansion, exclusive miniatures, revised rules, and a unique box to keep your collection!

The complete collection includes:
Core Game
Goblins Expansion
Fireys! Expansion
Humongous Expansion
Revised Rulebook
Card Game
Adventure Game
Ready, Steady, Worm!
Exclusive Miniatures Set
Exclusive Collector’s Storage Box

The main expansion is of course the Humongous expansion, featuring a giant miniature, a game board and a set of cards that allow you to play a very strategic and yet fun mini-game, either on its own, or integrated with your games of Labyrinth the boardgame. 
 Then, the next most exciting new expansion we think is the ‘Play as the Worm’ expansion, which adds a new character/player sheet to the game. Now you can play as the Worm, which has a very peculiar set of rules – a very different profile from the other main characters… as you can imagine he does not excel in Brawn or Speed, but his knowledge of the Labyrinth (and therefore his Wit) is without peer! And comes with the customary two cards for his Strength (‘Cup of Tea?) and his Weakness (‘I’m just a Worm!).
 The last two expansions are the Trash Lady and the Wiseman. These follow the same principle and format as the Goblins! and Fireys! expansions, providing new rules that you can use alongside the new miniature to modify the normal boardgame.
 The Wiseman is more static, he remains where he is drawn, and is potentially a source of helpful information that will boost your Willpower… but there is a risk that he will just waste your time with infuriatingly useless gibberish!
 The Junk Lady is more dangerous, she will challenge you as normal when drawn, but if you manage to resist her powers, every turn there is a chance that she will chase Sarah around the board to try again to sway her from her mission… watch out!
 Combined with the other expansions and the optional rules in the rulebook, these new characters allow you to enrich your experience of the Labyrinth boardgame and adapt it to your taste and level of experience. Have fun!
family: Series: Jim Henson's Board Game Collection (River Horse)
Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
These connection tiles, labelled as "Windfarm Bonus Tokens" during the Kickstarter campaign for Pampero, were given out as a reward to anyone who pre-ordered or Kickstarted the game.

The rules for these tokens are:
Receive $1 x the column with the rightmost A, B/C, or overall contract tile on your grid (as indicated on the tile)

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – The Secret Expansion
description:
Quench your thirst for adventure in the Secret Expansion by FOLLOWING THE CAMPAIGN and BACKING EARLY. Fortune and Glory await courageous Adventurers as you race against your rivals, take down Nazis, and solve 2000 year old mysteries.

Includes 4 Scenarios:

The Lost Idol
The E.O.P is asked to help an expedition into the Tomb. They are after a Golden Idol and they don't want it to fall into the wrong hands, Can they be trusted?

Raiders of the Lost Tomb
Beat a band of Nazis to a powerful relic which is central to their plans for world domination. They are trying to use the Tomb to get it!

Tomb of Doom
Enter the Tomb in search of a magical stone, and uncover an ancient evil which threatens all who come into contact with it.

The Tombs Crusade  
A past member of the E.O.P went missing in search of the holy grail. Can you decipher their journal and find them before the Nazis do?

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Ancient Myths and Legends Expansion
description:
Will you win the race for the Holy Grail against the German Super Soldier Maelstrom Squad? Or survive the night with a Tomb filled with vampire mobsters? Can you protect an ancient spider queen while she spins her magical web? Find Captain Nemo’s submarine before the Russians steal it. Explore these and more in Ancient Myths & Legends!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Reign of Terror Expansion
description:
Can you travel through time and space to stop the Time Terrorist before it’s too late? New items, story cards, companions, and more await you in Reign of Terror!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – The Great Apocalypse Expansion
description:
Will you survive the plagues of Egypt and escape the grasp of Death himself? Can your team undo the prophecy and make their own fate in a race against the Four Horsemen? Will you recover the secret to da Vinci’s greatest or worst invention to stop the dead from rising and destroying humankind? The Great Apocalypse is upon us!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Elite Missions Expansion
description:
Arriving at the tomb, you are tasked with an additional Elite Mission. These will alter your play and add new challenges, stories, and rewards. Many Will attempt these missions, but few will succeed. Will you?

Add a new level of depth and challenge to the Epic experience. Use one or more Elite Mission cards to overlay any existing Secrets of the Lost Tomb scenario, each with a diverse storyline and deadly consequences. Many will attempt these missions, but few will survive. Test your might in the Elite Missions!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Fate's Fortune Expansion
description:
Is Fate what you make for yourself? Or is it preordained? You’ll traverse the Tomb to find the hidden traitor from within the Eternal Order of Perseus, your Order. Find this traitor and their plan - and protect the world as we know it.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Passages and Perils Expansion
description:
Add a new dimension to the Tomb, giving you new forms of egress between levels, new strategies, and dangers to overcome. The Tomb becomes both easier to navigate and more deadly. Encounter new traps, set traps for creatures and even other competitive players! Stay on your guard in Passages and Perils.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – The Lost City of Atlantis Expansion
description: Dive deep into the lowest level of the Tomb and the most secret: The Lost City of Atlantis! In the newly discovered 4th level of the Tomb, you'll explore all new secrets and magical artifacts to protect and use against even stronger enemies! Atlantis calls out for The Eternal Order of Perseus to help it in its greatest hour of need. Will you hear its call?

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Indescribable Underworlds Expansion
description:
Welcome to the world (or otherworlds) of Lovecraft! Enter the Tomb and leave the world you know behind as you traverse strange and exotic realms that have merged themselves with the Tomb! Learn the secrets of Lovecraft’s true journey and how one of the most famous members of the EOP helped preserve our world as we know it.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Cults and Conspiracies Expansion
description:
Mind control devices, secret government cover-ups, aliens, clones, and more, what is true and what is just a false rumor? Pull back the veil and see what is really going on in Cults and Conspiracies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – The Pantheons of Power Expansion
description:
Gods playing games, the world becoming a Minotaur's Labyrinth, Gift horses, unleashing your inner spirit beast, the tomb has become a Pantheon, will you be able to stop Gods from wreaking havoc on the world? Find out in Pantheons of Power!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – The Meridian of Medusa Expansion
description:
The activation of Medusa’s Meridian has upset a delicate balance in the cosmos. If we cannot solve the Mystery of the Thirteen in time, the mystical energies of the Tomb will become unusable, ripping a hole in our space-time continuum. All in a day’s work.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Far East Far West Expansion
description:
Travel to The Wild West, delve into Asian folklore, communicate with Spirits throughout time, and find items imbued with spiritual energy that are more powerful than they appear. Help solve mysteries of ancient times and more in the Far East, Far West!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Mesoamerican Mysteries Expansion
description:
Legends of Mesoamerica are rich with magic and lessons learned... however, following the warnings of the Gods may be one of the hardest things to do... Islands Attack, Ancient Spirits of the Tomb speak out, and lost creatures return.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Wicked Hellscapes Expansion
description:
Deals with the Devil, Lost souls, fire, and brimstone, the heat is turned up! In this all-new expansion, choose your path carefully as you are pushed to the edge of sanity in Wicked Hellscapes!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Epic Edition – Search for the Lost Tomb Expansion
description:
An Epic Adventure! The Tomb has replicated itself worldwide, and players must move around the world map, going into each Tomb, exploring it, or trying to find the correct Tomb for a unique scenario experience. This is a new way to begin each game!

—description from the publisher
Introduces a new playable species to the Phantom Epoch experience: the Volanen.
Adds three new playable classes to the Phantom Epoch experience: the Arsonist starting class, and two secret advanced classes.

boardgame: Dawn of Madness: The Written Rift
description: Up ahead you see them: two more weary souls, drawing ever closer to our little band. One is a woman, her clothing decidedly upscale and the smell of expensive perfume clinging to her skin, even in this wretched place. You seem to remember seeing her picture before. Maybe in a magazine? In fact, you can almost think of her name. Anita... Clavell. That was it! Now you remember: she's that author who seems perpetually stuck on the bestseller lists. You've even read a couple of her books. You never did quite understand what people saw in them, but they certainly were popular. It seems you remember reading she was a lonely person, with no real family to speak of. But you know that sometimes the written word can take on a life of its own, becoming just like a dear friend or family member. In fact, sometimes it even seems like it can become a portal to other worlds.

boardgame: Dawn of Madness: The Forgotten Void
description: The Forgotten Void is a Wanderer expansion featuring Mordecai Ashford the Anthropologist. This expansion adds a whole new chapter to a Dawn of Madness campaign, complete with its own twisted story.

boardgame: Dawn of Madness: The Numina Abomination
description: The Numina is an Abomination Pack featuring the epic Abominations, the Numina. This expansion adds the Abomination family the Numina to any chosen campaign of Dawn of Madness.

boardgame: Dawn of Madness: The Executioners Abomination
description: The Executioners is an Abomination Pack featuring the Abominations the Executioners. This expansion adds the Executioner family to any chosen campaign of Dawn of Madness.

boardgame: Dawn of Madness: The Depressions Terror
description: The Depressions is a Terror Pack featuring the three Depressions. This expansion adds the Depression Terrors to potentially any chapter of Dawn of Madness.

boardgame: Dawn of Madness: The Watchers Terror
description:
The Watchers is a Terror Pack featuring the three Watchers. This expansion adds the Watcher Terrors to potentially any chapter of Dawn of Madness.

This expansion includes:
3 Terror Cards
3 Standees
3 Mental Capacity Trackers
3 Miniatures

A real-time children's game based on the poem by Julian Tuwim!

Players try to pull the turnip out of the ground as quickly as possible. To win, they must enlist the help of Grandpa, Grandma, the grandson, Mruczek, and the other characters from the poem!

Między wierszami is a series of games based on children's poems. Rzepka ("Turnip") is the first game in this series.

—description from the publisher
The Imperial Flavor Pack contains 9 new Events focused on the Holy Roman Empire.

The city is burning! A devastating fire spreads unstoppable, smoke and sparks swirl through the streets, and the desperate cries of trapped civilians echo through the night. But in the midst of this inferno, there is hope: the Fire Angels, an elite unit of firemen and women, rush to the rescue. Equipped with the latest technology and an indomitable will, they face the flames to save lives and fight the fire.

The cooperative board game Fire Angels delivers thrilling fire rescue action for 1 to 4 players. It’s made for adults and children of 8+ years and takes around 60 minutes to play. Each mission is different because the building gets defined randomly at that moment you spot rooms and corridors. Because of that "fog of war"-like effect, it’s also really thrilling to be played in solitaire mode.

—description from the publisher

This is a box that includes the Act II "Emperor's Legacy " and all applicable extra material from the Kickstarter campaign.

It includes all the 42 exclusive stretch goals from the campaign like the Shinigami, Sumo-Sapo or Shogun big miniatures as well as new scenarios, new cards, new tiles, custom insert, upgraded dices and many more miniatures.

Add new 4 Connnect 5 Shape Cards and 1 Objective Card to the game

4 Connnect Shape Cards - to calm these card, all numbers must be the same only. You will get 2 additional points.

1 Objectiver Card - Fill the row or column with 1 to 6 in ascending or decending order at least 1 line

Murder or accident? Several deaths must be solved on a country estate, and every detail counts! In the co-operative mystery card game Unsolved: Landgut des Grauens ("Estate of Horror"), the three chapters are solved exclusively by using pictures: All players look at picture cards and exchange information about possible clues that will help solve the case. Together they decide which eight cards they will lay out face up in order to clarify the most important questions of the case: Who is the victim? Is there a murder weapon? And who killed the victim and for what reason...or was it an accident?

—description from the publisher

In this 18XX-like game your objective is to accumulate wealth operating spaceships while avoiding the costly pitfalls of failing space ventures. To succeed, you must carefully manage your investments and manipulate the ownership of shares within the new interstellar frontier, all in pursuit of prosperity and dominance in the distant reaches of the cosmos.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Several civilizations have been lost to time during these periods of reset. The start of a new cycle is approaching. There are those who are aware of the end, and some who wish to stop it. If we can learn the secrets of the universe, we may be able to restore balance.

Command Your Legions. Expand Your Bases.

Legions of Will is a strategy-based card game.

Activate and expand your bases. Gain resource at the start of your turn. Upgrade to increase your capacity, defense and resource production. Attach traps and units to your bases. Attack with units, if they have the Will, and destroy each of of your opponent's bases to win the game.

—description from the designer

How long since the darkness? The sun does not rise. The light does not shine. Forgotten horrors emerge from the abyss. But there are rumors. Deep sailors swear they’ve seen it, submerged in the cold waters below: a drowned sun waiting to be found. Beneath the recesses of memory and sanity, where forsaken caverns remember your deepest fears, beneath the moonlight, lower, lower still… Will you follow its siren song? Will you find the Sun Beneath?

The Sun Beneath is a cooperative, asymmetric 2-player game. One player takes on the role of the Cavern, and the other takes on the role of the Captain. With limited communication, both players work together to navigate the Cavern’s shifting tunnels, fend off Horrors, and investigate Rumors to locate the Sun Beneath.

Starting with the Cavern player, players take turns performing actions in sequence until the game ends, either when they win by retrieving the Sun Beneath Token, or lose by accumulating too many Fear Cards or when their Supply Deck runs out.

—description from the designer

Unearth the fascinating world of fossils with Fossil Sweep! Fossils come in various forms, from hardened animal bones to intricate plant imprints.

In Fossil Sweep, players use strategy and decision-making to match the fossil layers on the cards in their hands to the card layers on the dig site. Cards have different point values, and the goal of the game is to discard as many cards as possible within each round. The player with the fewest total points at the end of the game wins!

This strategy and decision-making game can be played by 2-5 players ages 8 and up in 20 minutes.


    BONUS: Game includes a fun fact insert that teaches players about prehistoric eras when fossils were formed.


—description from the publisher

Hi, landscaper! There is a peaceful flower bed filled with elegant roses and lively trumpet creepers, eagerly awaiting you to make it blossom in full bloom. Together, players embark on a collaborative journey to build a stunning floral pergola.

In this game, you aim to complete as many full flowers as possible by connecting vines to vines and flowers with flowers of the same color. It takes strategy and a touch of luck. Special insects will have bonus effects! This brings thrilling moments to the gameplay. Each turn, players perform the following phases:


     Action Phase
     Fulfillment Phase
     Hummingbird Phase
     Replenish Phase


The game ends when the cloth bag tiles run out or the fourth Hummingbird tile appeares on the market. The player with the highest score wins.

As experienced employees, you must judge the spatial relationship of furniture combinations and make the most reasonable arrangement on the truck. It requires a delicate sense of balance, and a little luck.

In this game, each time has a total of 4 rounds. and each round consists of 4 phases in the following orders:

     Order phase
     Loading phase
     Rating phase
     End of round phase


Players take turns to roll the dice, then try to figure out how to load the different shapes of furniture onto the truck efficiently, and obtain the service rating points. Game ends after 4 rounds. The player with the highest service rating points wins, and receives the "Best Employee Award"!

Let’s get moving!

Nezapomeň na nohy ("Mind your feed") is a set of educational board games designed for children, their parents, and educators of kindergartens and elementary schools.

Through a playful and entertaining approach, children
acquire habits that support the proper development of their feet and the development of the musculoskeletal system in general. During the game, children actually exercise, making it engaging even for the most restless. The game is very playable even in larger groups. Included in the game is a manual for parents and educators, which helps to correctly understand the individual game tasks. Thematically, it loosely follows the successful illustrated book of the same name.

The game offers a total of five different variants, adapted to the age and number of children.

—description from the publisher

You have always wanted to become the new pizzaiolo of La Caso della Pizza. Unfortunately, you aren’t the only one with that dream… Today, it is up to you to show the current pizza maestro that you are worthy of being his successor. Of course, this means dealing with your devious delivery competitors. Victory is only guaranteed if and when you can sabotage, steal from, and slow down the others. Using the new missions and Chef’s notes, you further your agenda and leave the other delivery divas in their dust. After all, that pizza chef apron fits you best! Si?

The Mean Chef is the perfect sidekick to Pizzachef. You’ll need the original board game to play this expansion. Unleash your inner misch(i)ef with extra ways to spice up the fun. The additional Chef’s notes and missions amp up the interaction, and scenarios infuse new flavors into the game. La Casa della Pizza awaits, and every scenario brings a twist to keep you on your toes. No game is ever the same—get ready for a flavor-packed adventure!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rock Hard: 1977 – Jackie Fox Promo Character
description: This character promo comes with all the playing pieces that you'll need to play as Jackie Fox – the designer of Rock Hard: 1977.

Blockminers is a cooperative deck-building board game. You play as a group of hackers in a dystopian cyberpunk future, and your goal is to infiltrate the bank CapitAll. If you run out of money to pay the rent, you lose.

The game is played in days, where each day has three phases: start of day, when players draw cards, workday, when cards are played, and end of day, when rent is paid.

During the workday, the turn continually passes around the table, and each player plays one card. Some cards force players to draw encounter cards, which are almost always bad. However, players choose when to play them, and it is often possible to play the cards such that their downside is reduced.

When all cards are played, players pay rent and proceed to the next day. If rent cannot be paid, it's game over.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Corrupted by a crystal of unknown origin, various creatures from Chinese mythology have invaded Japan, and spread their influence to banish the Kamis, who for eons have protected those lands.

Led by the Azure Dragon Seiryū (Qinglong), the Chinese Celestial Beasts attempt to subjugate the inhabitants of Japan, and it will be the duty of several characters from the Chinese folklore to stop them before it is too late.

GAMEPLAY HIGHLIGHTS

This expansion combines the map, on which the Chinese creatures will be from the beginning, with epic battles against them, for which you will have 4 new characters to choose from, with new abilities and powers.
Also, the effects of the Shogun's influence will be replaced by permanent effects on the map due to the presence of the Celestial Beasts, among which you will have to choose the order against which you will face to unlock the path to the final combat against the Dragon himself.
In addition, this expansion will also feature new equipment cards and skills, with which to face the threat coming from China.

—description from the publisher

In this prequel to the events of Act 1 of Harakiri: Blades of Honor, we will follow the path of Jubei, who will be immersed in an intense adventure to try to change his own destiny.

The Oni King will be the final obstacle in the path that the ronin will have to follow through the events.

GAMEPLAY HIGHLIGHTS

Thought in detail for single players, in which you will be able to control Jubei, the most powerful character in the world of Harakiri.
 Be part of the evolution of the story and character, through components that will be revealed step by step during the campaign, and that will transform our protagonist into an uncontrollable force.
 Without the help of the Kamis, Jubei will rely on his ability to accumulate his energy and the Wrath mode, which adds a new tactical layer to the game that rewards strategy in combat.
 Take on the Oni King and his spectacular miniature representation in an epic finale to discover Jubei's fate.
 2 scenarios to face the enemies of this expansion with a group of 4 characters in one-shot sessions.

—description from the publisher

Who will hop the farthest?
4 Hopper families are on vacation on tropical islands: the seals, the crabs, the octopuses and the frogs. They decide to have a race for fun. Who will be the fastest?

Happy Hoppers is a fun family race with cute cubic sea animals, in which you will try to cross the finish-line the first and gather as much pearls (victory points) as you can.

Each player has 3 Hoppers and a finish-line of their color that they are aiming to cross sooner than the other players cross theirs. As soon as any 5 Hoppers cross their finish lines, the game ends.

A player’s turn consists of two steps:

     Roll 3 dice
     Move the Hoppers according to the following rules
         The dice indicate, which Hopper (big, medium or small) move and how many times.
         Hoppers move clockwise.
         If a Hopper stops on an island with another Hopper, it jumps on top of it forming a stack. When a lower Hopper moves, it moves with all the Hoppers it has on top.
         As soon as a Hopper crosses their finish-line, they take a place on a central island, which will score them the more points, the sooner they did that.
         When a Hopper stops their movement, they receive a bonus from the island.
    


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pairish: The Building of Sonnie Village
description:
Pairish: The Building of Sonnie Village is a fast cooperative board game for 2 to 8 players. You gather crops and care for pets and farm animals while building the picturesque Sonnie village. Pairish is suitable for big and small, experienced players and those new to the hobby.

Pairish has two play modes: Newcomers and Old-timers. The Newcomers mode is suitable for kids, people new to playing board games and for a first game so that you get familiar with the rules. The Old-timers mode is for more experienced players or those who want to feel a challenge. In both modes, the goal is for every player to collect a Pairish. You have collected a Pairish when at least 6 out of the 7 cards in your hand are equal by one of the stats - number of windows, floors or slopes.

In Old-timers mode you have to also complete the Mayor’s task. He decides what crops you should grow and what animals to take care of and in return, finances you with the village’s grant.

During one turn, you complete 4 actions: draw a card from the deck, build a house with the card face down, flip the card face up and give one card from your hand to the player to their right. To win, every player needs to have collected their Pairish so you have to take care not only of your Pairish but help the other players as well.

Will you succeed in completing the Mayor's task before the clock strikes? It’s up to you and your fellow villagers. Good luck! And take care of each other’s Pairish…

—description from the designer

Eau Claire Traction is a strategic game of investment and negotiation. Players place investments on the various locations around Eau Claire, Wisconsin, then influence the building of the trolley line to capitalize on their investments. Players can also invest in attractions in an Electric Park being built in Lake Hallie, and are able to influence investments and routes by using their connection to a prominent member of the city.

The game has been created by Oddly Specific Board Games as a project to bring obscure topics to the table. This game integrates an approachable rule set with high player interaction to create an experience that is enjoyable for the family as well as more heavy gamers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dorfromantik: The Board Game – Die Wetterau
description:
Dorfromantik: The Board Game – Die Wetterau is a three-tile expansion for the base game, with these tiles featuring regional motifs of the Hessian Wetterau, that is, fertile tracts of land watered by the Wetter tributary in the western state of Hesse. The tiles feature the Adolfsturm, the Pegasus, and the Winterstein with the Steinkopf television tower. (Pegasus Spiele is headquartered in the town of Friedberg in Hesse.)

It offers a new gameplay factor to explore and allows you to reach even greater highscores. This mini-expansion can be stored inside the Dorfromantik box.

The new ultimate party game that will light up your game night! Get ready to have your adrenaline pumping as you and your friends race against the clock to make it through 21 rules. The 45s timer lights up the board and flickers faster as players count from 1 to 21, each saying the next sequential number while following the hilarious rules on the board. Can you make it through all 21 before time runs out?

LIGHT UP GAMEBOARD: Light Up your game night with magical gameboard that has light printed within it. It’s a race against the clock to make it through 21 rules before the light runs out.


CAN YOU MAKE IT THROUGH 21 RULES? It’s a race against the clock to make it through 21 rules before the light runs out.

HILARIOUS CONTENT: You will be laughing during each rule as your try to make it through 21 rules.

ADULT CONTENT: Turn up the heat with the hilarious ADULTS ONLY cards!

COLLABORATIVE PLAY: It’s a party game that you play together! You and your opponents have to collectively make it through 21 rules before time runs out.

—description from the publisher

People crowd at the entrance of the coliseum and rush to get a good seat in the stands. Everyone is hoping to profit from this special event. The atmosphere is building, the voices get louder and louder, and then?

Silence. Caesar speaks. Let the games begin!

PUGNA is a game of betting and bluffing where you will manage where you will play your gladiators and where and when you will bet on them... both to win and to lose!


You will play 14 alternating turns (7 per player) where you will decide whether to place a gladiator or place a bet with their deck of 7 cards with values from 0 to 6.

But be careful! You can only bet twice and will be applying effects that may drastically alter the course of your strategy.

—description from the designer

1978: The Night THEY Came Home is a 1-2 player horror roleplaying game telling the story of a fateful showdown between Survivor (a clever and resilient person caught up in horrifying circumstances) and Slasher (a legendary force of evil). Using a deck of poker cards, dual rule books, varied resources, and shared play space, players will recount the events of a forsaken Halloween, zooming closer into the haunting folklore of a small Midwestern town and its inhabitants, and culminating in a violent battle for survival.

Run a 5-star coffee shop by providing exceptional customer service, in this co-op memory card game!

Players are baristas working together at the latest coffee in town. Your coffee shop starts with a rating of 3 stars. Your goal is to maintain stars and stay in business for 7 days. You do so by providing exceptional customer service to your regulars by memorising their names and coffee orders!

Each day customers will come into the store ordering coffee with their names. The daily order comes in the form of customer cards. On the back of the customer card will have a portrait of the customer. On the front will have a customer's portrait, name and coffee order. The head barista will call out the daily order with each customer having a name, face and coffee order that the barista must memories.

These customer cards will be dealt to each barista and held up so other baristas can only see the faces on the back of the card. Starting with the head barista, they will call out a customer from another barista's hand by calling EITHER a customer's name OR coffee order. If they are correct the customer is satisfied gets their order and they become a regular of the coffee shop! To provide "Exceptional Customer Service" a barista must call out a customer's name AND coffee order. If they are correct that customer will give a star rating to the cafe and will be regular! However, if the barista makes a mistake the customer leaves a negative review and the coffee shop loses a star rating and they will never come back. If it reaches 0 stars the coffee shop is shut!

Each day more and more customers will come into the shop with day 1 having 1 customer per barista, then day 2 having 2 per barista, then day 3 having 3 per barista etc. Survive 7 days of business to win!

—description from the designer

Number of Chapters in the Game: 2

We need you to intercept a golden trunk which is en route to Nottingham. What is inside… you have no idea, but you need to get that trunk before it arrives to the dastardly Sheriff. The best bet you have of stealing that trunk lies with joining the legendary Robin Hood and his Merry Men and planning a robbery of the coach that carries it. But not all is as it seems. Prince John is throwing a lavish festival and many nobles are attending, so the guards in Sherwood forest are on high alert. Even worse, spies in the castle tell us that the Sheriff of Nottingham is setting a trap for Robin Hood, so even the bravest of the Merry Men are a bit skittish. It will be up to you to plan and carry out the heist and to stop the Sheriff’s slippy scheme before it is too late!
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Predictable is a guessing game, testing how well you know what your friends would answer to weird and wild scenarios, as well as how well they know you. It's "Would you rather... ?" but with points!

Predictable can be played as individuals or as teams. Players  take turns being the Predictor. That person or team will silently choose a ridiculous or difficult scenario with two options for players to choose between, such as "Would you rather all vegetables taste good OR all deserts are healthy?"

The Predictor's job is to correctly guess which option each player or team will select for the chosen card. After the Predictor has secretly made his or her guesses, the remaining players will have an opportunity to openly discuss the options, then secretly make their selections. The Predictor earns points for each name he or she guessed correctly for each option, and bonus points if the majority of players chose the option that he or she predicted.

The role of Predictor then passes to the next player/team, and after each player has been the Predictor (or the desired number of rounds have been played), the player or team with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Some of the Chick-fil-A Cows are having a party to help spread their “Eat Mor Chikin” message, but silly them, they forgot to share the party location with all their friends! Can you and your Cow friends visit different locations around town, gather clues, and find the party?

Take turns spinning the spinner to get one of three options:  1. Mooooove 3-6 spaces towards location spaces where you can reveal location cards.  2. Get a clue card and check whether or not it's a clue to the location. 3. Advance the clock by one hour.

Once you find the three correct clues, investigate the location cards to find the one that matches all three clues.  But, you have to work together quickly, as the party ends at 8:00pm!

—description from the publisher and the rulebook

A Big Box edition from the Joyride Kickstarter. This edition includes all the contents from:

     JOYRIDE: Survival of the Fastest
     JOYRIDE DUEL: Next Gen
     Joyride: High Seas
     Joyride: Unleashed
     Joyride: Downtown
     Joyride: Big Day Out


Plus the rainbow bridge, energy drink, magnet, and crossover track guide.

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Are you ready for the best Vegas vacation ever? In the Greatest Game Vacation, you and your fellow players compete to experience the best vacation at Vegas.

In this roll & write game, you will roll your dice and attempt to score the highest in your choice of six Vegas-vacation themed activities. Each activity will present its own challenges and rewards. After five rounds players compare their scores to see who had the best Vegas vacation ever.

—description from the publisher

You work the night shift at the museum. When you start your rounds, you discover that the artworks come to life after dark! You will have to keep an eye on them or they will run away and wreak havoc in the city. If you survive the number of rounds according to the chosen difficulty level, you win!

This cooperative game allows children ages 7 and up to develop their knowledge of works from famous museums from different regions and historical periods.

There are four main rooms in the museum: Africa, The Americas, Asia, and Egypt. You are provided with a poster of each room with the works of art contained in each.

Shuffle the night shift, escape, and alarm cards in three separate piles. Flip one night shift card for each round, determining how many escape cards get flipped that round. Escape cards include a silhouette of a work of art. Players search the posters to find the work of art and its name. If all artworks from the escape card(s) that round are identified, you move onto the next round. If you cannot identify an artwork, flip over an alarm card and answer the trivia question correctly to be able to move onto the next round.

HotShot Pickleball is a fast-paced, pun-filled board game where you play a version of pickleball -  as pickles!

After picking a pickle like Kim Chee, Sally Kraut, Stewcumber, or Baby Dill, each player draws five rally cards allowing you to move to, hit the ball, or take advantage of combos and unique actions.

The game is played by each side taking turns to play a card or combination of cards to get to the ball and hit it back over the net.

If players can't play their cards or just want to walk on the wild side, they can pull from the Long Shot deck, which if drawn they must play from. Some of these cards are great like slams, while others aren't so great, like negative self-talk.

The first team to reach 5 points wins. There are different rules available for full scoring to match the sport as well.

—description from the publisher

In this game, test 5 ways to be "cap" (able): reflexion, physical, scene, music, dingo. Find more than 300 challenges each more crazy than the other. Whom will win the game?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Cap ou pas cap: Chiche! Testez 5 façons d'être cap : réflexion, physique, scène, musique, dingo. Retrouvez plus de 300 défis plus fous les uns que les autres ! Qui remportera la partie ?

—description from the publisher

Whimsy is an innovative game of storytelling where each player tells the story of a specific character on a unique personal quest which could be anything from saving the world to finding their lost hat. On their turn, players stack cards to create unique situations and tasks that they have to solve on the way to fulfilling their quest.

Players solve tasks by handing card-combinations representing possible solutions to each other. Whenever someone chooses the card-combination you have passed to them as their approach, your odds of succeeding in your own tasks go up.

Thus, Whimsy offers a unique combination of freedom and structure facilitating creative storytelling that is solidly supported by the mechanics.

Games typically last around 20-25 minutes per player.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Thunderstone Quest: Warlord – Saga of the Storm
description:
A Warlord-themed Side Quest to add to your game!

Defeat Monsters to add Legendary Medusan Heroes to your party! Also includes a new Guardian to battle!

This micro expansion contains:

     Rules Card


Prestige Class

     Medusan Lord


Guardian:

     Stormwraith - IV, V, VI, VII


Heroes:

     3x Lady Bhaine
     3x Life
     2x Archangel Elial
     2x Nassiral Hate
     1x Caer
     1x Emperor Khallaek


Side Quest

     Head of Snakes

The Zulu Prince Dabulamanzi kaMapande wants to exploit the victory at Isandlwana and gain honour, so he decided to attack with it's 4500 men the British outpost at Rorke's Drift held by just the B Company, 2nd Battalion, 24th Regiment ... It will be One of the most gallant actions of the British Army! (Description by the Designer)

Sled Runners of Pekari is a table-top racing game for 2-6 players. Each player is in charge of one or more sleds, each carrying a crate of precious gems. Each sled has two goblin “sled runners”. One sits up front to steer and brake the sled, the other sits in the back atop a large crate, keeping a wary eye out for other goblin sleds. The crate is overflowing with small boxes that contain precious gems and other useful items. Your sled must race down the treacherous, snow-covered mountain passes of Mt Pekari to deliver your valuable cargo to the markets at the foot of the mountain. Along the way you will encounter many obstacles, including the ornery sled runners from the other players! In this racing game, the winner is not the player to make it to market first, but the one that is the richest once all sleds have sold their cargo at market.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Other Side of the Hill: Twilight Generals Expansion
description:
This expansion adds 16 new general cards and counters, as well as field marshal cards and winds of war cards, which introduce new mechanics to the game:

Twilight Generals
More Generals! More variety! These Generals will bring more variety to your games. All are generals whose involvement took place at the end of the war, in 1944.

Winds of War
What effect might wolf packs have on The Other Side of the Hill? How might Admiral Canaris' activities be represented? Could players have more control over diplomacy rolls? The ten cards in the Winds of War deck add flavor and variability to the game by allowing players to relax some of the rules of the game, whether in combat, strategic warfare, technology projects, the Production phase, and so on. At the beginning of the game, two cards are dealt to each player, who keeps one of them and must discard the other. Each Winds of War card includes the prerequisites that must be met in order to play it. A Winds of War card can be used only once and then must be discarded from the game.

Field Marshals
Field Marshals were the highest rank in the German army of World War II. All generals aspired to obtain the coveted field marshal's baton. Field Marshal cards offer this prize to players who conquer key cities in Europe: Warsaw, Paris, Athens, Sevastopol, but also Moscow, Leningrad and Baghdad. This award can also be given for major victories on the battlefield or in Strategic Warfare. In addition to offering flavor and immersion, in terms of gameplay the Field Marshal cards are an alternative method for resolving ties when deciding the winner of the game in competitive mode.

—description from the publisher

Dino Survival is a strategic, ecosystem-building card game about prehistoric life. Unlike many dinosaur-themed games that use dinosaurs as mascots or simple battling pieces, Dino Survival focuses on how real ancient animals lived, interacted, and survived. Every card represents a creature that once existed, and every turn reflects how species competed, coexisted, or shaped their environment.

Players score points by building a stable ecosystem composed of dinosaur and prehistoric animal cards. Each card has a point value represented by gems. The total points needed to win adjusts based on the number of players. In multiplayer games, both herbivores and carnivores contribute to your score, but predators can attack other players’ creatures, shifting the balance of power.

The game also includes a single-player mode with a different scoring dynamic: herbivores you play count toward your score, while all carnivores (even ones you play yourself) count against you. Solo play becomes an ecological puzzle, balancing stability, diversity, and survival while controlling the spread of predators.

There are six major types of cards in Dino Survival, and each shapes your strategy in different ways. Sauropods allow unlimited hand size and protect against discard effects, forming the backbone of large, stable ecosystems. Defensive herbivores such as Stegosaurus can block predator attacks. Aggressive or armored herbivores, including Triceratops, Pachycephalosaurus, and Ankylosaurus, can eliminate carnivores outright if they are big enough, disrupting aggressive strategies. Smaller dinosaurs and early mammals allow players to cycle, trade, or filter cards, helping refine hand efficiency. Pterosaurs provide access to the discard pile, enabling clever retrievals and late-game reversals. Carnivorous theropods, such as Tyrannosaurus and Allosaurus, hunt and remove opponents’ cards from play, allowing bold strategic strikes that can change the course of the game.

And then there is the Asteroid. When played, it wipes out nearly everything on the table and forces players to go one more turn with what's left before the winner is crowned.

The base game focuses on dinosaurs and a selected group of prehistoric animals from the Triassic, Jurassic, and Cretaceous periods. However, Dino Survival is designed as a journey through all of prehistoric life on Earth. Our first expansion, The Cambrian Explosion, is nearing completion and depicts the earliest complex ecosystems: trilobites, anomalocaridids, early fish, and reef organisms in ancient oceans.

Future expansions will progress through time, allowing players to experience the Paleozoic seas, the Mesozoic reign of dinosaurs, and the Cenozoic ice ages of mammoths and saber-toothed cats. Players will be able to choose how to explore this timeline: playing each era as its own game, moving through Earth’s history sequentially, or combining all expansions into one massive deck to explore “what if” encounters across time.

Dino Survival invites players to do more than compete. It creates curiosity about the past, asking players to consider how life adapts, endures, and survives across millions of years. It is a game rooted in real natural history, built to inspire wonder, strategic thinking, and discovery.

—description from the designer

The North Pole is an 18-card game where you play as one of the helpers at Santa's Workshop. Each player is assigned two wish lists to complete. Players do this by drawing, playing, passing, and stealing cards.

On your turn you will start by drawing until you have two cards. Then you can either play a card and use its ability, or discard a card and draw a new one. Once you have taken your action, you check to see if you have completed a wish list.

Wish lists are completed by having 2 of the 3 items on your list in your hand by the end of your turn. The first player to complete both wish lists wins the game.

The game is for 2-4 players and plays in about 20 minutes. It is a great game for families to play together at the holiday times. You can combine multiple copies of the game to increase the player count!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Back to the world: Board Game
description:
The board game, Back to the World is about four players, released from prison on probation after 25 years. They are to go back into the world of mischief, immorality, and envy to accomplish their long-enduring goal. Despite the challenges the out-comers look to combat along the way, they are determined as ever to arrive at the top. But only one among them can make it to victory.

There are 6 Essentials of life on the board: Good health, accommodation, Family/Friends, Education/Employment, Finance, and Society Attitude. These are the properties the players need to achieve on the board to ascertain the victory they aspired for. They will compete in the form of hustling to accumulate points. But as easy as it seemed, the eyes in their respective ‘hustle box’ are the hypocrites, the envious, committed to stopping their dreams from coming true. Therefore, the struggle is between the players and the enemies. On the other hand, a battle between the players as they challenge each other to exchange one another’s seeds for their good

It is a competitive, challenging, interactive, and somewhat real-life experience!

Out there on the street of life, no matter how tough the struggle on the platform of time, the journey is to defeat the hypocrites, and to avoid going back to prison at the end of the day!

—description from the publisher

Something foul is afoot in the Tanglewoods. It's been weeks since you've heard from your grandmother, so you're cinching up your belt, tossing on your cloak, and throwing your trusty axe across your shoulder to venture into the heart of the woods, where her cottage is tucked away (Why does she live in the heart of the woods, you ask? Excellent question. It's super dangerous. Maybe ask her that when you get there).

Tanglewoods Red is one deck in the new trilogy of Tanglewoods decks for the solo-player 20 Strong system. Each deck in the 20 Strong series is built on a basic set of rules and plays using a single set of 20 dice (as well as one single chip). However each deck adds unique rules and twists to the basic system, resulting in each individual deck playing very differently from each other.

The object of a game of 20 Strong is to progress through a shuffled deck of cards, each card bearing a unique challenge. This challenge could be in the form of an enemy, a unique scenario, or some other requirement. Challenges usually require a certain number of successes to complete, which you earn by rolling a set of 17 dice with varying odds of hitting (these dice, along with 3 adjustable stat dice, make up the “20” in 20 Strong).

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
You find yourself lost in the woods, wanting nothing more than a soft bed to sleep in (but not too soft) and a nice bowl of hot porridge (but not too hot). Being lost in the woods wouldn't be so bad, though, if you were alone, but you're not... Somewhere in these woods, looking for you, are three apex predators (but not too apex).

Tanglewoods Gold is one deck in the new trilogy of Tanglewoods decks for the solo-player 20 Strong system. Each deck in the 20 Strong series is built on a basic set of rules and plays using a single set of 20 dice (as well as one single chip). However each deck adds unique rules and twists to the basic system, resulting in each individual deck playing very differently from each other.

The object of a game of 20 Strong is to progress through a shuffled deck of cards, each card bearing a unique challenge. This challenge could be in the form of an enemy, a unique scenario, or some other requirement. Challenges usually require a certain number of successes to complete, which you earn by rolling a set of 17 dice with varying odds of hitting (these dice, along with 3 adjustable stat dice, make up the “20” in 20 Strong).

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Goldilocks and the Three Bears

description:
The jealous Evil Stepmother has banished you into the dangerous forest, and then, thinking better of it, has sent a wide variety of goons to finish you off. Your only hope is to navigate the treacherous trees and get back to the Stepmother's castle, where you will fight her most trusted minions before bringing her to her knees for all of the evil stepmothering she's been doing. She's giving stepmothers a bad name! They're the mothers who stepped up!

Tanglewoods White is one deck in the new trilogy of Tanglewoods decks for the solo-player 20 Strong system. Each deck in the 20 Strong series is built on a basic set of rules and plays using a single set of 20 dice (as well as one single chip). However each deck adds unique rules and twists to the basic system, resulting in each individual deck playing very differently from each other.

The object of a game of 20 Strong is to progress through a shuffled deck of cards, each card bearing a unique challenge. This challenge could be in the form of an enemy, a unique scenario, or some other requirement. Challenges usually require a certain number of successes to complete, which you earn by rolling a set of 17 dice with varying odds of hitting (these dice, along with 3 adjustable stat dice, make up the “20” in 20 Strong).

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
Basra, Pisti, Pazur; Scopa has variations around the world! Scopa United combines these into one fun and simple to play modern game.

Across the world, the basic game play is the same, but each region calls it something different and have their own unique rules.

Scopa United combines the best features of variations from around the world into one fun and simple to play modern game, for the whole family.

Scopa United is played in rounds. On your turn, you must take one of the following actions:


     Play a card from your hand to capture a card from the table. eg. 8 of Gold can pick up an 8 of Relics etc.
     Play a card from your hand to capture multiple cards by matching the sum of their numerical values. eg. 8 of Gold can pick up a 5 Swords and a 3 of Diamonds.
     Play an action card according to the instructions on the card, along with a suit card from your hand.
     Discard a card from your hand by placing it face up on the table.
     Place all captured cards in a score pile in front of you for later.


If you capture all the suit cards remaining on the table, it is called a Scopa!

—description from the designer

eShop is a strategic economic game where 2-4 players manage competing online stores over four rounds. Players draw item tokens from a bag to stock their shops, then fulfill customer orders by spending matching goods. The core challenge lies in balancing inventory management with order completion while adapting to changing market demands.

Each round consists of two phases: stocking your shop with items, then fulfilling orders or removing unwanted inventory. Players earn money by completing order cards, which require specific combinations of colored item tokens. The key strategic element is collecting sets of same-colored order cards to form valuable collections - three or more cards of one color double in value and become protected from competitors.
The game features a dynamic turn order system where players with fewer savings go first, creating tension between spending money and maintaining advantageous positioning. Special star tokens provide discounts when fulfilling matching-color orders, adding another layer of strategic planning.
The climactic final round shifts focus to direct competition as players buy order cards from each other at fixed prices, racing to complete valuable collections before the game ends. Victory goes to the player with the most money from completed orders, active star bonuses, and remaining savings.

—description from the publisher

Embark on a Nordic Adventure with the map of Finland for the Rolling Trains game. Get ready to traverse the picturesque landscapes of Finland, which is originally full of pristine lakes, dense forests, and charming glades await. Your trains will take your cargo to Helsinki, the vibrant capital of Finland, to cities like Ivalo, Oulu and more! Fish is the most common to carry here, but the iron is most valuable.  Lay your tracks near the breathtaking views of the Baltic Sea. Get your conductor’s hat on, assemble your railroads, and let the Finnish adventure begin!

Provided components:

     map sheet of Finland in color version,
     map sheet of Finland in lowink version,

Alternative history game that starts with an attack of a combined Russian & Belorussian force against northern Poland, followed up with a NATO counteroffensive. The NATO forces include a Polish, US, Czech and Lithuanian contingets and a small free Tatar force.

The game features two large scenarios (divided into 2 smaller sub-scenarios each), the first focuses on the Russian offensive south, the second one on the NATO counteroffensive. Victory points are scored for control of key cities around the map.

Are you ready for the fastest draw in the Wild West?

Be the fastest cowboy and win all the duels!

Objective of the Game:
Be the first to get rid of all your cards.

Setup:

Shuffle the cards and deal them into equal piles, face down, to all participating cowboys.
Place one card in the center, face down, in front of all the cowboys.
How to Play:

Each player draws three cards from their draw pile to hold in their hand.
One player flips the center card over, and the game begins.

Players must identify what is common between the cards in their hand and the center card.
(COLOR, FIGURE, OR QUANTITY)

To play a card from their hand onto the center card, the player must announce the color, the name of the figure, or the quantity of figures shown.

As cards are played, players must draw more from their draw pile. At any time, a player can swap cards from their hand, but they must always have three cards in hand.

Note: Players can play consecutive cards on the discard pile if they meet the requirements of the top card.

The game continues until one of the cowboys has no cards left in their hand and draw pile.

Spy Code is a mathematics duel game in which each players is a spy who wants to solve the world!!

The game has 84 different challenges, and the player who collects five challenge cards first wins. To solve a challenge, you need to place dice of the correct value and correct color in the proper places so that you can achieve the requirements, then grab the challenge card first.

You can also play Spy Code as a solitaire logic puzzle without a real-time aspect.

The components are 8 dices, 42 challenge cards, board of each player and rulebook and solution book.

Red lolly, yellow puppy, green kitty, blue berry! How long can you hold on to the lollipop to ensure sweet victory?

In Red Lolly Yellow Lolly, you're challenged to hold the color-changing lollipop while looking at the card in play. Can you correctly say the flashing color with the name of the matching image, without twisting your tongue? Slip up, and you pass the lollipop to the player on your left!

With randomized colors, a new card flipped each turn, and your friends spinning the spinner for new directions, there will be no time to find your rhythm!

—description from the publisher

Scatter Brain is a quick-thinking, match-and-grab counting game. As the dice scatter, quickly snatch up cards that match the numbers on the dice. For bigger number cards, add two or more dice together.

The excitement of a football match in 69 cards!!! You will also have 48 ball tokens to keep the score of the match, your objective will be to score more goals than your opponents.

It is a game for 2 to 6 players, from 6 years old and with a duration of 15 to 20 minutes, you will be able to choose between 2 game modes.

You will start the game with 3 cards and you will have to form your team with forwards, goalkeepers and captains. If you get the chance to take a shot, try to score a goal. You will also be able to put defenders, yellow cards, red cards, dribbles, fouls, sign players, remove yellow cards or disallow goals for offside. Penalties will not be lacking and you can be lucky enough to score a golden goal. Will you win in injury time?

In the game Gramado, players take on the role of directors of chocolate factories and will have to produce the different types of chocolates to meet the city's tourist demand as well as begin distributing its products in Brazil. To do this, players will have to manage their resources by hiring specialists, investing in new technologies, advertising and expanding its stores.
The player with the most money at the end of the game is the big winner!

—description from the designer

About FounderEver had a great product idea and thought, ‘I bet people would buy this’?

Founder is a board game designed to put you in the driver's seat of your very own startup. With Founder, each player is an entrepreneur navigating the challenges of building an e-commerce business from the ground up. Manage your time and money wisely as you invest in producing inventory, generating demand, and shipping orders, striving to outmaneuver your competitors who are competing for the same customers‘ wallets.

Will you make it out of your mom’s basement?


				
				
					How to Play
    Open for business. Start with $100 in your pocket, capacity in your mom’s basement, and 50% profit margins.



    Make investments. Each round, decide who goes first by bidding. Whoever is planning to make the biggest investment, gets first choice on the action board. After all, it takes money, to make money. 



    Grow your business. Start with the fundamentals by producing inventory and finding customers, or take on projects like expanding warehouse capacity and recruiting team members to operate more efficiently. 



    Ship orders and make money. The more you ship, the more you’ll make. But keep an eye out for competition who'll steal your customers away if you keep them waiting too long.

Are there more feathers on a chicken or people who have ever climbed Mount Everest?

In Bang! Out of Order you don’t really need to know the answers…  just decide what's higher or lower and line them up. It’s easy at the start; but as the facts stack up so does the tension!

Teams take turns adding cards to the scale - hopefully in the correct order! As the chain builds, so do the chances of it all being wrong. When you think the order has gone awry, slam your fist down on the table and shout “Bang! Out Of Order”.

If your hunch was right then huzzah! your team gets the points! But if the cards turned out to be lined up perfectly then the other team wins the round. It’s a bit of a gamble.

Bang! Out Of Order is full of fun & fascinating facts that you never knew you needed to know and will test your nerve as you weigh up the weird & the wonderful.

—description from the publisher

The dating-App game where you find your soulmate!
Follow the hashtags given by your best friends and rates the best candidates.

You are comfortably lying on the couch after a long day. You just finished a phone call with your best friend: they have recently got engaged and can’t help talking about how great life as a couple is. You decide it’s time to heal your broken heart and move on after your last relationship. You download It’s a Match, a new dating app, on your phone, upload your profile choosing your best photo, and start scrolling through profiles in search of your soulmate. Are you sure to know what you’re looking for?

In each turn, the active player takes the role of a person looking for their soul mate. 
The players to their right and left take the role of two advisors who guide the active player’s choice, hoping the active player will choose one of their Profile cards.
Each turn is divided in 4 phases:

     Suggestion Phase: The 2 advisors draw 2 hashtag cards and choose 1 to play in front of the active player
     Selection Phase: All the players at the table play a Profile Card face down and the active player shuffles them and then look at them.
