     Choice Phase: The active player chooses 3 Best Profile cards, revealing them from the last to the First one;
     Match Points Phase: Players gain points according to the placement of their cards


After each player has been the active one, the player with the most Match points wins.

—description from the publisher

Sparks is inspired by the engaging, thought- provoking, and sometimes odd conversations that occur underneath a star-filled night sky. We created Sparks to recreate those moments no matter where you are and who you're with.

In the first three rounds, follow the stars by answering questions that explore each other's past, present, and future. Lunar Events add additional twists along the way. In the final round, a constellation is revealed with one last thought-provoking question for you to answer.

How to Play

     Follow the stars and explore each other’s Past, Present, and Future. 
     Lunar Events add additional twists along the way.
     Players win together by collecting all three star cards and revealing a constellation card with one last thought-provoking question to answer. 


—description from the publisher

Sync is inspired by the magical moments of connection when a single word or glance can say it all. We created Sync to recreate that exhilarating feeling.

Get your teammates to guess as many words as possible by using tokens to give clues. What you can say is limited so you need to choose your words carefully and read your teammates’ minds.

—description from the designer
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
The Cat Mitt Game, the hiss-terically fun party game designed for kids, teens, and adults! The game involves players donning over-sized cat mitts to feed their kitty customers.

To play, roll a meow meow (snake eyes), steal the mitts, knock treats out of the treat ball, and feed your kitties. But beware – another player could roll Meow Meow at a whisker’s notice, so you’ll need to be quick.

The Cat Mitt Game is incredibly easy to learn, with short, straightforward instructions that will have you clawing your way to victory in no time.

Suitable for 2-6 players, ages 7 and up, with a gameplay time of 20-35 minutes, it’s the ideal game for both kids and adults.

The game includes the ‘Proficient Puss’ edition for those looking to elevate the excitement. Once you become a more proficient puss, you can add a Catnip and Poison cube to the Treat Ball. When a Catnip cube falls out, the first player to scratch the table and “PURRR” gets to steal a treat from any other player. When the Poison cube falls out, the last player to hold up their hands and “HISS” loses 3 points.

The Cat Mitt Game is also 100% plastic-free and vegan, featuring a large treat ball made from natural rubber, cat mitts made from natural cotton, wooden dice, cardboard kitty cards, and order cards.

Will you feed your tabbies in time?

—description from the publisher

In the world of online dating, every swipe promises you the chance to meet the love of your life. But you have a sneaking suspicion that some of your matches aren’t who they claim to be.  To figure out who is real and who is a Catfish, you need to carefully examine everyone’s dating profile. But if you’re too obvious about “your type,” you give the Catfish an opportunity to fool you!

HOW TO PLAY
Players play as eligible singles with a specific shared trait (i.e. works at Goldman Sachs, refers to pets as children, etc.) However, one of the matches is a catfish and isn't aware of the shared trait.

To figure out who is who, the eligible singles asks dating profile type prompts to each other. The eligible singles win if they can identify the Catfish. The Catfish wins by fooling the singles or by figuring out the trait.

—description from publisher

You just adopted new houseplants. Now you just need to keep them alive! But with a growing plant collection, sprouting your plant babies is far from easy. To succeed, give your houseplants the right nutrients. But give too much and you'll wilt your leafy friends!

Sprout is a cozy, yet thrilling push-your-luck strategy game for 2-5 plant parents. Players take the role of roommates trying to sprout their newly adopted houseplants.

Choose how long to stay in your apartment collecting nutrients including Sunlight, Water, Plant Food, and even Love! The longer you care for your plants, the more nutrients you add into your pot.

Use nutrients to sprout your plant babies by meeting their picky needs. Every plant you sprout not only earns you bragging rights, but they also have unique sprout effects that help you further grow your plant family.

But be careful! If the same nutrient shows up too many times, you overwhelm your plants and wilt them! You’ll then need to give a little Love to bring them back to life.

You can also use Love to adopt new plant babies from the nursery. And with over 100 unique plants to choose from, there are infinite plant collections waiting for you to sprout.

Sometimes you'll lend your roommates a  helping hand by playing Roommate Cards. But other times, you'll have to do what's best for your own plants. Because at the end of the game, only one of you can claim bragging rights to the best plant collection!

2-5 Plant Parents, 45 min, Ages 10+

In the distant, uninhabited meadow, there are adorable gnomes who love to sing. Every day they gather together and sing in the meadows, each tribe competing with the others in singing.

But gnomes are a very particular species. Even when it comes to singing, there are strict rules to follow. There are rules about how to line up and where to stand.

Now, today, the gnomes have gathered once again. Let's sneak a peek at what they're singing.

In this game, each player takes turns placing gnomes on the board according to their size. Gnomes must be arranged vertically and horizontally in order of size: small-medium-large-medium-small. Victory points are earned for each gnome placed.

NAME is a beautifully illustrated matching card game. The deck consists of 44 cards, each depicting a stained glass window of seven panels. Each panel features a name of God used in the Bible, and no two cards are exactly alike. The player's goal is to find a matching set of panels between two cards, or in the rare case that there is no match, be the first to declare the substitute name "Elohim!" The player with the most cards at the end wins!

—description from the publisher

Torry is a sport game, similar to ice hockey. Each player has a team consisting of the goalkeeper and five strikers. The game is played with a die.

Setup: The team is placed in the starting position, the goalkeeper on space 1, the five strikers on spaces 2-6.
The player must now move any striker from his team forward on yellow or blue squares by the number of points he rolled. As often as he lands on a blue square, he remains in the game and can always roll the dice again. If he lands on a yellow square, he must give the dice to his opponent. Strikers can only move forward. Only free squares can be occupied. If he lands on a square occupied by a player from his own team, he can move forward by the same number of points.

A goal is scored by making ending up on one of the two red squares in the opponent's goal with a striker. The first player to score three goals is the winner.

—user summary

A trivia game where you have to answer with a number ranging from 0 to 100. Answers can be in length, width, height, temperature, speed, size, age and given in units like centimeters, years, hours, crowns etc. The game differs from other trivia games which are often limited by given answer options.

The game is played in one round, consisting of seven questions. The player with the lowest score at the end of the game is the winner.

There are a total of 175 unique questions.

A group of animal friends set out on an epic journey to catch rare foods and to combine them to make Legendary Recipes. Create the ultimate Recipe to help them unlock the secrets of the Umamiverse!

Umami Friends invites players to choose a umami chef, each with a distinct type and ability, providing unique advantages in culinary battles. The core of gameplay revolves around collecting ingredients to craft recipes.

These recipes evolve into more potent combinations, culminating in the creation of Legendary Recipes through strategic layering. With numerous character and ingredient combinations to discover, every cook-off is a fresh experience!

The player or team that has earned 30 Umami Points at the end of the game wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Teen Titans
description:
What do you get when a group of young heroes decide to step from under the shadow of their old mentors and band together? The Teen Titans! Led by the original Robin, this unusual team of teenage heroes must nonetheless contend with some of the biggest threats in the universe. Deathstroke, the world’s deadliest mercenary, has declared a vendetta against the Titans. But that’s not even the half of it, as they’re sworn enemies of the gargantuan cosmic demon lord Trigon, who’s not at all happy that his daughter is part of the little team.

Teen Titans is a Crowdfunding Exclusive expansion that brings the beloved team of teenage heroes to DC Super Heroes United. Each of them brings a wholly unique set of powers and abilities to the game, from magic to cosmic power, animal metamorphosis, and just good old training. All of that will be needed to face a couple of widely different villains: Deathstroke with his dangerous assassination techniques and the indestructible demon lord Trigon, who can be added as a Challenge to any game.

—description from the publisher

Metropolis is one of the largest cities in the United States, growing to even greater prominence as the adopted hometown of Superman. As with its favorite son, Metropolis stands as a symbol of hope and the best that humanity can accomplish. And as with Superman, Metropolis’ prominence often places it in the sights of the most dangerous Villains in the universe. Thankfully, its Heroes (whether Kryptonians or humans) are always ready to defend the City of Tomorrow!

This Add-On brings Metropolis fully into the fray. Protected by powerful Kryptonians and assailed by terrifying aliens, the City of Tomorrow will be the battleground for the most epic struggles. Besides the iconic Heroes, Villains, and Locations, this expansion also introduces a new feature to the game in the form of Support characters that help the Heroes in smaller ways, but that may need to be protected. It also brings the Kryptonite Shards Challenge, in case the Kryptonian Heroes start getting too big for their britches.

—description from the publisher

You found a box of old computer games at a yard sale and can’t wait to play them with your friends. To your dismay, modern computers won’t run them. Instead, you each race to build your own vintage computer out of spare parts. Welcome to the Computer Junkyard, the perfect place to find all the components you will need! Scavenge for parts, sabotage your opponents, trade  with your friends, and do it all with the cash in your pockets. Be the first to complete a computer good enough to run your game!

In Computer Junkyard, players assemble hardware tiles to construct a vintage computer. The first player to have a complete, bug-free computer, which meets the specifications necessary to run their unique computer game, is the winner.

The primary mechanic of Computer Junkyard is the physical assembly of hardware tiles. Each player attaches their hardware together like puzzle pieces, connecting them up to a central motherboard tile to fit within a 9x9 chassis grid.

Players collect hardware by purchasing tiles randomly drawn from the central Junkyard, or by freely making trades with other players. Players begin with a limited amount of money, which they must manage as hardware tiles increase in price as their supplies decrease.

During their turn, players may purchase hardware, attempt to steal hardware from their opponents, or sabotage an opponent by attaching a computer bug tile to their computer.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Journey to the Horizon (山河旅人) is an engaging game filled with exploration and discovery! Players travel across China's breathtaking landscapes, explore historical relics, witness massive engineering feats, immerse in bustling cities, savor local delicacies, and marvel at the natural wonders. With every exploration, this vast land reveals its wonders to the players. Open the box and embark on a magical journey across China!

Key features:

Comprehensive geographic knowledge
Immersive visual experience with real photographs and AI-enhanced illustrations
Inherited and advanced gameplay from the "Mountain and River" series with innovative mechanics and deeper strategic planning

Gameplay:

The game is divided into Travel and Rest phases. In the Travel phase, players roll three dice and place them in the High-speed Rail, Hotel, or Scenic Spot areas, executing corresponding effects. In the Rest phase, players move taxis based on dice order in the Hotel area to decide the next round's player order.
Collecting scenic spot cards involves a combination of actions and strategic planning. Collect souvenirs to buy travel services and alter points.

Victory is achieved by being the first to collect the required set of scenic spot cards:

     One set of seven different types of scenic spot cards
     Six cards of any one type
     Three cards of any three different types


Journey to the Horizon is a perfect family game, easy to learn and enjoyable for all ages. Gather your family and enjoy this fun and strategic game together!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Ultima Legends is a fantasy game set in the world of Ultima.  It is part of the Bandai Part Joy series and is numbered 82 in the series.

The game focuses on the characters performing quests all in an effort to defeat Exodus.  If the players have not accomplished their objectives within a timeframe, then the world succumbs to Exodus.
Assassin's Creed meets Magic: The Gathering in this crossover expansion featuring over 100 new cards and reprints based on the popular video game franchise.

In this crossover expansion from the Magic: The Gathering Universes Beyond series, Magic visits the world of Fallout with a set of four commander decks set in the post-apocalyptic wasteland that once was the United States of America.

Ground Combat. Cyberpunk setting. Individual skirmish PvP game for 28-32mm miniatures. Three models per side.

A complete game for two players, designed to simulate short, sharp actions between elite, three-person teams at contested locations over critical objectives.

Mission Critical uses the same terminology, dice mechanics, and basic procedures as the original Exploit Zero Cyberpunk Espionage and Mayhem rule set, but is modified to fit the smaller format, figure count, and scale of combat.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
P108 Fortezza Italiana is a solitaire card wargame depicting in just 36 cards the career of a Regia Italiana pilot aiming to fly the unique Italian quadrimotor airplane!

Choose your Crew among 4 different airmen, choose your objective and fly towards the enemy, by avoiding enemy Bristol Beaufighters and AA, aiming at the port with your bomb load.

Return to your base and try another action against another enemy harbour ... will you manage to hit the foes repairing the recurrent Failures of this airplane?

—description from the publisher

An exciting game of social strategy.
The town market is open for business and several vendors are selling their piggies in blind auctions. But there is a catch: although the highest bidder always wins an auction, they only pay the amount of the second highest bid. This means you will have to bluff and predict your opponents' auctions in order to succeed. The first one to hog four cards from a single vendor wins and goes wee wee wee all the way home.

boardgame: Piracy! Hunters of the Seven Seas
description:
It is a golden age of piracy and you are one of the captains that will set his name in history, or a lowlife no one will ever hear about again. Your goal is to amount 1000 gold in a span of 12 months and retire a wealthy man. It is time to plunder and sink ships, scare the poor merchants and avoid the noose.
Piracy! Hunters of the Seven Seas is a solitary game in one deck. It is encourage to write a diary, a captain’s log, as you play it, so landlubbers envy every minute of your life adventure.

Objective of the Normal game difficulty is to accumulate 1000 Gold in 12 months of time.
If you want to play a Harder game, you may try to accumulate the same amount of gold in less time. True pirate captain should be able to do it in 6-8 months.
If you think that game is too hard, you may increase number of months to 16, or lower the gold demand to 800 Gold.
So, Easy setting would be: 800 Gold in a span of 12 or 16 months.
If you manage to fulfill this demand before the time runs out, you may retire at any moment afterwards.
You may choose not to, as another objective of the game is to accumulate number of points, so you can check how good of a pirate are you. In any case, after reaching your goal of gold, you may retire after you have finished with all ship encounters of your current round.

With a deck of cards also comes a custom sheet which you can print for easier tracking of some game parameters. This is optional as you can do the same thing just by writing it down on any piece of paper.

—description from the designer

After cruising the Caribbean in search of fame and treasure as a privateer in Pirates of Maracaibo a new challenge awaits you with the Commanders expansion!

With your expertise, you now represent a notorious ship commander, whose special abilities bring even more tactical finesse to the game. In addition, you can now equip your ship with cannons to gain access to the fortifications of certain islands - because there are riches to be found too!

New explorer boards, cards, and rum trading take Pirates of Maracaibo to a whole new level. Hoist the anchor, the final voyage begins!

—description from the publisher

Find the lost animal inhabitants of Petopolis and free them from their yolky egg prisons! Race your rivals to have 3 mythical creatures face up in front of you at the same time. To do so, you must build up a pet army to provide action economy, attack rivals, and guard your own pets.

The game features:

     40 completely unique pet cards
     41 quick cards
     25 time stones gems
     a full color rulebook, featuring an intro comic


On your turn, you'll hatch all of your eggs on the table, and then perform a single action, whether it's:

     playing a quick card, using its effect immediately
     laying an egg (which will hatch on your next turn)
     or using the ability of a hatched pet


If you have time stones in hand, you can spend them to perform additional actions on your turn. A great deal of strategy revolves around finding methods (including pets) that will replenish your store of time stones.

Several cards allow you to attack your rivals, including pets and quick cards. For each of these attacks, there exist guard pets that will block or retaliate against these attacks. Since guard pets must be face up on the table to be active, attacking your rivals should be meticulously planned.

Guard pets can react to other guard pets, causing dizzying reaction chains. The simple cards are specially designed to create intricate webs of interactions.

The winner of the game is the first to have 3 mythical creatures face up in front of them at the same time. Don't get caught up in the end goal. It will be impossible without first building a solid collection of other pets.

—description from the publisher

What is the name of a popular dish of bratwurst and mashed potato often served in English pubs? What is the name of the typical bottle shape for Franconian wines? And which alcoholic mixture is the national drink of Madeira? These and other entertaining questions can be found in the Bon appétit culinary quiz.

There are 4 possible answers to choose from, but this does not always bring you closer to the solution. The detailed answer can be found on the reverse. Entertaining kitchen knowledge about legendary dishes and cookbooks, famous restaurants and chefs as well as regional specialities and current food trends.

The aim is to get most of the answers correct and show that you are a real gourmand.

Wie wird ein häufig in englischen Pubs serviertes populäres Gericht aus Bratwurst und Kartoffelbrei genannt? Wie heißt die für Frankenweine typische Flaschenform? Und welche alkoholische Mischung gilt als Nationalgetränk von Madeira? Diese und weitere unterhaltsame Fragen befinden sich in dem kulinarischen Quiz Bon appétit.

4 mögliche Antworten stehen jeweils zur Auswahl, was des Rätsels Lösung aber nicht immer näher bringt. Auf der Rückseite ist dann die ausführliche Antwort zu finden. Unterhaltsames Küchenwissen über legendäre Gerichte und Kochbücher, berühmte Restaurants und Köche sowie regionale Spezialitäten und aktuelle Ernährungstrends.

Ziel ist, die meisten Antworten korrekt zu haben und so zu zeigen, dass man ein richtiger Gourmand ist.

Myramid  is a triangular connection game for two players. In this game, players take turns placing triangular pieces on the triangular tiles of a triangular grid of side length ranging from 7 to 15.

Each turn, the player may either place a stone on an empty tile, or flip a "vulnerable" enemy piece to their own color. A piece is considered to be "vulnerable" if it has no empty adjacencies, and it touches more enemy than friendly pieces.

The goal of the game is to connect all three sides of the board with a connected group of your own pieces. Corners count as being part of both edges.

Myramid is inspired by the game Morphos by Luis Bolaños Mures.

—description from the designer

boardgame: King of Tokyo: Alligators in the Sewers Promo Card
description:
Card cost "3"

KEEP

At the beginning of your turn, each monster (including you) loses 1 heart.
After that, you may discard this card if you wish, and you get 2 Energy.

"Balzakken" is a Dutch word that translated to "ball sacks", i.e. scrotums.

Balzakken is a card game played with cards that have values ranging from -1 to 11. At the start of the game, players are dealt five cards. They then attempt to lower the value of their hand to below 5. Each turn, they can either discard one or more cards with the same value, and then draw one card, or they can exchange one card from their hand with the top card of the discard pile. If a player's hand value is five or less at the start of their turn, they can end the round. At that point, all other players score points equal to the value of their hand, and a new round begins.

In addition to the regular cards, the game features two action cards. A player can use these instead of performing a normal turn. As long as a player holds an action card, they cannot end the round.

The game ends when a player reaches 100 points or more. At that point, the player with the fewest points wins the game.

—user summary

Catch me あほ! Aho! is a 2-6 player game, where you need to think quick an precisely. The Bosozoku and cops drive in opposite directions on a circular, 6 tile street. A pink and a blue die set which route the Bosozoku and the cops take in the first night. After this, the player cards at the city district set the routes. You need to calculate in your mind if, in which night and which district they meet and fight. If you're wrong, you loose your lifes.

Grab a few good friends and it's time to play Sandwich!

Be the First player to collect all the cards.

Consists of 44 food cards (think sandwich ingredients), 3 sandwich burglar cards, and 8 sandwich monster cards.

Game play: Shuffle cards and distribute equally to all players(2-6)place any extra cards in the center.  Player to the left of the dealer flips his top card and places it on top of the pile in the center.  Any player may slap the center pile if there is a : 
Double, two of the same card on top of each other.
Sandwich, card sandwiched be two of the same card.
The player who first slaps the double or sandwich, gets to keep all cards in the pile.
If a Sandwich burglar card is turned up, yell "Sandwich Burglar" and try to be the first to slap the pile.  if they do, they get the pile.  If they didn't yell, they don't get the pile.  Then there is also the sandwich monster card....

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scratch the Deck: Game Changers Edition
description:
Scratch the Deck is a cool card game for people who love cats, the game itself is a fresh new take on the classic 'get rid of all your cards first' game mode, with some original mechanics.

This edition brings a selection of new characters, each with a unique special power, that will completely change the game and offer a huge advantage to the player who draws that card. The new super power cards can either be included one at a time per game or all in the same game for a super charged game play experience.

Each player is dealt 4 cards face up and 9 cards face down. The 4 cards face up are to be kept in hand and the 9 cards face down cannot be seen or used until the end of the game when there is no more deck cards to draw from (9 Lives End Game).

Each turn players must lay a card of equal or higher value than the player before, for example: on top of a 4, you can lay a 4 or higher. If you have multiples of the same number you can lay them at the same time. If you lay 4 or more of the same number, you can Scratch the Deck (discard the pile and lay a new start card). As long as there are cards left in the deck, players must keep a minimum of 4 cards in hand at all times, so, if you lay 2 cards, you pick up 2, if you lay 1 card, you pick up 1.

Every time you place a card of the same value of the player before, for example: you lay a 5 on top of a 5, you pick a card up from the 'Good Cat Bad Cat' deck. Good Cat Cards offer advantages to players that can be helpful to the player (especially during the 9 lives end game) and Bad Cat Cards make the game harder for the player.

Super Power Cat Cards (Can be placed on any card, almost):
1 = Can be laid on any card and is the lowest value card in the game
3 = Can be placed under the pile (Hide Card)
6 = Can be laid on any card, the next player must play a double (2 or more cards of the same value) or Alpha (11 or 12) card next
9 = Can be laid on any card, the next player must lay lower than a 9
10 = Can be laid on any card, the card pile is discarded and a new start card is laid (Scratch the Deck)
? = Wild Cat Card can be any card the player wants it to be (but not one of a kind)

1 in every 4 cards of a set is a 'One of a Kind' card, special cards cannot be played on top of these cards!

Every time you place a card of the same value of the player before,  for  example: you lay a 5 on top of a 5, you pick a card up from the  'Good  Cat Bad Cat' deck. Good Cat Cards offer advantages to players that  can  be helpful to the player (especially during the 9 lives end game)  and  Bad Cat Cards make the game harder for the player.

—description from the designer

With Uncharted Dungeons you can play HeroQuest solo or in full cooperative mode without needing a Game Master or the official app. Each door you open will reveal a randomly generated encounter, and the Monsters will act using an artificial intelligence system. No dungeon will ever be the same. This deck comes with a mini campaign of 6 Quests but you can create your own. This deck is also fully compatible with all "Advanced Game System" decks, which can be seamlessly integrated.

2D6 Dungeon is a classic style overland crawler, print and play, roll and write game designed for solo play. This is the official overland expansion for the 2D6 Dungeon our previous successful campaign from earlier in the year. You explore a randomly generated world, fight monsters, take on quests and collect treasure and resources.

An Expansion

2D6 Realm is the overland expansion for 2D6 Dungeon, the dungeon crawler designed by Toby Lancaster. You will need the original 2D6 Dungeon Core Rules to play this expansion even though there are many unique rules herein. This 2D6 Realm rule book begins with an outline of the setting, followed by the rules and the tables to generate the world within which your adventurer explores and lives. There is also an extensive Lore section that can be used to inject more narrative into your adventure.

This expansion is used alongside 2D6 Dungeon giving you the option to explore overland, with the same character, as well as the dungeon. You can use this expansion at any level of adventurer and there are new stories and creatures to experience in the realm. There is a wealth of new tables and new systems to sink your teeth into as well. Also this expansion can fully intergrates into your current game or a brand new one if you so desire.

—description from the publisher

Agents of the Crown is a bluffing game with a standard poker deck, for exactly 4 players.

Each player takes the role of a royal House, struggling to defend the Kingdom from a variety of threats, while also sabotaging their enemies. But no House stands alone; each player’s score depends on the success of the player to their left!

In brief, each player’s House matches one of the four suits, and your score depends on your own cards, as well as the success of the player to your left. Each round, players play four cards from their hand, trying to save the Kingdom from random threats, or ignoring those threats that only hurt their enemies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Harvest: A Fight City Card Game
description:
The Harvest. A Fight City Card Game is a game of crime and politics in historic Selaria, Mississippi, better known as Fight City. You need a 36-card deck and some chips for keeping score.

Players start with 21 health points, and the goal is to drive your opponent’s health to zero.

Each card has a value between 1 and 6 on the front, and another value on the back. The 36 cards represent all combinations of the numbers 1 through 6, front and back. Each card also has an ability on the front, and a reset cost on the back.

Players start with hands of three, with three more cards in the middle, called The Street. This setup can be random, or it can be chosen in an opening draft.

On each turn, you may either activate a card in your hand or in the Street; attack with a card in your hand; reset a card in your hand; or buy the top card of the deck.

When a player takes damage, such as from an attack, they may prevent some or all of the damage by moving cards from the Street into the attacking player’s hand. But they can only give change up to the value of the attack, with certain restrictions on answering one card with one card. The value of damage prevention depends on what cards are in the Street, and what cards the defender is willing to pass.

—description from the publisher

Fungi Contest is a strategic board game set in the whimsical world of Fungiland. Players become heirs battling for the throne after the king's untimely death. Utilizing personal tablets, dice, and unique character cards, players strategize to gain influence. The game features a battlefield for direct confrontations and sneaky cards for unpredictable twists. Filled with humor and cultural references, Fungi Contest offers a mix of tactical challenges and fun, suitable for both beginners and experienced players.

—description from the publisher

Pirate’s Bluff is a bluffing card game for long sea voyages. It is similar to the traditional game Liar’s Dice, in that players start with a random secret hand, and try to guess through bidding what every other player holds. Bidding happens in real time, with no turns, requiring lightning-fast decisions and nerves of pirate steel.

The game uses a 72-card deck plus coins or poker chips for keeping score. The Pirate’s Bluff deck contains eight copies each of the numbers 1 through 8, plus eight wild cards (rank 0), for a total of 72.

Once the action starts, players can call out a bid at any time, escalating the difficulty until someone calls their bluff. When a bid is called, all players show their hands. If the bidder is right, the bidder wins and the caller loses. If the bidder is wrong, then the caller wins and the bidder loses.

—description from the publisher

Crosswind is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point without forming any of these patterns:


     Naked diagonal: a pair of diagonally adjacent stones of the same color with no other like-colored stone adjacent to both.
     Knight bend: a 2×3 pattern with three empty points, two like-colored stones in opposite corners and a stone of the opposite color in another corner.
     Long switch: a 2×4 pattern with four empty points, two black stones in opposite corners and two white stones in opposite corners.
     Loose crosscut: a 3×3 pattern with five empty points, two black stones in opposite corners and two white stones in opposite corners.


Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally interconnected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					NotesDraws are possible. There are at least two different deadlock patterns.

Aragon 1938 (Aragón ’38) is a two player wargame simulating the Nationalist Spring 1938 offensive in Eastern Aragon, Valencia and Catalonia, from March to May 1938. Historically, the Nationalist armies (reinforced by an Italian motorized corps and German air units) inflicted a major defeat to the Ejército Popular de la República (EPR), reaching the Mediterranean and dividing the territory of the Republic in two halves. It was one of the most successful campaigns of the civil war, both in terms of territory conquered and units destroyed or routed.

Aragón '38 reimplements the Santander '37 game system published by Snafu Design. In this game, the Nationalist player is generally on the offensive, although the Republican Player will receive major reinforcements with which it can launch powerful counteroffensives or consolidate the frontlines, including the International Brigades or the best units of the Republican Army.

Chanoyu is a casual trick-taking and tableau-building game. To win, be the first player to score at least 15 points, but you will lose if no other player has scored at least 8 points. Win cards in tricks to build your tableau to score points.
Face cards cannot take tricks, but when taken, face cards may be added to either end of the trick winner’s tableau as a substitute for any numbered card. Playing a face card to a trick represents opportunities to give face. Tsukumogami (joker) cards also cannot take tricks, but when taken, they may be added anywhere in any player's tableau.

—description from the designer

Battle of the Bulge is a solitaire game that was included in the August 2018 issue of Gakken Historical Images Magazine.  The game was designed by Masahiro Yamazaki.

The Battle of the Bulge is an area game focusing on the German attack on the Allied sector. The player plays the Allies as they attempt to halt the advance. The sequence of play consists of two assault phases followed by a reinforcement phase.

The magazine also included the game Battle for Moscow, and the map for it is on the opposite side of the Bulge.  Counters for both games are on the same sheet, and the rules for each game are found in the same rulebook.

—user summary

Battle for Moscow is a 2-player game that was included in the August 2018 issue of Gakken Historical Images Magazine.  The game was designed by Masahiro Yamazaki.

The game Battle for Moscow is a simple 2-player simulation of Operation Typhoon.  The game is a typical hex-and-counter/CRT type with no step losses. It is suitable for beginners.

The magazine also included the game Battle of the Bugle--a solitaire game, also designed by Yamazaki. The map for it is on the opposite side of the Bulge.  Counters for both games are on the same sheet, and the rules for each game are found in the same rulebook.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Duel Up is a thrilling two-player game that combines strategy, quick thinking, and a bit of luck, all on a vertical tower!

Components:
Lasercut wooden tower with built-in scoring tracks.
40 two-sided wooden tiles, one side has numbers 0-9, the opposite side has letters A-Z (no Q).
One bag to hold tiles.

Two Players, Two Roles that alternate each of at least two rounds.

Objective:
Player 1: Strategically drops numbers (0-5) into the tower to ultimately add up to ten.
Player 2: Strategically drops letters to ultimately form three-letter words.

Setup:
Two players sit at a table facing each other.
Place the tower between two players.
Place the tiles in the bag.
Decide who will start by dropping numbers and who will drop letters in the first round.
Decode upon an even number of rounds to play, eg two, four, six.

Game Play:
Play in turns.
A player grabs a random tile from the bag.
Aligns the tile such that the designated letter or number side faces the player; that side was determined at the start of this round.
Drops their tile into one of the three columns in the tower.

End of the Round:
The round is over when the tower is full.

Scoring:
Each player scores their side, giving their opponent the opportunity to verify the results.
Letter Scoring: Score one point for each word made in any direction. The word must have at least three letters.
Number Scoring: Score one point for each group of at least three adjacent numbers that add up to ten.
Record your words and number of sums on paper.

Words and sums can be found in any of eight directions (forwards, backwards, upwards, downwards, four diagonals).

Allow your opponent to verify the results. If your opponent discovers a missed word or sum, they receive an extra point (for each discovery).

Record your score on the built-in scoring track.

At the end of the round, empty the tiles into the bag.
Players now alternate their targets: words versus sums.

Play at least two rounds to determine the ultimate winner with the highest overall score.

Watch as the numbers and letters stack up, creating a dynamic and ever-changing vertical game board.

Use your wits to outsmart your opponent and achieve your goals!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Sinking of the Prince of Wales
description:
Sinking of the Prince of Wales is a solitaire game that was included in the August 2019 issue of Gakken Historical Images Magazine.

Sinking of the Prince of Wales is a single-player game that recreates the aerial attack by Japanese naval aircraft against the Royal Navy battleship "Prince of Wales" and battlecruiser "Repulse" that took place off the coast of the Malay Peninsula in the South China Sea from December 9th to 10th, 1941. In actual history, the Japanese military achieved the historic feat of destroying both ships, but the player is asked to decide whether they can achieve the same feat.

The magazine also includes the game Carrier War, also designed by Yamazaki. The map for it is on the opposite side of the map.  Counters for both games are on the same sheet, and the rules for each game are found in the same rulebook.

—user summary

Carrier War is a 2-player game that was included in the August 2019 issue of Gakken Historical Images Magazine.

Carrier War is a two-player game set in 1942 in the "Japan-US Aircraft Carrier Battle (Second Phase Operation)." The player takes the role of the Japanese Navy's top brass (the Naval General Staff and the Commander-in-Chief of the Combined Fleet) who, after the "first phase" in the Pacific has been completed, are seeking to establish an even more advantageous position, and the commander of the US Navy's Pacific Fleet, whose top priority is to prevent this, and the player is forced to make "decisions" that will determine the course of the war.

The magazine also includes the game Sinking of the Prince of Wales--a solitaire game, also designed by Yamazaki. The map for it is on the opposite side of the map.  Counters for both games are on the same sheet, and the rules for each game are found in the same rulebook.

—user summary
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Battle for Normandy is a 2-player game that was included in the August 2020 issue of Gakken Historical Images Magazine.

The game Battle for Normandy recreates the famous "Normandy Landings" that began on June 6, 1944, a turning point on the Western Front of World War II, made famous by the movies "Saving Private Ryan" and "The Longest Day."  Players take on 
the role of the Supreme Commander of the Allied Forces, which oversees the US and Commonwealth forces, and the commander of the 7th Army, which is responsible for the German defense of Normandy, and compete to determine the outcome of the decisive battle.

The game map depicts the Normandy coast and surrounding areas in northern France, and the first week there is recreated over a total of seven turns.  The game is a typical hex-and-counter/CRT type with no step losses. It is suitable for beginners.

The magazine also includes the game Ste. Mere-Eglise--a solitaire game, also designed by Yamazaki. The map for it is on the opposite side of the Normandy map.  Counters for both games are on the same sheet, and the rules for each game are found in the same rulebook.

—user summary
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Ste. Mere-Eglise is a solitaire game that was included in the August 2020 issue of Gakken Historical Images Magazine.

Ste. Mere-Eglise is a single-player game that focuses on the "Battle of the US Airborne Forces" as part of the Normandy Landings. It recreates the parachute/glider landings of the US 82nd and 101st Airborne Divisions, and the advance and establishment of a beachhead by the US 4th Infantry Division, which landed at Utah Beach, during the Normandy Landings from June 6th to 7th, 1944.  This successful operation is recreated over a total of eight turns using a simple area system.

The magazine also includes the game Battle for Normandy, also designed by Yamazaki. The map for it is on the opposite side of the Ste. Mere-Eglise map.  Counters for both games are on the same sheet, and the rules for each game are found in the same rulebook.

—user summary

Hide bad cards under the carpet while trying to make matches of friendly cards.  Watch out for popcorn explosion and vacuum cleaner cards.

Players have 5 cards in their hands, on their turn they can either put any number of cards from their hands under the carpet to hide them, or can play a card to try to get a set/match.  However when the "What's under the Carpet?" card is drawn, the carpet is pulled up and players in turn order flip a card and it either takes away one of their cards, or if it's popcorn the player can use it to attack another players card.  However the vacuum cleaner can suck up the popcorn and protect the card.

Game ends immediatly when the draw stack is depleted.  Player with the most matches wins.

Example Matches:
Peanuts/Elephants
Teeth/Bubble Gum

Example Bad Cards:
Elephant/Mouse (The mouse scares the elephant)
Teeth/Tooth Fairy (the Tooth Fairy takes the tooth)

Each card has a "good" card and a "bad" card that go with it.

A tricky little build-your-own rollercoaster card game where all of the passengers are very fussy robots. Some like going up, some like going down....some like it easy...some like it wild! Try to satisfy the mechanical thrill-seekers by optimizing your ride, then see if you reached your desired F.E.Q. (Fun Efficiency Quotient)
Played in solo mode or with opponents, the game involves some basic math with a whimsical and creative attitude

—description from the designer

Expand your Megaquest experience with the ultimate Monster Deck!

Dive into epic adventures with this comprehensive set of 74 cards to enhance your dungeon-crawling quests. Whether battling fierce Orcs or facing the terrifying Zargon, this deck is a must-have for any Megaquest player.

What's Included:

68 Monster Cards:

16 Orcs: Unleash the fury of these brutal warriors.
6 Abominations: Face the twisted horrors of the dungeon.
12 Goblins: Swarm your heroes with these cunning creatures.
4 Dread Warriors: Confront the elite guards of the underworld.
8 Mummies: Beware the ancient undead guardians.
12 Skeletons: Encounter the relentless skeletal soldiers.
8 Zombies: Survive the onslaught of these undead fiends.
1 Gargoyle: Battle the fearsome stone sentinel.
1 Zargon (Dark Wizard): Challenge the ultimate dark sorcerer.
5 Reference Cards: Quick access to essential game information.

1 Link Card: Join the community on Discord and connect with fellow adventurers.

Card Features:

Stats: Vital statistics for each monster, providing detailed combat information.
Abilities: Unique abilities that add depth and challenge to your encounters.
Disciplines: Specialized skills and attributes that enhance gameplay.
AI Table: Each card includes an AI table on the back, perfect for solo play.

An 18xx game with a political campaign theme. Follows the 1830/full-cap branch of the genre.

Features include:

     Unique "exploratory committee" capitalization process.
     Tile manifest that is highly restrictive in type but permissive in amount.

Mysteriespelet is divided into two parts. In the first part you compete to be the first to match pictures. One player flips up a playing card showing four different pictures. He then rolls the die to determine what picture to find. Then all players tries to be the first one to find the picture on a grid. If you succeed you win the card.

In the second part you match tiles depicting different LasseMajas novels with the images found on the playing cards won in the first part by flipping up tokens and trying to match them with the cards.

The player who first gets rid of his playing cards ends the game. The winner is the one who won the most cards.

Galebari is a Croatian term for men who pursue short-term romantic encounters with female tourists, typically foreigners. These individuals are often characterized by their physical attractiveness, confidence, and sociability, renowned for their charm and ability to impress foreign women. While some view their activities as ethically questionable, Galebari remain an integral part of the local cultural landscape.

This cultural phenomenon inspired a card game of the same name, originally released in 2006 and set in the 1980s and 1990s. The game's 18th anniversary edition retains the original setting and artwork while introducing enhanced gameplay mechanics and adding a sixth playable character.

In this game, players take on the role of a "Galebar," competing to attract the highest number of female tourists.

At its core, it's a trick-taking card game with some role-playing elements. During gameplay, players use their unique set of cards representing pick-up lines to be the first to claim five girl cards. They can also upgrade their hand with additional pick-up line cards, introducing effects that increase their chances of winning in various ways.

Massacards is a hidden role social deduction game all about catching a Killer!

Timeline:
September 22nd: You are invited to a fancy dinner party by Master Massacre at their infamous Massacre Mansion.
September 24th 7:00pm: You attend the party.
September 24th 8:00pm: Master Massacre is found dead!

It's up to the partygoers to solve the crime and catch the Killer before they all find themselves dead! Luckily for them not only was there a Witness to the murder, but also a local Private Eye who snuck their way into the mansion!

The night begins with the Witness secretly gifting the partygoers three vague words that are meant to describe evidence surrounding the real Killer. From there it's up to everyone to uncover clues and question everyone knowing one of them is actually the Killer! If the Killer is found, it's up to the Private Eye to take the shot and kill the Killer once and for all. The party continues until the Killer is dead, or Massacre Mansion truly lives up to its name!
Explore Rooms to find more evidence and clues pointing to the true identity of the Killer and be on the lookout for any other suspicious characters!
With dozens unique playable characters, 7 playable teams, and possibly many many more on the way, step into the crazy world of Massacards and brace yourself for an amazing game night!

—description from the publisher

Massacards is a thrilling social deduction experience which innovates on many of the design ideas which came before it. Players will select one of many scenarios to determine which roles are in play, then hand each player a role and clues which could implicate them of the murder. What transpires is a race for the investigators to solve the murder before the murderer is able to eliminate all of them. The game plays out in rounds. In each round information is shared before roles get to use special abilities to change the course of the night.

—user summary

boardgame: LOL: Die ultimative NICHT LACHEN Challenge – Das Kartenspiel
description:
Whoever laughs loses! And that brings us to the basic rules of this game: You sit opposite each other and try not to laugh at all. Unfortunately, the jokes in this deck of cards are so weird that it's not that easy. So in addition to concentration and timing, you need a lot of self-control. Especially when new rules, props or the dreaded group mode are added. Trying not to have fun has never been so much fun!

Wer lacht, verliert!
Und damit sind wir auch schon bei den Grundregeln dieses Spiels: Ihr sitzt euch gegenüber und versucht, auf keinen Fall zu lachen. Dummerweise sind die Witze in diesem Kartendeck so schräg, dass das gar nicht so einfach ist. Neben Konzentration und Timing braucht ihr also jede Menge Selbstbeherrschung. Besonders wenn auch noch neue Regeln, Requisiten oder der gefürchtete Gruppenmodus hinzukommen. Der Versuch, keinen Spaß zu haben, hat noch nie so viel Spaß gemacht!

Have you ever wondered just how far you can push a game before it cracks? Have you ever wondered just how much milk a corporation can milk from its cash cow? Ever asked yourself how many mechanics you can add to a game before it is not itself anymore? This game is a research project into just that.
  In 1971, a card game was released which firmly solidified itself as the quintessential card-shedding game of its generation. This game was Uno, and its monumental success as a game has seen it reprinted and remolded in innumerable multitudes. Nowadays you can find Uno fit to whatever shell your heart desires: Uno with some extra cards; Uno video games; Uno with some extra fundamental mechanic which makes the gameplay completely different; Uno with necessary machines that supplement gameplay; even Uno with unique roles and collectible card packs are finding their way to stores.
  In 2024, a humble game designer laughed to himself after thinking about this and jokingly asked, "So, how would Uno play if you put every single special card and rule in one version?"
  ...And so it was, after months of discovering and cataloguing over 800 versions of the classic card-shedder, Chemicards was born. First person to shed their whole hand of cards wins. The game is simple- or one would think. As it turns out, things get a little more complicated with well over one thousand playable cards to shed.

Chemicards takes the classic card-shedding gameplay loop you are familiar with and dilutes it to a horrific sludge with dozens of new mechanics, hundreds of new specific rules and thousands of new cards. The entire game is being developed with as little physical bloat as possible, transforming esoteric and weird mechanics into new cards or dice rolls for as succinct a gameplay loop as possible.

—description from the designer

Navigate your crew through an ever changing archipelago in Isles of Odd. Your goal is to collect 7 gold and deliver it to the port before the other players. Maximize your dice rolls in clever ways to move your ships around and place new tiles to build out the map. Take careful note of your positioning however, because if enemy players can find an opportunity to capture your ship and steal its precious loot, they will. Never forget to recruit new crewmates either, as the unique abilities of these oddities can give you the engine you need to take your victory.

Everest Conquest is an exciting board game that simulates the legendary climb of Mount Everest. Players embark on a thrilling journey starting from Kathmandu, making their way through Lukla to Everest Base Camp, the adventure intensifies as players navigate the treacherous Khumbu Icefall before reaching Camp 1. From Camp 2 onwards, the game introduces the challenge of oxygen dependency, requiring players to acquire an O2 token to proceed. As they advance to Camp 3, players must skillfully navigate through avalanche-prone areas. Camp 4 presents its own set of challenges, including the threat of frostbite. The ultimate goal is to reach the summit of Mount Everest, overcoming each perilous stage of the climb.

The route taken in Everest Conquest follows in the historic footsteps of the legendary Tenzing Norgay and Edmund Hillary, the first climbers to conquer Mount Everest. Players get to experience the iconic path, reliving the remarkable journey of these pioneering mountaineers. The game’s route includes key landmarks and stages that mirror the actual climb, providing an authentic and immersive experience. As players progress, they encounter various challenges and obstacles, each requiring strategic decisions and resource management, echoing the real-life difficulties faced by climbers on their way to the top.

Designed by an experienced mountaineer with over a decade of thrilling adventures, including summiting the highest peaks on four continents, notably Mount Everest, Everest Conquest is a testament to the creator's passion for mountaineering. Drawing from this rich experience, the designer has crafted a captivating board game that offers players a safe yet immersive journey. Everest Conquest allows players to experience the exhilaration of climbing the world’s highest peak without the risks of a real expedition, making it an engaging and educational experience for adventure enthusiasts of all ages.

—description from the designer

In the spook-tacular talent show, “The Next MonStar”, the hair raising stars of tomorrow show off their fearsome talents. As soon as the monstrous soundtrack starts, you must choose quickly and collect the right tiles. You only score valuable popularity points if your MonStars’ talents match the jury’s tastes. The player with the most popularity points on the applause-o-meter after three rounds wins the game.

A rhythmic and exciting tile placement game.

     With monstrous soundtrack to set the pace
     Exciting rounds with no waiting
     With a cool power duo variation for more interaction


—description from the publisher

Clash of the Cryptids is a 2 or 4 player card game that holds a collection of 40 cards printed on premium cardstock in metallic ink.

Each card displays unique Cryptid art alongside a creature biography and five measured statistics. From Fear factor, Strength, Stealth, Mythos, and deadliness.

How to play

To start the game, shuffle and deal all the cards face down. Each player holds their cards so that they can see the top card only. The player to the dealer’s left begins by reading out one of the categories from the top card (e.g. Fear Factor 60). The other players then read out the same category from their cards.

The one with the best or highest value wins, and that player collects all the top cards, including their own, and moves them to the bottom of their pile. It is then their turn again to choose a category from the next card. If two or more cards share the top value or data is not available for that particular category then all the cards are placed in the middle and the same player chooses again from the next card. The winner of the hand takes the cards in the middle as well. The person with all the cards at the end is the Winner!

The 40 card deck features Cryptids from all around the darkest corners of the earth, from some you might have heard of to ones you are yet to discover...

To play That Escalated Quickly, read out a simple question (ex. I’m locked in a tiny room. What else is locked in here with me from cutest to scariest). Then everyone is assigned a secret number from one to ten. Next, each player answers the question, and the intensity of that answer is based on their secret number (ex. 1= a bunny, 10= a grizzly bear with chainsaw arms.) Finally, the person who read the question has to try and sort those answers into the correct order. If they get it right, everyone wins! This game is hilarious, energetic, and a great way to get to know your friends and family a little bit better.

—description from the publisher

This is a mini demo, including cards to play with 3-5 and 3 questions cards.

Shat on a Cat is the game of flinging shats and stealing cats. Use your wits to protect your cats, whilst dodging dogs and the thieving rats. Consult the lizard wizard to change the future whilst holding on tight to your defensive shat-zooka.

In this game each player must protect their cats while making other players lose theirs.

There are three types of cards. Action cards that can be used to shat on, steal or chase away other players' cats. Free cards that help you gain vital resources from the discard pile, and Special cards that can protect, clean or remove your cats or even fling a shat back.

On your turn you can play up to two action cards and as many free cards as you like. Then,you end your turn by picking up two cards, one at a time, from the deck. Watch out for those special cards, you may lose a cat!

This game has a large focus on responding to cards with other cards, and managing your hand to cause maximum damage to others. It is fast paced, quick to learn and very funny.

Games last between 10 and 20 minutes.

The game ends when only one player has a cat or cats remaining. That player is the winner.

Theft at the award show! Marie's father was supposed to receive an award for his film role, but the statue has disappeared. Now, the three !!! need to solve a case for themselves.

In Die drei !!! Rätselparty, the game leader distributes clues both for inside the apartment and out. Then the puzzle fun begins: In part one, the objects that will unmask the perpetrator are collected. In part two, the focus is on finding the statue — also in the form of a scavenger hunt.

Caveman Through Time is the co-op expansion Caveman Rumble!

Now take the role of your favorite caveman and travel through time to be able to stablelize the timeline and defeat the boss ruining it. Match colors, strategize, attack the boss through out different locations in time and win as a team for the whole cavemanverse!

Bitter Rivals is a build-em style party game where the first player to build their fruit controlled mecha suit wins!

There are two decks, the lemon deck and the lime deck. players use Sour Street cards to pick up from the lime deck and build their mecha suits, they end their turn by picking up from the lemon deck.

You can use action cards to slow other players down, like flipping their armour cards over, slicing an armour piece back into the lime deck, swapping a piece of yours with a piece of theirs or by playing battle cards to directly steal from another player.

Slice, Yoink, Flip and Battle to be the Zest player around.

—description from the designer

In Dragon's Domain, players embark on an epic adventure into the depths of a treacherous dungeon to confront the fearsome dragon, Arathor. Each game session features 4 unique heroes, each equipped with customizable skills from two categories, allowing for strategic play and character development. Additionally, each hero has a unique, powerful skill that can be used once per game, adding a layer of tactical depth and excitement.

The dungeon is constructed from double-sided tiles, revealing new threats and puzzles as the heroes advance. Treasure cards add another layer of strategy and excitement, providing temporary bonuses such as healing potions, strength potions, and other magical items. Beware of trap cards that can hinder your progress!

With varying dungeon effects and monster behaviors, every game offers a fresh and challenging experience. The ultimate goal is to navigate the dungeon, overcome its many hazards, and defeat the dragon to retrieve the legendary Crown of Eldoria, bringing unity and prosperity to the land of Eldoria. The game combines strategy, teamwork, and narrative elements, making it an engaging experience for both new and seasoned players.

—description from the designer

With the Enforcer Expansion, one player will be the Enforcer and face off against the Timelancers to save the historical timeline. The expansion increases the player count to 5 by adding a 1 vs many variant to the game.

The expansion can be played with 2-5 players and takes about 90 minutes.

—description from the publisher

SKIDZ - grab a pen and let your creativity run wild! Unleash your imagination when you combine words and put your ideas on paper in a flash.
Ideal for kids who want to learn through play and strengthen their teamwork and communication skills. Draw, guess and laugh - with SKIDZ every line becomes an adventure!

It is an interactive and integrative, educational and geragogical game that can be played in early childhood education, work with migrants, kindergarten, school, support facility, senior citizens' center or family, for example.

SKIDZ is more than just a game. It is an educational experience designed to promote and improve various skills. With its creative and entertaining game dynamics, it is perfect for children, adolescents, families or in the context of senior citizen work. The aim of the game is to combine two everyday terms and draw the result in order to then guess it together as a team. "SKIDZ also offers much more than just entertainment. By combining and drawing terms, the players' creativity, motor skills, responsiveness and concentration are strengthened and they learn to think and act under time pressure. It is also a valuable tool for improving reading skills and word knowledge. In addition, SKIDZ promotes teamwork, communication and sociability.
The lovingly designed graphics round off the valuable project.

—description from the publisher

The dice are calling all aspiring armchair generals! Your time has come to finally take control of a realistic battlefield board game.

This war gaming concept combines luck and fortune with combat, manoeuvres and oddles of loot into one simple and fast pace experience.

Go to war, or make strengthen yourself for a strong defence. Gain territory, ferry loot back to HQ - use your brain, not just two dice.

—description from the designer
family: Game: Battleground Historical Warfare (Your Move Games)

description:
Inspired by the legendary units of Romans, Roman Auxiliaries, Mercenaries, and Enemies of Rome, the strategy card game Tacticus Romanus was born. As the Roman Empire extends its dominion across the known world, you step into the role of a general competing for influence over this vast realm. Your success depends on careful troop management, tactics, and wise decision-making when choosing which fights to pick. And yes, a dash of luck can tip the scales.
The game consists of Territory Cards you are competing for with other players and Unit Cards which defines your military power. Additionally, the Dice is used to resolve the battles.
In a single round, players can deploy up to three cards (deployed sequentially, one at a time, in clockwise order). These cards represent Infantry, Cavalry, and Range units, which are used to engage opponents in combat. Special cards can strengthen your army too. When three different unit types are combined, an additional bonus is unlocked, granting extra dice for that round. 
The game ends when all territory cards are claimed, which makes 16 rounds in total.

—description from the designer

The "Star Wars: Spyfall" board game is an offshoot of the Spyfall series of games, which is a conversational parlor game. Basically, the changes affected only the setting, which turns this game into a real gift for both fans of previous games and Star Wars fans.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Настольная игра "Звёздные войны: Spyfall" – это ответвление в серии игр "Находка для Шпиона", которая представляет собой разговорную игру салонного типа. В основном изменения коснулись лишь сеттинга, что превращает эту игру в настоящий подарок как для поклонников предыдущих игр, так и для фанатов "Звёздных Войн".

—description from the publisher

Zonewreck is a a combinatorial abstract strategy war game for two players, played on a chessboard and special pieces. The goal of the game is to wreck the enemy zone. Players begin with 140 points and all pieces in reserve. Players take turns, starting with White.

Each turn, players optionally perform these two actions:


     You may add the piece with the smallest P from reserve to an empty position in your zone, if possible. Pieces start off-board with a power level (P). When placed in the zone, the player gives the piece at most 4*P stat points, distributed among these categories: Attack (A), Health (H), Speed (S), Distance (D). Subtract these points from reserve.



     Then you may move a piece in a straight line, up to D empty spaces. Movement must go towards your opponent, for non-attacking moves. If the destination is an empty space outside the enemy zone, the turn ends. Otherwise, if the destination is an empty space in the enemy zone, the piece attacks the enemy player (subtract A points from enemy reserve), and the turn ends. A piece already in the enemy zone may attack without moving. Finally, if, instead the destination is attacking an enemy piece, the two pieces begin mortal combat, and the turn ends once a piece dies. 


During mortal combat, the pieces alternate attacking each other. Highest S starts, active player starts if tied. Each attack deals damage equal to A and is subtracted from the H stat permanently. If H reaches 0, the piece dies. H is never regained, even after battle.

If a player has 0 left in reserve, they lose the game. There are no draws, ties, luck, randomness, or hidden information in Zone wreck.

—description from the designer

Florentynka Muu grew up in a small village in Polesie, among beautiful forests by a river. However, she always dreamed of traveling. So she packed her suitcase and set off on a great adventure together with her favorite toy, Pig. Who did the Florentynka meet and what did she find during the trip?

Florentynka Muu w podróży ("Florentynka Moo on the go" is a good fun and learning how to collect sets. In addition

     the game exercises perceptiveness
     supports the child's comprehensive development
     develops imagination and vocabulary
     builds social bonds


—description from the publisher (translated)

The game features two variants. In the first mode players display 20 cards on the table in a grid 4x5. On their turn a player has to find a category or a color of the card's boarder that 2 or 3 of the displayed cards share. They announce the category or the color and takes 1-2 (if announced the color) or 1-3 (if announced the category) cards from the display to her personal pile. When there is not enough cards to fill the grid, the player with the most cards is the winner.

In the second variant the players are dealt 6 cards each. On their turn the player discards 1-3 cards from their hand that match a category (1-3 cards), first letter (1-3 cards) or a color of the boarder (1-2 cards). If a player discards 3 cards, a chosen opponent has to draw 1 card. When a player discards the last card from their hand they score 1 point. The first player to score 2 points is the winner.

—user summary

Spy Guy Junior Zwierzaki ("Spy Guy Junior Animals") is a cooperative game in which you play the role of a young detective and try to catch the villain before he catches all the animals in the world and locks them in his private zoo. Practice your perceptiveness, ability to focus and cooperate by looking for clues on a colorful, cartoon, over a meter long board.

The Junior version is dedicated to younger players, so to make searching easier, the illustrations are larger and the board is less detailed.

Additionally, the package includes an atlas with all the animals on the board. Try to find them all and learn interesting facts about them.

—description from the publisher (translated)

With our own House brand and further specialization, we are trying to differentiate ourselves from the competitors. Emerging by-products such as beer waste need to be managed, and we are tapping into new markets via the railroad. Some small changes can offer some new decision-making opportunities.
This set contains 5 expansion modules that can be played individually or in any combination with the base game – however, we recommend not using more than 2 modules at the same time.
Additionally, now you can enjoy Beer Pioneers as a new challenge in solo play. The rules and the necessary components for solo play are also included.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to the ruthless world of fashion competitions, where anything goes.
Put yourself in the jury's pocket with your most convincing “arguments”! And watch out for your competitors, who will stop at nothing to get in your way. So be smarter and answer them. And if necessary, don't hesitate to bribe a member of the jury to get your way. Victory is yours.

—description from the designer

Bienvenue dans le monde impitoyable des concours de mode là ou tous les coup son permis pour arriver à ses fins. Mettez-vous les membres du jury dans la poche avec vos “arguments” les plus convainquant !

Et méfiez-vous de vos concurrentes qui seront prêts à toutes les bassesses pour vous mettre des bâtons dans les roues. Alors soyez plus malin et rendait leur l'appareil et si besoin n'hésitait pas à soudoyer un membre du jury pour arriver à vos fins car la victoire vous appartient.

—description from the designer (French)

boardgame: Glifos: More Symbols – Scarab and Cat
description:
The More Symbols expansions are two additional cards for Glifos, which add 2 new hieroglyphs to the game: the Cat and the Scarab, designed to make your games even more intriguing, since you will have new symbols to memorize!

The two cards are dealt separately. Collect several and add them all to your game!

RULES:

Mix the card with the others, shuffle and play normally, according to the original rules.

When the promotional card 'Scarab' or 'Cat' is among the 6 open cards on the table, the special rule comes into effect.

In this case, you can answer only 'Scarab' or 'Cat' for that card, regardless of what appears on the open card of the deck (color or symbol).

For example, if the 'Scarab' card is in the indicated position and the question is about the color, you can answer only 'Scarab' to get it right.

Prepare to become a legendary Slayer in this expansion to Dracula's Curse. This procedurally generated dungeon crawler plunges you and your fellow Slayers into a nightmarish realm, where no two playthroughs are ever the same. Choose from one of 7 Slayers each with their own unique abilities, backgrounds, and subclasses or embrace the darkness and play as Dracula himself, the ultimate lord of the night.

One among you take up the role of Cthulhu, wielding the power to summon eldritch minions, corrupt minds, create chaos, and manipulate reality itself. As Slayers, you must strategically work together to stop Cthulhu before he can be released. Be wary — alliances can shift as fallen Slayers have the chance to join Cthulhu's cult, working against their former comrades.

With stunning, handcrafted (AI-free) artwork from talented artists around the world and gameplay that balances strategy, deception, and teamwork, Cthulhu's Curse promises not just a game night but an unforgettable journey into the eldritch unknown.

—description from the publisher

Berserkers: Chaos Extension brings 5 new modules to further make the game more strategic. For you to integrate and combine as you wish.


     new Villager rewards to aid your quest
     new King's Requests for more adventures
     Sacrifice of the King: come back one more time to complete your mission
     the Market: spend gold for allies and fortune tellers
     Berserker Project: made by the community this module will add events to your games to make them unpredictable. These events will bring some narrative, but mostly beneficial and/or harmful effects to your games.

Deceiver After Dark is a party game of observation, deception and laughter. Get ready to deceive your family and friends and challenge your ability to blend in with the crowd and test your knowledge of your closest family and friends.

One player is dealt the deceiver card while everyone else is dealt a question card with 6 questions. By raising or not raising their hand to answer each question, players must figure out who the deceiver is to win.

Points are awarded for guessing the deceiver or folling everyone.  The player with the most points wins.

Paragon: Monsters & Mayhem is a fast paced fantasy TCG in which you command monsters and magic to dominate a gridded battlefield and eliminate your opponent's essence to be victorious! With an expansive array of creature cards, spell cards, ritual cards and item cards at your disposal, you'll strategize, adapt, and conquer in this thrilling game of tactics and glory!

In Paragon: Monsters & Mayhem you play as the Paragon of your choice. Players will take turns commanding cards into the different zones of the battlefield in order to reach victory. Winning the game consists of removing your opponents essence. Each time you strike a Player directly with one of your uncontested creatures or a direct damage spell they reveal one essence card and gain power from it. When a player with no essence cards is striked they are knocked out of the game!

Players construct 3 decks each with their own card types. One deck is your main deck which consists of creatures, spells, rituals and items. Your essence deck which consists of essence cards that you choose the order of before play to give you a tactical advantage against your opponent. And your mana deck of 10 to 20 mana cards that you will gain over the course of the match in order to pay for and command cards. The play area consists of the Combat Zone, a 4x4 grid where you command your creatures into in order to attack your opponents creatures or strike directly at your opponent, damaging their essence. The Support Zone, for playing your items and rituals which have longer lasting effects and can change the tide of battle. The Paragon Zone, for your Paragon to start in and use its abilities before advancing into the Combat Zone to join the battle.

—description from the publisher

Bottle Shock: The Wine Game is a hidden identity game based on the movie Bottle Shock. Gather around for grand wine tasting... and play as either an honest judge, deciding which wine is the finest, or as a rigged judge, guiding your bottle to victory through persuasion, tricking the honest judges, and manipulating votes to remove judges.

Each player will present one of the four wine bottles, attempting to subtly skew the other judges' perception depending on whether they wish the bottle to win the competition, or to advantage a different bottle. Players are also able to influence the winning bottle through responses to prompt cards, revealing other judges secrets, and by catching dishonest judges using code phrases to communicate.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Mighty Thor, Lady Sif, Thor, Hero of Midgard & Loki, Prince of Lies
description:
Jane Foster arrives as "The Mighty Thor" in the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Mighty Thor, Lady Sif, Thor, Hero of Midgard & Loki, Prince of Lies character pack, empowered by the magical hammer, Mjolnir. She is joined by master swordswoman Lady Sif, Thor, Hero of Midgard, and Loki, Prince of Lies. All four miniatures bring new opportunities for hobbyists and players to engage with new sculpts and new characters with new rules to include in their games.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
When bringing the heroes of Asgard to the table, why not bring the might and beauty of Asgard itself with the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Asgardian Shrine terrain pack.

The Asgardian shrine is a beautiful piece of terrain featuring crumbling ruins atop a raised dais before a statue honoring a valkyrie — one of the legendary warriors of Asgard who keeps watch over those who shelter in her abode. This set also includes optional rules for players to use with the terrain to keep things interesting.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Battle for Asgard
description: Bring the might and beauty of Asgard to your games with the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Battle for Asgard terrain pack, which is full of themed scatter terrain. These terrain elements feature ancient crumbling stone statues and ruins, flaming braziers, and more. This set also includes optional rules for players to use with the terrain to keep things interesting.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The Expedition - Wild Space is a small box game for 1 to 3 players. Combine it with The Expedition to expand the experience up to 5 players.

Discover wild new locations in this fast paced area control game.

Earn points by marking locations and  completing your Expeditions all while avoiding hazards and your rival Explorers.

Each turn players will move orthogonally around a randomized 3x3 grid of cards placed in the same orientation.  After moving, they will place a marker on the location they left signifying that they have explored that location.  Beware though, as there are some locations that might help you and some locations that might not!  Each location in Wild Space has it's own effect that alters your strategy!

The first player to either achieve 2 of their hidden Expedition goals or place all 6 of their markers on the location grid at the same time will trigger the end of the game.  All the other players get one more turn, then the game is scored!
