—description from the publisher

In Fliptown: The Lone Gun, you’ll embark on a solo journey that takes you through 12 chapters, each with its own “law of the land” (a unique rule just for that chapter) and “aim of the game” (a unique win condition just for that chapter).

With every new chapter, you must choose between one of two special items, which you’ll keep in your inventory from game to game. Thus, as the campaign advances, your character will evolve and get more powerful.

Featuring a simple “save” system to allow you to save your progress between games and a campaign point system to track your success over the entirety of the campaign, The Lone Gun offers a whole new way to experience Fliptown.

—description from the designer

description:
Wishland, The card game  is a compact version of Wishland.  It is played over a series of turns.

There are 5 different types of cards and each one of theme scores differently at the end of the game.

    Rides: Provide victory points depending on the total number of rides played in all parks
    Actors: Provide victory points depending on the total number of actors played on their park.
    Restaurants: Provide victory points depending on the total number of cards played on their park. Short parks gives more points.
    Hotels: Provide victory points depending on the total number of cards played on their park. Large parks gives more points.
    Mascots: Provide victory points depending on the number range where the mascot card is played.


Every turn, each player will lay down a card from their hand to build their park. The game ends when all players have laid down 10 cards (10 turns) or a player can not legally lay down a card on his turn.

Each player score the cards they have played. The winner is the player with the most victory points.

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description:
The continent Alula always has more surprises in store!

Two ancient people come to meet you: the Guides and the Three-Eyed Ones. They both bring new ways of acquiring fame...

By adding this expansion to the base game, you may even play Faraway with 7 players.

—description from the publisher

This expansion features 5 Region cards with a new gray Biome, Mystical Havens (with a band of icons across the center), which were already present on 25 Sanctuary cards in the base game (and 4 Sanctuary cards in this expansion). One new Region card scores 4 VP per Mystical Haven card (and requires 2x of each resource icon as a prerequisite - 6 total).

Discover and fall in love with Mecklenburg-West Pomerania

Get to know the beautiful state of Mecklenburg-West Pomerania in a playful way: from its regional capital of Schwerin across the whole state to the Baltic coast, through West Pomerania, along the Mecklenburg lake plateau, to the river Elbe and back to Schwerin. With small children, the game can be played like the popular game of 'ludo'. The specially marked spaces are then lucky spaces, which allow you to move three spaces forwards.

The winner is the one who gets to the regional capital first.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Lernen Sie das schöne Mecklenburg-Vorpommern spielend kennen.
Der Weg führt von der Landeshauptstadt Schwerin durch das ganze Land - an die Ostseeküste, durch Vorpommern, entlang der Mecklenburgischen Seenplatte, an die Elbe und zurück nach Schwerin. Dabei sind Fragen zu Natur, Geschichte und Kultur zu beantworten. Die insgesamt 466 Fragen mit über 1.300 Antworten und Glückskarten machen das Spiel abwechslungsreich und spannend, nicht nur für Touristen, sondern auch für Einwohner des Landes. Testen Sie Ihr Wissen!

—description from the publisher

description:
5 Second Rule just got even more spontaneous and unpredictable with the all-new Relay baton! It should be easy to name pizza toppings—but can you do it under the pressure of 5 seconds counting down, after another player puts you on the spot? List one thing in your category, then hand off the electronic baton to any other player—you decide who has to answer next! Time’s not on your side, so just say what comes to mind and risk ridiculous answers slipping out! For 2 or more players, ages 8 and up. Comes with 700 Questions on 350 Cards, 12 Life Cards, 12 Pass Cards, Relay Baton, and Rules.

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You are imprisoned on a doomed ship. Helpless in a cell, you hear the ship’s security forces being overwhelmed around you. As a last ditch effort, the ship’s captain has released you. Despite knowing how dangerous you are, he also knows your full capabilities. Fight the invading force and rescue as many innocent souls on the ship before time runs out. Perhaps, what you do here can atone for your past. Or maybe this is just a good chance for you to disappear into the galaxy… and keep running.

Extraction is a one to four player cooperative game that includes an intense core game, scenario-based challenges, and multiple single player campaigns with unique “level up” systems. Extraction has fluid and easy-to-understand game mechanics, but is not for the causal game group. The base game is high difficulty and extremely challenging. There is no scenario in which the ship you are on can survive. The only question is… how many can you rescue before it is too late?

At the game’s start, players will roll three dice that will determine what actions they can take. They will, additionally, have one character card to expand their action selection into more powerful and/or more efficient abilities. Players will gain Adrenaline as they run through the ship’s hallways, running and gunning, and evacuating as many innocents as they can. The Adrenaline they gain rotates through a track that may be used for two purposes: gaining spare actions and leveling up. Each time a player levels up, they must choose an additional action die or character action card that make those action die more valuable. When do you take that extra action die versus when do you expand your tableau of possible actions? Should you use the Adrenaline to have a powerful turn now? Or save it to level up next turn? You couldn’t possibly let that innocent person two rooms over perish… could you?

As players rescue innocents on the ship, they draw a Survivor Card depicting that person. All Survivor Cards in the game have unique art work. Each card tells a story of an innocent person on the doomed ship, and has temporary advantages and disadvantages that are granted to the player as long as they are escorting that person. Victory is obtained by rescuing a set amount of survivors. There is never a game of Extraction in which saving the ship is even an option. The only question in Extraction is… can you rescue enough people and get off the ship in time…

—description from the designer

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Justice Society of America
description:
The Justice Society of America holds a unique place as one of the very first teams of its kind. Their roster boasts pioneers of heroism, embodying the spirit of unity in the face of adversity. Despite their shared mission, clashes between heroes are inevitable, reflecting the diverse ideologies in pursuit of justice within their ranks. These conflicts serve as crucibles that test the strength of their bonds, which will be put to the ultimate test against Black Adam, a dark mirror to their ideals.

This Add-On brings the legendary Heroes and Locations surrounding the long-standing Justice Society of America, as well as the struggle between the two god-like beings imbued with the powers of Shazam! Animosity between Heroes is nothing new and the Heroes can be fully divided by using the Clash for Justice Player vs Player mode, pitting teams of Heroes against each other. Otherwise, the Heroes can be more united than ever by adding the JSA Team Deck.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Green Lantern Corps
description:
Heroes, unite!

In brightest day
In blackest night
No evil shall escape my sight.
Let those who worship evil’s might
beware my power…
Green Lantern’s light!

This Crowdfunding Exclusive character pack brings the awesome power of the Green Lantern Corps to DC Super Heroes United. This cosmic team relies as much on each other as they do the power of their will, channeled through their Green Power Rings to perform impossible feats. Each of these Heroes features a unique clear green part in their figure to represent their Green Lantern powers. United, the Green Lanterns are ready to defend all corners of the galaxy from evil.

—description from the publisher

Operation Isabella (OI) is an intermediate-complexity two player wargame simulating the possible events that could have unfolded across Iberia during the late summer of 1942 if either side’s planned but never run campaigns had been set in motion.

A complete game generally takes from two to three hours to complete, and it is suitable for solitaire play. Each hex on the map equals 20 miles (32.4 km) from side to opposite side. each full turn represents one week. every ground unit represents a regiment, brigade or division (or there ad hoc equivalent) of about 2,500 to 12,000 men and/or 50 to 350 armored fighting vehicles.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Specialist Pack – B.A.T. Assault
description:
ELITE FORCES MOVE IN!

Relentless, perfect, and unstoppable -- Cobra’s B.A.T.s (Battle Android Troopers) have been developed by Dr. Mindbender as the latest weapon in their arsenal against the Joes. These new elite foot soldiers escalate the fight, bringing Cobra one step closer to world domination. Cobra also brings their air superiority to bear with their Rattler specialist, Wild Weasel, who rains fire down from above. To face these new threats, you will need a great leader calling the shots. For G.I. JOE that leader is General Hawk, who will coordinate your forces to counter these new Cobra threats!

This is an expansion. Core Game is required to play.

Features

Features Hawk as a new playable character!
Wild Weasel joins as an all-new Lieutenant to battle against.
Introduces Elite Troopers to Mission Critical with the B.A.T.s.
Contents

1 Rule Sheet
1 Hero Figure
4 Elite Foot Soldier Figures
1 Lieutenant Figure
1 Character Card
10 Combat Cards
10 Elite Foot Soldier Enemy Cards
8 Lieutenant Enemy Cards
2 Vehicle Cards
5 Enemy Deployment Cards
1 Elite Deployment Token

—description from the publisher's website

Joy’s Bakery is a 2-6 players Cooperative Family game.

Joy and her friends in Magic school have gathered around to start a bakery shop business. All they have to do is to swing their magic wards, cast their specific cooking spells, and make yummy cakes and rolls to satisfy the customers. They have to show their talents and work altogether, to win as many stars of Reputation for their success. 
Bon Appétit!
 How to Play

     Start from the First Play with Master Chef Card, each player could choose only 1 of his/her Chef Cards to take 1 Main ACTION. 
     Some of the Main Actions are acted with no cost (Example:     drawing 1 Ingredient Cards from Draw Pile.)
     However, some of the Main Actions needed player to discard any 1 of the Ingredient Cards from Kitchen Worktop to the Trash Bin.
     At any time in your Turn, you may freely put any Ingredient Cards from your Worktop on any oven(s); Or put any Ingredient Card(s) from your Worktop on the Trash Bin, as your FREE ACTION. This Free Action could be acted before or after your Chef Card’s Main Action, but not in between. 
     Before you finish your Turn, check the limit of your Worktop (Max 6 Ingredient Cards). Any excess will be placed on the Trash Bin, by the choice of the player.
     After you finish your Turn, the player on your left will be the next phasing player and take a new Turn and so on. 
     The game continues until the Ingredient Draw Pile has exhausted. RESUPPLY Phase starts.


—description from the designer

8 Gates is a tile laying and hand building card game for three or four players.

Players will draw and place Gate cards in the middle, next to existing Gate cards to trigger abilities and score points, depending on where the Gate card is placed. Each player has a single opportunity to have a double turn, but doing so decreases their hand size. And hand size is important, because each player is also secretly building a scoring hand for the end of the game (either a Run or a Set).

The Archivist keeps track of scores and always takes the last turn, but begins with less cards than the other players. At the end of the Archivist’s 4th turn (5th in a 3 player game), the game ends, hands are revealed, and the player with the most points wins!

—description from the designer

COIN is a trick taking card game for four players, played over several Rounds. There are no Trumps. Instead, each player controls 1 of the 4 Colors, and will play cards from their own Color deck (13 cards, each with a Value of 1-13 and worth 1-3 Coins of their Color on the front). The back of each card has no Value and 1 Gray Coin. Cards can be played face up or face down, each with a different result.
​
Each Round, the 8 Trick cards are seeded randomly (and secretly) from each player’s deck. Meaning that every round, the Tricks, and the cards available to each player, will be different. At the end of 8 Tricks, the players score points for the Coins (and maybe cards) in their Bank (score area) different ways. At the end of several Rounds, the player with the most Points wins!

-description from designer

boardgame: Santa's Workshop (Second Edition): 2nd Christmas Promo Pack
description:
STARTING TOY CARDS - Six new starting toy cards with a common theme! When you finish your toy you receive a bonus action during that round!

OLIVE, THE OTHER REINDEER - A new, alternate reindeer for both family and advanced game modes! In advance games, Olive's powers revolve around the other reindeer.

Your team's helicopter has crashed on a deserted island, and the parts to repair it could be anywhere! To make things worse, a huge storm is on the way. Use this map to find all five missing helicopter parts and return to the helipad before the storm arrives.
Each player chooses a character with a special ability and places on the helipad. Each turn you may move around the island, and if you end on a dig space dig for a part. If you find one, add it to the parts tracker, if you don't, add it to the discard pile. At the end of the turn, draw a card from the storm deck and advance the storm or follow the instructions for a special event. 
When you get all the helicopter parts, get your whole team back to the helipad before the storm arrives!

boardgame: 抜歯歯デスゲーム (Tooth Extraction Death Game)
description:
"The tongue ever turns to the aching tooth"

Players each receive 6 white teeth tokens, and 2 gold teeth tokens (worth 8 teeth each).  Players are dealt six cards each.  Cards are numbered 1-34, and each card has a certain number of teeth on it.

On your turn you draw and then discard.  If you draw from the deck, you only draw one card and it can go immediately into your hand.  If you draw from the discard pile you may draw as many cards as you like (starting from the top card, and then set them aside until after you take the discard action).  When you discard, you may discard one card, or as many as you like if they are all consecutive.  When someone has less than five teeth they may declare "It's time for tooth extraction".  The number of teeth is your tooth count.  If the player who declared tooth extraction has the lowest (including tied for lowest), then they will not lose any teeth.   Everyone loses teeth equal to their tooth count.  If the player who declared tooth extraction did not have (or was not tied for) the least, they lose an additional 5 teeth.  
You can pay with any of your teeth, but there is no change (for overpaying with gold teeth).  Continue rounds until there is only one player with teeth remaining.
publishers: 米光と抜群のゲームデザイナーズ (Yonemitsu to batsugun no gēmudezaināzu)

description:
To play Give ‘Em The Finger, one player reads a question, then all players use their Finger Pointers to try to find the answer on the board in front of them. The first person to point to the answer wins the round. The first player to point to the correct answer and to shout wins the card. The first player to collect 6 cards wins the game!

It’s not so easy though. The two-sided boards contain tricky shapes, colors, fonts and point sizes, which add a double-take mental challenge to point to the right answer quickly.

boardgame: ADELLA: The Maritime Strategy Board Game
description:
Synopsis: Navigate your fleet through changing tides, strategize your movements, and outmaneuver your opponent.

Game-play: Adella is an intensely strategic maritime board game, similar to Chess and Go. Players must navigate the ever-changing tides to favourably position their armadas and wreck their enemies to control the high seas. Each head-to-head battle is a unique adventure where your strategic decisions will determine the victor.

How to Play: Players take turns moving the tides on the outside of the board which depicts how the ships can move (a key and complex part of the strategy). Players then take turns by moving each of their Galleons in position to attack their opponent. A player can attack their opponent by broadsiding it (arriving directly adjacent to their opponents). Each ship has two lives. The winner is the player with the last ships standing.

—description from the designer

This mini expansion brings Patron Cards into the game.

Each Patron Card belongs to the specific Experiment board. In the base game those Experiments are distinguished by letters only. Now, on every Patron card you can see the occupation of each Patron associated with the specific Experiment along with their description and image depicting them. From now on, Experiments will be referred to using names of their Patrons introduced by this mini expansion:

Experiment A -> Banker
Experiment B -> Captain of Industry
Experiment C -> Mistress Inventor
Experiment D -> Junior Minister
Experiment E -> Councilor
Experiment F -> Madame Engineer

Note: At this time, it's not clear whether Kingsburg (Third Edition) differs substantially from the second edition, so BGG is creating a listing for this item that will perhaps be merged away in time. In any case, the setting is the same:

Kingsburg: 3rd Edition includes the most updated rulebook and the best expansion modules developed since its 1st edition, such as the Soldier tokens, the Governor cards, and the extra buildings. Furthermore, the artwork and design have been restyled while components have been redesigned.

The realm of Kingsburg is under attack! Monstrous invaders are gathering at the borders, aiming to invade and plunder the realm! Your king has chosen you to take charge of a province on the border; you will manage your province and help defend the realm. To accomplish this, you must influence the King's advisors and the Royal Family to obtain gold, wood, stones, and soldiers to expand and defend your lands. But you are not the only governor seeking the aid of the advisers! The other players also seek to collect the best resources for their own territories.

King Tritus is waiting for you. Will you be able to be the most influent and powerful governor of the realm?

The game of Kingsburg takes place over five years, a total of twenty turns. In every year, there are three production seasons for collecting resources, building structures, and training troops. Every fourth turn is the winter, in which all the players must fight an invading army. Each player must face the invaders, so this is not a cooperative game.

The resources to build structures and train troops are collected by influencing the advisers in the King's Council. Players place their influence dice on members of the Council, and each adviser awards different resources or allocate soldiers, victory points, and other advantages to the player who was able to influence that adviser for the current turn. The player with the lowest influence dice sum is the first to choose where to spend their influence; this acts as a way of balancing poor dice rolling. Even with a very unlucky roll, a clever player can still come out from the Council with a good number of resources and/or soldiers.

At the end of five years, the player who best developed their assigned territory and most pleased the King through the Council wins.

Changed from 2nd edition:

New art style and updated (downgraded to cardboard) game components
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: On 140 pages, the rules and stories for all previously published characters are presented and told. There are also many new characters that make Longfall and the surrounding area unsafe. New scenarios complete the Content.

The second crew book for Freebooter's Fate. Filled to the brim with many new characters and their stories. A collection of the new rules and the scenario "The Alliance" - all against the shadows round it all off. The scenario can also be played as a single player scenario.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Return of the King: Saga Expansion
description:
While Frodo, Sam, and Gollum journeyed through the glades of Ithilien, Aragorn and his companions fought the battle of Helm’s Deep and rode to the wreck of Isengard to deal with the traitor, Saruman. After obtaining a Palantír from that encounter, Aragorn learned much of the Enemy’s movements, and now he and his companions must ride to the aid of Gondor while the Ring-bearer draws ever closer to Mount Doom…

Fantasy Flight Games is proud to announce The Return of the King, the final repackaged expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game! This expansion combines all of the contents from The Flame of the West and The Mountain of Fire.

Experience the conclusion of the journey to destroy the One Ring in this epic expansion. This expansion contains six scenarios that recreate the adventures featured in the third part of the legendary The Lord of the Rings saga. In this box, you’ll also find over 60 hero and player cards, which can be used to build or enhance your decks for any The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game scenario or campaign.

—description from the publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description: This battery-operated device keeps adding one light and tone to the sequence until the player fails to repeat the sequence using the four buttons. This game is designed to have on the floor while the player is seated.

boardgame: Travel Win, Lose or Draw Junior
description:
There are eighteen game cards, Each game card has fifteen numbered subjects to be drawn. One player is the artist and given the pad and paper. The other players are the guessers. A card is drawn at random and a guesser chooses a random number from one to fifteen. The drawer has one minute, as determined by the enclosed sand timer, to convey the item chosen on the pad. If anyone guesses correctly before time has expired, then both the artist and guesser score one point. The role of artist passes every turn. The first player to score six points is the winner.

This game is loosely based on the "Win, Lose or Draw" television series.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
A game of gaining and holding power

You are either a monarch of a small kingdom, or a member of the nobility under that monarch, owning land that provides you with an income you can spend to either pay taxes to the monarch, or hire troops to protect yourself and your allies, or attack your enemies.

Either way, you’ll need help from your fellow players. If you try to stand alone, you won't make it very long. But who should you trust, and how much can you really trust them?

This game has multiple winners. The monarch always wins. Depending on how many players are playing, either the one or two of the other nobles that have paid the most money in taxes to the winning monarch will also win. You’ll get gold and crops to sell every turn, and you’ll have to decide how to spend them to secure your future. Will you try to win by allying yourself to the current ruler? Or will you support someone else’s claim to the throne? Or will you try to take the throne yourself?

—description from the publisher

In Don't Wake the Dragon, players play the role of thieves, who venture to plunder the dragon's treasure while he sleeps. Each turn, players decide how many of the 4D6 dice to roll in order to collect the booty rewards.

During their turn, the players can decide to steal, pass, or retreat at any time.
If the player make noise three times, the dragon will awaken, taking all his treasures obtained in that round.

Whoever has accumulated the maximum profit after three turns wins!

—description from the designer

MONOPOLY TOKENS: Move around the game board using one of the exclusive tokens: A deer, a trucker hat, a chainsaw, a flamingo lawn ornament, a mounted bass fish, or a car up on blocks.

DAWGONE FUNNY: In this edition of the game, players buy trailers, then upgrade to cabins. Gameboard features hilarious spaces such as Bilbo's Bait Shop, and Big Stinky's Flea Market.

Includes gameboard, 6 tokens, 28 Title Deed cards, 16 Chance cards, 16 Community Chest cards, 32 trailers 12 cabins, spinner, money pack, and game guide.

You Little Stinker is a simple picture-matching game designed specifically for young children. It features a dog-shaped dice shaker that makes it easy for little hands to shake and roll.

To play, place the dice in the doggy shaker, give it a shake, and roll the dice. Match your dice with the picture cards. Bones are super lucky, but players must watch out for stinky mud puddles. A few lucky rolls and a few smart choices will win the game!

—description from the publisher

One of the most important and urgent issues affecting our world right now is climate change. It refers to the long-term alterations in Earth's climate, such as variations in temperature, precipitation, and weather patterns, that have been noticed during the previous century and are mostly brought on by human activity.

This game aims to educate players about the importance of climate change and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) that guide global efforts towards a sustainable future. Players will learn about the targets and indicators in environmental-related SDGs (6, 7, 12, 13, 14, and 15) that are particularly relevant to addressing climate change. They will also understand how collaboration can support the achievement of other SDGs.

—description from the designer

This game is the cat's meow when it comes to breaking the ice at gatherings. Whether you’re a cool gamester cat or curious kitten, Oopsy Poopsy bridges the generation gap and promises to whisker you away into a whirlwind of laughter and fun!
It's paw-sitively purrrfect for bringing together friends, family, and even those neighborhood strays who wander in.

With unique game play and hilarious callouts, simply be the first player to collect the required number of cats in your yard to win.

Ohhh but there’s sweet sabotage! The lead changes quickly as other players try to lure your curtain-shredder with a fresh fishy by saying, "Here kitty kitty!" Some may tell your little fur ball to, "Get the Mousy!" causing it to become lost. Still others may even force you to scoop the litterbox in a desperate attempt to slow you down!

With Oopsy Poopsy, you'll be feline frisky as you create unforgettable memories and strengthen bonds faster than a cat chasing a laser pointer! Say goodbye to boring get-togethers and embrace unforgettable moments that will have everyone saying "meow-some!"

Don't miss out on the purrr-fection of Oopsy Poopsy, the Litterbox Edition. Grab yours right meow and let the fur fly!

—description from the publisher

Conexio (from the Latin word for "connection") is a dice allocation game for 1-2 players, suitable for all ages. In this engaging two-player contest, you'll unleash your tactical prowess by strategically manoeuvring discs and meeples to match your colour across the table.

Goal of the game: Be the first player to connect all your meeples to your discs.

The player needs to provide the following wooden components or similar to play:

     10 White discs, 1 White die
     5 Black discs, 2 Black dice, 5 Black meeples
     5 Orange discs, 2 Orange dice, 5 Orange meeples


Disclaimer: the game can receive updates\small adjustments in the future following feedback from the players.

This is the game #01 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.


—description from the designer

Complete missions and challenges with 4 types of different cards. Sometimes you can get secret missions to complete without other players noticing. The game board takes you through the phases of a drinking night with divided sections. Try to reach the goal before others and have fun getting knowing your company better! No need to actually drink despite the name.

boardgame: CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist
description:
CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist is a strategic board game and simulation set in the technologically advanced Smart Island City, a bustling metropolis known for its interconnected infrastructure and vibrant community life. The game supports up to 32 players, divided into two teams: the Red Team, consisting of cyber criminals and hackers, and the Blue Team, composed of cybersecurity professionals and first responders. The backdrop of the game includes significant city events such as a championship game, fireworks, and a parade, which add to the complexity and urgency of the cyber warfare. The simulation components realistically cover traffic, crowds, and potential catastrophes, adding depth to the strategic gameplay.

The gameplay of CyberStrike24 blends physical and digital elements, offering a hybrid experience through its board game components and an interactive mobile app. Players navigate through 56 locations (expandable to 80 with the game's expansions), each with unique strategic importance. The game is turn-based, with each hour in the game representing a real-time turn. Players must carefully plan their moves, employing tactics such as hacking, social engineering, and crisis management to achieve their objectives. The interactive app enhances the experience by providing real-time updates and personal perspectives for each player. Players can acquire cyberweapons on the dark web and build a botnet to execute their attacks, adding a layer of realism to the cyber warfare.

The primary goal for the Red Team is to sow chaos and fear by hacking into critical systems, stealing data and money, and causing widespread disruptions. Their ultimate objective is to drive the city's population into a state of panic, thereby scoring the highest points and achieving an instant victory if the panic level reaches its maximum. Conversely, the Blue Team aims to maintain peace and order by defending against cyber-attacks, restoring compromised systems, and apprehending hackers. They score points for systems defended, data and money preserved, and hackers apprehended, with the possibility of an instant win if they manage to arrest all the hackers. For larger games, a mole can be introduced as an insider or an informant, adding an extra layer of intrigue and strategy.

CyberStrike24 also offers educational benefits by familiarizing players with key cybersecurity terminology and job roles. The game provides an immersive way to understand the different facets of cyber warfare, including the ethical considerations and technical skills involved in both hacking and defending. By simulating real-world cyber threats and defense strategies, it aids in grasping the complexities of cybersecurity, making it a valuable tool for both students and professionals in the field.

—description from the designer

boardgame: CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist, Volcano District Expansion
description:
The Volcano District Expansion for CyberStrike24 introduces new strategic elements and exciting gameplay to the base game. This expansion features key locations such as the Casino and Gold Mine, which serve as high-risk, high-reward targets. These additions compel players to develop innovative strategies to navigate and exploit these new environments effectively.

The expansion pack includes various new components that enhance the overall game. It brings two new hackers, the Prankster and the Scammer, each with unique skills and motivations that expand the Red Team's strategic possibilities. Additionally, two new defenders, the Data Privacy Specialist and the Security Specialist, join the Blue Team, offering new capabilities to counter the Red Team's threats. Furthermore, the expansion introduces two new types of catastrophes, additional maps, and vehicles, all of which add layers of complexity and variety to the game.

CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist is a strategic board game that immerses players in the high-stakes world of cybersecurity within a smart city. The game supports up to 32 players, divided into Red and Blue teams, each with specific roles and objectives. The Red Team aims to disrupt the city's systems and cause chaos, while the Blue Team focuses on defending these systems and maintaining order. The game combines physical board game elements with an interactive mobile app, providing a seamless hybrid experience that requires careful planning, quick thinking, and teamwork.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist, Remote District Expansion
description:
The Remote District Expansion for CyberStrike24 introduces critical new locations and challenges, enriching the game's strategic depth. This expansion features high-stakes areas such as the Supermax Prison and the Bio Research Facility, adding significant strategic points for both teams to target or defend.

The expansion includes several new components that enhance the core gameplay. Players gain two new hackers for the Red Team, the Cyberterrorist and the Digital Pirate. The Cyberterrorist focuses on disrupting critical infrastructure to advance a political or ideological agenda, while the Digital Pirate specializes in the unauthorized duplication and distribution of digital content. On the Blue Team, the Intelligence Coordinator and Infrastructure Specialist join the ranks, providing new defensive capabilities. The Intelligence Coordinator excels in gathering and analyzing threat data, while the Infrastructure Specialist focuses on the reliability and security of the city's essential services.

CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist is a strategic board game set in a high-stakes cyber warfare scenario within a smart city. The game supports up to 32 players, divided into Red and Blue teams, each with specific roles and objectives. The Red Team aims to disrupt the city's systems and cause chaos, while the Blue Team focuses on defending these systems and maintaining order. The game combines physical board game elements with an interactive mobile app, providing a seamless hybrid experience that requires careful planning, quick thinking, and teamwork.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist, Sentinel District Expansion
description:
The Sentinel District Expansion for CyberStrike24 introduces new strategic challenges and vital locations, significantly enriching the game's complexity. Set on an island featuring a towering extinct volcanic mountain, this district is split between a lush jungle-covered windward side and an arid leeward side due to the rain shadow effect. The island houses a critical and self-sufficient military base that includes a submarine port, shipyard, and nuclear power plant, along with essential support facilities such as a lighthouse and an ammunition depot. These locations add new dimensions to gameplay, requiring players to manage both military and civilian infrastructure strategically.

The expansion includes a variety of new components to enhance the gameplay experience. Players receive two new Red Team hackers: the Saboteur, specializing in physical and digital sabotage, and the Cryptographer, an expert in breaking and creating complex encryption codes. On the Blue Team, new defenders include the Naval Security Officer, responsible for protecting naval facilities, and the Vigilante, a wildcard character using unconventional methods to neutralize cyber threats. Additionally, the expansion introduces new maps and vehicles, such as a ferry and a police boat, further enhancing mobility and strategic options across the game board.

CyberStrike24: Smart City Takedown, Hacker Heist is a strategic board game set in a high-stakes cyber warfare scenario within a smart city. The game accommodates up to 32 players, divided into Red and Blue teams, each with specific roles and objectives. The Red Team aims to disrupt the city's systems and cause chaos, while the Blue Team focuses on defending these systems and maintaining order. The game combines physical board game elements with an interactive mobile app, providing a seamless hybrid experience that requires careful planning, quick thinking, and teamwork. Expansions like the Sentinel District ensure that the game remains dynamic and engaging, offering new scenarios and strategic challenges.

—description from the publisher

Washed socks search for their pair, 
Hang them on the line with care.
Paired socks in drawers we'll store,
No more mess, forevermore.

Collect the most pairs of socks in your drawer and win this interactive memory game.

But not everything is as simple as it seems. 
Players need to pay close attention to which socks are drying on other players' lines and be wary of holey socks. 
Younger players can also enjoy this game as a basic memory game.

—description from the designer

Experience the challenges of the spread of Islam in Arabian Peninsula, after the emigration of Prophet Muhammad to the conquest of Mecca.
In this game, the players in the role of Muslim tribes go to war with the infidels and after cleaning different cities, they build mosques in them.
Among other things that can be done in this game:

     Freeing the slaves from the infidels
     Education of slaves to become traders, scientists and soldiers
     Buying and selling items


And...
At the end, the player who has the most total points from building mosques, wars, items, etc. will win the game.

boardgame: Game of HAM: Expansion – Depression Pack: Volume 2
description:
This expansion captures depression in a bottle, or in this case, within a bunch of cards. It's a pack that contains the essence of depression or things that would cause it… after all, if we don't laugh a little about it, we just cry…

—description from the publisher

Harvest Town is a card engine construction game, combined with dice drive, allowing the game's strategy and luck to coexist. Use absolute strategy to make up for luck, or use absolute luck to surpass strategy. Players have different dimensions of play. This game is a light-to-medium German strategy game. The simple and easy-to-understand gameplay lowers the threshold for getting started, and the card placement of the game increases the number of games and changes, and each game has a different experience. In the game, players will play the role of the mayor of the town, away from the noisy battlefield, but even in a remote town, we want to develop better, but there are also competitors who compete with us. Let us use cards and dice to impress other players.

Experience an exciting sea battle between samurai pirates and claim the treasures of the deep ocean.
Players must roll 3 dice on their turn. Then choose 2 dice from among them to perform their action.
Then it is the turn of the other competitors to use the active player's selected dice to their advantage and activate the cards in their desired column.
By building an advanced engine, players go to war with other competitors and try to use their weaknesses to gain the most points in their favor.
Looting coins, artillery attack, healing the wounded, using strategist cards, etc., all will have points for the players at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

"The moment of the Final Clash has arrived! The Pope and the Doge hate each other, but most of all they hate you! You have challenged them by your actions, and now it's time for their revenge!"

Pope & Doge gives you the chance to finally challenge your archenemies in two intense new alternate Missions that will require all your skills to complete!
The two gorgeous miniatures of the Pope and the Doge not only represent the two super bosses, but can also be used as two new enemy types: the Bishop for Rome and the Patrician for Venice!

Get ready for the final confrontation!

—description from the publisher

¡Qué Dice Chile! is the Spanish-language version of the popular game Family Feud. This party game captures the excitement and fun of guessing the most popular answers to survey questions, making it a perfect choice for family gatherings and social events.

In ¡Qué Dice Chile!, players divide into two teams and compete to see who can guess the top answers to questions surveyed among a large group of people in Chile. One player acts as the host, reading the questions and managing the flow of the game. Each team collaborates to come up with their answers, aiming to match the most popular responses on the survey board. Points are awarded based on the popularity of the answers, with higher points given for more common responses.

The game is played over several rounds, each featuring a new survey question. First, one representative of each team try to guess the most popular question on the board, the team with the most popular guess gets to continue guessing. Each team member say their guesses out loud within a limited time frame and the host checks them against the answer board. When a team guesses incorrectly 3 times, the other team gets a chance to steal the accumulated points by guessing one more answer correctly. Each round can award single, double or triple the points based on the progression of the game. The team with the most points at the end of all rounds advances to the "Fast Money" round.

The "Fast Money" round is the final and most exciting part of ¡Qué Dice Chile!. In this round, two players from the winning team take turns answering five rapid-fire survey questions within a short time limit. The first player answers the questions while the second player is kept out of hearing range. After the first player finishes, their answers are revealed, and the second player is brought back to answer the same questions. If both players together score a total of 200 points or more based on the popularity of their answers, the team wins the game. If not, the opposing team gets a chance at the "Fast Money" round and can win the game if their total accumulated points (including Fast Money round) surpasses the leading team's. This high-stakes round adds an extra layer of excitement and challenge to the game, often determining the ultimate victors.
Small deck of 18 cards that adds Crew casualties and U-boat specific damage. This expands the narrative part of the game and adds lasting effects to your patrols.

What is a Spoonerism?
A spoonerism is a playful twist on language where the initial sounds, consonants, or vowels of two or more words in a phrase are swapped to create a humorous or nonsensical result. For example, "Smart Feller" might become "Fart Smeller"! PoBody's Nerfect takes this fun concept and turns it into a lively game filled with clever and silly spoonerisms that will have you and your friends laughing out loud.

Game Overview:
PoBody's Nerfect is a hilarious party game where players decode jumbled words and phrases—known as PoNos (short for "Spoonerisms"). These are intentionally scrambled phrases that sound amusing when spoken aloud. The objective is to correctly identify the original phrase before the timer runs out and earn the most points!

How to Play

1. In the box, you'll find:

     300 PoNo game cards: 100 Easy (Yellow), 100 Medium (Aqua), and 100 Hard (Navy).
     A 1-minute sand timer.
     2 Dice: the colored one as the Card Picker and the Alphabet die as the Question Selector.


Objective of the Game: Decipher the jumbled words and phrases, called PoNos, to rack up the most points!

2. Place each of the three colored card decks on the table with the question side down. Depending on your skill level, you can choose to play with all decks/difficulty levels or choose the level right for you!

3. The youngest player is Player #1. Roll both dice to determine which PoNo deck to pull from and which question to read. The Color die will determine which deck color to pull a card from, and the Letter die will determine which question to read.

4. Flip the timer to start the countdown and draw the top card from the deck that matches the dice color. Player ONE reads the PoNo or jumbled phrase aloud for the other players to guess.

5. For example, if the phrase is ‘A NOSY LITTLE COOK,’ the first player to say ‘A COZY LITTLE NOOK’ collects the card for points.

6. If a player guesses correctly before the timer runs out, they WIN the card. If no one guesses the answer, Player ONE keeps the card.

7. When the game is over, count up your points:

     Yellow cards are worth 1 point each.
     Aqua cards are worth 2 points each.
     Navy cards are worth 3 points each.


8. The player with the most points WINS!

Features

     300 Sturdy Spoonerism Cards (3 Decks = Easy, Intermediate and Difficult)
     1 Sand Timer
     2 Dice - One Color Die and One Letter Die
     Instruction Sheet


—description from the publisher

In this fun word game for two teams, players take turns as clue-giver, trying to get others to guess target words.  The trick is that the clue-giver is restricted by 4 random rule cards each turn (for example, you can say words that end in T, you can say words with exactly 1 O, you can make hand gestures with 1 hand).

Teams score points for guessing correct answers. After everyone has a turn as clue-giver, the team with the most points wins.

-description from publisher

Agromensor, the Kingdom's Royal Landscaper has died. The King has opened the doors of the castle in search for a new talent that would even come close to the prowess of the old designer. You are one of the many aspiring young souls vying for the title of Royal Landscaper.

You will need to build a grand plan of the kingdom, showing efficient use of resources, economic routes, and provide safety and security to the inhabitants of the kingdom in order to impress the King. Are you up to the task to mold the topography of the Kingdom and become the new Royal Landscaper? Let’s find out.

—description from the designer

The dark phantom, known as Father Time, grows weaker by the day as his reign nears its end.  Lead one of the four ancient abstracts, known as the Time Whisperers, through the ages to seize his throne before your opponents do.

The Time Whisperers is a competitive game for two to four players with a duration of about forty-five minutes and ages ten and up. (Youth variant: competitive game, 20 minutes, ages 7+).

Having to navigate a tight area control efficiency puzzle with hand management and game breaking asymmetrical player powers, you will need to consider both strategy and tactics to be successful.

Play action cards to move, strengthen, and invert your Time Whisperers, all the while applying special powers to radically bend the rules of the game in your favor.  Decide whether to use your dark strength to control the phantom, or switch to gold strength to increase your future potential.  After four rounds of plotting deception and retribution, will a new ruler of the Eternity emerge!

—description from the designer

Space Freight is an exhilarating board game where you step into the role of a space freighter captain, exploring the vast expanse of unknown space in search of fortune. As you navigate the cold black of the 'verse, you'll mine ore, salvage debris, trade and influence markets, complete missions, and confront the temptation of space piracy to amass your wealth in gold.

In this fast-paced game for 2-5 players, each captain takes two actions per turn, moving their ships between their base, space, planets, and trading ports. You'll mine, salvage, swap, and trade resources, all while managing the limitations of your crew. Expand your capabilities by recruiting new crew members, gain advantages with rare officers, and execute powerful turns with boosts. The game concludes when all the gold is in player storage, so you'll need to race to complete missions, trade for gold at the exchange, and leverage your crew and boosts to outmaneuver your opponents and engage in space piracy.

A typical game lasts about 15 minutes per player, ensuring a unique experience every time. Space Freight combines worker placement, deck building, pick-up and delivery, missions, and a dynamic trading exchange based on dice values to create a rich and engaging gameplay experience.

boardgame: Drops and Lobbers: Pickleball the Board Game
description:
Set up the net.. pick your player.. and roll the dice to a 5.0 victory! Players start off as a 2.0 skill level pickleball player. The goal is to become the first player to become a 5.0 player. From rolling the dice, to using various playing cards, each player has the ability to become a 5.0 player. Players can choose to play the game as singles (two players) or doubles (four players) from three different skill levels "Beginner" "Intermediate" "Advanced". Each player has their own unique hitting skill and ability. Players can earn or lose skills as they play through the game. Just like in a real game of Pickleball "Strategy" can be key.
Will you "Ace" your serve, roll a "Bad Shot" or use a "Strategy Card" and force a player to miss their serve? Gather some "Pickleball" friends and find out who truly is a 5.0 player!

Monty Python's Patsy Pack contains eight new cards for Monty Python Fluxx, including King Arthur's steed Patsy, the Bridge of Death, and Sir Not Appearing in This Game.

Other cards :
The King's Trusty Servant
Clippity Clop
The Quiet Ones
It's Terry Gilliam
Follow Patsy

While the pack contains 8 cards, SIr Not Appearing in this Game (using the same art work) was available in 2009 as an individual promo card.  See  Monty Python Fluxx: Castle Expansion

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – The Ministry
description:
The clan of faith, the clan of lies – the most light-fearing vampires are known by many names, currently preferring “The Ministry”. They revel in sin and seek to cross all boundaries, the holier the better. They want everyone to join in, using their powers of deception to lure innocent victims into misery.

This deck delivers a steady stream of mischief on your enemy, with tactics both direct and indirect. Your vampires do anything to elude defenders – sneaking, hypnotizing, even shape-changing! Your defense is limited, but you have some Anarch tricks up your sleeve.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Gangrel
description:
Clan Gangrel are outsiders, roaming wilderness and suburbs, straying from politics and intrigue. They keep in tune with nature and with their Inner Beast, and they value their freedom and are prepared to fight for it. The clan has now joined the Anarch Movement – they will not be pushed around, and their claws run deep.

This deck takes a defensive stance, planning for the late game while occasionally hitting opponents with precise attacks where it hurts the most. Your strengths are animal companions, Anarch tactics and the Protean shape-changing powers of your minions, which help to pass blockers, intercept enemies and excel in combat.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Brujah
description:
Clan Brujah are troublemakers, always questioning authority. They tend to Embrace anyone with a strong cause and a loud voice to their ranks, be it teen punks, gang members or any kind of activists. While many Brujah are skilled with words, they are also quick to rage – difficult debates are often best solved with a blur of fists!

This deck relies on combat supremacy. Your minions’ skills in Celerity and Potence will scare your opponents from blocking your Presence-fueled bleed actions. For defense you apply surprise Anarch tricks, or assault other minions directly, mangling them into the ground before they become a threat.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Banu Haqim
description:
The Children of Haqim were once a reclusive sect of vampiric warriors, sorcerers and assassins. In modern nights, they are torn between that heritage and an obsession for moral codes and swift justice. In vampire society, their role is often to enforce the law. Someone is always guilty, and the guilty must be punished.

This deck focuses on consistent aggression. Your minions act quickly, hide in shadows and wield powerful Blood Sorcery. They are well-suited to hunting down enemy minions or striking directly at your opponent’s influence. Your defense is limited but strong, putting down threats preemptively and punishing attacks on your domain.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – The Fall of London
description:
The vampiric society of London trembles. Mithras, ancient Kindred god-ruler of Britain, has returned to claim his domain. Will you join his cult, or will you dare to defy his plans? At the same time the Second Inquisition launches a massive operation to hunt down and destroy the city’s undead residents. Are these your final nights, or can you use this crisis to gain advantage over your enemies?

This set is a fixed assortment of 40 crypt cards and 14 library cards for Vampire: The Eternal Struggle, all brand new, never printed before, ready to mix with other cards for the game to construct your own deck (each player needs a deck to play).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Shadows of Berlin
description:
“The last Prince of Berlin died in the street, torn apart by an angry mob of Anarchs. That’s something to think about if you believe that the Camarilla is forever, or that the young, naive Kindred of the Anarch Movement can never present a real threat.”
– Vampire: The Masquerade Fifth Edition Camarilla Sourcebook

This set is a fixed assortment of 54 cards to add to your Vampire: The Eternal Struggle collection. It contains reprints of crypt cards previously only available in the exclusive Berlin Anthology set, as well as some other powerful, sought after cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Tzimisce
description:
The Tzimisce were once noble tyrants of Eastern Europe, but in modern nights they covet power worldwide. For them control is all. They aim to take what you thought was yours and then jealously guard their possessions like a dragon guards its hoard.

This deck is a versatile toolbox. Your vampires use mind-altering Dominate powers to gain influence and undermine opponents. In combat they can turn into bats or mist to escape, or rework their bodies into monstrous forms to seriously wreck their foes. For defense you rely on animal spies and hideous ghoul servants.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Salubri
description:
The Salubri are warriors and healers who tread the path of Golconda – ultimate redemption from the Kindred curse. But with this path comes suspicion and persecution, as the power to heal brings with it the power to harm. They are both hunters and hunted in their pursuit of enlightenment.

This deck relies on vigilance and protection. Your vampires use their powers of Auspex to foresee enemy moves and Fortitude to make their bodies nigh-indestructible in combat. When the time is right, you launch upon your prey, using mind-swaying Dominate powers to damage your foes.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – Ravnos
description:
The Ravnos are nomadic vampires, always staying one step ahead of the doom that lurks in their blood to preserve their lineage. These daredevils and tricksters seek risks in unlife and are often hired for espionage and sabotage, fitting well with their mastery of disguise and distraction.

This deck is about charm and misdirection. Your vampires use their powers of Obfuscate and Presence to distract and manipulate foes, undermining their power. For protection you rely on animal spies and guards, and yet more illusions to turn even the mightiest threat completely harmless.

This is a deck with 77 library cards and 12 crypt cards, ready for play out of the box or customizable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

Promotion the Game is a fast-paced card game that satirizes the cutthroat and often absurd nature of office life. Players take on the roles of ambitious office workers, vying for the coveted promotion by collecting sets of skill cards (like "Brownie Point Burgler" or "Scapegoat Seeker").

But beware, the path to the top is riddled with obstacles! Action cards like "Reorg" and "Power Play" allow players to sabotage their rivals, creating hilarious and unpredictable turns. The dreaded "Performance Improvement Plan" card looms over every player, threatening to derail their career aspirations.

To win, players must strategically collect skill cards, unleash timely action cards, and avoid becoming the victim of office politics. Whether you choose to play fair or embrace your inner office villain, Promotion the Game guarantees a laugh-filled experience that perfectly captures the highs and lows of the corporate world.

—description from the designer

Mutarum (from the Latin word for "change\exchange") is a dice allocation game for 1-2 players, suitable for all ages. In this abstract board game, players embark on a journey of cellular evolution, aiming to change a diverse group of discs into a harmonious, unified cell.

Goal of the game: The 1st player to form two cells with all of their discs unified.

The player needs to provide the following wooden components or similar to play:

     36 discs (12 Red, 12 Blue, 12 White)
     6 dice (3 Red, 3 Blue)
     4 Meeples (2 Red, 2 Blue)


Disclaimer: the game can receive updates\small adjustments in the future following feedback from the players.

This is the game #02 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.

—description from the designer

Warfig is a new fresh table top war game with a focus on quick deployable tiles combined with customistable cards!

Warfig is targetted at games who enjoy warfare and strategy and who like to flexibility of customising their armies with different weapons, spells and commands

Simple ruleset with the complexity at the card level

Warfig is focused on being the game for everyone

Warfig, built by Wargamers by Wargamers

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Gotham City
description:
Gotham City has long been known as a haven for crime and corruption. But from this darkness rose its greatest defenders: Batman and his extended Batman Family. In their desperate fight for justice, some of them may take things too far into villainous territory. But at the same time, even criminals may be inspired to act heroically from time to time. When the Bat-Signal shines in the night sky, the Heroes must unite or succumb to the darkness.

This expansion brings DC Super Heroes United to the mean streets of Gotham City. The Heroes of the Batman Family are always ready to take on the multitude of threats assailing the city, even as some of them occasionally fall so far as to become Villains themselves. This expansion also features the Bat-Signal Challenge, forcing Heroes to deal with additional emergencies, as well as much-needed Support characters to help the Heroes in the insurmountable task of cleansing the city.

The Gotham City expansion comes with:

1 Batgirl Figure
    1 Nightwing Figure
    1 Catwoman figure
    1 Red Hood Figure
    1 Bane Figure
    1 Alfred Figure
    12 Batgirl Hero Cards
    12 Nightwing Hero Cards
    12 Catwoman Hero Cards
    12 Red Hood Hero Cards
    9 Equipment Cards
    1 Catwoman Villain Dashboard
    12 Catwoman Master Plan Cards
    6 Catwoman Threat Cards
    1 Red Hood Villain Dashboard
    12 Red Hood Master Plan Cards
    6 Red Hood Threat Cards
    1 Bane Villain Dashboard
    12 Bane Master Plan Cards
    6 Bane Threat Cards
    6 Locations
    1 Alfred Support Card
    2 Challenge Cards
    5 Bat-Signal Cards
    1 Bat-Signal Token

Gamefound edition includes exclusive bonus characters: Batwoman and Commissioner Gordon, along with their cards!

1 Batwoman Figure
    12 Batwoman Hero Cards
    6 Equipment Cards
    1 Commissioner Gordon Figure
    1 Commissioner Gordon Support Card

boardgame: Crash Team Rumble: Crash Collector Card Game
description:
McDonald's Happy Meal Toy #1 in this series.
Promotion for the Video Game: Crash Team Rumble

Crash Collector Card Game is for 1-4 players.

The game includes 30 cards plus a rules card. The 30 cards each depicts a different character from the online multiplayer video game Crash Team Rumble. Each card also has 3 values ranging between 20 and 100 associated with each of three categories: Relics, Gems, and Wumpa Fruit. These 3 values always sum to 220.

From the rules:

Game Instructions Crash Collector Card Game

Set Up (1 Player)


     Split card deck into 2 even piles, face down.
     The pile on the right is the player's deck. The pile on the left is the "challenger's" deck.


Play

1) At the start of each turn, the player picks a category - Relic, Gem, or Wumpa Fruit.
2) The player flips over the top card from each deck.
3) Whoever has the highest number for that category wins the 2 cards and sets them aside.
4) If the numbers are the same, the cards are put back under each of their piles.

Win


     Once all cards are used, the player counts up the number of cards won by themselves and the "challenger".
     See how many times you can beat the "challenger"!


Set Up (Multi-player)


     Number of players needed 2-4
     Shuffle the cards and deal the same number of cards to each player.


Play

1) Players only look at their top card.
2) Player 1 (left of dealer) "calls out" their top cards highest number (Example: "Gem 100").
3) They place that card in the center.
4) Other players then look at their top card and "call out" their number for the same category (example: "Gem 70") and place it in the center.

Win


     The player with the highest number for that category wins all the cards (and adds them to the bottom of their pile).
     The winner (of the round) "calls out" the category for their next top card and play repeats.
     The player who gets all the cards wins.

boardgame: Memory Matching Card Game: Washington, D.C. Landmarks
description:
MEMO Washington, D.C., is a family-friendly card game with hand-crafted illustrations. It is best for family nights, exploring U.S. landmarks, and preparing for traveling. The card game is based on the classic memory-matching game, supplemented with special rules and action cards that make the game competitive and exciting.
Recommended for teens, adults, and seniors ages fourteen and up. Playing the MEMO Washington, D.C. card game, you practice memory and concentration, enjoy stylish illustrations, relax, learn exciting things, have fun, and build strong relationships.

Shuffle and lay 50 cards out in a grid face-down. You take turns flipping two cards each. If you find a matching pair, snag them off the board and get another turn.

If the cards don't match, flip them back over. The turn passes to the next player.

If you flip Silver Card #2, The Capitol card, you have two chances to find its twin. Flip a second card. If it's a match, continue as usual. If it's a miss, you get one more flip. Strike out twice? Pass the baton to the next player.

If you flip Gold Card #1, The White House card, you have an extra turn, even if your next card doesn't match up.

Heads up: Each player can use the superpower of the Gold and Silver Cards only once per game.

Other way to play:
Goal: Grab as many cards as you can! Shuffle and lay cards face-down in a stack. Players take turns flipping one card at a time. If you can name the item pictured on the card, snag it. If not, it goes back to the bottom of the pile. Next player's up! Keep the fun going until all the cards are gone.

boardgame: Mickey and Friends Walt Disney World: Clap Game
description:
McDonald's Happy Meal Toy #5 in this series.

The Clap Game is for 2-4 players. The included bonus game, Battle, is for 2 players.

The game includes 40 cards plus a rules sheet. There are 8 each of Mickey (red rank 1), Minnie (pink rank 2), Goofy (green rank 3), Donald (blue rank 4), and Daisy (purple rank 5). So, all the cards are triple coded with character, color, and rank.

From the rules:

Game Instructions: Clap Game

Players 2-4
Goal: Be the player with the most pairs at the end of the game
Setup: Deal all the cards to each player, face down
Rules:

     Player to the left of dealer begins, then continues clockwise
     On their turn, each player will flip their top card over and place in the center of all players, forming a pile
     If the top card matches the card directly beneath, players clap their hands together
     The first player to clap their hands takes the entire center pile and adds them face-down to the bottom of their deck
     Game continues until players run out of cards. The last player remaining is the winner


Bonus Game: Battle

Players: 2
Goal: Be the player with the most cards at the end of the game
Setup: Deal the cards evenly between two players. Each player places their stack of cards face down in front of them
Rules:

     Each player turns over their top card at the same time
     The player with the highest number takes both cards and sets them aside, forming a pile
     If the cards have the same number, it is Battle. In a Battle, each player turns over the next card in their deck. Whoever has the higher number takes all 4 cards and adds them to their pile
     If the cards are again the same number, repeat the play until one player turns over a higher number and takes all the cards that were played
     The game ends when each player has played all the cards in their stack
     Whoever has the most cards wins!

This is a free Print and Play game, containing 7 PDF files. It is a fully developed and comprehensive game of armored warfare of WW2.

Tank Board Game II is a tank game covering most WW2 European tank battles; from Poland 1939 to Germany 1945. The attacker's goal is to destroy the defender’s towns or exit his tanks off the gameboard edge, while the defender's goal is to destroy the attacker’s HQ. Players take turns moving their units on a grid and firing their guns. The game also plays very well as a solitaire game.

The game uses a new micro-detailed yet hyper-quick hit/penetration system so players can focus on playing the game instead of spending time calculating shots. This makes it easy to have tank battles with 40-50 units with each vehicle having a different plate for: Hull/Turret; Front/ Side/ Rear/ Top; instead of just one ‘defense-factor’.

All equipment data, both Axis and Allied, is listed on one 8 ½ x 11” data sheet for each front/year combination. This combines multiple data tables and, along with the color-coding for armor thickness and gun penetration, greatly speeds shot resolution and game play.

The eighteen tables cover most WW2 European tank fronts and includes over 130 vehicles and 30 artillery types. Artillery is quick and detailed with national differences, flash/sound-ranging, time-on-target, and other rules. Air units are also well developed with 19 different air unit types.

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Chips with 12 Japanese bugs on them are hidden under 6 different location cards. This is a memory game in which players flip over the theme bug card to find the location where the bug is hiding. The insects you find can be placed in a box that serves as an insect cage.

—description from the designer

12匹の日本の虫が書かれたチップが、6種類のかくれ家カードの下に隠れています。
お題の虫カードをめくって、その虫が隠れている場所を見つけ出すメモリーゲーム。
見つけた虫は、虫かごになっている箱に入れることができます。

—description from the designer (Japanese)

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Malkavian
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Malkavian are seers and lunatics both – eerie oracles of vampire society. While plagued by afflictions of the mind, they are gifted with supernatural insight.

This deck is a toolbox that mixes bleeding and political actions, focused on dealing direct damage against enemy Methuselahs. Its vampires forsake combat skills for stealth and cunning.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Nosferatu
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Nosferatu are horrors, grotesquely deformed by the Embrace. They hide in shadows and underground darkness, commanding animals as spies and allies in battle.

This deck builds a strong defence with many animal helpers. It slowly gnaws at its prey, but can also surprise by smashing enemy vampires into torpor.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Toreador
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Toreador are artists and divas, obsessed with beauty and passion. They are the social elite among the undead, mesmerizing and awestriking to behold.

This deck tries to take political control over the game, using diversion and other tricks to pass enemy defenses. It does damage with both bleeds and political actions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Tremere
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Tremere are vampiric warlocks, masters of rituals of Blood Sorcery. Their powers make them arrogant and deadly, and they always lust for more occult lore.

This deck takes a defensive stance, taking advantage of its vampires´ psychic powers. It weakens its enemies with persistent attrition and Blood Sorcery combat skills.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Ventrue
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Ventrue are the ”blue bloods”, the self-proclaimed rulers of all vampires and mortals. They seek to lead by any means necessary – manipulating the weak, destroying the strong.

This deck is a toolbox with a stable defence both in reaction cards and combat, and a “lock and bleed” offence capable of explosive turns when the opportunity arises.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Banu Haqim
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Banu Haqim spawns from an ancient bloodline of warriors, sorcerers and assassins. Now they are pillars of law and tradition in vampiric society, although they are not immune to intrigue and political disputes.

With this deck you use a combination of stealth, threats and violence to triumph. Your vampires have both high political status and excellent combat skills.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Brujah

New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

Clan Brujah were once great philosophers and feared warriors in vampiric society. In modern nights they are known as troublemakers, always quick to violence when words are not enough.

This deck relies on a combination of political shrewdness and Anarch tactics, and the supernatural strength and speed of your vampires will deter any opposition.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: Gangrel
