These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

The feral Gangrel clan are shapeshifters and beast whisperers. Most of them roam the outskirts of society, only interfering in vampiric politics to use sharp claws to safeguard their freedom.

This deck focuses on defense and attrition to take control over the game. Your vampires know how to hinder enemy actions and escape every battle unharmed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle Fifth Edition – New Blood: The Ministry
description:
New Blood is a collection of five 55-card preconstructed decks for introductory games of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. These decks are simpler than tournament level decks, but showcase all the basic mechanics of the game. They are balanced, for a fun and exciting first try of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle. Some of the cards included are reprints and some are new for this set, all ready to mix with cards from other sets.

The Ministry is a vampire clan of temptation. They delight in sin, pushing boundaries to the extreme and use deceptive powers to draw unsuspecting victims into doing their bidding.

This mischievous deck forsakes defense for strong offense. Your vampire minions use politics, entrancement and Anarch trickery to damage your enemies, and evade getting captured by use of mind-tricks and shape-changing.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle – The Endless Dance 30th Anniversary Deck
description:
Celebrating 30 years of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle, we present a re-imagining of a classic deck archetype for the Toreador clan:


     a 100 card preconstructed deck, “The Endless Dance”
     an extra 20 cards, including new Camarilla Justicars, Kindred of renown, and key reprints


The preconstructed deck includes not just reprints, but also entirely new crypt cards and several new library cards bolstering the political aspect of the game. It is also suitable for new players looking to start with a ready-made deck that explores the dealings, negotiations, and machinations of the Eternal Struggle. The deck is ready for play out of the box or customisable with other Vampire: The Eternal Struggle cards. Each player needs a deck to play.

—description from the publisher

Step into the shadowy world of The Felonry, a place where only the brutal and cunning survive, and emerge as the undisputed ruler of the global criminal underworld.  
 Embark on a treacherous journey across Felonry Island, where every move is a fight for survival. Amass an arsenal that will make your enemies tremble, recruit expert level mercenaries to attack from the shadows, and unlock devastating powers as you sharpen your villainous skills to perfection.  
 Then, steel yourself for the ultimate showdown as you approach the Arena. Here, you’ll confront the notorious crime lord, Fenian. Defeat him, and all the players enter the fray for a final standoff, a brutal king of the hill clash where only one of you can emerge victorious.  
 Each turn begins with a player scavenging for goods by drawing from the Scavenge deck, then they roll two dice and advance across the board, followed by choosing whether to attack another player.  Combat is decided with a combination of dice rolls and using the weapons cards and futuristic gadgets they've accumulated along the way. The player with the highest total from the dice and cards wins the attack and the loser relinquishes a health token. If a player loses all 3 of their health they are sent back on the board to the most recently passed Med-Bay.  As the players race across the board they increase their skills, improve their arsenal and acquire cybernetic upgrades all with the goal of taking on the end game boss for a final upgrade advantage over the other players. The game comes to a frantic conclusion with a king of the hill battle in the arena.  The last player standing is the winner of the game and granted the title, Leader of The Felonry.

—description from the designer

Stack the cards one on top of the other with a single goal: do not exceed 100! 
Set the pace of the game with special cards that have multiple effects: reverse the direction, target your opponents, reset the pile to zero, and many more. 
But above all, stay on your toes: a double can appear at any moment, triggering the dreaded We Turn ! 
And if that's not enough for you, spice things up with two additional variants explained in the rules, or be 100 LIMIT! Invent your own!

This is a turn-based tactical card game for two players.
Move your pieces while defeating your opponent's characters, and be the first to capture the opponent's country's castle to win.
Each character can move in different directions, and has different skills and attack power.
You can also awaken characters and power them up by fulfilling certain conditions.
Develop a strategy to defeat your opponent and capture their castle.

—description from the publisher

Dama is a classic two-player strategy board game that tests player’s tactical skills and foresight. Players take turns moving their pawns, aiming to capture their opponent’s pieces by jumping over them. The ultimate goal is strategically maneuvering your pieces to the opponent’s side of the board, crowning them and enhancing their movement capabilities.

With its simple rules yet deep strategic possibilities, Dama offers hours of engaging gameplay for players of all ages.

—description from the publisher

In Sendam, big corporations and entities are trying to gain control of every part of the corrupted city. The new Sendam Recruitment Authority (SRA) was founded to solve the corruption problem. It was supposed to be the people's last hope to hire candidates fairly, but things didn't go well. The big corporations and entities rigged the system from within the SRA. Ordinary job seekers had no clue that their careers were being manipulated. All decisions were made for them to satisfy hidden agendas, which placed Sendam's political parties and politicians up in this madness.

As the SRA is a crucial authority within Sendam city, the President of the SRA is considered one of the most influential members of Parliament. He has absolute immunity from any legal inquiry while making them. The current President constantly travels and leaves the office team behind, struggling for decisions. The President is nowhere to be found after the last trip, leaving his seat in Parliament empty.

Welcome to Recruit Madness where you are a member of the SRA team. You must grow in power within the SRA by hiring your people. Whenever you hire or support the right people and understand your rivals, you will rise in power until you become the next President of the SRA and  secure your seat within the Parliament.

—description from the designer

'Afrika Korps 41' represents the campaign in North Africa during WWII. In February 1941, Hitler sends the newly formed Afrika Korps under the command of Generalmarshall Erwin Rommel to assist the Italian troops who were being defeated by the British.

It can be played at three levels of difficulty, ranging from basic to advanced. It is recommended to start with the basic level to understand the game dynamics and once mastered, incorporate new rules to increase the difficulty until reaching the advanced level, where you will see everything that the game has to offer.

It is up to you to change history, this is your moment. "A good general not only knows how to find the way to victory, but also knows when it is impossible." - Erwin Rommel

boardgame: Seers Catalog: Lyin' Werewolf Promo Card
description:

boardgame: Thunder On The Baltic: Scandinavian Wars at Sea, 1676-1814
description:
Thunder on the Baltic: Scandinavian Wars at Sea, 1676-1814 is the fifth volume in the Flying Colors series of games on naval combat. It focuses on the rivalry between the two great Baltic Sea powers, the Kingdoms of Denmark-Norway and Sweden, specifically during the Scanian War (1675-1679) and the Great Northern War (1700-1721). Also included is a module of scenarios depicting the so-called “Gunboat War” between Great Britain and Denmark during the Napoleonic Wars (1807-1814).

Like its predecessors, this volume is completely independent of—but fully compatible with—the other volumes in the series. Nothing else is needed to play! This volume comes with an updated and expanded rulebook that adds details specific to the navies included herein—particularly, the introduction rules for the antiquated (compared to the Napoleonic period) rigging and ship design of 17th century men-o-war, fireships, bomb vessels, and special rules for Danish gunboats.

There are 18 scenarios depicting battles between the fleets of Denmark against Sweden and the British Royal Navy against the Danes and Norwegians. Each battle is fought by two players (or two teams of players) across one or several conjoined maps depicting shallows and shoals with individually named vessels that have been specifically rated for their abilities in combat. Players activate these ships through the command abilities of individually rated admirals present at each battle. Players may also easily create their own scenarios with "design your own" rules and even pit these fleets against those from other volumes in the Flying Colors series. The command system provides an excellent solitaire experience as well.

Can you, as the commander of the Swedish fleet, maintain control of the crucial sea lines of communication and supply to your Baltic holdings? Or, as the commander of the Danish fleet, retain control of the Sound and ensure the security of the Capital city of Copenhagen? Now you can find out in Thunder on the Baltic!

Time scale: 5-10 minutes per turn
Map scale: 100 meters per hex
Units: Individual ships
Players: 1-4

—description form the publisher

You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.

description:
Developed in Cebu City, Philippines, Chess Infinitum is a fantasy-based variant of chess played on a 10x10 checkerboard with its corner and its horizontally adjacent neighbor squares removed totaling 92 squares including the 2x2 "Infinitum squares" on the center of both sides.

As a chess variant, similarities with the original game include the piece moves being the same, existence of draw rules like stalemate, move repetitions and number-of-move rules, and promotion of a pawn reaching the other end of the board being present; however, major differences in gameplay include both sides only have 6 pawns, the starting position of the pieces are different, and the goal of the game is now to "capture" the opponent king instead of just checkmating it.

In addition, a piece placed on their own, differently colored, 2x2 "infinitum squares" can be moved as if they are occupying all other vacant squares in the said space. If another piece of yours has occupied one of the then-vacant squares in the "infinitum square" then those pieces will instead share the power of the remaining vacant squares. If all infinitum squares are occupied by a piece then this power becomes technically useless. You and your opponent cannot utilize the opposing infinitum squares but you could use one of your pieces to block its effect by occupying a square.

A more commonly used variant of the game is by adding the "Wizard" piece. This piece is initially placed on the player's infinitum square and moves similar to a Chess queen; its special power lies on it being uncapturable by the opponents king dealing a possible game-ending capture on the next move if not captured by another piece. Since it is a special piece, any pawn that promotes on the 9th or the 10th rank of the board cannot be promoted to a Wizard.

There is a commotion in the swamp – the frogs have decided to compete for the title of the Quarol of the swamp. The contender must be cunning, dexterous, and most importantly, able to jump high and far. For other details, you can refer to the board game Quarol of the Frogs.

On your marks! Attention! Jump! The competition for the crown from the board game Quarol of the Frogs takes place on a track of multi-colored water lilies. But before starting to move, each of the frogs secretly thinks about the length of their jump – so as to land on a water lily of their own color.

—description from the publisher

Виват Квароль! На болоте царит переполох – лягушки решили побороться за звание Квароля болота. Претендент должен быть хитрым, ловким, а главное уметь высоко и далеко прыгать. За остальными подробностями можно обратиться к настольной игре Квароль лягушек.

На старт! Внимание! Прыг! Состязание за корону из настольной игры Квароль лягушек происходит на трассе из разноцветных кувшинок. Но прежде чем начать движение каждая из лягушек в тайне от других задумывает длину своего прыжка – так, чтобы приземлиться на кувшинку своего цвета.

—description from the publisher (Russian)

Unleash your ultimate pack. Outwit your rivals. Dominate the wilds.

Rival Wilds is a competitive area-control board game where cunning strategy, brutal efficiency, and animal instinct decide who rules the land. Across a shifting wilderness of contested territories, rival packs clash for control, using their unique traits, companions, and tactical positioning to overwhelm opponents and seize dominance.

There is no safe ground. No shared territory. Only power.

In Rival Wilds, players build and command a team of animals, each with specialized abilities that shape how they move, fight, gather, and control space. Every decision pushes you closer to victory - territorial dominance, resource control, tactical disruption, or calculated aggression.

To survive, you must outmaneuver, outlast, and outplay.

FxCx stands for "Fighting Couple." This card game is designed for open-minded couples who want to have a fun evening. Each party receives a set of 20 individual tasks, ranging from knowledge to estimation and actions. The goal of the game is to be the first to discard all your cards. Very funny, very provocative, very exciting, and the tension in the air is palpable.

description:
Stawn is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons (board). Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones and pawns of their own color.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Place a friendly pawn on an empty cell.
     Move a friendly pawn one or more cells in a straight line, without jumping over enemy pieces. (Jumping over friendly pieces is allowed.) After moving a pawn, place a friendly stone on the cell from which the pawn was moved or on one of the empty cells it traversed. The pawn must end its movement on an empty cell or on a cell occupied by an enemy pawn, which is then removed. Capturing an enemy pawn in this manner is only allowed if the majority of pawns adjacent to it before your move are of your color.
     Replace all stones in an enemy field with friendly stones, provided that the majority of pawns adjacent to it are of your color. A field is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto adjacent stones of its color.


Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped. When neither player can move, the player with the higher score in the final position wins. Your score is the number of stones of your color on the board, plus komi in the case of White. The button is used to avoid ties.

				
				
					Komi and buttonKomi is the whole number of points that is added to White's score at the end of the game as compensation for playing second. Before the game starts, the first player chooses the value of komi, and then the second player chooses sides. Alternatively, experienced players may agree on a standard value for all games.

The button is a special token that is placed next to the board at the start of the game. On your turn, if neither player has taken the button yet, you may take the button instead of making a board play. At the end of the game, a half point is added to the score of the player who has taken the button.

-description from designer

Those of you who came on an adventure in the ruins, found many hidden treasures. As soon as one of the members tries to keep the treasure to himself during a fallout on the mountain, a mysterious power overflows from the treasure. Someone's soul was sealed inside the treasure. The hidden treasures that lie deep within the ruins hold mysterious powers.

Outsmart everyone else and bring back lots of hidden treasures! Use your cards for points, or use their special abilities for yourself or to thwart your opponents. Play in secret teams or in a free-for-all!

There are a total of seven suits, each with seven cards, numbered 1 to 7. Every suit is equipped with a unique ability. As part of the setup, you pick five suits to play the game with. Afterwards, shuffle these five suits together to form a deck. Each player receives seven cards. Before play starts, you will pick one card and pass your hand to the person on the left. The cards picked will form your hand throughout the rest of the game.

Once everyone has drafted 7 cards, the game starts. Players will play cards and decide to use them either for points, or for their abilities. Use the cards for their point value, or use them for special abilities instead. Double your points, force your opponents to discard treasures, or perhaps protect your own treasures from your opponents. When everyone has played all their cards, the player or team with the most with the most victory points wins!

Spanish Ulcer is a wargame that covers the seven year (1808-1814) campaign of the French Army against the Spanish, Portuguese & British Armies within the Iberian Peninsula. In Match Madness, players need to use blocks to recreate a pattern shown on a card. The first player to create the pattern wins the card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Younger children can enjoy all the fun of Match Madness with the new blocks of the Junior version. The game has adapted game rules for ages 4+ with each player getting a set of 4 blocks. The game includes 48 progressive puzzles in 4 different levels of difficulty.

boardgame: シンエンをノゾクとき (Shinen Game)
description:
In this game, Shinen means a perfect circle.
A perfect circle is a symmetric circle without any distortion.

How to play the game

There are cards with various circles drawn on them, including perfect circles, ovals, and circles that are not perfectly round.

Choose the circle that is closest to a perfect circle and turn it over. Then, points will be added to the back of the card according to the shape of the circle, and the closer it is to a perfect circle, the more points you can earn. If you collect 10 points, you will win, and the first person to win two games will be the winner.

However, if you turn over a perfect circle card, there will be an eye mark on the back that is looking at you. Please note that the person who turns over this card will be temporarily eliminated from the current game...

Also, if you turn over the card that is closest to a perfect circle among the cards that are not perfect circles, there will be a closed eye mark on the back. The person who turns over this card will immediately win at that point. It is a high-risk card, but it also has a high return. Each round, players line up one card (number of players + 1) and take turns turning over or passing once, then moving on to the next round.

—description from the publisher (translated)

本ゲームにおけるシンエンとは、「深淵」ではなく、「真円」です。
・真円…ゆがみがなく、完全な円であること。また、その円
◯ゲームの遊び方
真円を含め、だ円、微妙に真円ではない円など、様々な円が描かれたカードがあります。
その中で、より真円に近い円を選んでめくって下さい。そうすると裏面に円の形に応じてポイントがあり、より真円に近いほど高いポイントを獲得できます。このポイントを10ポイント集めると勝利となり、最初に2勝した人の優勝です。
ただし、真円のカードをめくってしまうと、裏面には目のマークがあり、こちらを覗いています。このカードをめくった人は一時的にゲームから脱落となりますのでご注意下さい…
また、真円ではないカードのうち最も真円に近いカードをめくった場合、裏面に目をつぶった目のマークがあります。このカードをめくった人はその時点で即座に勝利となります。リスクは高いですが、リターンも大きいカードです。
毎ラウンド人数+1枚のカードを並べて順番に1回ずつめくるかパスするかを行い、次のラウンドへ移るというのを繰り返していくという流れのゲームです。

◯ソロプレイルールについて
ソロプレイの場合、通常と異なるルールでのプレイになります。
全てのカードを山札にした状態で、1番上にあるカードから1枚ずつ順番に見ていき、真円だと思ったらめくる、真円ではないと思ったら、めくらないというのを繰り返していきます。
山札が無くなるか、真円ではないカードをめくったら終了となり、それまでに何枚の真円のカードをめくれているかがポイントになります。最大の10ポイントを目指しましょう。

—description from the publisher

In Transcribe, players assume the role of alchemists studying the art of transmutation and mastering Alchemical Processes to complete their Magnum Opus and become the most Renown alchemist in history.

Each turn players will simultaneously activate their Transmutation Engine, chart the Astrology Map, Upgrade their Engine and boast of their discoveries.
When a player completes their Magnum Opus, the game enters its final turns. The player with the most Renown (victory points) at the end of the game is the winner.

If you are familiar with board game genres, Transcribe: the Art of Alchemy is a programmed movement roll and write.

—description from the publisher

The charming village of Cantadd must choose an "add-a-lot" expert for the upcoming Mathematics Olympics.
But what should be a joyful celebration in honor of intelligence and numbers has not attracted the villagers...
However, this changes when they offer the honor of taking care of 'Horse,' the village mascot, to the winner.
Will you be able to survive this ruthless competition among neighbors and prove that no one is better than you at taking care of Horse?

MADZEMATICZ! is a strategic competitive card game for 2 players.
In it, each player competes to match a target number before their opponen. The first person to achieve it wins the game.
To do that, they must play "add" or "substract" cards on their own row (to try and win) or their opponents (to hinder their strategy). And don't forget to use the special cards that can change the tide of the game at any moment.

—description from the publisher

Chaque année, dans les terres de Cumbria, se déroule le grand concours Merl'Inn. La taverne ayant la meilleure réputation sera récompensée par la prestigieuse Chope d'Or! Serez-vous capable de remporter la chope cette année?

Comparez votre taverne avec celles des autres, envoyez votre aventurier accomplir des quêtes et gagner des points. Les points sont obtenus de différentes manières: cartes achetées, points obtenus lors des quêtes, et mobilier dans votre taverne. À la fin du jeu, le joueur avec le plus de points gagne.

Développez votre taverne avec construction de deck, placement de personnages, interactions entre joueurs, et asymétrie. Grande rejouabilité.

Le jeu a été en financement participatif en février 2026 sur Ulule.

Vous souhaitez acheter le jeu ? Toutes les infos ici:  https://www.merlinn.fr


Every year in the lands of Cumbria, the grand Merl'Inn competition is held. The tavern with the best reputation will be awarded the prestigious Golden Tankard! Will you be able to win the Tankard this year?

Compare your tavern with others, send your adventurer to complete quests and earn points. Points are earned through purchased cards, quest rewards, and furniture in your tavern. At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

Develop your tavern with deckbuilding, character placement, player interactions, and asymmetry. High replayability.

The game will launch on crowdfunding in early 2026 on Ulule. It is initially planned to only be in French.

Reporter is an investigative game designed to immerse players in the thrilling world of journalism. It puts the players in the shoes of journalists and focuses in particular on the verification work necessary before publishing information: where does it come from, is it reliable, and sufficiently verified via cross-sources? It would be unthinkable for a good journalist to relay fake news! The game offers two distinct scenarios.

The first scenario, lasting 45 minutes, serves as an introduction and allows players to familiarize themselves with the basics of journalism. A strange photo is causing a buzz on social networks: there is a monster in Lake Geneva! Is it a hoax, a statue or even a disguise? Only the clever use of your investigative tools will lead you to the truth, which may surprise you!

The second scenario, lasting approximately 3 hours, deepens the skills acquired by introducing a more complex and elaborate plot. You are sent to the shores of Lake Constance to produce a report on the theme of the border. During your stay, you get informed of a large quantity of debris on a beach not far away. Given the weather, the hypotheses are quite limited, but as you investigate, you find yourself immersed in an immersive story taking you around the lake.

Designed to be accessible without preparation or prior knowledge, Reporter is ideal for a family or educational experience, but also for playing with friends. This unique experience combines entertainment, learning and awareness of the challenges of modern journalism, while offering a captivating adventure through Switzerland and beyond.

—description from the publisher

Depending on the dice result, one player has to act (pantomime) or to describe or to paint a phrase selected from five open sided cards on the table. The other players have to find out what that should be. The one, who first  manage to do so, gets the card. The first person to collect six cards wins the game.

Eden: The New World is a miniatures game for 2-4 players that immerses you in a dark and mature post-apocalyptic universe. It is a game of deep tactical richness that combines a strong requirement in the placement of your fighters and the multiple combinatorial possibilities of combat systems and special abilities. Thanks to a unique and original movement system and the resources you will have to collect on the field, the game is not only fast (one hour per game on average) but also particularly immersive.

—description from the publisher

Idols TCG is a strategic card game that pits players against each other in an epic battle of summoning creatures, casting spells, and equipping powerful items. At the heart of each player's deck is their chosen Idol, a powerful entity that serves as both the cornerstone of their strategy and their ultimate win condition.
Players take turns drawing power cards, summoning creatures to the field, and enhancing their forces through leveling up and equipping items. The game's dynamic combat system allows for both offensive strikes and defensive maneuvers, with each action requiring careful consideration of resource management and tactical positioning.
The ultimate goal of Idols TCG is to defeat your opponent's Idol while protecting your own. This is achieved through a combination of strategic summoning, clever use of spells and abilities, and calculated attacks. With its blend of resource management, tactical combat, and the ever-present threat to your Idol, Idols TCG offers a deep and engaging experience for fans of competitive card games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: The Final Act – Murder at the Talent Show

Calling all amateur sleuths and private investigators! Can you crack the case and bring a killer to justice?

Ventriloquist magician, Paul Zechman, has died during an unauthorized rehearsal gone terribly wrong. The authorities call it an accident but Paul's wife, Leslie, suspects foul play. Now she needs your help to prove it and find Paul’s killer.

In this detective game, you'll work together or alone to uncover the truth about what really happened the day Paul died. Along the way, you'll investigate suspects, piece together clues, and break a few codes to unravel the mystery.

Rated 3/5 for difficulty and appropriate for ages 14 and up, this board game delivers 90-120 minutes of gameplay for one or more players.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Night's Watch Attachments 1

In their eternal vigil over the Wall, the Night's Watch has battled terrors and barbarians from the north lands since the Wall's founding. Their arsenal includes solid steel, hardened leather, and pure grit. None are more deadened to the horrors of war than the veterans that lead their junior Brothers in Black. These grizzled warriors have seen it all, and can keep their troops alive through even the fiercest fighting. Whether it's against Wildlings and their inhuman troops or regular military from the south, the Night's Watch stands a strong as the Wall they defend.
