Pitfall is a 3-8 player deck-building game in which players compete in a series of short combats, collecting treasure, forging and backstabbing alliances, and making strategic decisions to spend or save their power, all with the ultimate purpose of building the most powerful deck possible before entering the final combat: Pitfall. The winner of the Pitfall wins the game. With 400 cards, Pitfall contains a Rich tapestry of possibilities, and no two games will ever be the same!

—description from the designer

Do You Know Your Peeps? is a fun trivia game filled with amusing questions for all ages that will challenge you, your friends, and your family to see if you really know each other. One player reads a question and three possible answers out loud, then all other players lock their answers and the reader tries to guess everybody's response. With five categories (Favorites, School, Magic, Hobbies and Personality), and 200 questions, this game is packed with fun, covering everyday life...and unexpected situations!

HOW TO PLAY: One player is the Guesser each turn, they pick a Question Card from any category and read the question and 3 possible answers out loud. Then all other players lock their answer (A, B, or C) by placing their Answer Card face-down in front of them. In any order, the Guesser tries to guess everybody’s answer. If they guess more than half the answers correctly, they win the Question Card as a point. The goal is to be the first player to collect 1 Question Card from each category.

INCLUDES: 200 Question Cards, 27 Answer Cards (A, B, C), Illustrated Rules.

-description from publisher

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Suicide Squad
description:
For noble missions, there’s the Justice League, the Justice Society of America, or other heroic groups. For those shady missions the government doesn’t want people to know about, Amanda Waller gathered the most useful villains she was able to find in prison, stuck a nano-bomb in their head, and set them off on their task with the promise of a commuted sentence. If they survive. Odds of that are not great, given the team was dubbed the Suicide Squad. But hey, only the good die young!

This expansion brings a group of Villains with heroic tendencies. Or perhaps it’s a group of Heroes with villainous tendencies. Whatever the case may be, all members of the Suicide Squad can be played as either a Hero or a Villain! As Villains, they pair together to give Heroes a real fight. But they’re at their most fearsome when the whole Suicide Squad is put together to complete their nefarious mission. As Heroes, the members of the Suicide Squad live up to their moniker, always ready to roll with the punches with abilities that trigger when they are KO’d. Just make sure to keep your head about you if add the Nano-Bombs Challenge to your game!

The Crowdfunding Exclusive Suicide Squad expansion comes with:

1 Harley Quinn Figure
    1 Bloodsport Figure
    1 Peacemaker figure
    1 Polka Dot Man Figure
    1 King Shark Figure
    1 Rick Flag Figure
    12 Harley Quinn Hero Cards
    12 Bloodsport Hero Cards
    12 Peacemaker Hero Cards
    12 Polka Dot Man Hero Cards
    12 King Shark Hero Cards
    12 Rick Flag Hero Cards
    2 Equipment Cards
    1 Harley Quinn Villain Dashboard
    6 Harley Quinn Master Plan Cards
    3 Harley Quinn Threat Cards
    1 Bloodsport Villain Dashboard
    6 Bloodsport Master Plan Cards
    3 Bloodsport Threat Cards
    1 Peacemaker Villain Dashboard
    6 Peacemaker Master Plan Cards
    3 Peacemaker Threat Cards
    1 Polka Dot Man Villain Dashboard
    6 Polka Dot Man Master Plan Cards
    3 Polka Dot Man Threat Cards
    1 King Shark Villain Dashboard
    6 King Shark Master Plan Cards
    3 King Shark Threat Cards
    1 Rick Flag Villain Dashboard
    6 Rick Flag Master Plan Cards
    3 Rick Flag Threat Cards
    1 Suicide Squad Dashboard
    12 Suicide Squad Master Plan Cards
    6 Suicide Squad Threat Cards
    3 Mission Cards
    6 Weak Spot Cards
    4 Locations
    1 Challenge Card
    5 Nano-Bomb Cards
    7 Team Cards

Fast paced leveling up card game. Turn your secret identities into their Superhero or Villain counterparts as you race your opponents to the finish line on the BATTLEGROUND.

The Battleground is made of two rows with seven zones within them. The top zones are where you'll set down your protagonists, starting on the Neutral Zone and it's up to you to get them all the way to the last zone, The Nemesis Zone, defeat your opponent's Nemesis and win the game. The bottom row is for your deck, your HQ (discard pile) and five zones for your "Fate" cards.

Every turn you can advance each of your characters one zone and battle your within reach opponents to cripple their progress. In ExCowl, losing a fight isn't the end. When a character loses a battle you must invert the card with the number at the bottom now at the top acting like their new "strength."

Level up your protagonists and lead them to success or die trying!

—description from the designer

The Polish Campaign of 1939 begins when the German Army attacks Polish territory in September 1939. Also known as Operation Fall Weiss, it triggered World War II in Europe and ended the Second Polish Republic.

Poland'39 is a game for two or three players, one of them leads the Polish Army and the rest leads the German and/or Russian Armies, depending on whether they are two or three players.


                                
                            
                        
                    
                
            
        
    


La Campaña de Polonia de 1939 comienza cuando el Ejército alemán ataca el territorio polaco en septiembre de 1939. Conocida también como Operación Fall Weiss, fue el detonante de la II Guerra Mundial en Europa y acabó con la II República Polaca.
Poland’39 es un juego para dos o tres jugadores, uno de ellos lleva al Ejército polaco y el resto lleva a los Ejércitos Alemán y/o ruso, según sean dos o tres jugadores.

-description from designer

Horsing around is fun!

In Munchkin Pony Excess, you'll find new equine monsters, curses, and Items, plus a herd of new Steeds. Grab your Lucky Horseshoe, saddle up your Centaur of Attention, and ride to battle against the melodious menace of the Rocking Horse!

Shadow and Rune immerses 1-4 players in a deck-building struggle for power within a world shrouded in darkness for half a millennium. Here, an affliction known as shadow sickness ravages the mind and body, a dire consequence of endless nights. Stripped of memories and purpose, players find themselves teetering on the edge of total oblivion.

The game is versatile, allowing for quick competitive matches or longer adventures through the persistent campaign. You can play solo or cooperatively, making it perfect for both short and extended sessions.

Your decisions shape the world, immediately or many hours later in your playthrough. The narrative unfolds through journal entries, exploring your decisions, surroundings, companions, environments, and their impact on your psyche and evolving personality.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Alchemagica, a tableau-based trial of spellbinding strategy! Alchemagica is a competitive, light/medium-weight, card-driven tableau builder designed and illustrated by Alex Cheng at STUDIO KEINGÅ.

Congratulations on advancing to the final trial of your Alchemagical Apprenticeship - for your last test, you must prove your unparalleled mastery of the Alchemagical Arts. Build your Spellbook, cast powerful Spells, and wield versatile Transmutations to claim victory: power and glory await the one who rises above their Rivals to claim the title of Master Alchemagus!

Combine Element cards to craft powerful Spells for your steadily cycling Spellbook. Your double-function cards flip every turn to yield new Spell combinations for constantly shifting strategy. Your Spells focus on four key actions:

     GET Reagents to spend on cards or save for score
     BUY new Element and Transmutation Cards from the Lab to build your power
     USE your Reagents to enact powerful effects
     SAVE your Reagents for scoring


Balance your limited actions and resources between amassing might and saving for score as you race against your Rivals to prove your mastery of the Alchemagical Arts and emerge victorious!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Boone, Arcade and Cass
description:
Boone is an ex-NCR 1st Recon Sniper and town guard in Novac. His exceptional aim and hatred for the Legion make him a ruthless ally in the Mojave Wasteland.

Arcade on the other hand is more likely to use his mind as his primary weapon. Quick witted and intelligent, he has an independent streak despite his Enclave origins; even so, he’s still handy with a weapon when he needs to be for the right cause.

Cass is more in tune with the wasteland as it is and less how she’d like it to be. She’s a tough, no nonsense caravan runner looking to claw her way back from sad times and a run of bad luck, any way she can.

Contents:

1 x Boone
1 x Arcade
1 x Cass
3 Scenic bases

Château Gardens is a polyomino-drawing game where players compete to draw the optimal arrangement of plants and viewing platforms around a French Château. Players draw cards from a standard deck of playing card to learn what size, shape, and/or location of polyomino they must draw in their garden, or what noble's viewing platform they must add to combo off their existing elements. Make sure you always have room to add the next element revealed from the deck, or use your suppléant card to replace it for all players once per game. You may bow out at any time you think your garden is full, or press your luck just a bit more to see if you can eke out the most points! Once all players have elected to stop adding to their garden, add up your points and determine the winner.

-description from designer

In Crash the Party, everyone's invited to a party, but someone arrived expecting a different event than the others. Can you catch the Crasher?

Overview: 
Each round, players will draw an Event card and place it in the Decoder Box to see what party they’re at. The Decoder Box will secretly reveal the Event to all but one player. That player will see an entirely different Event when they look in the Decoder!

Each player is dealt eight Object cards. Going clockwise around the table, each player reveals one Object that they are bringing to the party and explains why they’ve brought it. Players then go around the table once more, revealing a second Object card.

If you think YOU are the Party Crasher, try to blend in! You’ll get bonus points if no one else finds you.

If you think you’re a normal Guest, try to find the Crasher! You’ll get bonus points if other Guests find the Crasher as well, so be sure to speak up!

After all Objects have been played, it’s time to find the Crasher! On the count of three, everyone points at who they think the Party Crasher is. Each player then says what Event they are at, which will reveal the Crasher.

The Party Crasher scores 2 points if they pointed at themselves, and an additional point if no one else voted for them. The Guests get 2 points if they identified the Crasher, and an additional point if at least one other Guest pointed at the Crasher as well.

Reset and start the next party round. The first player to reach 13 points wins!

-description from publisher

Escape villains and battle dragons in the world’s first graphic novel escape room! For kids and families who like thrills and comedy.


     Enter The Story: First follow the hilarious story in a beautiful graphic novel and video; then enter it yourself by solving puzzles to move the story along.



     Created by Award-Winning Screenwriters: Experience a hilarious adventure designed by Webby and Telly Award-Winning TV comedy writers.



     Screen And Screen-Free Options: Read the graphic novel or watch the semi-animated audiovisual experience, pausing to play the games and crack the puzzles.



     Complete Box for Epic Fun: The Starlings Box One includes an 80-page graphic novel, 6 immersive escape room puzzle games, a magnetic Starlings Cube, collectible cards, and a 40-minute digital audiovisual experience. 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Command of Nature: Solemn Swiper Convention Promo Card
description:
2024 Convention promo card:

Solemn Swiper
Elemental Warrior (Leaf)
STR 4 HP 4
ROW I
When this Elemental defeats an Elemental in your opponent's Row I, add 1 boost to up to 2 Elementals in your Row II
COST 5

Chromatic Decoder invites players into a vibrant and strategic world of color and deduction. Designed for 2-6 players and suitable for ages 9 and up, this game challenges participants to guess the secret color sequence created by the Code Maker. Each round, the Code Maker arranges a series of colorful cards and provides subtle hints, while the Code Breakers must use their logic and deduction skills to decode the sequence before time runs out.

Setup and Play:
Players begin by choosing between Standard Mode, for quicker games and beginners, or Advanced Mode, for a more challenging experience. The Code Maker selects a sequence of color cards and records it on a reference sheet. Code Breakers then take turns guessing the sequence, placing their own color cards on numbered tiles. The Code Maker provides feedback with tokens to indicate correct colors and positions, adding layers of strategy and tension to each turn. Special variation cards introduce unique rules and twists, ensuring no two games are ever the same.

Objective and Winning:
The goal of Chromatic Decoder is for Code Breakers to accurately guess the Code Maker’s sequence within a set time limit. Points are awarded based on the number of guesses needed, with penalties for incorrect guesses and bonuses for swift decoding. The game continues until all players have had a turn as the Code Maker, and the player with the highest score at the end is declared the winner. In case of a tie, a thrilling speed round determines the ultimate champion.

Chromatic Decoder combines educational elements with engaging gameplay, making it a perfect addition to family game nights or gatherings with friends. Its blend of strategy, colorful components, and dynamic rules ensures that it captures the imaginations of players young and old.

—description from the designer

Have you ever watched a movie? Yes? Then this game is for you!

With family or friends, get together to shout, mime or quote passages from popular films. From Elvis Gratton to 101 Dalmatians via Bon Cop, Bad Cop, revisit cinema classics like you've never seen them before!

Maybe you know the answer, maybe you don’t, or maybe your teammate does. Don’t worry we give you all the answers, the only problem is only Half of them are the Truth. This trivia party game will have you Second Guessing yourself, as you use your knowledge, intuition, and luck to win! Half Truth: Second Guess is compatible with the original Half Truth.

Each round, you'll get a question with 6 possible answers: three correct, three incorrect. More answers can earn you more points, but giving ANY incorrect answers scores you nothing.

-description from publisher

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Team Decks
description:
DC Super Heroes United is all about teamwork, and Heroes are known for forming specialized teams from time to time. The Justice League, Gotham Knights, Teen Titans, to name but a few. Each of these teams develops its own characteristics and strategies, though being part of a structured team also comes with limitations and drawbacks.

This expansion brings a set of 10 different Team decks, each composed of 6 Team cards and 1 Team Members Reference card, for a total of 70 cards!

When adding a Team Deck to your game, all Heroes being used must be part of the same Team, and the Hard Challenge difficulty level should be used. That Team deck is shuffled and placed facedown within view of all players, with 2 cards revealed to form the Team Display. These are essentially Hero cards, with Action symbols at the bottom and a Special Effect. On their turn, a Hero can choose to either play a regular turn or they can choose to play a Team turn. In that case they don’t touch their Hero cards (neither drawing nor playing) but choose instead one of the Team cards in the Display and play that card in the Storyline, as if it were their own card. Immediately a new card is drawn from the deck to fill the Team Display (until the deck runs out). This not only brings a different flavor to the match, but also gives Heroes more options of what to do on their turn. However, there is a downside to playing as a Team. Most Team decks have at least one card with a mandatory negative effect. This is in effect as long as that card is faceup on the Team Display. In order to shut it down, a Hero must take one for the team by taking a Team turn, choosing the negative card, and playing it facedown in the Storyline.

Full list of teams:
Justice League
Justice League America
Batman Inc
Birds of Prey
Teen Titans
Outsiders
Gotham Knights
Super Friends
Superman Family
United Heroes

-description from Gamefound campaign

Three different coloured clay pieces (the suits for the game) are torn into several pieces.  Each player is given a piece of each color, and then tears their piece into smaller pieces (if they wish),  and the size of the pieces determines the results of trick taking.

The game comes with three pieces of clay and a small scale with which to weigh pieces if there is not a clear winner in a trick.

boardgame: An Army at Dawn: Big Red One
description:
In two World Wars, the United States Army’s First Infantry Division - the “Big Red One” - led American forces into combat. The division was in the first wave of landings in North Africa in November 1942, and fought the Axis in Tunisia from January to May 1943. The division’s reputation brought it into the thick of the fighting in North Africa, as it would for the remainder of the war in Europe.

Big Red One is a Panzer Grenadier Campaign Study, with 11 new scenarios based on the actions of the American 1st Infantry Division during the Tunisian campaign. There are three chapters, each with a battle game to link the scenarios together. You’ll need An Army at Dawn (and only An Army at Dawn) to play all of the scenarios.

-description from publisher's website

As a newly hired park ranger at Pinewood National Park, your role is to preserve the parks natural beauty. Your primary tasks include: (1) educating visitors on responsible camping practices, (2) overseeing the Fire Tower Expansion project, and (3) analyzing the effectiveness of the Animal Diversity & Breeding program.

Park Ranger, a solo 18 card game by Logical Fox, demands strategic card placement. Each turn, you play one of the two cards from your hand into the playing area, always adjacent to an existing card. With each card placement, you must balance the park's environmental, structural and recreational needs. At the end of each season, your boss will evaluate your performance and may even promote you. Do you have what it takes to climb the career ladder?

—description from the publisher

Each player is dealt 3 Neon tokens.
These tokens can be used to increase the electricity cost of your energize action to gain more achievement tokens.
Three new contracts to be added to the game, focused on progressing on the income tracks.

In the dim, dank depths of a forgotten dungeon, two rogues awaited their grim fate. Shadows clung to the cold stone walls as the pair pndered their impending execution.

Suddenly, the silence was shattered by an earth-shaking crsh. A massive, mighty boar burst through the heavy iron door, sending splinters and dust flying. The best rampaged through the cell, its frenzied eyes darting around as it searched for something, nearly trampling the prisoners in its wild quest.

After a chaotic scramble, the boar's snout finally found its prize: a cluster of salty mushrooms wedged between the floor bricks. With a triumphant grunt, the boar devoured the fleshy fungi and, just as abruptly as it has appeared, bolted out of the dungeon, leaving the door ajar. Seizing the unexpected opportunity, the captives saw their chance to escape!

Bastion run is a two-player co-op game.
But solo mode is just as fun and challenging!
Player can control both characters one by one.

Heroes find the keys and stealthily navigate past the guards, moving to the exit across multiple dungeon levels, aiming to reach the final exit door for escape.

To maintain stealth mode, heroes must move tile by tile in a specific order determined by the dice they selected from the dice pool. The tiles come in three types: light, grey, and dark.

Guards can notice prioners and move towards them when a noise is made. Noise occurs whenever a prisoner moves a tile, ithout consideration of the die used for the step.

Our heroes are not just regular prioners: they know some sneaky tricks. Players can combine dice to add or subtract results, providing more movement options.

On their way to freedom, heroes can pick stinky toads to restore their stealth level...
... and salty mushrooms to draw ability cards that provide some useful abilities.

Remember the boar? It's also obsessed with mushrooms and will run around the dungeon trying to grab them all before the players can.

—description from the designer

Sagascade is the chess game of our medieval fantasy universe, 3rd Dawn! A fast-paced, original chess game for 2 to 4 players.

Can you master the different movements of the stones to see them align? Can you win 4 different stones before your opponent?

Your reserve is made up of pieces of different colours and symbols. Each of the 4 symbols has a particular effect on the other pieces already on the board. Each player places a piece and applies its effect until a line-up of 3 pieces of the same symbol or colour has been created. A line-up earns one point for the player who caused it. The game ends when a player has obtained 4 points with 4 different colours and 4 different symbols. The game lasts about 20 minutes.

This is a thinking game, where the key is to plan several moves in advance and anticipate your opponent's actions to score points!

—description from the publisher (translated)

You are a colour guardian of the Kingdom of Colours. There's a big party coming up and a lot of colours are expected! There's likely to be an attendance record, and the wait to get in is sure to be explosive! 
You're part of the team stationed at the entrance. You'll need to be careful, because when two identical colours are next to each other, they explode and can cause a chain reaction! Arrange the colours skilfully so that the party doesn't turn into a fiasco before it even starts!

Set Up :
1) Shuffle all the Pop cards and place 5 of them in a line, face up.
There can’t be two identical colours next to each other.
2)Setting up your hand
Shuffle the remaining Pop cards and deal 9 cards to each player to form their hand.

4) Setting up the draw pile
Place the remaining Pop cards face down

5)Setting up the Multicolour Chief card

In a 2- to 4-player game, place the Multicolour Chief card next to the draw pile. It counts as a player.

A game is composed of 3 rounds, each containing several turns that progress as follows:

1) Select Pop cards
Each player chooses 1 Pop card from their hand and places it face down in front of them.
2) Reveal the Pop cards
All players reveal their card simultaneously.

3) Place the Pop cards and check the explosions
The player who played the Pop card that triggered the explosion collects all the exploding Pop cards and places them in front of them.

Different game mode : 1 player, 2 to 5 players with cooperative and competitive modes

—description from the publisher

Place cards in the playing area one after the other. As soon as a player exceeds the element limit, they collect all played cards, give some of them to one or several other players, and keep the remaining cards for themselves. At the end of the game, the cards you collected make you earn or lose points.
The player who has the most points at the end of the game wins!


     Set the round card as “ROUND 1”.
     All players start the game with 5 cards.
     In 90 game cards, 72 Treasure cards and 18 special cards.In Treasure cards, each card consists of a “color” & “object”. There are 6 colors and 6 objects. Each color, each object will be counted as one “element”. For example, when the first player plays “red vinyl”, it has 2 elements. (one color(red) and one object(vinyl))
     From the first player, players play a card. The game goes clockwise.
     In each round, there’s the “ELEMENT LIMIT” and “PRESENT NUMBER”. While playing the game, after a player’s turn, if the "PRESENT NUMBER" exceeds the “ELEMENT LIMIT”, the round ends. The player gives “PRESENTS” among the played cards to other players as the number written in round card.  And then, the player gets all the remained cards.
     After the final round, the game ends.
     The player who has the most point wins the game.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: すりすりあんこう (Sliced Anglerfish)
description:
An auction game where the players take turns using their fingers to poke a stuffed fish to make determinations about what organs are inside of it.

Players are each given 30 gold worth of currency with which to bid.  Each round players take turns being the auctioneer, or the fish mongers trying to buy the best organs.  The auctioneer draws a card depicting what organs to place inside the fish.  The auctioneer does this secretly, out of sight of the bidders.  Next, the remaining players go around the table and have 15 seconds to poke the fish with their pointer fingers to make a guess as to what is inside before they bid.  The winning bidder gets the card depicting what was inside, which will be worth victory points at the end of the game.  The player with the most points (leftover gold counts for 1 point each) at the end of all of the auctions will be the winner.

The game comes with a stuffed animal fish, and sets of different organs (eggs, liver, etc) to place inside for the auctions.
publishers: るりるりゲームズ (ruri ruri games)

description: A spectacular rallyrace has begun. Who finishes the track three times First ? Press the hooter and take advantage of the dice of your opponent. But beware … ghosts Can sent you back to the start. Win with tactics and a bit of luck !

Trick-taking using music sheets. Match the rhythm of another player and earn points by playing with other players, not just by yourself. You get 1 point for getting a trick, but 3 points for playing with other players.

Players are dealt a hand of cards with rhythms on them.  The lead player will play to one of three different clefs which each have a different trick winning requirement.  In addition to trying to win tricks, players may find more success in playing to synchronize with another player to gain additional points for collaborating.
publishers: るりるりゲームズ (ruri ruri games)

description:
It is the year 2049 and mankind has reached the point where space travel to the moon and Mars is commonplace.  The next frontier is the asteroid belt.  Enter Vesta, one of the largest asteroids in the main asteroid belt, and surveys show it is loaded with Helium-3 (valuable as fuel for the latest fusion reactors) and various valuable rare-earth minerals. So, like the 1849 California gold rush, the rush is on to mine these valuable resources from Vesta.

Vesta is a cooperative game where players will work together to mine resources from one of several available mines and use them to build their base, use in their fusion reactors, or sell.  The mines consist of layers of tiles representing five different types of resources.  Players mine these minerals by removing pairs of matching tiles, except when using mining tools that allow them to break the normal mining rules.  Then regolith, rock, or metals are used to build up their bases and Helium-3 is used in your reactors for energy.  Building bases involves stacking tiles from the mine. Each type of material has special rules for how they can be stacked or flipped in your base. But watch out because there are asteroid pirates hidden in the mine who also want the valuable minerals, and they will attack you and cause damage to your base when revealed.  You can fight them off if you spend some of your earnings on weapons or force fields.

Players also get to play as characters who each have special abilities such as Gunther Hammer a mining specialist or Biff Parsec, a Space Ranger who is very handy at fighting off pirates.

To win, the players must build ten fusion reactors.  The players will lose if the mine becomes depleted or locked up where the players don't have the mining tools to break things loose.  The players can also lose if they build their base poorly and can't build anymore or if the pirates do too much damage to their bases.

-description from designer
A ten-card expansion adding seven more kittens, two actions, and one sushi to the deck.
Adds ten new cards to the deck, allowing for a fifth and sixth player. Includes three new targets, two weapons, one mayhem card, two money cards, and one lollipop.

An epic strategic wargame with magnetized mechs and tanks placed in Germany:

You start your campaign with a little troop of elite soldiers and select their traits and equipment. Then you arrive at the enemies territory and start a campaign against their massively superior forces. During multiple missions you setup your units and activate them one after another, and fight alone, or together with a friend against the enemies. Movement and attacking is grid based. Hit, crit, damage, and ammo checks are done with a single roll with a unique die system. The missions has various end conditions, from objectives like stealing your first mech, to sabotage critical infrastructure of the enemy and others. During the missions you also steal enemy equipment and units, and you try to use those resources during the campaign to finally survive and defeat the enemy.

-description from designer

The starting signal has been given for the craziest race you'll have on your table! Classic cars, revered in songs and honored in great movies, compete against each other in the grand Formula POP. Each player chooses a car, takes their cards, and the race begins. In each turn, players choose a card from their car's deck and reveal them simultaneously. Duplicate cards collide and are not activated, while the other cards are. Plan your strategy well, as each card has a special effect. Along the way, you may also find opportunities to perform risky maneuvers that can make all the difference. The turns repeat until a player crosses the finish line and becomes the winner.

In Formula POP, players play cards simultaneously, using identical decks. If players play the same cards, they collide and are not activated, making reading the table essential. When there is no collision, players move their cars along the track and apply the card effects. Additionally, players can obtain maneuver cards, which can be used along with car cards to grant temporary special abilities. The race track is modular and can be set up differently each game.

The first player to cross the finish line wins!

A game about an apartment landlord trying to get the most respectable tenants. Players take turns drawing cards from the deck and playing tenant cards (worth either positive or negative points) to their "floor" (a 3x2 grid) or playing action cards to sabotage other players or move negative tenants to someone else's floor. The game ends when one player has filled the six spaces on their floor and players score the value of their tenants, with the highest total winning.

Travel 20 Questions is the exciting game of knowledge, skill and luck that everyone can play. It's great fun for family and friends.

Each card contains twenty clues that describe the mystery identity of a well known person, place or thing. Reveal the clues one at a time, then try to guess the mystery identity-if you guess right, you win!

Based on the top-selling board game, Travel 20 Questions is specially created for fun on-the-go. It's perfect for travel, vacations, and parties.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Goal Zone Board Game is a soccer board game where players take turns rolling two standard white dice to control player and ball movements.  The offensive player or attacker uses moves from throwing two white dice in order to dribble, pass, or move other attacking game pieces into strategically advantageous squares to prepare to receive a pass or to shoot on the goal.  When the offensive player’s turn is over the defensive player rolls the two white dice and moves any of the defensive game pieces toward the attacker who has possession of the ball.  The defensive player “closes down” on the ball by moving a defensive game piece into the attacker's square who has the ball.  Next, the defender declares a “challenge” for possession and the two players use red dice to determine who wins the challenge for possession of the ball.  Whichever player rolls a higher number on the red challenge die wins the challenge.  The winner of the challenge then rolls the two white dice and moves away from the challenging defender by passing, dribbling or a combination of the two.  If the two players roll a tie on the red challenge die, then the defender or challenging player gets a “yellow card” and possession goes to the offensive player in possession of the ball.

Shots on the goal can only be taken from the red goal zone squares.  Each horizontal red goal zone square has a number from 1 to 6.  This means that the offensive player shooting at the goal must have that many moves remaining on their dice if they want to shoot from the distance indicated on the board.  For example, to shoot from the #6 line, the offensive player must have at least 6 moves left on their turn to be allowed to shoot at the goal.  When the offensive player declares “I’m taking a shot on goal,” each player rolls a red challenge die. If the offensive player rolls a higher number, it’s a goal. If it is a tie or the defensive player (goalie) rolls a higher number, then the goalie takes possession.

To win the game the first player to score 1, 2 or 3 goals wins depending on how much time you have to play the game.

-description from designer

PARTY ON WITH THE CARE BEARS: Enjoy this entertaining CareBears board game with friends and family as you journey across this vintage CareBears themed board game that's packed with imagery and inspired moments.

FINISHING THE GAME
In order to determine the winner, the first 2 players to reach the Finish square on the gameboard compete in a final Care Contest. This Care Contest will be the best 2 out of 3. Each player will take turns rolling the die; the highest roll wins the round. Any ties go to the first player to reach the Finish square. Any Care Bears cards collected throughout the game can not be used to affect the outcome. Good luck and have fun!

5 card games in one! High score wins in basketball, baseball, and football; low score wins in golf; first one with numbers covered consecutively wins LINE-O.

BASKETBALL

Players are dealt 5 cards and lay them face down in an X pattern with 1 card in the middle and the other 4 at the corners of it. Score using numbers on outside corners of cards. Each player may now look at the two cards closest to them. The cards will be replaced face down and must be remembered as the player is not allowed to look at them again. The other three cards cannot be seen at all. The top two cards may be replaced, but must be replaced sight unseen. The middle card cannot be replaced and remains as a mystery card sight unseen until the end of play. The player to the left of the dealer now begins play. He may select the card turned face up from the deck or select one from the top of the deck. If the number of the card is higher than either card seen, the player may replace it, or the player may choose to replace one of the top unseen cards. Remember, the player must change the top two cards without looking at them. When a top card is replaced it might be a higher card than the one used to replace it. However, the card replaced must be put on the pile face up in the middle of the table. If the card selected from the top of the deck is not higher the player may discard the selected card without replacing any of his cards. The player then puts the card replaced face up on the pile in the center of the table. The next player may now either take the card discarded by the previous player or take a card from the top of the pile. Each player then plays in similar manner as the previous player and so on around the table. Playing continues around the table and any player may change a card as often as desired. The center card cannot be replaced and remains unseen until the end of play. When a player feels that.he may have the highest score totalling up the 4 cards plus the mystery card in the center, he may call out "BASKET" Each player after him will then have one last turn including the dealer. Playing will then stop, (this gives everyone playing an equal chance) Should the dealer call "BASKET" playing will stop immediately as all will have had equal chances of play. If the player that calls "BASKET" has the highest number of points he will receive his total points PLUS an additional 6 points. Should several players have the same score as the one calling "BASKET", the one who calls will still receive the extra 6 points extra because he took the risk If another player has a higher score than the one calling "BASKET", the player who called "BASKET" will lose all the points on his 5 cards minus 6 points as a penalty. All other players will receive the total of the points on their 5 cards.

BASEBALL

Players are dealt 4 cards and lay them face down in a square pattern. As in the game of basketball, the 2 cards nearest the player may be seen only once. The top two cards cannot be seen at all. Play is then started and the game is played in the same manner as basketball. The difference - All cards may be replaced. In the basketball game the center card may not be replaced. Score using numbers inside baseball diamond. Baseball When a player believes the total score of his 4 cards is the highest he will call "HOME RUN". Play will continue through the dealer. (If the dealer calls, "HOME RUN" play will cease immediately. If the player's 4 card score is highest, the player will receive total of his score plus 4 points. If another player has a higher score, the one calling "HOME RUN" will lose all his points and minus 4 in addition. After the 4 cards are totaled, the total will be divided by 4.

FOOTBALL

Players are dealt 4 cards and lay them face down in a square pattern. Score using numbers inside Football Field - Rectangle. Game is played the same as Baseball only the person with the highest score calls "TOUCHDOWN". If player has the highest score on 4 cards or ties with another player, the player calling "TOUCHDOWN" will receive the total score plus 6 points. If the player does not have the highest score he will lose all his points minus 6 more. Bonus cards add 1 touchdown or 6 points. Total score of all cards in a player's hand will be counted as the score of the player's 4 cards 6 3 5 2 = 16. 16 points will be put in score box unless one of the cards is a Bonus card. Then he would have 16 + 6 = 22.

GOLF

Players are dealt 4 cards and lay them face down in a square pattern. Score using numbers inside the circle. Played the same as baseball and football. As opposed to other sports - golf is won by having the lowest score. When a player feels that he has the lowest score on his cards he will call out "HOLE IN ONE" Play continues to dealer and ends with him. If the dealer should call "HOLE IN ONE" play ends immediately. If the player's score is the lowest or tied for the lowest, the player would get 0 score for his hand. If another player has a lower score, the player calling "HOLE IN ONE" would receive 5 points score for his hand. Holes are considered PAR 4 so the 5 points would be considered a boggy in golf. The term boggy in golf means 1 over par. Players having Bonus cards subtract 1 point. Example: If player calling Hole-In-One has lowest score. His score would be 0. Should he have a Bonus Card it would be -1, Two Bonus Cards -2. If another player has a lower score, the one calling Hole-In-One would receive 5 points. Should he have a Bonus Card 1 point would be subtracted. His score would be 5 minus 1 = 4 points. All other players receive the total score on their cards. 9 holes is considered a game. The lowest score after 9 holes wins. As in real golf the game can be scored by the one who wins the most holes rather than total score. This is called match play.

LINE-O

Cut up card perforated into squares and give 5 to each player. Score using numbers in Center of card. Remove 2 cards of each number using center circle numbers. 2 each of 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. You will have a pile of 14 cards. Shuffle the 14 cards and place face down in center of table. Dealer shuffles the rest of the cards and deals 5 cards to each player. Player will now turn his cards over one at a time and place next to each other. From the pile of 14 cards in the center of the table, the dealer turns over 1st card. If the number 3 is in the center of the card all players who have number 3 on their first card should cover it with a square. Whoever is the dealer and turns the cards over one by one and calls out the number. Number 3 on any other card but the first card cannot be covered. You must have the cards in consecutive order. If the second card is a 2 and you have a 2 on your first card you may cover it. If your first card was a three and you covered it, you may now cover the number 2 card, if your next card is a 2. If the 2 is on your 4th card, you cannot cover it unless cards 1, 2, and 3 have been covered. When you cover all cards in consecutive order you may call "LINE-O" and you have won the game.

Are you a musical genius? In this fast paced game you will be asked to play tunes using xylophone pieces. Hesitation, delay or incorrect notes get you eliminated. Stay tuned and ensure that you are sharper than other players to win the game!

CONTENTS: The game includes 8 x drumsticks with which to play, and 10 x Stay Tuned tune cards with well known songs.

boardgame: Sirens: The Deep Sea – Water Phoenix
description:
Includes

Sirens (Kingdom of Ganea) - Xarta (berserker), Casira (rift mage), Halarus (hunter), Kitara (valkyrie)

New Buildings - Siren camp, Wathea altar, Spirit temple, Coral attack tower, Whale transport station

Earthquakes - triggered by the story of a scenario. Large blocks of stones fall down and can block paths, resources or buildings. But you can use the craftsman to free these areas. Or you can release blocks of stones to use them to your advantage.

Whirlpools - will pull you in and stun you/enemies for 1 game round if you are adjacent to them. In some scenarios, they are used as a “whirlpool portal” to get to other locations when areas are separated or divided by terrain tiles.

Streaming tubes - With the use of 1 movement point (MOV), you can enter and exit them at any point of the tube. They can also be combined to create large tubes with different shapes.

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Momiji.

To play this realm you must fill cards with numbers. But be careful, as vertically adjacent cards you fill must be 1 higher than the already-filled numbers. Will you find your way out of this realm and score benefits from horizontal rows and stars?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Space Base Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Space Base.

To play this realm you use dice to outline new benefits for your future activations. The more you activate, the stronger your turns will become. Can you find the optimal order to activate your dice?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Dinosaur Island Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Dinosaur Island.

To play this realm you use dice to either increase the security level of your park, or to add a dinosaur. But be careful, as your number of visitors may not exceed your security level. Can you attract enough visitors to safely score 6 stars?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games: Let's Be Veterinarians
description:
Oh dear, the animals are sick and need to be treated right away. The crocodile has a toothache, the elephant has a cold, the cheetah has a tummy ache, and the flamingo has a broken leg. Who will be the best veterinarian and the first one to match all the medicine tiles correctly?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Meine ersten Spiele: Spielesammlung Im Kindergarten
description:
The large game collection, centered around the Rainbow Preschool and the young animals, Katie Koala, Max Monkey, Tony Turtle, and Gabby Giraffe, offers ten different game ideas. From simple dice race games, sorting, memory, and counting games to fun interactive and activity games, there’s something for everyone.

—description from the publisher

It’s egg-laying season! Mama Vulture has been hard at work – she’s laid 8 large eggs. But her nest is far too small! All the children work together to help Mama Vulture build a large, sturdy nest. But the cheeky parrots are just waiting for something to fall. Put Mama Vulture and her eggs safely in the sturdy nest before the last parrot shows up and everyone wins the game together.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Knuckling Knights & The Big Dragon Spectacle
description: Dragon Knuckling Knight has invited everyone to the big dragon spectacle at his castle. All the knights crowd excitedly into the towers. When the secret doors finally open, everyone tumbles out laughing – but hopefully they won’t fall into the water! Whoever successfully makes their way through the turmoil and collects the most coins wins the game.

Streets of Tokyo is a cooperative drawing game in which players try to guess words drawn by other players on maps of Tokyo. The twists are that players draw and guess simultaneously, they may only draw on the streets of the maps.

The game is played with an evolutive timer: every time a word is correctly guessed, it is validated in the application, and players earn more time as well as points depending on the difficulty of the word.

The game ends either when players run out of time, they lose the game, or when they manage to validate a word on each of the 16 maps in the game. In either case, they get a final score that they can try to beat on their next attempt.

The game also features a campaign mode, starting easy and getting progressively harder with various constraints added with the levels.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: There’s been a fun-packed 20th anniversary party! And the sock monster has left all the socks in a huge mess! All the children rummage through the pile of socks at the same time to find matching pairs of socks. But what’s this? Holes in the socks?! The little moth must have a big appetite! The children must be careful not to collect too many holey socks. For an extra-tricky hand sock challenge, pull the enclosed socks over your hands! The player with the most pairs of socks at the end wins the game.

Follow Japan history between 10 chapters (10 independent games with the same material). Each game is based on one different character.

Chapter 0: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 5; Dificulty: 1/6; Game mechanic: Card battle
Chapter 1: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 5; Dificulty: 5/6; Game mechanic: Cooperative, Memory
Chapter 2: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 5; Dificulty: 3/6; Game mechanic: Simultaneous card placement
Chapter 3: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 3; Dificulty: 5/6; Game mechanic: Cooperative, move item
Chapter 4: Tutorial; Player: 2 to 6; Dificulty: 4/6; Game mechanic: Betray, deduction, asymetry, memory
Chapter 5: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 8; Dificulty: 3/6; Game mechanic: Push your luck, hidden role
Chapter 6: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 3; Dificulty: 3/6; Game mechanic: Cooperative, calculs
Chapter 7: Tutorial; Player: 2 to 6; Dificulty: 6/6; Game mechanic: Royal battle, deck building, card combo
Chapter 8: Tutorial; Player: 1 to 4; Dificulty: 3/6; Game mechanic: Card collection
Chapter 9: Tutorial; Player: 2 to 6; Dificulty: 2/6; Game mechanic: Open battle, bluff
Chapter 10: Tutorial; Player: 2 to 8; Dificulty: 1/6; Game mechanic: Trick-taking, asymetry

boardgame: Game Meets Puzzle: Wo leben Löwe & Co.?
description: Uh-oh, lion, koala, tortoise, and penguin are lost. Who can help them? Children select an animal and send it on a “round trip” with the help of the die. If their figure lands on a puzzle piece of the same color, the child takes this piece. The first player to complete the puzzle of their animal’s home wins.

boardgame: Game Meets Puzzle: Schatzsuche im Ozean
description: Deep at the bottom of the ocean lies an old shipwreck. The ship was loaded with a chest full of gold, jewels, and precious stones. Do you want to go on a treasure hunt? Then let’s go! Children take turns turning over puzzle pieces and placing them in the puzzle, if they fit. With each piece placed, they collect gold nuggets. The player with the most gold nuggets at the end wins the game.

Mia the cat and her intrepid crew are on a hunt for treasure. Evil Pirate Madame Goldtooth has stolen from many innocent sailors and hidden their treasures in secret places. Once you reach the island, lost gemstones show the crew the way – but beware, the thieving water rats are lurking everywhere. If all the treasures are safely on board before the water rats get on the boat, everyone wins together.

—description from the publisher

The clocks tick differently in Tocksville, which is why people ask for strange clock faces: some ask for even numbers only or the same time shown over the course of 4 hours. Strange? Yes. Impossible? No. With your wits and the luck of the dice, you can build the clock you need. The player with the most points after 12 rounds wins.

—description from the publisher

The players form two teams and take on the role of highly ambitious assembly robots who get every move right! The rules are very simple, the tasks surprisingly tricky: the aim is to move 4 components back and forth, turn them over, stack them and nest them without making any mistakes. The keys to success are overview and teamwork, a steady hand, speed and nerves of steel. The faster team wins the order card - and thus comes a little closer to overall victory.

—description from the publisher

Sambiki Saru is a Japanese expression that refers to the three wise monkeys known as Mizaru, who sees no evil; Kikazaru, who hears no evil; and Iwazaru, who speaks no evil. In this cooperative trick-taking game, the objective is to overcome a series of challenges. Divided into 3 colors, the monkey markers are distributed among players and secretly rolled, as if they were dice. The first player in the trick chooses one of their monkeys to put into play. The other players must play a monkey of the same color, if possible. The player with the most acrobatic monkey of the same color as the first monkey played wins the trick. Each scenario offers a challenge for players to overcome, defining how many tricks each player must win and establishing communication restrictions and different uses for banana tokens. If the round's challenge is overcome, players advance to the next level. How many challenges will your team be able to complete?

—description from the publisher

This modern chess variant doubles the excitement with a supersized 16x8 board, 2v2 gameplay, and 2 distinct game modes that demand both strategy and teamwork. It's the twist that let's you collaborate and dominate.

The game comes with the 64 pieces (full set in 4 colors). The goal is still the same; checkmate the opponent team's king to win the game. It's a new kind of 4 player variant.

—description from the publisher

Oblique is an Operational  level scenario-based block wargame covering eight campaigns of Frederick the Great during the Silesian Wars. Players maneuver around the map, attempting to seize strategic objectives while managing their Despair and bringing their opponent to a decisive battle. Your most important actions will require the expenditure of Supply Pieces, which are generated at periodic intervals and moved along supply lines – either by road networks or by waterways, though in the latter case these must move in the same direction as the river flows. Armies without supplies cannot lay siege, build networks of garrisons, recover from casualties, or fight battles; if you are attacked when you have no supplies, you do so at a grave disadvantage. Threatening supply lines and cutting the enemy off from supply is key to tilting the odds of battle into your favor.

These battles are fought using wooden sticks in The Battle Game, a fast-playing and chess-like luckless game of canny deployments, strategic reserves, and Frederick’s famous oblique order. Your capabilities within this Battle Game are determined by what blocks you’ve brought into battle. Losses can be quite heavy and one of these battles can drastically alter the course of the campaign. You’ll need to choose your battles carefully, and try to avoid fighting when the odds are against you.

Campaigns of the period are prone to all sorts of unexpected delays: bad weather, disease, stragglers, even the distraction of a pleasant castle where a weary commander might linger perhaps a bit longer than they ought. After each Prussian turn, a die is rolled to determine how much time passes. Perhaps you’ll have plenty of opportunities and use them well, or perhaps you’ll suddenly find the game approaching its end and you’ve neglected to pursue your objectives. This unpredictability is further supplemented by a series of scenario-specific event tables for each player, rolled at four key points during a match. Will your supplies be raided? Will troops enter the fray from another theater? Will Frederick be laid low by his hemorrhoids, or a bout of tearful melancholy?

Oblique is a game requiring skillful mastery of cutthroat logistics, daring maneuver, and sound tactics. Failure to excel in any one of these spheres will result in disaster. Though the game’s rules are quite simple, you’ll need to invest your time and energy in learning to play the game well. We think Oblique will reward this investment with many dynamic and memorable play experiences.

—description from the publisher

description:
34 Napoleonic Scenarios including Talavera campaign and the third invasion of Portugal.

This scenario book is for battalion sized units and suitable for Shako II and easily converts to other rule systems. OBs include troop strength and number of guns.

18 Napoleonic Scenarios with detailed orders of battles and maps suitable for use with most battalion level rule sets. This is one of 4 books on the 1809 campaign.

Includes Arnhofen, Achdorf, Altdorf, Abensburg - Center, Abensburg - south, Abensburg - north, Abbach, Dunzling, Eggmuhl - south, Eggmuhl - north, Geisenhausen, Hausen-Teugn, Landshut, Laichling, Neumarkt, Nittenau, Reinhausen, Regensburg.

18 Napoleonic Scenarios with detailed orders of battles and maps suitable for use with most battalion level rule sets. This one of 4 books on the 1809 campaign.

Includes
Sacile (south), Sacile (north), Sacile (grand), Volano, Soave, Castelcerino, Piave River (a.m.), Piave River (p.m.), San Daniele, Tarvis (1st), Tarvis (2nd), St. Michael, Klagenfurt,  Karako, Papa, Gyirmot, Raab, Graz

18 Napoleonic Scenarios with detailed orders of battles and maps suitable for use with most battalion level rule sets. This is one of 4 books on the 1809 campaign.

Includes Berg Isel, Pressburg, Nossen, Wagram 1st day center, Wagram 1st day west, Wagram 2nd day east, Wagram 2nd day center, Wagram 2nd day west, Wagram 2nd day south, Wagram 2nd day grand, Korneuburg, Gefrees, Laa, Hollaburnn, Schongrabern, Znaim 1st day, Znaim 2nd day south, Znaim 2nd day north.

18 Napoleonic Scenarios with detailed orders of battles and maps suitable for use with most battalion level rule sets. This is one of 4 books on the 1809 campaign.

Includes:  
Raszyn Schärding 1st, Scärding 2nd, St Martin, Salzburg, Eferding, Ebelsberg, Wörgl, Linz, Gracac, Aspern-Essling 1, Aspern Essling 2, Aspern-Esseling 3, Gospic 1st, Gospic 2nd, Zutalovka, Jedlinsk, Gorzyce.

The Game Creator Set is a set of 6 games (presented progressively) created by Micael Sousa (Academic researcher and game designer). It is a game box ready to play and a tool for creating new games.

In this game, you will be guided through every step of the mechanics and developmental stages of creating two different types of games: an abstract, strategic Eurogame, "Cube Control", and a collaborative adventure Role Play Game (RPG) ", Magic Flick" that will serve as inspiration for your own games.

Unleash your creativity with the Game Creator Set. With the customizable blank components at your disposal, you have the freedom to create, delete, and recreate as many times as you wish. This set is a canvas for your imagination. You can also just play the games we provide.

—description from the publisher

This expansion contains 6 Coral pieces + 1 Coral pawn for the Table Coral species. This component set can be used:

- As an alternative player component set in a 2-4 player game (instead of one of the component sets included in the box);

OR

- As the 5th player's component set in 5-player games. 5-player games use the same rules as 2-4 player games (except that setup cards can't be used).

SCRAP: Post-Apocalyptic Mayhem is inspired by the Mel Gibson 1980s movie, The Road Warrior. Each player plays the role of the survivors inside the compound about the time that Max Rockatansky (Gibson) arrives.

Players try to protect their compound from marauders while refining waste and oil into fuel, recruit more survivors, and scavengw vehicles, parts, and equipment to use in an escape.

Once a player declares their intention to escape, the game changes into a massive free for all race for survival where players work together... or against each other... to fend off the marauders and escape with the most survivors. Scrap is playable in co-op or competitive modes and has a "feel" similar to Galaxy Trucker, with strong post-apocalyptic theme.

One of the big innovations of Scrap is the scrap pile, from which players scavenge parts and weapons and tires, etc.. It is an unorganized pile in the middle of the table. Players may not touch tokens other than the one they choose to scavenge. You may grab some car parts only to discover that it reveals a much better piece beneath it. Dang!!! And all tokens are double-sided, so you may grab some tires only to find a much more valuable item on the other side.

Can you survive and escape!!!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mickey and Friends Walt Disney World: Memory Game
description:
McDonald's Happy Meal Toy #6 in this series.

The Memory Game is for 1-6 players. The included bonus game, Sequence Champion, is also for 1-6 players.

The game includes 32 cards plus a rules sheet. The cards come in pairs and depict Disney icons such as Mickey’s Face, Cinderella’s Castle, or Goofy’s Hat.

From the rules:

Game Instructions: Memory Game

Players: 1-6
Goal: Collect the most matching pairs
Setup: Shuffle the deck of cards and lay out all cards face-down in rows, forming a large rectangle
Rules:

     Player 1 selects a card, turns it over, then selects another card to turn over
     If the two cards match, the player takes both cards, then repeats their turn. If the two cards do not match, the cards are turned back over, and the next player takes their turn
     Game continues until all cards are picked up. The player with the most matching pairs wins


Bonus Game: Sequence Campion

Players: 1-6
Goal: Be the last player remaining in the game
Setup: Shuffle the card deck
Rules:

     Dealer begins by selecting 3 pairs of cards (Example 2 Mickey cards, 2 Pluto cards, 2 Flower cards). Dealer then separates the pairs into 2 rows and organizes each group of cards face-up in the same order (Example: Mickey, Pluto, Flower). The dealer keeps one group of cards in front of them
     Player 1 has 10 seconds to memorize the order of the cards. After 10 seconds, the Dealer flips all cards face-down. Dealer then takes the group of cards in front of Player 1, shuffles the cards, then gives them back to Player 1
     Player 1 will have 10 seconds to place the cards in the correct order
     Once complete, Dealer flips over the group of cards in front of them. If the order matches, Player 1 will remain in the game. If the order does not match, Player 1 is out of the game
     Play continues clockwise around the group. If more than one player remains after the first round, the Dealer will add another pair of cards and the game repeats.
     The last player remaining in the game wins

2-4 players, +8 ages, 15-30 minutes card game
A pizza festival is held every year in Naples, Italy. Order Master is a competition where the world's best pizza chefs challenge each other. The chefs at this year's festival are very successful. Some chefs claim that they can make the most delicious pizzas in the world even if they are blindfolded. Who will be the winner of the festival?

COMPONENTS
18 Ingredient & Recipe Cards

GAMEPLAY
The game consists of 3 rounds. In each round, players offer ingredients to the next player on their turn. When all players reach 4 ingredients, the pizza making phase begins and scoring is done. The flow of the game is clockwise.

GAME ORDER - TURN STRUCTURE
The deck is shuffled. The first player becomes the active player. The active player draws two cards from the deck, looks at them, and offers one card face up and one card face down to the player on the left.

The player receiving the offer takes either the face-up card or the face-down card. The player who made the offer takes the other card.

The turn passes to the player to whom the bid was made in the first round. The new player draws two cards as in the first round and makes an offer to the player on the left.

Rounds continue in this order until each player has 4 ingredients. Then the pizza making phase begins.

PIZZA MAKING STAGE
Players decide to make the recipe with any of the 4 ingredients in their hands and proceed to the scoring phase of the pizza they make by opening their cards. Players must use all the ingredients they have in the pizza they decide to make.

SCORING OF PIZZA
Players earn points equal to the sum of the prices of the ingredients they put on their pizzas at the top right.

COMPLETION OF THE ROUND
When all players have finished scoring, the round is completed and the ingredient cards are shuffled again and the game starts again with a new round.

END OF THE GAME AND WINNING THE GAME
After completing 3 rounds, players add up the points they earned in each round. The player with the most points wins the game!

DESIGNER: BUSRA & ONUR TOSUN

-description from designer

boardgame: The Fetterman Fight or A Hundred in the Hands
description:
The Fetterman Fight is a 2-5 player war game set in the 19th century American frontier. It pits Captain William Fetterman and his forces against the Native Americans. The game is also known as A Hundred in the Hands.

On December 21, 1866, 78 soldiers and 2 civilians under the command of Captain William Fetterman rode into the Peno Creek valley. The Americans chased a group of Native Americans, who had previously raided a local wood cutting party. Fetterman and his forces are out for revenge. Meanwhile, fifteen hundred members of the Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho tribes greet his forces in an ambush. Was Pipe's dream of 100 soldiers falling into his hands about to come true?

This design from Martin Anderson includes an 8-page rulebook, 34" x 24" map, 254 die-cut unit counters, 1 ten-sided die, and 1 six-sided die.

While many Heroes prefer to work alone, even the most closed off among them knows that having a sidekick by your side can be the difference between victory and defeat. And hey, some companionship never hurt anyone (or at least it rarely does).

This expansion adds another twist to the game, in the form of resourceful Sidekicks to help the Heroes in their battles. While this set includes 4 new animal Sidekicks, each with their own figure: Ace – Batman’s loyal hound, Detective Chimp – the super smart ape sleuth, Dex-Starr – the feisty Red Lantern cat, and Ch’p – the heroic Green Lantern space chipmunk, it also includes card sets to use the figures of several other characters from other expansions as Sidekicks, from other animals like Krypto and Gleek, to supportive humans like Lois Lane, Jimmy Olsen, Alfred, Jim Gordon, and the epitome of the sidekick himself: Robin.

The Crowdfunding Exclusive Sidekicks set comes with:

1 Ace the Bat-Hound Figure
    1 Detective Chimp Figure
    1 Dex-Starr figure
    1 Ch’p Figure
    3 Ace the Bat-Hound Sidekick Cards
    3 Detective Chimp Sidekick Cards
    3 Dex-Starr Sidekick Cards
    3 Ch’p Sidekick Cards
    3 Krypto Sidekick Cards
    3 Gleek Sidekick Cards
    3 Alfred Sidekick Cards
    3 Commissioner Gordon Sidekick Cards
    3 Jimmy Olsen Sidekick Cards
    3 Lois Lane Sidekick Cards
    3 Robin Sidekick Cards
    11 Sidekick Reference Cards

-description from campaign page

The Bounce-Off Duel 2-player game features high-energy, head-to-head action based on the original ball-bouncing pattern-matching challenge with no batteries required! Both players bounce balls into their side of the game tray at the same time, trying to match the pattern on the card. When one player completes the challenge, they slam their paddle and BOOM! The opponents' balls go flying! Time for a rematch!

boardgame: Car Wars Two-Player Starter Set: Orange/Purple
description:
Enter the autoduelling arena with Car War Orange/Purple! This two-player starter set is a great introduction to the Car Wars series and features streamlined rules for easier play.

The year is 2070 and autoduelling is now the most popular sport in America. Build your vehicle by adding weapons, armor, and more, then face off against your enemies in exciting bouts of vehicular combat.

Contents:

Two pre-assembled minis: Sawtooth (from Miniatures Set 4) and Pure Energy (from Miniatures Set 2)
    Miniatures come pre-assembled and unpainted
    Two car bases (one orange and one purple)
    Two dashboards (one orange and one purple)
    Four counter sheets (dashboard sliders, barrier and wreck tokens, two turning keys, and other assorted tokens)
    Dice pack (20 dice: six yellow, four blue, three red, three green, two black, and two white)
    Updated Rulebook now includes a sample car build with helpful hints for your first game and all-new quick-reference material on the back cover.

Combine Car Wars Orange/Purple with Car Wars Blue/Green and Car Wars Red/Yellow for even bigger battles!

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: Bullets and Brie
description:
Everyone's a suspect when murder is on the menu. Here's everything you need to serve up mystery, murder, and fun - party planner, invitations, recipes - plus a tasty murder mystery to solve. Just invite your friends to join you for dress up and to play the roles of devious suspects. Serve up a meal or light snack, and you're ready for some drop-dead fun!

You have been invited for dinner at the Official Residence of the Deputy Mayor of Casablanca. Morocco is a colony of France governed by the Vichy French regime. The guest of honor was to have been France's greatest living mime artist. He fails to arrive. he has been murdered!

Immerse into a very hot and dry desert where animals strive to stay hydrated against the radiating heat. These animals carry fruits from the native cacti in their mouths because cactus fruit (like Barrel Cactus fruit and Prickly Pear Cactus fruit) store water inside of them and are a quick snack for the animals in need. The goal of the game is to consume 3 sources of water, which are the cactus fruit, or to scare off your opponent’s animals, forcing them to abandon their sources of hydration.

Key Features:

A tactical 1v1 abstract strategy game with perfect information, some interesting asymmetric powers, and unique interactions.

The modularity of the board changes the terrain throughout the game allowing players to try out different methods of movement and strategies. No game will be the same. Rules can be adapted for new players and experienced.

Terreign is engaging for any level of player making it the perfect gateway game to modern board gaming.

—description from the publisher

This expansion pack includes 12 Quests that were originally, and exclusively, released on the Ye Olde Inn's companion Facebook group.

Sunday Quest: Adventures continues where the popular Hero Quest expansion Wizards of Morcar left off. It is assumed that players will have completed and be familiar with Kellar's Keep, Return of the Witch Lord, Against the Ogre Horde and Wizards of Morcar expansion packs. On occasion the Quests herein will also dabble with elements taken from the U.S. version of the game system's two additional expansion packs: The Mage in the Mirror and The Frozen Horror.

—description from the designer

Quest book containing five exciting adventures for our Heroes.

description:
This expansion pack includes 12 Quests, a selection of Fimir Magic Spells and a number of treasures.

Riches of Karak Azgul continues where Wizards of Morcar left off. It is assumed that players will have completed and be familiar with Kellar's Keep, Return of the Witch Lord, Against the Ogre Horde and Wizards of Morcar expansion packs to the original game.
