STAQS is a strategic two-player board game played on a 5x5 grid. Each player has four stackable pieces of their color (blue or white), which they place and move on top of 25 neutral stackable pieces. Players take turns moving their stacks one step at a time, aiming to create the tallest stack on the board. The game combines elements of territory control and vertical stacking, challenging players to outmaneuver their opponent through clever positioning and stack building. The game ends when no more moves are possible, and victory goes to the player who controls the tallest stack, with tiebreakers based on the number and height of secondary stacks.

Overview
STAQS is a two-player game played on a 5x5 grid. The goal is to own the tallest stack when there are no moves left.

Components
- 25 stackable neutral pieces
- 4 blue stackable pieces
- 4 white stackable pieces

Setup
1. Arrange the 25 neutral pieces into a 5x5 grid.
2. Players choose a color (blue or white).
3. Starting with blue, players alternate placing one of their pieces on top of a neutral piece.

Gameplay
1. On your turn, move one of your stacks (including any neutral pieces in the stack) one step onto another neutral piece.
2. Stacks cannot jump over other stacks or move over empty spaces.
3. If a player is unable to move, they must pass.
4. If both players pass consecutively, the game ends.

Winning
The winner is determined in the following order:
1. The player with the tallest stack wins.
2. If players have stacks of equal height (n), the player with the most stacks of height n wins.
3. If still tied, compare the next tallest stacks or most stacks of equal height until a winner is determined.

Freedom! Station 4X gained independence from the Ormar Thalassocracy. You must use politics, technology, and dragon mechs to rebuild and lead the new post-colonial nation.

Cards are multi-use: Actions, locations, resources, reinforcements, goals.

Actions: You start with 2 cards in your action tableau (1 leader card and 1 tech). The power of an action is determined by the slot the card currently occupies. Add cards here to expand and empower your choices.

Resources: Spend cards to play and upgrade action cards, locations, and reinforcements.

Locations: Add cards to the central map. Locations provide continual resources that give you discounts on action cards and reinforcements. But guard them well! They can be conquered by others.

Reinforcements: Cards may be played on locations to improve, defend, or conquer them.

Goals: Unused leader cards become goals for the game, keeping the win conditions for each game unique.

-description by the publisher

boardgame: The Kittens of Mont Saint Michel
description:
See them there, those little balls of fluff, so full of mischief and curiosity? You won’t believe it now, but this is how heroes start. Join the four kittens Leon, Adara, Nera and Guy in their adventures as they learn, spar and explore the streets and secret places of the ancient fortress island of Mont Saint Michel...and it’s not just play. They’ll need to work together to put their lessons of the Warrior Virtues to good use against a hidden foe before this little tale is done.
Before you can achieve great things, you must learn the little things. Let us get started, oui?

The Kittens of Mont Saint Michel is a co-op adventure card game where each players takes the role of one of the would-be heroes of Mont Saint Michel. In this game you will explore the town and castle while learning the virtues courage, patience, competence, selflessness, perseverance and integrity on the path to becoming a true warrior.

The object of the Kittens of Mont Saint Michel is for your heroic kitten to earn as many warrior virtues as possible and defeat the vermin army in the Final Battle.

After selecting your little furry hero and setting up the play area, you will have actions to perform each turn including moving to new locations, sparing with fellow kittens and earning warrior virtues.

The locations in Mont Saint Michel are represented by cards that occupy the center of the table. When your kittens venture there they will be able to test their skills to earn the corresponding virtue on the card as well as collecting loot items to use in their mission to save the monastery

The game is played over three chapters. Chapter 1: Train Hard is where the kittens earn Warrior Virtues by adventuring in the town, monastery and fortress of Mont Saint-Michel, represented by Location cards. In Chapter 2: The Search, the would-be heroes continue to earn virtue while seeking out the foe and Chapter 3: The Final Battle is the moment everyone has been working towards! Foil the Vermin Army’s plan to harm the monastery and its inhabitants.

—description from the designer

You and your team find a clearing where Draky lives. After meeting him, you want nothing more than to adopt him as your pet.
But first, you’ll need to work together to feed him every day, build him a shelter with , and tame him with.
To win, you must ask your companions to give you copies of woodland creatures. By combining them, you can gather wood to build the house (actually constructing it using folded cards) and food to feed Draky. You can also choose to draw a treasure card and perform the action written on it to increase Draky's affection for you.

To win, you need to reach the required number of hearts and constructions based on the number of players. But be careful: if the dragon goes unfed for a day, or if the draw pile is emptied, you lose the game.

Draky is a cooperative French game. It combines resource management, card house building, and challenges for 2 to 6 players, ages 6 and up.

—description from the publisher

The princes are sneaking into your party to slip you proposals. But you have invited three new strong princesses with special abilities.
Six new round events add even more variety to the party.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Introductory Set
description:
Welcome to the Age of Sigmar – an era of ceaseless conflict in which mortals, gods, and monsters clash upon the battlefield. It is an age of heroism, sacrifice, and unending war. Take to the field and lead your troops into battle – to defend civilisation or tear it down.

This set introduces you to Warhammer Age of Sigmar – a thrilling fantasy setting you can participate in by collecting armies, painting them, and waging war with your friends. The 48-page handbook contains everything you need to get started with all aspects of this exciting hobby – you'll learn to build and paint your models, discover the setting they belong to, and fight your first battles with them.

Inside this boxed set you will find:

25x Push-fit, plastic miniatures: 
– 5x Stormcast Eternals Liberators
– 20x Skaven Clanrats

1x 48-page handbook
1x Playing mat measuring 30"" x 22.4"" approx.
1x 12"" range ruler
6x Six-sided 'D6' dice

1x Citadel Colour Starter Brush
6x Citadel Colour paints:
– 1x Kantor Blue
– 1x Retributor Armour
– 1x Leadbelcher
– 1x Rakarth Flesh
– 1x Khorne Red
– 1x Steel Legion Drab

This kit comes supplied with 5x Citadel 40mm Round Hex Hole Bases, and 20x Citadel 25mm Round Hex Hole Bases. These push-fit miniatures can be assembled without glue, and are supplied unpainted – we recommend using Citadel Colour paints.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Starter Set
description:
The Age of Sigmar is an era of unending war. For centuries, the mighty Stormcast Eternals have battled against the forces of evil. Yet now they face their darkest time as the foul mutant ratmen of the Skaven hordes threaten to overrun the Mortal Realms.

This set is ideal for starting off with Warhammer Age of Sigmar – a thrilling fantasy setting you can participate in by collecting armies, painting them, and waging war with your friends. The 64-page handbook contains everything you need to get started with all aspects of this exciting hobby – you'll learn to build your models, discover the setting they belong to, and play thrilling, tabletop battles with them.

Inside this boxed set you will find:

35x Push-fit, plastic miniatures: 
– 1x Lord-Veritant with 1x Gryph-crow
– 3x Prosecutors
– 5x Stormcast Eternals Liberators
– 1x Grey Seer
– 1x Warlock Engineer
– 3x Rat Ogors
– 20x Skaven Clanrats

1x 64-page handbook
1x Double-sided gaming mat, measuring 30"" x 22.4"" approx.
2x 12"" range rulers
2x Double-sided reference sheets
6x Six-sided 'D6' dice

This kit comes supplied with 3x Citadel 32mm Round Hex Hole Bases, 3x Citadel 50mm Round Hex Hole Bases, 9x Citadel 40mm Round Hex Hole Bases, and 20x Citadel 25mm Round Hex Hole Bases. These push-fit miniatures can be assembled without glue, and are supplied unpainted – we recommend using Citadel Colour paints.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Ultimate Starter Set
description:
The Age of Sigmar is an era of unending war. For centuries, the mighty Stormcast Eternals have battled against the forces of evil. Yet now it seems the Hour of Ruin is nigh as the skittering, numberless hordes of the Skaven flood into the Mortal Realms. So potent is their arcane weaponry, that the God-king, Sigmar, has sent his most powerful champions to hold back the tide. At the head of this shimmering host march the Ruination chambers, warriors who have endured death so many times they are more akin to living weapons than the humans they protect.

This set is perfect for starting off with Warhammer Age of Sigmar – a thrilling fantasy setting you can participate in by collecting armies, painting them, and waging war with your friends. The 72-page handbook contains everything you need to get started with all aspects of this exciting hobby – you'll learn to build your models, discover the setting they belong to, and play thrilling, tabletop battles with them. Accompanying this is the Spearhead: Fire and Jade book, containing all the additional rules you need to play the fast-paced Spearhead game mode, along with a double-sided gaming mat on which to play out your Spearhead clashes.

Inside this boxed set you will find:

37x Push-fit, plastic miniatures: 
– 1x Lord-Vigilant on Gryph-Stalker
– 1x Lord-Veritant with 1x Gryph-crow
– 3x Prosecutors
– 5x Stormcast Eternals Liberators
– 1x Clawlord on Gnaw-Beast
– 1x Grey Seer
– 1x Warlock Engineer
– 3x Rat Ogors
– 20x Skaven Clanrats 
 4x Push-fit, plastic terrain pieces
1x 72-page handbook
1x 144-page Spearhead: Fire and Jade book
1x Double-sided gaming mat, measuring 30"" x 22.4"" approx.
2x reference sheets
6x Six-sided 'D6' dice
36x Spearhead cards
1x 6"" plastic range ruler
1x 3"" plastic range ruler

This kit comes supplied with 1x Citadel 75mm Oval Hex Hole Base, 1x Citadel 90mm Oval Hex Hole Base, 3x Citadel 32mm Round Hex Hole Bases, 3x Citadel 50mm Round Hex Hole Bases, 9x Citadel 40mm Round Hex Hole Bases, and 20x Citadel 25mm Round Hex Hole Bases. These push-fit miniatures can be assembled without glue, and are supplied unpainted – we recommend using Citadel Colour paints.

Plank - Krakatoa is a 2-5 person competitive treasure-hunting game where players must battle for control of supplies while they attempt to assemble a crew and claim a treasure for the win while avoiding the erupting volcanic lava blasts. Use your crew's unique abilities to roam the vast sea and plunder your opponents as you collect resources and the keys you need to claim the treasure. Don't like your current crew? Make them walk the plank and pick up new ones from sand bars. Once you think you have claimed the most treasure return to port and count up your score.

Embark on a treacherous voyage across the boiling seas of Krakatoa, where the thunderous boom of the mountain's eruption pierces the air, shaking even the bravest of hearts. Amidst this chaos, a pirate must seize the day by building a formidable crew, plundering opponents, and claiming treasures before the lava engulfs all. Will you emerge triumphantly with your bounty, or will Krakatoa claim another victim? The answer lies in your daring deeds and quick wits.


     Build your crew
     Avoid the lava blasts
     Plunder your foes
     Claim the treasures


—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. The picture die determines which puppet may move, while the number die determines how many spaces it may move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

The game was published between 1930 and 1942.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 40 by carriage or space 80 by train wins the game. The carriages use 1 die while the trains use two dice.

The game features the modern way of traveling by train (1900) and travel by coach from 100 years ago (1800).

boardgame: Jahu Safari: Zwei Tierwelten – gemeinsam sind wir stark
description:
The cooperative family game about friendship and encounters.

The stork returns from her trip to Africa and tells incredible things. There are said to be horses with stripes and other unusual animals there. Slip into the roles of the farm animals and set off on an exciting journey through Africa! You visit the African animals and invite them to a party together. Where else could you get to know each other better? On the way you cleverly collect everything you need for a party and try to get to the party on the island of Madagascar in time for the party before sunset.

The game was developed in an inclusive workshop by 5 people with multiple disabilities.

A House Divided Designer Edition is an enhanced and expanded version of Frank Chadwick’s classic American Civil War strategy game. This expanded and refined edition of the game includes all-new beautiful artwork by Wouter Schoutteten and Frank’s definitive ruleset incorporating over 40 years of input from thousands of players. Experienced players will find an expanded map, new militia recruitment rules, streamlined leader rules, and the addition of an Event Deck bringing new flavor and detail to Frank’s classic game.

A House Divided lets gamers play the entire American Civil War in a single session. Players take on the leadership of Union and Confederate forces as they recruit troops, promote Generals, and fight the key battles of the Civil War. A House Divided Designer Edition returns to the spirit of the original game by simplifying and refining a simple set of rules which create complex strategies; successful players will need to plan ahead from the start of the game, rewarding repeated play.

Key changes from previous editions include:

- Recruitment has been overhauled and updated, tying recruiting of militia to specific states, making capture of key cities a critical part of strategy.
- To support this new Recruitment ruleset and the need to move militia to the battles, the map has been expanded to include more of the states.
- A new Leader System with six different command abilities: Aggressive, Cautious, Defensive, Fast, Reckless, and Strategic. Some leaders have more than one ability, while others are specialists in one command ability.
- An Event Deck tracks the passage of time and leader promotion, while adding impacts of disease, weather, arms smuggling, foreign recognition, starvation, riots, and more.
- A modified Battle System which mitigates the impact of “killer armies” in a theater while making battles simpler to resolve.
- And more!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The 13th expansion introduces characters from the outside reaches of the empire. These distant people rarely leave their hometowns but those few that want to become adventurers in order to fight powerful monsters can certainly become valuable allies. Make use of these new heroes and do your best to subjugate the dragons!

This expansion requires the "Dorasure" base game to play.

Picture this: a bomb is about to explode and no one wants to be around when it does. The wick is burning and with each card the situation gets more explosive. You and your friends will try to pass this bomb on just like a hot potato.

In the deck there are cards that can speed up the detonation or send the bomb far away from you. But, be careful: not every card is revealed right away as they are played. That adds an element of surprise and strategy, because you never know when a play can change the game.

And don't forget about the shrapnel! When the bomb finally explodes, if you are too close to it, prepare to face the consequences. The question that remains is: who will be hold the bomb when it goes off?

—description from the publisher

An illicit poker game and a diamond heist on the Mississippi river lead to a suspicious death. Can you solve a murder that’s taken place? Players will need to examine case notes, witness statements, and all the evidence from the crime scene to find the killer before they jump ship.

Players will need to work together and deduce who the killer is from six suspects, which possession left at the poker table belongs to which suspect, uncover the dark criminal past each suspect is hiding, where they were seated and their final hand on the night of the murder.

You’ll need to answer questions about what transpired to close the case.

20 new Wonder cards that can be added to Volfyirion Guilds card game.
This was a promo and add-on for backers of the Volfyirion Guilds Kickstarter.

This family fun board game combines money hacks and epic life skills. Choose your career and start building your portfolio as you progress through the rounds, navigating ‘Life Happens’ cards that throw spanners in the works. You’ll have to outsmart your opponents as you save, invest, and use creative problem solving to get you where you need to go. Easily keep track of your finances, investments, and loans with the magnetic cards and portfolio as you make smart money moves.

Whether you want to be a plumber, pilot, or social media influencer, you can choose a career path and strategy that match your aspirations and ideals, collecting points as you build your deck. At the end of the game, calculate points based on money, happiness, environment, community, and HACKT!CS points.

This game will teach you all about saving, smart spending,
investing, and more as you develop real-life skills in the most epic way possible!

—description from the designer

Magic Jungle: An enchanted adventure board game where players explore a mystical forest, encountering magical creatures and overcoming fantastical challenges. Collect ancient artifacts, cast spells, and race to uncover the jungle's hidden secrets before your opponents. Will you become the ultimate jungle explorer?

Game Structure:


     Board: A winding path through a magical jungle, divided into spaces of different colors representing various terrains (river, dense forest, clearings, ancient ruins).
     Player Tokens: Each player chooses a unique explorer token.
     Creature Cards: Deck of cards representing magical jungle inhabitants (friendly and dangerous).
     Artifact Figures: figures representing powerful magical items.
     Dice: Standard six-sided dice for movement and challenge resolution.


Rules:


     Setup:
         Place the board in the center.
         Shuffle creature cards and place them face-down.
         Place artifact figures on designated spaces on the board.
         Each player selects a token and places it at the start.
    
     Gameplay:
         Players take turns rolling the die and moving their token.
         The color of the space they land on determines the action:
             Green: Draw a creature card
             Blue: Water challenge
             Brown: Forest challenge
             Gray: Ruins challenge
        
    
     Creature Cards:
         When drawn, the player must interact with the creature.
         Friendly creatures offer benefits (extra moves, protection).
         Dangerous creatures present challenges (lose a turn, move backwards).
    
     Challenges:
         Players must roll the die to overcome challenges.
         The required number depends on the challenge difficulty.
         Artifacts can be used to modify dice rolls.
    
     Artifacts:
         When landing on an artifact space, the player can attempt to claim it by rolling a 6.
         Each artifact has a unique power (e.g., extra movement, challenge bonuses).
         Players can carry up to 2 artifacts at a time.
    
     Player Interaction:
         If a player lands on an occupied space, they can challenge the occupant.
         The challenger and defender roll dice; highest roll wins.
         Winner can steal an artifact or send the loser back 3 spaces.
    
     Winning:
         The game ends when a player reaches the final space, "The Heart of the Jungle."
         To win, they must successfully overcome a final challenge by rolling a 6 (artifacts can be used).
    
     Alternative Win Condition:
         The first player to collect 3 artifacts and make it back to the start also wins.
    


—description from the publisher
Simply titled, "HeroQuest Quests Book", this book is a fan-made collection of eighteen new quests for the four Heroes: Barbarian, Dwarf, Elf and Wizard.

American Pursuit is a fast-paced, all-American, strategic card game where you unleash your inner hustler and chase the American dream in a slapdash race to the finish.

Step into the shoes of 6 cliched characters and lead them in their pursuit of greatness/craft beer/Instagram followers. Your objective is to embrace the American spirit and collect as many dreams as you can — But you’re not the only one chasing them!

Outwit your rivals with cunning action card tactics, collect cash cards, and gain popularity to collect dreams and come out on top. Life, liberty, and the pursuit of laughter — preferably at your rivals’ expense: that's the American Pursuit!

Gameplay:

     The aim of the game is to collect the most dreams. Players take turns and get to make up to 3 moves per turn. You can play an action or cash card from your hand, purchase a dream, or use your character’s special ability.



     Action Cards will either help you or sabotage your opponents, turning their dream pursuit into a nightmare. 



     Along the way you’ll also collect and play Cash Cards. These will help you buy dreams, or help fend off attacks on your existing ones, bringing you closer to victory.



     But money’s not all you’ll need to win. Make sure you increase your popularity along the way to achieve your dreams.



     Each character has a unique special ability that can be used once per game to snatch dreams – and victory – away from your opponents.



     Battle it out with your so-called “friends” until the dream river has dried up. The player with the highest number of dreams wins!


—description from the designer
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Embark on a solitary mission in “Diceolated,” a thrilling roll-and-write adventure set in the eerie silence of an abandoned space station. As a lone space specialist, your task is to navigate through a labyrinth of locked rooms, trying to recover vital “data packs” crucial to your mission. These enigmatic black boxes hold information, secrets, and perhaps even a way to your salvation. You have only 12 hours before the station will decay and all its treasure data will be lost!

Armed with nothing but your wits, a handful of dice, and your trusty data pad, you’ll face the challenges of isolation and limited resources. With each roll of the dice you acquire new insights with Color Chroma Data using them to piece together the puzzle on your Data Pad which can lead to further station investigation and gathering information of the station’s past.

You must use your Data Pad to hack doors, bypass security systems, and decrypt station technologies. Each room remains locked until you gather enough relevant data. Allocate data on your data pad wisely, choosing the right information to unlock each successive room. Analyze patterns and make strategic choices to access increasingly challenging breach locks into new chambers.

Each gameplay experience in Diceolated is unique, as at the start, the game determines the mission you must accomplish.

Are you ready to face the void and uncover the secrets hidden within the abandoned halls of space station in “Diceolated”? Your mission awaits!

Domino game with animals. Played simultaneously. You have to assemble the right head with the right body to score, if the color of the domino is the same as that of the head you score 2 points otherwise 1 point.

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Godzilla Vs Cthulhu
description:
You can choose to add Godzilla to any game. He isn't an enemy; investigators cannot interact with him. Think of him more as an uncontrollable force of nature. He starts in his Unruly Behavior phase, and every turn you roll a die to determine his actions. He might roar, causing investigators to lose sanity, stomp around, killing enemies and leaving Debris tokens that hinder investigators' movement, or even use his atomic breath to place fire and inflict wounds on all figures in a single zone.

Once the Elder One is summoned, Godzilla shifts to his Territorial Behavior. Things escalate as investigators witness a Kaiju battle between Godzilla and the Elder One unfolding before them. Godzilla still acts every turn, but now he moves towards the Elder One to confront him. However, the Elder One doesn't just take attacks passively; every time Godzilla acts, the Elder One strikes back, either physically or mentally, affecting Godzilla's sanity. Godzilla doesn't have a Health track; instead, each time the Elder One hits him, he becomes distressed. When the Distress track reaches its maximum, Godzilla gives up and retreats, causing the investigators to lose the game. Therefore, when Godzilla begins fighting the Elder One, investigators must assist him because if Godzilla retreats before all stages of the Elder One are defeated, they lose.

Racing Fun is a racing game. Theme : Formula 1 or Le Mans race. 
This is not a simulation, because this game emphasizes fun over precision, but you must carefully manage your tyres (soft, medium, hard), your speed (slow to fast), the multiple corners, and you must attack your opponents at the right moment, in order to win this race game. 
Players use dice in order to advance their cars.
Each car has a specific profile, and may obtain some upgrades throughout the championship.
TRUE solo rules are included, where each bot has his own personality and may evolve throughout the championship.

—description from the designer

An identity-hiding escape card game that can be easily enjoyed with simple rules.

Players take turns flipping over one of the face-down red door cards in the play area and follow the instructions on the card, aiming to escape from the labyrinth where a killer is lurking.

If you can collect three "Silver Keys" and find the "Exit", you successfully escape. However, if you find the "Killer's Key", you remember that you are the killer. You are the true culprit who trapped everyone in the mansion's labyrinth. If you can kill everyone else while hiding the fact that you are the killer, you win as the killer.

The game includes 36 cards from the doujin version of "BLACK MAZE", plus two cards not included in the first edition of "BLACK MAZE" and one new card. The difficulty can be adjusted across 40 levels.

Can you break through the endless black labyrinth?

Rooster Chase is a game set in Barcelos, a northern region of Portugal where King Dinis, Queen Isabel and Princess Constança reside in their castle. The royal family owns a rooster farm which becomes the centre of an exciting adventure. One day, due to the negligence of an employee, the gate of the farm is left open and the Roosters seized the opportunity to escape and invade the nearby village.

Realizing the chaos caused by the escaped Roosters, King Dinis takes immediate action. He calls upon the people of the village, offering rewards to those who can successfully catch and return the Roosters to the farm. This creates a frenzy among the villagers as they eagerly participate in the rooster chase.

To catch the Roosters, the villagers employ a clever tactic. They use the worms as bait to attract the Roosters and lure them back to the farm. Armed with the worms, the villagers strategically placed the worms to entice the Roosters and swiftly capture them.

In Rooster Chase, players must outsmart each other in their quest to catch the most Roosters. Will you be able to master the art of using worms as bait and emerge victorious in the rooster chase?

Rooster Chase is played over 2 rounds, with each player taking five turns per round to catch a total of 10 Roosters. The Roosters come in different types and each rooster has their own preferences when it comes to food. Although Roosters eat any worm, each as two types that they find most appealing. During your turn, you must spend the worm to catch a rooster. After successfully catching a rooster, you may have the opportunity to claim an objective. These objectives are based on specific patterns of worms or sets of Roosters, and by completing them you earn victory points.

-description from designer

The Rendang expansion is a delightful and flavor-packed addition to the beloved Nasi Lemak base game. With this expansion, players can indulge in a richer gaming experience as it introduces a trio of new and exciting characters: the clever crow, the savory rendang, and the mischievous Si Oyen. 
Each character brings a unique twist to the gameplay, adding depth and excitement to the already beloved world of Nasi Lemak. As players savor the mouthwatering blend of strategy and fun, they'll discover new strategies and ways to outwit their opponents. 
The Rendang expansion elevates the Nasi Lemak experience, making it an essential addition for those seeking to spice up their gaming sessions with a burst of irresistible flavor.

—description from the publisher

With Holy Week as the driving force, Processions invites you to experience Holy Week at any time of the year. Suitable for 2 to 4 players and lasting approximately 45 minutes, this strategy game is designed so that both young people and adults can enjoy each game.

The winner will be the player with most victory points. How do you get those victory points? Achieve main and secondary goals, get the proper cards for your procession and visit the most important places of the city.

Pick the best cart in the draft, then, use the collected food to move around the city and achieve the goals.

But be careful! Other players may block you.

At the end of the Resurrection Sunday, the player with the most victory points will be the winner.

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Arkham Asylum Breakout (Gamefound Edition)
description:
Located in the outskirts of Gotham City, the infamous Arkham Asylum keeps Batman’s foes who have been declared legally insane under lock and key. Or at least that’s the idea, but whether through the Villain’s cunning or the corruption in the institution, breakouts are far too common, forcing Batman and his allies to take matters into their own hands to keep society safe from this menagerie of deranged bad guys. They’re all crazy here, make sure you don’t become too!

This retail core box brings the world’s most infamous rogues’ gallery to DC Super Heroes United. Take the role of members of the Batman Family and face off against each of the worst criminally deranged Villains Gotham City has to offer. Or, for the first time ever, take on the role of these Villains in Breakout Mode, playing as Fugitives trying to escape from Arkham Asylum while Batman acts as the Antagonist doing his best to keep you locked up. This box is chock full of not only amazing characters but incredibly innovative gameplay. Its retail version will be expanded into a core box, but since all backers will already have the DC Super Heroes United core box, this expansion version comes with even more exclusives instead!

—description from the publisher

Two cards must be revealed and the numbers added. The result must match the default in at least one color, which is face up. If nothing indicates otherwise, the face-up cards are turned over again and it is the next player's turn.

Cluethulhu is a social deduction party game. It evolved from the famous Mafia and Werewolf games. It’s quite different though, in that the Cultists (ostensible bad guys) are the majority, and it is the Investigators (good guys) who must conceal their identity to destroy the Cult before their evil plan reaches fruition.

Each player is secretly assigned a role - Investigator, Cultist, or someone more ambiguous. There is also a Keeper who controls the flow of the game.

The game alternates between night and day phases. During the day the Investigators secretly detain and arrest Cultists, those arrested are out of the game. During the night, the remaining Cultist's vote on the player they suspect is an Investigator. That player is murdered and is out of the game.

Investigators win when there are an equal number of Cultists and Investigators. Cultists win when they have killed all Investigators. Other roles have more specific win conditions such as the Evil Child or the Blood Siblings.

This mini-expansion adds ten summit goal cards to Cascadia: Rolling Hills or Cascadia: Rolling Rivers. These cards provide competitive goals, which can only be scored in the same round that a player first completes them. Three promotional completion cards are also included, which can just be shuffled in with the ones from the base game.

Note: The summit goals are not compatible with solo play, but the completion cards are.

boardgame: Cascadia: Rolling Rivers – Forces of Nature Mini-Expansion
description: This mini-expansion adds ten Forces of Nature cards to Cascadia: Rolling Hills or Cascadia: Rolling Rivers. These cards provide each player with a unique starting bonus, along with a once-per-game special power. Three promotional completion cards are also included, which can just be shuffled in with the ones from the base game.

Dogfight! 1939-40: WW2 Air Combat Game is a 100-page, full color book containing a two-player (with solitaire option) strategy card game of World War II air combat, based on single plane-vs-plane dogfights.  The game utilizes the Battle over Britain game system, and allows you to simulate dogfights occurring in the European theater in the first two years of the war.  Players fly historical warplanes from Germany, Great Britain, France, Belgium, Italy, and Poland.  The digest-sized volume contains full rules (and advanced optional rules), individual scenarios, Campaign game, tables, game markers, historical and game notes, and Reference material, Dogfight Display, and two-dozen fully rated warplane counters and Plane Data Sheets.

Dogfight! is a strategy card game that pits authentically rated warplanes in one-on-one matchups.  The emphasis is on fast play and fun... not complexity and technical calculations.  Individual games can last two minutes or ten minutes... one false move can end it for the best of planes.  Sure, superior aircraft have an advantage, just as they did in actual combat.  But a weaker plane played well can turn things around.  Dogfight! is designed to capture the excitement of aerial combat that keeps you coming back for more.

Planes are rated in a number of categories, such as speed, agility, performance, firepower, and damage.  Comparing the strengths and weaknesses of the various aircraft is easy (just look at the plane's counter and ratings) and part of the fun.  Rated planes included are: Spitfire I, Hurricane I, Fairey Battle, Lysander, Defiant, Gladiator, Blenhein IV (Britain); Me-109E, Me-110C, Ju-87D, Henschel Hs-123, He-111H (Germany), MS-406, MB-152, D-520, Potez 631, Liore-et-Olivier 451 (France), MC-200 Saetta, CR-42 Falco, Ba-65, SM-79 Sparviero (Italy), P.Z.L. P-11C (Poland), Fairey Fox II, R-31 (Belgium).  Rules and planes are compatible with any others in the Battle over Britain  and Dogfight! series.  Game play is fast and furious, with the system emphasizes playability and authenticity rather than endless modifier lists, high complexity and bookkeeping. Gamers must supply a standard deck of playing cards, and a 6-sided die, to play.

Optional rules cover scenarios with medium/heavy bombers, solitaire rules, and Historical Scenario Generator.  Several Campaign Games are included:  POLISH BLITZKRIEG (1939), BATTLE OF FRANCE (1940),  SACRIFICE OF THE BELGIANS (1940), DUNKIRK: SPITS TO THE RESCUE (1940), BATTLE OF BRITAIN (1940), BATTLE OVER MALTA (1940), and NORTH AFRICA (1941).

Dogfight! 1939-40 includes individual Plane Data Cards (sheets) for each of the warplanes represented, facilitating play.

—description from the publisher

The Mermaids have been busy collecting sparkling shells on the island. But oh no! The oceans tide is washing them away!

Spin the spinner, choose a puzzle piece that matches the spinner color, and add it to the board. Spin a shell and take one away from the board. Work together to build the three mermaid puzzles before the tide washes away all of the shells and everyone wins!

Octopush is a light strategy game where players race their octopi across the board while trying not to get knocked off by other players.

Players will draw cards from a small personal deck and play cards to move their octopi around the board. Falling off the opposite side from where you started will score you big points, but pushing others off the board and simply surviving the round will get you more points as well. However, other players won't be the only thing pushing you around. The board is rife with helpful and hurtful currents to push you around and pits that suck your octopi away.

The modular environment will change from game to game and even round to round as you take on each color of octopi. Make the most of this environment to most effectively push yourself ahead and to push others off the board as you try to score as many points as possible!

The players take turns to roll and move. They move from the center space (Karussell) to their spaces at the edge of the game board and back again. The players get to choose the best route. Each player has his own shortcut. The first player to reach the center space again wins the game.

—user summary

Speedcicleta is a competitive tabletop bicycle racing game.

Players move bicycles along a randomly generated track of hexagonal tiles using movement cards, and contend with obstacles in the forms of pedestrians, automobiles, and animals. Players will also sabotage their fellow players with flat tires, broken wheels, and bicycle accidents.
The player crossing the finish line first wins!

The track is constructed from 30 track tiles allowing for excellent replayability while the obstacle cards add a take that element to the game.

—description from the publisher

Dark science fiction Escape Game

This is an independent game, but it takes place in parallel to the story of another of our games: Circus of Mhodryak.

You can play it without Circus of Mhodryak, but playing both games will be a more rewarding experience.

CONTENT

The game includes a folder full of documents, illustrations and even a pendrive with puzzles and files inside that will expand the lore of the game.

boardgame: 水浒传 天苍之星 (Water Margin: Celestial Star)
description:
In 水浒传 天苍之星 ("Water Margin: Celestial Star"), players step into the roles of heroic outlaws from the famous Chinese classic, Water Margin. Similar to the classic Monopoly, this game lets players buy or conquer each other’s strongholds, acquire deeds, and charge tolls to opponents. Battles are decided using hero cards, with special tactic cards allowing players to activate unique abilities for strategic advantages.

Recruit legendary characters, lead your band of outlaws, and employ clever tactics to dominate the game and become the ultimate leader.

Game components

     180 hero cards
     58 tactic cards
     34 land deed cards
     Game board
     Game rule
     6 game pieces and 5 dice


Victory Conditions
The game ends when:


     A player collects 8 land deeds or a predetermined number for victory.
     A player collects a set of 4 land deeds of the same color (excluding ancient battlefields).
     A player bankrupts another by landing on their territory without enough currency to pay the toll.

Dorasure's 14th expansion pack.

An ancient empire from days long past. The many secrets they forged still lying dormant in the land to this day. If you could awaken them and recall their ancient memories, they could provide great assistance in the battle against the dragons.

The Automata is an adventurer that fights using a new mechanic, a deck of "Memory" cards, providing a new and unique experience to the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Dorasure: Battle in the Imperial Capital Octavia
description:
Dorasure's third map expansion.

Setting the stage in humanity's largest city, the imperial capital Octavia, the "White-Silver Dragon Emperor" leads an army called the Dragon Legion upon the city.
This expansion can be played by up to 8 players.
This expansion bring a completely new set of rules compared to the base game. 
With all players cooperating and working together, we will drive back the White-Silver Dragon Emperor!

Warning — This expansion cannot be used in conjunction with new rules added in the expansions: 

     Dorasure: Fang
     Dorasure: Guardian of Magic Dragon
     Dorasure: Heritage

boardgame: The Season: Love & Drama in the Regency Era (2nd Edition)
description:
Step back into a world of timeless romance and captivating drama as another season begins. This second edition enchants with a whole new set of characters, delightful new events, exquisite new artwork, and enhanced expansion packs. Embrace the allure and sophistication of Regency society once more and let the season begin!

For the next few months, your journey has two delightful goals: to secure a (preferably) handsome and (also preferably) wealthy husband, and to elevate your family's social standing by increasing your reputation.

—description from the publisher

Love 2 Hate: Comics brings the world of superheroes and supervillains to the party! This 108 card expansion adds a slew of new sentence Starter and Finisher cards to the game. Mix them in with your standard Love 2 Hate cards or play the expansion on its own for a themed experience. Either way, you’ll soon be saying, “I love the way comics make my game super!”

It's a Family Feud Face Off! Parents vs Kids - who will have more correct answers?
Different cards for the kids and the parents.

160 Game cards, 3 "X" cards, and 1 instruction card included.

Who's the Boss?

It's time for kids and adults to go head-to-head in this entertaining trivia game.  Kids' or adults' questions - let the spinner decide!  Who'll be head of your house by the end of the game?

In this laugh out loud game players are paired up and asked to answer questions about each other.  Do you know your partners biggest fear?  How they would describe themselves? What their favorite possession is? Give the same answer as your partner to win a point.

—description from the back of the box
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Fill your week of spaceships and similar items, scoring the most points in the process. Each card has unique rules of play, with everything from an evil cube to a hyperspace web, as well as lots of interaction between card types. A short take-that strategic game. Each game comes with a deck for 2 players, with one deck needed per two people playing.

Each turn you draw a card or play a card until one player fills their week of space ships and similar items - then you add up the current scores to see who has won.

Fill your week of pirates, scoring the most points in the process. Lots of new cards, and the first of the Days series of games to include Dice - for blasting pirates with a Cannon card or duelling with an opposing pirate.  A short take-that strategic game.

Each turn you draw a card or play a card until one player fills their week of pirates - then you add up the current scores to see who has won.

One deck is needed per two people playing.

Hey party people!

Can you name the 5 best actors?
5 rock ballads?
How about... 5 signature fast food dishes?

This is the POP CULTURE Edition of your favorite game:
Compact and ready to party

Can you name 5?

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Fear of the Unknown: R'lyeh Rising
description: R'lyeh Rising: Fear of the Unknown edition updates the previous R'lyeh Rising edition to use the new Unknown Monsters. Instead of the previous cards for the monsters on the original core box, this edition doesn't have any monsters, you must have unknown monsters from the upcoming Fear of the Unknown core set or from other expansion packages, like Iron Maiden #1 and #3 to be able to play it.

Game similar to Heatway New Zealand Rally, but themed on Safari Rally Kenya. 
It's a typical roll and move where you move with a die and collect Points or Penalties along the board. 
Three different stages are held.

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Kivani first discovered her telepathic abilities as a child, when an alien ship crash-landed near her home. Befriending the wounded creature that crawled from the wreckage, Nessa, they became lifelong companions, connected at all times through their psionic circlets. With Kivani’s brains and Nessa’s brawn, they can take on anyone.

Components:

     Kivani Hero set: 1 Hero, 1 Health, 1 Minion, 3 Action cards.
     Kivani AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

Bloomburrow is a Magic: The Gathering expansion set in the idyllic, pastoral landscape of Valley in the world of Bloomburrow, where animalfolk live in peace and harmony—mostly. Attacks by Calamity Beasts, the great elemental predators that herald changing seasons and natural disasters, are normally rare, but lately they seem to be becoming more frequent. Something is threatening the natural balance of Valley, and a group of brave animalfolk heroes is banding together to find out why!

In addition to the usual array of randomized booster offerings, Bloomburrow's product lineup also includes four preconstructed Commander decks, as well as a Starter Kit with two 60-card decks.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Rogue Warriors: A Modern Warfare Skirmish Game
description:
Rogue Warriors provides rules for modern tabletop skirmishes with miniatures.  The game is miniature agnostic and sports figures in the 10mm to 40mm range, with players fielding 4-10 models each.  The rules are designed to be easy to learn and teach, while still providing strategic depth.

The game system uses alternating activations by individual figure.  Teams are assigned an expertise, which applies to all figures and individual team members are assigned their own specialty and weapon/equipment load out.  A mission builder is included, which allows for crafting of thousands of different scenarios via selection of various various prompts, locations, enemies, and objectives across 36 deployment maps.

Supplements provide support for Solo and Co-Op play, and predefined narrative campaigns.

Oishii is a delightful card-based tabletop game for lovers of Japanese cuisine and kawaii charm.

In Oishii, each player runs their own food stall, equipped with unique powers. Your job is to create tasty menu sets by pairing up the right food types, allowing you to serve big orders and earn more money. If the food sets you develop meet the guests' requirements, you will win them over.

At the end of the game, the player who has earned the most money from the guests wins.

—description from the publisher

Dorasure's 11th expansion. Two new adventurers (Alchemist and Treasure Hunter) join the fight against the dragon. Also introduces two new game elements: Ruins and Heritage.

Below the empire that we've previously ventured, the ruins and heritage of unknown civilisations lie forgotten. Uncovering the riddles of these ruins while repelling the dragon will be no easy task, but if you can get your hands on the heritage lying within they may change the odds into your favour in the battle against the dragon.

Dorasure's 10th expansion adds one new character (Tamer) and a new game mechanic in the form of three small companions. These small companions will provide some big help and become your powerful allies against the dragon.

This special expansion adds a new "Zombie" character to the characters "Dragon Fist" and "Land God" from the coterie magazine "TEARDORA" by Tera Akai, who is in charge of the main artwork. Go on adventures with characters with romantic abilities!

"Crazy Locust" is a party game for 2 to 6 players. A terrible curse has fallen on the forest village, turning everyone into insects! "What do we do now?" At that moment, a voice from the sky caught everyone's attention, "The one who best imitates the insect I describe can become human again. Gather in the square!" Confident in their ability to imitate, the gathered insects proclaimed, "Imitating insects is easy!" But is it really? The challenge is not to imitate just ordinary insects!

Place a game board, dice, and six cards with adjectives and insects depicted on theme. Each card has one adjective and one insect. The clue giver rolls the dice to determine which adjective and insect to imitate.
The other players must guess both the adjective and the insect being imitated. Correctly guessing both is a success.

On a peaceful summer day, otters gathered at the beach for a sandcastle building contest. The otter that builds the most beautiful sandcastle wins a delicious scallop. Each layer of the sandcastle shows the otters' passion and skill, but don't be too greedy! Sandcastles taller than 10 layers will collapse.

This game combines push-your-luck and memory mechanics. Players design the order in which cards are placed in a shared deck and try to predict when the wave card will appear, reading their opponents' psychology. Drawing a wave card can either trick the opponent or cause laughter if a player forgets their own placed wave card.

Here is a zebra filled with romance. Tired of his long single life, the zebra embarks on a journey with the hope of finding the perfect mate. However, it is not easy. Meeting a mate in the same region is difficult, and there are even hyenas disguised as zebras. Will the zebra be able to find a mate?

This game combines hand management and set collection, aiming to pair zebras from the same region to score points. Players draw one card from the opponent's hand, seeing only the back, and place it in front of them; they also place one from their hand. When zebras from the same region meet, they score points and a baby is born, triggering a combo system for delightful gameplay. However, hyenas lurk, requiring strategic choices.

Step into the ancient city of Aeminium, where you, esteemed architects, were chosen by Emperor Augustus to build a lighthouse dedicated to Mars, the Roman God of War. Your mission is to construct the magnificent Tower of Hercules, a structure destined to rival the legendary Lighthouse of Alexandria. But beware—the path to glory is fraught with challenges that demand both your strategic mind and your steady hands.

In this dexterity game, you and your fellow players will work together to build the tower using a set of 70 pillars, a tower base, a tower roof, 18 floors, and 32 cards. Each turn reveals a new card with a new challenge, forcing you to consider every aspect of your tower before constructing the next level. The goal is to complete the tower with minimum of 2(+1?) collapses. Strategic placement of pillars and skillful rebuilding after the inevitable collapses are the key to your success.

The Tower of Hercules awaits your skillful hands and strategic mind. Will you rise to the challenge and build a structure to rival the wonders of the ancient world?

—description from the publisher

Age of Change: Mining Operations is a lightweight, family-friendly board game that transports players to the early 20th century, where they take on the role of miners in search of valuable resources. Designed for 2-4 players, this game is easy to learn and provides around 30 minutes of engaging playtime, making it a great choice for family game nights.

Warning Age of Change: Mining Operations is a gateway game to introduce friends and families to the world of boardgames without scaring them with a massive rulebook. This game is more based on "push your luck" and learning some mechanics than deep strategic gameplay.

Objective:
The aim of the game is to collect the highest value of gold, silver, and coal by exploring mine shafts and strategically navigating through various challenges.

Components:
80 Mine Shaft Tiles: Create a unique mining landscape each game.
Cloth Draw Bag: Contains gold, silver, and coal counters.
Two 6-sided Dice: One with numbers, the other with colors and quake icons.

4 Player Pieces: Represent the miners.
1 Starting Tile: The entry point into the mine.
20 Gold, 30 Silver, and 50 Coal Counters: The resources players will be mining.

Setup:
Place the starting tile at the center of the play area.
Each player selects a playing piece and places it on the starting tile.
Prepare the draw bag with all the mineral counters inside.
Shuffle and place the mine shaft tiles face down in piles.

Gameplay:
Each turn, players roll two dice. One die determines the number of tiles they can move, while the other indicates where to place new minerals or triggers a mine collapse. Players explore the mine by placing new tiles, collecting valuable resources, and avoiding obstacles. Strategic decisions about movement and resource collection are key to winning the game.

Mineral Placement: Draw minerals from the bag and place them on tiles of the corresponding color rolled.

Mine Collapse: If a quake is rolled, a mine shaft collapses, adding a layer of challenge.

Movement: Move through the mine, adding new tiles and collecting resources along the way.

Collecting Minerals: After moving is done (or skipped if you so wish), collect minerals from the tile you end up on, according to the rules.

Scoring:
Coal: 1 point each
Silver: 5 points each
Gold: 10 points each

End of Game:
The game concludes when the draw-bag is empty, all mine shaft tiles are placed or no more tiles can be placed in the mine. Players tally their points based on the resources they've collected, and the player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

The Ferryman is a solo journey through a post-apocalyptic world where you'll explore zones, gather resources, enlist companions, embark on quests, confront dangers, and make impactful decisions for survival.

After enduring years of hardships in the wasteland, your weary body and fractured mind are now at a critical crossroads: either escape the territory or risk becoming just another hood ornament.

To win, you must gather enough resources and allies for the perilous exodus towards an uncertain, yet hopeful tomorrow.

—description from the publisher

A solitaire game where the player try to obtain a tower of drinks with cards, every card is a drink and this cards have multiple effects to resolve turns.

We have a deck of cards, and we will have beer, sangria and wine cards.

We must make a pyramid of drinks with 3 cards at the base, 2 on the second level and 1 card on the top level, that card must always be a sangria or wine card, never a beer card. You will win by completing this pyramid.

On each turn, reveal the top card of the deck and perform the effect you want or can according to the text. If the deck runs out and there are no discards, or if you cannot place cards in the pyramid twice in a row, you have lost the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Next Play Football Edition Card Game
description:
The Next Play Football Edition is a card game you play while watching a football game on TV. The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner. You earn points if you win the "Live Zone." The play in the football game determines the winner(s) of the “Live Zone.” The game is easy to learn, good for groups, and a fun strategic way to complement your football game.

—description from the publisher

In Maritiles, you and another player go head-to-head placing ships and sea-space, forming a shipyard that strategically favors your territory more than your opponents.

Earn points by cleverly positioning tiles so that your designated squares of ships and ocean become adjacent. Never will you have to shuffle tiles in this game, as they are all of the same design.

—description from the designer

An original card game designed by Classy Llama. Players daring enough to race their llamas in this treacherous journey should gather their endurance, strategy, and... spit! Will you be the first to get three llamas up the mountain?

This game was made as a promotional product by Classy Llama Studios, LLC

boardgame: One Deck Dungeon: Relics of the Forge
description:
One Deck Dungeon returns with a brand new full expansion! The Forge hungers for fuel, and it will reward your efforts with great power if you satisfy it.

During Setup, you'll choose a Relic to enter the dungeon with. This Relic will shape your strategies, as it will be your means for filling the Forge with fuel, and also a powerful way to expend it!

This expansion is stand-alone, and contains all new dungeons, heroes, monsters, traps.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Campaign Decks (Gamefound Edition)
description:
This expansion brings a very different way to play DC Super Heroes United, in the form of Campaigns! Each of these uses components from several different boxes to recreate iconic story arcs from the comics! Players must battle through a series of 4 or 5 games on average, facing events based on those comic storylines and living with the consequences of their actions.

The Crowdfunding Exclusive Campaign Decks set contains 8 different Campaigns, for a total of 85 cards:

- Reign of the Supermen
- Justice League vs Suicide Squad
- A Death in the Family
- The Long Halloween
- Throne of Atlantis
- Super Heroes United
- Green Lantern Rebirth
- Hush (Retail-Exclusive Core Box-based Campaign)

Hush is a unique campaign. As it's so centered around the revenge plot against Bruce Wayne, it is entirely played in Commander Solo Mode, with any Batman as the Main Hero and different members of the Bat-Family stepping in to help him as Supporting Heroes. Over the course of 7 games, Batman will face off against his notorious rogues' gallery (as well as the Poison Ivy-controlled Superman), unraveling the plot against him until he faces Hush himself.

Components from the Batman: Hush box are also used over the course of other Campaigns: Death in the Family, The Long Halloween, and Reign of the Supermen.

-description from crowdfunding campaign

The 'Stripey Gang' of six are at it again! The secret society 'Terror of the Stripeys' that you all are members of has heard that they have escaped the jail in Viirula and that each one of them has robbed five of the same item: Banks, cars, horses, watches, guns or gold jewels. Your mission is to travel around the six villages in the area, secretly turning around tiles in search of your assigned gang member and the missing loot stolen by him, then bring him back to the central jail in Viirula. Finding loot nets you money which is necessary for turning around the tiles, but ultimately the first player to turn in their assigned gang member wins. The memory element in this game is remembering which tiles have been previously rejected by most or all of the opposing players, and are therefore likely yours. Happy hunting!

(This rare Finnish game is likely from the 1970's.)

"Fun golf for the whole family. Tactics-Hazards-Excitement."

This Nelospelit publication likely from the 1980's (old Nelospelit logo) is yet another attempt at simulating golf as a dice rolling game. The board depicting a 9-hole golf course is rather large, and can be played twice for a full 18-hole course. The set of dice consists of six regular d6 - one for sideways deviation and five for distance - and one special d6 'putting die' with values from 1 to 3 which is used once reaching the green. Just as in regular golf, a player tries to reach the green and then put the ball into the hole with as few hits (dice rolls) as possible, while avoiding obstacles and hazards (water, sand, forest) which will either impede progress or cause penalties to the total amount of hits. The game can naturally be played solo, trying to reach the lowest possible par for each hole.

Beast Hunt is a pvp/coop fantasy RPG where you choose a Champion, build a party, battle Beasts, and defeat the King of Beasts!

Gameplay Highlights:

Choose Your Champion: Start the game by choosing a Champion who will lead your party.

Build Your Party: Use strategic card play to assemble a powerful party of Heroes to defeat Beasts and Lesser Beasts that impede your path to the King of Beasts

Dynamic Encounters: Engage in battles with Beasts and Lesser Beasts using Heroes, Curses, and Arcana, Ancient Arms and more to challenge the King of Beasts in a climactic showdown.

Staggering: Beasts don't go down easy. Unless luck is on your side, you'll have to battle a Beast more than once before it yields.

—description from the designer

Choo choo! Elmer the train is on the move. Each player gets a locomotive and then we start turning over carriage tiles one by one; if it's a cargo carriage add it to your train, if it's a passenger carriage you can turn over another, but if it's a conductor carriage your train is now complete and you're out of the game. The one with the longest train or most points win.

This small, simple 'tile game' is likely from the 1950's to 1960's.

Set in a 16th century naval route, in which players will have to overcome challenging sections to complete the route and win the game. High chance component. Initially the players will have a series of doubloons that they will have to exchange for cards of ships, crew or equipment in order to plan the route in the best possible way. Later on, they will get doubloons that they will exchange again.

At the start of the game, challenges will be the main obstacle to face. If the challenge is not met, there will be a penalty in ships or crew. In case of running out of cards of either of these two types (ship or crew), the player will be eliminated. On the other hand, if the challenge is met there will be a bonus/reward for the player.

The equipment cards will have 2 options (only one of them is eligible). They can be played at any time and will serve to manage the randomness of the challenges and make it easier to overcome them, while on the other hand, they can also be used to hinder or help other players in their turn to face the Challenge.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Ambientado en una ruta naval del Siglo XVI, en el que los jugadores tendrán que ir superando Tramos con desafíos para completar la ruta y ganar el juego. Componente alto en azar. Inicialmente los jugadores tendrán una serie de doblones que deberán de intercambiar por cartas de barcos, tripulación o equipamiento y así plantear la ruta de la mejor manera posible. Más adelante, volverán a obtener doblones que volverán a intercambiar.

Iniciado el juego, los desafíos serán el principal obstáculo al que enfrentarse. Si no se cumple el desafío habrá una penalización en barcos o tripulación. En caso de quedarse sin cartas de alguno de estos dos tipos (barco o tripulación), el jugador quedará eliminado. Por el contrario, si se supera el desafío habrá un bonus/recompensa para el jugador.

Las cartas de equipamiento tendrán 2 opciones (elegible solo una de ellas). Se podrán jugar en cualquier momento y servirán para gestionar el azar de los desafíos y facilitar superarlos, mientras que por otro lado, también se podrán usar para dificultar o ayudar a otros jugadores en su turno de enfrentamiento al Desafío.
 —description from the publisher

Day and night each bring wild bizarre-ities and stones of mysterious energy are falling from the sky. You’ll be fine.* Unsettled: Taawa is the 10th planet box for "Unsettled".

Taawa is a dichotomy of extremes. By day, a brutal furnace of rock and glass, by night… well, night is when all the life and the pretty happen. Night is also when the hailstorms—and the weird—happen.  Don’t get too comfortable with the strange, pretty skyrocks though—day comes fast on Taawa, so you have to stay on your toes to survive!

-description from the publisher

Tunnel your way through the labyrinthine caverns of a reality-bending contradiction of simultaneous worlds. Unsettled: Vo’qorr is the 11th planet box for "Unsettled".
    
On the surface, Vo’qorr isn’t much to write home about—but as the saying goes, bland is only crust deep. Beneath the surface is a secret. The wonderous labyrinth of caverns, tunnels and passages almost always… usually… sometimes… lead somewhere—but not always to somewhere here. It’s super easy to get lost in quantum-entangled catacombs—remember to bring space rope.

-description from the publisher

boardgame: トームくんのネコリリ (Tom's Cat Lily)
description:
A card battle where reading each other's minds is the key to escape!! Hide in buildings and trash cans, or sometimes move forward boldly, to successfully release cats without getting caught by the survey team!!!

Each player takes either the 8 Tom cards or the 8 Surveyor cards and take turns simultaneously playing 1 at a time to the table in a line. Each time comparing the situation depicted on the card. The Tom player is attempting to hide and release his cat and the surveyor is attempting to round both of them up. This continues a total of 3 possible rounds. The Tom player wins if they can release the cat twice. The Surveyor wins if they capture the cat once.

boardgame: 水浒传 天苍之星2 (Water Margin: Celestial Star 2)
description:
水浒传 天苍之星2 (Water Margin: Celestial Star 2) is a role-playing board game that evolves from the Monopoly-style mechanics of its predecessor into a narrative-driven RPG adventure.

In Celestial Star 2, players take on the role of a bandit leader and strategically explore the map to challenge the historical foes of the Liangshan heroes. As they progress, players can recruit legendary heroes from Liangshan to bolster their forces and make their way toward the ultimate adversary: Fang La.

Victory Condition
The game is won by the first player to defeat Fang La.

Game Components

     Player Cards: 5
     Hero Cards: 130
     Enemy Cards: 55
     Legendary Cards: 5
     Game Board: 58x58 cm
     Item Cards: 40
     Flag Markers
     Rulebook
     Four-Color Game Pieces and Dice

Mary Stuart, Queen of Scots is a set-collection card game set in 16th Century Scotland after the sudden death of King James V. Factions are converging to seize power to gain fame and fortune by controlling the young queen, the Royal Court, Scotland's estates and their resources.

Each player assumes the role of a powerful Lord or Lady, attempting to make their faction the richest and most influential in Scotland. Players manage Scottish estates and diplomatic relationships with France and England, produce resources and create wealth, and attend the Scottish Royal Court to influence and control state actors and events from Mary's life. The game is played in one or two Acts which cover specific timelines in Mary's life.

The goal is to have the most points at the end of the game. Players accumulate points for their estates and resources, for their influence and control over the characters of the Royal Court, and by being the first to complete any of an Act's four events. Wealth is needed to attend the Royal Court, and estates are needed to create wealth. Players must balance developing their estates with influencing the Royal Court while adjusting to the actions of fellow nobility.

boardgame: Zombicide: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Bebop & Rocksteady
description: Two abominations that expand the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle expansion for Zombicide: White Death. When they're on the board on their own, you'll find their easier to defeat than traditional abominations. But, together, they're an unstoppable duo.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

boardgame: The Shivers: Dog & Cat Character Pack
description:
The same playable pets that came in the Deluxe version, you'll enjoy using their catchphrases "woof" and "meow"!

Ocean the Cat has the Abilities STALKING (can chase down and catch small scurrying pests), LEAPING (can jump up to great heights). Can only carry 1 item. Fears are big dogs, cucumbers, bananas, umbrellas, ghosts.

Shep the Dog has the Abilities SENSES DANGER (general awareness of nearby foes or traps), STRONG BITE (can be used on minor foes, except in Endgame). Only can carry 1 item. Fears are loud noises, thunder and lightning.

Posers is a party game that tests your ability to fake it till you make it. Each round, you draw a card that tells you if you're a Normie or a Poser.
Normies will all have the same word written on their cards, which they have to dramatize by striking a pose.
If you're a Poser, that's where it gets interesting. Your card is blank, so you don't know what the word of the round is. Thankfully, your high level of charisma, confidence, and persuasion skills will enable you to convince the Normies that you know exactly what you're doing.
If you manage to get away with it, you'll earn a bunch of points. If you get discovered, the Normie who blows your cover will get them instead.
Will you take advantage of the Normies' naivety for your own gain, or will you be crowned the Poser you are?

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

—user summary

Add lieutenant colonel Georges, lieutenant Maurice Boyau and sous lieutenant Jean Bignon in the Nieuport 24 to your cockpit.

Stats: hull 19, engin 6, stability b, wings 16, mg 1, hek 4, peak high 4, fuel 35, except Jean Bignon 45

boardgame: Privacy: Scharf wie Chili – Promo Pack
description: This is a promo pack you can add to your normal Scharf and Chili privacy game.

Amidst the lingering shadows of the plague, the Empire seeks to transcend mere survival and strike at the heart of the epidemic itself. The Empire's resolve is unwavering as it sponsors the study and research of the very plague that once threatened its existence.

Embracing innovation and collaboration, the guilds of Thokaia have risen to the occasion, offering their expertise and resources to aid in this monumental endeavor. Together, we delve deep into the mysteries of the plague, unraveling its secrets and discovering weaknesses to exploit.

As the Empire's influence expands, so too does our understanding of the plague's intricate mechanisms. With each breakthrough, we inch closer to our ultimate goal: eradicating the plague once and for all. But beware, for the plague is a cunning adversary, evolving and adapting to our every move.

In Pest - Guilds of Thokaia, players will employ the help of the guilds in order to save the empire from peril. On your turn, you can pay influence to assign people and your Envoy in a particular guild in the Guildhall. If you do so, you will gain certain bonuses as well as a standing with the guild. Assigning people in the Guildhall also enables you to draw a guild cards, which have powerful effects that can be used during your turn, giving you the ability to perform different combos.

The Clusters module of the expansion adds another way that the Pest disease will spread on the board. Players will be able to track down the clusters and eradicate them with their plague doctors. Player will gain more points as they eradicate more clusters.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 三國志之武將瘋雲錄 (Three Kingdoms: Chronicles of Warlords)
description:
三國志之武將瘋雲錄 (Three Kingdoms: Chronicles of Warlords) is a classic board game that combines elements of card games and the traditional Monopoly-style gameplay, set in the historical period of the Three Kingdoms. This game was first published in the early 1990s by Tongcai Art Development in Taiwan and later introduced to Mainland China.

Game Components


     Game Map: Features locations and events inspired by the Three Kingdoms period.
     54 Character Cards: Depict famous generals from the Three Kingdoms with humorous illustrations and attributes for attack and defense.
     18 Emergency Cards: Represent unexpected events during gameplay.
     18 Strategy Cards: Provide strategic advantages to players.
     40 City Cards: Represent various cities players can control.
     25 Territory Ownership Deeds: Indicate ownership of different territories.
     5 Character Tokens: Used to represent players on the board.
     1 Die: Used for movement and actions on the board.
     Score Tokens: Used to keep track of players' points.


Gameplay Mechanics

Players take turns rolling a die to move around the board, landing on different spaces that trigger events or battles. Players use their character cards to influence these events, leveraging their attack and defense attributes. The goal is to accumulate wealth and power by controlling territories, similar to Monopoly, but with a strategic layer added by the character cards and other game elements.
