boardgame: 萌幻西游记 (Fantasy Journey to the West)
description:
萌幻西游记 (Fantasy Journey to the West) is a board game combining elements of Monopoly and Journey to the West.

Players move across a game board, acquiring property deeds and battling monsters. Similar to Monopoly, players collect and trade property, earn fees, and draw event cards that influence the game. When landing on monster spaces, players must battle using dice rolls to determine outcomes.

The game ends

     when the total number of monsters captured by all players reaches a certain amount
     or when one player goes bankrupt.


The player with the highest score wins.

Components

     1 Game Board (60cm x 40cm)
     6 Player Pieces
     8 Character Cards
     40 Monster Cards
     20 Property Deeds
     60 Event Cards
     1 Die
     Money Tokens
     Rulebook

Autumndale Bay has asked you to design a new city zoo. Lay out the zoo’s habitats, populate them with diverse animals, build attractions, and bring in lots of visitors. If you build the zoo that best meets the city’s needs, you win. || Features tile-laying, animeeples, drafting, variable objectives, and simultaneous play for 1-4 players!

In Zoo-ography, players will draft a zoo tile, take an action, and build their zoo. Players have to balance building different habitats to support the animals available while also building sufficient attractions to keep guests engaged.

Zoo-ography Extended Edition comes with:

Zoo-ography: 2nd Edition
Zoo-ography: Connections 2nd Edition
Zoo-ography: World Exhibits (NEW EXPANSION)
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
A visiting warlock is teaching a few courses this year at the university. It is a big deal since after two semesters, the famous warlock will choose one student as an apprentice. Do you have what it takes to become the warlock’s apprentice?

The game loop remains the same in Warlock’s Apprentice: you complete courses in two semesters, manage your sanity, and raise study buddies for bonuses. The expansion brings into play 3 new courses and 1 Tired card. These course cards function similarly to the rest of the cards but have some new twists. Newly, the Warlock courses cost you both sanity and thesis progression. The warlock unveils such mysteries that you need to reconsider and rewrite a chapter in your thesis. Furthermore, to get a bonus from a Warlock course, only a Tired card can be tucked under the completed course. Before, feeling tired was a hindrance to your studies but now, the warlock can feast on your despair and restore your sanity or give you an extra credit as a token of gratitude.

—description from the designer

Koala Kart 2nd Edition lets you race against each other on a crazy track across Australia or America. Along the way, you encounter all kinds of obstacles, but you can also get in your opponents' way. Whoever completes 3 complete laps first is the winner of the race, but anything can happen in the meantime. Are you ready for the start?

Players use two dice, one with 1-6 pips and one with a multiplier (or a STOP!) to move forward in different paces. Some spots on the tracks give you action cards, or make you turn around and go backwards on your next turn!
Action cards can give you various one-time benefits, like dropping a branch or fence on the track to hinder your opponents, or a fishing rod to pull an opponent in front of you, back to your space.
There's even a card that lets you jump over a broken bridge, cutting off a part of the track.

Roll high and play your cards wisely to beat your opponents in this high-paced racing game!

This differs game with the first edition:
Double sided board (Australia and America)
Trade action cards
New koala racers with upgrades

A strategy game that can be played by two or three players. It consists of a triangular board and three sets of seven pieces in black, white and gray.

The pieces are placed on the starting triangles opposite the corner (home triangle) with the same color. The object of the game is to place one of your pieces in the corresponding corner.

The two-player game has an interesting twist in that the gray stones are neutral and can be moved by either player, but they can be used to capture opposing stones.

A stone is eliminated if it is surrounded by two of your stones (in the three-player variant) or by two of your stones, two neutral stones, or by one of your stones and one neutral stone (in the two-player variant).

In addition, no more than four checkers of the same color can be aligned next to each other.

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: War of Light
description:
Since the dawn of the universe, entities representing the various emotions have guided and influenced the galaxy. Anger, Greed, Fear, Will, Hope, Compassion, and Love. Eventually, beings learned to tap into their power, forging Lanterns and Power Rings to wield incredible abilities. Thus, the various Lantern Corps were created. Initially, the Green Lanterns maintained order in the universe, but as other Lantern Corps rose, conflict was inevitable. Now, in the blackest of nights, Death itself has risen to conquer all, but Life also rises to meet it. The War of Light begins!

This expansion brings all of the various Lantern Corps together for the ultimate battle across the whole emotional spectrum. These incredibly varied characters fill the roles of Heroes, Villains, or anything in between. They each wield the power of their emotional color in a different way: heroically, selfishly, altruistically, or downright cruelly. Furthermore, the figure of each character features amazing clear plastic pieces in all 9 different colors wielded by these Lanterns, making for an awesome display. Put on your Lantern Ring and wear your emotions on your sleeve as you light up the universe!

—description from the publisher

In Yogi, players draw cards and either have to place them on their bodies or place them on the table, but follow instructions on what to do with some of their body parts. 
A player who drops one of the cards they are required to hold or cannot follow the instructions of yet another card, is eliminated. The last remaining player wins the game.

This second edition of Yogi offers many new challenges involving sounds and movements.

At the height of the Victorian orchid craze, botanists would race across all parts of the globe to find the latest and hottest in-demand orchids.

Orchid obsession adds new shared face up specimen cards. It's a race to see who can acquire them first!

“Remember the Alamo!”

Dive into history and heroism with The Alamo 1836, a solitaire book wargame that lets you experience the last stand of the Texian Militia at the Alamo Mission. Relive the courageous stand of 250 Texians led by Davy Crocket, James Bowie, and William Travis as they defend The Alamo against a professional Mexican Army numbering around 3,000 soldiers.
On the morning of March 6, 1836 the Mexican Army under General Santana’s command began their final assault. Texan sharpshooters, including the legendary Davy Crockett, took their toll on the attacking force, picking off officers and disrupting formations. Hand-to-hand combat erupted as Mexican soldiers swarmed over the southern wall. The Texians, fighting for their lives and their fledgling republic, used muskets, knives, and even cannons loaded with scrap metal to repel the invaders. The fighting was fierce and unrelenting.
The Texians, though outnumbered and outgunned, fought with courage and doomed desperation. However, as the battle progressed, the tide turned. Mexican soldiers, in wave after wave, breached the Alamo's defenses, pushing the Texians back into the interior of the compound. The fighting devolved into a brutal close-quarters struggle, with every doorway and building becoming a desperate battleground. By mid-morning, overwhelmed and surrounded, the last Texian defenders perished. Santa Anna, in a brutal display of power, ordered the execution of any survivors, including those who had surrendered. Can you hold on to The Alamo?

The "Battlegrounds" series from Art of Wargames brings iconic pre-WWI battles to life through streamlined, fast-paced book wargames. No need to mount maps and counters; all you need is an erasable marker and some dice to command your troops and relive the actions of these legendary conflicts.

Game Features
Scale – Tactical
Map – 250 yards
Units –Texians 250/Mexican Army 3,000.
Turns – 2 Days
Playing Time – 20-30 Minutes
Players – One (Solitaire Play)
Specific “Texian” Leader Actions
Easy to Learn and Play
Includes a detailed “How-to-Play”
Sets up in Seconds
No Maps or Counters to Mount
REQUIERED- erasable marker, and 4xd6 & 1xd10 dice or dice App.

—description from the designer

description:
Grand Tourney is a game that mixes worker-placement elements with card driven combat. It explores a piece of the Soulshards universe in which each player controls a character that has been invited to participate in a grand battle tournament.

During gameplay, players will make the most of their limited amount of turns by carefully placing characters and mercenaries. After the last day of preparation, the characters will fight until only one remains.

Grand Tourney combat features a card driven combat system, where players balance attacking, defending, and refreshing their hand. Each action has its own timing to consider, which further diversifies combat.

Soulshards is a fantasy setting focusing on the ups and downs throughout the history of the Keridwyn Empire. Grand Tourney takes place during one of the ‘ups’, a celebration of 100 years spent in relative peace following a brutal civil war. New characters will add their marks to the world just as previous characters have left theirs. Although each game and novel in the setting adds to the tapestry, no previous familiarity is required.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 四面楚歌 (Besieged on All Sides)
description:
Besieged on All Sides is a card-driven strategical game on the mid-late stages of the Chu-Han Contention. Players take the role of major factions of the time, Xiang-Yu, Liu-Bang, Han-Xin and other states. Players will need to try to pursuit their own agendas and the one with the highest score will be the victor who can shape the future of China.

Soon after the death of Qin Shi Huang, first Emperor of China, in 210 BCE, massive peasant uprising and rebellions from former six states quickly crippled the Qin-dynasty. In only a few years, the Qin Empire has fallen, and Xiang-Yu, the commander of Chu-rebel, take over as the hegemon and divided the former Empire into 18 Kingdoms to be ruled by other rebel leaders. This creates dissatisfaction among other rebels, and Liu-Bang, leader of Han, take the chance to strike at Xiang-Yu. To complicate the situation further, after his various victories, Han-Xin, the great general of Han starts to have other thoughts and hope to become an independent power. And the other states, while dissatisfied with Xiang-Yu, are sitting on the fences and hoping for a more advantages bargain before fully commit their strengths.

Each card in this game represents an iconic character of that time, from generals, strategists to leaders and kings. Each card not only allow the player to conduct the relevant actions when played, but also provides extra abilities which represents that character’s unique capabilities and their interpersonal relationships. This allows the 4 factions have their historical strength and weakness to be portrayed clearly:

    Xiang-Yu has some of the strongest military strengths, with Xiang-Yu himself been able to remove enemy unit before a battle is resolved. 
    Liu-Bang have various ability to conduct lobbying to win-over more characters for his use. Also is the only faction who have a steady stream of income from off-map areas.
    Han-Xin, on the other hand, can go head-to-head with Xiang-Yu militarily, but will need to try win over the internal-power struggle by gaining more prestige and wealth.
    The final faction: various other states, need to carefully place their position between Chu and Han, preserving their strength and hoard wealth to preserve their independence from the two major powers.


Combat in the game is simple and dice-less, other than the number of units, card effect and leader’s combat bonus are the telling factor. Players could also support their forces if adjacent regions are under their control, requiring careful planning and calculation from the players. While controlling region, hoarding wealth and military power all counts to victory points, players are also given objective cards. Fulfilling these cards not only provide VP, but also can resulting in other powerful effects, and even upgrading player’s character cards to a stronger version.

Components:
1 Mounted Map (594x420mm)
18 General token (18x18mm)
55 Game markers (13x13mm)
60 wooden cubes
4 Player panel
4 Player aids
71 cards
1 Rulebook

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: History: Warring States Period of China

description:
Are you ready to explore the fascinating world of pollination? Play flower cards into your garden, and use different color combos to attract the six different types of pollinators.

Each pollinator is active either at day or night, and can only be attracted during that time. Each flower is either Annual or Perennial: Annual flower cards must be discarded each time they are used, while Perennials stay, turn after turn.

Action cards can be used to help yourself or harm your opponents. Protect yourself against Pesticides with an Ecological Restoration card, or recover discards with a Compost card. Steal pollinators from other players by creating a more attractive garden. But don’t make them too angry, or they might hit you with a Heat Wave or Cold Front!

Gain bonus biodiversity points by collecting at least four pollinators, or all six colors of flowers.

—description from the publisher

Destination Antwerp is a 2020 classic work of the famous Japanese small wargame magazine "BONSAI". The game takes the Ardennes Offensive launched by the German army in December 1944 as the background and focuses on the armored formations as the protagonists.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Unlike the original release the Chinese edition also contains a variant of the game set in a medieval fantasy setting - including a different map, counters and some rules changes.

boardgame: Root: Jeu de base + La Rivière
description:
This French edition of the root game is a special edition which includes the base game and its expansion Riverfolk.

Root is a war adventure game in which players take on the role of one of four factions and fight to become the most powerful ruler of the vast Forest.

Root is a game that is played and told like a story. Players drive the narrative, and the differences between each role create a significant level of interaction and gameplay. 
Are you ready to fight for control of this vast wilderness?

PROGRESS OF THE GAME
The game has the particularity of taking place in several rounds, themselves divided into 3 very distinct phases:
Dawn
The day
Twilight
During his turn, the player completes these three phases in order. Their turn then ends and the player to their left begins theirs. The three
the phases are different depending on the faction played.
 A game can end in two different ways:
A player scores 30 victory points
A player complicit in a Domination card
Each faction scores points in its own way:

The Marquise de Chat wants to exploit the Forest and use its abundant resources to fuel her economic and military empire. She scores points by constructing buildings in the Forest.
The Canopy Dynasties seek to regain the glory of their former aristocracy and take back the Forest from the Marquise. They score points each turn by building and protecting perches in the Forest.
The Forest Alliance wishes to unite the people of the Forest to rise against oppressors. She scores points by spreading sympathy for their cause throughout the Forest.
The Wanderer seeks fame in the midst of this burgeoning conflict. He scores points by completing quests for the denizens of the Forest and helping or hindering other factions.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Four brand-new monster ambush cards add even more exciting gameplay variety in Cartographers or Cartographers Heroes! These ambush cards feature new monster shapes and abilities.

Just shuffle them with your existing ambush cards and you're ready to play!

Includes: 4 ambush cards (Naga Shock, Fungoid Outbreak, Treefolk Torment, Basilisk Stare)
