boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Conqueror Pack
description:
Crowdfunding Exclusive Starro and Batmobile, including Starro's oversized Villain figure and Batmobile oversized Support figure, 1 Villain dashboard, 12 Master Plan cards, 6 Threat cards, 12 Mind Control cards, Support card, and Equipment card!

The mighty Starro the Conqueror stomps its way to conquer everything around it, not necessarily by destroying everything, but by spreading its spores and taking control of everyone’s minds. It causes massive panic in the population, adding endangered Civilians all over the place, and spreading his spores which fills the game with Thugs. When Starro lands on one of its Threats, it releases even more spores, adding Crisis tokens to all nearby Locations. Removing these Threats will take not just heroics, but also violence. When Starro BAMs, it uses its hive mind to attack nearby Heroes using not only the Thugs and Crisis tokens in their Locations, but even mind-controlled Civilians!

When Starro BAMs, it also gives a Mind Control card to each Hero in its Location, attaching one of its spores to the Hero’s face (but the player can just keep the card in front of them). Mind Control cards may have several different effects, like forcing the Hero to play cards randomly, depriving them of their Special Effects, or even forcing them to Attack other Heroes! Heroes will need to help each other to remove the spores from their faces, using Heroic Actions to discard the Mind Control card of another Hero in their Location. If they fail to do that, Heroes may accumulate several Mind Control cards, compounding their effects, and once they have 3 cards they are immediately removed from the game. The remaining Heroes keep fighting until they either put an end to Starro or they all succumb to its control.

Starro’s figure stands 16cm tall and 15cm wide.

The Batmobile is used as a Support Character, similar to those in the Metropolis and Gotham City expansions and can be easily added to any game, with its own special Setup instructions. It is not controlled by any player, having its own rules for how it may be moved around and what advantage it grants.

When using the Batmobile, it should start on the Batcave Location or the Heroes Starting Location, and 1 or 2 random cards are removed from the middle of the Master Plan deck (depending on whether Batman is in the game). Any Hero in the Batmobile’s Location may use it to bring another Hero with them when they move. If the Batmobile’s Locations Overflows, however, it is removed from the game.

The Batmobile can also be used as an Equipment by Batman or Robin, granting them a free Move or a safe place to protect themselves from an attack. It can be recharged simply by calling the Batmobile to your Location.

The Batmobile figure is 10cm long and 5cm wide.

—description from publisher

Alternative R6 dashboards given as promos at Gen Con 2024. One for each player color.

The dashboards are gravel tires with 1 coast die and 4x /!\ limit on gravel tiles.

The Spell-and-Learn Game for Kids and Adults!

Different tiers for different spelling abilities (Bear or Anemone).

Points for knowing the word and points for picking the correct spelling. Fewer points for the top tier (graded on a curve).

Players earn points for naming the image and spelling it correctly. If they cant spell the word correctly, they can earn points for choosing from three options, only one of which is spelled correctly.
There's the option of playing decks with more challenging words, so players of all capabilities can compete against one another and everyone has a chance to win.
Includes an instruction sheet and 208-full color cards.
200 spelling cards and 8 spellchecker cards. For 2-8 players
Fun for Everyone: Spellchecked is ideal for anyone ages 8 and over, and it can be played with groups of 2–8 players. Play it with third graders or sixth graders. Play it with friends or aunts or grandparents. The more advanced the level, the tougher the words are to spell and the fewer points correct answers are worth. In this way, Spellchecked gives every player a chance to show what they know and to win.

Promo card with stats adding the new player character, Henrietta, to the game.
This card was released at Gencon 2024!

Henrietta's advanced side has:
SUMMON: Draw one additional card
SLING: Send one of your cards to another ritual circle
SWITCH: Swap the location of any two cards
GAMER: Choose another kitty; they take one card of their choice from your circle and place it anywhere in their circle, and you perform the spell under their spell token

The festival of explosions brings a new chaotic and explosive energy to the classic Kingless formula. We’ve focused on new fun mechanics, all tying into our crazy festival theme to elevate everything you loved about Kingless to the next level.

The new stand-alone sequel adds a new card type; attachments, you can place attachment cards on dwarfs and items to change their influence and add gaming-winning effects. You can use attachment cards to strengthen your board or to weaken your opponents.

We have also added 4 new actions; game, duel, swipe, and refresh. These actions give our players new and exciting ways to win the crown and most importantly, to make your friends the fool.

—description from the publisher

In Guards & Goblins, you are the last line of defense against a relentless goblin horde threatening to destroy the villages.

With the fate of innocent villagers hanging in the balance, you must deploy your fearless guards with precision and cunning. As you lay your cards on the table, strategize and position your guards to form an impenetrable shield, thwarting the goblins' every move.

Can you outsmart the horde and save the villages from certain doom? The battle awaits!

—description from the publisher

In King's Decree, you are the sovereign ruler, navigating the treacherous waters of leadership. Every card you play represents a pivotal decision that can either elevate your kingdom to greatness or lead to unforeseen consequences.

Play your cards cleverly to collect sets, fulfill your royal missions, and keep the impact of your inevitable bad decrees to a minimum. Balance the prosperity of your people with the harsh realities of rule, and strive to build a legacy that will be remembered for ages.

Will your reign be celebrated or cursed? The crown awaits your choices. Achieve the highest score possible and secure your place in history.

—description from the publisher

The Republic of Rome Remastered is a game of negotiation, power and betrayal that simulates the 250 years of history between the First Punic War (264 B.C.) and the Civil War that followed the assassination of Julius Caesar (44 B.C.)

Each player will lead a political faction which includes senators from the most prominent families in the Republic. Players will compete against each other for government positions, economic concessions, control of the armies, and to attract new members to their faction, all with the ultimate goal of becoming the dominant political faction in Rome. Players will not only have to contend with each other, but also with the game itself, as Rome will be in constant danger. All players will lose the game if wars are not properly managed, if discontent turns into popular revolt, or if the Republic goes bankrupt. At the same time, players will have to cooperate closely to push through laws to manage internal politics and preserve social peace, declare wars to expand the borders of the Republic, or confront enemies who seek to destroy Rome. They will also have to compromise to attend to the recreational and religious needs of the people, purge the corrupt from office, and much, much more.

Thus, in order to win the game, players will need to balance service to Rome with personal gain. Usually, the faction with the most total influence will win the game, if Rome prevails. However, if a single senator stands out individually, he may aspire to become Dictator for life and achieve victory for his faction. The most dramatic and difficult path to victory is to revolt against the senate to seize power by force of arms.

This is an all-new edition that builds on the original and adds many new features that will make The Republic of Rome a fresh experience both for old and new players. These are the most relevant new features in The Republic of Rome Remastered:

    All the typos and inconsistencies in the previous edition have been corrected, and some rules that slowed down or overcomplicated gameplay have been streamlined.
    The game’s graphic design has been entirely reworked, including new illustrations for all the game components.
    For the first time, miniature busts are included to represent the main political offices in the game, while the option of using tokens is maintained for those who prefer them.
    The board has been fully redesigned to reduce the demand for table space, and all tables will be moved to a Play Aid for the Herald of the senate.
    New Event cards have been added and some of the existing ones have been modified. Additionally, a new Fate deck has been introduced to help balance Event results.
    The influence of games and religious affairs in the Republic has been emphasized.
    The rules have been completely rewritten and numerous examples have been added to facilitate learning and playing the game. In addition, a Playbook has been included with extended examples of play.
    A set of new optional rules has been developed to mitigate the influence of luck during the game.


We are also working on a new solitaire mode to streamline solo gameplay and on the introduction of improved bots for 2- and 3-player games. All this and much more awaits you in The Republic of Rome Remastered!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Star Wars: Power of the Sith
description:
Stop the attack of the dark force! The game requires players to work together - if even one enemy squad breaks into the base of the Resistance, all players lose! The game also allows for 1 player play!

Players control rebel units. Their goal is to defend the base from attacks by First Order troops. As long as enemy units capture the fortress, players lose. If they defend the base, the one who destroyed the most attackers will be the winner.

Each player has character cards from the last Star Wars movie in their hand - The Force Awakens. These are used by players to destroy attacking units. To destroy a unit, the player must play a certain number of cards in a suit identical to the attacking unit and its attacking power. At the beginning of the turn, the player can draw any number of cards, but if he draws an explosion card, his turn ends and he must also discard all the cards obtained in the round.

The game ends when players destroy all First Order units, or when one of its units infiltrates the Rebel base.

The fun of the game is precisely in deciding whether to risk it and draw another card with the risk of losing all of them, or settle for the ones I have won. And tactics, when it pays to cooperate and when to play each other... Because even though everyone can lose, only one :) wins

—description from the publisher

The Darwin's Journey: Komodo mini-expansion introduces a new element to the museum: twenty new bonus tiles that replace the existing bonus coins.

Players can earn these new rewards by delivering specimens. After delivering a specimen, players will place a corresponding bonus tile in another bonus display. Additionally, players can access these bonus displays by researching the Komodo Dragon, which moves across the islands based on the specimens delivered.

—description from the publisher

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

William Harper, an established CFO, was murdered on New Year's Eve! Who could have done it? Players will navigate through his life and relationships, and through information surrounding his death, and to finally catch the killer!

Players will need to have good attention to detail, analytical mind and detective skills. There will be clues and evidence that will be connected throughout the game, and will definitely test players' deductive skills.

Publisher rated this game as medium difficulty. The game contains 5 suspects, 20+ pieces of evidence and 4 objectives to complete.

Turboprší is a reimplementation of the game Prší, a Czech variant of the fast-paced card game Mau-mau, also known as Pony in Europe.

It's played according to similar rules (card to card with the same number or symbol), but beware - everyone plays at the same time and throws to several decks at once. The first player to get rid of all the cards wins. A fun turbo game awaits you, where observation and quick judgement are the most important. Turboprší is a versatile game that the whole family can enjoy - from school kids and older children to parents and grandparents. Turboprší improves perceptual motor skills and encourages quick recognition of numbers and the number of symbols on the card.

—description from the publisher

One additional card to add to your Animal market deck to increase variety in your games: the Hornbill.

-description from the campaign

boardgame: Tomb Raider: The Crypt of Chronos
description:
Tomb Raider: The Crypt of Chronos is more than just a board game: this is a full Tomb Raider experience!  Lara Croft must embark on a journey to the elusive island of Kairos in search of an artifact said to hold the reins of time. She will have to navigate treacherous terrain, evade cunning traps, and confront an enemy from her past.

Tomb Raider: The Crypt of Chronos is a single-player adventure game. There are 2 different ways to play:
Adventure book - A structured adventure split into 20-60 minute missions.
Island campaign - A randomized complete campaign, lasting around 3 hours.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Pursuit of John Wilkes Booth
description:
The sixteenth President of the United States has been shot at Ford’s Theatre in Washington, DC, and Abraham Lincoln’s near lifeless body is carried to the Peterson Boarding House across the street. Secretary-of-War Stanton springs into action. Well-known actor, John Wilkes Booth, has been identified as the culprit and earns his place as the most reviled villain in US history. Meanwhile, blocks away, Secretary-of-State Seward and his family have been savagely attacked by Booth’s accomplice, Lewis Powell. Soon other accomplices are identified, and the pursuit of Booth and his cronies begins in earnest.

In The Pursuit of John Wilkes Booth, players control Detectives searching for the assassin. Historically, Union authorities did not know where Booth might head since it would be reasonable to expect that Booth might go to Canada, the less-populated West, or to a Northern or Southern port with an eventual goal of reaching Europe, Mexico, or South America. As Detectives in the game, players must process Leads for Clues and uncover Booth’s Trail as he attempts his escape. Players may call on Stanton to aid them in their task. Detectives must eventually complete a successful Search and Raid on Booth’s exact location and win the game by killing or capturing him.

—description from the publisher

Backstabbers Advanced expansion bring new powers to characters that you already know.
Replace the tradicional characters with the advanced ones to a new level of caos.

Don't trust anyone, not even THE GAME!
With the Constitution expansion, now you have to survive while eliminating the other players!
The game starts to play against you using new laws that are added to change temporarily the rules.

Assault on Ancient Japan: Kyüshü 1945 is a game that recreates the first operation of the planned invasion of Japan by the United States, specifically, the southern part of the island of Kyüshü, codenamed "Olympic".

This should have begun on November 1, 1945 as the final act of the Pacific campaign and World War II, had it not been for the surrender of Japan through the dropping of atomic bombs and the declaration of war by the Soviet Union on the Empire of the Rising Sun.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Six centuries have passed since the Great War shattered the once-united kingdoms of Verne. Now, adventurers from distant shores arrive in a fractured land filled with opportunity, danger, and ambition. Some seek to rebuild… others to rule.

The Lost Lands of Verne is a game of deals, duels, and dominion for 1 to 6 players, blending the strategic depth of classic fantasy RPGs with the accessibility of beloved family board games. Players take on the roles of bold adventurers vying to reclaim Verne by exploring dangerous lands, collecting powerful items, constructing mighty castles, and clashing in thrilling battles.

During the game, you’ll:

Rule shires and unite kingdoms to grow your influence.

Wield items, spells, and equipment to outwit rivals and gain the upper hand in combat.

Encounter monsters, bosses, and hidden dangers as you journey through Verne.

Form alliances—or betray them—as you negotiate, trade, and scheme your way to victory.

Your ultimate goal is to earn Quest Points through exploration, combat, and control. Whether through diplomacy, might, or cunning, the first adventurer to get 4 Quest Points takes the throne and reshapes the fate of Verne.

With easy-to-learn mechanics, dynamic player interaction, and endless replayability, The Lost Lands of Verne is perfect for both newcomers to fantasy gaming and seasoned veterans alike.

-description from the creators

boardgame: Pizza Pizza Yum Yum: Spicy Jalapeños
description:
Your pizza shop is getting some buzz and you need to make the most of the publicity. Time to kick it up a notch with a spicy new menu!

Spicy Jalapeños 6-card expansion introduces a new ingredient, jalapeños. As in the base game, players must use their 6-card hands to create the best pair of pizzas they can manage. However, each of the expansion cards also includes a new specialty topping combination. These flexible scoring conditions reward players who can work together to maximize their points.

A board game about political intrigue in renaissance Florence.

Players take the roles of political parties and compete for the seats in the Senate.

The seats are bound to six political issues which progress in their value over the course of the game. The player who dominates the most important ones wins.

Player draft cards from a deck containing characters and action cards and then perform four actions with which to play them, receive money, gather followers or attack their opponents.

At the end of each turn, a political agenda needs to be resolved and players secretly vote on their desired outcomes.

Deals and collaboration are equally important for this as lies and deception at the right time.

After five turns (terms of office) the game ends and the victory points are calculated.

POLITICO offers:


     drafting cards
     compelling card play
     resource management
     territory control (sort of)
     strong emphasis on communication and interaction
     beautiful, all human artwork from painters of the era


—description from the designer

GOTA is a two-player abstract strategy game played on a hexagonal board. Players use stones in four colors: Orange and Teal for one player, Black and White for the other.

Objective:
Connect the most stones in one of your colors by the end of the game.

Setup:
1. Start with an empty board.
2. Players take turns placing one stone each until four stones are on the board.
3. Decide who goes first.

Gameplay:
On your turn:
1. Move one of your stones N steps in a straight line to an empty cell.
2. Place a stone of your other color N steps away from the moved stone, also ending on an empty cell.

Notes:
- Stones and board edges block movement.
- The game ends when no more moves are possible.
- The player with the largest connected group of stones in one color wins.

-description from designer

Take on the role of a Forest Folk, carefully crafting the perfect woodland, alongside the ideal copse, to inspire those seeking enlightenment.  Use the powers that surround you to shape & manipulate the forest.  Guide your pilgrims to the safe sanctuary of your copse.  The player who most closely represents the wishes of their pilgrims will become the master of the forest.

During each turn of Forest Folk, you will be playing cards to two separate locations.

The Forest - this area is shared by all players & can be manipulated by all players.  At the end of the game, you will score your pilgrim cards based on the nature of the forest.

The Copse - This is each player's personal area.  Only you will be able to play cards to & manipulate your Copse.  At the end of the game, you will have exactly 5 cards in your Copse.  You will score double points from your pilgrim cards in your Copse area.

The game features artwork by the incredible Kate Davies, who brings the Forest Folk alive. 
 There are 8 powers, which can be accessed in the game; Destruction, Exchange, Explore, Move, Offering, Tangle, The Risk, & Transform.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dragon Rampant: A Panzer Grenadier Story
description:
Dragon Rampant (known as Kiwi Armour prior to release and now sometimes as Dragon Rampant (Kiwi Armour)) is Golden Journal #56 and adds counters for the 1st New Zealand Army Tank Brigade and scenarios using them against the Germans and Italians in Tunisia and against the Japanese in New Zealand.  Due to the name change late in development, many webpages related to it may still refer to by one of the older or alternative names.

New Zealand formed the 1st Army Tank Brigade in October 1941, intending it to fight alongside the 2nd New Zealand Division in the Middle East. The Japanese attack on Allied territories two months later caused it to be withheld to defend the homeland. Most of its tank crews eventually saw action in Italy manning Sherman tanks in the 4th New Zealand Armoured Brigade.

Dragon Rampant (Kiwi Armour) gives you the Valentine tanks with which the 1st Army Tank Brigade was equipped, and some extra pieces for our Campaign Study New Zealand Division so you can play those scenarios with proper Kiwi pieces instead of substituting British ones. You get 24 new die-cut and silky smooth pieces and 10 new scenarios for so you can play with the 1st Army Tank Brigade as New Zealand commander Bernard Freyberg wished. To play all the scenarios you will need Panzer Grenadier: An Army at Dawn, Panzer Grenadier: New Zealand Division, Panzer Grenadier: Parachutes Over Crete, and Panzer Grenadier: Saipan 1944.

-description from publisher's website
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

description: This expansion to Pick a Pen: Gardens includes 3 new scoring sheets, which can only be used in conjunction with the base game. The expansion introduces new game elements, such as tools, fences and forbidden gardens. For even more variety and challenge!

Resistencia Nativa is a competitive strategy and ecology game for 2 to 4 players, designed by brothers Dany and Chilo Varela. In this game, participants take on the roles of natural forces, controlling either native or exotic species in south-central Chile. The goal is to dominate a disturbed territory by dispersing seeds to germinate plants and colonize new areas. Each player must employ clever tactics to ensure their seeds thrive on a game board that simulates a highly eroded environment due to wildfires or deforestation. Progressing through the seasons, each with its own effects and bonuses, players must adapt their strategies to succeed. Resistencia Nativa not only provides entertainment but also educates about the importance of ecological balance and the impacts of biological invasions.

—description from the publisher

Resistencia Nativa es un juego de estrategia y ecología competitivo para 2 a 4 jugadores, diseñado por los hermanos Dany y Chilo Varela. En este juego, los participantes asumen el rol de fuerzas naturales, controlando especies nativas o exóticas en el centro-sur de Chile. El objetivo es dominar un territorio perturbado, dispersando semillas para germinar plantas y colonizar nuevas áreas. Cada jugador debe emplear tácticas ingeniosas para asegurar que sus semillas prosperen en un tablero que simula un entorno altamente erosionado por incendios forestales o deforestación. Avanzando a través de las estaciones, cada una con sus propios efectos y bonificaciones, los jugadores deben adaptar sus estrategias para prosperar. Resistencia Nativa no solo ofrece entretenimiento, sino que también educa sobre la importancia del equilibrio ecológico y los impactos de las invasiones biológicas.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

boardgame: The Republic of Rome Remastered: Brot und Spiele Promo Card
description:
Commemorative card of the official presentation of the game during SPIEL 2024.

This card is a special type of Concession that will rotate among the players and will allow them to obtain a good sum of money (at the cost of becoming corrupted and losing Popularity) or to obtain influence by contributing to the games celebrations.

—description from the publisher

Test your knowledge of the Normandy landings in this unique edition of Trivial Pursuit with 600 questions!

Can you answer every question correctly?

Includes a collector's token to add to your Family Edition!

The Dark One's eclipse has blotted out the sun. Darkness has swallowed the Empire and is coming for your outpost next! You must gather resources, perform arcane rituals, and attempt to survive. But beware! The shadows that attack your outpost are not the only threat. The Dark One may have corrupted the mind of a teammate, meaning you can no longer trust them. Can you hold this last bastion of humanity together and banish the Dark One?

In Touched by Darkness, teams of up to 4 players each cooperate to survive while competing to complete their team-specific goals. Teammates have varying responsibilities and access to different areas of the game, including:

     Producing and trading consumable resources to maintain their faction’s Morale.
     Preparing for and participating in battles to fight back the forces of the Dark one.
     Attending Council to draft and ratify game-wide legislation.
     Coordinating a game-wide Ritual to banish the Dark One.


Players must work together to survive. However, they are hampered by the presence of Shadow Agents: secret traitors undermining the rest of the players in service to the Dark One. Whether or not the Dark One is banished determines who wins in the end.

Touched by Darkness requires a Control team of 2-3 members to moderate and manage the game. These Control members perform timekeeping, arbitrate rules, and direct the story elements of the game. Players are encouraged to propose out-of-the-box ideas that aren’t covered by the game’s mechanics. These ‘Freeform Actions’ are also adjudicated by the Control team, allowing for every game to be a unique experience.

Touched by Darkness is a full print-on-demand Megagame for 15-42 players. Inspired by Den of Wolves with gameplay tweaks and a new setting, this Megagame-in-a-box includes everything needed to play (except lanyards and writing utensils). Designed and adapted with first-time Control members in mind, the Control Handbook has step-by-step instructions and resources to help you organize and run a successful game. If you’ve ever wanted to start a megagaming group in your area, this game is for you.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Paleo Atacama is a strategy and paleontological exploration game for 2 to 4 players, created by the brothers Dany and Chilo Varela. Participants take on the roles of team leaders managing museums in the Atacama Desert. The goal is to discover, collect, and exhibit fossils to increase the prestige of their museums through strategic excavations and the creation of thematic exhibitions.

Players compete for victory points earned by collaborating with experts and developing thematic rooms that maximize the educational and tourist value of their exhibitions. The game emphasizes resource management, strategic planning, and educational relevance about paleontology and the conservation of natural heritage.

Each game of Paleo Atacama offers a unique immersive experience in the exciting quest for paleontological treasures, combining strategic challenge with a deep appreciation for natural history in one of the planet’s most extreme environments.

—description from the publisher

Paleo Atacama es un juego de estrategia y exploración paleontológica para 2 a 4 jugadores, creado por los hermanos Dany y Chilo Varela. Los participantes asumen el papel de jefes de equipos paleontológicos, gestionando museos en el Desierto de Atacama. El objetivo es descubrir, recolectar y exhibir fósiles para aumentar el prestigio de sus museos a través de excavaciones estratégicas y la creación de exposiciones temáticas.

Los jugadores compiten por puntos de victoria obtenidos al colaborar con expertos y desarrollar salas temáticas que maximicen el valor educativo y turístico de sus exposiciones. El juego destaca por su enfoque en la gestión de recursos, planificación estratégica y la relevancia educativa sobre la paleontología y la conservación del patrimonio natural.

Cada partida de Paleo Atacama ofrece una experiencia única de inmersión en la emocionante búsqueda de tesoros paleontológicos, combinando desafío estratégico con una profunda apreciación por la historia natural en uno de los entornos más extremos del planeta.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

The Party game where you create your own crazy stories.

The judge reads a story with missing parts, the players write down words or phrases to Fill in the blanks.  The judge chooses their favorites and reads the whole story.

Players get a point when their word or phrase is chosen by the judge, the person with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

Anache is a game where the goal is to occupy both of the opponent's corners with your pieces, or to completely immobilize the opponent.

On your turn, you can move a connected group of up to 3 men one space forward. After your men cross the central line, they become Knights and can move in groups of up to 5, as well as gaining the power of being able to move sideways. This ability to manipulate large groups every move alongside a 12* protocol makes the game fast-paced and dynamic, although it takes place on a 12x12 board with 40 pieces per player.

Anache is a tactically rich game that never slows down to a crawl, and has features of both traversal and battle games. In particular, the capture mechanisms in this game are directly inspired by the battle game Mak-yek, with custodian capture (and its opposite, Intervention).

—description from the designer

In Squirrels Gone Wild, You'll be tasked with helping Chester the squirrel scour through an adorable, silly and devious scrapbook that belongs to his (possibly, slightly unhinged) ex-squirrelfriend, Lucy. This scrapbook is packed full of happy memories, sentimental artifacts, AND BETRAYAL!

Every page of the scrapbook contains one puzzle and offline hints are included on the back of each page if you'd like an extra nudge or two :)
You'll need to solve all the puzzles to find Chester's stolen nuts and save the day. Make it to the end for a prize and an important choice!

Overs, The Cricket Card Game, is a fast paced game where two players face off to see who can outwit their opponent and score the most runs to win.

Take turns to play as the batter, choose your attitude, and attempt to play the right shot against each of your opponent's deliveries. Be careful, as playing the wrong shot might result in the loss of a wicket, and just like in real life losing too many wickets will result in an early end to your innings!

If you are a larger group of friends you can play Overs in two teams, with everyone taking turns to bat and to bowl.

Choose to play a quick game in just 20 minutes, or play a standard game which takes about 45 minutes. You can even play two games back to back and add the scores together if you'd like to emulate a test match!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A softback reprint of the Beyond the Beaches section of the original Overlord campaign supplement.

Includes 7 army lists: German Panzer, Infantry and Fallschirmjäger Divisions, British Armoured and Infantry Divisions and American Armoured and Infantry Divisions.

Please note this is a supplement and a copy of the Battlegroup ruleset is needed to play the game.

Schrottplatz Rally is an exhilarating board game where players race across a dynamic junkyard track using a variety of customizable vehicles. The game begins with the base set, featuring four iconic cars that players can tune and modify. The track is modular, consisting of six unique sections, allowing for endless reconfiguration and replayability. Players must navigate through challenging terrains, such as mud pits, icy lakes, and burning workshops, each requiring strategic planning and quick thinking. Repairs and upgrades are crucial, as vehicles need constant maintenance to stay competitive. As the game progresses, additional modules and vehicles can be integrated, enhancing the gameplay with new challenges and possibilities.

Objective:
To be the first to cross the finish line by skillfully navigating the track, managing vehicle upgrades, and outmaneuvering opponents.

Game Components:

Vehicles: Sixteen vehicles ready for customization.
Track Sections: Up to 20 modular pieces that can be rearranged for different race setups.
Additional Modules: Expand the game with new vehicles and track pieces, each introducing unique obstacles and strategic elements.
Gameplay Overview:

Setup: Arrange the track using the modular pieces. Each race can have a different layout, ensuring varied gameplay.
Starting the Race: Players choose and customize their vehicles.
Racing: Navigate through different track sections, each presenting unique challenges like the mud pit, icy lake, and burning workshop.
Repairs and Upgrades: Manage vehicle maintenance and upgrades strategically to gain an edge over opponents.
Winning: The first player to successfully navigate the track and cross the finish line wins the race.
Expansion and Replayability:

Modules: Additional modules introduce new vehicles and track sections, expanding the gameplay.
Customization: Each race offers different customization options, ensuring that no two games are alike.
Target Audience:

Fans of tactical racing and vehicle customization.
Board game enthusiasts looking for a game with high replayability and strategic depth.

Conclusion:
Schrottplatz Rally combines the thrill of racing with the strategic depth of vehicle customization, offering an engaging and ever-evolving gameplay experience. Whether you're a seasoned board game veteran or new to the hobby, this game promises excitement, strategy, and endless fun.

—description from the designer

Schrottplatz Rally ist ein aufregendes Brettspiel, bei dem die Spieler über eine dynamische Schrottplatzstrecke mit einer Vielzahl anpassbarer Fahrzeuge rasen. Das Spiel beginnt mit dem Basisset, das vier ikonische Autos enthält, die die Spieler tunen und modifizieren können. Die Strecke ist modular und besteht aus sechs einzigartigen Abschnitten, was unendliche Umgestaltungsmöglichkeiten und Wiederspielbarkeit ermöglicht. Spieler müssen durch herausfordernde Gelände navigieren, wie Schlammgruben, vereiste Seen und brennende Werkstätten, die strategische Planung und schnelles Denken erfordern. Reparaturen und Upgrades sind entscheidend, da Fahrzeuge ständig gewartet werden müssen, um wettbewerbsfähig zu bleiben. Mit dem Fortschreiten des Spiels können zusätzliche Module und Fahrzeuge integriert werden, um das Gameplay mit neuen Herausforderungen und Möglichkeiten zu erweitern.

Ziel des Spiels:
Erster die Ziellinie überqueren, indem man die Strecke geschickt navigiert, Fahrzeug-Upgrades verwaltet und Gegner überlistet.

Spielkomponenten:

Fahrzeuge: 16 Fahrzeuge zur Anpassung.
Streckenteile: 20 modulare Teile, die für verschiedene Rennaufstellungen neu angeordnet werden können.
Zusatzmodule: Erweiterung des Spiels mit neuen Fahrzeugen und Streckenteilen, die einzigartige Hindernisse und strategische Elemente einführen.
Spielübersicht:

Aufbau: Die Strecke wird mit den modularen Teilen arrangiert. Jede Rennstrecke kann unterschiedlich gestaltet werden, was abwechslungsreiches Gameplay sicherstellt.
Rennstart: Die Spieler wählen und modifizieren ihre Fahrzeuge.
Rennen: Navigiere durch verschiedene Streckenabschnitte, die einzigartige Herausforderungen bieten, wie die Schlammgrube, den vereisten See und die brennende Werkstatt.
Reparaturen und Upgrades: Verwalte die Fahrzeugwartung und -upgrades strategisch, um einen Vorteil gegenüber den Gegnern zu erlangen.
Gewinnen: Der erste Spieler, der die Strecke erfolgreich navigiert und die Ziellinie überquert, gewinnt das Rennen.
Erweiterung und Wiederspielbarkeit:

Module: Zusätzliche Module führen neue Fahrzeuge und Streckenabschnitte ein, die das Gameplay erweitern.
Anpassung: Jede Rennstrecke bietet verschiedene Anpassungsoptionen, wodurch kein Spiel dem anderen gleicht.
Zielgruppe:

Fans von taktischen Rennspielen

—description from the designer (German)

The Nuragic Civilization had its home in Sardinia, where they would build large megalithic towers known as Nuraghes, which give the name to the civilization. They also were able forgers, making little trinkets and weapons out of bronze, they practiced viticulture and had many types of religious practices involving the Cult of the Waters.

In People of the Isle, you'll be tasked with leading one of the many tribes of the island, trying to make it emerge as the best one.

Gameplay Overview
People of the Isle is played over 6 rounds, where players will go in turns taking various actions, like buildig megalithic structures, researching inventions, trading, and of course, claiming territory. However, the tribes weren't hostile to each other; when entering a tile already owned by a player, the population there will happily coexist, unless the population limit per tile has been already reached; in that case, a "Push" will happen and one of the population meeple will have to migrate to an adjacent tile.
Territory isn't the only source of competition. Players are also able to claim cards representing Religious Structures, which give additional ways to score points.
At the end of the 6th round, the player with the most points wins.

—description from designer

This game is a singing contest. It contains cards which either contain a specific word or a category. There are three game modes:

- Hurry Up, Buttercup!
One player flips open a card from the stack. The first player to sing a song that either contains the word on the card or a word that fits in the category on the card, claims the card. The player with most cards after a given time, wins.

- Gimme More
One player flips a card and has 30 seconds to (start) sing(ing) a matching song. Then the next player must sing another matching song, and so on. Those who cannot think of a song are out of the round. Last player remaining claims the card.

- I Want It All
9 cards are laid out on the table. Players take turns finding a song which match as many of the words/category cards as possible, claiming all the matched cards. Then other players can start singing to claim the remaining cards, etc.

A very simple set of miniatures skirmish rules that can be printed on a single A4 page. Can be use for about any setting, but example profiles are given for Medieval/Fantasy, Wild West, and the 41st Millennium.

boardgame: The Way of Horse and Bow
description: Based on historian Karl Friday likening Genpei War era fighting between mounted archers to dogfighting aeroplanes, this set of skirmish rules is based on the aerial wargame Bag the Hun, with suitable adaptions for the differences between horses and planes and bows and machine guns.
family: History: Conflicts of the Heian period – Japan
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
It's a hot summer day! For a long time, you have dreamt to participate in the fruity part at Bora Fruta park and taste a famous ice pop...

Every turn, a player rolls a dice to determine its movement  than resolves a challenge card. It can be a solo challenge or a duel challenge that rewards coupons according to the level of success.

Challenges are often observations or cognitive activities such as finding difference, memorizing an image than answering questions about it, a sudoku speed contest, rearranging letters to form a word, etc.

When a player collects enough coupons, he can buy a part of the multi-coloured ice pop by ending his movement on one of the four shop spaces.

The first player to collect three ice pop parts is the winner.

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Vehicle Pack #5
description: The G.I. JOE Mission Critical Vehicle Pack #5 contains 4 new Vehicle Cards (Barracuda, Ghoststriker X-16, Road Toad BRV, SLAM)

Set in the Cities of Venus Universe, DUST Reclamation is a trick-taking game that Venusian miners play to pass the time. The game is played over four rounds. It is a must-follow trick-taking game where the trump suit is determined by the Reclamator in the center of the play area. At the beginning of a player's turn, they can swap out a card in the current trump slot (either a trump card or one matching the number in that slot), thereby changing the trump suit mid-hand before playing their card.

There are four DUST cards in play throughout the game. These cards represent the highest number of all suits, making them automatic trick-winners unless another card is played after them in the same trick. The value of DUST cards varies each round, being either negative 1 point or positive 1 point, as determined by a four-card mini deck shuffled at the start of each round.

Additionally, there are work orders in the form of the number of tricks taken that award bonus points if achieved by any player in that round. Scores are tracked by handing out V-nite Ore at the end of each round, and the player with the most Ore at the end of the fourth round wins!

—description from the publisher

"He who thinks that games are just games and work is just work, has not understood much about either." -Heinrich Heine

Musta kuula ("Black ball") is a Boggle-like word game, originally designed as an aid for rehabilitation and to fight against dementia. The game consists of three wooden boxes, each with a letter grid of 6x6 recesses in the bottom and a transparent plastic cover. Inside the boxes are nine steel balls, one of which is black. Gameplay is simple; shake the box, let the balls find their places in the recesses and from those letters you then have to find as many words as possible, the only limit being usual word game rules and that the black ball letter has to be included in every word. To beat the game, players together have to achieve a "good" result (15 words or more) but you can also try for "excellent" (30 words) or even "glorious" (50 or more, with 8- and 9-letter words included). The three boxes represent three levels of difficulty in the mix of the available letters; Apprentice, Journeyman and Master.

The original game is meant to be played as a co-op with no clear time limit, with each player finding a word and passing the game box to the next player until the players decide that no more words can be found, but there are also competitive variants and others using a bag of 27 wooden Scrabble-like letter tiles, supplied as a separate add-on.

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Dirty Deeds Expansion Box
description:
Life in the Würstreich is a constant struggle for survival among the crooked cops, desperate adventurers, half-starved monsters, insane wizards, grotesque ghosts, obnoxious goblins, deranged demons & the like. Living is made more difficult because everyone is jostling for position like crabs in a bucket. Trying to eke out a living as an adventurer is even more challenging, the lows are far lower & the highs, though infrequent, are much higher. Most don’t have the wits, cunning & lack of boundaries to succeed in this flea-ridden, dog-eat-dog world & stick to the relative safety of the known. To endure & thrive in this level of struggle one must be ready to take on any number of Dirty Deeds.

Dirty Deeds contains a booklet with rules & lore. Featuring four new playable characters: Wasted Youth, Crooked Cop, Bombast Belter & High Alchemist
With a deck of mini missions, Factions cards, new Loot, Skills & Weaknesses, Settlement Encounters, Monsters, Danger cards, Counters & more! Box is 9”x6”x2” (the same size as Mean Streets).

Bringing a new method of mini-missions, a “Dirty Deeds Deck” to give your adventurers something to do in between missions, a slice of degeneracy, featuring a book profiling the rules & more lore about Brüttelburg! 4 new playable characters lifted straight from Brüttelburg: Wasted Youth, Crooked Cop, Bombast Belter & High Alchemist, a hefty new deck of Settlement Encounters to make every trip to town unique, engaging & dangerous, new Loot, new Monsters & more!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Goblin Mode Expansion Box
description:
This box lets you play as a Goblin! Goblins are nasty, filthy, sadistic creatures that have been the focus of folk tales, nursery rhymes & curses for as long as anyone can remember. Countless numbers of irritations have been attributed to the fiddly destructiveness of the Goblins. As the Goblins gain in number - the trouble becomes malignant, they start stealing larger things like horses, carts & barrels, breaking windows, scratching the mortar away between stones until walls mysteriously fall apart, rotting wooden beams, spoiling grain & defecating everywhere. If the unclean reign of Goblins is not met with sanitation, order & death, they start building their terrible nests where the sun doesn’t shine. This is when Colin the Goblin has decided not to take his seat on the Goblin Throne & the next in line don’t want that target on their backs. It is from here that you must engage in Goblin Mode.

Goblin Mode contains a booklet with a new campaign, rules & lore. Four playable Goblin characters: Bog Pimple, Skive Gristle, Khaz Wart & Filch Mold. With new Loot, Skills & Weaknesses, Monsters, Encounters, Danger cards, Counters & more! Box is 9”x6”x2” (the same size as Mean Streets).

Goblin Mode lets you play Goblins & views the Würstreich from a Goblin's eye view. Taking place after the Hand of Doom has been vanquished as the Goblins are on the move & trying to establish themselves as the primary threat to Imperial power. Featuring the new Colin the Goblin campaign, where you play as Colin the Goblin Dandy, trying to just live your life but you are next in line for the Goblin Throne. You have to escape the Goblin Horde who are trying desperately to capture Colin & restore the Goblin Hierarchy. Including a book of the rules & more lore about the Goblins & their history from a Goblin perspective. 4 new playable Goblin characters; Colin the Goblin & three other yet to be discovered characters, loot, encounters, monsters & more!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Moon Madness Expansion Box
description:
The Moon ushers in a time of uncertainty & illusion, when nothing is what it seems. It is revered as a deity by the pagan Gütter. There is a reason why the moon holds so much mystery. It is reputed to hold an entire arcane civilization. The mists of confusion are increased by the fact that it seems you are only able to travel to the Moon in dreams or with the aid of magic & distorted consciousness & no one takes anything back from there except their memories. The pagan Gütter are trying to harness the power of the Moon & the Imperial Würst want to stop them. The Moon Wizard has other ideas. You must travel to the Moon & discover the mysteries of the power of the Moon Wizard. You must discover the cryptic, archaic Moon & not succumb to the Moon Madness.

Moon Madness contains a booklet with a new campaign, rules & lore. Four playable characters: Eccentric Astronomer, Gütter Witch, Lunatic Lush & Night Knight. With new Loot, Skills & Weaknesses, Encounters, Monsters & Danger cards, a mini board of the Moon, counters & more! Box is 9”x6”x2” (the same size as Mean Streets).

Moon Madness takes place before the Hand of Doom menaced the Würstreich! A new campaign pitting you against lunar cultists trying to draw down the moon & eventually exploring the moon itself! Your party must travel to the Moon using dreams by eating a special cheese & going to sleep at the Mondgasthaus. You wake up in Mondheim, the biggest city of the Moon! Featuring a book with the rules & lore about the Moon! 4 new playable characters; Eccentric Astronomer, Gütter Witch, Lunatic Lush & Night Knight, associated loot, skills, weaknesses, encounters, monsters & a mini map/board of the moon!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Super Heroes United: Gamefound Promos Box
description:
Crowdfunding Exclusive box to hold all Stretch Goal promos!

26 Hero Characters, 5 Dual Mode Characters, 20 Villain Characters, 5 Team Decks, 1 Challenge, 2 Modules, 1 Location, 4 Equipment Cards, 2 Solo Modes, 6 new Super-Hero & 4 new Super-Villain cards.

Full list in Community Wiki section

—description from publisher

Chemistry and nuclear energy themed game. Grid-based playfield like Chess. Cards with different numbers and colors are placed and removed from grid according to simple rules. The goal is to have the fewest cards in hand at the end of the game. If the board state falls apart or becomes highly restricted, it affects everyone! Hence, the name, Mutually Assured Destruction. The more this game is played, the more one sees the nuanced places for strategy. High variance and replayability. Easy to learn, hard to master. Medium amount of RNG, mostly strategy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: SHIN MEGAMI TENSEI THE BOARD GAME
description:
Journey into a post apocalyptic world and fight to gain control of a demon infested Tokyo. Use your abilities to negotiate with demons and take control of regions in Tokyo, and then compete in an epic battle.

This new strategic board game is based on the world of SHIN MEGAMI TENSEI.
  
In SHIN MEGAMI TENSEI THE BOARD GAME players compete to gather demonic allies to their side by using demon negotiating abilities and then using demon fusion to create even more powerful beings iconic to the SHIN MEGAMI TENSEI video games.  Players need to use their judgement wisely throughout the game to help mold their destiny.

This game will feature detailed and high quality demon figures.

—description from the publisher

CA09 CARD PACK 2023
“We’ve Got A Crisis On Our Hands!”

Everything changes. After every battle, the balance of power shifts; after every story, a new one begins. So too do the very Crises on which heroes and villains clash change with the world around them. What was old has become new; but the will to fight remains!

In this 2023 Crisis Pack you will find 10 updated Extract Crisis Cards and 12 updated Secure Crisis Cards, refreshing the game with a tweak to its heart. Suit up your Squads, keep an eye on The Daily Bugle, and watch these Crises play out on the tabletop in Marvel: Crisis Protocol!

—description from the publisher

A tortoise, a rabbit, and three cows are competing on who makes it to the pasture first on a racetrack with puddles to be avoided along the path! If a player’s path is interrupted by another player or puddle, their journey is halted until the path is clear again. On players turn, she takes a move card and moves every charachter according to the number of steps on their cards. Every player uses their own character, but everyone moves other characters. Some cards lets you place the puddle in front of other players, and some give you instant boost of speed.  First player to cross the finish line, wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Tortoise and the Hare

description: Expand the basic Cities game with a new achievement board. That includes the main attractions of the cities of Amsterdam and Antwerp for even more variety.

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

Olivia Rose, an established business mogul in her family company empire, was murdered on her annual Valentine's day Gala! Who could have done it? Players will navigate through her life and relationships, and through information surrounding her death, in order to solve the case and finally catch the killer.

Publisher rated this game as hard difficulty. The game contains 6 suspects, 35+ pieces of evidence, 5 objectives to complete.

Les Reines du Shopping (Shopping Queens) is a card game inspired by a TV game show of the same name. The principle of the show: five candidates must run around the shops to find an outfit that matches the imposed theme. For example: cocktail by a swimming pool. On their return, each candidate parades in front of their competitors who rate them. On the last day, each is rated by a professional stylist. The one with the best average wins the competition.

The board game is played in several phases.
- Phase 1. The theme.
A theme card is drawn at random.
- Phase 2. Shopping.
The "Clothing", "Shoes", "Accessories" and "Makeup" cards are spread out on the table. Timed by an hourglass, the players choose their cards. At the end of the regulation time, they must reject any items that would exceed the budget allocated by the theme.
- Phase 3. The catwalk.
Each player displays her cards and explains what motivated her choices. The other players give her a score. The average is then calculated.
- Phase 4. Stylist score.
On the back of the theme card is a colored star. Any card in the players' hands that has a star of the same color as the one corresponding to the theme earns its owner a point. The average of the fashion show scores is calculated with the stylist's score. The player with the best final score wins the game.

Abyss: De Profundis is the third expansion for Abyss. The 10 Outcasts reach the gates of the Kingdom! Stripped of their titles as Lords, and banished in the farthest reaches of the abyssal depths, they evolved to survive and prepare for their return.

When playing with the De Profundis expansion, the base game rules don't change, although new elements are added:

10 Outcasts cards
1 Rulebook

Outcasts have their deck alongside that of the Lords. They are recruited in the same way but have different powers. And two Outcasts allow you to steal a Location from an opponent.

—description from the publisher

Play a Tie-tiebreak (First at 7 points wins)with cards that represent real shots and situations in a padel match. The game is made up of defensive, offensive and special cards based on the padel skills. Choose fine to be the first player to score 7.

Coal & Guns Big Battles: Lissa 1866 is a very agile rules-booklet (two pages) designed for 3D wargaming, which allows you to replay in two or three hours the famous battle of Lissa of 1866 between the fleet of the Kingdom of Italy and that of the Austro-Hungarian Empire. The rulebook includes simple silhouettes of the main ships that took part in the battle, so that even those who do not yet have the three-dimensional models can play it. The PDF contains everything you need to play (except the dice, of course...).

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Tide & Tangle is a cheerful yet tactical game where players take on the roles of sea otters diving for valuable (and often tasty) sea treasures. But the ocean is never still. Navigate waves to stay away from the coast, wisely choosing your diving spots and how deep you wish to go. And hey, lending a helping paw might just make the ocean feel a little less lonely and a lot more fun!

On the surface, the game is simple: swim around the board, push your luck diving for treasures, or flip your otter on its back to raft, preparing for the challenges ahead. But if you want to score big and be the most joyful otter in the Pacific, you’ll need sharp timing to ride those fresh waves just right: Tide & Tangle features a unique modular board system with moving wave tiles, requiring players to adapt to the ever-changing nature of the ocean.

—description from the designer

Quiz game Znáte Evropu? (Do you know Europe?) tests your knowledge of the European continent across 6 different categories - history, geography, nature/science/technology, culture, sports and miscellaneous. A total of 1350 questions await you, but you can choose the difficulty level. 
Each question in the game is awarded a cash prize, which you can win if you answer it correctly.

More adventurous players can also choose the action variant: Bet your chips on a teammate or take their advice and you both earn money. You can also use the chip to change the value of the questions, perhaps even reversing the outcome of the game.

The player with the most knowledge (the most money) wins.

—description from the publisher

Never wanting to find their treasure stolen again, the pirates hid it in a number of treasure boxes, some containing treasure, others bombs! The outlaws who stole the treasure were afraid to open the treasure boxes, and each of them was left to their own fate.

Are they lucky or unlucky, or is it a foregone conclusion?

There are two ways of victory, as listed as the followings:
Conventional Victory
When a player gets three bombs, the game is over or the player is eliminated from the game.
Of the remaining players, the player with the most coins wins.
In the case of a coin tie, the player with fewer bombs wins.
In the case of a tie, the game continues until a winner is determined.
Special Victory
The game ends instantly any moment a player possesses 10 coins, and that player wins!

In Haifischen, all 25 memory cards are shuffled and spread out in the middle of the table. A player throws both dice to determine which animal in which color needs to be rescued. When fishing for animals, the player turns over a card that must match the dice to take it. If the card doesn't match the dice, the card stays in the middle and the next player gets to throw the dice. The game ends when all the cards have been fished out of the lake. Then, the players reveal their rows. The sharks eat one colorful animal each from the player's row. The player with the most rescued animals wins; in the event of a tie, the player with fewer sharks in their row wins.

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Heroes For Hire – Voodoo Ranger
description:
Behold the Voodoo Ranger x Heroes of Barcadia Game Expansion.

In the alleys of Brewtown, where the air is thick with the scent of hops, roams the mysterious Voodoo Ranger. This skeletal brewmaster, adorned with goggles, a feathered hat, and a bandolier brimming with IPAs, is the epitome of undead cool. His journey began in the cryptic cellars of the Voodoo Brewery, where he experimented in hopmancy — conjuring IPAs that are as bold and hazy as his sense of humor.

Product Specifications:
- Voodoo Ranger Hero for Hire Character Cup
- Replace 1 Heroes of Barcadia Base Game Character with a Hero for Hire!

Includes:
- 1 Plastic Health Bar Character Cup (Cup features character art on the front, and the measurements for the Liquid Life System™ on the back.)
- 1x 20-sided colored die
- 1 Signature Move Card
- 1 Box

Recommended hand-wash only

ALL cards in this add-on are made of PVC and are 100% waterproof
Requires Heroes of Barcadia Base Game to play
Full game NOT included

Players are dealt hands that they must quickly memorize then switch with their partners from which they then select a card that they think will match with each other.

Players compare their chosen card with that of their partner. The goal is to match color and/or character. Matching either just the
color or just the character scores that team 1 point. Matching both color and character scores 2 points for that team. No points are awarded if there is no match. Keep track of points using the scorecards provided

First team to win 12 points are crowned the Memory Masters.

Includes: 108 game cards, 4 score cards.

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

Amelia Sinclair, an established Chocolatier, was murdered on the Annual Easter Egg Hunt at Meadowbrook in her shop! Players will navigate through evidence and will have to find their way through a labyrinth of clues, conflicting alibis, and hidden agendas in order to solve the case and finally catch the killer.

Publisher rated this game as medium difficulty. The game contains 5 suspects, 20+ pieces of evidence, 4 objectives to complete.

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

Oliver Riverton, an up and coming actor that has been making the rounds in the news lately has been murdered while filming for his new movie! Who could have done it? Players will navigate through Oliver's life and relationships, and through information surrounding his murder, in order to solve the case and finally catch the killer.

Players will need to have good attention to detail, analytical mind and detective skills. There will be clues and evidence that will be connected throughout the game, and will definitely test players' deductive skills.

Publisher rated this game as easy difficulty. The game contains 5 suspects, 20+ pieces of evidence and 4 objectives to complete.

Set 66 million years ago, players lead a dinosaur tribe, aiming to expand and strengthen it by hatching dinosaur eggs and overcoming challenges. Players roll dice to move, hatch eggs, and engage in battles with other tribes to capture their eggs. The game blends fun with education, teaching players about different dinosaur species and prehistoric survival. Featuring cute yet accurate dinosaur illustrations, it's an excellent tool for inspiring children's interest in natural sciences.

Gameplay

Movement and Events:
Players roll two green dice to move their pieces on the board. Movements must avoid water areas and may trigger special events.

Hatching Dinosaur Eggs:
Upon reaching a cave with dinosaur eggs, players can hatch the eggs to receive corresponding dinosaur cards, which vary in strength.

Combat:
When two dinosaurs meet, one player can initiate an attack. Both players use dinosaur cards of the same color and roll a combat dice. The sum of the card's value and the dice roll determines the combat strength. The player with the higher combat strength wins and takes an egg from the loser, who is also pushed back four steps.

Victory Condition

The game immediately ends when any player reaches a score of 10 or more. The player with the highest score at this point is declared the winner.

Components


     Modular game boards: 9
     Dinosaur tokens: 4
     Combat dice: 1
     Effect dice: 1
     Movement dice: 1
     Dinosaur egg markers: 24
     Dinosaur cards: 56
     Event cards: 54
     Player reference cards: 4
     Supply zone boards: 2

This fourth scenario for Kronologic: Paris 1920 is an expansion that requires the base game. It adds five new puzzle to solve with a difficulty ranging between 4/5 and 5/5.

Be the first to solve the case by interrogating the suspects and figure out who is the culprit among them.

Two fugitives are actively wanted by the police after having terrorised at the Paris Opera. According to the police, the culprits, a man and a woman, were alone when they planted the bomb that exploded when the evening ended.
Find out who is guilty and identify the victims of the explosion.

This is a deduction game where players look for clues to solve the case.

Explore the town of Eldridge in Catalog of Suspicion - a thrilling murder mystery game filled with suspense and intrigue. Solve the case of stolen books and a shocking murder, challenging your detective skills and keeping you guessing until the end. Ideal for game nights and gatherings.

Publisher rated this game as hard difficulty. The game contains 5 interrogations, 16 pieces of evidence, 6 objectives to complete, 37 optional clues (when you get stuck).

This fifth scenario for Kronologic: Paris 1920 is an expansion that requires the base game. It adds five new puzzle to solve with a difficulty ranging between 4/5 and 5/5.

Be the first to solve the case by interrogating the suspects and figure out who is the culprit among them.

During a delightful evening filled with the mesmerising performance of the Paris Opera Orchestra, the guests enjoy themselves under the shiny chandeliers and the golden mouldings. Among the crowd, two lonely souls wander without ever meeting. Who are these cursed lovers of the Paris Opera?

In Order Up! Sushi, two players compete to fill sushi orders to get to 50 points first. Each player has four chefs to complete tasks in the kitchen and get the right ingredients to make sushi. Watch out for the other team! They will get in your way while they collect their ingredients. Get your orders completed quickly, because the board changes every time a player scores, revealing new scoring opportunities and actions!

Order Up! Sushi is a print and play game that can be downloaded for free on itch.io using the official link.

Originally designed for the 2022 9 Card PnP contest.

—description from the designer

The life of a possum is not easy at all. Dangerous tigers, huge orcas and the occasional adorable panda.

Play your cards cautiously and be careful not to touch the number 21 or you will die forever.

There's only one chance, pretend you're dead and maybe you'll cheat death just this time.

Each turn play an opossum card or an animal card, Each card have a number (0-7), and each card played is added to the previously played card, if at any time you run out of cards to play or the sum of the cards is greater than 21 then you must discard a token of "playing the dead"

If someone cannot play a "playing the dead" token they lose the game and the player with the most tokens wins the game.

Special cards allow you to skip turns, have the rest of the players discard cards, or return the card counter to a lower number.

Or you can discard pairs of cards that add up to 14 to do effects like gain life, or recover cards from the discard

—description from the designer
Game about skill! Try to put all the medals in a tomb full of moving serpents. The goal of the Game it's get all the magical medals with the magical staff

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Wasteland Warmasters Enemy Supplement #2
description:
The Wasteland Scavengers are a motley crew of criminals, cutthroats, and raiders! Brutal warriors lead by powerful Warlords that don’t just want to survive in the barren and lawless wastes, but rather to thrive there! Some Warlords gain a fearsome reputation and high level of infamy from their savage exploits. These most feared Warlords are known as Wasteland Warmasters!

This set adds new large Enemy Record Sheets for two of the dangerous Wasteland Warmasters: Ree’Jek of Marn and Ko’kon’tar, the Red Destroyer, making them full Enemy mini-bosses. description:

How much can you remember of what others tell you? Let's put it into play!

+Sociable is a cooperative game to talk about different topics and get to know each other more. The objective is to reach the best score by remembering what others responded to each topic.

The game consists of two phases: In the first, we will draw a card from the deck and respond to the topic it proposes. Depending on the number of players, more cards will be taken from the deck and each one will respond. These cards remain in the center of the table for everyone to see. The rest of the deck is set aside as it will not be used for the rest of the game.

In the second phase, in turns, we will have to answer what another player said to one of the cards from the previous phase. By doing it correctly, we will increase our score as a group. All this is carried out thanks to the row card, which will tell you which card to respond to.

Likewise, the game also works as a tool to chat freely about different topics and get to know each other more.

In Spiky Go!, you'll help Spiky the dog bring back safely the farm animals that have escaped from the barn: the pigs, the cows and the horses. To do this, you'll compete with the other players to see which animal gets to go after it.

The escaped animals are in a deck of cards and each animal is represented by a hand signal. In each round, four cards are opened showing which animals have been seen and which players can go after. In each round, everyone plays together. At the same time, everyone shows the hand signal of the animal they want to take home, just like rock-paper-scissors.

But the animals are smart and know how to escape. If there's a tie and you can't divide the animals on the table, the animals escape and no one wins.

But if no one has placed the same signal as you, you get to take all the animals of that type.

At the end of each round, whoever has the most animals of each type places a marker in their barn. Rounds continue until someone manages to place 5 animal markers inside their barn.

Who will be able to get the most animals back to the barn? Be smart and observant in this game for the whole family!

Hello, Pirates! Welcome to the pirate ship of pets! As a good pirate, you have to pay attention to what is happening on the board and in your crew to win this challenge.

Each player will have pet cards in their hand with 4 characteristics each, indicated on the card. For example, the pet can be wearing a hat or not; it can be a cat or a dog.

On your turn, you will place a card on the board, next to another card that is already there. Your mission is simple: Pay attention to the characteristics and be able to point out 4 animals in a line with the same characteristic. It seems easy, but the line can be horizontal, vertical or diagonal.

Pets on Board also has two difficulty levels with two different decks. In the easiest mode, you play with Clear Waters and the characteristics are shown on the cards, which helps you identify them. In the hard mode, the waters are turbulent and you have to identify the characteristics only with the image of the characters, without help.

Pets on Board is guaranteed fun for the whole family. Be quick and don't drift with Pets on Board.

DuoLex is a letter stacking, tile flipping, transformational word game for two players.

Components: 105 two-sided tiles or cards in a small bag.
Letters are the same on both sides except reverse black versus white. Tiles have single letters, double letters (bigrams) and trigrams.

Goal: Get the highest score by claiming the most letter points with tiles of your color. Note that that is not a crossword-style word game.

Setup:
Either player picks their color: black or white.
Shuffle the tiles within the bag.
Each player draws six tiles as their initial "hand".

Game Play:
Taking turns, each player lays tiles adjacently on the table to form words in a straight line as noted below. After placing your tiles, replenish your hand to a maximum of six tiles.

Forming Words:
There are several ways of forming a word. 
- place contiguous tiles from your hand to make a whole new word.
- place a tile on top of an existing tile to make a new word
- insert a tile between existing tiles to a word into a new word.
- take one or more tiles from another word, in addition to one or more tiles from your hand, to form a new word - the source of tiles must still be a valid word (even if reshuffled).

Note that when placing each tile, flip it use your color.

Ending the Game:
The game ends when no more tiles can be placed.

Scoring:
Both players add up the points of all tiles of their color and deduct the value of any unplayed tiles.

-description from designer

Hive Secundus was once Necromunda’s shining jewel.

Until over a century ago, something insidious was discovered lurking at its heart, and it was razed to the ground – the ruins declared quarantine extremis. Meow Men will work together to play cards in ascending order to repair their snow mobile and descending order to repair their communication device. But beware-- certain members may actually be assassins in disguise! If the Meow Men can successfully repair both devices while combating polar bears, wolf attacks and the Assassins, they will escape from the North Pole and win! If the Assassins successfully stop the repairs, the Meow Men will have one last chance to win, by successfully calling out which players are the Assassins.

Oh no, it seems little rabbit has lost his friend! Good thing he has the help of great detectives!

The clues will involve the shape, color and feeling of the card. The first detective to claim the correct card gets a point and becomes the new rabbit friend. The player who gets the most cards wins.

Will you be a great detective?
