Similar to the 3 phases of Tripoley, Quadrupoley has 4 phases.
Phase 1: Auctioning of the extra hand.
Phase 2: Passing cards, as determined by the current dealer.
Phase 3: Playing cards onto spaces of the board, taking chips from the space played on. Player gets to choose which cards to play. Continue 'til a player has played all his/her cards, or all players pass.
Phase 4: Holder of the highest remaining card gets 1 chip from each other player.

—description from the designer

ChessBriscola is an exciting and strategic trick-taking card game for 2 to 6 players. The goal in ChessBriscola is to take cards that contribute the most points to your score. Whether playing individually or in teams, strategic play and careful planning are essential to outscore your opponents.

Easy to learn and highly engaging, ChessBriscola is suitable for both adults and children aged 7 and up, making it perfect for family gatherings and travel entertainment.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kabal: A Game of World Domination
description:
Kabal is a semi cooperative deckbuidling area control game where players start as black hat players collaborating against a formidable AI opponent called Nemesis.

Players can flip allegiances and join the white hat team. This can cause Nemesis to also flip. Players, including Nemesis, take turns to carry out a number of actions based on their control of artefacts or crystals.

There are various currencies - M0, M9 and blue power. These effect two primary tracks - Alpha and Omega which give team VPs. These are added to VPs awarded to individual players for completing missions, battles, activating heroes and other factors, including control of regions.

Kabal presents a unique blends of strategy, cooperation and competition.

—description from the designer

Word Wiz (千词百会) is an educational board game designed for English language learners. Each player aims to enhance their English vocabulary and spelling skills through repetitive word formation and sentence construction, reinforcing memory retention. The basic gameplay involves playing letter cards to form English words. Players who remember spellings efficiently will play their cards faster and more effectively. The game features five different advanced rules to accommodate varying levels of vocabulary proficiency, allowing players to select a gameplay mode that suits their skill level.

Objective

Play letter cards to form English words.

Victory Condition

The game ends when a player uses all their letter cards or the draw bag is exhausted. The player with the fewest remaining letter cards wins.

Components


     120 Letter Cards
     4 Card Racks
     1 Instruction Manual
     200 Word Cards
     1 Draw Bag

A deck of 55 cards has the name of a movie on one side, and a deliberately bad description of the movie on the other side, along with three hints about the title. The cards are shuffled and placed in the center of the table with the movie titles face down.

The first player is the reader, who draws the top card without letting anyone see the answer, and reads the description. The other players shout out the movie title that they think the description matches. If someone is correct, they keep that card as a way of keeping score. Play then passes to the left and the next person becomes the reader.

If no one guesses correctly, the reader then reads the first hint, and everyone guesses again. If no one guesses correctly, the second hint is read. If no one still gets it, the third hint is read. If there are no correct guesses after the third hint, the answer is read to the players, and that card is set aside in a discard pile.

Players can either go through the entire deck, or stop after someone reaches a predetermined number of points, or stop after a certain amount of time has passed. The player with the most point wins.

羽觞飞花 (Yushang Feihua) is an educational and entertaining board game designed to help players learn and memorize classic Chinese poetry.

Gameplay
Players take turns drawing cards and must recite poetry based on revealed elements when drawing the "Shang" card. Use QR codes for audio explanations and recitations, making learning poetry a delightful experience. The player with the most cards at the end wins.

Components


     20 Poetry Cards
     55 Element Cards
     3 Poetry Stickers

Buccaneers of the Americas is a solitaire action roleplaying game set in the "Golden Age of Buccaneering", specifically in and between the years 1650 and 1700, when privateers and pirates, known under dozens of similar names in several different languages, cruised the waters of the Caribbean Sea, taking prizes and sacking cities with the often overt support of the imperial European empires abroad.

Inspired in part by the 1987 Commodore 64 classic, Sid Meier's Pirates!, Buccaneers of the Americas presents players with opportunity to plunder ships and sack settlements of the Spanish Main with a strong emphasis on historicity (no buried treasure, 90-gun pirate galleons, or "jolly rogers" here!). Players assume the role of a buccaneer commander initially aligned to one of four empires: The Kingdom of England, the Kingdom of France, the Dutch Republic, or the Spanish Empire. From there, the game is inherently open-ended in nature, and captains are free to perform any available action or travel anywhere on the authentic hex-grid map of the Middle America region until the end of the game when, hopefully, they are still alive and can retire rich and in fair health.

Buccaneers of the Americas requires two ten-sided (d10) dice to play, and may be (but is not required) enhanced by taking notes and keeping a log on a separate sheet of paper, or digitally with any free word-like program, as your captain's story develops.

—description from the designer

A fast-paced card guessing game that rewards memory luck. Players take turns guessing the color or suit of the next card in their deck. Correct guesses allow players to remove cards from the deck; incorrect guesses let their opponents have a turn. The first person to get rid of their cards wins.

The stage is set for the largest clash of late-war heavy armour.

The Red Army is spearheaded by the powerful Russian tank corps with units like the T34, SU100 and IS2. Germany pins her hopes on the last of the panzer divisions to stop the Soviet advance. The Wehrmacht fields its fearsome tanks like the Stug III, Nashorn and Tiger II for the upcoming battle.

Tank Clash : Eastern Front is the new standalone casual warfare strategy game for 2 players that a noob or skilled player can enjoy. It features its trademark checkers-style armour tracking system with dice rolling and hand management for tactical choices.

It is designed to be modular and 100% compatible with its predecessor Tank Clash : Western Front. Combine Western Front with the Eastern Front for 12 different tank types to battle on a huge terrain map with up to 4 players! Plus all of Eastern Front's new features like battlefield tokens and event cards can be used in Western Front.

All Tank Clash games are designed to be easy-to-learn, quick to setup and played within an hour. Replayability is high as there are different secret missions, tactical & morale cards and modular terrain boards. There is now also a solo mode for both Western and Eastern Front games.

—description from the publisher

It’s 1985 and the city of Miami is being terrorized by a serial killer dubbed the “Miami Madman.” TV star, Craig Flint, has just been discovered dead in his car with the letter "M" cut into his chest. Play detective to crack this gripping murder mystery game, and bring the criminal to justice.

36 PIECES OF PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find newspaper articles, crime scene police reports, photographs, coroners reports, a map, witness statements, and more.

SEARCH THROUGH A POLICE DATABASE - In addition to the physical evidence supplied inside of the box, you'll need to access a database to help you decide which suspects are innocent and which are guilty - but it's not easy...you'll need to be a smart investigator to gain access!

A GREAT NIGHT IN - With approximately 3 hours of game play (depending on your skill level) and a difficulty rating of 4.5/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller will provide a fun night in for all who choose to participate. If you can't crack the case without a secret clue, hints and answers are available online.

Snaffle Original Edition - The game of multiple answer questions, where each player tries to get rid of all their cards first by using each one as the starting letter for their answers.

Steal cards from other players to avoid penalties and block them from winning! A great game for the whole family with a bit of luck, a bit of knowledge and a lot of ‘Snaffling’!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Hardwood Duel, the thrilling two-player basketball card game! From shooting three-pointers to grabbing rebounds to energizing the crowd, Hardwood Duel is everything that we love about basketball! Each player commands their own team, using a deck of cards that represent everything you’d see in a basketball game.  You alternate slapping down cards to throw down huge dunks, hit deep threes, call out quick coaching adjustments, and even get the crowd screaming and yelling and cheering! As you play, you can use the applause generated by your eager fans to add advanced, game-changing cards to your deck. The goal? Score the most points to claim victory!

The game takes moments to learn and only a few minutes to play.  While the rules are simple, mastering Hardwood Duel is a challenge that will keep you coming back over and over and over!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Solar City: Promo Set 6 – Florist Hall
description:
You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.
The promo set contains 2 single-sided tiles.

After this Public Building is built, resolve the activation effect of all Skyscrapers adjacent and diagonally adjacent to this object (max. 8) in any order. To activate some Skyscrapers, you must specify the activated row or column.

description:
You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.
The promo set contains 2 single-sided tiles.

Each time when another player (not you) builds a Public Building in their City, take 2 e-credits from the stack.

boardgame: Solar City: Promo Set 8 – City Hall
description:
You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.
The promo set contains 2 single-sided tiles.

Collect 2 e-credits from the stack each time when you built a new type of Skyscraper in your City.

description:
Popular mini game from Final Fantasy VII Rebirth.
Queen's Blood is a grid-based card game played on a 3x5 board (three rows/lanes and five columns). 
You and your opponent take turns placing cards until all tiles are filled or both of you pass your turn on the same round.
You build a deck which requires 15 cards.
Cards have power/strength denoted by a number on the top-right. There's also a cost to place them on the grid as denoted by pawns on the top-left.
You increase the rank of a position by placing a card.
Many cards have abilities that only affect specific tiles. The goal is to have the higher score in a row/lane compared to your opponent. At the end of the match, only rows where a player had more points will be counted toward the total score.

boardgame: Solar City: Promo Set 9 – Zen Modules
description:
You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.
The promo set contains 2 single-sided tiles.

At the end of each round, you may swap the ZEN MODULE with any other tile in your City. If there are e-credits or VPs on the tiles being swapped, move them along with the tiles.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.
The promo set contains 2 single-sided tiles.

After building the ORANGERY, take 1 e-credit from the stack for each occupied space in your City.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You could have received this promo set for free if you purchased the core game at game conventions or trade shows in Poland.
The promo set contains 2 single-sided tiles.

During final scoring, take 1VP from the pool for each player (including yourself) participating in the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Super Basket is a game of skill. The equipment consists of a cardboard basketball court, two plastic baskets, and two teams of 5 plastic players. The court is divided into 20 squares. Each basketball player is fixed on a catapult-shaped base.

The game begins - as in real sport - with a jump ball. A square can only be occupied by one basketball player. The basketball player who has the ball can either pass to a teammate or shoot. The human player uses the catapult property of the figurine's base. In the event of a successful pass, the thrower can be moved to another empty square. If the pass or shot falls on an empty square, or a square occupied by an opposing basketball player, possession of the ball goes to the opposing team. If, during a pass or shot, the ball hits an opposing figurine, the latter benefits from two free throws.

Halloween mischief is afoot in Girls vs Ghouls, a competitive team game of hidden identities and strategic neighborhood control.

Girls vs Ghouls is a hidden identity game designed for extroverts and introverts alike where victory is achieved through strategic action. With identities hidden until the neighborhood is won, players are free to bluff, betray, befriend, or be silent, enabling everyone to interact in their preferred style on their path to neighborhood dominance.

Set in a cul-de-sac besieged by the undead, a team of cunning Ghouls in disguise scheme to ensnare unsuspecting trick-or-treaters in their haunted houses. Meanwhile, the gutsy neighborhood Girls plot to take back the block, willing to sacrifice their hard-earned Halloween candy to lure the young kids to safety.

Players are secretly assigned to one of the two teams, each side maneuvering for control of eight flippable tile houses around the cul-de-sac. As players move about the neighborhood, they use Treat cards to publicly influence where the trick-or-treaters go, and Trick cards to secretly boobytrap the houses.

Each round ends with the innocent trick-or-treaters visiting the house with the most appealing treats, awarding points and securing the house for its controlling team. Victory goes to the team with the most points after three rounds, tallied from the trick-or-treaters led to their houses plus bonus points for each controlled house.

-description from publisher

Memory-style game to build middle age German swearwords out of pairs. Just for the fun of learning or revisiting middle age  words.

The game plays like memory type B. One word is the first half of the full word, one the second (1:1 match). To avoid unclarities, the matching parts have the same color and similar illustrations - wrong combinations result in funny new creations, adding to the fun. The cards contain also an explanation what the resulting words actually mean (for those that did not know the word before).

boardgame: In vino veritas: Das lateinische Weisheitenspiel
description: Memory-style game to build Latin sayings out of pairs. Just for the fun of learning or revisiting Latin words. The game plays like memory type B. One word is the first half of the full saying, one the second (1:1 match). To avoid unclarities, the matching parts have the same color and similar illustrations - wrong combinations result in funny new creations, adding to the fun. The cards contain also an explanation what the resulting saying actually mean.

Step into the enchanting world of Tilelandia, where creativity meets strategy in a Scrabble-like tile-laying adventure!

Perfect for game night, Tilelandia challenges you to build stunning landscapes while outsmarting your opponents.

Why You'll Love Tilelandia:

Innovative Gameplay: Combine tiles to create intricate personal patterns or use others’ tiles to score big with universal patterns. Every move counts!

Endless Strategy: With just five tiles at a time, you'll need to think ahead and plan your perfect layout to maximize your score.

Interactive Fun: Engage with friends and family as you build off each other’s tiles, creating a dynamic and ever-changing game board.

Beautiful Design: Each tile is a work of art, making the game visually stunning and enjoyable to play.

Whether you're a seasoned gamer or new to tile-laying games, 
Tilelandia offers endless hours of fun and excitement. Get ready to immerse yourself in the ultimate world-building experience!

—description from the designer

In this print at home roll and write game, your fleet has been sent to explore a new alien world. Your mission is to visit three different continents and gather information about the planet and its inhabitants. There’s a promotion in it for the Captain who returns with the most valuable discoveries.

On each continent you will face a range of locations: dealing with aliens, surveying landscapes, exploring ruins and risking the wrath of angry hordes. Choose a team to send on each mission and hope they are the best one to handle the challenges you will face; or maybe you’ll get lucky and a teleporter failure will save you from slaughter. Each round you roll two dice to find a location on a 6x6 grid. After the first roll you can escape, or roll again to find out exactly what you've encountered. If you dare to stay long enough, there are big bonuses for completing rows, columns and diagonals on the grid.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fighting Fantasy Quest: The Warlock of Firetop Mountain
description:
Ever wondered what it would be like to step into the pages of one of the most iconic Fighting Fantasy books written by Sir Ian Livingstone and Steve Jackson? Relive the magic of the book in an exhilarating new format! Your quest begins now!

Fighting Fantasy Quest: The Warlock of Firetop Mountain strictly follows the events of the original book, and it takes players through an extensive campaign—not a randomized dungeon system.

The game brings the legendary book to life as an immersive board game. Encounter every monster, discover every item, and relive every thrilling scene from the original, but with an exciting twist! The story has been expanded, offering brand new content and surprises at every turn. Even seasoned fans will find new challenges and adventures to conquer. Like in the original book, Firetop Mountain challenges you at every step and failure is expected. Learn from your experience and overcome the dungeon with renewed vigor and wit.

Dive into the adventure with up to 4 players (SOLO + COOPERATIVE GAME) as you explore the treacherous mountain! Battle your way through both familiar foes and brand-new enemies! Engage in epic boss battles that demand fresh and unexpected tactics to emerge victorious!

—description from the publisher

Battle Eternal is a trick taking game played over a random number of hands in which the deck evolves as you play. Each hand, or Battle, players will attempt to gain Trophies to score Victory Points to add to their score while trying to undermine the scoring opportunities for their opponents. Cards come and go from the main deck as the abilities of the various fighting Factions come into play.

Panem et Circenses ("Bread and Circuses") is a light hearted symbol matching card game set in ancient Rome. You are performers in a Roman Arena, desperately trying to satisfy the entertainment needs of an ever changing clientele. Each round you will play cards to try to score highest in the categories desired by the current spectators in an attempt to earn the most money.

Hear me, spirit, and speak true; a secret cracked marks the sharpest clue!

Specter Secrets is a deduction card game set in 1940s Egypt, where players can either be guests at a chilling séance or the haunting spirit returning from the dead to reveal dreadful tales of murder.

With 15 thrilling mysteries across three difficulty levels to choose from and 30 occult cards featuring original artwork inspired by Middle Eastern folklore, the game immerses players in a world of suspense. Guests use the deck to commune with the Specter, ask questions, and follow clues to solve the mystery. The first Guest to crack the case wins, alongside the Specter, who acts as gamemaster.

Choose a hero and his dragon's egg and begin a long journey through 4 increasingly dangerous realms to Dragon Mount. Roll a dice to move and fight different monster, collect equipment, buy upgrade and do heroic act to be the Hero with the most points at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Choississez un héros et son oeuf de dragon et entreprenez un long voyage a travers 4 royaumes de plus en plus dangeureux jusqu'au Mont Dragon.

—description from the publisher

The Blues kick off and take the even numbers, i. e. they are entitled to cast dice and to storm into their adversary's half — one number by every cast — until their charge is stopped by rollingan odd number. In this event, the Reds, taking the odd numbers, lead the game and endeavor to regain the lost ground and to reach the goal of the Blues. As long as odd numbers are thrown, the Reds push forward. When they fail, the Blues take the lead. In an interesting match the winning chances go to and fro until goals are scored. Goals can only be kicked from Nr. 7. or the penalty spot.

According to mutual understanding, winner will be who has scored 3 or 5 goals.

—user summary

A postcard game distributed in BANZAI Magazine No. 14, first game of the irregular series "World Bad Strategy".

The Swedish army, along with the Danish army, planned an operation to retake Denmark on May 18, 1945, but by then the war had ended with Germany's surrender.

What if the plan is activated a little earlier than planned? Or what if the end of the war had been postponed?
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A card game where you earn points by adopting cats and caring for them. There are 4 kinds of cats: Creative, Gourmet, Sports, and Fancy. There are also 4 kinds of care cards: Entertain, Food, Play, and Fancy. The Fancy cards do special things. Cats are adopted by paying the cost for them in care cards. Care cards can also be played on cats you've already adopted. Fancy care cards are special and can be played in many different ways, some on any cat.

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo – Gen Con 2024 Window Pattern Card
description:
Foil promo card sold or given out at the Floodgate Games booth during GenCon 2024.
This unique pattern card can be used in game.

boardgame: Things in Rings: Pop Culture Promo Pack
description:
30 extra Thing cards related to our favorite objects in cinema, fantasy, comics, and more. Expand your gameplay and enjoy more great art, for cards like:

Hoverboard
Mjölnir
Starfighter
Ring
Necronomicon
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: This promo pack adds three new map cards for Landmarks.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Double-Necked Lute Promo Card
description:
A prize card given out as a promo during the 2024 convention circuit and also during the 2024 Extra Life event. Text reads:

You may play this Prize as an Action Card with the effect "Each other player pays you 1 Gold." When you play this card, add it to the Prize Bin.

HOW TO PLAY: Start by shaking the dice in the shaker included in our family game for kids and adults, and rolling them out to reveal your letters! Use the bonus point cards to enhance your word combinations and score points. Play against the sand timer, challenging yourself and your friends during family game night to think fast and create words on the fly. Perfect for 2+ players, Word Roll is an endless card game full of creativity, making it an ideal addition to your party games!

—description from the publisher

Work of the JCG is a tabletop exercise based on the operations of the Japan Coast Guard, assuming the first half of the 2020s.

The player takes on the role of an integrated response coordinator (a fictional role) who is responsible for the Security Division, Security Information Division, Rescue Division, Environmental Disaster Prevention Division, and Marine Information Division of the Japan Coast Guard's Security and Rescue Department, and aims to operate patrol boats, aircraft, SST (special security team), special rescue team, and mobile disaster prevention team in an "appropriate organization" and "appropriate scale."

The map for the tabletop exercise covers the "vast" Japanese territorial waters and exclusive economic zone under the jurisdiction of the Japan Coast Guard.

Every large patrol vessel owned by the JCG is included as a single unit, and all major types of medium-sized and smaller patrol vessels also appear.

In this tabletop exercise, you will experience the difficulties of engaging in a wide variety of "works," including territorial waters security, such as the Senkaku Islands, which have become a hot topic in recent years, as well as rescue missions and suspicious ships, which were a focus of attention by Umizaru, marine surveys of the continental shelf, and environmental protection, such as marine pollution.

Battle of Wada is a two-player game of Wada Yoshimori's rebellion against the Kamakura shogunate in 1213.

50 counters are divided into 19 Wada's units, 27 Kamakura shogunate's units.

Each turn represents about 1 hour. Each unit represents about 50-200 soldiers.

Issue game of "Bushi Life" #5.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Welcome to San Jose, "the capital of Silicon Valley," where California's third largest city has something for everyone.

Visit favorite local eateries Original Joe's and Peters' Bakery, stop by the Winchester Mystery House, or take time to smell the flowers at the world famous rose gardens. Spark your curiosity at museums such as the Children's Discovery Museum and then take the afternoon off and pay homage to the industry for which San Jose is known around the world, at The Tech Interactive.

Better yet, ditch the car on gameday and take any of the convenient Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's trains or buses to one of the numerous sporting venues, and catch a game or event.

There is no shortage of things to do, see, learn, and experience in the San Jose edition of MONOPOLY.

Includes a Custom Gameboard, 1 Instruction Sheet, 1 Deck of 60 Cards, 1 Money Pack, 8 Tokens, 2 Dice, 32 Houses, 12 Hotels

Gameplay of the San Jose Edition of Monopoly is the same as the classic version of Monopoly, where players travel around the board purchasing properties and improving them while hoping to avoid paying rent on other players' properties. Avoid bankruptcy and end the game with the most money to win.

Embark on an exhilarating adventure with our game expansion! Expect gameplay brimming with excitement, as events unfold and each player gains a unique goal to strive for. The beach is scattered with surprises, and fate might favor you – or take you by surprise!

Inside the box: 9 Surprise tokens and 35 event cards including events, challenges and asymmetric starting cards.

—description from the publisher

How far will you go to seize power? Choose your allies wisely in the race for the papal throne...

1492, the Borgia Pope ascends the papal throne following a series of underhanded plots... you, of course, disagree and will use the same tactics to take his place. Defrocked priests, bloodthirsty sanctified soldiers, and adulterous courtesans will become your everyday associates.

With the game and a simple die, you will compete against your friends for possession of Ally cards residing in the city. Choose your Allies carefully among different families: Borgia, Condottierri, Artista, Ambasciatore, and Sanctifico, each offering unique advantages.

You must skillfully maneuver to give your budding empire a chance to overthrow the Pope card and achieve victory. Decide whether to enhance your Corruption skills or master the art of offering Redemption to Ally cards. Spend your Virtue points to gain an edge over your opponents and avoid precious time spent in Penitence.

Finally, take on roles with the Personality and Influence cards, which will only provide benefits if you fulfill silly tasks. Debase yourself, cast aside your principles, take the risk of ridicule, and you might have a chance to become Pope before your opponents.

boardgame: Barcelona: Passeig de Gràcia – New Building Bonus Tiles
description:
Promo from Board&Dice when you pre-ordered the Passeig de Gràcia expansion at their website. Was also available at Gen Con 2024.

Content: 4 alternate building bonus tiles.

Set-up: Replace all 4 of the Building Bonus tiles with the Alternative ones. Place them face up, at random, in their dedicated spaces on the main board during step 1 of the setup. Then, proceed with the setup normally.

Description: Their effects are:
Gain 1 Cloth or 1 Coin
Gain 5 Victory Points
Increase the joy value of the Gràcia Pedestrian by 1, and gain 2 Victory Points
Increase the joy value of the Barcelona Pedestrian by 1, and gain 2 Victory Points

boardgame: Reef Project: New Sea Tiles Promo
description:
Small promo expansion for the Reef Project. It includes 2 new Seafloor Exploration Tiles and 2 new Oxygen Tank Tiles.

Components:
2 Seafloor Exploration tiles
2 Oxygen Tank tiles

Was available at Gen Con 2024.

Small promo expansion for the Reef Project. It includes 5 additional Mission Cards.

Was available at Gen Con 2024.

boardgame: Turn Up Sharks Returns: The super B-class shark strikes back!
description: Turn Up Shark Returns is a card game inspired by B-movie shark thrillers. In the game, you play as a character in a shark movie, trying to survive until the end while being pursued by sharks. The rules are simple: on your turn, you flip over one or two cards. If the total shark strength exceeds the scene's maximum limit, your character dies. To survive, you’ll need to strategically use weapons and flags to keep the sharks at bay.

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: A Wealth of Murder
description:
When six billionaires get together for a weekend wellness retreat to find meaning in their lives, their host’s personal assistant, Tim, winds up dead. The other assistant there, Tim’s friend Jill, now needs your help to uncover the truth about what happened and bring Tim’s killer to justice. The only problem is, no one saw anything suspicious that night. At least, that’s what they all say.

Cash in your deduction skills to uncover which rich guest killed Tim!

The gameplay features multiple codes and relies heavily on identification, close reading, and logic.

All the evidence in this case is physical - no need for internet access, so you can take it with you anywhere.

Rated 4/5 difficulty.

Squad, up with the plushiest version of the classic family game you'll ever play! This original Squishmallows Collector's Edition of MONOPOLY lets players buy, sell, and trade their favourites from the Classic Squad to the Fantasy Squad, including Fifi the Fox, Rosie the Pig, Leonard the Lion, and Benny the Bigfoot! Start your journey with an exclusive 4" Cam the Cat plush, then pick from one of the iconic custom Squishmallows tokens: will you choose the Headphones, the Rainbow, or the Feelin' Mallow logo? Set up valuable Squads and Collections to charge your opponents more rent, and pick up Squish Squad and Mallow Days cards for unexpected prizes and penalties. Will you end up partying with Cam, or stumble across a rare Squishmallows edition? Collect every space on the board and outsmart your opponents to be the last one with Squishcash and win the game! Just watch out for those tax spaces and try not to end up in Jail!

Includes: 1 Game Board, 6 Collectible Tokens (Cam Silhouette, Fellin’ Mallow, Headphones, Rainbow, Sunglasses, and Hang Tag), 28 Title Deed Cards, 16 Chance Cards renamed “Squish Squad” Cards, 16 Community Chest Cards renamed “Mallow Days” Cards, Custom Monopoly Money, 32 Houses renamed Squads, 12 Hotels renamed Collections, 2 Dice, Rules.

Warhamster The Game is an incredible collection of miniatures for 3D printing (STL) and at the same time a complete board game in Print and Play format. Although you can also enjoy the range of miniatures or the board game independently (in the latter case, the miniatures are replaced by tokens).

A project prepared with great doses of enthusiasm, care and, above all, humor. Ideal for introducing the youngest or novices to the world of wargames, board games and miniature painting. But also very enjoyable for those of us who are more experienced in this world!

Warhamster The Game is a tactical miniatures game for 2 players, ages 8 and up. Each of them controls a band of three Hamsters that face each other on a square Board with the aim of getting more Victory Points (VP) than the rival.

Each character has Attributes and Special Skills, as well as a Dice Pool that must be managed to perform actions.

The game ends after five Turns and has an estimated duration of 45 minutes.

All board game content will be available in English and Spanish.
Lost Roots is the 1st official expansion for Foxpaw. It includes the forgotten 5th house Evergrow, also a Solo Mode and some modules for the core game.

The gates of Hell opened up and swallowed our world whole. Brave heroes stand against the hordes from Hell.

Hell: Legends is a hack & slay dungeon crawl board game you play cooperatively or in solitaire mode. Slay monsters and complete objectives to gain XP and level up your characters to make them more powerful. Each character offers a specialized skill tree. Each skill offers various levels. In combination with powerful items, each character is built exactly as you want.

Hell has 3 game modes Legends (story-driven - requires an app), Hunt, and Defense, allowing players to change up their game experience and victory requirements.

This game has mature themes!

—description from the designer

This game is attached to issue 26 of the ioGioco magazine.
A small group of rebel Elf Dragon Riders (you) will attempt a desperate sortie with a precise mission: to target the prison with vials of wildfire to create havoc and facilitate the escape of as many prisoners as possible.
Make reconnaissance flights, aim carefully at flammable structures (warehouses, kitchens, barns, etc.) and at the right moment free another rebel brother. He will know how to reward you by enhancing your powers!

In the game we have to free the fugitives by satisfying the requests of their objective cards. To do this we will have to place tiles on the prison map by covering the symbols and creating areas with contiguous symbols. These moves will advance the tokens on tracks, if the required values ​​are reached on these tracks the fugitive will be free.

boardgame: So, You've Been Cursed.: Weird Livestock promo
description:
The sheep going missing was bad enough, not to mention the villagers who are quite fond of them also going missing.

But this? This… is a truly worrying development… A whole menagerie of aberrations seems to be appearing in the village, and not only at night!

It seems like that nasty curse that’s been going around has jumped species and resulted in some really weird animals showing up in the pastures.

Now, the villagers are crafty, and with the Wizard’s help, they can probably make good use of them. But then again, the Monster might want to diversify their diet…

The Weird Livestock promo introduces six fantastic animals that scare but also fascinate the village.

As the Wizard, you will be rewarded with coins or ingredients by taking the right Weird Livestock animal to the right villager.

As the Monster, eating one of the Weird Livestock animals offers strange new abilities that can augment your regular turns.

The Legendary Box comes with the Barrage base game and the Far Companies Expansion. This expansion includes rules for two new companies to be used in addition to the four base ones and the fifth one already included in The Leeghwater Project expansion. These two companies have unique characteristics that significantly distinguish them from the standard companies both in terms of game mechanics and components.

- The Japanese company uses Bases and Elevations inspired by the “Earth-fill dams”: on one hand, it is possible to use the Elevations as if they were Bases, greatly increasing the number of Dams that can be built; on the other hand, these dams will be limited in height and thus in capacity. Finally, the machinery used for the Elevations are Excavators instead of Concrete Mixers.

- The Brazilian company specializes in versatility: Bases, Elevations, and Conduits can be built in any order from its board, with a greater choice of strength and type of bonuses; however, the elements related to stronger bonuses will result in the permanent loss of one machinery or more.

Both companies unlock a special company ability when the third powerhouse is built.

The Legendary Box was also sold by itself (no games included, just inserts).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Unconscious Mind: Tantrum House Season 10 Promo Card
description:
Unconscious Mind promo from Tantrum House season 10 Kickstarter

Manifest Dream card: Get 3 therapie (insights) and receive 3 therapy points (hearts) and 5 victory points

Some dog jobs out there are just a little bit... weird. But the most skilled recruiters know how to harness the unique talents of these dogs and tackle some incredible work!

The Odd Jobs expansion works by allowing you to swap a set of dogs in and out with your base game, offering a unique deck for each match.

This game was included with issue 30 of the ioGioco magazine.

Ryoan-ji (Temple of the Peaceful Dragon) is located in the northwest area of ​​Kyoto and is the ideal refuge to escape the chaos of the city and spend time immersed in the quiet of the Zen garden: the perfect place for meditation. The garden of Ryoan-ji is formed by a rectangle of 248 square meters and can be observed from the veranda of the hojo, the residence of the monastery's abbot. It features fifteen stones of different sizes arranged in five groups: a group of five stones, two groups of three and two groups of two. The stones are arranged in the middle of a raked gravel floor. The garden was conceived as a koan, a paradoxical riddle that aids in meditation. The arrangement of the stones was designed so that, sitting on the veranda, from whatever angle you look, you cannot see all fifteen stones at the same time. Traditionally, it is believed that only by reaching enlightenment you can see them all at once. Who will be able to do this?

Participants compete to set up a zen garden that can guarantee the best meditation conditions.
Dice will be rolled each round, allowing everyone to obtain resources with which to complete shapes to draw in the garden. By acquiring the most advanced shapes, obtaining bonuses and achieving the objectives of the Stone Cards, they will obtain meditation points. The player with the most meditation points will be the winner.

Suit up your team with the latest supplies! Whether it's sleek eye protection or tactical outerwear, the mechanics in this expansion will kick your team into high gear!

Gear cards work by giving your team brand new special 1 time actions! They are earned by completing jobs, drawing 3 pieces of gear and selecting 1 to add to your hand. Any unused gear at the end of the game grants you 2 additional points each.

Intrepid turns Moonrollers into a Moonrakers expansion. Use the Moonrollers dice to modify an odd collection of Moonrakers ship parts, crew, and contracts.

These 20 exclusive Moonrakers cards are only available with the purchase of Moonrollers. The actions each card will take depends on the roll of the Moonrollers' dice, and hopefully your luck won't run out!

Race your way to victory with Dungeon Kart, an all-new game set in the world of Boss Monster. Dungeon Kart transforms the multiplayer mayhem of kart racing into a turn-based tactical tabletop experience. Play as one of the baddest Bosses around while using Spells to disrupt other racers, grab coins, and power yourself up! With a lot of skill and a little luck, you can cross the finish line first and show your opponents who’s Boss!

Unique dashboards allow players to rev up their engines and increase their speed. Be careful though…if you go too fast then you have more chances to run into hazards and obstacles that will cause you to crash or spin out!

Use spells to ensure victory! Spells are items that you can use to power your Boss up or to disrupt other racers! The power of Spells depend on the place of your racer…so be careful as you move along the track since other players are trying to sabotage you!

—description from the publisher

Keep track of your races with the leader board. In the Gold edition you can now race over icy terrain with two new racers and abilities. Also, new Void tiles create additional hazards to avoid.

Gold Edition includes:

Base Game
Cerebellus Racer and card
Silver Slicer Dashboard
Void Tile Set
Icy Peak Tile Set
Brothers Wise Racer and card
Dry Erase Leader Board
40 Upgraded Metal Coin tokens

Base game contains:

- 15 Double-Sided Map Tiles
- 8 Spell Markers
- 10 Acrylic Racers
- 1 Ghost Kart Standee
- 10 Racer Cards with Placement Tokens
- 1 First Player Coin
- Hazard Tokens
- 8 Dashboards with Stands
- 11 Epic Hero Tokens and Cards
- 3 Decks of Spell Cards
- 3 Placement Tracking Boards and Finish Lines
- 2 Dice
- 8 Reference Cards
- 8 Team Markers
- 1 Token Bag
- 1 Rulebook
- 1 Racing Guide

Average Performers is an innovative party game that turns traditional gaming on its head by making mediocrity the goal! Perfect for groups of 4-8 players and suitable for ages 10 and up, this game dares you to perfectly balance your skills, luck, and strategic thinking to achieve a score as close to zero as possible. Each fast-paced round lasts between 30-45 minutes, making it an ideal choice for family game nights, teen gatherings, or adult parties.

The game includes 200 Task Cards that mix quirky challenges with strategic Advantage Cards, 112 Rank Cards to assess performance, and 8 Dry Erase Boards and Markers for dynamic in-game tasks, along with a 30-second Sand Timer that adds a thrilling rush to each turn. Whether it's a casual evening with friends or a competitive game night, Average Performers offers endless fun and laughter. Its unique scoring system, where being average is the key to victory, provides a refreshing twist and makes it a standout addition to any board game collection. Dive into the hilarity of achieving perfect ordinariness with Average Performers—a game where every player can enjoy the thrill of being extraordinarily average!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Eclipse Realms: Rise of the Cosmic Sovereigns
description:
Eclipse Realms: Rise of the Cosmic Sovereigns is a captivating strategy board game that immerses players in a richly detailed sci-fi universe. Designed for 1-6 players, this game combines deep strategic gameplay with dynamic storytelling, making each session a unique experience.

Players take on the role of powerful leaders vying for control of the galaxy. Through diplomacy, tactical battles, and resource management, they must navigate a web of alliances and conflicts to rise as the ultimate Cosmic Sovereign.

—description from the designer

Witching Hour is a special promo box that not only adds two new characters to Marvel United: Agatha Harkness as an Anti-Hero and Scarlet Witch, for the first time as a solo Villain, but also includes the four alternate Hero decks that were previously available mainly at conventions: Grey Hulk, Juggernaut, Mister Sinister, and Deadpool (X-Force).

To fully flesh out these alternate versions, this box also includes copies of their original game pieces, cast in their new appropriate colors. The back of their cards are marked as “2nd Printing” to distinguish them from the original convention runs.

boardgame: Lewd Dungeon Adventures: The Card Game
description:
From the creator of the popular tabletop role-playing game for couples comes a card game of the same name, giving players a chance for a fun, boozy, intimate adventure they can pick up and play. The game is relatively simple, with the objective to collect ten cards from a mix of DRINK, MONSTER, and SEX cards. DRINK cards require you to compete with your mate in a drinking game. MONSTER cards will have you battling it out. SEX cards require competing in an intimate competition, most revolving around foreplay. SPELL cards are thrown into the mix to aid in battling monsters or sabotaging your opponent. It’s all the best of Lewd Dungeon Adventures condensed into a 42 (possibly more with unlocked stretch goals) card deck, creating a date night you won’t soon forget.

Object: The first player to collect 10 cards in total wins the game. Only Drink, Monster, and Sex cards count toward this total.

GAME MODIFICATIONS FOR FRIENDS AND NON-DRINKERS
We realize that not everyone drinks alcohol. And sometimes, you just want to take a game and play it with your friends. Much like our tabletop role-playing game, we've come up with rules modifications to make the game replayable in a variety of situations.

If you’d like to remove one type of card (either DRINK or SEX), the first player to collect 5 cards wins.

If you’d like to remove both the DRINK and SEX Cards, the first player to collect 3 cards wins.

boardgame: Lewd Dungeon Adventures: The Card Game – Gobblin Monster Promo Card
description:
Exclusive Gobblin Monster Card for Backerkit backers

Card text:

GOBBLIN
(Health) 7
(Roll to hit) 4
Close: 1d4
Ranged: 1d4
Normandy Tank Battle 1944 is a game of tactical combat between German tanks and Allied tanks during the Battle of Normandy, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #69.

During this cooperative game, each player will have to make the others guess their travel picture with the help of smells that can recall its geographic location, its colour or even what it basically represents.

This game presents 70 travel pictures taken across the world along with 30 smells. Some associations are easy to make, like the smell of lavender for a Provence landscape in France. But other times, players will have to get creative because the smell of leather, algae or lemongrass are not always associated with the same ideas depending on the players.

With this olfactory trip, get ready to live some fabulous sensory adventures with your family or friends.

Thalamus is an intense abstract strategy game where players are aiming to eliminate their opponents by navigating and manipulating an ever-changing board that is filled with dangerous Deflector Disks.

Players take turns utilising one of the six available actions: MOVE, PUSH, PULL, SHOOT, ROTATE, or REMOVE to trap opponents and avoid being eliminated themselves.

Thalamus has simple yet strategically rich and exciting gameplay designed to invoke feelings of traditional abstract strategy games.

—description from the designer

Dogs and Cats have been battling since the beginning of time. Go head to head in this 2 player card game to decide which faction will come out on top.

Use strategy, card actions, resource management and dice rolling to win the battlefield.

Players start with 6 characters each and the object of the game is simple, defeat all of your opponents. Using gems you can summon characters or purchase actions and weapons. During your turn roll dice to match attack requirements and  complete attacks. Use weapons or actions cards to boost your attack damage.

—description from the publisher

An edition of Monopoly set in the Italian city of Monopoli, taking advantage of an obvious pun since the two words are homophones (the back of the box reads: "Mr. Monopoly torna a casa" - translated: "Mr. Monopoly goes back home!"). The spaces on the game track are named after real roads and monuments from the city; the gameplay stays the same as the original.

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Cardinal / Say's Firefly Promo Card
description:
Promo Card handed out at Gen Con 2024. Card P008.

Cardinal Ability: Draw 1 card per Shrub in your play area. 5 Points
Say’s firefly Ability: points according to number of fireflies in your play area.

An exciting Bot pack for Rumblebots!
 These 5 new Bots come with alternative endgame strategies, additional building dynamics and tricky low-tier surprises. 
When you're fighting for the crystals... better call for reinforcements!

This 11-card pack includes two copies each of five new Bots (Smashroom, Flock Commander, Bearserk, Necroforge, and Botsai), and a card with rules and FAQs.

—description from the designer

Iràs i no Tornaràs is a cooperative board game where players play as heroes of Balearic fairy tales. Your mission is to explore the map, gather resources, obtain magical tokens, and overcome obstacles set by the devil to complete the tale and win the game.

At the start, players choose a tale to play and set up the corresponding map as indicated by the first card in the tale deck. One player is designated as the main hero, and each player selects a hero card and places their token on the starting celestial tile. The main hero reads the next two cards from the tale deck to set the scene for the adventure.

The game map consists of 20 hexagonal tiles representing mountains, plains, and coasts. Tiles start unexplored and are revealed as players progress, each revealing unique locations. Heroes begin on the celestial tile, while the infernal tile remains off-limits but plays a special role in the game.

Each hero has a unique ability and must pass virtue tests to overcome challenges. These tests, based on wisdom, ingenuity, or courage, require rolling virtue dice to meet or exceed the difficulty level indicated on the challenge card. Successfully navigating these tests and working together will allow the heroes to complete their quest and advance the tale.

Expand your roster of heroes with a powerful magician! Summon mighty elementals and experience a fantastical side-quest. The box includes the Hero Deck for the Summoner of Elements and a side-quest for your Aventuria campaign.

—description from the publisher

Expand your roster of heroes with a tricky rascal! Play tricks on your opponents and experience a unexpected side-quest. The box includes the Hero Deck for the Champion of Mischief and a side-quest for your Aventuria campaign.

—description from the publisher

Expand your roster of heroes with a mighty shapeshifter! Transform into a manticore and experience a dangerous side-quest. The box includes the Hero Deck for the Scion of the Manticore and a side-quest for your Aventuria campaign.

—description from the publisher

Chrono Bibliotheca immerses players in a mental world where they gain access to a mystical library that consolidates all kinds of information. Players utilize this vast repository of knowledge to overcome significant events in the real world, find and rescue comrades in distress, and introduce unknown technologies to society. The library's unique characteristic is its connection to all moments in time, allowing players to move forward or backward along multiple timelines during the game.

The gameplay revolves around strategic time travel and information gathering. Players must navigate various time periods to solve problems that were initially unsolvable and save allies who were previously unreachable. Each time a player rewinds time, it advances steadily, and players are constrained by the rule that they cannot return to a time before a moment they've already traveled back to. Additionally, the game is limited to 8 rounds, representing the finite time flow within the library.

The main goal of Chrono Bibliotheca is to save the world and rescue comrades by efficiently utilizing the ability to rewind time and the knowledge obtained from the library. Players must make the most of their limited time and carefully plan their actions across different timelines to achieve these objectives.

This game challenges players to think critically about time management and strategic planning while exploring the intriguing concept of a library that holds the key to navigating and manipulating time.

—description from the publisher

Your backpacking trip through the lush greenery of the Irish countryside is interrupted by a strange visitor. This giant man, calling himself The Dagda, presents you with a series of challenges to overcome. Only the clever and pure of heart will solve them all and gain entry to Tír na nÓg!

Journey to Tír na nÓg is a game in the 18Escape series of cooperative puzzle games from Grand Gamers Guild. In each game, you solve a full-length, escape-room-style mystery using just 18 playing cards! Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

This game uses a website to check your answers and provide hints and information. When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and you will be instructed on what to do next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use.

—description from the publisher

The villains have created a coordinated plan to set bomb traps throughout the city. The heroes need to use their skills to chase the villains and arrest enough of them before the traps explode. However, the villains are cunning and try to confuse the heroes, causing them to accidentally trigger the traps instead of saving the city. Guardian Super Force is a cooperative game where all players work together to help the heroes capture the villains and save the city. Are you ready to help the heroes defeat the villains?

A cooperative or semi-cooperative game for the whole family that reinvents the idea of the memory game, where knowing how to use the powers of the cards is essential.

—description from the designer

A secret promo released at GenCon 2024.

A small, two-sided square card that can be used in any game of Final Girl and gives the following power:

"During a game of Final Girl, if you flip your black Final Health token and it is blank, treat it as if it had a single [heart]. *This card is good for only a single use."

The goal of the game is to get the same result on your five dice as quickly as possible.
Each player starts by rolling their dice and placing them in front of them and drawing a card into their hand. On your turn, you draw a card and then play one of the cards and do what is written on the card. Some examples of what can happen then are that you can change any die on the table to whatever you want, you can flip any number of your dice, everyone sends 1 die to the right and more.
The first player to successfully change their dice to the same thing wins.

In this card game for 3 to 5 players, the cards represent not only the four quarters of the Medicine Wheel, but also several other elemental features of Anishinaabe culture. The aspect of the game that sets it apart is the “sowing” of cards around the table in a mancala fashion to build sets for yourself, at the same time sharing cards with other players.
Each player has a tableau of four cards to build sets on, and a hand of four cards to play from. There is also a set of "resource" cards that players can access under certain conditions. By "sowing" cards around the table, they can build sets on other player's tableaus to add to their own score.

—description from the designer

Spectra is a game for two to four players that employs the same type of logic as the game of Nim. Placing cubes on the board, players hope to end each round with a majority of cubes on as many spaces as possible, thus scoring points. But the order of play and distribution of cubes can have millions of potential combinations. It’s a bit of a brain-teaser, but easy enough so that players of any age (above 8) will have fun working out a strategy. The small format makes Spectra a good choice to take on vacation or family outings!

—description from the designer

Mermaid's Song is a card game for 2-6 players where you try to claim leadership of the seas by enticing the creatures that inhabit it to your side.

Sing your Song: Use your Cards to play mermaids, and have them sing one of two songs to gain points. Be careful, opposing songs cancel each other out, and the other candidates are more than happy to mess with your melody!

Gain their Support: Once your song hits a crescendo, finish your song to entice one of the available denizens of the deep to give you their support! Do you finish early to grab one of the more easily swayed but less influential creatures? Or do you risk your opponents ruining your song to aim for the real powerhouses?

Beware the Kraken: Hiding amongst the cards lies the mighty Kraken. Once played, all players must remove their most influential mermaid! Always keep it's threat in mind, and set up your opponents for failure one he arrives.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Little Mermaid

description:
Warriors & Writings is a print & play, roll & write, fantasy dungeon crawler. The Sword, Spell & Squid expansion includes the following new content:

The Bladecaster is a versatile fighter that balances the use of magic and martial skills instead of specializing in either. Like the Mage, they use mana to power some of their actions and talents, but they regain spent mana by making melee attacks instead of by walking. They have a mix of talents, some of which enhance their melee attacks, while others add ranged and area-of-effect options.

In The River Rush, your boat is rushing down the river towards the maw of a kraken. The giant squid is trying to tear the boat apart with its tentacles before you can reach the deadly beast. And these armored tortoises are just getting in your way! This encounter features a boat which is a movable terrain feature that can also be destroyed

—description from the designer

Trivia game about flags and countries with 500 questions in 5 categories.
The game is played with cards only, no board. From a deck of different categories, each player plays one card at a time and is then asked a question related to that category.

Mischievous Kitten family board game: your goal is to get your cat from backyard to the blanket using the Cat's Fate spinner. You can choose one of the 16 character to do so. During your journey you MUST do one of a lot of basic movements, but also you can do more sophisticated movements, paying with a skein of wool - you can consider this as money in this game.

—description from the designer

W tej przeuroczej grze planszowej Waszym zadaniem będzie dotarcie swoim kotkiem z pola START na kocyk z napisem META przez kolorowe, pełne kocich ścieżek podwórko, zanim zrobią to inni gracze. Po drodze czeka Was wiele wyzwań i psotek: będziecie biegać, skakać, popychać się, drzemać, polować na skróty, rzucać motkami sobie lub innym graczom, odciskać kocią łapę, chodzić przez rozwidlenia - emocje gwarantowane!

Jaką taktykę wybierzesz dla swojego kotka? Będziesz go faworyzował czy raczej postanowisz utrudnić realizację pomysłów na zwycięstwo innym graczom? Będziesz niegrzeczny czy ambitny? Czy nauczysz się przegrywać, żeby jeszcze lepiej smakowała wygrana przy kolejnej rozgrywce?

—description from the designer (Polish)

Send your characters flying down the ramp, and get the most points by doing advanced tricks and landing perfectly.
Super Ski Jump is played in three rounds. Players take turns jumping once each round. At the beginning of each round, a player can choose freely from among the four characters and game pieces. At the start of each round, players can choose to change the height of the ramp, which has three different heights of difficulty.
Once a player has selected a game piece, it is placed at the top of the ramp. To start the jump, the player turns a wheel to send the character down the ramp. At the bottom of the ramp is a lever that the player presses to send the character flying. You keep track of the number of rotations and landings and get points based on the selected game piece and the number of rotations and landings.
The player with the most points after three jumps wins.

Our sheep are grazing peacefully and freely in the green meadows, but disaster is looming in the form of a huge, evil tornado that will tear up everything in its path. As responsible shepherds, our mission is to get our flock to safety and lead them safely to the pens, but this is not as easy as it seems.
What´s more, our decisions will have an effect on the flocks of other players. The first player to get their flock to safety is the winner! The path hides many perils and traps, and the tornado will always be waiting and changing direction.

Each player in turn rolls the two dice, where one shows the number of steps and the other which event occurs. The different events are that you either move the tornado (a plastic tornado with a built-in fan), put out or move a fence or do nothing and lose your turn. If one of your sheep is caught by the tornado, try to catch your sheep as it flies into the air. If one misses, that sheep has to start all over again. The first player to get their sheep to safety wins.

More Cards Against Humanity comes with 600 expansion cards that instantly double the replayability and girth of your deck.

- If you’ve never bought an expansion and you want more Cards Against Humanity, buy More Cards Against Humanity.
- It’s got all the best jokes from our old Red Box, Blue Box, and Green Box expansions, plus 50 cards we’ve never printed before.
- Shiny!

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Out of Line
description:
Out of Line is a big expansion and a whole new way to play Cards Against Humanity.

- Each round, you secretly rank five white cards along an interesting spectrum—such as “Good in butt” to “Bad in butt”—and then everyone else has to figure out how the fuck you ranked them.
- Comes with 100 different spectrums and 150 all-new white cards.
- Works with all the CAH cards you already own.

-description from publisher's website

The only Cards Against Humanity pack written by a foul-minded cat.

- 30 new cards selected live by Noelle of @bap.kat.
- Includes an actual lock of Noelle's actual hair.
- Packed in our incredible "Pussy Pouch" clutch alongside an exclusive Cards Against Humanity catnip toy and a heart locket for the hair.
- This pack will be disgusting, we promise.

-description from publisher's website

Europe's 45 countries have been divided into 6 regions, with each region having its own special color. Get around the game board's flag squares, visit countries and answer questions. If you answer correctly, you will be rewarded with money (EQ). But it is in the unique Knowledge Level that you can make the really big cut! Here you have to answer 5 questions in a row and the more countries you have visited in a region, the more money you can earn.
Much of what happens during the game you keep track of with the help of your EQ pass. Here you mark which countries you visited and when you went through the Knowledge Steps. When a player or team has gone through the Knowledge Stage 1 time/region, that is 6 times, the game is over. The player who has then accumulated the most money (EQ) has won.

boardgame: House of the Dragon: Dark Dealings
description:
In House of the Dragon: Dark Dealings, players must use both their strength and their wits to emerge victorious. Will you forge alliances and honor your word...or will you betray and deceive as you strive for control of the realm?

Each player starts the game with a deck of fifteen cards. Each deck features cards valued 1-3 in three suits — economic, military, and political — as well as character cards with effects and deceit cards that show one suit at top and another at bottom.

In each round, players each start with four cards in hand and simultaneously reveal a strength token to indicate the suit of their highest-valued card; in a tie, they choose which suit to reveal. The round's start player places one card inside a vault, stating which suit they played, whether true or false. They then choose who adds a card next, and the vault has a tiny window in it that allows this person to see the suit color and value of the card previously added.

Once everyone has added a card, open the vault and see which suit has the highest combined strength. Deceit cards count as their bottommost suit in this tally; character effects can adjust these totals. Each player who contributed a card to this suit draws a scoring card matching this suit; scoring cards are worth 2-4 points. If all cards are of the same suit, only the round's first player scores.

After a set number of rounds, players tally their final score, counting the value of their highest scoring card in each suit, plus 1 point for each additional card.

boardgame: Guess My Ride! 2-Player Automotive Card Game!
description:
Welcome to Guess My Ride! an exciting addition to family game night! Created by two kids who share a love for cars and card games, this game promises hours of fun for all ages.

A guessing game where you have to ask Yes or No questions to figure out who your opponents driver is! Like is your car blue? Does it have a spoiler? Are your rims red? and more!

With its engaging gameplay and car-themed illustrations, Guess My Ride is the perfect way to bring the family together for a night of laughter and friendly competition.

Whether you're a car enthusiast or simply enjoy a good guessing game, this card game is sure to become a family favourite. Great for kids! Get ready to put your deduction skills to the test and see who can guess the most rides!

—description from the designer

Unleash your need for speed with this thrilling trivia game about supercars! With 100 multiple-choice questions, you'll put your knowledge of these high-performance machines to the test. Whether you're a gearhead or just a fan of fast cars, this game is perfect for players of any age.

Challenge your friends and family to see who knows the most about the sleek designs, powerful engines, and mind-blowing speeds of the world's most iconic supercars. From classic models to modern marvels, this trivia game covers a wide range of supercar trivia, ensuring hours of fun and friendly competition.

Simply take a random card from the deck, read off the question and the multiple choice options to your friends, and see who can guess the answer the quickest! The winning player then takes a turn asking the next question! The game itself is pretty flexible, you can ask 3 questions, or the winner takes the next turn, ask one person or a group or just simply take turns! The game ends when there are no more questions left.

Compact and portable, it's the perfect way to pass the time on road trips or during family game nights. Get ready to shift your brain into high gear and prove your supercar superiority!

Welcome to the brand new car game called Built Not Bought! A 2-8 player strategy game where you compete to build the fastest car!

This is a turn based strategy game. The concept here is to build and modify your car! On our game the concept is to build the car with the most points. You can place boost cards into your build like a 'Twin Turbo', 'Supercharger' and a variety of different engines, transmissions and tires! You can also ruin another players build with a hazard like a 'Nail in Tire', 'Out of Gas' or 'Moose Crossing'!

Gain sponsorships and enter into roll races, drag races to earn extra points on the action cards! These cards are played one at a time after each players turn. They can help the player who turned the action card over win points by being a 'Car Thief', Or pickup a card from the 'Salvage Yard', or they can lose points by running into the Police or it's a group game where everyone plays in one of the race cards!

—description from the designer

An older Swedish card game with a Disney theme based on the classic card game Fan Tan.

During the course of the game, a number of funny comic strips appear on the game table. The player who gets rid of all their cards first wins.

Match Up! is an eventful and fast-paced family card game, where you have to collect as many cards as possible. This is done by pairing equal amounts with each other. The game also has a number of different special cards that can quickly change the conditions in the game.

SandLand Tactical Card Battle is a two-player duel in which each player has their own deck of cards; a deck consists of one leader and thirty asset/tactic cards. The first player to win two skirmishes wins the entire game. To win a skirmish, a player needs to:


    Have the water marker on their side of the board after round #3, or
     Bring the water marker to space #5 on their side of the board, or
    Eliminate all assets (units and locations) in the opponent's play area.


Players start with eight cards in hand. At the start of a skirmish, each player draws four cards, then places eight cards face down in the 2x4 area on their side of the playing area and the remaining four cards in the scrap.

Players take actions until both pass. To take an action with a face-down card, reveal it, then pay its cost in scrap and take an action with it. Used tactic cards are removed from play, whereas asset cards are exhausted (turned sideways).

This is the Helsinki version of the Kaupunkipelit (City Games) series. The current capital of Finland after Turku since 1812, Helsinki is now by far the most populous city in the country and the hub of a metropolitan area with a total of around 1 million inhabitants, but originally started out as a small fishing hamlet which was given town rights and expanded in 1550 in one of the Swedish king Gustav Vasa's constant attempts at creating a viable commercial competitor for Tallinn on the opposite shore of the Gulf of Finland.

Unlike most games in the series, this one (along with Turkupeli) came in two different editions; an earlier edition in a styrofoam box inside a cardboard sleeve fastened by velcro (similar to the concurrent Swedish Lokalspelet series) and a later edition which is similar to most of the other games in the series.

Kaupunkipelit is a series of games, each of which is basically a light version of Monopoly localized to a city/region of Finland. The games promote local companies, councils, educational institutes etc. and were designed as a fund raising effort for local sports clubs.

The main differences between these games and Monopoly are:

-Instead of a "street" each square on the board represents a local business.
-There are no hotels or houses, but each square has two title deeds instead of just one, allowing two players to jointly own any location on the board.
-The majority of the event cards are trivia questions about the locality the game is set in.
-While the effects of the first two corner squares (Go and Jail in Monopoly) remain the same for every edition (Go and Stock Market Crash), the effect of the other two corners varies from edition to edition and is the only real difference between them (except for the trivia content, of course). They are typically also local businesses but without title deeds and thus no rent either, but some special effect instead, such as pass a turn or get an extra throw.

The conveyors are out of control and there is candy all over the place. Sugarworks employees rush to adjust the conveyors to coax the unruly candy into the appropriate packages. Fulfilling orders will earn points. Unfortunately, the candy lines are all mixed up and the candy is colliding and moving all over the place. How will you fill your orders?

Players are each given objectives in the form of Orders, which are worth points at the end of the game. Three conveyor lines are moving candy along them toward their destination packaging, and several candy outputs keep putting more candy onto the conveyors. As the candy collides, it will jump to different locations based on the candy involved in the collision.

The conveyors can be manipulated in an effort to properly fulfil your orders. Other players, unfortunately, are also trying to complete different orders which may not align with yours. As soon as one type of candy has been completely processed, the packages will ship, and the players will score their completed orders.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Waterloo Promo Card
description:
Available at Gen Con 2024.

Level I Wonder

Immediate effect: gain 1 Military Legacy and 1 Military Research Point

Worth 2 VP

Counts as a Military card

Card number GW-47

boardgame: Lunar Rush: Wondrous Replicator Promo Card
description:
GenCon 2024 and the 2024-25 season promo card for Lunar Rush.

An additional Gold Module card, increasing the possible variations during setup.

This card allows the players to convert lunarium ore and lunethyst shards into various useful resources.

Players compete to be the first to replace, reveal, and rearrange a set grid of 10 x face-down starting cards until they are in the correct order from 1 to 10. But it is not so straight forward as the game introduces unique power cards, which allow you to manipulate the order of other player's grids (or your own) and take advantage of the numbers to win.

—description from the designer

The Glade tile is being published at Spiel in October 2024 along side the Collector's Edition of The Glade. The same creatures can be found on the tile as are found on The Glade box lid.

The Glade tile is a winter tile. Score 3 points for each adjacent tile in the player's village that displays a winter icon.

-description from designer

The Keyside is a winter boat tile. Receive 12 resources, which arrive on the tile at the end of winter and so cannot be moved from the tile.  The new resources can be scored by other tiles, although not those that require the resource to be transported onto the tile - i.e. those with white hex icons. Debuted at Spiel 2024.

boardgame: Leśne Rozdanie: Żubr / Bocian biały Promo Card
description:
Promo card celebrating Polish fauna.

Bison - 2 points per Beech and Oak symbols
Stork - 1 point per Insect and Amphibian symbols

Welcome to the Urban Development Department!

This is Vientole City, a beautiful place with vast flower fields, lush hills, and historic buildings. The market is bustling with people. As the leader of the city's development project, you will strive to improve the lives of the citizens. You'll plan public works, prioritize citizens' well-being, and create a balanced urban environment. Your excellent leadership and wisdom will greatly impact the growth of Vientole City.  Now, let's make this city an attractive and livable place with your hands!

The game consists of 4 rounds. Each round is divided into three phases: Planning Phase, Placement Phase, and Confirmation Phase.


     During the Planning Phase, players draft prepared land tiles.
     In the Placement Phase, players place the drafted land tiles onto their city boards. The key point is that the tiles are placed in reverse order of the drafting sequence.
     During the Confirmation Phase, players check the completion of milestone cards.


This sequence is repeated for 4 rounds, and in the end, each player will have placed 16 land tiles on their city boards. The player with the highest score wins the game.

—description from the publisher

É Pizza Memo! ("It's Pizza Memo!") is a children's memory game in which players are trying to assemble their own pizza slices by figuring out where the correct ingredients are in the memory grid.

Each round, as in a classic memory game, the player opens two tokens from the center of the table, trying to make a pair. But for your pair to count, it must also be one of the ingredients that is part of your pizza slice. The player who manages to complete their pizza first wins the game.

From a certain age, the game also has a variant with special action tokens, randomly distributed at the start of the game. Each one has a unique use during the game and is discarded after use, generating a range of effects, from opening more chips on the table to stealing an ingredient from the opponent.

Players must collect 4 cards of a set but this not a regular Quartett game.

Rules of the game
You need eight coins for this game. The game is played with 36 cards that are divided into nine families of four cards with the same theme. The four cards of the same family all have the same number (from I to 9).
The game can be played by three to nine players. Use as many groups of four cards as there are players. In other words, if there are 3 players then only 12 cards are used (for example, all the cards numbered 1,2 and 3); if there are four players then 16 cards are used and so on.
A randomly chosen player shuffles the cards and working clockwise deals out four cards to each player. As many of the coins as there are players minus one are placed in the middle of the table.
Each player tries to make families. They try to get 4 cards in their hand of the same family. Each player looks at their cards and decides which card to throw away. When the dealer says, ‘READY’ everyone places their chosen card in front of them, face down. When the dealer says ‘START’ each player gives their card to the player sitting to their left and receives a card from the player on their right. If no player has completed a family then the game continues. A player can throw away the same card they have received from their neighbour or swap it and throw away a different card. If a player receives a card that completes a family they shout, ‘STOP’ and at that moment everyone tries to grab a coin from the middle of the table. The player that’s too slow and doesn’t grab a coin, loses the game and all the others win.

—user summary

Console Wars The Card Game satirizes the contentious side of the video game industry. Players collect incredible games, build a dedicated fanbase, and compete to become the true market leader in the console wars.

In order to win the Console Wars, content is king, and intellectual property ownership of the best games is everything. To emerge victorious, players will need to battle for ownership of 4 full sets of games across multiple genres, from action-packed first-person shooters, iconic platformers and deep, complex RPGs.
