Throughout play, you’ll try to amass as many fans as you can – without them, you have no momentum, and arguably no platform at all – so keeping fans is critical to holding on to your precious gaming franchises. You'll also be able to power up your platform with unique 'perks' that provide different buffs.

But the gaming industry is a volatile place, and anything could happen. Global Events can cause change in an instant. It could be in your favour - or lead to disaster.

Will you emerge victorious? Can you survive the relentless ups and downs of the video game industry? Find out if you have what it takes to truly win the Console Wars in Console Wars The Card Game.

—description from the designer

Arkhtide unfolds in a post-apocalyptic, dark fantasy universe, where the once-thriving civilization of Réa has been abandoned and left to decay by its Creator. Centuries have passed since this calamity, yet humanity endures, barely clinging to existence. The key to a semblance of better life now lies in the Arkhtides—mystical phenomena that are fiercely contested by the world’s remaining powers. Players step into the shoes of a Seeker, leading a devoted party on a quest to claim these Arkhtides and extract the invaluable Pure Arkhé.

Arkhtide is a tactical skirmish card game that demands cunning and strategy. As a Seeker, players will navigate a chosen group of elites, alongside summonable creatures, wield special and magical powers, and an arsenal of equipment in pursuit of triumph. Success in the game revolves around capturing and maintaining control over distorted projections of the world called Realms or decisively defeating the opponent’s Seeker by crushing their Willpower. Players are constantly engaged in strategic decision-making, from deploying and maneuvering their forces across dynamically changing Realms, balancing offense, defense, and timing. Arkhtide offers deep deck-building opportunities and requires strategic planning and adaptability, with matches that unfold over two to four rounds and last between thirty to fifty minutes, providing a deep and engaging gameplay experience.

—description from the publisher

It's the present day. Mushijingi, a fighting sport where players control insects to fight, has become popular all over the world. The trick is in the Mushijingi cards. These cards are replicas of the legendary treasures that allow players to freely control insects, and are sold commercially.

Mushijingi is a battle game played using decks. Players aim to defeat their opponents by making full use of the various effects of each card. Players alternate between their own turns and their opponent's turns, and the first player to meet the victory conditions wins. To play this game, players need 20 cards from each of their decks, for a total of 40 cards.

Victory Conditions


     Attack the opponent's main body with the opponent's territory reduced to 0.



     The opponent surrenders.



     If either player has no cards in their deck when drawing and can no longer draw cards, the player with the most territory at that point wins.


If both players have the same number of territories, it results in a draw.

How to read the cards
There are three types of Insect Artifact cards: Insect cards, Strengthening cards, and Magic cards. To use a card, you must pay the cost written in the upper left corner.

—description from the publisher (translated)

時は現代。虫を操って戦わせる闘虫競技「虫陣戯（ムシジンギ）」が世界中で流行し始めていた。その仕掛けは「蟲神器形代（ムシジンギカード）」にある。それは、虫を自在に操れるという伝説の宝具、「蟲神器」の能力をコピーしたレプリカとして作られ市販されたものだった。
蟲神器はデッキを使って遊ぶ対戦ゲームです。各カードが持つ様々な効果を駆使して、相手に勝利する事を目指します。自分のターンと相手のターンを交互に繰り返し、先に勝利条件を満たした方が勝利します。このゲームで遊ぶには、自分のデッキと対戦相手のデッキ、各20枚、計40枚のカードが必要です。
〇勝利条件
　　・相手の縄張りを0にした状態で相手本体を攻撃する。
　　・相手が降伏する。
　　・どちらかがドローの際に山札がなく、カードを引けなくなったら、その時点で縄張りの数が多い方が勝利。
　　　縄張りの数が互いに同じときは引き分けとなる。     
 〇カードの見方
蟲神器のカードは虫カード、強化カード、術カードの3種類があります。カードを使用するには左上に書かれたコストを支払う必要があります。

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Elk / Troll Promo Card
description:
Promo card (P011) celebrating Nordic motives and (part of) mythology.

Elk - green binocular action: Draw 1 card, take another turn; 2 points per Tree Sapling, Douglas Fir or Birch icon (cost 3 cards, Birch icon)

Troll - Draw 1 card from each opponent’s hand and put those cards in the clearing. 1 point per Tree symbol, (cost 1 card, Douglas Fir icon) Illustration John Bauer, 1913

Promo card for Austrian region.

Kuckuck - 7 points, can be played only on top of the other bird that is on top of Shrub or Tree
Chanterelle - draw a card when you play Tree symbol

boardgame: The Quest Kids: Realms of the Kingdom
description:
The Quest Kids are back in a new expansion for the acclaimed dungeon crawler for young players 5 years old and up!

The Dwarven city of Stonedale has been captured by the pesky Orcs of Treasure Falls! A young Dwarven Warrior named Mason has escaped the city and made it to the Quest Kids tree house. He needs their help to the Orc Gate and free Stonedale!

The Quest Kids: Realms of the Kingdom introduces a new Quest Kid hero (Mason the Wise Dwarf), new realms of Treasure Falls Kingdom to explore, new Tolk Gems to discover and an exciting new portal mechanic that allows young players to decide what realm to visit based on what abilities they require.

For 2-5 players ages 5 and up Realms of the Kingdom continues the Quest Kids tradition of creating high-quality fantasy gaming experiences for young heroes and their families.

A Quest Kids quest is never done!

-description from designer

A simple WORDLE-like word deduction game for two people. Grab a couple of paperback books to play!

Guess each others’ secret word as found on any page in their paperback book.

Flip open your book to two pages. Start with a random 3-letter word, circle yours, ask questions, record guesses and answers until someone decodes their opponent’s word. The first word must come from either page. The remaining must be query words.

Response will be number of matching letters and my word is higher or lower. Maintain scores at the top of the form.

Play several rounds with increasingly longer words.

Introducing Etymology: The Card Game - where the Game Master reads a Greek or Latin root, and players must explain its meaning and give example words derived from it. Challenge your friends, learn together, and have a blast with this educational and entertaining game!

Educational and Fun
Etymology: The Card Game turns homework into a game, combining learning with entertainment. By focusing on understanding Greek and Latin roots, prefixes, and suffixes, as well as loanwords this game broadens vocabulary and deepens linguistic knowledge. The game is designed to be both fun and educational, making it an excellent tool for students, teachers, and language enthusiasts alike.

Interactive and Engaging Gameplay
The game features a variety of card types, including Greek and Latin roots, prefixes, suffixes, and loanwords (words that English has borrowed from other languages). Players must explain the meanings of these elements and provide example words, encouraging interaction and discussion. The inclusion of loanword cards, where players guess the origin language, adds an extra layer of challenge and learning. The role of the Game Master, who reads the roots and judges the answers, adds a unique dynamic to the gameplay.

Easy to Pick Up, Hard to Master
Etymology: The Card Game is easy to pick up because everyone knows a lot of English words, but mastering it requires some study and can be challenging. This balance ensures that the game remains accessible while providing a rewarding challenge for those who want to deepen their knowledge.

Encourages a Fascination with Words
One of the game's standout features is how it encourages a fascination with words. Players often find themselves wondering how a derived word relates to its root, prompting them to research and discover more about its origin. This process fosters curiosity and a deeper understanding of language, making learning an engaging and ongoing journey. It doesn't matter as much what we cover; it matters what you discover.

Highly Re-playable and Expandable
The game's format makes it highly portable and easy to take anywhere. With potential for additional card sets that include more roots, prefixes, suffixes, words, and loanwords, the game stays fresh and engaging.

The Interconnectedness of the World
The game highlights the interconnectedness of the world and the diverse cultural influences there have been on the English language, enhancing both vocabulary and cultural appreciation. With high replay value and the opportunity for endless discovery, Etymology: The Card Game offers an enriching experience that players will return to time and again.

Who knew that 'hurricane' (originating from the Taíno word 'hurakan,' meaning God of the storm) and 'barbecue' (from the Taíno word 'barbacoa') came to us from the Spanish, who acquired them from the Native American Taíno people.

—description from the designer

"Bluff your way through a series of Mexian standoffs. Get the most hostages and keep the people of value alive." -- publisher description

Every player has an identical set of eight randomly shuffled cards (characters).  Each character has a different hostage value and shooting strength. In every round, each player decides to do one of the following:


     Give up their character to someone else as a hostage. You neither gain nor lose points, but the person who receives your hostage gains 3 points.
     Demand hostages. Without revealing your card, you are implying a strong hand and hoping to earn points through hostages.
     Start a shootout. The card with the highest shooting strength wins. The losers keep their cards as negative points.


You decide whether to bluff and try to convince your opponents to turn over hostages. Beware - losing a shootout could cost you a lot of points! Negative scores are common. You win if you have the most points at the end of the game.

-description from designer

In Vi algo nas sombras ("I Saw Something in the Shadows"), a group of explorers searched for an ancient catacomb that legend promised would be full of treasure but suddenly the floor opens and everyone fell.
The good news is that they found the mythic catacombs and that there really is the treasure! 
Now, bad news... There is no exit in sight, and everyone has the feeling that there is something watching them during the journey.
Fortunately, the group has a sufficient supply of torches to, with some planning, explore the location, gather treasure, and find their way out... or so it seems.  
Work as a team, stay close, keep the torches burning and be careful not to be alone!

—description from the designer (translated)

Em Vi algo nas sombras um grupo de exploradores buscava por uma antiga catacumba que a lenda prometia estar cheia de tesouros quando o chão cedeu abaixo deles.
As boas notícias são que eles encontraram as catacumbas, e que realmente há um tesouro! 
Já as más notícias... Não há saída a vista, e todos tem a sensação de que há algo os observando enquanto exploram o local.
Felizmente o grupo tem um estoque de tochas suficiente para, com algum planejamento, explorar o local, juntar tesouros e encontrar a saída... ou pelo menos é o que parece.  
Trabalhem em equipe, se mantenham próximos, mantenham as tochas acesas e cuidado pra não ficar sozinho!

—description from the designer

Pawvocados is The Game of Pawesome Pairs! The Pawvocado pets have lost their noses…these cute pups, cats and bunnies will need your help to find their Pawvocado pair and get their nose back. On each turn, roll the original avocado dice: the adorable fruit will break open and may reveal the little paw seed! Which side you land on will determine how many cards you win. Make pairs with your cards or exchange them for fun items to delight your avocado buddies.

—description from the publisher

The evolution of the game Names, Things, Cities!
Parole Quiz, the game with more than 3000 questions.
1 topic, 1 letter. You have 12 seconds to answer and find the corresponding word.
The faster you answer, the more points you get. The voice guide will take care of everything: from the explanation of the rules, to the questions, to the counting of points. The artificial intelligence analyzes the actions of the players and adapts to them.

The game "Silvestre in the Rif" is a two-player game that simulates the last six years of the Rif War (1920-1926). One player controls Spain, led by General Silvestre (Sylvester), and the other player controls several Riffian tribes led by Abd-el-Krim.

This wargame cover the Rif War, focusing specifically on the conflict between Spanish forces and Rif tribes. It is a turn-based war game that uses cards, dice, and tokens on a map of the Rif. Players take turns playing cards that allow them to perform actions such as moving units, fighting battles, laying siege to cities, and managing resources. The game includes mechanics such as interception, retreats, supply lines, attrition, and historical events.

The objective of the game for the Spanish player is to obtain 10 or more victory points at the end of the sixth turn. Victory points are earned by controlling key territory, eliminating Rif tribes, and maintaining governance in the Rif. The Riffian player wins if the Spanish player does not reach 10 victory points at the end of turn 6 or if the Spanish player obtains 3 or less governance points during the political phase.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game: Moonlit Howl
description:
Get poisoned by Viper Tobi-Kadachi, singed and chased by Yian Garuga and Scarred Yian Garuga, hunted by Ebony Odogaron, electrified by Zinogre, and terrified by Stygian Zinogre!

Included free in all Hunter's and Monstrous Pledges, Moonlit Howl is the Kickstarter exclusive expansion where most of the unlocked monsters from the Kickstarter are stored. The only exceptions are variant and subspecies cards for monsters in other boxes, which are included in those other boxes instead.

Inside, you'll find:
• 3 brand new XL monsters + variant gameplay
• 1 XXL monster + subspecies gameplay
• 18 new campaign quests (hunt 6 monsters at 18 different levels) 
• 18 new arena quests for one-shot boss battles against any of the monsters, at any level
• New choice-driven quest narrative
• Every craftable weapon type for each monster, for the Hoarfrost Reach core box hunters, from the video game

It also introduces THREE new ways to win your Hoarfrost Reach campaign: Scarred Yian Garuga, Zinogre, and Stygian Zinogre all come with 4-star quests, giving you multiple options for hunting your way to victory!

Monopoly: Miraculous Serbia presents a new edition of the cult board game with Serbian motifs. Instead of the locations you've seen in previous editions of this game, this time players will encounter landmarks across Serbia such as Kalemegdan, Đavolja varoš or Petrovaradin fortress.

Travel through Serbia and see its beauty. You will need to invest money, roll the dice, buy the best properties and make your opponent pay you rent. As you move around the board, you will have fun visiting forts, natural wonders, archaeological sites, monasteries.

From Đavolja varoš (Devils town) go to the Tara canyon, rest into the shades of the Manasija monastery, climb to the top of Rtanj montain, explore old roman town of Viminacium, have dinner in bohemian district of Skadarlija, walk around Knez Mihailova street and have fun exploring Serbia.

In Trust Issues, players navigate the intricate world of family dynamics and inheritance. As members of a wealthy and influential family, players strategically position their Heir cards on the family tree to secure the Family Fortune. With the inclusion of Dispute cards, players can manipulate, deceive, and disrupt each other's plans, making every move critical.

The game features unique Heir subtypes, such as Schemers, Wildcards, Protectors, and more, each with their own abilities and strategies. The Heritage Clock tracks the lifespan of the Grantor, adding a layer of urgency and strategy as players vie for control.

Will you secure the Family Fortune, or will you fall victim to the cunning schemes and treacherous plots of your relatives? Trust Issues challenges players to outwit and outlast their opponents in a game of strategic inheritance and family intrigue.

—description from the designer

In the party game Taboo, you're trying to give clues to your teammates so that they'll guess a particular word, but you can't say just anything you like. Some clues are off limits!

When you're the active player, hold the deck of cards so that you and the opposing team can see the top card. At the top of the card is the word your teammate must say to score the card, and you can anything you want to help them figure out what to guess other than the word itself (duh!) or the five words/phrases listed on the bottom of the card.

For example, can you get your teammates to say "bacon" without saying "pig", "eggs", "breakfast", "sausage", or "eat"? If you do, you score the card, then move on to the next card, trying to guess as many cards as possible before time runs out. However, if you say a taboo word (or make gestures), the opposing team will buzz a buzzer and score the card themselves.

Taboo Horror uses the same gameplay as Taboo, but features films, characters, objects, locations, and more from all aspects of the horror genre.

Wonderland is in a bit of turmoil right now, so there are FOUR White Rabbits hurrying to the Tea Party. However, only one of them will get there in time.

In this competitive game, you watch a race and support Rabbits by feeding them with cakes. Rabbits are not assigned to any of you, so you can choose any Rabbit to give cakes to.

Besides ordinary cakes, you have a set of special effects on your hand which you can use to make the Rabbits’ run easier or harder. It’s a tough race: Rabbits will get trapped, hide behind Shields, avoid the Queen of Hearts, fall into Rabbit Holes, and look at their Pocket Watches to run faster.

Think carefully before making a move — after all, you want the first Rabbit on the finish line to have more of your cakes than anybody else’s.

-description from publisher's website

An original family game with elements that will amaze the whole family ! Investigate, guess and use your strategy to win.
Like every year MONSTER PALACE welcomes the international conference of frightful monsters and fantastic creatures for the election of the «Monster of the Year». But a villain has stolen the trophy!
The police have called upon the most renowned fortune tellers of the fantasy worlds.
The crystal ball, the pendlum, the tarot cards and the presage box have no secrets for you and will allow you to find the culprit. Monster Palace is a family game, you’ll enjoy it as an adult too.

Here are the steps for each turn :

     Open 2 doors of your choice
     If the discovered monsters have power, apply the effects immediately
     Launch the runes, and open a door among the 9 at your disposal
     If you have opened all the doors indicated in color on one of your "mission cards" then show the card to your opponents and discover the clue (at the beginning or end of your turn). A discovered clue can be reviewed again during the game if necessary.
     Close half of the doors used to discover the index (ignore this step for shorter parts or with young children).
     If you have discovered the culprit you can announce it to the


Other players (it must be visible)

The player who has identified the culprit must verify his answer with the "Monster Astrology Tray" and the star maps.

In this remastered version of Kronos Epilogue you will find new mechanics and an overall improved version of the game. This deluxe edition comes with two expansions that will take your experience to the next level. The “Oracle of the Dead” that allows you to take on the role of Hades, King of the Underworld and become the Tyrant that the other players will fight. As well as the “Ursa Major” which allows you to take on the role of the demon Κallisto and include in your games her astral hideout that has gathered the most skilled adventurers of the universe.

There is, in a distant land, an ancient Mexican pyramid full of mysteries and mathematical challenges. Legend has it that at the top of the pyramid, there is a magical treasure that can only be reached by those who have mastered mathematical operations.

Eight young adventurers, each with unique skills in mathematics, decided to take on the challenge. They knew that for each correct operation they solved, they would climb a step of the pyramid. However, the journey would not be easy, as each step presented a new mathematical enigma to be unraveled.

With courage and determination, the adventurers began to solve the problems. With each correct answer, a magical glow illuminated the step, and they climbed another step towards the top.

Objective of the Game:
Reach the top of the Pyramid of Kukulcán first.

Playing the game:

     At each round a new mathematical operation is opened (addition, subtraction, division or multiplication);
     The fastest player to place the correct answer card on the operation, climbs one step in the Pyramid of Kukulcán of Chich'en Itza;
     If the player enters the wrong answer, he goes down a step;
     The player who reaches the top of the Pyramid of Kukulcán first wins.


—description from the designer

Há, em uma terra distante, uma antiga pirâmide mexicana cheia de mistérios e desafios matemáticos. Diz a lenda que no topo da pirâmide, há um tesouro mágico que só pode ser alcançado por aqueles que dominam as operações matemáticas.

Oito jovens aventureiros, cada um com habilidades únicas em matemática, decidiram aceitar o desafio. Eles sabiam que para cada operação correta que resolvessem, subiriam um degrau da pirâmide. No entanto, a jornada não seria fácil, pois cada degrau apresentava um novo enigma matemático a ser desvendado.

Com coragem e determinação, os aventureiros começaram a resolver os problemas. A cada resposta correta, um brilho mágico iluminava o degrau, e eles subiam mais um passo em direção ao topo.

Objetivo do Jogo:
Chegar ao topo da Pirâmide de Kukulcán primeiro.

Jogando o jogo:

     A cada rodada uma nova operação matemática é aberta (adição, subtração, divisão ou multiplicação);
     O jogador mais rápido a colocar a carta de resposta correta sobre a operação, sobe um degrau na Pirâmide de Kukulcán de Chich'en Itza;
     Caso o jogador coloque a resposta errada, ele desce um degrau;
     Vence o jogador que chegar no topo da Pirâmide de Kukulcán primeiro.


—description from the designer (Spanish)

description:
Sungka, pronounced soong-kah, is a traditional Filipino game where two players compete to capture the majority of the shells.

The game is typically played on a long, wooden board that has 9 holes. Each player has seven smaller holes (houses) in front of him and two large holes (head) at each end of the board. Seven shells are distributed evenly among the 14 small pits.

Both players start by taking the shells from one of the houses on their own side and distributing them in each consecutive hole around the board in a counter-clockwise direction. The player puts a shell in their own large hole on the left side of the board but excludes the large hole of their opponent when distributing the shells.

If the last shell drops into a pit with other shells, the shells in the hole are picked up and distributed in the same counter-clockwise direction. If the last shell drops into the player’s head, then the player can distribute the shells from another house on his side.

If a shell is dropped into an empty house, the player’s turn is over and his opponent can choose a pile of shells from his side of the board to distribute. If the shell is dropped in an empty house on the player’s side, the contents of the pit on the opposite side are taken and added to the player’s head.

When there are no more shells left to play, the player with the most number of shells wins the game. There are variations of sungka that allow the winner certain privileges when continuing the game. In one variation, the player fills the houses with the shells he accumulated. If the player doesn’t have seven shells to fill a house, they go back into the player’s head. Any houses that aren’t filled are burned and cannot be used in the game by either player. The winner starts the next round and the game continues until one player’s side has been completely burned.

Sungka is considered to be the Filipino version of the mancala game, which dates back to the seventeenth century, according to an American Museum of Natural History’s research note. Indonesia, Malaysia, the Maldives and the Philippines share a particular set of rules in their version of the game.

Veto is for 2-4 players. If you have more players, add another deck to deal out more fun!

Veto is an easy shedding-type card game spiced up with special functions of action and wild cards. Players try to get rid of all their cards first, stacking equal or higher-ranking ones on each other, unless the action card has been played to make low cards win! Play a VETO card to stop from having to pick up cards when a player matches your card. Don't be the last one with cards left at the end!

The Deal – Shuffle the deck and distribute the cards in any number of piles to form draw piles. Each player selects cards from any pile to create Ground piles. The number of Ground piles, ranging from 1-4, may be decided at the beginning of the game. These piles shall remain face down and must have 4 cards in each. Each player then selects 4 cards to create their hand, which a player will look at and play from first. Combine remaining undrawn cards into one pile, face down, and form the draw pile.

Game play – To decide what player starts the game, each player turns over a top card from one of their Ground piles. In case of a tie for the highest value, turn over the top card of another Ground pile until the highest card is revealed. The player that turned the highest valued card will start by discarding any card from their hand (not from the Ground piles) to create a discard pile. Do not draw a card from the draw pile. Ground piles cannot be played from until a player’s hand cards have all been played. Play will continue clockwise. The next player must play a card equal to/higher than the last played card, or a wild card, following the instructions on any action cards. If the player does not have a valid card to play, they must pick up the entire discard pile and add the cards to their hand. If a player matches the last played card, the previous player must draw 3 cards from the draw pile, unless they can play a VETO card to stop the pick-up. Once a player has played all the cards from their hand, they may begin to play from the Ground piles. When the top card of a Ground pile is played, do not turn over the next card. These cards remain face down and are played blind. If the card is played and is not a valid play, the player must pick up the discard pile to create hand cards.

Winning – A game may end when either one player is the first to play all their cards or until there is only one loser - the last player with cards remaining.

—description from the publisher

Spells and Wizards is an easy to learn card game that is fun for ages 7 and up. The game can be played with 2-6 players and can be played with partners or as individuals. Spells and Wizards is played with a custom 60-card deck. There are four different colors of cards in the deck and the card values range from 1-15 in value. However, there are no 3’s or 8’s in the deck. Instead there are special photo cards - Spells and Wizards. Winning the game will take strategy as players can team up with the other players and ultimately use them to their own advantage. While Spells and Wizards can be played as a team game, there is only one individual winner. In this game, the game is over by making someone gain over 200 points or by being the first to reach negative 100. Just make sure you are the lowest when the game ends and you win!

4 Suits of 15 cards each; each suit contains 13 number cards, a spell photo card and a wizard photo card.

Object of the game is to be the player with the lowest score.

Capture Wizard cards to deduct points.

Capture all 4 Spell cards for an extra scoring bonus.

—description from the publisher

Snow Planner: New Season enriches the base game with additional guest cards, event cards, talent cards, and project cards, enhancing strategic options and depth. This expansion for 1-4 players seamlessly integrates with the original game, offering new ways to optimize hotel operations and attract guests without altering the fundamental gameplay.

Players continue to strategically manage an inn in snowy mountains but now have more tools at their disposal. The new talent cards offer unique abilities that broaden strategic choices, and the new project cards introduce compelling goals that keep each playthrough fresh and exciting.

While the core game mechanics remain unchanged, the expansion offers strategies that can be employed at players' discretion, ensuring that even those familiar with the base game will find new challenges and enjoy replayability.

Embrace the changing seasons in Snow Planner: New Season. Whether you're a seasoned planner or new to the game, this expansion offers fresh experiences and strategic depth, enhancing your quest to build the most renowned inn amidst the snowy peaks.

With unexpected opponents, unique gameplay for every game, and ever-changing outcomes, the adventure is thrilling until the very end. Players must collect the specified number of points by defeating Monsters, or in standard play, the first to reach 30 points wins. Play begins by drawing a Quest Card, which determines the number and level of Monsters to be defeated. Players then use Attack Cards to battle Monsters. The player who delivers the final blow that reduces a Monster's HP to zero or defeats it outright will earn the points. The first player to accumulate the required number of points wins!

Face the Albatwitch in the forest. The coven must help the Albatwitch by capturing Creatures that will help guide the Albatwitch away from the village, or confront and destroy the Albatwitch to protect Wildegrens' food. After completing the 7 Missions, check if you have captured the 3 Creatures.

Southern Surf Stakes is a surf-themed trick-taking game with a unique design utilising both sides of the cards. Techniques are ranked on the front, while wave sizes are on the back. Earn points by choosing higher ranked techniques, but be cautious not to exceed the wave size to avoid losing points. Anticipate the next wave's size when selecting your technique!

Join Muppets Kermit (Mayor Green), Fozzie (Colonel Mustard), and more as they put on a murder mystery show in Clue: The Muppets. Explore the Muppet Theater and track down clues leading to classic Muppet props to figure out WHO did it, WHERE it happened, and WHICH weapon was used.

description:
Last night I dreamed of a barber with a banana in his hand taking the measurements of a rubber duck in my bathroom... But how did it go on? Suddenly everything became polka-dotted! Will you be able to remember the dream even though the Dream Eater will try to transform your Sweet Dream into a more... lively dream?

Each turn players will have to remember the previous players' dream and add a card to continue the dream. While the dream eater will try to get you wrong.

Connectables is a fast-paced, engaging and strategic tile-laying game where players connect patterns, words, and numbers to outsmart their opponents. Each tile features a unique combination of a number, shape, and letters on its edges. The goal is to strategically place tiles to form connections along the edges, creating a dynamic and ever-changing game board. The first player to successfully place all their tiles wins the round.

It's easy to learn but offers enough depth to keep players coming back for more.

Components:


     77 wooden tiles with numbers, letters and symbols along four edges. Black and white so no color blindness issues.
     Box or Tin.


Setup:

Shuffle the tiles.
Grab one tile and place between the players.
Give each player eight tiles.

Game Play:

Players take turns placing one tile at a time. Tiles must be placed so that at least one edge matches an adjacent tile’s edge in one of three ways:

     the two adjacent numbers add up to 10.
     the adjacent letters spell a word (2-5 letters).
     the adjacent halves of patterns form a complete pattern.


If a tile cannot be placed, draw an additional tile and end your turn.

Play until one player has eliminated all their tiles. That player is the winner of the round. Play at least two rounds to determine the overall winner.

Features:

Strategic Depth: Players must think ahead and plan their moves to maximize connections and block opponents.

Dynamic Gameplay: The game board evolves with each tile placement, creating new opportunities and challenges.

Educational Value: Promotes pattern recognition, word formation, and numerical skills, making it a fun and educational experience for players of all ages.

Replayability: With a variety of tiles and endless possible connections, no two games are ever the same.

Target Audience: Perfect for families, casual gamers and party games.

—description from the publisher

In the Bumble Isles mini-marts sell bugchum "minis", newly hatched bug companions everyone wants! There's lots to do at these shops – stocking items, checking terrariums, feeding bugs, helping customers and making improvements.

In Bugchums – Mini-Mart Merchant, you'll try your hand at running your own shop. Raise your merchant ranking by hatching different kinds of bugchums. Sell these ugly-cute creatures to eager patrons looking for new bug friends. Earn the most zinny (Z) by close of business to win.

boardgame: Patrician: Towers of Influence – Decree Cards
description:
Patrician designer Michael Schacht introduces dynamic new ways to score, inspiring strategic choices for players. Each Decree is a unique scoring rule, and during setup, a single Decree card is revealed. Decrees may reward specific tower placement, allow leftover portraits to earn points, change the values of prestige tokens, and more!

These 8 cards were exclusives for backers of the Patrician: Towers of Influence Kickstarter.

Smyle is a competitive memory card game for 2-5 players and ages 8+. Each player begins the game with 3 facedown Teeth cards worth either positive or negative points, and draws a hand of 4 Tooth Fairy cards. Players will know what Teeth cards they have at the start of the game but can't check their row of cards, called their Smyle, unless an effect allows.

At the start of each Round, every player simultaneously plays 1 Tooth Fairy card, which determines whether they are going to Steal a Tooth from an opponent, Give 1 of their Teeth to an opponent, Swap a Tooth with one of their opponent's Teeth, or Draw a brand-new Tooth card from the Spare Tooth Deck or activate a Gadget with a unique effect.

But players aren't left completely in the dark. At the end of each Round, players must flip 1 of their Teeth cards face up for all to see. Choose wisely as some Teeth generate negative points, called Tooth Decay, when Revealed! Will you take the time to care for and clean your Teeth, or pass off your Decay-ridden Teeth to someone else?

After 6 Rounds, the player with the best-looking Smyle wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Confront the Legendary Beast with the Drop Bears: Ol'Bruce Expansion

Prepare yourself for the ultimate showdown in the Australian bush with the Drop Bears: Ol'Bruce Expansion. This thrilling addition introduces a new and formidable adversary—Ol'Bruce, a legendary giant Drop Bear of ancient myth, known for his immense size and terrifying ferocity. Awakened by the intrusion of campers, Ol'Bruce is now on the prowl, and the stakes have never been higher.

In this expansion, you and your fellow campers must gather your courage and work together to take on the great beast. But survival won’t come easy—Ol'Bruce is relentless, and his hunger knows no bounds.

The Ol'Bruce Expansion introduces Boss Fight mechanics that challenge players to work together, gather key gear, and strategise to take the fight to this monstrous adversary. But beware, Ol'Bruce can grab your campers, forcing the team to rescue their captured comrades before they become the next meal for this great beast.

The pressure is on as campers face time-sensitive endeavours, racing against the clock to prepare for the inevitable confrontation with Ol'Bruce. Every decision counts, and every second matters as the campers fight for survival.

—description from the publisher

description:
C'est Carré ("Squared") is a card collection and placement game.

50 points or more and you win!
If not, start a new round.


     Play the top card of the discard pile or deck, to make a 9-card square (3x3).
     Activate the card you've played: negative cards allow you to play other cards.


Positive cards allow you to draw other cards.

     Your square earns you value points + areas of the same Earthenware (color).

Prepare yourself for another harrowing adventure deep in the Australian bush with the Drop Bears: Ancient Oonah Expansion. This thrilling addition introduces a terrifying new adversary—Ancient Oonah, a giant prehistoric platypus with razor-sharp teeth, awakened from its ancient slumber by unsuspecting campers.

As tension escalates, you and your fellow campers must rely on your wits, teamwork, and strategic thinking to survive against this insurmountable force. Ancient Oonah is a formidable creature, fiercely defending its watery kingdom with poisonous spurs and deep-seated paranoia. The stakes are higher than ever as you navigate the perilous wilderness, evading not only the lurking Drop Bears but also the wrath of Ancient Oonah. But it’s not just a battle for survival—it’s a battle for territory.

New Gameplay Dynamics

The objective of the campers (players) is to ruin the home of Ancient Oonah by polluting its dens, a tragically human behaviour that has now been woven into the game’s mechanics. Players must decide how best to carry out their destructive mission while avoiding the deadly creatures of the bush. But Ancient Oonah is not defenceless—it must protect its territory from these destructive human invaders, as well as from the Drop Bears that have followed them. This expansion adds a new layer of complexity as Ancient Oonah fights not just for its home, but for its very life.

—description from the publisher

Wyld is a competitive tableau building card game for 2-5 players where each player seeks out creatures and cryptids called Wyldlings to rehabilitate them in their own Habitats.

At the start of each Round, the Leader plays a Wylding card belonging to one of four Habitats: Mountains, Grasslands, Graveyards, or Deserts. Every other player must then play a card belonging to the same Habitat if they can. Once every player has played a card, they take turns drafting those cards and adding them to their personal Habitats. The person who played the Wylding card with the highest Initiative of a matching Habitat gets the first pick!

At the end of each Round, count up the points in your Habitat with the most Wyldings and your Habitat with the fewest Wyldings. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins! But, there's a catch:

Your Habitat with the most Wyldings at the end of the first Round is ignored at the end of the game. If you want to score with the Wyldings in that Habitat again, you'll need to use your Wyldings' Abilities to move them into a new Habitat.
With 5 alternate rule options, Wyld offers the ability to mix and match to fit your table's playstyle! The game is for ages 8+ and takes 20 minutes to play.

—description from the publisher

Cryptex is an innovative board game that immerses players in the thrilling world of cryptocurrency trading and investment. Navigate a board filled with real estate, crypto, and company spaces, all while adapting to the ever-changing bear and bull market cycles.

In Cryptex, your financial decisions are influenced by market conditions, making each move critical. Acquire crypto coins, invest in companies, and earn rent based on the current market phase. Strategic thinking and a keen eye for market trends are essential as you strive to outmaneuver your opponents and build your empire.

Whether you're with friends or family, Cryptex offers an engaging and entertaining experience that brings the excitement of the crypto world to your game night. Will you rise to the top as the ultimate market master?

—description from the designer

Welcome to the Lawns of Nome, where you are a Lawn Gnome Wizard!

Build you Tower to cast more powerful spells, and attract Gnomes to you Lawn.  Be the first to build your Tower tall enough and you win the game!

There are four spheres of magic you can utilize, Blue (water), Brown (mud), Green (plants), and Yellow (sunlight).  Mix and match the colors for more combos or focus on one color for that colors strongest spells.

description:
屁者先知 ("Fart Prophet") is a hilarious party game of hidden roles and bluffing set in an elevator. Players take on quirky roles, with one secretly playing as the "Farter," releasing gas on specific floors and causing chaos.

Each round, players use action cards to protect themselves or accuse others, blending humor with tension as they debate, vote, and try to identify the Farter. Perfect for parties, this game offers laughter and strategic deception as players navigate escalating challenges. Will you expose the Farter, or be left holding your breath?

Color Climbers: Join Purl the curious cat on a colorful climb, in this dynamic card game where players mix basic colors into more complex ones. Amass color points in fast-paced rounds of strategic encounters as players balance risk, resources and wit to outmaneuver fellow Color Climbers in making timely color swaps.

With over 30 colors across three different tiers, every match has players interacting with a variety of mechanics to come out on top:

     Stash and Swipe abilities to keep your opponents on their toes
     Meow Missions that reward different approaches in scoring endgame points 
     Action and Chroma cards to mix things up


Adapt your playstyle to be as agile as a cat in this family game-night favorite!

- Description by Board Buffs

Dynamic Circular Chess is a new strategy board game that evolves from traditional chess where players make fast action power movements to capture the enemy pieces.

Following the mechanics of traditional chess playing by turns, performing checks and creating new checkmates but simpler, faster and better.

Each player has 10 pieces portraying Egyptian mythology Anubis wielding a khopesh, Horus attacking with a bow and shooting arrows, a Queen and a Pharaoh, without leaving out the mighty Pyramids, with new powerful and creative movements victory can be achieved rapidly, with the  objective of surrounding and capturing  the enemy Pharaoh.

Destroy the enemy lines quickly to clear the board creating several ways to attack; and then choose which way you to checkmate the enemy in the smaller number of possible turns.

Dynamic Circular Chess is the perfect exercise for creativity while competing in war like simulation between 2 armies, where even though many pieces can be sacrificed you can still emerge victorious at the final showdown.

Unleash your raw strategic power boost your potential and upgrade your creativity.

—description from the designer

Rebellion & Punishment: War Of The Alpujarras is a 1-2 players strategical card-driven game that recreates the war between the rebel Moriscos who attempted to re-establish the Muslim Kingdom of Granada and the Christian troops of the Kingdom of Spain who fought to prevent it.

Cards are played as Action Points or as Events, allowing players to advance across the terrain or perform actions specific to their faction. The game map reproduces the entire territory of the former Nasrid Kingdom of Granada and includes the areas where uprisings and battles took place.

Are you ready to dive into the eerie, enchanting world of Spooky Forest? This simple yet strategic "tile-placement" (more like a flip-and-write to be honest) game will have you racing to achieve the top score while navigating through a haunted woodland. Think of yourself as the city planner for the local spooky forest. It's a living, amirite? Or is it non-living...

Each turn, flip a card and draw the symbol on the grid, but the new symbol cannot be orthogonally adjacent to the previous one. Try to fill the grid by the time the deck runs out.

At the end of the game, your score is based on pairs of skulls and ghosts, and groups of trees.

—description from the designer

In Dead Serious you play as the Lords of Decay (Nurgle would be proud!) and use your abilities to modify the geo-physical-chemical conditions in which the body rests to obtain the most interesting forms of decomposition.

At the start of the game, there are some corpses (number depends on the number of players) in a neutral state. Players take turns to modify the conditions of the enviroment (pH, oxygen or without oxygen etc) by playing cards. Some cards are only for one body, some have AoE. You could interrupt other players and destroy their work by using cards that modify conditions only on their corpses.
At the end of each turn there is a verification phase if the corpses are in the required states to be counted as refined corpses and if yes, the player gets the points for those.

The scored corpse is reduced to basic, neutral corpse again.

Refined corpses are defined by the random draw at the beginning.

A classic economic-strategic game (where money is victory points), located in the mid-20th century in a hypothetical country occupying the Iberian Peninsula, which simulates the economic-productive cycles with great realism.

The game alternates expansion phases (creation of industries and transportation networks) with operational phases where the production processes are executed, covering the entire chain from the beginning of extraction, through the generation of energy and raw materials, the manufacture of goods until their final commercialization.

In addition to production and transportation, many other economic concepts are also involved, such as markets (prices of goods and resources according to supply and demand, imports and exports), energy (production, distribution and pricing), human and demographic aspects (such as employment, its price, migrations movements, final consumption), financial aspects (bank administration, cash flow management, loans and interest) and even fiscal aspects (taxes and tax evasion).

All these elements are interconnected and interrelated in a game that tries to emulate in a few hours the macroeconomic evolution of a quarter of a century of a country and where the players (in the shoes of avid businessmen) will have to deal with possible crises and economic imbalances that can arise.

boardgame: Heroes of Might and Magic: Battles
description:
Collect, build and paint your favorite Heroes of Might and Magic armies, before pitting them against your enemies in a battle for Erathia. Play like never before in this “rank and file” wargame designed by Jervis Johnson (Warhammer 40k, Fantasy Battles, AoS) & Andy Chambers (Starship Troopers, Warhammer 40k, Fantasy Battles), and produced by the same team that brought you HoMM III: The Board Game.

Heroes of Might & Magic: Battles is a tabletop wargame that brings the iconic Heroes of Might and Magic experience to life. Players assume the roles of Enroth's heroes, leading armies of intricately sculpted miniatures in strategic battles to expand their kingdoms and defeat enemies. The game captures the essence of the original PC game, offering a visually stunning and accessible experience that appeals to both newcomers and seasoned players.

The gameplay focuses on building and commanding armies, with each unit and hero possessing unique abilities that reflect their in-game roles. The rules promote dynamic, interactive battles where timing and strategy are key, with heroes playing a central role in enhancing their troops. Players can customize their armies from various factions, ensuring endless replayability in a game that masterfully blends classic wargaming with modern design.

—description from the publisher

description:
Skulls of Sedlec: Holiday Features is a 3-card expansion to add to your solo games.

Each double-sided card adds either Christmas, Halloween or Valentine features for seasonal play.

The game can be played by 2 to 6 players, aged 9 and up. Its peculiarity consists in putting together the playful component with the knowledge of the famous Italian territory.
The game has precise rules that take into account the deterministic component - which rewards the skill of the individual player - and the fatalistic component which makes the game unpredictable, cheerful and always new.
The game is full of traditional pieces, such as cards, pawns, board and dice; with particular attention to the material and safety.Its uniqueness consists in being entirely dedicated to Puglia: it thus becomes a real handbook, with which to travel to over one hundred locations, to discover the most typical places, the history, the artistic and landscape beauties, the local museums, the gastronomy and folkloristic events.
There are 700 quiz questions and video questions, a high number that allows you to play numerous times, and have fun with curious questions - sometimes in dialect - which will enrich your knowledge of the area. Plus the visual impact of over 250 photos that tell of traditions and places that touch the heart.

boardgame: Altay: Dawn of Civilization – Seafarers Promo Set
description:
The Seafarers promo set adds to your games of Altay – Dawn of Civilization the possibility of journeying through large bodies of water, using the talents of a Shipwright and the knowledge of Astronomy, together with a new Wonder, the Lighthouse. Seafarers includes 13 new game cards and 1 rule card.

—description from the publisher

An 18xx magazine game set in Boston to Washington corridor, based on 18 India.

Depicting the Boston to Washington corridor, 18 BosWash uses 18 India's main rules and components.  You must own a copy of 18 India to play this game.

boardgame: Waste Knights: Second Edition – Kickstarter Exclusive Miniature Pack
description:
There's storm brewing in the west that is going to engulf the whole continent. It's up to you and others of your kind to stop it. Pack your gear, fill your tank to the brim and roll out to the Waste. Be the one to face what fate keeps in store for all survivors.

This expansion enables you to get the KS-exclusive content from Waste Knights: Second Edition campaign:


     The Red Lord miniature to use with the Rose of the Red Lord adventure from the Tales (from the Outback expansion)
     3 new Knights: Mr. Mall, the Huntress and the Scientist, each with their own miniatures, sheet, and Personal Upgrade Cards
     2 special Vehicle Sheets for your travels: Battle Rig and Harry the Camel


—description from the publisher

An 18xx magazine game, set in the Boston to Washington corridor.

1822: Rust Belt Railroads uses all of the rules and components from the 1822: The Railways of Great Britain game.  You must own a copy of 1822: The Railways of Great Britain to play this game.

"The fun way to discover and explore the world of work".

Do you fancy becoming a gardener, a nurse or a community worker?  The basic game has you trying to win cards by comparing various jobs by comparing their values such as physical effort or the average salary for that specific job.

A game for 2-6 people can play. The cards are dealt out among the players. Each player holds their cards face up but may only look at their top card and slowly work through their hand.

To start the first player chooses the a job statistic such as how much physical effort that job entails and the other players read out the value their top card has for that statistic. The winner of that trick has the best or highest value wins and takes the other players hand and pops that into their deck. The winner then choose the statistic for the next round.

The special quality of this game, which separates it from other trump card variants, is that you are able to think about careers and what it takes to get that kind of job.  The main game and the other suggested variants all offer the opportunity to discuss and explore the world of work and for the group to think and challenge their ideas about careers.

This is a card based educational game in the style of the Top Trumps but where players can learn about various careers and what they entail.  There are 50 career cards showing a wide range of occupations such as hair dressers, teachers and sport scientists.  What better way to explore the confusing world of work than playing this game?  The future is a scary and uncertain place but this game helps break down the various work roles are, what they do and how someone might try and enter that field.  Its also a creative game where players can talk about what work actually is and how individuals, communities and business operates.

The game also offers play variations such as creative writing, story based acting improv and a three player word association game.

This game would work well with a teacher or career guidance officer to help explore potential education and job choices with a group.

The cards can also be used as a springboard for a project or creative writing exercise as the designers suggest doing further research and arguing for or against the cards ratings.

The career cards give a basic outline of what a specific job as well as a fun fact.  An example, such as for the role of DOCTOR, advises that "Doctors examine, diagnose and treat patients, usually in hospitals or general practice.  There are over 60 specialist areas of work which can often involve long and irregular hours.  A confident, calm and caring nature is essential".  The fun fact for this card is that "There are more than 600 individual muscles and 206 bones in the human body".

Each career has 6 categories players can use: Study/training, physical effort and brain power as well as social good, average salary and robot risk where the chance that automation or AI might take over and make post holders potentially redundant.

SDGs industrial planner is a board game with a carbon-neutral background, urban development planning as a scenario, and the underlying logic of the 17 UN Sustainable Development Goals. Players play the role of industrial planners, leading their teams to carry out “Development”, “Production”, “Orders” and other work, and in developing the city's industry and economy, do a good job of “Governance” this work, finally, the player who has contributed the most to the city wins the prize. Through the game, players can understand how to balance the Golden Mountain Silverhill and Green Mountains and understand the importance of sustainable development.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game (Second Edition)
description:
Welcome to Legendary®: A Marvel Deck-Building Game! Evil Masterminds like Red Skull, Doctor Doom, and Loki lead a horde of powerful Villains to crush the Marvel Universe. Every time you play, the Mastermind will choose a different evil Scheme to pursue. Only you can stop them, leading awesome Marvel Heroes like Spider-Man, Iron Man, and Wolverine! Celebrating over 10 years after Legendary® first launched, this is the expanded-and-improved Core Set Second Edition.

In this game for 1-5 players, each player starts with their own deck of basic Hero cards. At the start of your turn, you play the top card of the Villain Deck, representing moments when Villains invade the city, capture Bystanders, and create special events. Then, you play Hero cards from your hand to generate Attack, Recruit, and special abilities. You use Attack to fight Villains. You use Recruit to gain stronger Heroes to improve your deck.

Whenever your deck runs out of cards and you need more, you shuffle your discard pile to make a new deck, including all the new Heroes you recruited. This way your deck gets stronger and stronger over time. Build up enough Attack and you can fight the evil Mastermind! But be careful: if the players don’t defeat them quickly enough, then the Mastermind will complete their dark Scheme, win the game for evil, and all players lose!

Even with about 250 cards that have been redeveloped to be more flavorful, fun, and balanced, this Second Edition Core Set is completely compatible with previous Legendary Marvel releases.

Contents:
550 Cards
1 Rubber backed playmat
1 Rulebook

In the Cloud Kingdom, monsters are getting out-of-hand, and your group of adventurers has been called on to clean up the kingdom!  Starting off with just some health and mana, you drop through the clouds, fighting monsters, collecting loot, and hoping that you gain that all-important parachute before you fall off the final cloud!

The gameplay is easy to pick up, and each game is fast and forms a campaign, where you use the loot from the last game to increase your prospects in the next one.

The game can be played either cooperatively or competitively.

—description from the designer

Bousou 1894 is one of the works in the 18xx series, which began with Francis Tresham's 1829 (1974), and depicts the Bousou Peninsula. The history of railways on the Bousou Peninsula (Chiba Prefecture) began in 1894 with the opening of the Ichikawa-Sakura section by the Sobu Railway Co., Ltd.  This is a pen-and-paper game in which you manage a railway company while aiming to be a successful investor.

Like most 18xx games, the game is played in alternating stock rounds (SRs) each followed by two operating rounds (ORs). This cycle (SR, OR, OR) is repeated five times, and the player with the most valuable assets in shares and money, wins. During setup, players will pay for the President’s share of their “main company” (they are president of and can run it) from their initial capital. Then, starting from the last player who chose a main company, players draft two “sub-companies” (a company that only lays tracks, free of charge). Company ownership is recorded on a stock market board with player color pieces. All accounting is tracked in personal booklets.

In a complete series of 1 SR and 2 ORs:

     You can sell as many shares as you want in an SR turn, and then purchase 0-2 shares of stock per turn. You may act as investor or as your main company when conducting your SR turn, paying from each respective pool of funds.
     You can lay 0-3 rail tracks per OR turn for the main company, and only after the sub-companies lay tracks.
     You can purchase up to 2 trains per company OR turn.


In ORs, a single d6 roll will determine your selection of tiles or tile upgrades from a chart. Tracks are independently drawn in your personal booklet, and routes are independently run. However, revenue for companies is publicly announced to the entire group, to account for shared payouts through cross-investments.

After the 10th OR, the final score is calculated through each player’s net worth, totaling personal investor capital plus stock values. Company assets do not affect score. The player with the largest net worth wins. Tied player(s) share victory.

The game consists in a deck of 150 cards, each of them describes  instructions for a word game (i.e.: solve an anagram, find rhymes or palindromes, write an acrostic, etc.) on the front face, then shows the correct answer or some tips in the rear face.
Three levels of difficulty are playable, as each card shows three questions marked with different colors (green=easy, blue=medium, red=hard).

For each turn a card is drawn and all the players distinctly and simultaneously write down their answer/soluton on a note sheet.
The first player that declares to have finished to write his response can check the solution on the rear of the card. If the solution is correct, he can collect that card and the turn ends.

Once a player collects three cards, he wins the game.

The game is strongly bound to the Italian language, as all quizzes require deep knowledge of it.

Wake Up Stars is a game designed for young children that focuses on storytelling, emotions, and self-expression. The game invites players to collect stars by sharing personal stories that reflect the emotions depicted on the stars they choose.

Players spin a spinner to select stars, then share stories related to the emotion on each star. The player who collects the most stars by the end of the game wins.

In Siclen Valley, a competitive deck-building game, players gather resources on Earth choked with flavium, a yellow gas that made the planet uninhabitable, and deliver them to Siclen Valley and the cities built around it. Siclen Valley is an orbital station and a former HQ of the biggest IT corporation of the Old World. These days, it does not matter anymore, but former corporation's couriers have become sackers — the only people who own capsules, cargo ships suitable for round trips. Players take the roles of the sackers and fulfill contracts received from those surviving in orbit.

On their turn, a player chooses between card effects on their hand: a discard effect or an inventory effect (locked and kept for further activations). They play as many cards as they want but must stay within the available mask filter count that gradually decreases. Effects allow players to navigate the Earth’s surface and collect resources for crafting or contract bidding. The round ends when the players run out of available mask filters. The bids are checked, and all the players receive their bonuses.

The game is played in rounds, and the main goal is to earn as much money as possible. To do so, you can fulfill contracts and visit different places of interest on Earth. Money is represented as SiDo, local currency, and it is the only thing that sackers care about. After all, in the past, they were the worst-paid caste in the corporation.

Players can also upgrade their capsules, purchase new cards, and encounter Mosses, the only survivors on the surface — these encounters can be beneficial, too.

Siclen Valley is based on the novel, released together with the game in 2025.

—description from the publisher
japanese Dominion retheme and reshuffle.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description: New cards for Touhou Shisouroku, a re-themed Dominion.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description:
The river is dangerous, but many treasures dot its banks. Dire tortoises will block your path, while the dreaded river kraken threatens both you and your boat. Move quickly. Grab what treasure you can. But, be careful not to be left behind by the boat on the rushing river.

Encounters are single maps for Warriors & Writings. They can be played with any hero from across the Warriors & Writings line. This is not a stand-alone product. A core set of Warriors & Writings is required to play.

Encounters are intended to break the mold of what you might expect from Warriors & Writings. Encounter 2: The River Rush introduces a moving terrain feature in the boat. The boat moves along the length of the map as the rounds go by, opening up new paths as it progresses. In addition, some enemies may attack the boat, and you must prevent it from being destroyed, leaving you stranded.

—description from the publisher

Your movement quickens as mana pulses through your body. You call forth lightning and thunder as a storm rages at your finger tips. Your blade tastes your foes' flesh and your power surges again.

Adventurers are single heroes for Warriors & Writings. They can be played with any map from across the Warriors & Writings line. This is not a stand-alone product. A core set of Warriors & Writings is required to play.

Adventurers are intended to represent more niche fantasy archetypes. Adventurer 1: The Bladecaster presents a hero who combines martial prowess with magical power. She is a very mobile hero who specializes in attacking with her sword, while supplementing that with ranged and area-of-effect spells.

—description from the publisher

Choose 3 dogs to prepare for the All-Breed dog show. Discipline,  train, groom and enter them into the show, by collecting and trading preparation tokens.

Once a player has completed all 3 of their dog cards, the preparation round ends and it's time for the properly prepped pups to head to to the show!

At the show, dogs compete for the judges in 5 rounds of fierce, furry competition.

Each round, players must collect points cards while avoiding mishaps to collect the most points. At the end of 5 rounds, the dog with the most points wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Ultimate Dibs: Gen Con Things Promo Card
description: "Gen Con Things" Category card that was available at Skybound's booth at Gen Con 2024.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
It's the year 1799.

Friedrich Wilhelm Alexander Von Humboldt, famous prusian geographer, naturalist and explorer, receive the Spanish Crown support to embark on a sicentific journey across the Atlantic Ocean. His goal: explore and document territories, societies and natural species from the New World, a far away territory dominated by the main european powers from the epoch.

This travel will change the scientific world forever. Humboldt will document flora and fauna, hike across volcanoes, discover natural phenomena, write about native communities and thoerize about the political future for the Americas.

In this travel, Humboldt will build some of the most important pillars of modern science... abd he's not alone.

In Humboldt, players will control the great scientist and other three characters that will acompany him across five eras during his journey on South America. Each one of the four explorers will travel deep on unknows territories, will found new territories and write treaties to become the greatest scientist of the time.

—description from the rulebook

Humbold is a tile-placing and resource management game, were players need to discover and explore moving around these tiles that represent new territories, species and native communities. These tiles provide different kind of "knowledge" that can be used to discover everything in the game, and then, you will report your finds to get even more points.

—user summary
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces traffic situations hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach the "Ziel"-space wins the game.

—user summary

Liars & Looters Market Mayhem is an expansion to the Liars & Looters base game and introduces 

     Liars and Looter Action cards
     Tribe Phases 1+2
     Blank Event cards
     German translation of event cards

Practitioners of Magic battle for Ultimate power using their unique magical spells.
The White Witch, the Dragon Wizard, The Storm Sorcerer and Heirophant Druid duel each other to climb the steps of ascension. Pitting their own brands of magic in an attempt to outwit and beat their opponents in an elegant game of bluff, guile and wit.
Each player has a repertoire of spells in the form of a deck of cards. Every player places their cards into play and the active player chooses which 2 spellcasters face off against each other. The results of the battle elevate the winner towards Ultimate power.

—description from the publisher

Gold is flowing down the river! Smuggle, gamble and steal your way to fortune.

Gold Hustle is a fast-paced, wild west card game where players simultaneously pick characters from their hand to try and win the most gold.

—description from the publisher

Face the gloomy Basilisk and fight for the Phoenix's favor in an exciting solo experience.

Contents: 
12 sector cards
3 twist cards
12 story cards
1 rulesheet
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Six new event cards and six new revelation cards to enrich your gameplay.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: 地主来了: 大丰收 (Landlord's Coming: Bumper Harvest)
description:
地主来了-大丰收 (The Landlord Arrives: Bumper Harvest) is a competitive board game inspired by traditional agriculture in China. Players take on the roles of farmers or landlords, competing to harvest crops and earn the most points by the end of the game. The game captures the essence of rural life, where strategy and resource management are key to achieving a bountiful harvest.

Gameplay
Players begin by drawing role cards that assign them to either the landlord or farmer teams. The landlord operates independently, while the two farmers work together. The game progresses through several phases, including card drawing, crop harvesting, and strategic use of action cards. Players accumulate points by combining crop cards to produce goods like oil or alcohol. Special action cards can disrupt opponents' strategies, adding a layer of tactical play.

Objective / Victory Condition
The objective is to amass the highest score by the end of the game. The landlord and farmers compete, with the farmers combining their scores against the landlord. The player or team with the highest total score at the end of the game wins.

Llengut is a game based on questions to learn about Catalan and the rest of the world's languages.

Get all the answers right to become the smartest in the group!

---

Llengut és un joc basat en preguntes per aprendre sobre el català i la resta de llengües del món.

Encerta totes les respostes que puguis per convertir-te en el més llengut o llenguda del grup!

---

Llengut es un juego basado en preguntas para aprender sobre el catalán y el resto de lenguas del mundo.

¡Acierta todas las respuestas que puedas para convertirte en el más llenado o llenada del grupo!

Pispa is a family and educational card game, with visual and mental skills. Recommended for ages 10 and up, from 2 to 6 players, with games lasting between 15 and 40 minutes.

Pispa Letters is a word-making game with a very fun feature: you can't look at your cards, but you can see the others'! Which letter will you point out to the player on your right? It's a matter of strategy.

By looking at the other people's cards and using your own, you have to imagine what words you can make. Get all the letters you need!

But can you remember where you were told you had the A?

—description from the publisher

Pispa és un juego familiar y educativo de cartas ,de habilidad visual y mental. Recomendado a partir de 10 años de edad , de 2 a 6 jugadores, con partidas de entre 15 y 40 minutos de duración.

Pispa Letras es un juego de hacer palabras con una particularidad muy divertida: no puedes mirar tus cartas pero ves las de los demás! ¿Qué letra señalarás al jugador de tu derecha? Es cuestión de estrategia.

Observando las cartas de los demás y utilizando de las tuyas, tienes que imaginar qué palabras puedes hacer. ¡Pilla todas las letras que te hagan falta!

Pero, ¿Eres capaz de recordar dónde te han dicho que tenías la A?

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

After a brief period of tranquility, the empire faced an unexpected challenge that no one could have predicted.
A new figure emerges, initiating a conflict that will not only affect the inhabitants of the plane of the living, but will take the action far beyond, to the Yomi, where yokai and spirits seek their chance to break a balance that has remained unchanged for eons.
Our new group of protagonists, including some old friends, will have to face a threat that will test these mere mortals, as they try to find their way back home.

—description from the publisher

Each player is assigned a color on the game board, so that he has 10 round Spaces, which are combined in a pentagram and 3 round spaces inside the cauldron.
Each player spins the top once in turn. If a red side falls upwards, the player covers any space of his pentagram with a cover token. However, if a black side falls upwards, the player must cover a space of his color in the cauldron.
If a player has to cover all 3 spaces in the cauldron before he has covered all the spaces of the pentagram, he is eliminated from the game.
The winner is the first person to cover all the spaces of his pentagram.

—user summary

Hybrid/Symbiote Companions is a new hero promo expansion for Marvel United, released for San Diego Comic Con in 2024.

It consists of 13 cards:

     3 "Agony" hero cards
     3 "Riot" hero cards
     3 "Lasher" hero cards
     3 "Phage" hero cards
     1 Rules reference card


The pack can be played one of two ways.


     Symbiotes as Companions: Choose one of the four symbiotes and shuffle their 3 hero cards into your deck and play as normal. Each hero card can be played as if it were your own.



     Symbiote Hero (Hybrid): Shuffle all 12 hero cards together to make the Hybrid hero and play as you would any other hero. Take any existing symbiote (Venom, Carnage, Agent Venom, Anti-Venom,, etc.) as your player piece.

This is John Bohrer's original 2002 Age of Steam: Ireland expansion. This is not the 2003 Martin Wallace map with de-urbanization and ferries. This expansion provides the actual accurate entirety of Ireland and addresses the challenges brought by the decision to build railways in broad gauge. A novel mechanism assists in the creation of the Irish Railways. It is best with 3 or 5 players. The expansion can be used with every licensed Age of Steam game, from the original Winsome printing, the later Warfrog printings, the early Eagle printings and the latest Eagle printing of Age of Steam Deluxe.

Get ready to get random with this game of questionable situations! With 100 madcap, made-up scenarios, two teams compete to see who can give the best answer. From far-out and fantastical to truly smart ideas, you'll have to really think outside the box for a chance to scoop up the points.

Guaranteed for side-splitting laughs as you try to outstmart the other team!

Showdown 2 (一决胜负2) is the sequel of Showdown. It builds upon the foundation of the original by adding new characters, abilities, and strategic depth.

Gameplay
Players engage in intense one-on-one duels, alternating between offensive and defensive turns. Each player manages their character's health and balance, strategically playing skill cards to attack, defend, and manipulate the battlefield. The dynamic between health and balance adds layers of strategy, as depleting either can lead to a critical state.

Objective/Victory Condition

     The main objective is to defeat your opponent by reducing their health to zero or breaking their balance to a "KO" state, followed by delivering a finishing blow.
     After each duel, the victorious player earns coins, which can be used to redraw the deck.
     The first player to accumulate 10 coins wins the game. In the event of a tie, a final duel determines the winner.

The once grand empire Ethradinar has shattered. The now divided provinces are on the brink of collapse and neighboring kingdoms smell weakness.

Players take on the role of Ethradin monarchs each leading a province. Every turn players choose to draw a card from one of 3 decks to collect resources and find magical powers. They use these resources to mobilize their armies and initiate attacks. By eliminating other players a monarch begins to reforge the once grand empire under their rule.

Reforged is a light, fast paced, family friendly game with high player interaction.

—description from the publisher

Terra Eterna is a  fast-paced, semi-cooperative fantasy dungeon crawler focused on intense battles, character building, and leveling up while completing bounties and quests to earn victory points. While other dungeon crawlers gets bigger boxes, bigger minis, bigger campaigns, longer play time, Terra Eterna does the reverse: Condensing the dungeon crawler experience so you have time to play more of it!

The game begins with the Adventure phase, where players decide which quest to tackle and strategize on how best to approach it. When players encounter monsters, they trigger the Battle phase, where they roll dice and play situational cards from their hands to defeat the enemies.

The objective is to accumulate as many victory points as possible, surpassing a certain threshold by completing quests and individual story objectives.

—description from the designer

Mini expansion for Daxu.

SET UP

Deal 1 card to each player (face-down) at the start of the game. The player may look at the card but keeps it private.

SCORING

Count the two shop types on your secret patron card as if they are cards in front of you.

RULES

You may reveal your card at any time between rounds to gain 2 reputation immediately. Only the first player to do this receives this benefit. The other player may not use this benefit as soon as the other player reveals (they simply keep their card hidden until end of game scoring).

Four Parties with competing interests (Clergy, Nobles, Merchants, and Rebels) wheel and deal to shape their city-state's new Constitution via a shared-scoring system. Meanwhile, Independent players seek to achieve their own unique objectives.

Parties primarily earn points by adding Articles to the Constitution that align with their party’s Virtues. Every Article corresponds to one of four Virtues: Order, Wealth, Freedom, and Justice. Since each Virtue is shared between two parties, no one scores alone! While your party is small, you share common goals with almost everyone. Winning requires shrewdly building and breaking alliances, and manipulating the convention’s processes.

Players are split into Councils. Over five rounds, each Council’s members bid Influence to determine which Article their Council will add to the Constitution The player at the top of each Council board is the Council Chair, a position with powerful privileges. Persuading the Council Chair to act in your interests (or ousting them) is key. The Council Chair breaks ties and resolves Edicts, special laws that are passed as Articles are added to the Constitution.

—description from the publisher

Well, despite our least efforts, it looks like the world is ending.

Billionaire Bunker is a roll-and-write game where one player is the selfish Billionaire, building a bunker in anticipation of the forthcoming reckoning. Find a suitable spot to build, spread propaganda, and get prepped before the hordes of disaffected Civilians come to teach you about a little concept called 'redistribution of wealth'.

The other player is the Civilians, who will be using the Billionaire's leftovers to try and increase their ranks, smuggle into the bunker, and build a giant guillotine. WONDER WHAT THAT'S FOR.

After 20 rounds, the Civilians will storm the Billionaire's map and try to reach the greedy jerk before they're picked off one by one.

How well has the Billionaire prepared? How strong are the Civilians?


     Play as either the Billionaire or Civilians, each with their own distinct game mechanics.
