     Easy-to-learn but with a lot of strategic depth, as each side must decide what to prioritize and how best to plan for the endgame.
     Unique mechanic where one player enters the other player's map.


—description from the publisher

The core of the Institute’s forces are the Gen-1 and Gen-2 Synths, robots built to mimic human form but skeletal and bare metal – though no less deadly for that. They are deployed to defend strongholds and facilities of interest to the Institute, and attack in large groups to overwhelm and devastate their foes.

Gen-1 Synths are the first prototypes the Institute created to copy basic human skeletons and structures. Robotic androids designed to be Radiation proof and combat ready, they continue to fight even when limbs are removed. Only complete destruction of the processors and torso are enough to stop them - and even then, they can often be salvaged and repaired, ready to fight again.

Gen-2 Synths are the Institute’s first attempt at full human mimicry. Clad in plastic approximations of human flesh, they are hardier than their Gen-1 antecedents and more tactically aware. Gen-1s often will throw themselves at an enemy, headless of the cost to themselves, but the Gen-2s are cannier, more likely to evaluate the target first. Far from cowardly however, when necessary they with still run headlong into a fight to serve the Institute’s purposes – as long as the target is neutralised, the cost is worth it.

This boxed set contains six 32mm scale multi-part high quality resin Institute miniatures, each with unique scenic bases, to allow players to expand their Fallout: Wasteland Warfare games. Players will need the Institute Wave Card Expansion Pack to use the contents of this box.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – NCR Core Box
description:
The New Californian Republic rose from the ashes of the great war to rebuild and rejuvenate swathes of what as the western united states. Starting from a small trading town, it expanded and grew, and now covers tracts of land all the way up to New Vegas and the Hoover Dam. Their aims are to build and protect, but not all believe in this new system or it’s protectors.

With this in mind the Republic has built up its fighting forces to include rangers and troopers, all capable warriors with skilled tactical leaders and firepower to boot. They are ambitious, seemingly righteous, and ready to take on all comers in their fight for the land they see as theirs.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Trailblazer: The John Muir Trail – Upgrade Kit
description:
This kit upgrades the retail edition of the game with most of the Kickstarter-exclusive content.
It includes 20 wooden tracks tokens, the mission deck, the skills deck, the Black Bear and Bluebird mini expansion, and four additional map packs.


Note that the 14 unique trail cards are included in the Hiking Pack, along with the six additional skills cards.

This board game recreates such a historical time by portraying Portugal and Holland's race to conquer the riches of Southeast Asia. The game aims to claim all the islands marked by a number present on the board.
The board is subdivided into 368 squares (23 × 16), each defined by coordinates of longitude (columns) and latitude (rows). The players have three types of ships that move according to the value of dies at different speeds. Each ship has a specific feature. The board is interspersed with islands with allocated values (treasures) that the players must collect and protect with fortresses and ships. 
Treasures and ships are jeopardized by pirates and typhoons that can wipe out these elements from the board and that move around the board randomly using spinners.
The players need to decide how to move their ships around and how to allocate resources, and the presence of pirates or typhoons adds randomness to the game. The board and components can be expanded, for instance, to accommodate four players and additional ship types.

—description from the designer

Paladins of Glass is set in the world of Feoras, a world in a never ending cycle of decay and rebirth. This game is set at the end of a period of decay. The Carrion Covenant, being carrion feeders, are trying to lock the world into never ending decay. You and your allies need to gain the power of at least three of the spirits that watch over this land and defeat the Covenant before they can complete their ritual.

Energy as a resource - Players spend as much energy as they wish on a turn taking actions like encountering enemies, exploring the land, going to market or attempting the tests of the spirits. BUT! They only recover a limited amount at the start of their next turn. And if a player takes damage when they have no more energy left, they permanently lose shards.

When attempting to overcome an enemy, explore, or do other tasks, you may be asked to do a check. Players roll dice equal to the attribute for the check, looking to get a target number of successes (4, 5, or 6). Players can spend energy to reroll a die until they succeed, leaving players to decide whether to push to exhaustion or play it cautious.

Player choice is at the heart of the game, allowing players to experiment, explore, and find their own path through the world. While there are optional preset characters, players are encouraged to build their own characters. Exploration cards always present an option to how players approach the given obstacle, and even enemies allow players to fight, persuade, or intimidate, with impactful differences between these choices.

With an open decision space supported by a few straightforward mechanics, Paladins of Glass is a great mid weight game aimed at mixed level tables that encourages teamwork and can be enjoyed by anyone.

—description from the publisher

Cruise your canal boat around "the cut": the English and Welsh canal and river system that connects Rippon to Reading, London to Llangollen, and Bath to Bradford.

Your journey will be slowed by long canal tunnels and spectacular flights of locks.  Chance events may hold you up, hinder other players, or speed you on your way as you race to be the first player to visit three destinations before returning to Start to be crowned King of the Canals.

Two different ways of playing are possible: "The Canal Exploration Race" where players race to visit the same three, randomly chosen, destinations before returning to the start to be crowned "king of the Canals";

In the more strategic "Collectors Game" players travel to three different locations collecting as many canal brasses as they can before returning to the start.

In both versions players throw a die and move their boat. If a six is thrown an event card is taken, read and followed.  Sections of the canals with lots of locks, tunnels, moored boats and bends slow movement - so choose your route carefully.

—description from the designer

In this bluffing game, players collect cards that add to 12.
Some players offer the cards you need. Others are bluffing. Be clever in who you trust or you may be left with a dropped pie!

Contents: 63 colorful cards and rules for 5 different games.

In this active game, everyone is getting rid of cards. If you have a number 1 more or 1 less than the last card, then give it away! Cards are always changing in this number-hopping game.

Everything stops with the Crazy Frog! Cards jump to a new number and the action begins again! Two different ways to play.

Place two cards in the center. Deal the remaining deck. Players start with five cards in their hands. When you have a number 1 more or 1 less than the top card, give it away. Numbers are always changing! Frog cards can jump on any number and the action begins again! The first out of cards wins.

Contents: 66 cards and 2 sets of rules.

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Buttons & Bugs – Narrow Escape Scenario
description:
2.5: Narrow Escape


A new promo scenario originally available at Gen Con 2024.

This scenario is meant to (optionally) be played after scenario 2. After completion you have the choice between Piercing Silver Arrow and Shrunken Short Sword items and then you proceed to Scenario 5.

Al-Jabr is a competitive game for 2 to 5 players. During a round, each player will try to reach a randomly chosen target number. The player who comes closest to the target number wins the round. 
-description from publisher's website

In each round two digit cards are opened, forming a target number. Each player receives 5 random digit cards and a series of mathematical operators. At the signal, everyone must try to organize all their cards, using the mathematical operators, in such a way as to create an algebraic equation to arrive at the target value or as close to it as possible.
When a player thinks their account is ready or close enough, they call an end to the round and turn over an hourglass. Now the other players only have the hourglass time to finish their turns.
After the time is up, everyone checks that their equations are correct and the player who came closest to the target number wins a point.
The player who gets 3 points first wins the game.

The game also comes with a manual for teachers, teaching them how to use this game in the classroom.
-user summary

Uncle Beary's Bedtime is a family-friendly game designed to help young children develop early counting skills while having fun.

How to Play
Players take turns rolling the die. Based on the roll, players will either move the bear cubs from a toy tile to their bed tile or from their bed tile back to a toy tile, or move Uncle Beary to the next room. The player with the most cubs on their bed tile at the end of the game wins!

Components

     27 Bear Cubs
     5 Toy Tiles
     4 Bed Tiles
     1 Uncle Beary Pawn
     1 Six-Sided Die
     1 Rules Booklet


Educational Focus
The game supports skills in counting, focus, and self-control.

In Lily Pond, players roll a die and hop their frog around the pond on the gameboard landing on or jumping through the lily pad letters needed for their word cards. The first player to hop through their letters in the correct order and then hop back to the center wins the game.

Components


     1 Gameboard
     60 Word Cards (three-letter, four-letter, and five-letter words)
     4 Frog Pawns
     4 Letter Trackers
     1 Die
     1 Rules Booklet

Set in a loose depiction of the Caribbean Sea, this pirate based board game affords the gamers many different strategies to choose from. Each with its own path to victory.
The game play is turn based.
Each player starts the game with a base vessel and a captain. You can choose to loot other pirates' ports or risk being out from port far enough to draw a pirates fate.
Within the pirate's fate deck are a multitude of good and bad cards that increase ship stats or abilities or wreak all sorts of havoc. draw a weak merchant vessel and on board both loot and spoils after successfully defeating it. The further from home you are looting the rarer the loot and the higher the value.
As Loot and spoils are gathered each pirate can upgrade their ship on their turn or purchase new ships and add another captain to their fleet.
When the deck is played through add in the expansion cards and the nasty fates and reshuffle the deck.
Be the first to gather 60 loot in your home port at one time and win the game.
Shorten the game by invoking carpe diem at the start and give each pirate the ability to purchase an additonal pirate vessel on their first turn or upgrade the one they start the game with.
There are a few alternate goals and rules. 
The majority of the artwork in the TGC version of the game is as prompted AI and left as it was originally generated.

A new land has emerged from the ashes of the old empire. Factions have risen to compete for the throne. Can you be the first to lay claim to the island of Ibyron?

Ibyron: Island of Discovery is a two-tiered worker placement game wherein players all work the land of a newly-discovered island to build the city the to its capacity. Using resource gathering, building construction, exploration and movement mechanisms for the Wilderness Phase, supported by an additive Worker Placement mechanism during the Village Phase, each player will design their own city; each building adding to options in game play.

Each player will start out in the Wilderness phase each round. Actions will consist of using workers tokens on the island tiles, some actions requiring 1 or more workers to be used to perform that action. Players will continue in the Wilderness phase until they run out workers to perform said actions. But if a player finishes their wilderness actions before others, they don't just sit around; they head into the Village phase and may get better placements for their workers on the Village board.

In the Village phase the players will use the same workers that worked for them in the Wilderness phase in this phase as well, but will instead perform money-making, shipbuilding, hiring horses, taxing, and farming actions. Each space in the village requires 1 more worker for each additional use, no matter the player. After the workers are again spent, they go into the rest box to wait to be paid to work again in the following round, in cash or food.

Each building that is constructed will require permanent residence i.e. worker tokens will be used as the building itself. Using worker tokens as buildings will cut down on a player's supply of workers available in following rounds, so knowing when to construct buildings is an important timing constraint.

There is a race to the end game, but getting there will support many strategies. The first player to build a complete working city (all but 1-3 worker tokens used in buildings), wins.

It was heavily inspired by the Splotter games: Roads & Boats and Antiquity. It was not designed to replace those games, but to deeply entrench you for a good 2 hours.

—description from the publisher

Originally available at Gen Con 2024.

Rules allowing up to 4 players to play a series of 3 games against each other.
Each game uses 1 of the 3 Great Facades expansions.

From 1950 to 1953, in the great War to Resist U.S. Aggression and Aid Korea, the Chinese People’s Volunteer Army shouldered the historical mission. With incomparable bravery and wisdom, they fought and defeated U.S. imperialism.

Units are mainly composed of divisions, brigades and regiments. Each hex on the map represents 18 kilometers in reality.

This game contains several independent scenarios:


     People's Army Southern Offensive
     The People's Army's Nakdong River Offensive
     The Allied Forces' Incheon Counterattack 
     The Volunteer Army Arrival
     The Third Battle of Seoul


—extract from the back of the box (translated)
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Try to catch most bugs before the time runs out! There is a frog timer for 60 seconds. There is 2 ways to play, catch most bugs with frog tongues before time is out or help fireflies to rescue as many of their friends before the frog gets them.

In this card game, you are treasure hungry pirate looking to steal treasure and protect it in your treasure hoard. Rival pirates are everywhere looking to rob you blind. Find pairs of treasure cards and bury them in your treasure hoard. The further down they are buried, the safer they are. Use single cards to steal treasure from other pirates. If you play all of your cards, that's "booty" and you dig for treasure on the treasure island. The pirate with the highest valued treasure at the end of the game wins. Are you cunning enough to steal, block and re-steal your way to the largest treasure hoard?

The Achab Corporation has deployed its deadliest weapon in defense of its Database: Prometheus! The first I.C.E. Titan-class, an opponent so fearsome that to survive the Breachers will have to put aside their differences and… cooperate!

Prometheus expansion introduces Co-Op mode to The Breach. A new Avatar is here to face Prometheus: Nero is ready to sow flames and destruction within the Network!

In Cooperative Mode, players share a common goal: to defeat Prometheus before it removes all the Gates from the Database. To do this, they must first disable parts of the Titan’s body and then assign Viruses to defeat it.

—description from the publisher

The card pack expansion Pagan: Behind the Veil reveals the ancient history of Roanoke, its secret rites and forgotten pacts that haunt the very heart of the centuries-old trees.

The witch has sought out these secrets to bend them to her will, ready to strengthen her curses with the raw essence of eras past, while the hunter does everything in his power to break these bonds and destroy his sworn enemy.

To play this expansion, you need to own the base game Pagan and the Beyond the Palisades expansion.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Japanese Breachers join the fight against the Achab Corporation!
With Avatars inspired by the lore of their country, they are ready to make fast and deadly raids in the Database, where they will prove that they are second to none in skill and determination. Their programs and technology are innovative and ingenious, so the Breachers community has already learned to respect and fear them. But this will not satisfy their thirst for fortune and glory!

Kabuki Nights expansion includes the components needed to play The Breach games with up to 6 players. New Breachers will join the Database attack and use new Avatars Gura and Kusari to infect its Rooms.

—description from the rulebook

With the card pack expansion Pagan: Through the Mist, players wander through dark and treacherous lands that test the limits of their perception.

For the witch, the mist is an inexhaustible source of darkness; for the hunter, it's both a means of hiding from prying eyes and an obstacle that hides the clues he desperately needs. Will it make an ally... or an enemy?

To play this expansion pack, you need the base game Pagan and the Beyond the Palisades expansion pack.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Erebus introduces Team Mode in The Breach in which each player has to cooperate with other members of their team and counter their opponents.

Many ex-Breachers have betrayed the cause and their companions, and now control the Agents in the database. This new generation of sentient enemies will give the Breachers a real run for their money, and they will have to unite against these common enemies.

This expansion introduces Team Mode in which players, divided into Breacher Team and Corp. Team, must coordinate within the Erebus Database to lead their team to victory.
Each player on the Breacher Team controls an Avatar in the Database, while each player on the Corp. Team controls an Agent and Firewall I.C.E.s.

In Team Mode, the game ends when one of the two Teams wins the game.
To win the game, the Breacher Team must complete all the Objective Cards they have.
To win the game, the Corp. Team must complete all Corp. Objective Cards and the I.C.E. Cube on the Corp. Track must have reached or passed the Debug icon.

—description from the rulebook

The Pagan: Until Proven Guilty card pack expansion focuses on the social fabric of the colony as it unravels.

The doubt and mistrust sown by the Witch is spreading like the plague, and darkness is darkening the hearts of the villagers as they turn on each other. In the midst of this chaos, the Hunter strives to remain clear-sighted and tries to discover the truth in this sea of lies.

To play this expansion pack, you need the base game Pagan and the Beyond the Palisades expansion pack.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The high-pitched whine of an FPV drone is the modern equivalent of the Stuka siren from the Second World War. From a clear blue sky, a drone appears, traveling so fast that it nearly outpaces the noise of its motors. Those who hear it may have only seconds to live. Drones have changed the face of modern warfare.

Game of Drones™ is a trademark of Historic Wings & Thomas Van Hare. It is a solitaire wargame set in Russia’s Ukraine War during the Summer of 2024. The game puts you in command of a four-person Ukrainian drone team fighting against Russian forces in Donetsk.

To begin, you name your drone team. Examples of actual combat teams in Ukraine are: “Asgard”, “Sky Lords”, “Code 9.2”, “Limbo”, “Magyar”, and “Peaky Blinders”. Then you equip your team with upgraded equipment and deploy into combat along the zero line. Your brigade HQ directs you to search for certain types of Russian forces. When you spot them, you may call for artillery fire, drop bomblets and bombs with your drone bombers, including oversized Baba Yagas, or perform direct attack missions with FPV drones.

Meanwhile, the Russians will employ electronic warfare and jamming to knock your drones out of the sky. You may endure shelling from Russian artillery, mortars, and Grad rockets. Above all, you must avoid getting targeted by the most deadly weapon of all, the FAB, or Fugasnaya Aviatsionnaya Bomba (which translates to “high-explosive aerial bomb”). Amidst injuries, you will struggle to maintain morale, and sometimes you will be forced to evacuate if things get too rough.

The drones of 2024 are not the ones we know from the Global War on Terror. This war is not fought from air-conditioned trailers or offices far away in safety but from rat-infested bunkers a few kilometers from the zero line, under fire, and often in desperate conditions – at least you might find a stray dog or cat to adopt as a mascot.

This will be your team’s first tour of duty. You will be in the Krasnohorivka sector for two months during June and July 2024, a pivotal time in this war. The game has you in the thick of it.

Every effort has been made to make gameplay as realistic as possible. Multiple unclassified sources were involved in the production of this wargame book. Ukrainian drone pilots and team members were interviewed and their firsthand experiences provide a unique window into what it is like to fight with drones in this new era of war.

Game of Drones is a narrative-style wargame. The experiences you create while playing the game are so realistic that you will be able to keep a narrative diary of the events of each day as it happens. This written narrative forms the heart of your wargaming experience. Those who feel that they cannot write well will find the game enjoyable, even if played without the narrative diary. Like all Historic Wings games, this is not a “roll and write” or a typical “book game”, it is a full-featured wargame – just one that happens to be presented in book form.

As you read and play, you will learn about the war in Ukraine and Russia. You will recognize the hardships and challenges faced by drone teams when carrying out missions. You will encounter the risks they face and suffer the consequences of any bad decisions you make. To win, you must operate boldly yet with care. You are always one mistake from death.

You fly Mav and Matrixe quad-copters; Classics and Baba Yaga drones as bombers; and FPVs in pinpoint attack missions. If you are discovered, you will be targeted by the Russians, who know that drones are the key to victory on today’s battlefields. In other words, your team is one of the highest priority targets in the war.

Your mission is to destroy as many Russians as possible and survive your first two-month deployment. Along the way, your team will build up their skills and equipment for the future.

Slava Ukraini! Слава Україні!

—description from the designer
family: Series: Air War Book Games (Historic Wings)

description:
Create a beautiful undersea chain by matching domino picture cards featuring Rainbow Fish and his friends.  Whoever gets rid of all his cards first wins!

This classic matching game, using art from Marcus Pfister's bestselling Rainbow Fish books, will entertain young children for hours; help build cognitive, matching, and fine motor skills; and develop social awareness - taking turns, following rules, sharing, and friendly competition.

—description from the box

DRN-Utah Beach is the last of the "Beach" games that completes the Death Ride Normandy series, at least for the main beaches.  It sees the entrance of the 4th Infantry Division and the 101st Airborne Division into the battle zone.

The airborne drop here is very messy and replicates the real drop very well with extremely dispersed units and many units losing strength as they land.

At Utah the beach landings proceeded fairly easily and although the landing craft drifted to the south, this was a lucky break for them, as they landed in a less well protected area.

—description from the designer

A party game crowdfunded on Kickstarter by a bunch of weirdos.

Each round, you must convince the "Boss" that you are the right weirdo for a daring mission. The catch? Your resume consists of random traits and experiences your friends wrote for you. It's “Mad Libs” and “Cards Against Humanity” meeting in a job interview.

Gameplay

Each Player writes in 3 "resume traits" in the following categories on small slips of paper, then drops them in piles in the center of the table.

     Appearance
     Inventory
     Experience


Each category is shuffled and redistributed. You might end up with something like:

     left arm is a breaded chicken wing
     a bazooka
     literally doesn't stop whistling


Then you have to use each trait to persuade the player judge (The Boss) why you'd be the best for a the Mission card drawn, which could be something like:

     survive the full moon on werewolf infested campgrounds
     steal the declaration of independence
     teach a robot how to love


The Boss pays out Weirdo Bucks to the winners, then a new round begins with a new Boss. The Player with the most Bucks at the end wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: EPOS: A Gentes Game – Carawansery and Tablets of Law
description:
These are two cards offered as a promo to the game:
EPOS: A Gentes Game. They were offered as a gift and add-on. These are two civilization cards.

The Caravansery card needs a requirement of 2 Soldiers, 2 Craftsmen and 2 Merchants to be played. The rewards when played are an immediate two points and during the reform phase it supplies a cube to the Temple area. It also has all four Synergy symbols for one time scoring when played (not for future scoring!).

The Tablets of Law Card needs a requirement of one city built, 2 Soldiers, two Scholars, one Craftsman and one Merchant. The rewards when played are an immediate 3 points, but at a reduction of one population. During the Flourish phase it also allows a one time cube removal from a card's ability or city on the board to be able to do the action again. It also has all four Synergy symbols for points during acquisition.

boardgame: EPOS: A Gentes Game – Cult Site and Stand Assembly
description:
These are two cards offered as a promo to the game:
EPOS: A Gentes Game. They were offered as a gift and add-on. These are two civilization cards.

The Cult Site card needs a requirement of 2 Nobles, 2 Priests and 2 Scholars to be played. The rewards when played are an immediate two points and during the reform phase it supplies a cube to the Oracle area. It also has all four Synergy symbols for one time scoring when played (not for future scoring).

The Stand Assembly Card needs a requirement of 2 Nobles, two Priests, one Craftsman and one Merchant. The rewards when played are an immediate 3 points, but at a reduction of one population. During the Flourish phase it also allows a one time cube removal from a card's ability or city on the board to be able to do the action again. It also has all four Synergy symbols for points during acquisition.

Chimney: dead in explosion 【Police Side】

A new Character card that is a promo for Essen Spiel 2024.

It is used to replace one of the following Character cards:
Young Lady;
Holy Warrior or
Suspicious Person

If this card is played face up, all Police Dogs, played before or after it regardless of location, lose their effects.
designers: イイダ タカアキ (Iida Takaaki)

description:
Deadly Missions is the first expansion set for Ulandi Wars. Elevate your game by collecting and earning mission tokens. Seize victory by being the first to complete your Mission Card and unleash chaos on your opponents with an arsenal of new deadly event cards.

This expansion set revolutionise the Ulandi Wars landscape by offering alternative victory conditions. Inside the box, you’ll find everything you need for an immersive gaming session:


     1 x 50-Card Missions Event Deck
     10 x Mission Cards
     1 x Token Sheet
     1 x Double-Sided Rulebook, complete with an A2 size Ulandi Galaxy poster


Whether you’re a seasoned Ulandi Wars veteran or a newcomer eager to explore new tactical dimensions, the Deadly Missions expansion set is a must-have addition to your gaming collection.

—description from the publisher

This promo, labeled P08, was included with Nucleum at Essen SPIEL '23.

It is an farm enhancement card. 
The effect on the top is: whenever you lower the waterlevel by 2 or more gain a tool.

The effect on the bottom is: 2 VP for any of your Windmills adjacent to an "gain any tulip" area.

It's time to turn the heat up!

If you've always thought 'I could do better that that lot on the TV', now is your chance to flex your foodie muscles and show what you are made of.

MasterChef Italian Game Night whisks you off to the land of pasta, gelato and Parma ham and pits you against each other in a series of mini games, all designed to test your cooking reflexes and gastronomic genius. Collect the best ingredients by beating the others in the Mini Games but remember that this game is far from cut and dried - it all boils down to the final meal!

With laughs guarenteed, MasterChef Italian Game Night will entertain, whether you're the next culinary star or can barely hold a skillet!

—description from the publisher

This expansion adds both a new Boxfish suit and Antibody cards to the game.

The Boxfish card allows a 6th player to play the game. It also adds a new type of toxicity for the advanced games : toxic by Mucus. When the Boxfish suit is the lead suit, the card with the middle value wins. If an even amount of boxfish cards is played, the card with the lowest number among the two middle cards wins the trick.

The six Antibody cards correspond to each of the suits of the game. One is given to the player which is currently losing the game at the end of each round : they get the antidote card corresponding to the suit they had the most on the previous round. In the subsequent round, if that player manages to win the most cards of that suit, they gain a Poison token.

boardgame: Rifle Squad: US at Omaha Beach – A Solitaire Wargame
description:
The D-Day landings took place on June 6th, 1944. Taking Omaha beach was the responsibility of US soldiers. The main objective was to secure a beachhead five miles inland and to link Gold beach with Utah beach. Just over half the German defenders were battle hardened combat soldiers, and were generally positioned in strongpoints along the coast. In the game, the Player will take control of a single Rifle Squad consisting of 12 men. The exploits of the Squad can be played out in a Campaign across 3 Missions, chosen at random on each playthrough. The first Mission will consist of a beach landing, the second Mission will require the soldiers to push inland and the third Mission will require them to take the final Objective for victory. Injured soldiers, unless healed by the medic, will be ineffective for the remainder of the Mission, but will recover for the next Mission. Soldiers killed in action in any Mission will be unavailable for the rest of the Campaign.

The aim of this game is to present a set of clear and easy to read rules, beautiful Mission Maps and clear, useful game counters. Individual Missions within a Campaign can be played in around an hour or so, and each one should provide endless replayability.

How the game works
This game is a solitaire wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game. Ideally, you will need 9 standard six-sided dice, although the number of dice needed can easily be reduced simply by rolling fewer dice several times as required. It is highly recommended that the counters provided on the final page of the game are used to play this game. Simply stick them to card and cut them out. A video demonstrating how generally to do this can be found on the Mike Lambo Solitaire Book Games YouTube channel.

Players: 1
Playing time: 1 hour or so per Mission

-description from designer

Compitum (from the Latin word for "crossroad") is a dynamic mancala movement game for 1-2 players, suitable for all ages. This abstract strategy game combines classic mancala mechanics with a unique cross-shaped board, challenging you to master the intersection and control the crossroads.

Goal of the game: To be the 1st player to claim 5 discs of his colour.

The rulebook can be printed, in order to play the player needs to provide the following wooden components or similar to play:
- 27 discs (9 White, 9 Black, 9 Red)

Disclaimer: the game can receive updates\small adjustments in the future following feedback from the players.

This is the game #04 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.

-description from designer
Stack small salmon and eggs on the back of a large salmon.

boardgame: Ghost Gate Chronicles: Part 1.5 – Skulk's Brew
description:
In this standalone expansion to our roll&write game The Divide, you play as the Skulk, a group of Fox Witches who siphon the Dreams, Souls and Magics of the world to create concoctions of great power.

Created as an Advanced version of The Divide, in Skulk's Brew you can move symbols into Cauldron areas to gain access to new powerful items, gain coins to move through the rivers and ink in new symbols as you use your Brew Cards to create combos and new ways to score.

Control the power of spirits and use the power of the moon phase to go head-to-head with your rivals in a strategic battle card game for 2-6 players. You’ll play as a powerful shaman that would use spirits to summon powerful spells, totems, special weapons and armors in order to fight to prove prowess. Your goal is to collect 15 moon spirits in order to become the master of the moon by destroying gear cards, using sacrifice spells or by stealing spirits from unprotected shamans. The moon needs a master, are you fit to the role?

—description from the designer

Finnelis is an educational and family game that aims to be a first contact and an experience for children about everything that represents the daily money management of a household.
Through a simulation of family finances, the aim is to encourage a reasoned use of one's own resources that includes aspects such as awareness, personal initiative and responsibility.

Each player has a deck of 10 cards with 2 copies of each number (from 1 to 5) from which he/she will use one card at random each turn to move his/her pawn on the 30-square circular board, so that all players, with their last card, will return to the initial square, which will simulate the passing of a month in the daily life of the players.
In their turn, in the square where they fall, the players will get money income, unexpected expenses or cards where they will have to make decisions on how to manage their money to get happiness points.
A game consists of 3 complete runs around the board.
At the end of the game, the money a player has will be converted into happiness points.
The player with the most happiness points from those earned during the game and at the end of the game is the winner.


Finnelis es un juego educativo y familiar que pretende ser una primera toma de contacto y una experiencia para niñas y niños sobre todo aquello que representa la gestión monetaria cotidiana de un hogar.
A través de una simulación de las financias familiares, se quiere fomentar un uso razonado de los recursos propios que abarca aspectos como la concienciación, la iniciativa personal y la responsabilidad.

Cada jugador dispone de un mazo de 10 cartas con 2 copias de cada número (del 1 al 5) del cual usará una carta al azar cada turno para mover su peón en el tablero circular de 30 casillas, de manera que todos los jugadores, con su última carta, volverán a situarse en la casilla inicial; la cual cosa va a simular el paso de un mes en la vida cotidiana de los jugadores.
En su turno, en la casilla donde caigan los jugadores obtedrán ingresos de dinero, gastos inesperados o cartas donde deberán tomar decisiones sobre como administrar su dinero para conseguir puntos de felicidad.
Una partida consta de 3 recorridos completos al tablero.
Al final de la partida, el dinero que tenga un jugador se convertirá en puntos de felicidad.
El jugador con más puntos de felicidad entre los obtenidos durante la partida y al final, será el ganador.


Finnelis és un joc educatiu i familiar que pretén ser una primera presa de contacte i una experiència per a nenes i nens sobretot allò que representa la gestió monetària quotidiana d'una llar.
A través d'una simulació de les finances familiars, es vol fomentar un ús raonat dels recursos propis que comprèn aspectes com la conscienciació, la iniciativa personal i la responsabilitat.

Cada jugador disposa d'un mall de 10 cartes amb 2 còpies de cada número (de l'1 al 5) del qual utilitzarà una carta a l'atzar cada torn per moure el peó al tauler circular de 30 caselles, de manera que tots els jugadors, amb la seva última carta, tornaran a situar-se a la casella inicial; la qual cosa simularà el pas d'un mes a la vida quotidiana dels jugadors.
Al seu torn, a la casella on caiguin els jugadors obtindran ingressos de diners, despeses inesperades o cartes on hauran de prendre decisions sobre com administrar els seus diners per aconseguir punts de felicitat.
Una partida consta de 3 recorreguts complets al tauler.
Al final de la partida, els diners que tingui un jugador es convertiran en punts de felicitat.
El jugador amb més punts de felicitat entre els obtinguts durant la partida i al final serà el guanyador.

Player take it in turns to assemble their gang. A gang is made up of 4 members - Muscle, Brains, Vehicle and Weapon. Within the game there are varying strengths of each gang member ranging from 1 to 10.

Once a gang has been assembled it is now time to take on the security. You must pass 2 rounds of security in order to steal a vault. The security may target certain members of your gang or target the combined strength of your gang.

If successful, you may chose a vault to steal. Within each vault there is a varying amount of loot.

The game continues until all vaults have been stolen. The player with the highest value of loot at the end of the game wins!

-description from publisher

An expansion to the auction game RA, Traders adds a new tile type to your experience.

Leverage the Nile River to send vessels around the region to grow your Dynasty through trade and communication. Bring back precious treasures and rare goods to extend your reach and solidify your place among the Gods.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Robot Raiders: Swarm of the Spider Titan
description:
Take control of magical armor known as Robots to plunge into the deadly miasma and fight back the Monsters! You and two other friends can choose to fight the Monsters, or one of your friends can take control of the Monster in a 1v3 PVP epic battle!

Gameplay Description: Robot Raiders features cooperative and solo play for 1-4 players. Players build their decks, manage resources, collect loot and choose upgrades for their robots to battle the monsters, or play as the monster that spreads corruption as you stomp the Raiders into the ground with overwhelming might. The game includes modular robot customization and dynamic battlefield conditions, ensuring a fresh experience every time. In our first All-in-the-Box experience,the Armored Holy Conduit, Magical Combustion Unit, and the Rapidfire Archery Scout face the terrifying Vindra, the Spider Titan. Vindra's minions include the cunning Doran, Spider Cultist and the colossal Gassix, Hatchery Guardian, together they form a trio of deadly bosses for the raiders to overcome or tools for the Monster to annihilate with.

Key Features: Customize your robot with loot, augments, and abilities. Experience over 5000 unique adventures, suitable for both new and advanced players. With multiple difficulty settings everyone from new players all the way up to TCG veterans will find a challenge worth overcoming. Each card features art created by our partners the Little Devil Company, with strictly zero AI generated content

—description from the publisher

A perfect card game to play with friends or family.
It consists of 100 cards, on each of which we find a statement that can be true or false, and the game consists of guessing it.
The curiosities are about the human body, nature, history... They have a very varied and fun theme.
Instructions for the game: You choose who starts by reading a card and who has to decide whether what they hear is true or false. Then the game continues, but reversing the roles.

Speldown is America's newest and most exciting word game. It is also the most versatile and challenging word game ever devised. Any age that can create worjs can enjoy Speldown (10 to 100 years old). Although 2, 3, or 4 people can play Speldown, the solitaire version is probably the most unique and challenging of all. Other than a crossword puzzle, what other word game can you play solitaire? Whatever version you like, you'll find that Speldown is really fun as well as being educational.

Rules (taken directly from the rule booklet):

Each card is uniquely designed with two letters facing in opposite directions with a prominant center dividing line. Each letter has its own point designation plainly visible next to the letter.

Speldown can be played in many ways, but the unique basis of all Speldown variations is to create words in a vertical manner. Words are created by sliding one half of a card under another card thereby hiding one letter and exposing the desired letter.

The playing rack included is added as an aid to hold the cards in place while playing the 2-person and 4-person games. The playing rack has no practical use in playing a 3-player or solitaire game.

In solitaire mode, you must complete eight words vertically, plus, the combined point total of al the letters must be 400 points are more.

In multi-player modes, players take turns drawing cards and building words. Points are scored as each word is created, therefore, when words are extended the letters can score multiple times. 4-player mode utilizes two teams of two players.

—description from back of the box

Du bisch vo Zug is a regional board game designed specifically for fans and residents of the Swiss canton of Zug. The game is a light-hearted and educational journey through the canton, where players explore different landmarks, culture, and traditions unique to Zug. The objective is to collect points by answering trivia questions, completing challenges, or navigating through the local geography represented on the board. The game is suitable for all ages and is especially popular among families and locals who want to test their knowledge of the area. It's an excellent way to foster community spirit and celebrate the local heritage while having fun.

The game typically accommodates 2-6 players and can be completed in about 30 to 60 minutes, making it a quick and engaging option for game nights. Du bisch vo Zug often includes questions or activities that reflect the everyday life and history of the Zug region, making it both an entertaining and informative experience.

boardgame: Dungeon Saga Origins: The Dice Game
description:
The exciting “Push your luck” Dice Game set in a dungeon full of monsters and treasure!

A local fortress, long thought abandoned, has become infested with evil.  You are plucky adventurers, sent by the worried city council to delve deep into the dungeons of an ancient castle to explore its passageways – and clear it of its dark denizens.  Defeat as many as you can, and all the treasure you find is yours to keep.  Who of you can recover the most gold and claim the most glory?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In 1592, Toyotomi Hideyoshi invaded Korea with some 150,000 troops. His objective was conquering Joseon Korea and eventually, Ming China. Within three months, the Japanese captured Hanseong, the capital of Korea, and Pyongyang, completing the occupation of large portions of the Korean Peninsula.

Ming China saw this invasion as a challenge to the Chinese Imperial tributary system and sent troops to Korea. The joint Korean-Chinese offensive at land at sea forced the Japanese army to withdraw from Pyongyang to the south, where the Japanese continued to occupy Hanseong and most of the southern regions. The Ming and Joseon armies tried to advance into the south, but were halted by the Japanese army at the Battle of Byeokjegwan in January 1593.

This game is a simulation of that battle. Published in Command Magazine Japan #178
Bonus Cards 2 Hot Dogs cards expansion.

Tech Deck Legacy is a legacy genre game where players play an Olympiad of five games selected from the family of 17 Tech Deck games. Each game in the series has unique game play and outcomes that affect subsequent games. Each Tech Deck game has variations from the original rules for enhanced play. Look on Boardgamegeek for Tech Deck Series.

OBJECTIVE – Players are trying to win games in the series of Tech Deck games. Players engage in special variants of the original Tech Deck games.
GAME PLAY – Players select a Tech Deck game from the family of 17 Tech Deck games and play. They continue to play four more games in an Olympiad of games.
GAME END AND WINNING –  The sequence ends after five Tech Deck games.

—description from the designer

Unleash your creativity with Smalltown Tales, the storytelling card game where imagination drives the adventure. Set in rural Smalltown, players collaborate to craft epic stories filled with fascinating characters, captivating plots, and vibrant settings.

What makes Smalltown Tales unique?

Each Story card offers expert guidance, helping you and your fellow players shape scenes and weave them into a cohesive narrative. With five card categories focused on characters, plots, settings, and collaboration, this game nurtures your storytelling skills, making every session a journey of shared creativity.

How To Play

Start by reviewing the Player’s Guide to get to know Smalltown's citizens and key locations. Decide if the game will be cooperative or competitive.

Choose a genre and plot structure to guide your story's flow. Then, select a character, their actions, and a setting to create your opening scene. On each turn, develop a new scene that advances the overall narrative.

As you play, collaborate with others, enhancing their plots to earn favor. In the final epilogue round, wrap up your plots and celebrate the imaginative journey you’ve crafted together.

—description from the designer

Shadows Deep is a cooperative strategy board game for 1-4 players, where each player assumes the role of a Vampire striving to defeat the Hunters, manipulate the Citizens, and seize control of the city.

Outwit your archenemies—human and werewolf Hunters—in rounds that feature deck building, combat, feeding, and thematic, emergent gameplay. Strike down your foes and claim the city with your sinister influence.

Set in a 18th century Victorian city, you and up to three other players take the form of Vampires, feeding on unwary citizens, raising Ghouls and enthralling Servants as you engage in a deadly game of cat and mouse with a cast of tenacious Hunters, each with their own unique hunting strategy.

The Vampires will need to work together to maintain control over the city and prevent the Hunters from nurturing enough Faith that the population turns against its Vampire lords. That said, this ruckus presents a rare opportunity to seize control from the other Vampires.

Invest in Vampiric powers of varying value and strength, balancing your efforts to repel the hunters with your own pursuit of power. Carry out forbidden Quests to enhance your own power and exploit the positions and vulnerabilities of other Vampires to gain an edge.

Build your deck to shape your abilities, command your Servants and complete macabre Quests to claim your position as the dominant Vampire of the city.

—description from designer

As an avid explorer of lost civilizations, you make an extraordinary discovery — an ancient shrine dedicated to honoring the radiant majesty of light and its divine sources: the Sun and the Moon. Your discovery, however, might be in jeopardy. Someone else has been following the same clues, and they are not stepping down from claiming the exploration as their own. A celestial spirit, guarding the sanctum, invites the two of you to a pristine challenge known as the Duel of Light in which you will test your skills to determine who is truly worthy of the ancestors' heritage.

Harness the mystical power of light in Shrine: Duel of Light, a game steeped in the mysteries of an ancient culture that features an LED board vital to gameplay. Both players will be dealt a sacred code, and they will take turns placing a translucent, colored piece — an arcane lens in game terms — on the ancient contraption illuminated by light. They compete for each space individually by creating the correct color, sometimes combining multiple lenses to achieve the desired hue, such as placing red on blue to create purple, while yellow combined with red or blue results in orange or green.

Building the largest contiguous area of matching colors, investing your treasure strategically, and knowing when to pass or take on some debt from the spirit will be key to your success in this adventure movie-like gaming experience, further enhanced with the pattern-building module Radiant Shapes, as well as other available expansions.

At the end of the game, players earn treasure for the matching colors in their code, and whoever has the most treasure wins.

-description from publisher

Gnock is a family-friendly card game for players ages 6 and up. The game teaches simple addition and subtraction in the fantastical setting of Gnomerheim, the great city of gnomes.

The game is divided into multiple rounds, with a total score tally kept by collecting Gnomish gems between rounds. Become the next Merchant Lord of Gnomerheim by becoming the first to win 5 Gnome tokens in this fast-moving, round-taking game great for adults and kids alike.

A player ‘Gnocks’ under the table or on the floor to announce they have a low point total of 9 points or below in their hand to win the round. After the player has ‘Gnocked,’ the play goes around the table one more time. When the round returns to the ‘Gnocking’ player, the play stops and cards are added together. The other way to win is by a player announcing a military coup to signify they have 50 points in their hand through a combination of 5 Military cards to stop the round immediately and win the round.

But beware! You may activate an instant action when certain cards are played! Will the rules change? Will you have to give your best Gnome dance? Will you have the swap cards with other players? Will a Troll attack?!

—description from the publisher

The second game in the 18 card Rescue game series:

Otters to the Rescue is a game of 18 cards full of clever combinations for scoring and play for 1 or 2 players. Players arrange the cards in the shape of a triangle and each turn will rescue one of the cards from the triangle which represents the ocean. Each card features a swimmer in one of five colors, one of six fish, and one of three tools. The unused cards each game are used for the scoring area which affects both players.

In this game of Nautical rescue otters, players will have to cleverly select a card each turn that helps their chances of winning or keeps their opponent from scoring. Additionally, the turn order changes depending on the time value of the card selected. Sometimes a player will want the higher time value as it has a color swimmer or tool they need, but doing so opens them up to going second on the following round if their opponent takes a lower time value. As each card is rescued from the mountain, the selection of cards shrinks. Taking certain cards opens up other cards which may help an opponent or it may hinder their path. Each choice of card presents a path back to shore.

Otters to the Rescue is played over 3 rounds and presents over 800 different scoring combinations! Players will find plenty of fun in this small card game that packs a lot of strategy.

Otters to the Rescue can be combined with Safe and Hound to play up to 4 players!

-description from designer

A Pass and Write game!

Star light, Star Bright, First star I see tonight, let it be part of a constellation!

In Stargazers, you're all trying to draw the constellations you see in the night sky, but naturally each of you sees something different.  In this play-and-pass game, you'll spot stars, then connect them with lines to make constellations. Each star can only be used in one constellation, so be fast to ensure your constellation uses the stars first! It's a mix of planning ahead and thinking in the moment.

Players will place 1 Star then take an action which includes drawing a Constellation card for scoring, drawing another Star, or protecting a Star they have previously drawn on the sheet. After the player finishes their turn, they pass their sheet to the left. This mechanism really shines with trying to maximize positions for yourself while also minimizing beneficial Stars for your opponent.  Players will find plenty of strategy as they search for the right constellations to match the sheets as they have been passed.

-description from designer

“It’s alive?! Oh Igor, please let it be true this time!”

Roll and Reanimate taps into that special story of life, the flower bloom, the trees sprout leaves, the birds sing, and the dead shall rise! Bring your own monster back from the beyond with a series of clever dice rolls.

Each turn the player will roll dice and hope to score as many as possible to attach and link of the various body parts their Igor has scavenged. Where are these from again? I guess it doesn’t matter. The important bit is making sure your monster has it all together before you throw the switch and send 10,000 volts jolting through it to bring it back to the land of the living! 
 Your machinations have not gone unnoticed by those nosey neighbors. In fact, they’re lighting torches and grabbing pitchforks. You’d better hurry up and get your project moving.

-description from designer

The goal of the game is to get back to the starting space with as few penalty points as possible. If a player collects more than 35 points he is eliminated from the game.
On a player's turn, he may leave the gearshift lever in its position or shift it up or down a gear. They he moves the speed selector on the right of the dashboard one space to the right: the new color that appears indicates the speed on the speedometer. For example: green in third gear is 80 km/h. 10 km/h corresponds to one space, so you can move 8 spaces forward.
On multi-lane roads, you can overtake opponents, but only one car can be on a space at a time. On a single-lane road, you must stay on the space behind the car in front of you. An opponent is overtaken by moving past him sideways. The solid red line must not be crossed under any circumstances!
If you drive to fast you get penalty points. On some Spaces you have to draw event cards.

boardgame: Super Things: Rivals of Kaboom – Juego de cartas
description:
The battle for the Kazzom has started! Fight the villains, capture them anv rescue the precious source of energy from their evil hands.
The villains have captured the Kazoom and they have brought it to their HQ. Players must capture the villains in order to free the Kazoom.
Every player has a SuperThing to play with, and some cards. In their turn, every player can attack, recharge (get new cards), use equipment special cards (to prepare future attacks) or use the superpower of the SupetThing.
If the player attacks, other players can help by adding cards from their hand to ensure the villain is defeated; in this case, the one who plays the more cards with the higher value gets the villain.
A player that has defeated at least 3 villains can try to rescue the Kazoom, playing two attack cards of each type. The other players can not participate in this final move.
The player who gets the Kazoom is the winner.

Wargame rules for the American daylight bombing offensive against Germany 1943-1944.  Flights of B-17 bombers press through enemy defenses on their way to the target, protected only by limited fighter escort and their own "combat box" defensive formations.  Multiple combat boxes would fly in support of each other, with the lowest and last in line being the most vulnerable, sometime earning the nickname "Purple Heart Valley".

Games are played in turns where both attacking and defending fighter aircraft pre-plot a maneuver for the turn from lists tailored to the attitude the aircraft ended its last maneuver in; either climbing, diving, or level.  Maneuvers are then resolved simultaneously across two phases where aircraft may move and/or change altitude in either or both phases depending on the chosen maneuver.  At the end of each phase is an opportunity to engage enemy aircraft that are within range and arc of fire of your weapons.  An example of a maneuver is D1 (or Dive 1).  This maneuver is executed by decreasing one altitude level in the first phase (which might bring the aircraft into or out of range of an opponent for the following attack opportunity), followed by a move of one hex directly forward in the second phase (again perhaps altering the possibility of attacks in the second attack opportunity).  There is also a 1D maneuver, which is the reverse; move in the first phase and dive in the second.  B-17's themselves do not move except for a very limited set of allowed maneuvers to try and rejoin formation should they fall out because of damage sustained.  The majority of the actions taken by B-17's is firing their bristling gun positions at incoming enemy fighters!

Weapon attacks by all aircraft are resolved by rolling a D6 on a table with to-hit target numbers that depend on the type of aircraft being targeted (fighter or bomber), the range to it in hexes or altitude levels (max 3 in both cases), and the general orientation of the attack (head-on, astern, or all others).  A very small number of parameters might modify this dice roll either up or down and fighters can choose the length of burst they are firing from their limited store of ammunition.  Successful hits require the target to roll percentile dice on damage tables that are specific to either fighters or bombers, and to the orientation of the attack.  Damage to the hit aircraft is marked on an aircraft record sheet, but only what the attacker could "see" is reported to them, providing a nice little bit of fog of war.  For example, a fighter attacking a bomber would not know if a particular crew member was killed in the attack, but would see if bits of the bomber were blown off, an engine started to smoke, or no apparent effect was caused.

Games can be played as one-off engagements between bombers, escorts, and attackers, or as a campaign which simulates an entire bombing mission from take-off to target.  This is done by taking off in England and then flying through up to 5 abstract zones (depending on the length of game being played) that start at the French coast, cross Western Europe, and end over Berlin.  Each zone going deeper into enemy territory is limited in the type of escort fighters that can accompany the bombers.  When playing missions in 1943 the last zones might be unescorted since long range Mustangs have not yet entered service.  The Luftwaffe player(s) assigns their available fighters before the campaign is begun to any of the zones in play.  Starting with Zone 1, when the bombers (and any escorts) reach the zone an engagement is played out if it is defended by enemy fighters.  If it is not, a light flak attack is resolved against the bombers and then they are moved to the next zone.  Upon reaching the final zone, which is the bombing target, either an engagement is played out if it is defended, or a light flak attack if it is not, and then another flak attack is resolved at a randomly determined intensity depending on the depth of the zone.  The deeper the zone, the more likely the flak will be heavy!  Then any surviving bombers make an abstract bombing attack which will add a random number of victory points to the USAAF total.  Additional victory points are scored for each surviving bomber, scaled to how deep a zone the raid was conducted in; the deeper the zone, the more victory points earned.  The Luftwaffe player(s) scores victory points for each enemy bomber and escort fighter destroyed, minus points for each friendly pilot lost.

一刀斋 (Ittōsai) is an expansion pack of 一决胜负 (Showdown).
It introduces a new character who wields the 一刀流 (Ittō-ryū or "one-sword school"): Ittōsai.

Components


     1 Character Card

     1 HP Card + Balance Card
     1 Character Token

Expansion pack for Showdown 2, featuring a new character: Zorro, the Rogue Swordsman.

Components


     Character Card: 1
     Bonus Card: 1
     Skill Cards: 35
     HP Card: 1
     Balance Cards: 1
     Character Token: 1

A small force of the Continental Army under the command of Morgan was marching through South Carolina in order to forage for supplies and raise the morale of local colonial sympathizers. As he considered Morgan's army a threat to his left flank, British General Cornwallis dispatched cavalry under commander Tarleton to defeat Morgan. Tarleton, bolstered by British reinforcements, set off in hot pursuit of the American detachment.  Morgan resolved to make a stand at Hannah’s Cowpens, a position on a low hill in open woodland, with the expectation that the aggressive Tarleton would attack headlong. On January 17, 1781, Morgan deployed his army in three main lines. Tarleton's army, after an exhausting march reached the field and attacked immediately; however, the American defense-in-depth absorbed the British attack. The British lines lost their cohesion as they hurried after the retreating Americans. When Morgan's army went on the offensive, it wholly overwhelmed the British in the only double envelopment of the war, nearly eliminating Tarleton's force.

Game turn: 20 min.
    Units: regiments and battalions
    Map: one hex. = 40 m
    Complexity: 5/10
    Solitaire: ​​8/10
    Playing time: 2 hours

VaeVictis #176

boardgame: 地主来了: 大团圆 (Landlord's Coming: Grand Reunion)
description:
地主来了-大团圆 (Landlord's Coming: Grand Reunion) is a competitive point-based board game for 1-5 players. Players take on the roles of either landlords or farmers. The Landlord has resource advantages and stronger skills, while farmers must collaborate strategically to defeat the landlord.

The game involves harvesting crops, using tactics, and employing tools to synthesize more agricultural products and earn points, ultimately competing for victory.

New Features

As the PLUS version of "Landlord's Coming - Bumper Harvest", it introduces new characters, tools, strategies, and plants, significantly enhancing the game's depth and enjoyment. The cards proportions have been optimized, with some older cards removed or adjusted, resulting in a smoother gameplay experience.
