boardgame: In Harm's Way: Naval Battles of the Dutch East Indies, 1942
description:
“I wish to sail in harm’s way…”
--John Paul Jones

In Harm’s Way portrays four famous naval battles fought in early months of WWII for control of the Dutch East Indies (Badung Strait, Balikpapan, Sunda Strait and Java Sea). Although severely out numbered by the Japanese, the American, British, Dutch and Australian (ABDA) naval units fought valiantly, earning their places in naval history. Players will be challenged to do as well, or better, than their historical counter parts.

Each copy of In Harm’s Way is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch map with game record keeping tracks
•    170 unmounted, double-sided units
•    Four sets of rules (one for each game, each of 5 or 6 pages)

Naval units represent one vessel, and hex side and turn length varies for each of the four games. Player's use a standard deck of playing cards (a custom card set is available from the publisher) to activate units and six-sided die to resolve combats. The game is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh and features graphics by Bruce Yearian.

-description from designer

Go back to the beginning with Pokémon Trading Card Game Classic! This timeless collection includes three 60-card decks featuring the original first partner Pokémon inspired by the Pokémon TCG Base Set and quintessential cards from throughout the game’s long history, all as foil cards and ready to play right out of the box. In addition to the vintage favorites, six new cards—including Suicune ex, Lugia ex, and Ho-Oh ex—make their debut. Enhance your experience with high-quality gameplay accessories like a foldable two-player game board, stackable damage counter cones, and a toolbox case that keeps everything conveniently stored for your next battle.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Mashup of Quickshot (Kanai Factory / Arclight Games) and Gun and Gun (Keepdry)

Gameplay is based on a mix of the very short 4-round game Quickshot, and the one-on-one gun-themed duel game Gun and Gun.

Where in quickshot you have a single hand of random cards from which you choose one to play per round with four rounds in total, in this game you have two guns per player which each have their own set hand of four, and the order of each hand is random and fixed. The players choose which gun to flip a card for, and the game continues until one player has won four rounds.

In this combination of the two games, you have to look at your opponents’ two guns and abilities and guess which gun they will use and guess which of the remaining cards for that gun would come up, and determine which gun you should play to maximize your chance of winning each round.

This set can also be used to play the original Quickshot if you use only the bullet cards from 1-8 and apply the rules of the original, as the bullet cards have the same effects as the attack cards in the original (though rules for the original are not included).

The box also contains a promotional gunner card (variant of Hibana) for the original Gun and Gun.

boardgame: Middara: Bounty Pack – Fury of Nature
description:
Middara: Bounty Pack – Fury of Nature contains a Bounty Book, a new Boss Monster, and an Item card. This Bounty Pack expansion will require Act 1 to play.

PERFECTION THROUGH ADVERSITY
Bounty Packs expand the Adventure Mode with insanely hard and uniquely diverse side quests. These Side Quests can be attempted at lower levels than the intended difficulty recommends for an even hard challenge.

Each Bounty Pack adds a side quest that can be attempted during specific moments of the Adventure Mode. The Bounties of Elenia line of Bounty Packs represent all of the horrible monstrosities that threaten the realms of Elenia.

Each Bounty Pack contains a plethora of new content. New tokens, a new Bounty Book, new miniatures, new tiles that expand old sets, new unique loot, new upgrades, as well as optional content to change the possible combatants that might spawn in the Unintentional Malum Core Game and Adventure Expansions.

Each round, one player draws a card which determines which Axolotl is theirs for the round. The other players take turns drawing two questions and choosing which one to ask. At any point, a player can point to the the Axolotl they think is the clue giver's. If they're right, they score the card, if they're wrong, they have to hand one of their previously scored cards to the player with the least Axolotls in their score pile.

In Babylon Rise 1-4 players will compete to make most magnificent and most majestic tower of ancient wonder. They will employ their master workers and ships to gather precious resources and build various buildings to speed up their work. In the end, whoever scores most points with his tower will be the winner of the contest.

Babylon Rise is a roll & write game for 1-4 players, that can be played in 30-45 min. It uses specific action selection system and players are mostly drafting dice instead of rolling them.

Objective of the game is to have most points at the end. Babylon Rise can also be played solo.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Blitzkrieg, a battle royal solitaire game, best entertainment of the 21 century, 2029! Your goal is to survive 6 arenas, going from one to another, traversing a large map of the unnamed city ruins and be the one who made the most eliminations and scored the most points. This is a solitaire card game

compacted in one deck of 54 cards that can be played under 1 h.

GAME OBJECTIVE and GAME DIFICULTY

To win the game you must score enough points and survive all 6 Arenas. If you are playing an easy game you must score at least 70 points and you can start the game with one weapon. Medium game difficulty demands that you score at least 80 points and you may choose to start with one weapon. Hard game is only for the veterans of this sport, you must score at least 90 points and if you are true warrior you will not take one weapon at the beginning of the game.

GAME SETUP

To setup the game, shuffle 48 Blitzkrieg cards and place them face down in the center of your play area, use space to the right of the deck as your discard

pile. Then draw 3 cards from the top of the Blitzkrieg deck, keeping them face down and unrevealed, and place them somewhere near as these cards will represent your ’lives’. Therefore, you start with 3 lives for the whole game and you can’t increase that number beyond 3 cards.

After that draw one card from the Blitzkrieg deck into your hand as your starting weapon. If you prefer more realistic Battle royal experience, you may skip this part and start with no weapons at all! We recommend this only if you are veteran of the sport. Next thing you need to do is to shuffle Arena deck and draw 3 cards from it, you need to choose your ‘drop point’. Basically, out of 3 cards you drew, you can

ARENA CARDS (6 of them)

BLITZKRIEG CARDS (48 of them)

POISON GAS CARD (only one)

Choose your starting Arena. After picking one card to keep, shuffle other two back into the Arena deck. Place your starting Arena card above the Blitzkrieg deck and set the Poison gas on the left side of the chosen Arena card. Keep the Arena deck close by as you’ll need it later.

GAME TURN and ACTIONS

Each turn in Blitzkrieg starts by drawing 3 cards from the Blitzkrieg deck face down on the table. Then, you can always flip one card face up and choose one out of three actions: ENGAGE, LOOT or MOVE. You can’t do the same action twice (unless some card effects allows it) and you must do and resolve all three actions during your turn. After you finish your actions, you will go to the next turn, again drawing 3 cards and continue with your actions until you either deplete the deck or have to move to next Arena.

MOVE ACTION
After flipping one of the cards from your queue, you my decide to resolve card event by using a MOVE action. If you do, first resolve the text, then discard the card you performed action on. Events can give you additional actions, shuffle cards from the discard pile back into the Blitzkrieg deck and other way around or end your fight in the current arena and give you an option to move to the next one. After resolving the card event, check if the card hasPoison Gas icon, if it has you will have to move the Poison Gas card.

LOOT ACTION
Always remember that you can flip one of the cards from your queue before resolving an action. In case of looting, you add that card to your had as your weapon.You can’t carry more than 4 weapons (unless some Arena effects change that rule) and you can carry duplicates of the same weapon.

ENGAGE ACTION
After flipping one of the cards from your queue, you my decide to engage the target. First, choose which weapon from your arsenal you want to use. All targets have Strength and Range on which you’re engaging them. To kill the target you need to deal damage with your weapon that is equal or higher then their Strength. As every weapon has different damage depending on the range you’re firing at, split into three brackets x/y/z, which represents how much damage that weapon deals on CR, MR and LR (Close Range, Middle Range and Long Range), you want to use the weapon that can deal the most damage to a target on that range.
Example:
If you’re engaging a target with Strength 5 in MR range with a Pistol that has 3/3/1 damage, you are using a middle number (3) against its 5 strength!

To resolve the engagement, you always draw a card from the Blitzkrieg deck and check the Bonus Damage numbers. You add the bonus depending on the range on which you use the weapon and that represents your final damage that is dealt to the target. If you kill the target, add it to your set (you can keep the track of your killed and eliminated targets somewhere on the table).

WHAT IS ELIMINATION and HOW TO SCORE POINTS

If player manages to kill the same target twice (he adds a second card of the same target into his set) that is called an ELIMINATION. After he moves to another Arena, he can choose which one of two target cards to keep as his points. Points are the Strength of the card. If he manages to kill the same target multiple times during a fight in one Arena, he can choose to keep more cards thus gaining more points. One killed card must always be returned to the Blitzkrieg deck after player moves to next Arena. This means that if he manages to kill same target three times, he can keep 2 cards and return one, and if he manages to kill same target 4 times, he can keep 3 cards and return 1. You can’t take all 4 cards of the same target.In any case, there are 10 contenders in this Battle Royal and each of them has 4 cards of his/her type. Contenders are listed by their nicknames and they are:
Bravo, Doctor, Goliath, Natasha, Noir, Pink, Ranger, Skull, Snake and ZR99.

GAME END

If you lose all 3 lives you are eliminated and you lose the game. Game also ends when you go through all 6 Arenas. Then you need to score all your points from the Eliminations and see did you manage to win (depends on a difficulty level you chose).

The large game collection contains 9 varied game ideas about the popular unicorn and her friends. Dice race, glitter dominoes, unicorn Mau Mau, crystal balancing and much more. Boredom? None! A magical gaming experience for unicorn fans big and small. With simple rules, magical illustrations and high-quality game material.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Deeds of Daring: Individual Combat During the French and Indian War (1754 – 1763)
description:
Contains rules, army lists, and seven scenarios. Miniature rules for the black powder era using individual mounted figures. Each player commands 3-4 figures with a 6-player game using only 9-12 figures per side. A 6-player game will be decided within 3-4 hours. Quick, fun, and bloody like a wargame should be.

—description from the publisher's website

With the Expansion Pack, apply new SKILLS, avoid extra TROUBLE, and have more LUCK, on 2 unique 18 HOLE courses.

The SUBURBAN course features ROADS, ROOFS, YARDS, and CLUMPS. The FOREST course features TIGHT LINES, SLOPES, CLUSTERS, and PONDS. The ACTION cards are specific for each course. The SKILLS, DEVIATIONS, CALLS, and LUCK on the SUBURBAN course are based on its SKIPPING nature. Meanwhile, the ACTION cards in the FOREST are based more on the ROLLING aspects of the course.

Another category of DISC is introduced: FAIRWAY. The DISCS are Backhand and Forehand and tend to travel as far as MID-RANGE DISCS, but they are more understable than most DISCS. Some FAIRWAY can also fly diagonally, which helps to bypass obstacles like FORESTS, CLUSTERS, or CLUMPS. Additionally, all FAIRWAY DISCS in the EXPANSION PACK are resistant to the SKIPPING of the SUBURBAN course or the ROLLING of the FOREST course.

Also included are 4 double-sided team CHARACTER cards along with matching MEEPLES. At the start of a round, you can choose a CHARACTER and a card side with varying abilities. Each CHARACTER has 2 abilities, one of which can be used once per 9 HOLES.

—description from the publisher

Card Sharks is a crazy, fast-paced, real time game where each player uses their 3 shark cards to eat the tastiest and highest scoring fish represented by dice based on a series of randomly drawn scoring condition cards.

Each player throws dice into the middle of the table and draws a Scoring Condition Card which shows what captured/eaten dice combinations score points for that game turn, like for example 3 of the same colour or 3 of the same value etc.

Each player uses one shark card held from the tail-end to slide the shark mouth under a die in order to catch/eat it. Once the first player gets three dice captured, they shout "Chomp" and all the other players need to complete their 3 die combinations on their shark cards then you proceed to scoring. The first player to hit 20 pits triggers the end turn and the highest player above 20 points wins.

Let the feeding frenzy begin!

—description from the publihser

boardgame: Submultiple Game (腦王爭霸之超級因數)
description:
This game is developed and derived from the popular TV reality show  (Super Brain) in Mainland China. It is published under the product series named 燃燒吧大腦 (Burn Brain).

Players are dealt with a sequence of 6 numbers hidden from other players. They race to outguess the numbers of other players by using clues of prime factors and multiples.

—user summary

超級因數
抽取六張卡牌按從小到大暗置排列，每輪宣言一個質因數，所有含有該質因數的卡牌面前放上對應數字Token，根據質因數提示猜卡牌數字，數字被猜完的玩家出局，最後只有一位玩家會獲得腦王稱號！
腦王爭霸戰等待你的挑戰！

—description from the back of the box

Buy, Sell, and Trade your favorite Kings Island rides, restaurants, and attractions as you grow your very own amusement park empire with KINGS ISLAND-OPOLY!

-description from publisher's website
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Tikal Legend is a game of exploration, placement, and majority in which the players explore the Central American jungles and search for lost temples and treasures.

As in the award-winning Tikal, your ultimate goal is to score the most prestige points during the in-game scoring phases, but the toolbox to do so in Tikal Legend becomes way wider!

In addition to exploring the jungle, controlling temples, and collecting treasures, you get to pick and perform actions in the order of your choosing — and spending your action points is only one of the available options! Claim different terrains, benefit from card effects, manipulate the map, recruit additional expedition members, and do so much more — all to excavate a whole new level of tactical and strategic planning in Tikal Legend!

				
				
					Note:Two different versions of the game (standard and deluxe with upgrade components) will be available in both English and French. With either version, you'll get everything to play the base game of Tikal, a whole new game of Tikal Legend, and a new solo mode developed by John Albertson, Aleksandr Saranac, and Jeremy Avery, with Dávid Turczi. It is NOT possible to play Tikal Legend with only a copy of Tikal.

Plunderlust is a versatile living card game for one or more players about tormented raiders scraping by in the dying universe! Take command of your crew to plunder derelicts solo or face off against a friend with a deck for each player.

Gameplay in Plunderlust is RPG-inspired, using a set of polyhedral dice to make Attack, Damage, and Armor rolls. Each turn, you'll make risky decisions about whether to fight Monsters or flee from them, which dangerous Location to explore, and what Crew and Equipment to buy from your hand with your limited Creds. Kill a powerful Boss Monster to win the game before you suffer seven Torments and your universe ends!

In solo play you can delve into a pre-made adventure or build a custom deck to tell your own story. In versus play, you'll choose how you explore, but your opponent will torment you with monsters all the while.

Requires: 1 Deck per player, Polyhedral dice, Dice or Tokens for tracking HP, Volts, and Infection

Revenants Deck Contents
8 Revenants Scum cards
10 Merc cards
12 Monster cards
15 Location cards
11 Equipment cards
4 Favor cards
3 Reference cards

boardgame: Jujutsu Kaisen: Fléaux! Le jeu de cartes
description:
In this 50-card game, you will find all your favorite Jujutsu Kaisen characters!
It's up to you to get rid of your cards as quickly as possible by placing them according to their values and family. Collect your bonuses to expand your territory, but watch out for Sukuna's fingers that will block your movements.
Will you succeed in your mission before the end of the game?

Dans ce jeu de 50 cartes, retrouve tous tes personnages préférés de Jujutsu Kaisen ! À toi de te débarrasser le plus rapidement de tes cartes en les posant selon leur valeur et leur famille. Récupère tes bonus pour étendre ton territoire, mais attention aux doigts de Sukuna qui bloqueront tes mouvements.
Réussiras-tu ta mission avant la fin de la partie ?

5 additional roles to mix up your game nights. The Shadow mimics other roles. The Crafter adds house rules. The blank card can be anything!


     Crafter
     Klutz
     Maestro
     Shadow
     Blank (with Muscle art)

A beer-themed Monopoly (or Matador, since it is Danish) derivative. You roll two dice to move around, buying properties and avoiding bankruptcy.
Some spots on the board have special effects (for instance "only roll one die until you pass GO"), while others will let you draw a "Destiny" card.
You win either in the ordinary way (by being the only player not gone bankrupt) or by fulfilling three mission goals.

The last 'Gold Rush' in the lower 48 states began outside of Goldfield, Nevada in December, 1902.  By 1904 it was a boom, and by 1907 was becoming the largest community in Nevada.  Miners arrived in the area and began looking for signs of gold veins.  Once found, the miners would stake a claim, submit a Certificate of Location, give the claim a name, and begin digging for ore.

Stories of this boomtown as seen in newspapers, legal documents, and the local cemetery, include claim jumpers, joint ventures, disease, gunfights, miner strikes, gambling halls, and a red-light district.  Some claims strike it rich, while others run dry soon after discovery.

Goldfield 1907 is a game that brings all this history to life.  You will be the miner dealing with every day 1907 challenges.  Through a bit of strategy and luck, you may become rich in this historically accurate board game!

—description from the designer

BISTRO is a bluffing game for 3 to 6 players aged 8+, in which everyone gets together at the local bistro to share good humor and conviviality... while trying to avoid accumulating glasses, or they will have to pay the bill!

Each round, someone offers a drink card - possibly accompanied by something to nibble on - to a friend. This one now chooses whether or not to accept toasting... unless he prefers to pass it to a third mate or try to guess the true nature of the offered glass?

In all cases, the player who is wrong places the glass card in front of him and immediately starts a new round. The round ends as soon as a player accumulates 3 identical glasses or at least 1 glass of each type.

This player then draws an addition token at random: if he now has 3 or more addition symbols in front of him, he loses the game, which ends immediately. All the other players win!

—description from the publisher

For centuries, the Camino de Santiago (St. Jacques’ Way) has brought together pilgrims from all over the world who, for different reasons, decide to undertake the journey year after year. Get a better social, gastronomic and travelling experience than the other players and you will win the game.

Objective of the game
To complete the last 5 stages of the Camino, which are compulsory to obtain the Compostela, and to arrive in Santiago as the player who has accumulated the most experiences in terms of encounters, gastronomy and emblematic places along the Camino.
Whoever manages to collect the most cards of different experiences and completes his or her Compostela will have the most points and win the game.

The Catapult Feud Holiday Ammo Pack includes both the Tree (Tannenbaum) and Secret Santa ammo.

2 x Tree Ammo
2 x Gift Ammo

Tree Ammo Rules:
During setup each player places one Tree on the highest viable place in their castle. If/when the Tree falls or is knocked off, the player whose tree fell gets to fire it at the attacking opponent in retribution for such an unceremonious felling of the festive tree. When firing it at your opponent, players are encouraged to yell "Tannenbaum!!!".

Secret Santa Rules:
Each player gets one Secret Santa gift ammo. During your turn, instead of launching a boulder, you may launch the gift instead! In addition to any damage, if the ammo lands inside your opponent's castle, the closest troop picks it up and opens the gift. They are so moved by the kindness of the gesture that they decide to switch teams! Remove that troop from the enemy's castle, get a troop matching your color from the box and place them following the Troop Placement rules in your castle!

Build your street with adorable animals numbered 1 to 7! But neighbors have to be different…the same animal can’t be neighbors, and they can’t share the same hoodie either. Create a full street of animals to win! NeighborHoodies card game for kids can be played by kids ages 5 and up.

—description form the publisher
Five new smart10 cards with 10 new questions about board games. These promos are released by SD Games and can be obtained from the publisher in fairs and conventions.
Five new smart10 cards with 10 new questions about videogames. These promos are released by SD Games and can be obtained from the publisher in fairs and conventions.
A set of 6 new questions for very young kids created by Playfunlearning. It's a promo to show how teachers can use Smart10 during their school time.

At the beginning of the game, each player will create their own adventurer card, choosing their name and giving out a few customization points.

Then, go out into the world to live adventures! The player takes the first card, reads its description and starts discovering.

The game cards hide elements that you can interact with, identified by a number. These are characters you can talk to, places you can visit or objects you can collect. When you decide to activate one of them, you just have to look for the card identified with that number and read its description.

On each player's turn, they will also take a random event card, which will present situations that we will encounter on our travels. These can be strokes of luck, enemies trying to stop us and other possibilities.

Sometimes we will have to check if our explorer is able to overcome a fight or another obstacle in his path. To do this, we will roll the dice and add the result to one of the attributes of our card. If we match or exceed the value that the card asked for, we will have succeeded! Otherwise, we will suffer the setback that the test marks us.

You'll also be able to upgrade your adventurer, collect items, and become stronger to face challenges.

Players will have to work together to find the legendary Ophiuchus armor!


Al principio del juego cada jugador creará su propia ficha de aventurero eligiendo su nombre, repartiendo unos pocos puntos de personalización.

A continuación ¡sal al mundo a vivir aventuras! El jugador toma la primera carta, lee su descripción y comienza a descubrir.

Las cartas de juego esconden elementos con los que puedes interactuar identificados con un número. Son personajes con los que puedes hablar, lugares que visitar u objetos que recoger. Cuando decides activar uno de ellos, solo tienes que buscar la carta identificada con ese número y leer su descripción.

En el turno de cada jugador tomará también una carta de evento aleatoria, que presentará situaciones que nos cruzaremos en nuestros viajes, pueden ser golpes de suerte, enemigos que intentan detenernos y otras posibilidades.

A veces habrá que comprobar si nuestro explorador es capaz de superar un combate u otro obstáculo en su camino. Para ello, lanzaremos el dado y sumaremos al resultado a uno de los atributos de nuestra ficha. Si igualamos o superamos el valor que nos pedía la carta ¡habremos tenido éxito! de lo contrario, sufriremos el revés que nos marque la prueba.

También podrás mejorar tu aventurero, conseguir objetos y hacerte más fuerte para enfrentarte a los retos.

Los jugadores tendrán que colaborar para encontrar ¡la legendaria armadura de Ofiuco!

boardgame: Jim Henson's Labyrinth: The Board Game – Humongous! Expansion
description:
This expansion allows you to play a mini-game when you encounter Humongous in the Core Game.

Use your wit to remove the helmet and your brawn to defeat the giant!

This expansion, which is exclusive to the 40th Anniversary Collection campaign on Game found, also comes with:

     the Jim Henson's Labyrinth the Board Game: Exclusive Collector's Box to hold your entire collection,
     Jim Henson's Labyrinth the Board Game: Revised Rulebook
     All Unlocked Exclusives from the campaign: Jareth and Sarah ballroom miniature, Junk Lady miniature, Wiseman miniature, crystal ball Willpower tokens, upgraded thicker character boards, Firey #5 miniature, extra dice set, Tobey miniature, and Jareth on his throne miniature​.


The main expansion is of course the Humongous expansion, featuring a giant miniature, a game board and a set of cards that allow you to play a very strategic and yet fun mini-game, either on its own, or integrated with your games of Labyrinth the boardgame. 
 Then, the next most exciting new expansion we think is the ‘Play as the Worm’ expansion, which adds a new character/player sheet to the game. Now you can play as the Worm, which has a very peculiar set of rules – a very different profile from the other main characters… as you can imagine he does not excel in Brawn or Speed, but his knowledge of the Labyrinth (and therefore his Wit) is without peer! And comes with the customary two cards for his Strength (‘Cup of Tea?) and his Weakness (‘I’m just a Worm!).
 The last two expansions are the Trash Lady and the Wiseman. These follow the same principle and format as the Goblins! and Fireys! expansions, providing new rules that you can use alongside the new miniature to modify the normal boardgame.
 The Wiseman is more static, he remains where he is drawn, and is potentially a source of helpful information that will boost your Willpower… but there is a risk that he will just waste your time with infuriatingly useless gibberish!
 The Junk Lady is more dangerous, she will challenge you as normal when drawn, but if you manage to resist her powers, every turn there is a chance that she will chase Sarah around the board to try again to sway her from her mission… watch out!
 Combined with the other expansions and the optional rules in the rulebook, these new characters allow you to enrich your experience of the Labyrinth boardgame and adapt it to your taste and level of experience. Have fun!
family: Series: Jim Henson's Board Game Collection (River Horse)

boardgame: Heroscape: Rising Tide – Knight Primus Adelbern and Ordo Borealis Army Expansion
description:
Heroscape: Rising Tide - Knight Primus Adelbern and Ordo Borealis Army Expansion includes four new, highly detailed, and fully assembled unpainted (or Painted if you get the Premium Painted) Heroscape figures: Knight Primus Adelbern and 3 members of the Ordo Borealis Squad.

These ursine fighters are a formidable addition to any army, especially when paired with Knight Irene and the Frostclaw Paladins from the Heroscape: Age of Annihilation Master Set.

Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win! This Army Expansion is fully compatible with all previous and upcoming Heroscape content.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Rising Tide – Sonlen and Halushia, Scion of the Wild Army Expansion
description:
Heroscape: Rising Tide - Sonlen and Halushia, Scion of the Wild Army Expansion includes four new, highly detailed, and fully assembled unpainted (or Painted if you get the Premium Painted) Heroscape figures: Sonlen and Halushia, Scion of the Wild.

These figures bring a unique blend of mystical abilities and primal fury to your Heroscape battlefield. Together, they embody the balance between nature's nurturing spirit and its untamed ferocity, making them powerful allies or fearsome adversaries.

Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win! This Army Expansion is fully compatible with all previous and upcoming Heroscape content.

—description from the publisher

They’re at it again! The cat and mouse are chasing each other around Mrs. Olson’s cottage, knocking over lamps and tearing up the old sofa with reckless abandon. Stay out of their way or you’ll end up in a whole mess of trouble! 
 Scratch and Scramble is a trick-taking game where you’ll have to cleverly play your tokens to win the most tricks while avoiding getting stuck with the cat and mouse. Win tricks by playing the highest-numbered token of the lead suit. However, with every numbered token played, the cat and mouse move clockwise around the table, passing from player to player. Push your luck too far and you may end up stuck with the cat and mouse together, which will end the round and turn all the tricks you’ve won into worthless broken lamps. Take the most tricks without too many broken lamps to win the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heroscape: Rising Tide – Hellforge Mandukor Army Expansion
description:
Heroscape: Rising Tide - Hellforge Mandukor Army Expansion includes one new, highly detailed, and fully assembled unpainted (or Painted if you get the Premium Painted) Heroscape figure: The imposing Hellforge Mandukor

Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win!

It is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions.

As part of the Rising Tide story arc, Hellforge Mandukor introduces a unique combination of raw power and tactical versatility, making it a formidable addition to any army.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Rising Tide – Queen Maladrix and Festering Honor Guard Army Expansion
description:
Heroscape: Rising Tide - Queen Maladrix and Festering Honor Guard Army Expansion includes new, highly detailed, and fully assembled unpainted (or Painted if you get the Premium Painted) Heroscape figures: Queen Maladrix and the Festering honor Guard Squad.

These units are perfect for players who prefer aggressive, relentless tactics, leveraging the strength of their leader and the overwhelming numbers of her minions.

Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win! This Army Expansion is fully compatible with all previous and upcoming Heroscape content.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Rising Tide – Oathbound Phalanx and Oathbound Legionnaires Army Expansion
description:
Heroscape: Rising Tide - Oathbound Phalanx and Oathbound Legionnaires Army Expansion includes 5 new, highly detailed, and fully assembled unpainted (or Painted if you get the Premium Painted) Heroscape figures: Oathbound Phalanx and Oathbound Legionnaires

The Oathbound Phalanx and Oathbound Legionnaires, elite units who excel at adaptive battlefield tactics and overwhelming force. Together, they bring a unique combination of mobility, versatility, and devastating power to your Heroscape army.

Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win! This Army Expansion is fully compatible with all previous and upcoming Heroscape content.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Rising Tide – Wing Commander Tuck Harrigan and Vorid Glide Strikers Army Expansion
description:
Heroscape: Rising Tide - Vorid Glide Strikers and Wing Commander Tuck Harrigan includes five new, highly detailed, and fully assembled unpainted (or Painted if you get the Premium Painted) Heroscape figures: Vorid Glide Strikers Squad and Wing Commander Tuck Harrigan.

These figures specialize in high-mobility tactics, providing players with unmatched flexibility and striking power from the skies.

Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win! This Army Expansion is fully compatible with all previous and upcoming Heroscape content.

—description from the publisher

Stay in the game by using your memory skills to repeat the existing action sequence before performing the action on the card. Make a mistake, and you are out!

Skills: Memorization, Gross Motor Skills

—description form the publisher

Gameplay
Players take turns drawing cards that instruct them to perform specific actions. The challenge lies in recalling and repeating the entire sequence of actions performed by all players so far, before adding the new action from the card they just drew. If a player makes a mistake in the sequence, they are out of the game. The game continues until only one player remains.

Objective
The goal is to be the last player remaining by successfully performing the growing sequence of actions without making a mistake.

—user summary

How do you win? Sliding, of course! Practice eye-hand coordination to slide accurately, because where you land determines what you win.

—description from the publisher

Pickles' Slide to Win is a dexterity game designed for 2 to 4 players, aimed at children ages 4 and up. The game emphasizes hand-eye coordination and teaches basic turn-taking and patience, making it a perfect fit for young players.

Gameplay
Players take turns using a marble slider to push a marble through a tunnel in an attempt to land on various stuffed animal images or a blue ribbon on the opposite side of the board. The objective is to win prizes by accurately sliding the marble to specific target areas. Each successful slide rewards the player with a stuffed animal prize, with the first player to collect five prizes declared the winner.

Objective / Victory Condition
The game ends when a player successfully collects five prizes. For added challenge, players can opt to require landing on the blue ribbon to win or increase the number of prizes needed for victory.

—user summary

Can you find the butterflies with the matching wings? Spin to see what kind of match to make - a color or shape or both. Everyone at the same time searches the butterfly field to collect as many matching butterflies as they can, so act fast.

—description from the publisher

Watch My Wings is a family-friendly, fast-paced matching game designed for 2 to 5 players aged 4 and up. The game revolves around quick thinking and visual perception, as players compete to find and collect butterflies with matching wings. Each round, players spin a flower spinner to determine the matching criteria—whether it's a specific color, shape, or a combination of both—and then race against each other to spot butterflies that fit the criteria in a shared field.

Victory Condition
The game ends when there are not enough tiles left to fill the field, and the player with the most collected butterfly tiles is declared the winner.

Components

     55 Butterfly Tiles
     1 Flower Spinner
     1 Rules Booklet

The Bases Loaded Edition contains all previously released Baseball Highlights: 2045 gameplay content as well as the All-Star Expansion.

American baseball was on its last legs as a spectator sport. Football had become the predominant national pastime — that is until the year 2032, the year baseball decided to revolutionize the game and regain the throne! Starting in 2032, baseball games were shortened to six innings. Pitchers were encouraged to have bionic arm implants to improve their pitching. These cyborgs, or 'Borgs as they're affectionately known, were immediately popular and soon ruled the league. In 2041, robotic players were introduced to get more offense back into the game. These robots were similar to designated hitters in that they were used only to bat and did not field. However, recent reports indicate fielding 'Bots are on the way.

Now in 2045, human players are still in the game and known as Naturals. They are the best fielders by far but are sorely challenged when it comes to hitting and pitching. Some Naturals have learned to hit by swinging before the pitcher starts his windup, which gives them a chance to hit the ball. Although it's hard for a Natural to get into the league, those who do are popular. Many Naturals have named themselves after the great players of pre-2032 baseball by taking a first and last name borrowed from different star players of the past. The fans love them, and their presence on the team ensures good revenue!

Baseball Highlights: 2045 is like watching TV highlights of early 21st-century baseball games, with the gameplay being full of theme with no outs or innings and without bogging down in a play-by-play baseball simulation. In this quick and interactive game, two players build their teams as they play, combining both strategy (building your team) and tactics (playing the game) without any of the downtime. During each "mini-game", each player alternates playing six cards to simulate a full game's highlights. The mini-game includes defensive and offensive actions, and your single card play may include elements of defensive and/or offensive plays.  Players buy new free agents after each mini-game to improve their roster, and the team who wins the most mini-games in the series is the champ!

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The All-Star Expansion brings some heavy hitters to the game as well as the following keywords: Golden Glove, which cancels 3 hits; Magna Glove Plus, which adds an additional effect to the Magna Glove; Bunt, which converts all Gloved or Canceled Hits to Bunts; Leaping Catch which cancels 2 Home Runs; and more.

-description from publisher

This is Expansion #11 in the Baseball Highlights 2045 range. It contains
- 15 All-Star cards
- 12 Promo cards

The All-Star cards are very powerful, and not to be used in the same way as the other expansion cards.  See this post by the game's designer for more information:

How to use the new All Star Expansion and promos in Baseball Highlights Bases Loaded

Merlin's Crucible is a mid-weight strategic board game that immerses players in the world of alchemy. Players collect various magical ingredients and use them to brew potions. Each potion has unique effects, and players must carefully manage their resources to maximize their efficiency.

The game features a blend of resource management, engine building, and combo-driven mechanics, requiring strategic thinking and planning.

Objective
The primary objective is to be the first player to achieve a specific number of victory points, which are gained by successfully brewing potions and summon various characters. Players must also adapt to the changing market conditions for ingredients and outmaneuver their opponents to claim the most valuable resources.

Background
Set in a world inspired by Arthurian legends, Merlin's Crucible invites players to step into the shoes of legendary alchemists who studied under Merlin himself. The game blends fantasy elements with strategic gameplay, offering a rich thematic experience.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
I would Buy Bitcoin is a standalone expansion for everyone's favorite new party game, I Would Kill Hitler.

From cryptocurrency to dog memes, this deck brings you 200 exclusive cards that you won't find anywhere else and can be played on its own or mixed in with the deck from I Would Kill Hitler. The gameplay is the same: Each player has 60 seconds to answer a question posed by the Hypothetical Cards by using the Plot Cards to craft a "true to them" response and the best one wins!

This game can be played on its own or combined with other I Would...sets for enhanced play.

I Would Meet Jesus is a divinely inspired standalone expansion for everyone's favorite new party game, I Would Kill Hitler. From pope culture to downright sacrilege, this deck brings you 200 exclusive cards that you won't find anywhere else and can be played on its own or mixed with I Would Kill Hitler.

Each player has 60 seconds to answer a question posed by the Hypothetical Cards by using the Plot Cards to craft a "true to them" response and the best one wins!

Includes a Jesus Box expansion pack, complete with themed cards intelligently designed in our Savior's image.

This game can be played on its own or combined with other I Would...sets for enhanced play.

boardgame: Lunar Rush: D.I.C.E. Tower Promo Gold Module Card
description:
Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

This is the 2024-2025 Promo Card Gold Module that is created with the DICE Tower promotion.

It expands the gold module collection in the base game to add more variety to the game.

Text says: The "Dedicated Interplanetary Container External Tower" allows you to increase one of your ship's (up or down route) capacity by 1 item.

-description from publisher

WEBB is a board game for 2-6 players that launches you on a mission to capture images from the distant corners of the universe. Use your knowledge of the cosmos and the strategy of tile-placement games to collect data and solve puzzles. With its easy-to-learn rules, stunning visuals, and dynamic gameplay, WEBB is the perfect game for families and space enthusiasts of all ages.

As die-hard space fans at Golden Bough Games, we’ve enjoyed following the image releases of the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), but think it’s a shame for such momentous photos to only capture the public’s attention for a news cycle or two. The mission behind this game is to introduce a novel tile-based game mechanic with JWST's images as the stars of the show. This creates an engaging platform for groups of board game and space connoisseurs to gather around and appreciate these photos time and time again.

—description from the publisher

This Gamefound-exclusive promo bundle contains two small expansions for Stupor Mundi:

1. Frederick's Sons

Frederick's sons enter the group of allies in the game. Both will reward you for staying consistent with the loyalty of your allies.


     Konrad IV will reward you if you have 3 or more loyalist allies in your court
     Henry VII will reward you if you have 3 or more independent allies in your court.


2. "Gates" Mini-expansion

This mini-expansion will earn players extra VP as they try and achieve different goals during each round.

boardgame: Camelot Quest: The Fall of Camelot
description:
Camelot Quest is a third-party expansion for HeroQuest that comes with:


     32 page Quest Book with 9 quests
     40 cards in a tuck box including Artifacts, Equipment, Treasure, Spells and more.
     Unique and original Monsters
     Access to a shared Google drive with optional tiles and tokens. 


There are two new heroes: The Black Knight and Mordred. Each character boasts unique Skill Cards and weapons.

boardgame: Blood Quest: For the Blood is Life
description:
Blood Quest is a third-party expansion for HeroQuest that comes with:


     32 page Quest Book with 9 quests
     38 cards in a tuck box including Artifacts, Equipment, Treasure, Spells and more.
     Unique and original Monsters
     Access to a shared Google drive with optional tiles and tokens.

History of the World retells the story of humanity, starting from ancient Sumeria and ending with modern day USA. The map emphasizes those lands where civilizations flourished, with over sixty peoples featured on empire and event cards.

Capitals, cities, monuments, and forts are brought into play and then, just as quickly, are reduced by the armies of rivals. Control of buildings and lands enables players to collect gold. Whosoever has the most gold at game's end wins.

Compared to earlier editions of History of the World, this edition:

• Allows you to play with or without dice rules.
• Has a different distribution of empire cards and event cards
• Features new event cards and gold collection mechanisms
• Allows for a new "area tokens" strategy
• Includes new "honor tokens" that provide benefits
• Has a new player array

In general, the spirit of the game has been left intact, while incorporating elements from the earliest editions of the game as well as later developments. "Catastrophe", for example, has now become an honor, and "Weaponry", which was always too strong an event, has been removed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Atlantis Quest: The Doom of Atlantis
description:
Atlantis Quest is a third-party expansion for HeroQuest that comes with:


     32 page Quest Book with 9 quests
     45 cards in a tuck box including Artifacts, Equipment, Treasure, Spells and more.
     Unique and original Monsters
     Access to a shared Google drive with optional tiles and tokens.

Dread Abilities is a third-party expansion/addition for HeroQuest. Dread Abilities is a tuck box containing 38 cards including the rules on how to use them.

Dread Abilities allows Zargon to give skills and abilities to his minions, elite warriors and bosses which can be applied to quests you’ve previously played, giving endless re-playability of these quests.

The abilities also mean Zargon can tailor the difficulty level for his forces, giving a fun and challenging experience to players. When elites have these skills and abilities, it really adds to immersion and creates ‘living’ opponents and stories.

boardgame: uChi Gueva!: Minsei vs. Zenkyoto, Battle of the University of Tokyo Campus
description:
uChi Gueva!: Minsei vs. Zenkyoto, Battle of the University of Tokyo Campus is a board wargame that deals with the "internal feud" between Minsei (Democratic Youth League of Japan) and Zenkyoto (All-Campus Joint Struggle Committees) during the University of Tokyo protests that occurred from 1968 to 1969.

One player, as the Zenkyoto leadership, aims to blockade all faculties at the University of Tokyo or to prevent the selection of a delegation to a university-wide assembly.

The other player, as the Minsei leadership, aims to prevent the blockade of all faculties at the University of Tokyo or to select a delegation to a university-wide rally.

Each player takes on the role of one of the six Moogle siblings. Each Moogle has a unique set of Battle Cards.

The aim is to be the best in the mob hunt, defeating monsters in various regions.

Monsters are placed between each player and in the centre of the play area. Players take turns preparing to defeat them by playing Battle Cards. However, each monster has different characteristics, so sometimes a little more strategy is required than simply playing the strongest card. Players can also play cards face down to hide the strength and abilities of the cards they have played. Use strategies that serve you best against each monster and bluff with face down cards to gain more victory points.

The game takes place in the FFXII world, with illustrations by FFXII's art director Ryoma Ito.

boardgame: Disney Lorcana: Scrooge McDuck Uncle Moneybags Promo
description:
Scrooge McDuck Uncle Moneybags Promo

Ability:
TREASURE FINDER Whenever this character quests, you pay 1 ink less for the next item you play this turn.

Cost: 2, Strenght: 1, Willpower: 3

Flavour:
A strange new world is the perfect place to make a strange new fortune!

Atlantis is in turmoil. Leaders of the most powerful Houses are in constant conflict, vying for control of the city Lately, their focus has turned to the Council of Elders, an ancient institution that rules Atlantis from the shadows.

As the head of one of these Houses, you will need to send out Agents to try and persuade the Elders to ‘see things your way’. You will need to skilfully navigate the Council’s ever-changing laws to secure your grasp on the world’s most magnificent city.

Players will split into 2 teams (or choose the All versus All mode). Each team tries to be the first to collect the 5 different Elder cards.

Elders are collected by playing the right card from your hand as either a Follower or Rebel. Followers take priority in collecting Elders, but Rebels can swing the Councils majority. This can be a huge deal, because the Council will determine which special rule will be active for the rest of the round.

At the end of each round, all the cards that were played advance to the Council and become the new Elders for the next round.

—description from the designer

After the nuclear war, our planet turned into a hot desert. There are only a few underground springs left that continue to produce the most important resource of the modern world - water. Surviving people have organized themselves into clans that compete for this dwindling resource. The only places of hope for survival are the remnants of military organizations...

About the game
Intention is a survival strategy in a post-apocalyptic world.
The main goal is to capture the central hex on the playing field and hold it for a certain number of rounds.

Gameplay

Preparing for the game:

Before the start of the game, each player chooses the color of his clan and places his chips opposite one of the six edges of the playing field, according to the chosen color.

Each player has three types of mutant warriors:

Bony are melee fighters who are ready for direct contact with the enemy.
Handsome - riflemen who can shoot from a distance.
Smart guys use mental power to inflict damage on enemies.
Players also have access to fast-growing plants with large thorns that can be used as defensive barriers.

Each type of warrior has three levels of mutation with its own combat characteristics and other important data that are indicated on the cards. Improvements and temporary abilities affect all warriors of the same type on the board, not just one specific warrior. As for the speedsters, they have three levels of enhancement, and temporary enhancements do not apply to them.

The course of the game:

Each player chooses his clan and places his warrior chips on the appropriate places on the playing field. Hexagons numbered “0” - “3” determine the place of landing and the counter of winning points.

Players take turns, and each turn consists of three phases:

The tactical phase is the war phase, where the player moves his mutant warriors around the field and attacks enemy pieces. Each warrior can move according to its movement characteristics and attack opponents with its health. After the attack, the pieces are flipped over, and the player can skip a turn or attack enemy pieces and his own warriors.
Extraction Phase - the player collects resources indicated on the cards of sources, bunkers and ray towers. Source cards are exchanged for currency tokens, bunkers provide upgrades for warriors, and ray towers allow you to receive and use special cards without other players discovering them.
Economic Phase - players spend their accumulated currency tokens to buy new warriors, warrior upgrades, grow fast, and make various purchases on the black market.

End of the game
The game ends when one of the players successfully captures the central hex on the board and holds it for a specified number of rounds.

The game has different modes, such as Long Game, Blitz Game, and Team Game, each with its own victory conditions.

—description from the publisher

The campaign is an expansion for the game Bulánci: Speciální jednotky.

In the role of young cadets you are trying to accomplish your first trivial task. But nothing is as easy as it may seem at first glance. A total of 40 original missions await you, during which you will sometimes be subjected to ruthless firefights, other times it will require a sensitive approach, you will experience tension, joy and sadness, but we believe that above all a lot of fun.

—description from the publisher


The rules are the same as in the cooperative game. However, individual missions modify the rules slightly to suit their own needs. It is therefore always necessary to read Mission Specialty section carefully. The Mission Difficulty paragraph usually states how many points each player must achieve to successfully complete the mission.
  
Sometimes the difficulty is scaled according to the number of players.
  
In a 4-player game, 1 player can only fail if the players are all doing the same task (each on their own).
   
The newer edition of Bulánci (Commander's Edition, 2024) already contains this expansion in printed form.

Big Orange: Good Luck is a light strategy board game that supports 2-4 players. Players will play as a "heavy orange enthusiast" and strategically plan to fight, peel, and gather oranges to complete one task after another, ultimately becoming the highly anticipated "Orange King". Hurry up and invite your friends to a thrilling orange party with splashing juice!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Mana Spring, an exhilarating card game for 2-6 players where mages clash in a magical battle for dominance over a realm rich in mana. In this fantasy-driven game, players assume the roles of powerful heroes, each wielding unique abilities and spells as they vie for control of the mystical Mana Spring.

In Mana Spring, strategic gameplay revolves around managing your mana reserves and using them as a resource to cast spells and disrupt your opponents. The innovative mana overload and surge mechanics adds a layer of tactical depth, as players must balance their mana accumulation and expenditure to avoid taking in too much mana and harming themselves.

Game Features:

Unique Hero Abilities: Each hero comes with a distinctive spell and passive ability that shapes your strategy and offers diverse gameplay experiences.

Engaging Spell System: Use a mix of offensive and defensive spells, and engage in counterplay with the game's counterspell mechanic to stay ahead of your opponents.

Dynamic Mana Management: Carefully manage your mana to harness its power without succumbing to overload and its detrimental effects.

Surge Cards: These wild cards introduce unexpected twists and chaotic fun to every game, keeping each session fresh and unpredictable

—description from the publisher

Promo expansion for Whale to Look.

A golden lucky charm! It's a bit larger than the whale already in the game. The Shachihoko will appear wherever there are exactly 10 fish and any players in that area receive 2 points.

Ark Nova: Zoo Map Pack 2 features five new zoo designs.

The “Tournament 1” design is on all four game boards so that players can compete with the same layout.

Four different zoo boards with new and interesting abilities are on the opposite sides of the Tournament game boards:

Artificial Intelligence
Caves
Drawing Board
Lagoon
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Robo Rally: Turn & Burn consists of three new double-sided game boards featuring new elements such as chop shops, repulsor fields, and trap door pits, as well as five new upgrade cards.

“Two kinds of people are staying on this beach—the dead and those who are going to die.”
Colonel Taylor, 16th Infantry Regiment, 1st Infantry Division, 6 June 1944

Omaha beach was the most fiercely contested of the five Normandy invasion beaches. After the initial landings at 0631 hours, the success of the Omaha operation remained in doubt until well into D-Day. Fully half of the Allied casualties suffered on the five invasion beaches were incurred on ‘Bloody Omaha’. At noon, General Omar Bradley was contemplating abandoning Omaha and re-directing follow-on forces to Utah beach. This would have been a tremendous setback, jeopardizing the success of the entire Normandy invasion. At stake was the victory of freedom over fascism.

Now you are in command, and the decisions are yours…

D-Day: Omaha Beach Solitaire has 24 games. You take the role of Major General Leonard Gerow commanding the US Army V Corps for 12 games against an opposing German autonomous player side, referred to as the German BOT side. And you take the role of Generalleutnant Dietrich Kraiss for 12 games commanding the German 352nd Infantry Division against an American BOT side. Each opposing BOT side is rated for its skill: Challenging for 4 games, Veteran for 4 games, and Tough for 4 games.

One side (either American or German) is played by you and uses their player actions. The BOT opponent uses the solitaire actions for the other side.

A player can issue one action per game turn from their available actions. You also have the option of using a Flank Attack with a supporting flanking action to the one main action played. Your choices can cause Die Roll Modifiers (DRM) that affect attacks with results that cause unit eliminations and movement to occur.

The object of the game for the American player is to defeat the Germans by the end of the game. This is accomplished by eliminating ALL German strongpoints and ALL units from at least 2 of the 3 Towns, at the end of any turn.

The object of the game for the German player is to eliminate ALL American units in any 3 or more bluff areas, at the end of Turn 18.

All you will need is a pen, a die, and a sense of adventure to play the game.

—description from the publisher
Robo Rally: Contamination consists of three double-sided game boards featuring new elements like radiation, radioactive waste, and one-way walls, along with five new upgrade cards.

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Violent Outburst Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Ragin Raptor vinyl mini figure and Violent Outburst promo card.

Card text:
VIOLENT OUTBURST
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then move a Disaster card from your Disaster Area to the bottom of the Disaster deck.

An official expansion pack for DOOM PILGRIM board game. 
The pack includes 3 expansions:
FUTILE HAVENS: 18 cards that make the game easier.
FATAL ENDS: 18 cards that make the game harder.
PAGAN ECHOES: 18 cards that bring the paganism theme.

—description from the designer

The Forgotten Gods is a board game in which each player must try on the role of one of the Norse gods (only the player on the left knows your real name, and using the playing cards you must bet on your guess) and compete with others in their ability to tell stories about themselves and others (this should be done based on the cards in your hands and the symbols depicted on them). Other players who have the right cards for this can interfere with your story and change the outcome of your game. Try to be the first to remember who you really are.

The game is based on combinatorics, which by the method of exclusion will help players make the right assumptions. The game also encourages imagination of interesting narration, because at the end of the circle of stories, other players are obliged to reward someone with a special token, which will allow to exclude one assumption.

boardgame: Mission Control: Critical Orbit – Aethernaut Resupply Pack
description: Expansion for Mission Control: Critical Orbit that contains 1 rules card and 6 new mission objective cards, collectively contain 36 Freeze Dried Sub-Quantum Objectives.

Misty of Mood (心灵迷局) is a family board game designed to help children learn about and manage their emotions in a playful and engaging way. The game focuses on emotional intelligence, guiding players through different emotional scenarios and teaching them how to handle both positive and negative emotions.

Objective
The goal is to navigate through the emotional landscape and reach the final space on the board, where the first player to do so wins the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
YOUR CHRISTMAS MISSION, SHOULD YOU CHOOSE TO ACCEPT IT:
Give each of your guests a Mince Spies coaster at the beginning of the evening. Guests must carry out the secret mission printed on the bottom of each coaster throughout the course of the evening without the other Mince Spies discovering what it is! Can you successfully complete your mission without anyone catching you out?

Battle your friends using Thoughts (cards) to inflict or deflect Trauma (damage) in this turn-based, combat-strategy card game. Players use the simple deck building mechanic to construct one of 20 playable decks; that's nearly 400 possible 1-on-1 match-ups right out of the box! Each card represents an action or item, but also acts as part of your resource pool, as well as your life total! Trauma forces cards to be discarded in an attempt to get the upper hand on your opponent. Once a player has accumulated 15 or more Trauma, they are out of the game. The game ends with the last player remaining declared the victor.

The Forevergone is an XCG, or Expandable Card Game, and as such, includes everything you need to play the full core game as part of the initial purchase. No additional purchase is necessary for two people to go head-to-head!

And because everyone loves pretty stuff, we have also included a collectible/tradable component in parallel with the game for people interested in our stunning artwork and talented artists! These cards are purely for additional fun, and have no bearing on the game at all. Each art card in the collection features the artwork from one of our supremely talented artists, the title of the image, name of the artist, and where you can go online to find out more about that artist. We love our artists, and we'd love for you to love them too!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Journey to the West: Eighty-one Perils
description:
Journey to The West: Eighty-one Perils (西游释厄传) is a competitive ladder climbing card game. Players embark on an adventurous journey filled with challenges inspired by the classic Chinese novel, Journey to the West.

Gameplay
Players take turns playing cards from their hands, with each card representing different characters or creatures from the Journey to the West universe. The objective is to follow up on the previous player's card with a higher strength card or a card with special ability. These abilities faithfully reflect the characters' powers and relationships from the original story. Failure to do so forces the player to collect all the cards on the table, which become "Peril Cards." The game continues until the deck and the players' cards are both exhausted.

Victory Condition
The player with the least number of Peril Cards at the end of the game wins.
publishers: 星空桌游 Starry Sky Games

boardgame: Dr Seuss Thing One and Thing Two Where Are You? Game
description:
"A hide-and-seek game of fun with Thing Two and Thing One! Where are the Things hiding? Flip a tile and ask a question to get a clue. When you go look, search the house up and down! Find the Things to win!" A Dr. Seuss game. Babysitter approved. 
1) The Hider hides Thing One and Thing Two. 2) The Seekers ask questions and get clues from the Hider! 3) The Seekers hurry and look!
family: Books: The Cat in the Hat

description:
In Three Kingdoms Go Go Chess (三国走走棋), players engage in a strategic board game that blends classic MOBA elements with tactical warfare. The game features generals, soldiers, and war vehicles, with a map comprising barracks, arrow towers, and riverways. Players must strategically deploy their forces and utilize cards to gain an advantage, blending tactical placement with card-based strategies.

Objective
The goal is to outmaneuver and outwit your opponents using a combination of tactical positioning and card play. Players aim to achieve one of several victory conditions :

     Destroying enemy fortifications
     Capturing key positions
     Eliminating all opposing generals
     Seizing the enemy’s central stronghold
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
三盗 (Three Thieves) is a deduction and bluffing game for 2-4 players. Set in the world of traditional Bejing opera, players assume the roles of either Thieves or Heroes in a race to outwit each other.

The game starts with the Thieves drawing two secret role cards from a deck of twelve; the remaining cards are designated as Heroes. Throughout the game, players move character pieces along various paths up a mountain. Thieves aim to steal the precious Jade Cup, while Heroes work to prevent the theft by locking characters in place at specific checkpoints.

Objective
The Thieves win by successfully stealing the Jade Cup if at least one Thief avoids capture by the end of the game.
The Heroes win if they manage to lock up both Thieves before they can complete their heist.

Background
The game is inspired by the Beijing opera "三盗九龙杯" (Three Thieves and the Jade Cup), combining elements of traditional Chinese storytelling with modern deduction gameplay.

Haywire is a fast paced, class based, tactical skirmish scale game for both solo and coop play set in the modern days.

Deploy on the battlefield one of the 16 default units available or assemble your own by using one of the 30 available classes from a breacher to an undercover operator or a dog handler.

Choose where to deploy your team or pick one of the 30 random infil points like a covert van, an up armored humvee or a crashed helicopter. Select your mission from one of the 30 available, anything like a recon mission to a HVT capture or a bomb defusal.

Gain tactical superiority by deploying strategic support like a recon drone or a combat air support strafing run but be careful of the fog of war event deck that can completely change your plan at any moment.

Haywire comes with more than 200 print and play cards offering endless gameplay possibility and replayability.

There was a fierce battle over the succession of the throne and the estate. Win with one powerful stroke.

Meet East and West's historic strategy game in one game!
'Omok' means five eyes. Omok is a strategic game that is easy and fun for everyone from children to adults in Asia, and the one who puts the five stones side by side first wins.

Omok chess is a strategic game that combines the rules of Omok and the rules of chess to create new fun.
Omok chess is a game in which five chips are placed side by side. The one who completes 2sets of 'Omok' faster than the opponent wins.

Win the Hundred Years' War by Overpowering Your Opponents with Outstanding Strategies!

Components

     Chips 40ea (20ea*2colors)
     Chess men 8ea (4ea*2colors)
     Point chips 10ea
     Crown Chips 8ea
     Game Board 1ea


Set of Game

     Place the board on the table. 
     Distribute the chips to each of the players.
         1 color Chips 20ea / person
         1 color chess men 4ea / person
         Point chips 5ea / person
         Crown chips 4ea / person
    
     Set the order and start the game.


Playing the Game

     When it's your turn, place one chip on the game board. Every time you put one chip, you get one point chip. You can get up to five point chips.
     Place your five chips side by side to complete the wall. The one who completes the two walls first wins.
         With three point chips, you can put a chessman chip on the game board.
         Two pointe chips allow you to move your chessmen, attack your opponent's chips or chessmen and send them off the game board.
         Chessmen exported off the game board can't come back. Regular chips are reusable.
    


End Game

     The one who completes the two walls first wins.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Don't Let It Die: Dead Valley – Character Pack 1
description:
In the unforgiving lands of Dead Valley, a tribe of six rugged and feral warriors faced a grim reality. Resources had dwindled to near extinction, and hope for a solution was as rare as a green oasis in their barren wasteland. But one fateful day, Yurt stumbled upon a crudely etched picture on a weathered boulder. After inspection it seemed to be some kind of map depicting a tantalizing vision of a lush, hidden land beyond the once-frozen, now-thawing mountains.

Fueled by the promise of salvation and the hunger gnawing at their bellies, the Dead Valley tribe resolved to escape their desolate home and brave the treacherous ascent over the newly thawed peaks.

Their journey was fraught with peril. As they scaled the towering cliffs, among other horrible things, they encountered fierce sabertooths, their eyes gleaming with hunger. With makeshift weapons and sheer determination, the tribe fought off the primal predators, their battle cries echoing through the mountains.

As they all descended from the treacherous mountain, they were met with a breathtaking sight. Spread before them was a lush valley teeming with life and abundant resources, a stark contrast to the desolation they had left behind in Dead Valley.

Their joy was short-lived, as they noticed another tribe not too far from their current position. This new tribe appeared to be encircling a peculiar sight - a glowing pile of trees, radiating an otherworldly luminescence.

Curiosity piqued, the Dead Valley tribe cautiously approached the other tribe. They exchanged wary glances, unsure of what to expect. As they drew nearer, it became clear that this glowing pile of trees held a profound significance to the other tribe.

Gathering around the mysterious phenomenon, the two tribes began to communicate, overcoming their initial suspicions. They learned that the glowing tree pile possessed special powers and that the other tribe was trying to figure out how to make another one.

In this newfound alliance, the tribes shared their knowledge and skills, realizing that together, they could thrive in this abundant land. And so, side by side with the other tribe, whose origins were as diverse as the landscape itself, the new tribe forged a community.

—description from the publisher
A set containing a solo mode for Hannibal.

boardgame: Tiny Mini Golf: Lone Shooter Expansion
description:
The Lone Shooter expansion introduces a solo experience with the Tiny Mini Tour — a series of pre-drawn holes that challenge the same design and playing skills crucial in the base game’s multiplayer modes, but in a completely new way. It also includes rules for replaying previously created courses and a remote play mode for two or more players using electronic devices with an internet connection.

-description from designer

Part collector's item and part multiplayer dueling game, 99 Heroes combines the beauty of collecting with the joy of executing awesome card combos. 99 Heroes packs contain giant linen finish cards with gold foiling. These cards feature beautiful art from your favorite Level 99 Games. You can use these cards to create a team of heroes and duel one, two, or even four of your friends during a game night! All you need are some heroes and playing cards!

—description from the publisher

Local police have been alerted by multiple reports of suspicious activity at the mysterious hilltop mansion. Concerned residents claim to have heard disturbing sounds, from the roar of chainsaws to terrifying screams, causing commotion on the town's streets.

Rumors have spread quickly, keeping the community in suspense due to the uncertainty surrounding the enigmatic house.

Bajo Amenaza is a semi-cooperative suspense and group deck-building game, for 3 to 6 players with a duration of 20 minutes.

Wicked & Wise: Reflections is an expansion that adds in a new type of Basic card to the Wicked & Wise game. The new cards can count for two, three, or four suits adding in new strategies to the game. They can allow you to sneak a suit into your opponent’s winnings that they didn’t plan on and can get you to your goals faster, but might mean that it’s not as easy to play your Gem cards.

There are two new goals and 6 new treasures. The treasures focus on manipulating the cards you win or lose in tricks. Will your goals be easier or more difficult to reach? That all depends on the teamwork between dragons and mice!

-description from publisher

Hydras & Otters team up to unlock satisfying combos while exploring reefs and ship ruins for treasure in Reef & Ruins, a Roll & Write that gives you the satisfaction of a crunchy large sheet, small font Roll & Write spatial puzzle engine builder in a smaller, quicker, more approachable manor.

There’s one die for each head of the Hydra, either for them to venture into shipwrecks to search for treasure, work together to enchant their treasure, or send otters to the reef to get pearls. Each head of the hydra typically wants to do something different, but they occasionally can agree to work together, for hydra’s sake.

At the start of each game, players will choose Ship Ruins, Enchantments, and Reef sheets and all compete to unearth the most valuable items. Players play simultaneously, each choosing between sending their hydras into the ruins for treasure, their otters to enchant the treasures, or to send both into the reef to gain bonuses there. After 18 rounds, players figure out the values of their found treasures, then enchant them for more points, then add in any reef benefits. The game ends before players will be able to finish everything, so they must make the best choices for what Ruins to complete, how many enchantments to work on, and when to go searching the Reef for pearls!

Reef & Ruins is a roll and write standalone game set in the Wicked & Wise universe.

-description from publisher

Logic 99 is a mathematical reasoning game designed for children. The game challenges players to use logic and mathematical concepts, such as prime numbers and multiplication/division, to deduce the values of their opponents' cards.

It was released as part of Yaofish's Math Game Set 4-IN-1.

Gameplay

     Each player draws 10 cards and arranges them in ascending order. 
     Players take turns guessing the values of their opponents' cards based on the hints provided on the back.
     The first player to accurately guess all opponents' cards and reveal their values wins.

Roll dice and use the times table to see if you collect either a crew card, a ships cat or an action card, or a rat.

Collect a full crew and a pirate ship to win, but get rid of any rats on the ship first.

Play Duel, Walk the plank and Seize a ship cards to help yourself or foil your opponents.

QIC (Quickly Intermixed Connections) is a simple, fast-paced transformational card stacking game for one or more  players aged 4 and up. It’s a game where the playing field changes with each drop of a card. QIC is a bit like a slot machine with symbols, words and numbers.

The goal is to construct rows of words, symbols and numbers
and collect the most points.

COMPONENTS
• 36 two-sided cards
• 50 point-tracking cards.

SETUP
Shuffle the cards and form a stack between the players.
Draw the top three cards and place adjacently as the tableau.
Separate the point cards and place face up next to the draw 
pile.

GAME PLAY
Taking turns, each player picks up the next card from the draw pile and examines both sides, then places the card on top of one of the existing three cards. The player attempts to construct one or more rows with these conditions:

1. A three-letter word appears
2. All symbols are identical
3. The sum of all the numbers is 10.
 Instant Scoring:
A player gains points for each row made as follows:
One point per constructed row.
Three points if two rows are made.
Five points if three rows are formed.
Collect matching points cards upon construction.

Ending the Round:
The round ends when the draw pile has been exhausted.
Players keep their point cards. Someone shuffles the cards and a new round starts.

Ending the Game:
After playing two rounds, the winner has the most points.

SOLO GAME
The goal for one player is to collect the most points after one round. Repeat several times to get higher scores.

-description from designer
